PN CN113041833-A
TI High-activity calcium-based absorbent containing ion transport channel prepared by e.g. introducing sodium ion in absorbent lattice, associating ion replacement with large vacancy defect and forming ion transport channel.
AU LI M
   REN L
   GUO Y
AE UNIV SHENYANG TECHNOLOGY (UYTE-C)
GA 202178754Y
AB    NOVELTY - High-activity calcium-based absorbent containing ion transport channel, prepared by introducing sodium ion in the absorbent lattice, associating ion replacement with a large vacancy defect, forming an ion transport channel in the carbonation product layer of the carbon dioxide adsorption process, is claimed. The absorbent crystal grain size is 150-300 nm, the associated vacancy and grain stack form comprises 1-2 nm of micro-pore, 2-10 nm of mesoporous and 100-500 nm of wide covering range of porous structure.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-activity calcium-based absorbent containing ion transmission channel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of high-active calcium-based absorbent with improved conversion efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for preparing a high activity calcium-based absorbent containing ion transport channel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The preparation raw material of the absorbent is a natural limestone calcined product, and a hydrated ion adsorption process is adopted to fully contact and mix sodium ion and calcium ion. The preparation method utilizes natural limestone, ethanol, sodium carbonate powder, and calcium oxide. The prepared high-activity calcium-based absorbent has a carbon dioxide adsorption rate of 85-90%.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC J01-E

PN JP2021092224-A; JP7004887-B2
TI Engine used for burning and producing oxygen and hydrogen for producing electricity, comprises hydrogen supply unit, oxygen supply unit, and water supply unit, and ignition unit for igniting oxygen and hydrogen introduced into engine.
AU IZUMI K
AE IZUMI K (IZUM-Individual)
   IZUMI K (IZUM-Individual)
GA 202165454B
AB    NOVELTY - An engine comprises hydrogen supply unit, oxygen supply unit, and water supply unit, which are provided to introduce hydrogen, oxygen, and water into the engine, and ignition unit for igniting oxygen and hydrogen introduced into engine and combusting. The introduced water absorbs heat in the combustion chamber to generate water vapor (A) in which volume is expanded and steam (B). The steam (A) and steam (B) generated by combustion of the oxygen and hydrogen are discharged as exhaust.
   USE - Engine used for burning and producing oxygen and hydrogen used as fuel for producing electricity. Uses include but are not limited to parking lots, motor vehicles, boats and ships, farming apparatus, electric trains, construction machine, forestry working apparatus and aircraft.
   ADVANTAGE - The engine has simple structure with reduced greenhouse gas emissions and does not emit hydrogen and carbon dioxide.

PN CN112899034-A
TI Performing circulating fluidized bed gasification method involves feeding raw coal and fluidized gas into a circulating fluidized bed gasifier through a feed port, then reacting with the gasification agent at the bottom of the furnace.
AU TANG F
   WANG G
   XING X
   ZHANG W
   XIAO D
   HU S
   PI J
   XIAO X
   XIA Y
   XU J
   XIA W
AE CHINA WUHUAN ENG CO LTD (CNCE-C)
GA 202166952E
AB    NOVELTY - Performing circulating fluidized bed gasification method involves feeding raw coal and fluidized gas into a circulating fluidized bed gasifier through a feed port, then reacting with the gasification agent at the bottom of the furnace, and discharging the reacted crude synthesis gas from the top of the circulating fluidized bed gasification furnace. The fluidization gas contains at least 20 vol.% or more of the crude synthesis gas from the subsequent process after washing. The gasification agent contains at least 50 vol.% carbon dioxide gas removed from the subsequent process.
   USE - Gasification method for a circulating fluidized bed.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple process flow, low system operation cost and investment cost, high dust removing efficiency, low inert gas content in raw gas, and high effective gas content in synthesis gas and good adaptability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fluidization gas is composed of crude synthesis gas and carbon dioxide gas. The gasification agent is composed of carbon dioxide gas and steam. Preferred Conditions: The fluidization gas is composed of 20-60 vol.% crude synthesis gas and 40-80 vol.% carbon dioxide gas. The gasification agent is composed of 50-80 vol.% carbon dioxide gas and 20-50 vol.% steam. The nitrogen content in the crude synthesis gas at the top of the circulating fluidized bed gasifier is controlled to be below 3 vol.%.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

PN CN112853545-A; CN112853545-B
TI Preparing boron-boron co-doped carbon nanofiber            material used as electro-reduction catalyst, involves            mixing reactant and additive with organic solvent for            heating for reacting, and calcining boron imidazole            framework material.
AU LIU D
   MA X
   WANG X
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 202161753T
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing boron-boron co-doped                carbon nanofiber material, involves uniformly                mixing reactant and additive with organic solvent                under inert gas or nitrogen environment for heating                and reacting to obtain boron imidazole framework                material, and calcining obtained boron imidazole                framework material to obtain nitrogen-boron                co-doped carbon nanofiber material, where the                reactant comprises zinc salt and/or boron imidazole                ligand.
   USE - The method is used for preparing boron-boron                co-doped carbon nanofiber material as                electro-reduction catalyst for electrochemical                reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts easy-to-obtain raw                materials, is simple, economical,                environmentally-friendly and suitable for scale up                production, and the prepared nitrogen-boron                co-doped carbon nanofiber material has rich pore                structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The zinc salt is zinc                  nitrate hexahydrate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  zinc salt and boron imidazole ligand in reactant                  is (2-4):1. Preferred Components: The                  boronimidazole ligand is potassium                  trimethylimidazole borohydride. The reactant                  further comprises dimethylimidazole, and the                  molar ratio of zinc salt, boron imidazole ligand                  and dimethylimidazole in reactant is                  (2-4):1:(1-10). The additive is trimesic acid,                  where the organic solvent comprises mixed                  solution comprising N,N-dimethylformamide,                  ethylene urea and ethanol, where the ratio of                  N,N-dimethylformamide, ethylene urea and ethanol                  in organic solvent is (10-30) ml:(1-10) g:(10-30)                  ml. Preferred Conditions: The method involves                  uniformly mixing reactants and additives,                  dissolving in organic solvent, and heating at                  70-100 degrees C for 3-5 days. The calcining                  process of boron imidazole framework material                  involves increasing the temperature to 800-1200                  degrees C at a certain heating rate of 1-10                  degrees C/minute for calcination, and maintaining                  the temperature for 1-3 hours.

PN KR2021031804-A
TI Bacterial detection system for detecting food spoilage using mobile terminal, has detection device which immediately determines whether food is spoiled or not, and easily attaches detection sensor and seals storage container.
AU YOUNG L J
AE YOUNG L J (YOUN-Individual)
GA 202129873U
AB    NOVELTY - The system has detection device which detects whether food is spoiled when it is doubtful, and immediately determines whether food is spoiled or not. The device easily attaches a detection sensor and seals the storage container.
   USE - Bacterial detection system for detecting food spoilage using mobile terminal.
   ADVANTAGE - The device eliminates concerns about health risks caused by eating spoiled food. The system detects a characteristic unique chemical component that appears when the food spoils in order to check whether food is spoiled. The system immediately checks whether the food is spoiled when the generation of carbon dioxide is detected or other components are detected. The sensor detects chemical components during food spoilage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the bacterial detection system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)

PN KR2021000260-U
TI Electric mask, particularly electromotion mask, comprises suction pipe that is connected with suction port connected with fan, and filter is formed on rear side of fan, and exhaust hole provides air sucked by electric motor.
AU LEE J Y
AE LEE J Y (LEEJ-Individual)
GA 202113062P
AB    NOVELTY - The electric mask comprises a suction pipe that is connected with a suction port connected with a fan. A filter is formed on the rear side of the fan. An exhaust hole provides the air sucked by the electric motor. A funnel-shaped hard material is spaced from the mouth by a predetermined distance. A filter is formed behind the fan and the motor. The funnel-type air injector is sprayed through the mouth from the side of a fixture. The fine dust is filtered through the mucous membrane on the nose hair of the nose. The pipe and the filter are formed of a frame made of a hard material.
   USE - Electric mask, particularly electromotion mask.
   ADVANTAGE - The fine dust, and carbon dioxide entry is prevented for electric mask.

PN KR2211640-B1
TI Membrane used for separating mixed gas, comprises polymer separator or porous polymer support, and copolymer selection layer formed from monomer containing UV reactive functional group, and polyethylene glycol-based monomer.
AU CHANG B J
   KIM J
   PARK C Y
   MOON S
   KONG C I
AE KOREA RES INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (KRIC-C)
GA 2021129884
AB    NOVELTY - A gas separation membrane comprises a polymer separator or a porous polymer support, and a copolymer selection layer fixed and crosslinked on a surface of the polymer separator or the porous polymer support. The copolymer is formed from a monomer containing an UV reactive functional group and a polyethylene glycol-based monomer. The copolymer selection layer is formed by forming a covalent bond with a polymer separation membrane or a porous polymer support.
   USE - Membrane is used for separating mixed gas e.g. carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, hydrogen, oxygen, nitrogen, methane, steam, ethylene, ethane, propylene and propane (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The membrane has excellent stability, weather resistance and process performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for gas separation method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The UV reactive functional group is at least one chosen from benzophenone group, acetophenone group, cyclohexyl phenyl ketone group, 2-hydroxy-2-methylpropiophenone group, vinyl group and acrylic group.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyethylene glycol-based monomer is chosen from polyethylene glycol methyl ether methacrylate, polyethylene glycol acrylate, polyethylene glycol methyl ether acrylate, polypropylene glycol methyl methacrylate and polypropylene glycol methyl ether acrylate. The polymer separator or porous polymer support is made of at least one of polysulfone, polyethylene, polypropylene, polyvinylidene fluoride, polyimide, polyamide, polyetherimide, polyamidoimide, polydimethylsiloxane and cellulose acetate. The copolymer selection layer includes at least one phenyl group and at least one hydroxy group. The copolymer comprises at least one phenyl group and at least one carbonyl group. Preferred Composition: The copolymer contains 0.1-40 wt.% ultraviolet-reactive functional group.

TI Kit for useful for improving stability of reagent for detecting calcium ion by o-cresolphthalein complex ketone method, comprises buffering, stabilizing agent, o-cresol phthalein ketone, natural or synthetic polysaccharide, and glycerol.
AU WU X
AE BIOSINO BIO-TECHNOLOGY & SCI INC (BIOS-Non-standard)
GA 202113479V
AB    NOVELTY - A kit for detecting calcium ion comprises reagent 1 for buffering and reagent 2 for developing color; the reagent 1 comprises stabilizing agent; the reagent 2 comprises o-cresol phthalein ketone; the stabilizing agent comprises natural or synthetic polysaccharide, or glycerol; the concentration of the stabilizing agent in the reagent 1 is 250-500 g/l.
   USE - The kit is useful for improving the stability of the reagent for detecting calcium ion by o-cresolphthalein complex ketone method (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The reagent increases solution viscosity, blocks diffusion action of carbon dioxide in the gas-liquid interface and the solution, effectively prolongs the opening-cover stable period more than 4 times, prolongs pH value and has obvious effect on detecting accuracy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The stabilizer includes glycerol, mannitol, trehalose, sorbitol, gelatin, dextran, hydroxyethyl starch, polyvinylpyrrolidone, and/or sodium hydroxymethylcellulose. The reagent 1 further comprises a buffer and a surfactant. The surfactant is a nonionic surfactant, and the concentration in reagent 1 is 1-15 g/l. The buffer solution includes ethanolamine, ethylenediamine, triethylamine, ammonia, potassium acetate, hydrochloric acid, ethylene glycol, boric acid, sodium hydroxide, 2-amino-methyl-1-propanol and/or trimethylolaminomethane, the hydrophilic group of the surfactant is glycerol, polyethylene glycol or sorbitol, the lipophilic group is a long-chain fatty acid, a long-chain fatty alcohol, an alkyl group or an aryl group and the lipophilic group is combined with the hydrophilic group as an ester group or an ether group. The reagent 2 further comprises includes a masking agent, and the masking agent includes 8-hydroxyquinoline, hydrochloric acid, benzoic acid, tartaric acid, glutathione, dithiothreitol, dithioerythritol, dimethyl sulfoxide, methanol and/or ethanol. The buffer solution includes two of ethylenediamine, tris, ethanolamine or AMP, and is 70-200ml/l. The surfactant includes two kinds of hydrochloric acid, Triton (RTM: Octyl phenol polyethoxylates), and polyoxylauryl ether, which are 1-15 g/l.

TI Retrofitting biomass host plant comprises installing reactor system within or adjacent to plant, conveying co-product to reactor system, pyrolyzing co-product using system, oxidizing pyrolysis off-gas and recycling and utilizing energy.
AU MENNELL J A
   DESPEN D J
   REAMER D
AE CARBON TECHNOLOGY HOLDINGS LLC (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 202109265K
AB    NOVELTY - Retrofitting a biomass host plant, comprises: (i) installing a reactor system within or adjacent to the host plant; (ii) conveying a co-product to the reactor system, where the co-product comprises carbon; (iii) pyrolyzing the co-product using the reactor system, thus generating a carbon-containing biogenic reagent and pyrolysis off-gas; (iv) oxidizing the pyrolysis off-gas, thus generating carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and energy; and (v) recycling and utilizing the energy in the host plant.
   USE - The method is useful for retrofitting a biomass host plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes: the reactor system which has a throughput capacity of 10-1000 ton/day on a dry basis, preferably 50-500 ton/day on a dry basis; and the oxidation unit which has an energy-generation capacity of 10-20 million Btu/hour.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method comprises recycling and utilizing the carbon monoxide in the reactor system. Preferred Components: The biomass comprises softwood chips, hardwood chips, timber harvesting residues, tree branches, tree stumps, leaves, bark, sawdust, maize, maize stover, wheat, wheat straw, rice, rice straw, sugarcane, sugarcane bagasse, sugarcane straw, energy cane, sugar beets, sugar beet pulp, sunflowers, sorghum, canola, algae, miscanthus, alfalfa, switchgrass, fruits, fruit shells, fruit stalks, fruit peels, fruit pits, vegetables, vegetable shells, vegetable stalks, vegetable peels, vegetable pits, grape pumice, almond shells, pecan shells, coconut shells, coffee grounds, food waste, commercial waste, grass pellets, hay pellets, wood pellets, cardboard, paper, paper pulp, paper packaging, paper trimmings, food packaging, lignin, animal manure, municipal solid waste and/or municipal sewage. The co-product comprises wood waste, sawdust, fines, bark, distillers grains, residual solids from fermentation, lignocellulosic residues, lignin and/or carbon-containing ash.

PN RU2740349-C1

AU ZYRYANOV V V
AE AS SIBE SOLID STATE CHEM MECHANOCHEM INS (ASSO-Non-standard)
GA 202112571E
AB    NOVELTY - Method involves correction of fuel composition by introduction of inorganic additives, allows use of mixed fuel for directed production of energy-intensive microspherical materials with controlled dispersion. PN CN111944950-A
TI Processing converter Ruhrstahl-Heraeus steel carbon deoxidation and slag modification comprises tapping Ruhrstahl-Heraeus steel leaves station after converter to add carbon-based deoxidizer to ladle for slag modification, and reacting.
AU WEI J
   XUE C
   GUO Z
   WANG J
   ZHANG R
AE BENGANG STEEL PLATES CO LTD (BSTP-C)
GA 2020B7205F
AB    NOVELTY - Processing converter Ruhrstahl-Heraeus (RH) steel carbon deoxidation and slag modification comprises tapping the RH steel leaves the station after the converter to add a carbon-based deoxidizer to the ladle for slag modification, opening the bottom of the ladle and blow argon to make ferrous oxide and carbon, carbon-based deoxidizer reacts to generate carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide gaseous substances to reduce ferrous oxide and TFe (sic) in the slag, where the carbon-based deoxidizer that has not completely reacted in the steel slag undergoes carbon monoxide diffusion deoxidation during transportation to RH, and reducing ferrous oxide and TFe in the slag.
   USE - The method is useful for processing converter Ruhrstahl-Heraeus (RH) steel carbon deoxidation and slag modification.
   ADVANTAGE - The method saves the amount of aluminum deoxidation alloy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ash content in the carbon-based deoxidizer is less than or equal to 13%, the volatile content is less than or equal to 10%, the total sulfur content is less than or equal to 0.60%, the total moisture content is less than or equal to 10%, and the calorific value is greater than or equal to 10%. The middle ash of the carbon-based deoxidizer comprises 20-60 wt.% silicon dioxide, 10-35 wt.% aluminum oxide, 5-35 wt.% ferric oxide, 1-20 wt.% calcium oxide, 0.3-4 wt.% magnesium oxide, 0.5-2.5 wt.% titanium dioxide, 1-4 wt.% potassium oxide and sodium oxide, and 0.1-12 wt.% sulfate. The volatile matter refers to volatile thermal decomposition products, including nitrogen, hydrogen, methane, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen sulfur trioxide. The ash is derived from the ash formed after the combustion of minerals, including clay, gypsum, carbonate, and pyrite, which are decomposed and combined during the combustion of carbon powder, and part of it becomes gas and escapes, and the remaining residue is the ash.

Preferred Method:                  The ultrasonic composite temperature is room                  temperature and the reaction time is 4 hours. The                  drying temperature is 80 degrees C and the drying                  time is 4 hours.

GA 202091493A
AB    NOVELTY - Processing directional desulfurization and                regeneration of tire rubber involves using a                swelling solvent as medium under the condition of                low temperature and oxygen-free environment, and                using mercaptan sulfides as desulfurizers to                desulfurize and regenerate waste tire rubber. The                swelling solvent is supercritical carbon dioxide,                supercritical ethanol or an organic solvent that                dissolves rubber well.
   USE - Method for processing directional                desulfurization and regeneration of tire                rubber.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensure the desulfurization and                regeneration of tire rubber in a short time in a                low temperature and oxygen-isolated environment.                Under the premise of effectively protecting the                rubber main chain (C-C), the degree of                de-crosslinking of tire rubber is maximized and the                main chain linear reclaimed rubber with high                integrity is obtained, with high degree of                de-crosslinking and high molecular weight.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Processing directional                  desulfurization and regeneration of tire rubber                  involves using the mercaptan sulfide as one or                  more of dithiothreitol, diphenyl disulfide,                  n-hexane mercaptan, isopropyl sulfide or                  dibenzamidodisulfide. The organic solvent is one                  or a mixture of of n-heptane, cyclohexane,                  toluene, xylene, petroleum ether, n-butanol,                  tetrahydrofuran or chloroform. Preferred                  Conditions: Under low temperature conditions,                  devulcanizer and pretreated tire rubber are                  subjected to mechanical shear mixing with                  1000-2000 rotations/minute speed at                  80-120&#176; C temperature for 10-30 minutes,                  stopping heating and continue stirring, then                  cooling to 30&#176; C under stirring                  conditions of 20-80 rotations/minute. When                  supercritical carbon dioxide or ethanol is used                  as the medium, the reaction conditions for                  desulfurization regeneration are                  140-200&#176; C reaction temperature, 8-18                  Mpa reaction pressure, 1-6 hours reaction time                  and 8-15% desulfurizer dosage. When supercritical                  carbon dioxide is used as the medium, the                  reaction conditions for desulfurization                  regeneration are 120-140&#176; C reaction                  temperature, 14-18 Mpa reaction pressure, 3-6                  hours reaction time. When supercritical ethanol                  is used as the medium, the reaction conditions                  for desulfurization regeneration are                  140-180&#176; C reaction temperature, 8-14                  Mpa reaction pressure, 1-3 hours reaction time.                  When an organic solvent is used as a medium, the                  reaction conditions for desulfurization                  regeneration are 100-120&#176; C reaction                  temperature, 1-3 hours reaction time and 8-15%                  desulfurizer dosage.

PN CN111495358-A; CN111495358-B
TI Preparing formaldehyde removing agent catalytically active at room temperature involves adding ethylene glycol solution of sodium hydroxide to aqueous solution of chloroplatinic acid under stirring conditions, mixing, heating, and roasting.
AU HUANG Y
   WANG W
   LI L
   CAO J
AE INST EARTH ENVIRONMENT CHINESE ACAD SCI (EART-Non-standard)
   INST EARTH ENVIRONMENT CHINESE ACAD SCI (EART-Non-standard)
GA 202077312T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing formaldehyde removing agent catalytically active at room temperature involves adding ethylene glycol solution of sodium hydroxide to the aqueous solution of chloroplatinic acid under stirring conditions, and mixing to form a precursor solution. The precursor solution is heated to obtain platinum colloid. The platinum colloid is added into the porous material, stirred, filtered, washed, and dried to obtain platinum-loaded porous material precursor. The platinum-loaded porous material precursor is subjected to roasting to obtain the desired product.
   USE - Method for preparing formaldehyde removing agent catalytically active at room temperature (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The synergistic effect of the porous material and platinum in the formaldehyde remover can make the removal rate of formaldehyde and the selectivity of carbon dioxide at room temperature both greater than 90%, that results in complete decomposition of formaldehyde.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The concentration of sodium hydroxide in ethylene glycol solution is 3-5.5 mg/mL, and the concentration of chloroplatinic acid aqueous solution is 2.5-5.5 mg/mL. The volume ratio of the ethylene glycol solution of sodium hydroxide to the aqueous solution of chloroplatinic acid is (7-9):1. The heating temperature is 130-150 degrees C, and the time is 0.5-2 hours.The stirring time is 6-12 hours. The mass ratio of platinum colloid to porous material is (5-10):1. The roasting is performed at 150-300 degrees C for 2 hours. Preferred Components: The porous material is selected from molecular sieve, alumina, honeycomb ceramic and silica gel.

   ADVANTAGE - The sheet has excellent hydrophilicity, reduce the tension of the water, is strong in adhesion and wet wipe resistance, achieves long anti-fog period, degrades into carbon dioxide and water within 400 days and is environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the sheet involving (i) mixing polylactic acid, perfluorohexylethyltriethoxysilane and monoglyceride to obtain a mixture, (ii) drying the mixture in a desiccator equipped with dew point meter, drying until the dew point meter shows value of -50, and crystallizing after heating up, and (iii) melting the crystallized material obtained in step (ii) and extruding through an extruder, and through three-layer co-extrusion, three-roll calendering, cooling, trimming, and cutting to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The sheet comprises 97 pts. wt. polylactic acid, 1 pt. wt. perfluorohexylethyl triethoxysilane and 2 pts. wt. monoglyceride. Preferred Method: The polylactic acid is prepared using corn starch. The step (iii) involves melting and feeding the crystallized material using melt pump and extruding using 180 planetary screw extruder after three-layer co-extrusion, three-roller calendering, cooling, trimming and cutting. Preferred Conditions: In step (i), the stirring temperature is carried out at 85 degrees C for 30 minutes. In step (ii), the drying temperature is carried out at 105 degrees C for 80 minutes. In step (ii), the crystallization process is carried out at 130 degrees C for 2.5 hours.

PN DE102018121230-A1; WO2020043886-A1; EP3844210-A1
TI Additive manufacture of workpiece for forming aircraft interior component, involves performing flame retardant finishing of polyamide material using red phosphorus, phosphorus-containing compound and/or melamine-based flame retardant.
AU KLUESENER J
AE AIRBUS OPERATIONS GMBH (EADS-C)
   AIRBUS OPERATIONS GMBH (EADS-C)
GA 202019043J
AB    NOVELTY - Additive manufacture of workpiece (102) involves performing flame retardant finishing of a polyamide material using red phosphorus, a phosphorus-containing compound and/or a melamine-based flame retardant.
   USE - Additive manufacture of workpiece used for forming aircraft interior component.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of workpiece having excellent surface smoothness, workability, tensile strength, flame retardance, rigidity, long-term stability, and resistance to fuel and lubricant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of flame-retardant polyamide material in the form of powder for additive manufacture of workpiece, preferably by selective laser sintering or selective laser melting.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an apparatus for manufacturing workpiece.
   Laser beam powder bed manufacturing device (100)
   Workpiece (102)
   Carbon dioxide laser (104)
   Powder bed (108)
   Powder application device (112)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The method preferably involves providing a flame-retardant polyamide material in the form of a powder, applying the powdered flame-retardant polyamide material on a base, forming a powder layer, performing selective irradiation of area of the powder layer, repeating formation of powder layer and selective irradiation until the workpiece is made of the flame-retardant polyamide material, sintering or melting and solidifying, and separating the workpiece. The powdery flame-retardant polyamide material is obtained by mixing polyamide material and flame retardant, preferably in the form of a cast block or a granulate, crushing by cryogenic crushing, and sieving. Preferred Component: The polyamide is a blend of one or more polyamide with one or more miscible, compatible or immiscible polymer, preferably chosen from polyolefin, polystyrene, thermoplastic elastomer, and polyacetal, and one or more phosphorus-containing compound chosen from aromatic and aliphatic ester of phosphoric acid, preferably resorcinol bis(diphenyl phosphate), bisphenol A bis(diphenyl phosphate) and their oligomers, triphenyl phosphate, tris(2-ethylhexyl)phosphate, tricresyl phosphate, isopropylated triphenyl phosphate, ethylenediamine diphosphate, ammonium phosphate, ammonium polyphosphate, phosphinate, preferably diethylene phosphinate aluminum, diethylene phosphinate zinc or aluminum phosphinate, aluminum phosphate, phosphonate ester, oligomeric and polymeric derivatives of methanephosphonic acid, 9,10-dihydro-9-oxa-10-phosphorylphenanthrene-10-oxide, ammonium polyphosphate, phosphacenes, vinylphosphonic acid, melamine, melamine derivatives, melamine condensation products, melamine salts, benzoguanamine, allantoin, polyisocyanurate, melamine cyanurate, melamine phosphate, dimelamine phosphate, melamine pyrophosphate, melamine polyphosphate, melamine metal phosphates, preferably melamine aluminum phosphate, melamine zinc phosphate, and melamine magnesium phosphate, pyrophosphate, polyphosphate, and poly-(2,4-(piperazin-1,4-yl)-6-(morpholin-4-yl)-1,3,5-triazine). The polymer blend comprises 30 wt.% or more, preferably 90 wt.% or more polyamide. The polyamide is preferably chosen from polyamide 6, polyamide 11, polyamide 12, polyamide 6.6, polyamide 6.9, polyamide 6.10, polyamide 6.12, polyamide 10.10 and their copolyamide. The polyamide material comprises 30 wt.% or more, preferably 90 wt.% or more polyamide 6, and 0.1-20 wt.%, preferably 1-15 wt.% flame retardant. The polyamide material further comprises component(s) chosen from antistatic agent, dyes, preferably soluble inorganic and/or organic pigment and/or insoluble dye, fillers, preferably glass fibers, chalk, graphite and carbon black, lubricant, stabilizer, and plasticizer. Preferred Property: The powdery flame-retardant polyamide material has a particle size of 40-110 mu m.

PN CN110813312-A
TI Magnetic nanocomposite material used for            catalyzing decomposition of formaldehyde, comprises            magnetic core and mesoporous layer wrapped on the            surface of magnetic core, and mesoporous layer is            loaded with noble metal.
AU HU K
   DING L
   WANG X
   LAO C
   FENG Z
   CHEN H
   WANG K
AE ZHUHAI GREE ELECTRIC APPLIANCES INC (GREZ-C)
GA 202018713L
AB    NOVELTY - Magnetic nanocomposite material comprises                magnetic core and mesoporous layer wrapped on the                surface of magnetic core, and mesoporous layer is                loaded with noble metal.
   USE - The magnetic nanocomposite material is used                for catalyzing decomposition of formaldehyde                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product has high conversion and                selectivity to formaldehyde; avoids secondary                pollution caused by production of intermediate                products during reaction; can completely catalyze                the conversion of formaldehyde to carbon dioxide                and water at room temperature; and can realize                recovery and utilization of catalyst through                applied magnetic field, thus reducing the cost. The                method is simple and low-cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of magnetic nanocomposite material                comprising wrapping mesoporous layer on magnetic                core and soaking magnetic core wrapped with                mesoporous layer in salt solution of noble                metal.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The loading amount of                  noble metal on mesoporous layer is 0.25-0.35                  wt.%. The ratio of ferric chloride, sodium                  acetate, polyethylene glycol and first solvent is                  (1.35-13.5) g:(1-10) g:(1-10) g:(40-250) mL. The                  ratio of iron oxide magnetic core, ethanol,                  water, ammonia, octadecyltrimethylammonium                  bromide and tetraethyl orthosilicate is (0.1-1)                  g:(80-400) ml:(20-100) ml:(1-20) ml:(0.2-4)                  g:(0.2-4) ml. The ratio of iron oxide magnetic                  core, ethanol, water, platinum chloride, and                  sodium borohydride is (0.1-1) g:(50-400)                  ml:(20-120) ml:(10-100) mg:(10 -100) mg.                  Preferred Components: The mesoporous layer has                  specific surface area of 220-280 m3/g and average                  pore diameter of 2-4 nm. The size of magnetic                  core is 200-250 nm. The thickness of mesoporous                  layer is 30-50 nm. The material of magnetic core                  is ferrite magnetic material. The ferrite                  magnetic material is iron oxide, manganese                  ferrite, nickel ferrite, copper ferrite and/or                  zinc ferrite. The material of mesoporous layer is                  silicon dioxide and/or titanium dioxide. The                  noble metal is platinum, palladium, and/or gold.                  The soluble platinum salt is platinum chloride.                  The reducing agent is sodium borohydride.                  Preferred Method: When magnetic core is iron                  oxide magnetic core, mesoporous layer is silica                  mesoporous layer, and when noble metal is                  platinum, the product is prepared by dissolving                  ferric chloride, sodium acetate and polyethylene                  glycol in first solvent, performing first                  reaction to obtain iron oxide magnetic core,                  dispersing iron oxide magnetic core in second                  solvent, adding ammonia,                  octadecyltrimethylammonium bromide, and                  tetraethyl orthosilicate, performing second                  reaction, calcining to obtain magnetic core                  covering mesoporous layer, dispersing in third                  solvent, adding soluble platinum salt and                  reducing agent and performing third reaction. The                  first reaction is performed at 180-220 degrees C                  for 7-8 hours. The second reaction is done at                  20-30 degrees C for 5-8 hours. The calcination                  step is done at 280-320 degrees C for 5-7 hours.                  The third reaction is performed at 20-30 degrees                  C for 10-14 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first solvent is                  ethylene glycol. The second solvent is ethanol                  and water at volume ratio of 80-400:20-100.

PT P
PN CN110819587-A
TI Identifying gel dryness of mesenchymal stem cell involves culturing mesenchymal stem cell on induction culture medium to obtain three-dimensional gel of mesenchymal stem cell, and inoculating stem cell into adipogenic culture medium.
AU ZHANG J
   TAN Y
   MA H
   ZHANG H
   HAN Z
AE SHANDONG QILU CELL THERAPY ENG TECHNOLOG (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020186153
AB    NOVELTY - Identifying gel dryness of mesenchymal stem cell involves culturing mesenchymal stem cell on induction culture medium for 7 days to obtain a scaffold-free three-dimensional gel of mesenchymal stem cell. The obtained stem cell is inoculated into adipogenic, osteogenic and chondrogenic induction culture medium for 14 days, respectively, and then culture solution is changed every 72-96 hours. The culturing is performed at an environment of 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide incubator. The oil red O is identified by adipogenic staining after differentiation induction, alizarin red is identified by osteogenesis, and alixin blue is identified by cartilage staining.
   USE - Method for identifying gel dryness of mesenchymal stem cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to identify gel dryness of mesenchymal stem cell, which maintains mesenchymal stem cell multi-directional differentiation ability with a dryness.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The mesenchymal stem cells maintain ability to differentiate into adipocytes when oil red O is identified by adipogenic staining, and intracellular oil droplets are stained with oil red O to bright red. The mesenchymal stem cells maintain ability to differentiate into osteocytes is maintained when alizarin red is subjected to osteogenesis identification, and calcium precipitate is stained red by alizarin red. The mesenchymal stem cell has ability to maintain differentiation into chondrocytes when chondrocyte microspheres are bright blue that is stained with alixin blue. The mesenchymal stem cells remain dry when mesenchymal stem cells stain positively with 2-3 types of staining reaction. The source of mesenchymal stem cell is umbilical cord, fat, bone marrow or cord blood. The adipogenic induction medium is mesenchymal stem cells basal medium (MSCBM) containing 5% UltroGRO (RTM: Pure cell culture supplement), 10mg/mL insulin, 1 mu M dexamethasone, 100 mu M indomethacin and 500 mu M isobutylmethylxanthine. The osteogenic induction medium is MSCBM medium containing 5% UltroGRO, 0.1 mu M dexamethasone, 50 mu M ascorbic acid and 10mM beta-glyceryl phosphate. The chondrogenic induction medium is a Dulbecco's Modified Eagle Medium culture medium containing 110 mu g/mL sodium pyruvate, 10ng/mL transforming growth facto- beta 3, 0.1 mu M dexamethasone, 50 mu g/mL L-ascorbic acid-2-phosphate, 40 mu g/mL L-proline, 1% ITS and Premix 100.

TI Composition used for e.g. preparing medicine for treating and preventing seborrheic alopecia comprises gingerol, volatile oil, felon herb stem and branch volatile oil, naringin essential oil, and ginseng ethanol percolate.
AU CHEN G
   ZHOU Y
   YANG Y
   TAN H
   WU Z
AE CHONGQING ACAD CHINESE MATERIA MEDICA PH (CQTM-C)
   CHONGQING ACAD CHINESE MATERIA MEDICA PH (CQTM-C)
GA 202011779L
AB    NOVELTY - A composition for preventing alopecia comprises gingerol, volatile oil, felon herb stem and branch volatile oil, naringin essential oil, and ginseng ethanol percolate.
   USE - The composition is used for preparing medicine for treating and preventing seborrheic alopecia, including spray (claimed). It is used for improving skin microcirculation, inhibiting scalp pathogenic microorganisms, inhibit promoting epidermal keratinocytes and dermal fibroblasts proliferation.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition has synergistic effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation method of the composition comprising using steam distillation to extract volatile oil of felon herb stem and branch, using steam distillation and extracting to obtain naringin essential oil, using percolation extraction to extract ginseng effective component, and using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and polyamide resin purification method to extract and separate gingerol from ginger.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composition comprises 0.1-0.5 pt. wt. gingerol, 1-7 pts. wt. felon herb stem and branch volatile oil, 2-6 pts. wt. naringin essential oil, and 20-30 pts. wt. ginseng ethanol percolate. The Artemisia argyi branch and stem volatile oil is obtained by blast drying fresh felon herb stem and branch at 80 degrees C up to water content less than 10%, then cutting into segments, distilling using steam distillation, collecting volatile oil, then centrifuging, taking upper oil phase, and drying to obtain felon herb stem branch volatile oil. The pummelo peel essential oil is obtained by cutting fresh shaddock peel into 5cm by 5 cm small blocks, extracting using steam distillation method, collecting volatile oil, then centrifuging, taking upper oil phase, and drying to obtain naringin essential oil. The ginseng ethanol percolate is obtained by crushing ginseng, soaking using ethanol, finally percolating and extracting the ginseng powder, and collecting the percolation liquid. The ginseng ethanol percolate comprises crushing the ginseng, then sieving by 16-mesh sieve to obtain ginseng powder, soaking the ginseng powder for 24 hours with 30% denatured ethanol, finally percolating the ginseng powder at diacolation speed of 3ml/(kg.min), then collecting the percolating liquid of 10 times of the percolating cylinder volume. The spray comprises 0.1-0.5% gingerol, 1-7% felon herb stem volatile oil, 2-6% pummelo pericarp essential oil, 20-30% of 30% ginseng ethanol diacolation, 0.2-0.8% Benzyl salicylate, 0.2-1.7% glycerin, 1-9% butanediol, 0.1-0.5% laurocapram, and 44.5-75.4% of 30% denatured ethanol.
   ACTIVITY - Endocrine-Gen; Dermatological.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Reductase inhibitor.

AE UNIV TEXAS SYSTEM (TEXA-C)

   USE - Separation of compound(s) e.g. alkene, alkyne, alkane, nitrogen or carbon dioxide (all claimed) in petrochemical industry.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separation of two or more compounds involves (a) combining the metal-organic framework with a mixture comprising a compound (i) and a compound (ii), and (b) separating the compound (i) from the compound (ii) within the metal-organic framework comprising a divalent metal-containing repeating unit of formula (I): ML, or its hydrate.
   M = divalent metal ion;and
   L = ligand of formula (II).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal-organic framework is of formula: M(H2O)L, preferably Ca(H2O)L. The compound (i) or compound (ii) or both are gas molecules. The metal-organic framework is adhered to fixed bed surface. The separation is carried out in an absorber packed with the metal-organic framework. Preferred Composition: The ratio of compound (i) and compound (ii) in the mixture is 1:999-1:1, preferably 1:99-1:1. The ratio of compound (ii) and compound (i) in the mixture is 1:999-1:1. Preferred Properties: The separation process is carried out at pressure of 0.1-10 bar, preferably 1 bar, and carried out at room temperature of 0-75 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The compound (i) contains 8C or less alkene, preferably ethylene, 8C or less alkyne, preferably ethyne, or carbon dioxide. The compound (ii) contains 8C or less alkane, preferably ethane or methane, or nitrogen.

PN CN110270361-A
TI Preparation of multiphase carbon doped graphitic carbon nitride composite photocatalyst for e.g. photocatalytic degradation, involves calcining lotus root powder and precursor of graphitic carbon nitride at high temperature.
AU LIU R
AE NANJING RUNKE ENVIRONMENT CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 201985650D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of multiphase carbon doped graphitic carbon nitride composite photocatalyst involves calcining lotus root powder and precursor of graphitic carbon nitride at high temperature under the protection of an inert atmosphere.
   USE - Preparation of multiphase carbon doped graphitic carbon nitride composite photocatalyst for photocatalytic degradation, water decomposition, carbon dioxide reduction and nitrogen fixation (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite photocatalyst has improved photo-generated electron-hole separation efficiency and utilization rate due to the carbon component forms a planar heterojunction through a covalent bond with a heptazine ring and is supported on a two-dimensional material in the form of carbon particles, and the photogenerated electrons can synchronously transition to two carbon structures to participate in the reduction reaction with unique carrier two-channel migration mode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation of multiphase carbon doped graphitic carbon nitride composite photocatalyst involves (a) adding lotus root powder to deionized water, heating to 80-90 degrees C to form a uniform colloid, cooling to room temperature, adding the precursor of graphitic carbon nitride, stirring, freeze drying to remove the aqueous phase, obtaining a homogeneous mixture, (b) carrying out under inert gas, heating the homogeneous mixture obtained in step (a) to 500-550 degrees C, keep warming for 4-6 hours, cooling naturally to room temperature, washing with deionized water, drying, and grinding. Preferred Component: The precursor of graphitic carbon nitride in the step (a) is chosen from one of melamine, dicyandiamide, monocyanamide, urea, and thiourea. The inert gas in the step (b) is nitrogen or argon. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of lotus root powder to graphitic carbon nitride precursor in the step (a) is 1:30-300. Preferred Condition: The rate of heating in the step (b) is 5 degrees C/minute.

   USE - The method is used for preparing garlic spicy                sauce.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The raw materials are                  30-40 pts. wt. salted pepper, 25-35 pts. wt.                  broad bean paste, 1-3 pts. wt. tomato sauce,                  10-20 pts. wt. sugar, 1-2 pts. wt. garlic powder,                  1-5 pts. wt. chicken flavor, 2-6 pts. wt.                  monosodium glutamate, 2-6 pts. wt. edible salt                  and 1-4 pts. wt. vinegar. Preferred Method: The                  brewing step is done at 85-95 degrees C for 10-20                  minutes. The pressure of closed reactor is                  controlled to 16-22 MPa at 42-52 degrees C. The                  sweet garlic is obtained by taking 7.5 pts. wt.                  fresh garlic and 5-10 pts. wt. white sugar and                  mixing with 30-35 pts. wt. water. The garlic                  powder is prepared by taking 6 pts. wt. garlic                  oil, 31 pts. wt. maltodextrin, 2 pts. wt. sodium                  starch octenyl succinate and 1 pt. wt.                  emulsifier, mixing and spray-drying. The                  emulsifier is prepared by high shear                  emulsification of Tween 80 (RTM: polyoxyethylene                  (20) sorbitan monooleate) after 16 kr/minute. The                  spray-drying inlet air temperature is controlled                  at 185-195 degrees C, and the outlet air                  temperature is set to 85-95 degrees C.

PN CN110141935-A; CN110141935-B
TI Functionalized ionic liquid phase change system used as solvents to realize system phase change and carbon dioxide capture, comprises functionalized ionic liquid diethylenetriamine triazole, n-propanol and water.
AU LV B
   JING G
   ZHAN X
   ZHOU Z
AE UNIV HUAQIAO (UYHQ-C)
GA 201974057X
AB    NOVELTY - Functionalized ionic liquid phase change system is a ternary mixed system consisting of functionalized ionic liquid diethylenetriamine triazole, n-propanol and water, where the total concentration of diethylenetriamine triazole is 0.5-1 mol/L. The volume ratio of n-propanol to water is 5:5-8:2.
   USE - Functionalized ionic liquid phase change system used as solvents to realize system phase change and CO2 capture through the regulation of CO2 loading(claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The functionalized ionic liquid phase change system can effectively reduce the regenerative energy consumption of the technology and also has efficient CO2 capture performance, and biggest difference from the traditional phase change absorbent is that the main absorbent is a functionalized ionic liquid, which can overcome the defects of large viscosity and difficult regeneration of the rich organic amine phase change body, and has wide application prospects.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The ternary mixed system has phase separation performance. The ternary mixed system is a homogeneous solution before absorbing carbon dioxide, and after being absorbed by carbon dioxide, it is converted into a liquid-liquid two-phase phase change system, and carbon dioxide is concentrated in the water phase. The ternary mixed system is used for absorbing carbon dioxide in a volume ratio of 5-20 vol.% in pure carbon dioxide or a mixed gas, an absorption temperature of 30-60 degrees C, and an absorption load of 1.5-1.8 mol carbon dioxide (CO2)/mol of an absorbent. In the saturated solution of the phase change system, the volume of the rich liquid accounts for 40-60% of the total volume. The saturated solution of the phase change system is regenerated by thermal desorption, the regeneration temperature is 100-120 degrees C, the regeneration time is 80-100 minutes, and the regeneration efficiency is 60-95%. The absorption load is 1.5-1.8 mol CO2/mol of absorbent. The aqueous phase of the rich liquid is collected after the phase change of the system, and is regenerated by thermal analysis, and the regeneration efficiency is 60-95%.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); E13 (Heterocyclics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

   USE - The protein powder is useful in a product, preferably a health care product for improving immunity and sleep (all claimed), and calming nerves.

The step (b) involves extracting at 35-65 degrees C under 18-28 MPa for 20-80 minutes, where the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 8.4-10.8 l/hour. The step (c) involves drying using a vacuum dryer at 40-60 degrees C. The step (d) involves ultrafine crushing at less than or equal to -10 degrees C. Preferred Components: The spina date seeds are dry and mature wild jujube seeds. Preferred Property: The particle diameter of refined protein powder is 0.1-75 mu m.
   ACTIVITY - Immunostimulant; Hypnotic; Tranquilizer.

PT P
PN CN109675603-A
TI Preparation of carbon-based catalyst used in            electrocatalysis, involves preparing coated product            containing tetraethyl orthosilicate, catalyst, carbon            source, subjecting to high temperature carbonization            and removing silica coating layer.
AU HU C
   BAI S
   QIU J
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 201940787Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of carbon-based catalyst involves                dissolving tetraethyl orthosilicate, the catalyst                and the solid carbon source containing metal and                nitrogen in mixed solvent of water and alcohol,                stirring at 20-80 degrees C for 0.5-3 hours , after                completing the reaction, filtering the precipitate,                washing, drying to obtain coated product,                subjecting the coated product to high temperature                carbonization at 800-1200 degrees C for 1-5 hours                in an inert atmosphere to obtain an intermediate                product, subjecting intermediate product to                pickling with 5-20 wt.% hydrofluoric acid and                washing for 0.5-2 hours, etching and removing the                surface of silica coating layer.
   USE - Preparation of carbon-based catalyst used in                electrocatalysis such as electrocatalytic reduction                of carbon dioxide, oxygen reduction, hydrogen                evolution and nitrogen fixation (all                claimed).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is acid                  or base. The acid is formic acid, hydrochloric                  acid, oxalic acid or sulfuric acid. The base is                  sodium hydroxide, sodium carbonate, potassium                  hydrogencarbonate or aqueous ammonia.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The solid carbon source                  containing metal and nitrogen is zeolitic                  imidazole ester skeleton structural material,                  metal complex modified carbon material or metal                  doped carbon material. The zeolitic imidazole                  ester skeleton structural material is bimetallic                  imidazolate framework. Preferred Process: The                  preparation of zeolitic imidazole ester skeleton                  structural material involves dissolving                  transition metal nitrate, zinc nitrate and                  dimethyl imidazole in methanol, stirring and                  centrifuging, where the molar ratio of transition                  metal nitrate and zinc nitrate is 1: (4-30). The                  preparation of metal complex modified carbon                  material involves dissolving carbon material in                  50-65 wt.% concentrated nitric acid,                  hydrothermally reacting at 60-90 degrees C for                  8-16 hours to obtain mixed liquid, where the                  carbon material is carbon black, activated                  carbon, carbon fiber, carbon nanotube or                  graphene, filtering, washing the mixed liquid                  until the pH is neutral to obtain functionalized                  carbon material, dissolving the functionalized                  carbon material and the metal complex in water or                  ethanol, stirring at 25-35 degrees C for 0.5-2                  hours, filtering, drying and subjecting to                  low-temperature heat treatment, where the metal                  complex is phthalocyanine or porphyrin complex of                  iron, cobalt, nickel, copper, zinc, tin, silver,                  platinum or ruthenium. The preparation of metal                  doped carbon material involves dissolving the                  carbon material and the metal salt in 50-65 wt.%                  concentrated nitric acid, stirring at25-35                  degrees C for 0.5-2 hours, adding water and                  thermally reacting at 60-90 degrees C for 8-16                  hours to obtain a mixture, where the carbon                  material is carbon black, activated carbon,                  carbon fiber, carbon nanotube or graphene and                  metal salt is metal nitrate, sulfate, acetate,                  chloride, bromide or iodide, filtering, washing                  the mixture until the pH is neutral to obtain a                  precursor, mixing powdered urea with precursor                  and calcining at high temperature.

PT P
PN CN109681880-A
TI Processing organic matter-containing high-salt solid waste comprises carrying out incineration of organic matter-containing high-salt solid waste in an incinerator to produce waste flue gas and waste salt.
AU MA Z
   LUO J
   TIAN B
   MENG J
   YANG W
   YI J
   PEI W
   LI B
   XIAO S
   WANG R
   CAO Y
   WU Z
   ZHANG Z
   ZHOU X
AE CHANGZHOU INST TECHNOLOGY (UYCZ-C)
GA 201941051W
AB    NOVELTY - Processing organic matter-containing high-salt solid waste comprises carrying out incineration of organic matter-containing high-salt solid waste in an incinerator at 1000-1300 degrees C to produce waste flue gas and waste salt; passing the waste flue gas through exhaust gas treatment process, recycling the separated carbon dioxide into alkali production process; subjecting the waste salt to waste salt treatment process to obtain saturated sodium chloride solution, recovering and utilizing via alkali reaction; where one part of the heat in the waste flue gas and the waste salt is supplied to the power generation through flue gas heat exchanger and the waste salt heat exchanger, and the other part is used for alkali reaction.
   USE - The method is useful for processing organic matter-containing high-salt solid waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical and saves energy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises transferring the waste salt using a ceramic sheet conveyor during incineration process, where the connecting piece of the ceramic sheet conveyor belt is made of carbon fiber. The exhaust gas treatment process includes desulfurization and denitration process (using niobium-based catalyst), alkali absorption process and gas separation and compression process; where the separated nitrogen and water are emitted as exhaust gases, and the separated carbon dioxide gas is recycled into alkali production process. The waste salt treatment process includes connecting the waste salt heat exchanger to a water pipe, converting the heat in the waste salt into water vapor by waste salt heat exchanger, heating the water vapor by flue gas heat exchanger to form high-temperature and high-pressure steam for power generation, cooling the waste salt during this process, dissolving the cooled waste salt in water to obtain waste brine solution, filtering through a filter of a filter press to obtain saturated sodium chloride solution and recycling into the alkali reaction. The alkali production process is carried out in an autoclave, providing carbon dioxide with a flow rate of 72-120 m3/hour by an exhaust gas treatment process, providing heat from the flue gas heat exchanger with a pressure of 0.01-0.2 MPa, providing saturated sodium chloride solution with a flow rate of 45-62 m3/hour by a waste salt treatment process, reacting with ammonia and carbon dioxide at 30-45 degrees C for 3-7 hours, then recovering ammonium chloride, sodium carbonate and sodium bicarbonate. The carbon fiber is prepared by: preparing nanocrystalline silica polymer mixed solution by mixing nanocrystalline silica with block polyether amide copolymer and organic solvent; soaking the carbon fiber in nanocrystalline silica polymer mixed solution, and the carbon fiber is extracted by pulling. The niobium-based catalyst is prepared by: (a) preparing niobium oxide nano-material using niobium salt, tungsten salt and molybdenum salt; and (b) adding platinum salt solution into nano-materials. Preferred Components: The filter is a porous polyvinyl amide block copolymer filter.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).);

PT P
PN CN109574055-A; CN109574055-B
TI Preparing light calcium carbonate and magnesium sulfate heptahydrate in a salt sludge comprises adding calcium sulfate to the salt mud produced by the brine refining process, and adding water for beating and carrying out carbonization.
AU LIU R
   LIU X
   ZHAO H
   WANG Y
   HAN X
AE UNIV HEBEI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYKJ-C)
GA 201933513T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing light calcium carbonate and magnesium sulfate heptahydrate in a salt sludge comprises (1) adding calcium sulfate to the salt mud produced by the brine refining process, and adding water for beating, (2) carrying out carbonization by introducing carbon dioxide into the slurry obtained in the step A, converting calcium sulfate into calcium carbonate, converting magnesium hydroxide into soluble magnesium sulfate, and carrying out filtration to realize separation of calcium and magnesium, (3) drying the filter cake obtained in the step B to obtain a light calcium carbonate product, (4) using filtrate obtained in the step B, concentrating, crystallizing and drying to give a magnesium sulfate heptahydrate product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing light calcium carbonate and magnesium sulfate heptahydrate in a salt sludge.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ratio of the amount of magnesium hydroxide contained in the salt puree and the substance added to the calcium sulfate in the step (1) is 1:0.95-1.05. The solid-liquid ratio of the slurry in the step (1) is 1:4-15. The carbonization reaction temperature in the step (2) is 20 -60 degrees C. The carbonization time in the step (2) is 20 to 120 minutes. In the step (2) dry the filter cake obtained in the step (2) at 100-110 degrees C for 5-10 hours, carrying out pulverization to obtain light calcium carbonate product. The step (4), concentrating the filtrate at 100-110 degrees C, cooling and crystallizing, drying to obtain magnesium sulfate heptahydrate product. The carbon dioxide used in the carbonization process is a pure carbon dioxide or a flue gas having a volume fraction of carbon dioxide greater than 10%. The salt mud is derived from the ammonia base or chlor-alkali industry. The method specifically comprises adding calcium sulfate to the salt mud produced by the brine refining process, and then adding water for beating, the ratio of the amount of magnesium hydroxide and the substance added to the calcium sulfate in the salt mud is 1:1, the solid-liquid ratio of the slurry is 1:8-10, carrying out carbonization by introducing carbon dioxide into the slurry obtained in step A, calcium sulfate is converted into calcium carbonate, magnesium hydroxide is converted into soluble magnesium sulfate, and filtration is carried out to achieve separation of calcium and magnesium, the carbonization reaction temperature in step B is 35-45 degrees C, drying the filter cake at 110 degrees C for 8 hours and pulverizing to obtain a light calcium carbonate product, and using filtrate obtained, concentrating at 105 degrees C, cooling and crystallizing and drying to give a magnesium sulfate heptahydrate product.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

PN CN109574250-A
TI Water quality purificant useful for static water area, preferably landscape water and aquaculture water, comprises iron powder, bamboo charcoal particle, metal ion chelating agent, ceramic powder, and expanded vermiculite.
AU SONG H
   LI B
   QUAN Y
   ZHAO X
   LI S
   MA M
   SONG X
   TAN Y
   LIU L
AE MEILI GUOTU BEIJING ECOLOGICAL ENVIRONME (MEIL-Non-standard)
GA 2019335092
AB    NOVELTY - Water quality purificant comprises 30-40 pts. wt. iron powder, 15-25 pts. wt. bamboo charcoal particle, 12-20 pts. wt. metal ion chelating agent, 20-30 pts. wt. ceramic powder, and 4-10 pts. wt. expanded vermiculite.
   USE - The purificant is useful for static water area, preferably landscape water and aquaculture water (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The purificant is environmentally friendly; and alleviates the technical problems of poor purification effect of existing chemical purifying agents and causing water pollution, reduces carbon dioxide concentration, ammonia nitrogen concentration, hydrogen sulfide concentration, malodor and sludge accumulation in static water, promotes the growth of beneficial microorganisms, and improves water quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the purificant, comprising mixing uniformly the raw materials.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The purificant comprises 35-40 pts. wt. iron powder, 15-20 pts. wt. bamboo charcoal particle, 15-20 pts. wt. metal ion chelating agent, 20-24 pts. wt. ceramic powder, and 4-10 pts. wt. expanded vermiculite. Preferred Components: The metal ion chelating agent is ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid. The purity of the iron powder is more than 99% and the expanded vermiculite is organic modified expanded vermiculite. The particle size of the iron powder is 100-500 mesh, preferably 200-400 mesh, and the particle size of the bamboo charcoal particle is 100-500 mesh, preferably 200-400 mesh. The particle diameter of the ceramic powder is 300-1000 mesh, preferably 300-500 mesh and the particle diameter of the expanded vermiculite is 50-150 mesh, preferably 80-120 mesh. Preferred Method: The method comprises firstly mixing uniformly expanded vermiculite and bamboo charcoal particles, adding ceramic powder and iron powder, mixing uniformly, adding the metal ion chelating agent and uniformly mixing to obtain water quality purificant. The purificant for the still waters is agglomerated or blocky, where the mixed raw material is compacted into a lumpish or a block by a compactor and the expanded vermiculite is subjected to acidification and calcination treatment.

GA 201930418A
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to metallurgy, in particular to the process of pyrometallurgical processing of oxidized nickel ore containing non-ferrous metals and iron, with the production of ferronickel and iron. Method comprises preheating the initial charge together with flux additives without producing liquid melts, subsequent smelting in an electric arc furnace to produce ferronickel and slag, in this case, as a reducing agent, a reducing gas is used, which is obtained by jointly supplying a natural gas and an oxidizer to the near-electrode zone of the furnace, which is steam, oxygen or carbon dioxide, in a ratio of 1:(0.5-1.7) per 1 nm3 CH4.

PN CN109438285-A
TI Methylation of phenylacetonitrile based on pressurized distillation technique, involves reacting phenylacetonitrile, sodium alkoxide and dimethyl carbonate, and distilling product under reduced temperature and pressure.
AU CHEN X
   ZHANG Z
   WANG M
   QIAN C
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2019248916
AB    NOVELTY - Method for methylation of phenylacetonitrile, involves mixing phenylacetonitrile, sodium alkoxide and dimethyl carbonate in a molar ratio of 1:0.1-0.4:4-10, and then reacting at a pressure of 2 plus minus 0.2 MPa and a reaction temperature of 160-200 degrees C for 4-8 hours, lowering temperature until atmospheric distillation is carried out after the completion of reaction, and carrying out distillation under reduced pressure to collect a fraction of 108-113 degrees C to obtain 2-phenylpropionitrile as a product.
   USE - Methylation of phenylacetonitrile based on pressurized distillation technique.
   ADVANTAGE - The reaction by-product methanol and carbon dioxide are produced by rectification. The reaction is carried out within a short period of time. The use of strong alkali sodium alkoxide as a catalyst reduces the amount of catalyst used.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The alcohol is condensed and collected after the gas discharged during the reaction is cooled, and the carbon dioxide is absorbed by the exhaust gas. A fraction of 88-90 degrees C is collected to obtain a recyclable dimethyl carbonate. Preferred Components: The sodium alkoxide is sodium methoxide, sodium ethoxide, sodium n-propoxide or sodium t-butoxide.

TI Preparing alumina-chitosan aerogel complex comprises e.g. adding chitosan to acetic acid solution, magnetically stirring, pouring the chitosan-containing acetic acid solution into mold containing alumina fibers.
AU ZHA P
AE ZHANGJIAGANG WUHU NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOG (ZHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019233559
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing alumina-chitosan aerogel complex comprises (i) adding specific amount of chitosan to an acetic acid solution having a volume fraction of 1%, and magnetically stirring for 2-3 hours to obtain acetic acid solution containing chitosan, (ii) pouring the chitosan-containing acetic acid solution into a mold containing alumina fibers, adding specific amount of propylene oxide, sealing, gelatinizing at room temperature, aging for 24-48 hours, and drying to obtain alumina fiber-chitosan complex, and (iii) placing the alumina fiber-chitosan complex into a supercritical drier, soaking the alumina fiber-chitosan complex by adding an ethanol solvent, and introducing excess liquid carbon dioxide at 4-8 degrees C, removing ethanol solvent and water.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing alumina-chitosan aerogel complex.
   ADVANTAGE - The alumina-chitosan aerogel complex: has high specific surface area and a large pore diameter.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the ratio of the volume of the propylene oxide to the chitosan-containing acetic acid solution is 1:1.5-5. In the step (iii), the conditions in the supercritical dryer are a temperature of 35-50 degrees C and a pressure of 13-18 MPa. The alumina fiber is prepared by (a) taking crystalline aluminum chloride, adding deionized water, and uniformly mixing to obtain an aluminum chloride solution, (b) adding aluminum powder to the aluminum chloride solution at a ratio of n (Aluminum chloride hexa hydrate)/n (Aluminum) = 1:2-4, stirring, and heating under reflux in a constant temperature water bath of 80-100 degrees C for 10-20 hours to obtain colloidal mother liquor, (iii) adding the fiber forming aid to the colloidal mother liquor, stirring uniformly and concentrating at 40-100 degrees C for a certain period of time to obtain alumina sol, (iv) forming the alumina sol into a fiber at room temperature and drying to obtain alumina coagulum Glue fiber, and (v) heat-treating the alumina gel fiber at 1200-1400 degrees C.

AE QIDONG CHUANGLV LANDSCAPE ENG CO LTD (QIDO-Non-standard)
GA 2018A36711
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing copper-doped ZIF-8 nano composite comprises (i) adding 10-14 pts. wt. 2-methylimidazole, 20-30 pts. wt. zinc nitrate hexahydrate and 7-9 pts. wt. cupric chloride dihydrate into 65-75 pts. wt. anhydrous methanol and adding 90-100 pts. wt. aqueous ammonia; and (ii) stirring to react at 25-35 degrees C for 6-8 hours, washing solid product for 4-6 times with a water-methanol mixed solution with volume ratio of 2:1 after completing reaction and drying at 60-70 degrees C for 11-13 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing copper-doped ZIF-8 nano composite.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, rapid and easy to operate; and produces nano composite with large adsorption capacity for carbon dioxide in large scale.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The stirring to react is carried out preferably at 30 degrees C for 7 hours. The drying is carried out preferably at 60-70 degrees C for 12 hours.

PT P
PN CN108893610-A
TI Processing selenium comprises sintering selenium with sodium carbonate,mixing with sulfuric acid leaching, filtering, ion-exchanging, desorbing anion exchange resin, crystallizing, and treating copper indium gallium solution.
AU PAN Y
   GE P
AE HANERGY NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HANH-C)
GA 2018971486
AB    NOVELTY - Processing selenium comprises sintering selenium with sodium carbonate to obtain carbon dioxide and a sintering material, where the sintering material is a mixture of sodium selenite and copper indium gallium oxide, mixing sintering material with sulfuric acid leaching, filtering to obtain mixed solution containing copper sulfate, gallium sulfate, indium sulfate, and sodium selenite, ion-exchanging the mixed solution containing copper sulfate, gallium sulfate, indium sulfate and sodium selenite through an anion exchange resin to obtain copper indium gallium solution, desorbing the anion exchange resin to obtain selenite solution, crystallizing the selenite solution to obtain selenite or processing the selenite solution to obtain high-purity selenium, and treating the copper indium gallium solution to obtain copper, indium, and gallium.
   USE - The method is useful for processing selenium.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces consumption of acids and strong oxidants.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The anion exchange resin is a strongly basic anion exchange resin. The sodium carbonate is added in an amount of 20-30 wt.% based on the weight of the copper indium gallium selenide. The sulfuric acid is dilute sulfuric acid, and the dilute sulfuric acid is used in an amount of 1-5 wt.% of the copper indium gallium selenide. Preferred Method: The anion exchange resin is desorbed by sodium chloride or ammonium chloride to obtain a sodium selenite solution or an ammonium selenite solution.The method further comprises adding sodium carbonate into the copper indium gallium selenide for sintering, adding the copper indium gallium selenide and sodium carbonate into the thick slurry, extruding, granulating, drying, adding into the electric furnace, introducing air into the electric furnace, controlling the temperature in the electric furnace at 450-650 degrees C, and sintering for 1-2 hours to convert selenium into sodium selenite, and copper indium gallium into its corresponding oxide. The treatment of the copper indium gallium solution to obtain copper, indium, and gallium includes performing copper extraction, performing indium extraction, and performing gallium extraction. The copper extraction comprises adding a copper extractant into the copper indium gallium solution for copper extraction to obtain copper-containing extract phase and an indium gallium-containing raffinate phase, and subjecting the copper-containing extract phase to stripping and electrowinning to obtain high-purity copper. The indium extraction comprises adding an indium extractant to the raffinate phase containing indium gallium for indium extraction to obtain an indium-containing extract phase and a gallium-containing raffinate phase, extracting the indium-containing extract phase by stripping, replacing, and electrolyzing to obtain high-purity indium. The gallium extraction comprises adding a gallium extractant into the raffinate phase containing gallium for gallium extraction to obtain a gallium-containing extract phase and a gallium-extracted residual liquid, subjecting the gallium-containing extraction phase to stripping, alkalizing, and electrolyzing to obtain high-purity gallium. The method further comprises recovering cadmium and zinc by adding sodium hydroxide into the remaining solution after extracting gallium to adjust the pH to 8-9 to obtain a precipitate, which is filtered to obtain zinc hydroxide and cadmium hydroxide product. The copper extraction further comprises adjusting solution containing copper indium gallium to pH 2-3, extracting with a copper extractant, the extraction ratio is 3-6, the stripping agent in the stripping is 150-250 g/l sulfuric acid, the ratio of 1:1-5 in the stripping, and the number of stripping stages 1-2, carrying out the electrowinning in an electric storage tank, the anode of the electrowinning is lead alloy, the cathode is stainless steel, the electrolysis voltage is 1-3 V, the current density is 200-300 A/m2, and the electrowinning time is 12-72 hours. In the indium extraction, the ratio is 1:1-5, the extraction level is 1-6, the indium extraction stripping agent is hydrochloric acid, the stripping is compared to 1:1-5, and the extraction series is 1-3 level, replacing the stripping solution obtained by stripping by zinc powder, electrolyzing into an indium electrolysis cell to obtain high-purity indium, the electrolysis cell has a voltage of 1-5 V and a current density of 200-3000 A/m2. In the gallium extraction, the ratio is 1:1-5, the extraction level is 1-6, the stripping extract of the gallium extraction is water, the stripping is compared to 1:1-5, and the extraction series is 1-3 level, alkalizing the stripping solution obtained by stripping by sodium hydroxide and electrolyzed into a gallium electrolysis cell to obtain high-purity gallium, the cell voltage of the electrolysis is 1-5 V, and the current density is 200-3000 A/m2.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
