PN CN110590703-A; WO2021073044-A1; CN110590703-B
TI Synthesizing rubber vulcanization accelerator            dibenzothiazole disulfide involves adding            2-mercaptobenzothiazole to aqueous solution of sodium            carbonate and heating until completely dissolve to            obtain solution A.
AU LUO G
   TIAN J
   WANG K
AE UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
   UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 2019A75924
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing rubber vulcanization accelerator                dibenzothiazole disulfide involves adding                2-mercaptobenzothiazole to aqueous solution of                sodium carbonate and heating until completely                dissolve to obtain solution A containing                2-mercaptobenzothiazole sodium, adding hydrogen                peroxide to solution A obtained, and adjusting pH                of solution to 5-10 for oxidation reaction, and                obtain sodium bicarbonate solution B containing                dibenzothiazole disulfide solid after reaction,                filtering solution B obtained, washing and drying                obtained solid to obtain dibenzothiazole disulfide.                The sodium carbonate solution obtained by filtrate                obtained is heated to release carbon dioxide, and                is used as alkaline solution to dissolve                2-mercaptobenzothiazole and repeat to prepare two                dibenzothiazole sulfide.
   USE - Method for synthesizing rubber vulcanization                accelerator dibenzothiazole disulfide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to synthesize rubber                vulcanization accelerator dibenzothiazole disulfide                that is more environmentally friendly and reduces                large amount of salty wastewater.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The molar ratio of                  2-mercaptobenzothiazole and sodium carbonate is                  1:1-1:10. The mass fraction of sodium carbonate                  in the sodium carbonate aqueous solution is                  10-20%. The mass fraction of sodium                  2-mercaptobenzothiazole in solution A is 3-30%.                  Preferred Process: The method involves subjecting                  sodium carbonate solution obtained after filtrate                  is heating to release carbon dioxide which is                  used as alkaline solution to dissolve                  2-mercaptobenzothiazole and can be recycling more                  than 5 times. The solid obtained is washed with                  water and dried at 80-120 degrees C to obtain                  dibenzothiazole disulfide.

PN DE102018209346-A1; WO2019238562-A1
TI Producing vinyl acetate, comprises e.g. reacting gaseous mixture containing ethylene, acetic acid and oxygen in presence of supported catalyst in heterogeneously catalyzed, continuous gas phase process, and processing gas mixture.
AU DAFINGER W
   PATSCH B
   RUDOLF G
AE WACKER CHEM AG (WACK-C)
GA 2019A3667K
AB    NOVELTY - Producing vinyl acetate, comprises reacting a gaseous mixture containing ethylene, acetic acid and oxygen in the presence of a supported catalyst in a reactor in a heterogeneously catalyzed, continuous gas phase process, processing the gas mixture leaving the reactor, discharging a partial stream for the recovery of ethylene, and returning the remaining gas mixture to the reactor after loaded again with ethylene, acetic acid and oxygen, where oxygen with a proportion of less than or equal to 1000 vol.ppm argon is used.
   USE - The method is useful for producing vinyl acetate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces vinyl acetate acetate monomer with high selectivity, thus lowers by-product formation e.g. carbon dioxide and ethyl acetate, and is simple and economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method, oxygen with a proportion of 200-950 vol.ppm argon is used. Preferred Components: The partial stream which is discharged for the recovery of ethylene corresponds to 0.2-1.5 vol.% of the ethylene content in the gas mixture leaving the reactor.
   EXAMPLE - Spherical supported catalyst (prepared by impregnating bentonite with 2 wt.% palladium, 2 wt.% gold and 3 wt.% potassium) was reacted with 65 vol.% ethylene, 10 vol.% carbon dioxide, 13 vol.% acetic acid and 8 vol.% oxygen and balance amount of methane, ethane and nitrogen to obtain vinyl acetate with selectivity of 93.5%.

PN CN110527588-A; CN110527588-B
TI Carrying out subcritical extraction of corn oil involves crushing the corn seeds to obtain corn seeds powder, and adding ethane-isobutane-dimethyl ether as subcritical extractant to corn seeds powder to perform extraction.
AU LI Z
   CUI B
   TIAN Y
   SONG X
   ZHAO X
AE SHANDONG XINGQUAN OIL CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV QILU TECHNOLOGY (UYQQ-C)
GA 2019A2964S
AB    NOVELTY - Carrying out subcritical extraction of corn oil involves crushing the corn seeds to a particle size of 40-60 mesh to obtain corn seeds powder, and adding ethane-isobutane-dimethyl ether as a subcritical extractant to corn seeds powder to perform extraction at 45 degrees C for 30 minutes to get corn oil. The corn oil is passed into carbon dioxide containing dimethyl ether and methanol at a flow rate of 5ml/minute, and the extraction is performed at 40-45 degrees C and 35-40MPa for 120-150 minutes to obtain secondary crude oil. The two crude oils are combined and refined to obtain corn oil.
   USE - Method for carrying out subcritical extraction of corn oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to retain the flavor and nutritional components of corn seeds to the maximum.

PN CN110252300-A
TI Preparation of silver/manganese(IV) oxide catalyst by adding ammonium oxalate and silver precursor in potassium permanganate solution, stirring and mixing, hydrothermal reacting, filtering, washing and drying, and reduction reacting.
AU GUAN C
   LANG J
   WU H
   ZHAO G
   HUANG Z
AE BEIJING HYPERION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201991312M
AB    NOVELTY - A silver/manganese(IV) oxide (Ag/MnO2) catalyst is prepared by adding ammonium oxalate and silver precursor in potassium permanganate solution, uniformly stirring and mixing to obtain mixed solution, hydrothermal reacting, completely reacting, filtering reaction product, washing and drying to obtain powder, and reduction reacting under hydrogen atmosphere.
   USE - The method is used for preparing silver/manganese(IV) oxide catalyst for degrading formaldehyde under normal temperature and normal humidity (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and environment-friendly. The catalyst is easy to be industrialized, has long service life, high stability, atomic utilization rate reaching to 100%, relatively low precious metal loading and complete conversion of formaldehyde to non-toxic carbon dioxide and water under normal temperature and humidity conditions, and exhibits atomic dispersion of silver on surface of MnO2 support.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of potassium permanganate to ammonium oxalate is 1:2-2:1, preferably 1:2. The mass ratio of silver in silver precursor to manganese in potassium permanganate is 0.01-0.05:1, preferably 0.01-0.03:1. The silver precursor is silver nitrate and/or silver chloride, preferably silver nitrate. Preferred Method: Hydrothermal reaction is done at 80-200 degrees C for 12-48 hours, preferably 100-120 degrees C for 24 hours. Reduction reaction is done by placing powder in muffle furnace, reacting in hydrogen atmosphere from room temperature to 300-500 degrees C at rate of 2 degrees /minute, preferably 400 degrees C for 4 hours, and recovering to normal temperature. Degradation is done at 20-30 degrees C at relative humidity of 20-50%.
DC E31 (Compounds of V, Nb, Ta, Cr, Mo, W, Mn, Tc, Re, Fe, Ru, Os, Co, Rh, Ir, Ni, Pd, Pt, Pa, U and subsequent actinides.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN110144299-A
TI Fermenting edible fungus liquid by activating edible fungus strains, expanding, culturing activated edible fungus mother seed, mixing carbon source, nitrogen source and water, heating, homogenizing, cooling and fermenting.
AU HOU J
   SANG H
   XING J
   GUAN R
   ZHAO Z
   LI C
   YANG B
   YUAN F
   QIU J
AE CHENGDE SENYUAN GREEN FOOD CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201974578Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for fermenting edible fungus liquid involves (i) activating edible fungus strains to obtain activated edible fungus mother seed, expanding and culturing the activated edible fungus mother seed step-by-step to obtain fermented seed, (ii) mixing carbon source, nitrogen source and water to obtain mixture, heating the mixture at 60-66 degrees C, homogenizing, heating at 90-120 degrees C, continuously heating at 110-136 degrees C, heat-preserving for 6-30 seconds, and cooling to 30 degrees C to obtain fermentation culture medium, (iii) inoculating the fermentation seed into fermentation culture medium, and fermenting to obtain fermentation culture medium.
   USE - Method for fermenting edible fungus liquid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has low carbon dioxide emission and low energy consumption and is economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises inoculating edible fungus strain into parent culture medium for slant culture, where the matrix culture medium comprises 100-200 pts. wt. potato, 16-26 pts. wt. glucose and 16-26 pts. wt. agar, culturing the slant culture at 26-22 degrees C and humidity of 60-70% for 7-12 days. The step-by-step process of step (i) comprises inoculating activated edible fungus mother strain into primary culture medium for culture to obtain first-stage seed, inoculating the first-stage seed into second-stage seed culture medium for culture to obtain second-stage seed, inoculating the second-stage seed into third-stage seed culture medium to be cultured to obtain third-stage seed, and inoculating the third-stage seed into four-stage seed culture medium to be cultured to obtain fermentation seed. Preferred Composition: The raw material components of primary seed culture medium comprise 100-200 pts. wt. potato, 16-26 pts. wt. glucose, 2-6 pts. wt. peptone, 1-3 pts. wt. yeast extract powder, 3-6 pts. wt. monopotassium phosphate, 1-2.6 pts. wt. magnesium sulfate and 16-26 pts. wt. agar, where the temperature of the solid culture dish is 26-22 degrees C and humidity of 60-70%, and the culture time is 7-12 days. The secondary seed culture medium comprises 10-30 pts. wt. glucose, 2-3 pts. wt. peptone, 2-3 pts. wt. milk powder and 2-3 pts. wt. casein powder, and performing second-stage seed culture using solid flat vessel, culturing at 22-26 degrees C and humidity of 60-70% for 7-12 days. The three-stage seed culture medium comprises 10-20 pts. wt. glucose, 10-20 pts. wt. crystalline fructose, 10-20 pts. wt. sucrose, 2-3 pts. wt. casein powder and 2-3 pts. wt. milk powder, where the three-stage seed culture is liquid shake flask culture, and culturing at 22-26 degrees C and at a speed of 100-200 rpm for 60-66 hours. The four-stage culture medium comprises 10-20 pts. wt. glucose, 10-20 pts. wt. crystalline fructose, 10-20 pts. wt. sucrose, 16-22 pts. wt. casein powder and 16-22 pts. wt. milk powder, culturing at 22-26 degrees C and gas flux of 2-6 m3/hour, at a speed of 60-120 rpm for 21-36 hours. The weight ratio of carbon source, nitrogen source and water is (10-30):(10-20):(920-960). The mass ratio of fermented seed and fermentation medium is (6-10):(90-96). Preferred Components: The carbon source comprises glucose, sucrose, starch, maltose and cornmeal. The nitrogen source comprises soybean protein powder, peptone, yeast extract powder, casein powder, milk powder and whey protein powder. Preferred Conditions: The step (ii) involves homogenizing at 60-66 degrees C and pressure of 16-26 MPa. The step (iii) involves fermenting the culture in fermentation tank at 22-26 degrees C having a gas flux of 6-20 m3/hour at a speed of 60-120 rpm for 36-360 hours.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC D05-A04C

PT P
PN IN201721042668-A
TI Producing methane rich syngas involves providing biomass feedstock, feeding the biomass feedstock into reactor, where reactor is loaded with metal catalyst, providing gasifying agent into reactor and exposing biomass feedstock.
AU GHOLKAR P V
   TANKSALE A
   SHASTRI Y
AE IITB-MONASH RES ACAD (IITB-Non-standard)
GA 2019634844
AB    NOVELTY - Producing methane rich syngas involves providing a biomass feedstock, feeding the biomass feedstock into a reactor, where reactor is loaded with a metal catalyst, providing a gasifying agent into the reactor, exposing the biomass feedstock in the reactor on to the metal catalyst in presence of the gasifying agent for reactive flash volatilization (RFV) reaction of the biomass in the reactor and extracting methane rich syngas from the reactor, where methane rich syngas comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and methane.
   USE - Method for producing methane rich syngas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce methane rich syngas improves the catalytic activity, increases steam content of the reaction, and it is rapidly oxidizes the decomposed products to form gasses and prevents condensation reactions that is responsible for carbon and tar formation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The multiple transition metal elements are at least three of metals selected from nickel (Ni), copper (Cu), palladium (Pd), platinum (Pt), rhodium (Rh), rhenium (Re), ruthenium (Ru), iron (Fe) or a combination. The post transition metal element comprises aluminum (Al) or gamma alumina. The biomass feedstock is selected from Scenedesmus species. The alkali and alkaline metals comprises sodium, potassium, magnesium and calcium non-metals, phosphorus, metalloids of silicon, metals of zinc, iron, and post transition elements of aluminum. Preferred Compositions: The biomass feedstock comprises 11 wt.% ash, 35-42 wt.% carbon, 4-8 wt.% hydrogen, 4-10 wt.% nitrogen, 0.1-2 wt.% sulfur and 40-60 wt.% oxygen. Preferred Conditions: The metal catalyst comprises a multiple transition metal elements supported on a post transition metal element, maintained at 400-650 Angstrom . The each transition metal element comprises nanoparticles of metal or metal alloy having particle size of 20-100 nm. The biomass feedstock comprises microalgae in a dried powdered or slurry form, fed at room temperature and pressure. The biomass feedstock is dropped onto the metal catalyst by a screw feeder and allowed to react in the reactor for 0.5-100 sec. The reaction temperature is maintained in the range of 550-600 Angstrom . The reactor is heated externally at a temperature of 550-700 Angstrom . The metal catalyst is preheated at 400 Angstrom and reduced in presence of hydrogen. The gasifying agent comprises steam having temperature of 150-180 Angstrom provided into the reactor at an atmospheric pressure. The concentration of steam in the reactor is adjusted to maintain a ratio of carbon-to-steam in the reactor in the range of 0.1-0.8. Preferred Process: The volume percentage (v/v) of molecular methane in the extracted methane rich syngas is in the range of 5-20 wt.%. The 1 gram metal catalyst is loaded in a furnace of the reactor followed by reduction of metal catalyst with 5 wt.% ratio of hydrogen and nitrogen gas mixture at 400 Angstrom for 4 hours. The biomass feedstock is converted into gaseous phase, before contacting the catalyst surface or the biomass feedstock gets pyrolyzed by contacting a bed formed due to transformation of elements into tar and gas and reacts in the metal catalyst bed formed due to the element transformation. The catalytic conversion of biomass in to gaseous phase leads to formation of porous bed of slag. The slag contains transition elements, non-metals, post transition elements, metalloids, alkali and alkaline earth metals.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

TI Room particulate penetrant coefficient detecting method, involves determining natural sedimentation coefficient according to penetrant coefficient, determining fresh air amount in room, and performing air purifying operation by air purifier.
AU PENG C
   XI G
   NI P
   TIAN W
   ZHOU D
   FAN L
   ZHANG Q
   TANG Y
   ZHOU L
   HE M
   ZUO Z
   CHEN T
   SHEN T
   ZUO J
AE UNIV NANTONG (UYNT-C)
GA 2019571692
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining air volume data. Room particulate matter concentration is determined according to the air volume data. Particulate penetrant coefficient is determined based on the room particulate matter concentration. Natural sedimentation coefficient is determined according to the particulate penetrant coefficient. Fresh air amount in a room is determined. Fresh air purifying operation is performed by an air purifier. Carbon dioxide background concentration is determined according to the fresh air purifying operation.
   USE - Room particulate penetrant coefficient detecting method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simplifying room particulate penetrant coefficient detecting process and improving detecting convenience and detecting accuracy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a room particulate penetrant coefficient detecting system.

PT P
PN US2019184379-A1; CN109939687-A; TW201927731-A
TI Metal oxide catalyst comprises copper, zinc, aluminum and silicon, and further indium and/or cesium.
AU LO M
   CHEN Y
   HSU H
   LU M
   XU X
AE IND TECHNOLOGY RES INST (ITRI-C)
   IND TECHNOLOGY RES INST (ITRI-C)
   IND TECHNOLOGY RES INST (ITRI-C)
GA 201951696R
AB    NOVELTY - Metal oxide catalyst comprises: 35-65 pts. wt. copper; 20-50 pts. wt. zinc; 2-10 pts. wt. aluminum; and 0.1-5 pts. wt. silicon, where the metal oxide further comprises 0.05-5 wt.% indium and/or 0.05-5 wt.% cesium.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for converting carbon oxide into methanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst increases the efficiency of converting the carbon oxide into the methanol in a single reaction in a single fixed bed reactor, thus enhancing the methanol yield; and achieves the purpose of reusing carbon and mitigating carbon emission.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for converting carbon oxide into methanol comprising putting the catalyst into a fixed bed reactor, and introducing a gas mixture of hydrogen and the carbon oxide into the fixed bed reactor, and performing a first hydrogenation reaction under the effect of the catalyst to form the methanol, where the carbon oxide comprises carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon oxide comprises carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide. The catalyst comprises 0.1-3 wt.% indium and 0.1-3 wt.% cesium. The carbon oxide further comprises carbon monoxide. The first hydrogenation reaction further forms carbon monoxide and water. The supplementary gas comprises carbon dioxide and hydrogen. The first hydrogenation reaction and the second hydrogenation reaction is performed at a gas hourly space velocity of 3000-10000 per hour at a temperature of 200-300 degrees C, under a pressure of 30-80 kg/cm2. The process is performed in a single fixed bed reactor. Preferred Process: The process further: performing a second hydrogenation reaction, and the second hydrogenation reaction comprises after separating the methanol and water out of the product of the first hydrogenation reaction, introducing the remaining carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, and hydrogen and additional supplementary gas into the fixed bed reactor to form the methanol under the effect of the catalyst; and repeating the second hydrogenation reaction until the total yield of methanol achieves 100%.
   EXAMPLE - Liquid A (comprising 54.3 g of cupric nitrate, 39.1 g of zinc nitrate, 6.6 g aluminum nitrate, and 0.95 g of silicon (sol) were dissolved in 1500 ml of de-ionized water) and liquid B (comprising 140 g sodium bicarbonate was dissolved in 500 ml of de-ionized water) were added into liquid C (comprising 400 g de-ionized water was poured into a flask) with constant stirring at an addition rate of 10 ml/minutes. After stirring for 24 hours, the mixture was filtered, and filtered cake was washed with de-ionized water several times to remove the sodium ions in the filtered cake. The filtered cake was baked at 110 degrees C and then calcinated at 600 degrees C to obtain catalyst precursor I. The catalyst precursor I was cooled down to room temperature, and 10 g of catalyst precursor I was put into a pear-shaped bottle. The solution of 0.026 g of indium(III) nitrate hydrate was added to the pear-shaped bottle. The mixture was then dried and then baked at 110 degrees C and calcinated to obtain metal oxide catalyst.

PT P
PN CN109647373-A; CN109647373-B
TI Black indium oxide ultra-thin nanosheets useful in the fields of electronics, optoelectronics and environmental energy.
AU OUYANG S
   QI Y
   SONG L
   NING S
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 201939472F
AB    NOVELTY - Black indium oxide ultra-thin nanosheets, is claimed. The nanosheets are prepared by dissolving sodium oleate and indium chloride powder in deionized water, placing resulting solution in a hydrothermal reaction kettle, naturally cooling down after heat preservation, taking out suspension, centrifuging, washing, drying, grinding, placing sample in a photothermal catalytic reaction system to obtain product with average thickness of 3-5 nm.
   USE - The nanosheets are useful in the fields of electronics, optoelectronics and environmental energy.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly and simple; and product is pure and free of other impurities.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing nanosheets, as mentioned above; and
   (2) use of nanosheets in photothermal catalysis, comprising taking nanosheets which directly utilizes UV light in sunlight, visible light and infrared portions, and producing carbon-monoxide by photothermal catalysis of carbon-dioxide and hydrogen mixed gas in a volume ratio of 1:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amount of sodium oleate is 0.1-4 wt.%, and indium chloride is 0.1-4 wt.%. Preferred Method: The hydrothermal reaction temperature is 100-180 degrees C and time is maintained for 1-5 hours. The hydrothermal reaction has a suspension rate of 10000 revolutions/minute and above, and the solvent to be washed is a mixed solvent of cyclohexane and ethanol in a volume ratio of 1:3. In the photothermal catalytic reaction system, a mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon-monoxide and/or carbon-dioxide injected to the system pressure at a ratio of more than 10 kPa.

PN CN109608079-A
TI Performing in-situ carbonization method for improving structural performance of carbonized carbide product involves molding carbonized product and adding alkali-resistant bacteria which is capable of performing urea hydrolysis reaction.
AU WANG F
   ZHAO S
   LIU Z
AE UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
GA 201935822N
AB    NOVELTY - Performing an in-situ carbonization method for improving structural performance of a carbonized carbide product involves molding carbonized product before mixing raw materials, adding alkali-resistant bacteria which is capable of performing urea hydrolysis reaction of raw material, and distributing it uniformly in raw material. The water-forming product is replaced with urea nutrient solution during molding of product. The product is immersed in carbamide nutrient solution to continue curing after forming and demolding.
   USE - Method for performing an in-situ carbonization method for improving structural performance of a carbonized carbide product.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform an in-situ carbonization method for improving structural performance of a carbonized carbide product, which has simple process, low cost, improves carbonization degree of carbonized product, improves compactness and strength, and increases carbon dioxide pressure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Conditions: The carbonized carbide product is a hardened calcium silicate product. The alkali-resistant bacteria is Bacillus or Rhodobacter sphaeroides. The alkali-resistant bacteria and raw materials are mixed in a mass ratio of 5-15, and put into a ball mill for 2 hours. The curing is carried out at a temperature of 30 degrees C for 12-48 hours. Preferred Compositions: The urea nutrient solution comprises 20-80g/L urea, 3g/L beef extract, 5g/L peptone and 5g/L sodium chloride, and has neutral pH.

AB    NOVELTY - The processing method of zero discharge of ore                dressing wastewater involves preprocessing ore                dressing wastewater, adding 300-600 g/m3 iron salt                and hydrogen peroxide, stirring at 50-80 rpm for                15-30 minutes and then oxidizing, removing organic                mineral processing chemicals to mineralize organic                component into carbon dioxide and water, adding                sodium hydroxide, adjusting pH value to 10-11,                hydrolyzing iron salt, reacting for 15-30 minutes,                adding 2-2.5 pts. wt. sodium carbonate and 2-10                g/m3 coagulant additive, reacting for 2-5 minutes,                performing solid-liquid separation and processing                using reverse osmosis membrane and electrodialysis                membrane.

GA 201908081U

PT P
PN CN109046388-A; CN109046388-B
TI Removal method of antibiotics in water using copper sulfide/vanadate heterojunction photocatalyst comprises adding to-be-treated water with photocatalyst, stirring, and photocatalytically reacting under illumination condition.
AU LAI C
   ZHANG M
   LI B
   HUANG D
   ZENG G
   XU P
   HUANG F
   LIU S
   LI M
   HE J
   ZHANG Y
AE UNIV HUNAN (UYHU-C)
GA 2019010878
AB    NOVELTY - A removal method of antibiotics in water using copper sulfide/vanadate heterojunction photocatalyst comprises adding to-be-treated water with copper sulfide/vanadate heterojunction photocatalyst. The photocatalyst comprises copper sulfide and bismuth vanadate.
   USE - Method for removal of antibiotics in water using copper sulfide/vanadate heterojunction photocatalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environment-friendly, can mineralize antibiotics (such as ciprofloxacin) into water and carbon dioxide, can effectively perform solid-liquid separation without secondary pollution, does not need large equipment, and has simple operation, low cost, and high processing efficiency. The photocatalyst is recyclable and can efficiently degrade antibiotics in water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the photocatalyst comprising preparing bismuth vanadate and copper nitrate trihydrate into bismuth vanadate and copper nitrate suspension, adding sodium thiosulfate pentahydrate, and water bath reacting.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of copper sulfide and bismuth vanadate is 1-11:33. The barium vanadate is 1-2 mu m thick barium vanadate block. The copper sulfide is copper sulfide particles. The antibiotic-containing water body has antibiotic initial concentration of 8-10 mg/L and pH of 5-6. Preferred Method: The bismuth vanadate is prepared by preparing bismuth nitrate pentahydrate and ammonium metavanadate to bismuth nitrate and ammonium metavanadate suspension, mixing with urea, reacting, washing and drying. The bismuth nitrate and ammonium metavanadate suspension is prepared by dissolving bismuth nitrate pentahydrate in 1 mol/L nitric acid solution, adding ammonium metavanadate at ratio of 3 mmol:3 mmol:16 mL, stirring for 10-30 minutes, heating to 70-80 degrees C for 23-24 hours, washing for 4-6 times with water and ethanol respectively, and drying to 50-70 degrees C for 20-24 hours. The removal method is done by stirring reactants under dark atmosphere for 30-60 minutes until adsorption-desorption equilibrium is reached, and photocatalytically reacting under illumination condition of 400-760 nm and at stirring speed of 550-600 revolutions/minute for 0-90 minutes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The antibitioc in antibiotic-containing water body is ciprofloxacin and/or tetracycline.

PN CN108990743-A; CN108990743-B
TI Promoting crop growth in greenhouse in winter involves mixing straw decomposing agent and compost material into greenhouse soil for crop planting, where composting raw material comprises one or more of straw, weed, and deciduous.
AU ZHANG X
   CAO J
   LIAO J
AE SHANGHAI CAOYE AGRIC DEV CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A3728W
AB    NOVELTY - Promoting crop growth in greenhouse in winter involves mixing straw decomposing agent and compost material into the greenhouse soil for crop planting, where composting raw material comprises one or more of straw, weed, deciduous, vine, peat, garbage, vegetable waste, and kitchen waste.
   USE - Method for promoting crop growth in greenhouse in winter.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enalbes to promote crop growth in greenhouse improves temperature of the greenhouse and the content of carbon dioxide, improves utilization ratio of the greenhouse in the winter crop, promotes growth of crops in the greenhouse, improves crop yield, and saves time cost, increases revenue, process is simple, easy to operate, safe and environmentally friendly, has good repeatability and low cost, and is favorable for widespread application.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The straw and straw decomposing agent is filled into the soil of the greenhouse to carry out crop planting, where weight ratio of the straw and the straw decomposing agent is 5:1-10:1, trenches in the greenhouse soil in winter, pulverized straw and the straw decomposing agent, and mixed uniformly according to the weight ratio of the straw and the straw decomposing agent of 5:1-10:1. The straw and straw decomposing agent mixture is filled into the ditch and the soil surface of the shed is leveled after covering the soil. The crop is sown and cultivated in the greenhouse. The soil trenching is performed on both sides and in the middle of the greenhouse. The groove has a width of 70-90 cm and a depth of 50-70 cm. The straw comprises one or more of corn stover, wheat straw, and rice straw. The straw decomposing agent is obtained by lactic acid bacteria, yeast, anaerobic actinomycetes, photosynthesis bacteria, cellulolytic bacteria, lignin-decomposing bacteria, and Bacillus subtilis. The mixture of the straw and the straw decomposing agent is filled into the ditch, and the amount of the mixture of the straw and the straw decomposer per square meter in the greenhouse is 15-50 kg. The covering soil has a thickness of 8-12 cm. The number of soil trenches is three, one on each side and one in the middle of the greenhouse. The groove width is 80 cm, the groove depth is 60 cm. The weight ratio of the straw and straw decomposing agent is 10:1. The straw and the straw decomposing agent mixture are filled into the ditch, and the amount of the mixture of the straw and the straw decomposing agent per square area of the greenhouse is 15-30 kg, the thickness of the covering soil is 10 cm. The lactic acid bacteria, the yeast bacteria, the anaerobic actinomycetes, and the photosynthesis bacteria are anaerobic fermentation, the cellulose decomposition bacteria, the lignin decomposition bacteria, and Bacillus subtilis is an aerobic fermentation.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); P11 (Soil working, planting (A01B, C).); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)

AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of zinc oxide catalytic material, involves mixing aqueous zinc acetate dihydrate solution with hexamethylenetetramine in a molar ratio of 1:1.5-2.5, obtaining precursor solution and performing hydrothermal reaction on the precursor solution.
   USE - Preparation of zinc oxide catalytic material for catalytic organic degradation, dye-sensitized solar cells and photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of zinc oxide catalytic material with planar homojunction structure and high photoelectrocatalytic efficiency, and effectively promotes the separation of photogenerated carriers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for zinc oxide catalytic material which is provided with a dumbbell-shaped zinc oxide and a zinc oxide nanosheet attached to the surface of the dumbbell-shaped zinc oxide. The zinc oxide catalytic material has a diameter of 3-6 mu m and a length of 15-18 mu m.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass concentration of zinc acetate dihydrate aqueous solution is 15-30 mg/mL. Preferred Conditions: The hydrothermal reaction is carried out at 70-120 degrees C and 100-275 kPa for 7-12 hours. Preferred Process: The zinc oxide nanosheet is supported on the (101) crystal plane of the dumbbell-shaped zinc oxide.

The aqueous                  nano-cellulose dispersion includes hydroxypropyl                  methylcellulose aqueous dispersion, hydroxyethyl                  cellulose aqueous dispersion, hydroxy terminated                  nano-cellulose aqueous dispersion and/or                  poly-anionic nano-cellulose aqueous dispersions.                  The aqueous nano-cellulose dispersion has weight                  fraction of 5-15 pts. wt.

PN CN108862266-A; CN108862266-B
TI Preparation of graphene oxide nano-material by            preparing graphene oxide aqueous solution, adding            butanol, standing, water bath heating, standing,            suctioning lower layer liquid, and adding remaining            liquid with, e.g. tetrabutyl titanate.
AU LI P
   CHEN H
   LV J
AE UNIV LIAONING TECHNICAL (ULNT-C)
GA 2018955319
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of graphene oxide nano-material                comprises sonicating graphene oxide powder in                deionized water to obtain graphene oxide aqueous                solution, adding to round bottom flask, slowly                adding butanol, standing for 10 minutes, slowly                introducing nitrogen or carbon dioxide from the                bottom of flask through quartz tube while water                bath heating at constant temperature, and standing                for 2 hours; suctioning lower layer liquid through                glass pipette, continuously shaking flask for 5                minutes, transferring liquid in flask to a beaker,                and standing for 5 minutes; and preparing mixed                solution of tetrabutyl titanate and absolute                ethanol, slowly dropping to beaker through glass                dropper, stirring with glass rod, centrifugally                washing, drying residual precipitate, and heating                under air atmosphere.
   USE - Method for preparation of graphene oxide                nano-material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The sonication is done for                  30 minutes. The water bath heating is done at                  50-80 degrees C for 2 hours. The drying of                  precipitate is done at 80 degrees C for 24-48                  hours. The centrifugal washing is done at speed                  of 3000-6000 revolutions/minute. The heating                  under air atmosphere is done at 120-300 degrees C                  for 30-45 minutes. Preferred Components: The                  graphene oxide aqueous solution has concentration                  of 0.5-2 mg/mL and volume of 300-500 mL. The                  lower layer liquid has volume of 15-45 mL.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alcohol has volume                  of 200 mL. The volume ratio of tetrabutyl                  titanate and absolute ethanol is 1:4-1:20. The                  tetrabutyl titanate has concentration of 0.2-1.2                  mg/mL.

PN CN108456334-A; CN108456334-B
TI New granular nano-porous thermosetting nitrocellulose useful for preparing nano-composite energetic material basic framework.
AU ZHOU X
   LIU Y
   ZHANG W
   BAO T
   DENG L
AE UNIV NAT DEFENSE TECHNOLOGY (UNDT-C)
   UNIV NAT DEFENSE TECHNOLOGY (UNDT-C)
GA 2018689874
AB    NOVELTY - Granular nano-porous thermosetting nitrocellulose, is new. The mass percentage of the nitrocellulose thickness is greater than 93% and has an average particle diameter of less than or equal to 600 mu m and porous structure having average pore diameter of less than 30 nm.
   USE - The nitrocellulose is useful for preparing nano-composite energetic material basic framework.
   ADVANTAGE - The nitrocellulose has uniformly distributed nano-hole structure and low density.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing granular nano-porous thermosetting nitrocellulose, comprising (i) adding commercially available ordinary nitrocellulose, curing agent and reaction catalyst into acetone, and mixing uniformly to obtain nitrocellulose solution; (ii) adding the nitrocellulose solution into dimethyl silicone oil, stirring, dispersing the solute into small droplets, stirring continuously to obtain nitrocellulose wet gel and aging; and (iii) subjecting the nitrocellulose wet gel into solvent replacement and drying by supercritical carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The thermosetting heat decomposition has an onset temperature of less than 190 degrees C, a peak temperature of not greater than 195 degrees C, and a termination temperature of less than 200 degrees C. In the step (i), the curing agent includes 2,4-toluene diisocyanate, isophorone diisocyanate, diphenylmethane diisocyanate, polyphenylpolymethylene polyisocyanate and/or hexamethylene diisocyanate, the curing agent 2,4-toluene diisocyanate is in an amount of 30-40% by mass of commercially available ordinary nitrocellulose, the reaction catalyst is di(dodecylthio)dibutyltin, dibutyltin dilaurate, stannous octoate and/or dibutyltin diacetate and the reaction catalyst is dibutyltin dilaurate in an amount of 1-5% by mass of the commercially available nitrocellulose. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the commercially available ordinary nitrocellulose has a nitrogen content of 11-13%, the concentration of commercially available ordinary nitrocellulose added in acetone is 40-100 g/l. In the step (ii), the volume ratio of the commercially available ordinary nitrocellulose solution to the dimethyl silicone oil is 1:5-1:3, and the stirring speed is 800-1000 revolutions/minute and stirring at 30-50 degrees C. In the step (iii), the supercritical carbon dioxide drying temperature is 45-55 degrees C, drying pressure is 12-15 MPa, and the drying time is 3-12 hours.

In the step (i), the Saccharomyces cerevisiae fermentation liquid centrifuged at 7000 revolutions/minutes for 10 minutes, and then centrifugally washing. In the step (iii), the uniformly mixing the protective agent and bacteria body freezing drying at -80 degrees C for 4 hours, and the cold trap temperature is -55 degrees C and the drying time is 20 hours. In the step (i), the rotational speed of the centrifugal washing is 6000-8000 revolutions/minutes; preferably 7000 revolutions/minutes, the time is 8-12 minutes; preferably 10 minutes and the temperature is 4 degrees C.

The plum solution is obtained by immersing the plum in a sugar-alcohol aqueous solution, extracting the plum, and centrifuging the juice by adding juice into an electric juicer provided with a pressure vessel, applying steam and pressurizing the plum under a steam pressure for 90-180 minutes.

Preferred Components: The sweetener is chosen from one or more combinations of acesulfame, aspartame, neotame, sodium saccharin, stevioside, sucralose, high-intensity sweetener and glycyrrhizin. The preservative is chosen from one or more combinations of chlorobutanol, ethylparaben, methylparaben, butylparaben, ethylparaben, thimerosal, benzyl alcohol, sorbic acid or its potassium salt and benzoic acid or ts sodium salts. PN CN107902728-A
TI Tin/antimony-granular activated carbon particles useful for treatment of 4-chlorophenol wastewater in three-dimensional electrochemical reaction.
AU LI M
   CHEN Y
   QIN Q
   LIAO A
   XIE S
   LAN P
   XIE T
AE UNIV GUANGXI NATIONALITIES (UGXN-C)
GA 201830095B
AB    NOVELTY - Tin/antimony-granular activated carbon particles are claimed. The tin/antimony-granular activated carbon particles prepared by impregnating tin and antimony metal ions with modified particulate activated carbon.
   USE - The tin/antimony-granular activated carbon particles is useful for treatment of 4-chlorophenol wastewater in three-dimensional electrochemical reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves 4-chlorophenol waste water treatment efficiency; reaches removing rate more than 83%; and has simple process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of tin/antimony-granular activated carbon particles for treatment of 4-chlorophenol wastewater in three-dimensional electrochemical reaction comprising taking tin/antimony-granular activated carbon particles as particle electrode, dimensionally stable anode electrode as anode, titanium plate as cathode, build a bipolar three-dimensional electrochemical reactor, carrying out three-dimensional electrochemical reaction under electrochemical oxidation and degrading 4-chlorophenol to carbon dioxide and water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises dissolving tin(IV) chloride pentahydrate and antimony trichloride in organic alcohol solvent to obtain mixed solution, soaking granular activated carbon in the mixed solution, stirring at 150-200 revolutions per minute for more than 2 hours, drying and baking at 300-500 degrees C to obtain tin/antimony-granular activated carbon particles. The method further comprises washing granular activated carbon with deionized water and drying. The three-dimensional electrochemical reaction temperature is 40-60 degrees C. The method comprises carrying out decomposition of 4-chlorophenol into hydroxy-containing intermediates, and eventually degrade to carbon dioxide and water (in the three-dimensional electrochemical reaction), where the hydroxy radical-containing intermediate is p-benzoquinone, 4-chlorocatechol, quinol, fumaric acid and/or oxalic acid. Preferred Components: The mass ratio of tin(IV) chloride pentahydrate, antimony trichloride and granular activated carbon is: 8-16:0.4-2:60-80. The electrolyte used in the three-dimensional electrochemical reaction is sodium sulfate, sodium chloride, potassium sulfate and/or potassium chloride. The 4-chlorophenol wastewater concentration is 100-500 mg/l, the simulated wastewater volume is 200-300 ml, the electrolyte concentration is 2-4 g/l, the plate spacing between cathode and anode is 2-4 cm, the reaction current is 1-2 A, and the particle electrode dosage is 10-20 g (in the three-dimensional electrochemical reaction). The removal rate of 4-chlorophenol in the 4-chlorophenol wastewater is greater than or equal to 83%.

PN CN107822991-A
TI Feminine hygiene tissue useful for preventing e.g. microbial infection, is prepared by mixing rhizoma atractylodis, rhizoma coptidis, Eucalyptus and mint, subjecting to supercritical fluid extraction and adhering filtrate to base cloth.
AU MENG J
   LI F
   TENG H
   JIANG J
   WU S
AE QIANJIN PHARM CO LTD ZHUZHOU (QJPM-C)
GA 201824454U
AB    NOVELTY - Feminine hygiene tissue is prepared by (a) collecting, drying, crushing and mixing rhizoma atractylodis, rhizoma coptidis, Eucalyptus and mint to obtain the mixed raw material, (b) subjecting the mixed material to supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction with an aqueous ethanol solution, as an extractant to obtain an extract, (c) diluting the extract with a solvent comprising ethanol and propylene glycol and filtering the diluted extract to obtain filtrate and (d) adhering the filtrate to the base cloth to obtain the product.
   USE - The feminine hygiene tissue is useful for preventing diseases and microbial infection caused by unclean female genitals for preventing urinary system infection, bacterial, fungal and trichomonal vaginitis and non-specific vaginitis.
   ADVANTAGE - The feminine hygiene tissue has excellent stability and long-term effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The extractant is 70-90% aqueous ethanol solution. The solvent comprises ethanol and propylene glycol in a volume ratio of (1-2):(5-7). The natural flavor is chosen from one or more combinations of oregano, borneol, Artemisia capillaries, mint, and Artemisia. The artificial flavor comprises plant essential oil and/or chemically synthesized flavor. The plant essential oil is chosen from one or more combinations of Torreya grandis essential oil, wild chrysanthemum essential oil, Daphne odera essential oil, pine cone essential oil, felon herb oil, thyme essential oil, Eucalyptus essential oil, tea tree essential oil and camphor tree essential oil. The base cloth comprises non-woven fabric and/or wet strength paper. Preferred Method: In step (b), subjecting the mixed material to supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction with 1-10 %mass aqueous ethanol solution as an extractant at 20-40 MPa and 30-50 degrees C to obtain an extract. In step (c), adding natural or artificial flavor and then filtering the diluted extract. The method involves adhering the filtrate to the base cloth by soaking the base cloth in the filtrate or spraying the filtrate on the base cloth. The spraying involves atomizing the filtrate and spraying on the base cloth under high pressure. In step (a), collecting 20-30 parts rhizoma atractylodis, 10-30 parts rhizoma coptidis, 5-15 parts Eucalyptus and 1-5 parts mint.
   ACTIVITY - Gynecological; Uropathic; Antibacterial; Fungicide; Protozoacide; Antiinflammatory.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PN RD646005-A
TI Method for diagnosing diesel oxidation catalyst performance, involves locating selective catalytic reduction component downstream of diesel oxidation catalyst, and determining hydrocarbon conversion efficiency of diesel oxidation catalyst.
AU 
AE ANONYMOUS (ANON-C)
GA 201812498J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves locating a selective catalytic reduction (SCR) component downstream of a diesel oxidation catalyst (DCC). The DCC is functioned to oxidize hydrocarbons emitted by an engine and convert the hydrocarbons into carbon dioxide and water. Known quantity of hydrocarbon emission is intentionally created from an engine during relatively steady state operation to obtain stable temperature and flow rate conditions. Hydrocarbon conversion efficiency of the DCC is determined by measuring and tracking SCR's nitrogen oxide conversion efficiency using onboard nitrogen oxide sensors.
   USE - Method for diagnosing DOC performance.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables monitoring performance of the DCC and diagnosing failure of the DCC for exhaust system architectures including the SCR component located downstream of the DCC.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a method for diagnosing DOC performance.

PT P
PN WO2018019841-A1; CN109689876-A; EP3491141-A1; US2019169654-A1; JP2019525761-W
TI Producing at least one alcohol from a carbon source, comprises producing alcohol in aqueous medium under aerobic conditions and extracting alcohol from aqueous medium by e.g. contacting alcohol in aqueous medium with extracting medium.
AU HECKER A
   HAAS T
   BECK S
   BACK S
AE EVONIK DEGUSSA GMBH (EVON-C)
   EVONIK DEGUSSA GMBH (EVON-C)
GA 201809038J
AB    NOVELTY - Producing at least one alcohol from a carbon source, comprises: (a) producing alcohol in aqueous medium under aerobic conditions; and (b) extracting alcohol from aqueous medium by (b1) contacting alcohol in aqueous medium with at least one extracting medium for a time sufficient to extract alcohol from aqueous medium into extracting medium, and (b2) separating extracting medium with extracted alcohol from aqueous medium, where the extracting medium comprises oleyl alcohol, the alcohol comprises at least 3 carbon atoms, and the carbon source comprises carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for producing at least one alcohol from a carbon source.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is an energy-efficient, environmentally friendly and bio-based method which is alternative to the petro-based production process of alcohols.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (a) (when the alcohol is propanol) comprises: (a1) contacting the carbon source with a reaction mixture comprising a first acetogenic microorganism in an exponential growth phase, free oxygen and a second acetogenic microorganism in a stationary phase, where the first and second acetogenic microorganism is capable of converting the carbon source to the acetate and/or ethanol; and (a2) contacting the acetate and/or ethanol from (a1) with a third microorganism capable of converting the acetate and/or ethanol to the propanol in the aqueous medium. The step (a) (when the alcohol is at least one higher alcohol) comprises: (a11) contacting the carbon source with a reaction mixture comprising a first acetogenic microorganism in an exponential growth phase, free oxygen and a second acetogenic microorganism in a stationary phase, where the first and second acetogenic microorganism is capable of converting the carbon source to the acetate and/or ethanol; (a12) contacting the acetate and/or ethanol from step (a11) with a fourth microorganism capable of carrying out the ethanol carboxylate fermentation pathway and converting the acetate and/or ethanol from step (a11) to form an acid; and (a13) contacting the acid from step (a12) with the reaction mixture of step (a11), where the first and/or second acetogenic microorganism is capable of converting the acid to the corresponding higher alcohol in the aqueous medium, and the higher alcohol contains 4-10 carbon atoms. The steps (a) and (b) are carried out in a single fermenter. Preferred Components: The first and second microorganism is e.g. Clostridium autoethanogenum DSMZ 19630 (preferred), Moorella thermoautotrophica, Ruminococcus productus, Oxobacter pfennigii, Methanosarcina acetivorans, Desulfotomaculum kuznetsovii, Clostridium formicoaceticum, Clostridium butyricum, Lactobacillus delbrueckii, Propionibacterium acidipropionici, Anaerobiospirillum succiniciproducens, Bacteroides amylophilus and Clostridium carboxidivorans. The first acetogenic microorganism in the exponential growth phase has a growth rate of 0.01-2 hour-1. The aerobic conditions are a result of oxygen being at a concentration of 0.000005-1% volume in the gas phase. The third microorganism is a propionogen. The third microorganism is a genetically modified organism comprising increased expression relative to the wild type cell of propionate coenzyme A-transferase, lactoyl-coenzyme A dehydratase and acryloyl-coenzyme A reductase. The third microorganism is Clostridium neopropionicum. The fourth microorganism is Clostridium kluyveri or Clostridium carboxidivorans.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The higher alcohol is 1-butanol, 2-m ethyl- 1-butanol, isobutanol, 3-methyl-1-butanol, 1-hexanol, 1-octanol, 1-pentanol, 1-heptanol, 3-methyl-1-pentanol, 4-methyl-1-hexanol, 5-methyl-1-heptanol, 4-methyl-1-pentanol, 5-methyl-1-hexanol and/or 6-methyl-1-heptanol.
   EXAMPLE - Clostridium ljungdahlii was inoculated with an OD600 of 0.1 with autotrophically grown cells. Thus, Clostridium ljungdahlii was grown in complex medium under continuous gassing with synthesis gas consisting of 67% hydrogen and 33% carbon dioxide at a rate of 3 l/hour in 1 l serum bottles with 500 ml complex medium (comprised of e.g. 1 g/l ammonium chloride, 0.1 g/l potassium chloride, 0.2 g/l magnesium sulfate heptahydrate, 0.8 g/l sodium chloride, 0.1 g/l monopotassium phosphate, 20 mg/l calcium chloride dihydrate, 1 g/l yeast extract, 0.4 g/l L-cysteine-hydrochloride, 20 mu g/l biotin, 20 mu g/l folic acid, 100 mu g/l pyridoxine-hydrochloride, 50 mu g/l riboflavin, 50 mu g/l nicotinic acid, 1 mu g/l vitamin B12 and 50 mu g/l lipoic acid). The gas was introduced into the liquid phase by a microbubble disperser with a pore diameter of 10 mu m. The serum bottle was continuously shaken in an open water bath at 37 degrees C and a shaking rate of 150 minute-1. The cells were harvested in the late-logarithmic phase with an OD600 of 0.67 and a pH of 4.69 by anaerobic centrifugation. The supernatant was discarded and the pellet was resuspended in 10 ml of above medium. This cell suspension was then used to inoculate the co-culture experiment. Parallel to that, Clostridium kluyveri was grown heterotrophically in 200 ml complex medium (comprised of e.g. 0.25 g/l ammonium chloride, 0.2 g/l magnesium sulfate heptahydrate, 0.31 g/l dipotassium phosphate, 0.23 g/l monopotassium phosphate, 2.5 g/l sodium bicarbonate, 1 g/l yeast extract, 20 g/l ethanol, 0.25 g/l L-cysteine-hydrochloride, 6 mu g/l boric acid, 100 mu g/l vitamin B12, 80 mu g/l para-aminobenzoic acid, 20 mu g/l biotin, 200pg/l nicotinic acid, 300 mu g/l pyridoxine-hydrochloride, 200 mu g/l thiamine-hydrochloride monohydrate) in 500 ml serum bottles on acetate and ethanol. The serum bottle was continuously shaken in an open water bath at 37 degrees C and a shaking rate of 100 minute-1. The cells were harvested in the late logarithmic phase with an OD600 of 0.81 and a pH of 5.96 by anaerobic centrifugation. The supernatant was discarded and the pellet was resuspended in 10 ml of above medium. This cell suspension was then used to inoculate the co-culture experiment with an OD600 of 0.2 after 96 hours of the running experiment. After inoculation of Clostridium ljungdahlii cells began to grow and continuously produced acetate. Concomitant to the production of acetate, ethanol was produced in a lower rate compared to the production of acetate. After 96 hours Clostridium kluyveri was then inoculated into the reactor. The simultaneous production of butyrate and hexanoate was then measured in the following 113 hours of the experiment. Parallel to the production of butyrate by Clostridium kluyveri, Clostridium ljungdahlii converted butyrate to butanol to a maximum concentration of 20 mg/l butanol at the end of the experiment. For the extraction with 70 wt/wt.% polypropylene and 30 wt/wt.% hexadecane, 50 g of a butanol solution with 0.522 g/kg was shaken at ambient temperature (20-25 degrees C) in a separation funnel with 46 g of the extracting medium for two minutes. The two phases were separated and butanol concentration in the aqueous phase was determined. After extraction, the butanol concentration in the aqueous phase had decreased to 0.251 g/kg. The distribution coefficient calculated by mass balance was 1.17. For the extraction with oleyl alcohol 4.81 g of a butanol solution with 5.97 g/kg was shaken at ambient temperature (20-25 degrees C) in a centrifuge tube with 2.02 g extractant for 30 minutes. The two phases were separated and the concentration in the aqueous phase was determined. After extraction, the butanol concentration in the aqueous phase had decreased to 2.40 g/kg. The distribution coefficient calculated by mass balance was 2.97.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)

PN CN107570202-A
TI Molecular sieve type catalyst for purifying            formaldehyde, comprises ZSM-5 type molecular sieve            carrier, noble metal active component, and alkali metal            solution.
AU JI H
   RUI Z
   DING J
   LI H
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 201805516Y
AB    NOVELTY - A molecular sieve type catalyst comprises a                ZSM-5 type molecular sieve carrier, 0.01-2 %mass                noble metal active component, and 0.1-10 %mass                alkali metal solution.
   USE - Molecular sieve type catalyst is used for                purifying formaldehyde.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst can be prepared by a simple                method, uses less amount of noble metals, enables                complete conversion of formaldehyde into carbon                dioxide and water, and can be operated stably at                room temperature for a long period of time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of catalyst, which involves stirring                and dispersing ZSM-5 type molecular sieve carrier                in 0.01-1 mol/L alkali metal solution, adding noble                metal salt solution, stirring and evaporating at                40-90 degrees C, drying at 90-120 degrees C, and                calcining in hydrogen atmosphere at 200-600 degrees                C.

TI Hydrogen generation system comprises multi-port            wave reformer in which shock and expansion waves are            created to cause multi-stage shock compression where            reacting gases are heated and compressed to thermally            crack or decompose one or more fuel sources.
AU DAVIDSON M
   TUECHLER S
   COPELAND C D
   AKBARI P
   COPELAND C
AE NEW WAVE HYDROGEN INC (NEWW-Non-standard)
   NEW WAVE HYDROGEN INC (NEWW-Non-standard)
GA 2022B16648
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrogen generation system comprises a                multi-port wave reformer in which shock and                expansion waves are created in a manner causing                multi-stage shock compression where reacting gases                remain for a longer time within the multi-port wave                reformer and are heated and compressed to thermally                crack or decompose one or more fuel sources, such                as hydrocarbon fuels, to generate a fuel product                containing hydrogen.
   USE - Hydrogen generation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is capable of generating clean                hydrogen without emitting carbon dioxide with lower                energy consumption than existing hydrocarbon                reforming methods. The wave reformer functions as                an efficient energy exchanger where the                high-pressure driver gas leverages the pressure of                the driven gas (e.g. methane fuel), resulting in a                rapid heating the driven gas to temperatures                sufficient to crack fuel into hydrogen and black                carbon as a solid product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                six-port wave reformer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a wave diagram showing the                inner working principles and the unsteady flow                process within a wave reformer.

   ZHANG M
   DONG D
   XU K
   HUA T
   DU Y
   LI G
   JO J
   XU H
AE HEBEI LIXIN NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 202268199Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing super-dispersed functional calcium                carbonate comprises (i) taking calcium oxide and                water for digestion reaction, subjecting the                obtained slurry to magnetic separation, enriching                by a magnetic separator, and collecting the                outflowing calcium hydroxide slurry; (ii) aging the                calcium hydroxide slurry, sieving and diluting with                water to obtain a calcium hydroxide suspension;                (iii) adding the calcium hydroxide suspension into                the reactor, adding 2-amino-2-methyl propanol,                introducing carbon dioxide gas for carrying out                carbonization reaction and stopping carbonization                reaction when the reaction system pH value is                reduced to 6.5-7 to obtain calcium carbonate                slurry; and (iv) dehydrating, drying, pulverizing                and sieving the calcium carbonate slurry.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                super-dispersed functional calcium carbonate is                useful as filler for rubber and paper with                semi-reinforcing effect.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares super-dispersed functional                calcium carbonate with large specific surface area,                good dispersibility, and low oil absorption value                and resistance to agglomerate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method of magnetic                  separation enrichment comprises turning on the                  excitation current, adjusting the magnetic                  separation intensity of the magnetic separator to                  140-160 mT, starting to inject slurry for                  magnetic separation enrichment, subjecting the                  slurry to magnetic separation and enrichment,                  collecting effluent again into the magnetic                  separator for repeated magnetic separation and                  collecting the effluent that has been repeatedly                  magnetically separated for more than 2 times to                  obtain calcium hydroxide slurry. The speed of                  injecting the slurry is 30-40 ml/s.Preferred Components: The concentration of                  calcium hydroxide in the calcium hydroxide                  suspension is 12-15 wt.%. The weight volume ratio                  of calcium oxide to water is 1:7-8, the digestion                  reaction is carried out at 80-85degrees Celsius,                  aging is carried out for 9-10 hours and the sieve                  aperture diameter is 180-200 meshes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The addition amount of                  2-amino-2-methylpropanol is 0.7-1.2 wt.%. The                  volume content of carbon dioxide gas in the                  reactor is 25-30%.Preferred Method: The carbonization reaction                  is carried out at 35-40degrees Celsius. The                  turbine stirring is carried out during the                  carbonization reaction at the rotating speed of                  480-500 revolutions/minute.

The growth factor is controlled by controlling the internal and external ventilation of the plant factory type seedling system with an environment setting screen for setting continuous, daytime, nighttime or emergency situations. The method involves batch setting for collectively controlling the growth factors with the control data, and controlling the alarm setting indicating an error in the control range or other setting screen operated by at least any one of data logging for inputting and outputting result values to internal and external devices.

PN CN114011387-A
TI Preparing composite sludge dehydrating agent, comprises mixing diatomite and sepiolite, grinding, sieving, heating, ultrasonically processing, adding composite powder, acrylamide monomer, urea, deionized water into reaction kettle, reacting and adjusting pH.

AE HUBEI KANGCHUANG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 202228831K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing composite sludge dehydrating agent, comprises (i) taking diatomite, sepiolite mixing uniformly, adding into the ball mill for grinding, sieving to 80 m sieve residue less than 10 %, heating to 500-600 degrees Celsius for 1-2 hours under carbon dioxide atmosphere, cooling to room temperature, soaking into 1 mol/l nitric acid solution, ultrasonically processing for 20-30 minutes, filtering, water washing, heating to 250-300 degrees Celsius, drying, grinding to obtain the composite powder; (ii) adding the composite powder, acrylamide monomer, urea, deionized water into the reaction kettle, stirring uniformly, introducing nitrogen, adding initiator, reacting for 1-2 hours, adding polyethylene glycol, reacting for 1-2 hours, adding ethylene diamine tetraacetic acid to adjust pH to 7-8 after the reaction is finished.
   USE - The dehydrating agent is useful in sludge de-watering treatment (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The dehydrating agent has synergistic effect and good stability; ensures adsorption dehydration effect is stronger; and can be stored for more than 18 months without layering.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for composite sludge dehydrating agent prepared as mentioned above.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of the diatomite and sepiolite is 1: 1-5. In the step (i), the grain diameter of the composite powder is 1-10 microns. In the step (ii), the mass ratio of the composite powder, acrylamide monomer, urea, polyethylene glycol and deionized water is 1-2:10:0.5-2:2-5:20-40. In the step (ii), the acrylamide monomer is acrylamide, methacrylamide, or N-ethylacrylamide. The average molecular weight of the polyethylene glycol is 1800-4400. The initiator is a mixture of ammonium persulfate and sodium thiosulfate, and the mass ratio of ammonium persulfate and sodium thiosulfate is 1:1-3. The amount of the initiator is 0.5-0.8% of the mass of the acrylamide.

PN CN113759041-A
TI Separation and detection of enantiomeric residues of clenbuterol in pig urine by ultra-high performance convergence chromatography, by extracting pig urine sample with ethyl acetate, and purifying by cation-exchange solid-phase extraction column, and adopts gradient elution.
AU YI X
   CUI F
   CHEN T
   SHEN W
   LI Y
   HAN C
   HOU J
   WANG L
   XIE W
   XU D
   ZHANG W
AE ZHEJIANG ACAD SCI & TECHNOLOGY INSPECTIO (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202202513L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for separation and detection of enantiomeric residues of clenbuterol in pig urine by ultra-high performance convergence chromatography, where the enantiomers of clenbuterol are (+)-clenbuterol and (-)-clenoid Terro enantiomer, involves extracting the pig urine sample with ethyl acetate, and purifying by a cation-exchange solid-phase extraction column, and adopts CHIRALPAK IA-3 (RTM: polysaccharide-based column) separation, and uses supercritical carbon dioxide 0.5 vol.%, ammonium acetate methanol solution as mobile phase, and adopts gradient elution, and has the flow rate of 2 ml/minute, and system back pressure of 13.8 MPa, and injection volume of 10 mul, and column temperature of 40degrees Celsius, and detection wavelength of 241 nm.
   USE - The method is useful for separation and detection of enantiomeric residues of clenbuterol in pig urine by ultra-high performance convergence chromatography.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has excellent separation effect, fast analysis speed, and less amount of organic solvent, and develops new drugs with less side effects.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The extraction method involves accurately pipetting 10 ml pig urine into a 50 ml plastic centrifuge tube, adding 20 ml 0.02 mol/l ammonium acetate solution, vortexing to mix, adding 2.5 ml 50% trichloroacetic acid solution, evenly vortexing, centrifuging at 8500 rpm for 5 minutes, filtering, adjusting pH of the filtrate to 10plus minus0.05 with 20-2 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution, adding 20 ml ethyl acetate, evenly vortexing, centrifuging at 4700 rpm for 3 minutes, taking the supernatant into a 100 ml concentration bottle, repeating the extraction once, combining an organic phases, concentrating to dryness, dissolving with 3 ml 2% formic acid aqueous solution, and purifying. The method involves taking the solution to be purified in the activated PCX cationic solid phase extraction column, loading, rinsing with 3 ml 2% formic acid aqueous solution and 3 ml methanol, discarding the eluent, draining, eluting with 6 ml 5 vol.% ammonia methanol solution, drying the elution with nitrogen in a water bath at 4degrees Celsius, cooling the test tube, accurately adding 0.2 ml n-heptane to reconstitute, evenly vortexing, passing through a 0.22 mum organic filter membrane, adding to a sample injection vial with a lined tube for testing. The preparation method of this method racemate standard solution involves accurately weighing 1 g/l clenbuterol stock solution: 0.01 g clenbuterol racemate standard, accurate to 0.1 mg, dissolving in methanol, and diluting to 10 ml, making up to 1 g/l standard stock solution, where the clenbuterol racemate standard has a purity of greater than or equal to98%. The preparation method of the enantiomeric standard solution of this method, involves accurately weighing 0.01 g (+)-clenbuterol and (-)-clenbuterol standard to the nearest 0.01 g respectively 1 mg, dissolving in methanol, diluting to 10 ml to make a standard stock solution of 1 g/l, accurately drawing standard solution, and diluting with n-heptane to a mixed working solution of 50, 100, 200, 1000, 5000, 10000 mug/l.Preferred Components: The mobile phase comprises carbon dioxide, and 0.5 vol.% ammonium acetate methanol solution gradient elution procedure having 0-2 minutes (7%B), 2-2.1 minutes (7-20%B), 2.1-6 minutes (20%B), 6-6.1 minutes (20-7%B), 6.1-8 minutes (7% B).Preferred Conditions: The method has a large linear correlation coefficient between the two enantiomers of clenbuterol of 50-10000 mug/l at 0.9998, and the method limit of quantification (S/N=10) is 1 mug/l, and the additive recovery of clenbuterol enantiomer in pig urine of 1-20 mug/l by this method are 76.4-94.5%, and the relative standard deviation is 3.6-6.6%. In this method, n-heptane is used as the constant volume reagent, and the two enantiomers of clenbuterol are effectively resolved within 6 minutes.

The feed adjusts the sow's basic diet formula in the late pregnancy and lactation period to realize the nutritional regulation of the sows in different periods.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a feed for pregnant sows, which comprises the above-mentioned feed additive and a basic diet.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The feed additive is added in an amount of 2-3 g per kilogram of the basic diet. The basic diet is a corn-soybean meal type diet. The corn-soybean meal type diet comprises 61 pts. wt. corn, 10-13 pts. wt. bran, 14-17 pts. wt. soybean meal, 0.8-1.3 pts. wt. fish meal, 6-6.3 pts. wt. puffed soybeans, 0.6-0.8 pt. wt. stone powder, 1.5 pts. wt. calcium hydrogen phosphate, 0.5 pt. wt. salt, 0.8 pt. wt. soybean oil, 0.3 pt. wt. lysine, and 1 pt. wt. premix. The premix comprises 1 mg vitamin B1, 2.3 mg vitamin B2, 1.5 mg vitamin B6, 0.01 mg vitamin B12, 1160 IU vitamin A, 178 IU vitamin D, 10 IU vitamin E, 32 mg vitamin K, 12 mg niacin, 10 mg pantothenic acid, 0.5 mg folic acid, 0.09 mg biotin, 300 mg choline chloride, 4 mg copper, 53 mg zinc, 53 mg iron, 0.12 mg iodine, 2 mg manganese, and 0.13 mg selenium. The corn-soybean meal type diet has a digestible energy content of 13.60 MJ/kg or 14.31 MJ/kg, a crude protein content of 14.5% or 16.8%, a lysine content of 0.7% or 0.85%, a crude fiber content of 4.5% or 4.8%, a calcium content of 0.75% or 0.85%, and a phosphorus content of 0.55%.
   ACTIVITY - Immunostimulant.

   USE - The method is useful for the preparing the iron-doped nickel sulfide oxygen evolution electro-catalyst.

The preparation method is simple, not only can reduce the over potential of oxygen evolution reaction, but also is expected to replace traditional iridium oxide and ruthenium oxide based catalysts, and can reduce the cost of oxygen evolution reactions i.e. hydrogen production by electrolysis of water, reduction of carbon dioxide, and electrochemical fixation of nitrogen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for iron-doped nickel sulfide oxygen evolution electro-catalyst.

TI Preparing aluminum hydroxide using aluminum ash to            remove nitrogen and fix fluorine clinker comprises e.g.            leaching clinker-A with water, performing solid-liquid            separation, carrying out carbonation decomposition            reaction and crystallizing.
AU CHEN X
AE UNIV ZHENGZHOU (UYZZ-C)
GA 202162365L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing aluminum hydroxide using                aluminum ash to remove nitrogen and fix fluorine                clinker comprises (a) using aluminum ash as the raw                material to roast to make a crude zeolite product,                namely clinker-A, (b) leaching the clinker-A with                water, performing solid-liquid separation to obtain                filter cake-B and filtrate-C, and drying the                obtained filter cake-B to obtain a zeolite product,                (c) passing carbon dioxide gas into the filtrate-C                to carry out carbonation decomposition reaction,                and subjecting the product to solid-liquid                separation to obtain white precipitate-D and                filtrate-E, and (d) drying the white precipitate-D                to obtain an aluminum hydroxide product,                concentrating and crystallizing the obtained                filtrate-E to obtain sodium carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing aluminum                hydroxide using aluminum ash to remove nitrogen and                fix fluorine clinker.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not discharge wastewater and                waste residue outside, which is environmentally                friendly; and does not produce red mud, which meets                the production requirements of industrialized                factories.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (a), the                  aluminum ash is used as a raw material to roast a                  crude zeolite product. Preferred Method: In the                  step (b), the liquid-solid ratio is controlled to                  be 2-8:1, preferably 4-8:1 and the leaching time                  is 20-90 minutes, preferably 45-90 minutes when                  the clinker-A is leached with water. In the step                  (c), the time of the carbonation decomposition                  reaction is 5-45 minutes, preferably 15-35                  minutes when the carbon dioxide gas is                  introduced, the flow rate of the carbon dioxide                  gas is controlled to be 0.2-5 l/minute,                  preferably 1-5 l/minute. In the step (d), the                  sodium carbonate is returned to the roasting                  process for recycling as a medicine.

TI Europium-doped porous titanium dioxide supported cerium oxide photocatalytic degradation material comprises e.g. adding titanium sulfate and europium nitrate to deionized water solvent, uniformly stirring, and transferring to reactor.

PN CN112296351-A
TI Preparing superfine silver powder with high tap density comprises e.g. adjusting pH of silver nitrate solution, adding mixed reducing solvent into the silver nitrate solution, and reacting.
AU ZHOU W
   SHI W
AE HUNAN NUOERDE MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 202113903K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing superfine silver powder with high tap density and high density comprises (i) adjusting the pH of the silver nitrate solution to 10-12, (ii) dropping the mixed reducing solvent into the silver nitrate solution, the mixed reducing solvent comprises glucose and glycerol, adding ammonia water to adjust the pH to 10-12, reacting for 30-60 minutes at 60-100 degrees C, introducing carbon dioxide into the reaction system, (iii) adding dispersant A, carrying out ultrasonic homogenizing to obtain the primary reduction reaction solution, (iv) dissolving ascorbic acid in sodium hydroxide solution, dropping to the reaction solution of the primary reduction, reacting for 30-60 minutes at 40-60 degrees C, filtering the mixed liquid after reaction, washing with water and organic solvent in turn, drying to obtain the silver powder crude product, (v) grinding grinding ball milling the silver powder crude product and dispersant B obtained in step (iv) to obtain superfine silver powder.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing superfine silver powder with high tap density.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare superfine silver powder with high tap density.
DC M22 (Casting; powder metallurgy - including foundry moulding, moulding machines, patterns, moulds, cores and metal casting (B22).); P53 (Metal casting, powder metallurgy (B22).)
