   USE - Organosilane for preparing a high-density polyethylene resin used as a raw material for polyethylene extrusion foaming (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The organosilane has characteristic of high crystallization temperature, fast crystallization rate, high crystallinity and high melt strength, performs extrusion foaming by using carbon dioxide or nitrogen as a foaming agent, and replaces low density polyethylene with butane or pentane as foaming agent to extrude and foam to prepare foamed polyethylene material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing high-density polyethylene resin, which involves:
   (A) polymerizing ethylene monomer in presence of a catalyst and hydrogen to obtain a polymer; and
   (B) subjecting the polymer to water vapor treatment, and adding organosilane to the polymerization system during the polymerization reaction of the ethylene monomer to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The X is F, Cl or Br. The amount of organosilane is 1x 10-6-20 \ and hydrogen is 1x 10-4-10 based on 100 \ of ethylene monomer. The polymerization is performed at pressure of 0.1-3MPa and temperature of 30-150 degrees C for 0.1-6 hours. The water vapor treatment is performed at temperature of 80-120 degrees C for 5-60 minutes. The long-chain branch density of high-density polyethylene is 0.1-50 long-chain branches per 10000 carbon atoms, preferably, density of high-density polyethylene is 0.915-0.940g/cm3, melting temperature of the high-density polyethylene is higher than 110 degrees C, crystallinity of high-density polyethylene is greater than 30%, and melt strength of the high-density polyethylene is 18-45cN. The catalyst is Ziegler-Natta catalyst and chromium-based catalyst.

PN CN112138664-A
TI Preparing carbon-based electro-catalyst, comprises e.g. crushing plant shell, preparing hydrothermally activated plant shell particles, mixing, ball milling, carbonizing, soaking plant shell-derived carbon material and carbonizing.
AU XU C
   QU J
   PAN G
   HUANG S
   WU K
   LI Q
AE UNIV GUANGZHOU (UNGZ-C)
GA 202104886G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon-based electro-catalyst, comprises (i) crushing the plant shell to obtain plant shell particles, (ii) mixing the plant shell particles, sodium chlorite, surfactant and water, and then carrying out hydrothermal reaction to obtain hydrothermally activated plant shell particles, (iii) mixing the hydrothermally activated plant shell particles, potassium bicarbonate, basic copper carbonate, calcium carbonate and solvent, then ball milling, and then placing in carbon dioxide atmosphere for carbonization, then soaking in acid solution for soaking to obtain plant shell-derived carbon material, and (iv) soaking the plant shell-derived carbon material in metal salt solution for soaking, and then placing in the atmosphere containing heteroatom dopant for carbonization to obtain final product.
   USE - The carbon-based electro-catalyst is useful in electro-catalytic oxidation reactions (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon-based electro-catalyst: has large specific surface area, and different levels of hole structure; and can be matched with different types of electro-catalytic reactions by doping of different heteroatoms.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for carbon-based electro-catalyst prepared by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (a) cleaning, drying, crushing and sieving the plant shell to obtain plant shell particles, (b) mixing the plant shell particles, sodium chlorite, surfactant and water and heating at 150-250 degrees C for hydrothermal reaction, then separating the solid product, water washing, drying, crushing and sieving to obtain hydrothermally activated plant shell particles, (c) mixing the hydrothermally activated plant shell particles, potassium bicarbonate, basic copper carbonate, calcium carbonate and solvent, then ball milling, and then placing in carbon dioxide atmosphere and heating at 700-1000 degrees C at a rate of 10-20 degrees C/minute for carbonization, cooling at room temperature, then soaking in acid solution for soaking, and then separating the solid product, water washing and drying to obtain plant shell-derived carbon material, and (d) soaking the plant shell-derived carbon material in metal salt solution for soaking, then separating the solid product, drying and grinding, then placing in the atmosphere containing heteroatom dopant, and heating at 600-900 degrees C for carbonization, cooling at room temperature, washing with ethanol and water and drying to obtain carbon-based electro-catalyst. In step (b), the hydrothermal reaction time is 8-24 hours. In step (c), the carbonization time is 1-4 hours. In step (d), the carbonization time is 1-2 hours. The electro-catalytic oxidation reaction is electro-catalytic oxidation reaction of methanol, the electro-catalytic oxidation reaction of ethanol, the electro-catalytic oxidation reaction of glycerol, the electro-catalytic oxidation reaction of urea, the electro-catalytic oxygen evolution reaction or the electro-catalytic hydrogen evolution reaction. Preferred Components: In step (i), the plant shell is Litchi shell, longan shell, mangosteen shell, sunflower seed shell and/or peanut shell. In step (ii), the surfactant is polyvinylpyrrolidone, sodium dodecyl benzene sulfonate, hexadecyl trimethyl ammonium bromide, sorbitan monooleate and/or sodium lauroyl sarcosinate. In step (iv), the metal salt is iron(III) nitrate, nickel(II) nitrate, cobalt(II) nitrate, copper(II) nitrate, indium(III) nitrate, bismuth(III) nitrate, manganese(II) chloride and/or ammonium molybdate. In step (iv), the heteroatom dopant is oxalic acid, urea, arginine, thiourea, sulfur powder and/or sodium hypophosphite.

GA 2020A47875
AB    NOVELTY - Process for automatic metal active gas                welding, involves setting welding process                parameters through the program, welding using an                operating machine according to the preset program,                controlling the operation of the operating machine                by an internal servo motor, where the current is                set to 250-400 A, the voltage is set to 18-30 V and                the welding speed is set to 250 mm/minute or more,                and the shielding gas comprises a mixture of carbon                dioxide and argon, and the mixed gas flow rate is                20-25 l/minute. The operating machine comprises a                control unit, a training unit, an operating unit, a                wire feeding unit and a welding gun.
   USE - The process is useful for automatic metal                active gas welding for threshold pipe beams.
   ADVANTAGE - The process adopts fully automatic                manipulator, improves the welding quality, and                ensures uniformity, provides constant welding                parameters for each weld, and has less influence of                the factors, which reduces the requirements for                workers operating skills and stable welding                quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The protective gas                  comprises 90 wt.% carbon dioxide and 10 wt.%                  argon. The chemical composition of the deposited                  metal of the flux-cored welding wire comprises                  0.06 wt.% carbon, 0.45 wt.% manganese, 0.05 wt.%                  silicon, 0.01 wt.% sulfur, 0.01 wt.% phosphorus                  and 0.11 wt.% vanadium. The chemical composition                  of the deposited metal of the welding wire                  comprises 0.06 wt.% carbon, 0.45 wt.% manganese,                  0.05 wt.% silicon, 0.01 wt.% sulfur, 0.01 wt.%                  phosphorus, 0.06 wt.% vanadium and 0.05 wt.%                  vanadium bromide, where the flux-cored welding                  wire has a tensile strength of 645 MPa, a yield                  point of 450 MPa, an elongation rate of 29%, and                  an impact energy of 110 J.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - MECHANICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Conditions: The welding speed is                  greater than 400 mm/minute, a special flux-cored                  welding wire is used, where the diameter of the                  welding wire is 1.6 mm, and the welding wire                  extends from 2.1-2.9 cm. Preferred Device: The                  control unit is a data processing and memory                  device of the operating machine, and the                  memorized training data controls input and output                  signals and various interfaces, when the training                  unit is connected to the controller for training                  operations, the operating machine is manually                  controlled through the training unit to input all                  the trajectories and operating conditions                  required for automatic operation, and the fully                  automatic manipulator can perform all operations                  including welding conditions by operating the                  training unit. The operating unit is a device                  used after the automatic operation mode is                  selected, comprising an emergency stop button, a                  pause button and a servo power button, when                  multiple workstations are used for automatic                  operation of multiple jobs, a starter box can be                  added, and the next start function is reserved or                  cancelled.

PN IN201921010663-A
TI Manufacture of nanoporous organic framework of metal chelated triaminoguanidinium used as heterogeneous catalyst, involves forming intermediate framework by using triaminoguanidinium halide precursor molecule and chelating with metal oxide.
AU PATRA A
   CHANDE A
   HUSSAIN W M
   BHARDWAJ V
AE INDIAN SCI EDUCATION & RES BHOPAL INST (INSC-Non-standard)
GA 2020A1495V
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of nanoporous organic framework of metal chelated triaminoguanidinium involves selecting a precursor molecule of triaminoguanidinium halide, polymerizing the precursor molecule with aromatic di- or trialdehydes in presence of an anhydrous organic solvent to obtain an intermediate framework, and chelating the intermediate framework with a metal oxide.
   USE - Manufacture of nanoporous organic framework of metal chelated triaminoguanidinium (claimed) used as heterogeneous catalyst for heterogeneous fixation of carbon dioxide for cycloaddition reactions for forming cyclic organic carbonates, water treatment and antimicrobial surface coating applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of nanoporous organic framework of metal chelated triaminoguanidinium having excellent thermal stability, catalytic activities, antimicrobial performance against gram-positive and gram-negative bacteria and antiviral activities against HIV-1 and Vesicular stomatitis virus (VSV)-G. Due to the ionic character and the high nitrogen content, the network polymers can effectively bind with the metal in the form of oxide. The framework is efficient heterogeneous catalyst for cycloaddition reactions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the nanoporous organic framework of metal chelated triaminoguanidinium, which is of formula (I).
   X = divalent metal ion chosen from Cu, Zn, Co and Ni;and
   X1- = chloride, bromide or iodide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The halide is chosen from chloride, bromide and iodide. The metal is divalent metal ion chosen from copper, zinc, cobalt and nickel. Preferred Properties: The framework has surface area of 200-800 m2/g, and pore size distribution of 0.7-50 nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aromatic di- or trialdehydes are terephthalaldehyde and 1,3,5- benzentricarboxaldehyde. The anhydrous organic solvent is chosen from commonly used solvents for Schiff base condensation.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The polymerization is performed at 80-150 degrees C.

PN CN111679031-A
TI Method for measuring antimony in crude lead by precipitation separation-carbon reduction cerium sulfate capacity, involves adding Cerium sulfate standard solution to metal antimony, judging content of metal antimony in crude lead sample.
AU QI B
   WANG D
   LU J
   LIU J
   LU C
   JIN L
   WANG Z
   HOU X
   YANG Z
   ZHANG W
   ZHAO K
   DENG Y
   ZHAO Z
AE NORTHWEST RES INST MINING & METALLURGY (NWRE-Non-standard)
GA 202093585R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves preparing standard solution of cerium sulfate with mole concentration of 0.025 moles per liter. Metal antimony is calibrated by the cerium sulfate standard solution. The crude lead sample to be detected is dissolved by using a sieve. The antimony is separated in crude lead. Precipitate is dissolved on a filter paper with concentrated sulfuric acid 20-40 milli liter. Cerium sulfate standard solution is added to the metal antimony when the solution is converted from red to light yellow to obtain a final point. Content of the metal antimony is judged in the crude lead sample.
   USE - Method for measuring antimony in crude lead by precipitation separation-carbon reduction cerium sulfate capacity.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables completely separating antimony precipitatein from interference elements, reacting excess precipitant with sulphuric acid or hydrochloric acid to generate water or carbon dioxide, dissolving antimony precipitate and filter paper in concentrated sulphuric acid for reducing antimony to avoid loss caused by ashing or precipitation re-dissolving process so that accuracy and precision detection is improved.

MC S03-E09D

PN WO2020177839-A1; EP3930662-A1; US2022175657-A1
TI Treating human scalp and hair for increasing blood circulation of scalp, by applying non-cleansing composition comprising particles, massaging, rinsing, applying cleansing composition, applying aqueous aerosol composition and rinsing.
AU KLAWITTER I
   LIPINSKI N
AE KAO GERMANY GMBH (KAOS-C)
   KAO GERMANY GMBH (KAOS-C)
   KAO GERMANY GMBH (KAOS-C)
GA 2020875688
AB    NOVELTY - Method for treating human scalp and hair involves (a) applying first aqueous non-cleansing composition comprising dispersed particles with size of 1-1000 pm measured by light microscope, and massaging it into the scalp and hair and optionally rinsing it off, (b) applying second aqueous cleansing composition onto scalp and hair and massaging into scalp and hair, (c) rinsing the hair off, (d) applying third non-cleansing aqueous aerosol composition onto hair and scalp comprising carbon dioxide propellant, massaging it into scalp and hair, and optionally rinsing it off, (e) optionally applying hair conditioning composition, and (f) optionally rinsing the hair off and drying.
   USE - The method for increasing blood circulation of human skin and/or scalp (claimed), and protecting from inflammation, dandruff and hair-loss.
   ADVANTAGE - The method protects the skin and works against wrinkles, and many other medical and cosmetic problems, increases effectiveness, blood flow, and stably foams and has excellent spreadability and provides non-sticky skin feel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a kit comprising individually packaged compositions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: In step (a), the average particle size of the dispersed particles is 5-500 pm, preferably 50-400 pm measured using light microscopy. In step (a), the total concentration of dispersed is 0.5-15 wt.%, preferably 2-8 wt.% calculated based on the total of the composition. In step (a), the composition further comprises 0.1-5 wt.% thickening polymer, preferably 0.25-2.5 wt.% calculated on to the total of the composition. In step (c), the total concentration of cleansing surfactants is 5-50 wt.%, preferably 8-25 wt.%. In step (d), the composition comprises 0.5-10 wt.% carbon dioxide, preferably 1-5 wt.% calculated to the total of the composition. The pH of the compositions of steps (a), (c) and/or (d) is 3-8. Preferred Components: In step (a), the composition comprises dispersed particles of cellulose particles, jojoba ester particles and/or hydrophobic compounds having melting point of above 25 degrees C, preferably 35 degrees C under atmospheric conditions, and their mixtures. In step (b), the composition comprises cleansing surfactant(s) comprising anionic and/or zwitterionic/amphoteric and/or non-ionic cleansing surfactants. In step (d), the composition comprises silicone compounds having viscosity of 1-1000 mPa.second, preferably 10-300 mPa.second measured with a Brookfield viscometer at 60 rpm with rotor number 1 at 25 degrees C under atmospheric conditions. In step (d), the components comprises 1-15 wt.%, preferably 1.5-10 wt.% polyol calculated based on the total of the composition, thickening polymer comprising associative thickening polymers, and 0.1-2.5 wt.%, preferably 0.15-1.25 wt.% surfactant comprising non-ionic surfactants calculated to the total of the composition.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological; Vasotropic; Antiinflammatory; Endocrine-Gen. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.

PT P
PN CN111481483-A
TI Preparing agarwood pure hydrosols by crushing agarwood, sieving, extracting particles, cutting extract using molecular distillation device, mixing middle segment product and water, and distilling by gradient heating.
AU FENG Z
   GAO J
   ZHU B
   PU S
AE GUANGZHOU TIANRANFANGXIANG SCI & TECHNOLOGY RES INST CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202076860T
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing agarwood pure hydrosols, involves (a) crushing agarwood, and passing through a sieve to obtain agarwood particles, (b) extracting the particles using supercritical carbon dioxide method or water distillation method to obtain an extract, (c) cutting the extract using a molecular distillation device under a cutting vacuum of 0.01-1000 Pa to obtain a first, middle and tail segment products, discarding the first and tail segment products, and retaining the middle segment product, where the first product refers to the product within 10-30 minutes of precise cutting, the tail segment product refers to the product within 90-120 minutes of precise cutting to the end, and the middle segment product refers to the product between the first segment and tail segment, and (d) mixing the middle segment product and water in a mass ratio of 1:(1000-4000), and continuously distilling by gradient heating to obtain a saturated solution, which is the hydrosols.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing agarwood pure hydrosols (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared hydrosols have less impurity content, excellent permeability, and pure fragrance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves selecting the agarwood tree having excellent fragrance-bearing portion of the agarwood tree aged 2 years or more, and passing through a 20-60 mesh sieve. The step (b) involves extracting by adopting the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process 50-65 degrees C under an extraction pressure of 18-40 MPa and a flow rate of 50-300 l/minute for 90-120 minutes or extracting by adopting the water distillation extraction process which is carried out by mixing agarwood particles with water in a mass ratio of 1:(5-20), heating at 90-100 degrees C under a pressure of 90-110 kPa, and distilling for 240-1440 minutes to obtain the extract, where the density of the agarwood extract is 0.8-1.2 g/cm3. The molecular cutting process involves scraping using a first at a speed of 100-300 rpm, cutting at 75-100 degrees C, collecting at 10-50 degrees C, scraping using a second column at a speed of 100-300 rpm, cutting at 100-170 degrees C, and collecting at 10-50 degrees C, where the molecular distillation device is an MDS-80 wiped film molecular distillation device. The gradient heating is a three-stage heating which is carried out by increasing the temperature to 70-80 degrees C at a rate of 5-15 degrees C/minute during first stage of heating, increasing the temperature to 90-95 degrees C at a rate of 1-5 degrees C/minute during second stage of heating, and increasing the temperature to 97-100 degrees C at a rate of 0.5-1.5 degrees C/minute during third stage of heating.

   ADVANTAGE - The core-shell structured silicon dioxide paraffin nanoparticle is eco-friendly and cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CALIM is included for a method for preparing core-shell structured silicon dioxide paraffin nanoparticle, which involves:
   (A) mixing paraffin, nanometer silicon dioxide and N, N dimethylcyclohexylamine;
   (B) stirring to dissolve paraffin completely;
   (C) adding emulsifier A and stirring until it is completely dissolved;
   (D) obtaining the oil phase;
   (E) preparing the water phase by adding emulsifying agent B into the de-ionized water;
   (F) stirring to completely dissolvingthe emulsifier B to obtain water phase;
   (G) preparingO/W emulsion;
   (H) stirring and emulsifying, obtainingO/W emulsion preparation;
   (I) introducing carbon dioxideinto the O/W emulsion, paraffin precipitation and silicon dioxide paraffin nanometre particles;
   (J) introducing nitrogen into the a protic solvent to obtain N, N-dimethylcyclohexylamineand emulsifier aqueous solution; and
   (K) recycling and reusing the mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The particle size of the silicon dioxide paraffin nanoparticle is 60-120nm. The mass ratio of the paraffin and nano silicon dioxide is 6:4. The electrical conductivity of the O/W emulsion obtained is 0.075-0.1 mS/cm. The electrical conductivity of the system after carbon dioxide is introduced is 7.0-8.0mS/cm, the conductivity of the system after passing nitrogen inis 0.12-0.18mS/cm. The mass ratio of the emulsifier A andemulsifying agent B is 1:9 to 2:8. The mass ratio of the water phase and the oil phase is 2:1.    R1 = 1-18C alkane, aryl, cycloalkyl, alkenyl                or alkynyl;
   R2-R4 = chloro, bromo, alkoxy, ester group, H,                alkyl, aromatic group or cycloalkyl group                (hydrolyzable); and

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  (I) comprises reacting halogenated imide or imide                  salt or imide compound with mercaptoalkylsilane                  compound or corresponding sulfenyl chloride                  compound of mercaptoalkylsilane. Preferred                  Method: The method comprises dissolving                  halogenated imide or imide salt or imide compound                  in organic solvent under an inert gas atmosphere                  to obtain 0.4-0.8 mol/l reaction solution, adding                  mercaptoalkylsilane compound or                  mercaptoalkylsilane corresponding sulfenyl                  chloride compound dropwise into the reaction                  solution, reacting at room temperature with a                  speed of 500-800 revolutions/minute for 3-12                  hours. The method comprises removing the solvent                  of the reaction system after reaction completes,                  and then performing column chromatography using                  400 mesh silica gel powder, where the eluent                  system is petroleum ether and ethyl acetate, and                  gradient washout polarity selection range is                  20:1-6:1, and preparing imide-terminated                  mercaptosilane coupling agent. Preferred                  Components: The molar ratio of the halogenated                  imide or imide salt or imide compound to                  corresponding sulfenyl chloride compound of                  mercaptoalkylsilane compound or                  mercaptoalkylsilane is 1:1-1.2.
   SPECIFIC COMPOUNDS - 6 Compounds (I) are specifically claimed,                  e.g.                  1-(3-(triethoxysilyl)propylthio)pyrrolidine-2,5-dione                  (Ia),                  N-acetyl-N-(3-(triethoxysilyl)propylthio)acetamide,                  2-(3-(triethoxysilyl)propylthio)hexahydro-1H-isoindole-1,3(2H)-dione,                  2-(3-(triethoxysilyl)propylthio)isoindoline-1,3-dione                  and                  4,7-dimethyl-2-(3-(triethoxysilyl)propylthio)isoindoline-1,3-dione.

TI Preparing ultra-thin graphene powder involves using graphite rod as working electrode, adding DC voltage to the electrolyte, electrolyzing for period of time, and then going through solid-liquid separation, ultrasonic dispersion, washing.

GA 202033907D

The ratio of the graphene precursor to the solvent is 10-60 g / L. The ultrasonic temperature is 30-50 degrees C, the ultrasonic power is 400-800W for 2-5 hours. The temperature is 25-50 degrees C, the pressure is 5-30 MPa, and the supercritical treatment time is 2-6 hours. The pressure reduction rate is 5-20 MPa / s. PN CN111007107-A
TI Carbon based flexible humidity device dew point measuring method, involves adsorbing water vapor on surface of humidity sensor to accurately identify and obtain dew point temperature through changes in capacitance.
AU NIE J
AE UNIV BEIHANG (UNBA-C)
GA 2020322356
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves sticking a polyimide tape on a silicon substrate. An interdigitated carbon electrode is burned on the polyimide tape by a carbon dioxide laser and liquid polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS). A flexible PDMS is embedded with interdigitated electrodes after the liquid PDMS is solidified and separated from the polyimide tape. Graphene oxide solution is sprayed on the flexible PDMS with interdigitated electrodes by electrospinning. A carbon electrode is sprayed with graphene oxide solution and flexible PDMS to form an all-carbon-based flexible humidity sensor. A dew point sensing device is assembled with an active temperature control function. Measured gas is passed through a gas chamber under cooling of a semiconductor refrigerator. A water vapor is adsorbed on a surface of the humidity sensor to accurately identify and obtain dew point temperature through changes in capacitance.
   USE - Carbon based flexible humidity device dew point measuring method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables ensuring humidity sensitive device anti-corrosion property.

PN CN110760367-A
TI Refining Antarctic krill oil comprises e.g. thawing frozen Antarctic krill, heating, crushing to obtain Antarctic krill mud, adding anhydrous ethanol, extracting, and adding n-hexane to obtain Antarctic krill oil/n-hexane mixed solution.
AU QU Y
   OUYANG X
   FAN L
   YANG L
   YU D
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG OCEAN (UYZO-C)
GA 202013756K
AB    NOVELTY - Refining Antarctic krill oil comprises (i) thawing frozen Antarctic krill at low temperature, heating at 50-60 degrees C, and crushing to obtain Antarctic krill mud, (ii) adding anhydrous ethanol, extracting in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, and adding n-hexane in the extract to obtain Antarctic krill oil/n-hexane mixed solution, (iii) adding sodium silicate solution to Antarctic krill oil/n-hexane mixed solution, stirring at constant temperature, centrifugally removing precipitate, and collecting first supernatant, (iv) adding potassium bromide solution to first supernatant, stirring at constant temperature, centrifugally removing precipitate, and collecting second supernatant, (v) adjusting the ratio of Antarctic krill oil and n-hexane in second supernatant, allowing to stand to precipitate at low temperature, and collecting third supernatant , and (vi) adding enrichment extractant to third supernatant to perform phospholipid enrichment to obtain Antarctic krill oil.
   USE - The method is useful for refining Antarctic krill oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally friendly, protects active ingredients e.g. phospholipids, and uses easily removable gel extractant, thus avoids solvent residues.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (iii), the concentration of sodium silicate solution is 400-500 g/l. In the step (iv), the concentration of potassium bromide solution is 80-120 g/l. Preferred Process: In the step (v), the precipitation is carried out at 5-10 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the adding amount of anhydrous ethanol to Antarctic krill mud is 10-25 ml/g. In the step (iii), the volume ratio of sodium silicate solution and Antarctic krill oil/n-hexane mixed solution is 0.5-0.8:1. In the step (v), the volume ratio of Antarctic krill oil and n-hexane is 1:2-5. In the step (vi), the enrichment extractant comprises phase A and phase B, where the phase A is ethanol, and the phase B is temperature responsive hydrogel.

PT P
PN CN110606496-A; CN110606496-B
TI Continuously producing monocyanamide involves filtering monocyanamide feed liquid made of e.g. mother liquor and stabilizer, and sequentially performing precise filtration treatment and acidification on outgoing monocyanamide.
AU ZHANG C
   LIANG W
   LIU G
   CUI W
   ZHANG N
   CAO B
AE SHANDONG EFIRM BIOCHEMISTRY & ENVIRONMEN (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202000935N
AB    NOVELTY - Continuously producing monocyanamide involves: (a) continuously injecting a mother liquor and a stabilizer into a synthesis kettle, then passing carbon dioxide from the bottom of the kettle under stirring, simultaneously adding lime nitrogen to react to obtain a monocyanamide feed liquid, where the reaction temperature is less than or equal to 30 degrees C, and the pH is less than or equal to 8.5; (b) continuously filtering the feed liquid through a filter to obtain a filtrate and a filter residue, respectively, and recycling the filtrate to the kettle for continuous reaction; (c) when the content of monocyanamide in the filtrate reaches a predetermined value, controlling the mass ratio of the filtrate circulation amount to the outflow amount at (2-5):1, and simultaneously adjusting the amount of continuous injection of mother liquor and stabilizer into the kettle to maintain the liquid volume in the kettle unchanged; and (d) sequentially performing precise filtration treatment and acidification on the outgoing monocyanamide.
   USE - The method is useful for continuously producing monocyanamide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can realize continuous production and reduce the product loss and explosion risk caused by the polymerization of monocyanamide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The stabilizer in step (a) is cuprous chloride, ferric chloride, sodium sulfate, sodium sulfide and/or ammonium thiocyanate. The amount of carbon dioxide introduced in step (a) is 60-225 Nm3/hour. The added amount of lime nitrogen is 200-250 kg/hour.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves: continuously injecting the mother liquor into the synthesis kettle from a mother liquor tank at a feed rate of 15-25 time/hour, stopping injection after the liquid volume in the kettle is maintained at 2-6 m3; and before the mother liquor is introduced into the synthesis tank, continuously injecting the stabilizer into the mother liquor, where the amount of the stabilizer is 0.005-0.015% of the mass flow rate of the mother liquor. The step (b) involves after the reaction is performed for 1-3 hours, turning on a transfer pump, controlling the flow of the transfer pump at 1-2 m3/hour, continuously diverting the monocyanamide feed liquid in the synthesis kettle to the filter and filtering to obtain the filtrate and the filter residue, respectively. The precise filtering treatment in step (d) is performed with an activated carbon-type precision filter. The filter element of the activated carbon-type precision filter is an activated carbon filter element, and the material of the cylinder component is stainless steel. The step (b) further involves washing the filter residue with water, transferring the washing water obtained after washing the filter residue as a mother liquid to the synthesis kettle to keep the liquid amount in the synthesis kettle unchanged, and simultaneously injecting the stabilizer with the mass flow of 0.005-0.025% of the mass flow of the mother liquor. The mass ratio of the water washing water to the filtrate is (0.8-1.2):1. Preferred Conditions: The stirring speed in step (a) is 100-300 rpm. The pH value after the acidification in step (d) is 3-6.
DC E35
MC E32-B
IP C01C-003/16

PT P
PN US2019328816-A1
TI Chinese medicinal composition used for expelling parasitic Toxoplasma gondii, comprises fructus mume, Artemisiae annuae herba, Taraxaci herba, Polygonati rhizoma, poria, Angelicae dahuricae radix, Dioscoreae rhizoma, and Euphorbia humifusa.
AU CHEN H
AE ZHUHAI HANTANG BIOTECHNOLOGY ENG CO LTD (ZHUH-Non-standard)
GA 2019900177
AB    NOVELTY - A Chinese medicinal composition comprises 5-10 pts. wt. fructus mume, 50-60 pts. wt. Artemisiae annuae herba, 5-10 pts. wt. Taraxaci herba, 10-15 pts. wt. Polygonati rhizoma, 5-10 pts. wt. poria, 5-8 pts. wt. Angelicae dahuricae radix, 5-15 pts. wt. Dioscoreae rhizoma, and 5-10 pts. wt. Euphorbia humifusa.
   USE - Chinese medicinal composition used for expelling parasitic Toxoplasma gondii (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The Chinese medicinal composition inhibits reproductive activity of T. gondii, and reduces its activeness thus gradually expelling T. gondii out of the bodies of humans or animals. It is non-toxic and has no side effects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the Chinese medicinal composition, comprising weighing, cleaning, and drying fructus mume, Artemisiae annuae herba, Taraxaci herba, Polygonati rhizoma, poria, Angelicae dahuricae radix, Dioscoreae rhizoma, and E. humifusa; mixing, crushing to granules, adding water to the granules, and boiling the granules to obtain a concentrated solution; performing supercritical extracting of the concentrated solution using carbon dioxide as an extracting agent to obtain an extract; and spray drying the extract to obtain powder of the extract.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The concentrated solution is transferred in a supercritical extraction fluid production apparatus, added with distilled water, and supercritical extracting is carried out at 25 degrees C. Spray drying comprises sterilizing the extract; concentrating the extract under a vacuum condition to remove 75-85% water in the extract; and under centrifugal force, spraying the concentrated extract as atomized particles using an atomizer, and drying the concentrated extract to obtain the powder of the extract. Sterilizing the extract comprises heating the extract at 90-100 degrees C for 10-15 minutes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Component: The Chinese medicinal composition comprises 6-8 pts. wt., preferably 8 pts. wt. fructus mume, 25-30 pts. wt., preferably 28 pts. wt. Artemisiae annuae herba, 5-8 pts. wt., preferably 8 pts. wt. Taraxaci herba, 12-15 pts. wt., preferably 15 pts. wt. Dioscoreae rhizoma, and 8-10 pts. wt., preferably 8 pts. wt. E. humifusa. A ratio of concentration of the concentrated solution is 20:1.

TI Iron-cerium-aluminum oxide catalyst used in carbon monoxide oxidation reaction, comprises gamma-aluminum oxide carrier, and active component iron as ferric oxide and cerium as ceric oxide.
AU DONG L
   AN D
   SUN J
   YU Y
   TANG C
   GAO F
AE UNIV NANJING (UNAJ-C)
GA 201982595R
AB    NOVELTY - An iron-cerium-aluminum oxide catalyst comprises gamma -aluminum oxide carrier, active component iron and cerium, and active component is present in the catalyst in the form of ferric oxide and ceric oxide.
   USE - Iron-cerium-aluminum oxide catalyst used in carbon monoxide oxidation reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has large specific surface area, excellent catalytic performance, economical and easy availability of raw materials, rich resources, low energy consumption, and environmentally-friendly in vehicle exhaust gas carbon monoxide elimination. The catalyst can convert 10-100% of carbon monoxide into carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of iron-cerium-aluminum oxide catalyst, which involves using co-impregnation method, dissolving gamma -aluminum oxide carrier, ferric nitrate nonahydrate, and cerium nitrate hexahydrate in deionized water after drying and roasting, stirring for 2 hours, evaporating in oil bath at 100 degrees C, drying in an oven at 110 degrees C for overnight, and roasting at 500 degrees C for 4 hours in an air atmosphere; and
   (2) use of the catalyst of in a carbon monoxide oxidation reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of gamma -aluminum oxide, ferric oxide and ceric oxide is 1:0.093:0.199. The mass ratio of the gamma -aluminum oxide carrier, ferric nitrate nonahydrate, and cerium nitrate hexahydrate is 1:0.202:0.503. Preferred Condition: The preparation of catalyst before oil bath evaporation involves grinding and calcining obtained product after stirring in an air atmosphere at 500 degrees C for 4 hours, obtaining ceric oxide/ gamma -aluminum oxide, loading ferric nitrate nonahydrate by the dipping method, and stirring for 2 hours. The carbon monoxide oxidation reaction involves purging catalyst at 200 degrees C for 1 hour under a nitrogen atmosphere, reacting catalyst with a gas stream of carbon monoxide, oxygen, and nitrogen in a mass concentration ratio of 50 mg: 1.6 vol.%: 20.8 vol.%: 77.6 vol.%, and the gas stream velocity is 30000 mL.g-1.h-1.

PN CN110066299-A; CN110066299-B
TI Complex is prepared by taking first solution containing palladium chloride and taking second solution containing guar ring compound as raw material.
AU CHEN R
   CAO M
   LI H
   CAO R
AE FUJIAN MATERIAL STRUCTURE INST (CAFU-C)
GA 2019681275
AB    NOVELTY - Complex is represented by linear formula.
   USE - Complex.
   ADVANTAGE - The complex is improves the selectivity and stability of the catalyst, effectively reduces the overpotential of the carbon dioxide reduction reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Complex is represented by linear formula that is L 2(H3O) (PdCl4) xH2O, where L represents a melon ring compound molecule, x represents the number of moles of free water per mole of the complex from 6-8. The ligand is belongs to the monoclinic system, the I2/a space group, and the crystal cell parameters are alpha is equal to gamma is 90 degrees , beta is 95.0-95.1 degrees , and Z is 4. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for following:
   (1) a method for producing a complex, which involves taking first solution containing palladium chloride and taking second solution containing guar ring compound as raw material;
   (2) a nanomaterial, which is obtained after reduction of a complex that is one selected from the group consisting of the complex; and
   (3) a catalyst, which comprises a nanopalladium supported on a guap ring obtained by reduction of a complex that is one selected from the group consisting of the complex.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The melon ring compound molecule is one selected from the group consisting of a five-membered melon ring compound molecule and a six-membered melon ring compound molecule. The complex is further represented by linear formula that is (Me10CB(5)-H2O )asterisk2H3O (P dCl4)asterisk6H2O. Preferred Conditions: The solution diffusion method uses an H-type diffusion tube, and the diffusion time is not less than 3 hours. The first solution includes hydrogen ions of 0.025-0.035 mole per liter. The concentration of palladium chloride in first solution is 0.001-0.017 mole per liter. The concentration of the guar ring compound in second solution is 0.001-0.010 mole per liter. The hydrogenation is performed at 250-350 degrees C for 1-10 hours. The composition of the hydrogen atmosphere is hydrogen having a volume fraction of 5 wt.% and nitrogen having a volume fraction of 95 wt.%. Preferred Process: The method further involves adding deionized water and hydrochloric acid to palladium chloride to form a transparent aqueous solution of palladium chloride, dissolving Me10CB (5) in deionized water under stirring to obtain a second solution. The first solution and second solution are separately transferred to both sides of the reaction apparatus, and dispersed to obtain the complex.

PN CN110027157-A; CN110027157-B
TI Method for continuously manufacturing foamed material, involves transporting material to first reaction zone, removing pressure, and expanding and foaming material in second reaction zone to obtain foamed material.
AU ZHANG L
AE SUZHOU SHENSAI NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 201965333K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves filling foaming agent into a material in a first reaction zone, where foaming agent is nitrogen or Carbon dioxide. The material is conveyed from the first reaction zone to a second reaction zone. Pressure is applied to the material in the second reaction zone. The material is heated to a foaming temperature. The material is transported from the second reaction zone to a third reaction zone. The pressure is removed. The material is expanded and foamed in the third reaction zone to obtain a foamed material. The material in the second reaction zone is pressed in a growth direction of a bubble.
   USE - Method for manufacturing foamed material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables heating the material to foaming temperature under condition that internal bubbles are not grown, foaming the material instantaneously after leaving foaming pretreatment device, determining uniformly controllable cell pore size and expansion ratio, and realizing uniform cell pore diameter and large thickness in solid foaming process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a device for manufacturing foamed material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of a device for manufacturing foamed material.

PN CN109852442-A
TI Preparing composite diesel oil by mixing catalytic diesel oil with coking diesel oil, carrying out hydrogenation desulfurization, uniformly mixing with combustion catalyst at normal temperature and pressure, leaving and canning.
AU PENG H
AE SICHUAN MAFU TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 201953856Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing composite diesel oil involves mixing catalytic diesel oil with coking diesel oil, carrying out hydrogenation desulfurization until the sulfur content is less than 10 ppm, uniformly mixing with the combustion catalyst at normal temperature and pressure, leaving for 8 hours and canning after liquid clarification.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing composite diesel oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method mainly solves the problem of marine diesel oil, engineering machinery oil and non-road machinery diesel oil are upgraded due to environmental protection standards, fully utilizes the capacity of the original catalytic diesel oil, solves the problem of serious emission pollution during non-road mechanical use and the insufficient combustion of traditional diesel oil in an engine and generating a large amount of carbon dioxide, hemocyanin, nitrogen oxide, promethium and solid pollutants, reduces carbon deposit in the engine, achieves self-cleaning effect, ensures environmentally-friendly composite diesel oil, improves the fuel utilization rate, greatly reduces the pollutant emission, meets the national environmental protection requirements, which is found by catalytic diesel oil and exerts greater social and economic benefits.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves mixing 40-90% catalytic diesel oil with 10-60% coking diesel oil. The combustion catalyst raw material is prepared by adding 0.8 kg solid combustion catalyst/1 kg aviation kerosene and uniformly mixing. The method involves adding 0.1-1.2 kg combustion catalyst to each 1000 kg catalytic diesel oil and coking diesel oil which are subjected to hydrogenation and desulfurization.

PT P
PN CN109772408-A
TI Nickel-nitrogen co-doped carbon electrocatalyst is a nickel-nitrogen co-doped porous carbon nanosheet, and is prepared by adding sodium benzoate, nickel acetate and manganese acetate to dimethylformamide.
AU HOU Y
   WANG X
   YANG B
   LI Z
   LEI L
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2019470258
AB    NOVELTY - Nickel-nitrogen co-doped carbon electrocatalyst is a nickel-nitrogen co-doped porous carbon nanosheet.
   USE - Nickel-nitrogen co-doped carbon electrocatalyst used for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (CO2) to carbon momoxide (CO) (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nickel-nitrogen co-doped carbon electrocatalyst has high selectivity and high catalytic activity, and the preparation method is mild and easy to operate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing nickel-nitrogen co-doped carbon electrocatalyst, which involves:
   (A) adding sodium benzoate, nickel acetate and manganese acetate to dimethylformamide (DMF), dissolving and adding terephthalic acid to obtain a mixed solution;
   (B) heating mixed solution to form a metal organic framework compound with nickel ion (Ni2+) and manganese ion (Mn2+) as the central metal ions and terephthalic acid as the organic ligand, and separating;
   (C) calcining metal organic framework compound in an ammonia atmosphere, carbonizing terephthalic acid in the metal organic framework compound, and converting Ni2+ forms a coordination bond with N, and the Mn2+ into manganese oxide; and
   (D) removing manganese oxide by acid washing to obtain a nickel-nitrogen co-doped porous carbon nanosheet.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The nickel-nitrogen co-doped porous carbon nanosheet has a BET specific surface area of 300-1000 m2 g-1. The nickel-nitrogen co-doped porous carbon nanosheet has a BET specific surface area of 900-1000 m2 g-1. The molar ratio of the nickel acetate to the manganese acetate is 1:1.0-1:19. The temperature at which the mixed solution is heated is 120-210 degrees C for 8-14 hours. The calcination process is: a temperature of 600-700 degrees C, a heating rate of 2-5 degrees C per minute, and a baking time of 1-3 hours.

   ADVANTAGE - The flexible metal-organic framework material                znbd-scut has good hydrophobic property under low                humidity conditions, realizes the separation of                carbon dioxide/nitrogen/methane and                olefin/paraffin.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing microporous flexible metal-organic                frameworks (MOFs) material znbd-scut comprises (i)                taking terephthalic acid, 3, 5 '-1, 2, 4-triazole                and zinc metal salt, adding water and                dimethylformamide and mixing solvent, (ii) adding                the mixed solvent to reaction kettle for reaction,                washing with water and dimethylformamide, filtering                and to obtain the crude target product ZnBD-SCUT,                and (iii) exchanging the solvent with the low                boiling point solvent to obtain crude target                product , vacuum heating to remove the residual                solvent to obtain ultra-microporous flexible MOFs                material ZnBD-SCUT and purifying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  zinc metal salt is zinc nitrate, zinc chloride or                  zinc acetate. In the step (iii), the low boiling point solvent                  is dichloromethane, methanol, ethanol and/or                  acetonitrile. Preferred Method: In the step (ii),                  the reaction temperature is 130-200 degrees C.In                  the step (ii) the reaction time is 24-96 hours .                  In the step (iii), the activating temperature is                  60-300 degrees C.

TI Converting feed stream containing olefins by            introducing feed stream containing methane and oxygen            to reaction zone, reacting to form reaction zone            product stream, transporting the mixture another            reaction zone and introducing fresh stream.
AU FISH B B
   GROENENDIJK P E
   NIESKENS D L S
   MALEK A
   STEARS B A
   MAREK A
   NIESKENS D L
AE DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
GA 2019363808
AB    NOVELTY - A feed stream containing 2-5 carbon-containing                (2-5C) olefins is converted by introducing a feed                stream containing methane and oxygen to a 1st                reaction zone; reacting the methane and oxygen in                1st reaction zone to form a 1st reaction zone                product stream containing a mixture of 2-5C                alkanes; transporting the mixture of 2-5C alkanes                to a 2nd reaction zone; introducing a fresh stream                of greater than or equal to 1 of ethane and propane                to 2nd reaction zone; converting the mixture of                2-5C alkanes to 2-5C olefins in 2nd reaction zone;                producing greater than or equal to 1 product                streams in 2nd reaction zone, where sum of greater                than or equal to 1 product streams comprises 2-5C                olefins; and producing a recycle stream containing                hydrogen in 2nd reaction zone, where recycle stream                is transported to the 1st reaction zone.
   USE - Conversion of feed stream containing                olefins.
   ADVANTAGE - The method converts feed carbon to desired                products while minimizing the conversion of the                feed carbon to carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - MECHANICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Devices: The second reaction zone                  comprises a cracker. The second reaction zone                  comprises a propane dehydrogenation                  reactor.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Introducing a fresh                  stream of greater than or equal to 1 of ethane                  and propane to 2nd reaction zone includes                  introducing a fresh stream of ethane into the                  cracker. Introducing a fresh stream of greater                  than or equal to 1 of ethane and propane to 2nd                  reaction zone comprises introducing propane into                  propane dehydrogenation reactor. Introducing a                  fresh stream of greater than or equal to 1 of                  ethane and propane to 2nd reaction zone comprises                  introducing propane into propane dehydrogenation                  reactor and introducing ethane to cracker.                  Preferred Components: The recycle stream further                  comprises methane. The first reaction zone                  product stream does not comprise carbon dioxide.                  The first reaction zone comprises a hybrid                  catalyst. The hybrid catalyst comprises a                  methanol synthesis component and a solid                  microporous acid component having 8-MR access.                  Ratio of methane to alkane is less than or equal                  to 1.17, preferably less than or equal to 1.14.                  Ratio of oxygen gas to alkane is less than or                  equal to 1.1. Preferred Parameters: Carbon                  efficiency is greater than or equal to 0.93,                  preferably greater than or equal to 0.96.

PN CN109516783-A
TI Preparing environmentally-friendly ceramic by extracting aluminum hydroxide from alumina comprises e.g. placing alumina waste in crushing device, crushing and grinding crushed alumina through grinding device to obtain alumina powder.
AU XIE W
   ZHANG J
   ZHENG X
   SUN F
AE FUJIAN WANQI SCI & TECHNOLOGY CERAMICS C (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 2019294851
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing environmentally-friendly ceramic by extracting aluminum hydroxide from alumina comprises (i) placing alumina waste in crushing device, crushing and grinding crushed alumina through grinding device to obtain alumina powder, (ii) carrying out water washing on alumina powder, drying and screening to obtain tri-alumina powder, (iii) placing alumina powder into sodium hydroxide solution, heating and stirring liquid to obtain sodium metaaluminate solution, (iv) introducing excess carbon dioxide into sodium metaaluminate solution, and allowing to stand to obtain aluminum hydroxide precipitate, (v) removing precipitate, cleaning and grinding by grinding device to obtain aluminum hydroxide powder, (vi) mixing kaolin and aluminum hydroxide powder, using kaolin as raw material to obtain clay, placing ceramic clay into rotary table, forming ceramic mud into blank body, and airing blank body and (vii) glazing of air-dried blank body, placing into kiln, and calcining at high-temperature.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing environmentally-friendly ceramic by extracting aluminum hydroxide from alumina.
   ADVANTAGE - The ceramic improves the whiteness, increases the beauty of the ceramic, improves the quality and is beneficial for people to use.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii) the water washing of tri-alumina powder at 25-120 degrees C for 1-8 hours, the drying at 25-120 degrees C for 1-5 hours and the particle size of tri-alumina powder is 20-100 meshes. In the step (iii) the temperature of liquid heating at 40-80 degrees C for 10-30 minutes; the mass ratio of tri-aluminum to sodium hydroxide solution is 51:40. In the step (iv) the continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas into liquid until no precipitate is generated in liquid, allowing to stand at 25-60 degrees C for 20-120 minutes. In the step (v) the grinding device is wet-type stirring mill and the particle size of ground aluminum hydroxide is 1.7-1.9 mu m. In the step (vi) the weight ratio of kaolin to aluminum hydroxide powder is 1:3 and the air drying blank body for 48-72 hours. In the step (vii) the temperature in kiln is 1000-1300 degrees C for 26-40 hours.
DC L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)

PN CN109502585-A; CN109502585-B
TI Preparing magnesium-nitrogen co-doped high specific surface area biomass activated carbon comprises e.g. cleaning hemp core, drying, cutting, soaking in mixed aqueous solution of zinc chloride and magnesium chloride, sintering and drying.
AU LIU S
   QIN Q
   ZHOU D
   WU Y
AE GUIZHOU TECHNOLOGY INST (GZTI-C)
   GUIZHOU TECHNOLOGY INST (GZTI-C)
GA 201928677E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing magnesium-nitrogen co-doped high specific surface area biomass activated carbon comprises (i) cleaning hemp core, drying and cutting into small sections; (ii) soaking hemp core in a mixed aqueous solution of zinc chloride and magnesium chloride, filtering and then drying at 78-82 degrees C for 12-26 hours; (iii) sintering dried hemp core segment at a high temperature under inert gas protection for a period of time and cooling naturally under the protection of an inert gas to obtain a carbonized product; (iv) washing charcoal with acid under vacuum for 10-14 hours and washing with distilled water until pH reaches to neutral; and (v) soaking carbonized product in sufficient ethanol solution of an aminopyridine compound and drying at 78-82 degrees C for 12-26 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing magnesium-nitrogen co-doped high specific surface area biomass activated carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces activated carbon with high specific surface area and excellent carbon dioxide gas adsorption performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The drying ion step (i) after washing is carried out at 98-102 degrees C and cutting hemp core into small pieces having a length of 1-5 cm. The step (iii) comprises sintering at high temperature of 400-900 degrees C at a rate of 5-10 degrees C/ minute and maintaining temperature for 2-5 hours. The soaking in step (v) is carried out for 4-12 hours. Preferred Composition: The concentration of the mixed solution of zinc chloride and magnesium chloride in the mixed aqueous solution in step (ii) is 20-60%. The mass ratio of zinc chloride and magnesium chloride in the mixed aqueous solution in step (ii) is 1:1-4:1, mass ratio of zinc chloride and impregnated hemp core is 2:1-4:1 and soaking hemp core for 18-48 hours. The concentration of the aminopyridine compound in the ethanol solution of the aminopyridine compound in step (v) is 0.2-0.5 g/ml. Preferred Components: The inert gas in step (iii) is nitrogen or argon. The acid in step (iv) comprises dilute hydrochloric acid, dilute sulfuric acid or dilute nitric acid. The aminopyridine compound in step (v) is 2-aminopyridine, 2,3-diaminopyridine or 2,6-diaminopyridine.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN RD659081-A
TI Semiconductor processing equipment, has laser system for converting fuel droplet into plasma to generate radiation for lithographic apparatus, where laser system generates laser pre-pulse to condition fuel droplet.
AU 
AE ANONYMOUS (ANON-C)
GA 201923844R
AB    NOVELTY - The equipment has a laser system i.e. carbon dioxide laser, for converting fuel droplet into plasma to generate radiation such as EUV radiation, for a lithographic apparatus. The laser system generates laser pre-pulse (PP) to condition fuel droplet for receipt of laser main pulse (MP). The laser MP converts the fuel droplet into plasma. A control loop structure controls relative energy and/or timings of primary and secondary MPs. The control loop structure uses a radio frequency control mode (RFCM) controller. The laser system comprises an electro-optic modulator (EOM) that is triggered within a timeframe for generating Q-switched output pulse from a MP seed laser that produces the primary MP.
   USE - Semiconductor processing equipment.
   ADVANTAGE - The equipment ensures that width and/or delay of the primary and secondary MPs are varied to actuate the drive laser gain so as to control amount of residual gain seen by the primary MP, and control energy of the primary MP.

PT P
PN CN109280634-A; CN109280634-B
TI Performing in vitro isolation and culture of inner ear hair cells involves placing hair cells of mouse cochlear basement membrane face up, placing on laminin-coated cell culture well plate, adding inner ear hair cell culture medium.
AU CHEN Y
   ZHANG L
   GUAN M
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 201915868V
AB    NOVELTY - Performing in vitro isolation and culture of inner ear hair cells involves placing the hair cells of the mouse cochlear basement membrane face up, placing on a laminin-coated cell culture well plate, adding inner ear hair cell culture medium pre-warmed at 37 degrees C, culturing at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide, and replacing the fresh culture medium at intervals of 24 h to obtain inner ear hair cells. The inner ear hair cell culture medium is a high glucose dulbecco's modified eagle medium medium (DMEM) containing a final concentration of 2-15% horse serum, a final volume concentration of 2-15% fetal bovine serum, and a final concentration of 1-2% N-2 cytokine additive.
   USE - Method for performing in vitro isolation and culture of inner ear hair cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform in vitro isolation and culture of inner ear hair cells, which prolongs the survival time of the basement membrane hair cells by more than one time.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The inner ear hair cell culture medium is a high glucose DMEM medium containing a final concentration of 5% horse serum, a final concentration of 5% fetal bovine serum, and a final concentration of 1% N-2 cytokine additive. The laminin coats the cell culture plate by uniformly laminating laminin, polyornithine and fetal bovine serum at a volume ratio of 2:2:1. The mixture is then carefully added to the cell culture well plate. The edges of the culture wells are then blocked with a parafilm and incubated for 30 minutes in an incubator at 37 degrees C to complete the coating. The culture medium is replaced by replacing half of the volume of the culture medium with fresh inner ear hair cell culture medium each time.

TI Cleaning agent for cleaning exterior wall, a glass curtain wall, and an outdoor glass comprises fresh cow dung extract composite liquid, and iron leaf extract liquid.
AU ZHU H
   ZHANG D
AE ZHU H (ZHUH-Individual)
GA 201913269R
AB    NOVELTY - Cleaning agent comprises 100 pts. wt. fresh cow dung extract composite liquid, and 2.5-5 pts. wt. iron leaf extract liquid.
   USE - The cleaning agent is useful for cleaning exterior wall, a glass curtain wall, and an outdoor glass.
   ADVANTAGE - The cleaning agent is economical and environment-friendly, has excellent decontamination effect, and utilizes waste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the cleaning agent comprising (i) adding fresh cow dung to an equal mass of aqueous sodium hydroxide solution, adding 10-30% absolute ethanol of fresh cow dung, stirring uniformly, mashing and homogenizing at 10000 rotations/minute(rpm) in a mashing homogenizer, carrying out heating at 80-90 degrees C, continuous stirring, soaking for 20-30 hours, to obtain a mixture of cow dung, (ii) adding diatomite to the resultant, stirring uniformly, passing carbon dioxide gas, sealing the reaction, stirring, reducing the pH to 8.0-8.5, allowing to stand at room temperature for 1 hour, precipitating, and taking upper layer suspended liquid, and (iii) adding limestone fine powder to the resultant and stirring uniformly
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The iron leaf extract is prepared by (i) taking the whole plant fresh iron leaf and the acetic acid solution, where the ratio is 1:1, (ii) chopping whole plant of fresh iron leaf, adding with acetic acid solution, soaking for 48-50 hours under sealed condition, and filtering. In the step (i) the fresh cow dung has a water content of 80-85%, and the sodium hydroxide aqueous solution has a concentration of 150-200 g/l. In the step (ii), the mass ratio of cow dung mixture and diatomite is1:0.05-0.5. In the step (ii) the diatomaceous earth has a particle size of 800 mesh. In step (iii) the mass ratio of upper suspended liquid amd limestone fine powder is 1:0.01-0.05, where the limestone fine powder has a particle size of 800 meshes. The cow dung extract composite liquid and the ferri port extract liquid are separately shaken, uniformly mixed according to the proportion, diluted 5-20 times, used within 30 minutes, when using mechanical or manual spray gun, spray the cleaning agent uniformly on the surface of the object to be cleaned, allowing to stand for 10-30 minutes, and rinse the surface with clean water.

PT P
PN CN109054575-A
TI Co-production of coating of wood-plastic material and sulfuric acid involves preparing plastic material by mixing phosphogypsum and additive to obtain mixture, adding PVC, mixing, and producing sulfuric acid by floating mixture.
AU CHEN X
   LIU L
   ZHAO P
   QUAN B
   WANG L
   HUANG R
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 201900884S
AB    NOVELTY - Co-production of coating of wood-plastic material and sulfuric acid involves mixing phosphogypsum, high-sulfur bauxite, additive and modifier to obtain material, grinding, calcining in kiln to obtain clinker, water-grinding clinker, filtering to obtain mixed liquid, adding calcium oxide, mixing, dividing mixed liquid into mixture (a) and (b), heating mixture (a), stirring to obtain sodium metaaluminate solution, passing carbon dioxide to sodium metasilicate solution to obtain aluminum hydroxide precipitate, washing aluminum hydroxide precipitate with distilled water, drying, grinding to obtain aluminum hydroxide and mixing with PVC, urea-formaldehyde resin, fumed silica, chlorinated paraffin, zinc borate, sodium molybdate and coupling agent to obtain mixture, adding curing agent and propanol to mixture, and stirring, and floating mixture (b), separating sulfide, calcining to generate flue gas, reacting flue gas and vanadium pentoxide, and absorbing by concentrated sulfuric acid.
   USE - Co-production of coating of wood-plastic material and sulfuric acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, economical and environmentally-friendly, and effectively utilizes the raw materials. The prepared wood plastic material has excellent flame-retardant property.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The kiln is an industrial rotary kiln, an industrial tunnel kiln or an industrial vertical kiln.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The additive is sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate or caustic soda. The modifier is anthracite, carbon or coal gangue. The calcium oxide is analytical grade calcium oxide, and amount is 7-12 g/L. Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of phosphogypsum and high-sulfur bauxite is 1-1.7:1-1.4. The additive addition ratio is 1:1 according to the molecular ratio of the sum of sodium oxide, alumina and ferric oxide contained in material. The mixing ratio of modifier is 14-28% of the total weight of the material. The liquid-solid ratio at the time of elution of water-grinding clinker is 5-7:1. The concentration of carbon dioxide is more than 38%, and has dust content of less than 20 mg/hm3. The sulfide has oxygen-rich content of 40-45%. Preferred Conditions: The calcination of material is carried out at 950-1350 degrees C for 0.5-4 hours. The heating and stirring of mixture (a) is carried out at 80-90 degrees C for 1.5-2 hours. The drying of mixture (a) at 95-115 degrees C, after passing carbon dioxide. The stirring of curing agent and propanol is carried out at 80-90 degrees C for 10-30 minutes. The calcination of separated sulfide is carried out at 1100-1200 degrees C for 2-5 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The coupling agent is KH-570 (RTM: gamma -methacryloyloxypropyltrimethoxysilane). The curing agent is T31-type curing agent. AE SICHUAN AIYIJIA FURNITURE CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2018A5160U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of furniture varnish, involves adding castor oil, trimethylolpropane, and phthalic anhydride to a four-mouthed flask with a stirrer and a water divider, introducing carbon dioxide gas as protective gas, heating and reacting (I) at 200-230 degrees C for 2-4 hours, removing esterification and etherification water to obtain hydroxy-containing alkyd resin, adding toluene diisocyanate, heating (II) to 260-300 degrees C, reacting (II) for 1-2 hours to obtain isocyanate-terminated prepolymer, adding modified polyisocyanate while stirring at 650-800 rpm, preferably 750 rpm, adding propylene glycol methyl ether acetate, butyl-etherified amino resin, and acrylic acid resin, dispersing at 1100-1300 rpm for 10-15 minutes, preferably at 1200 rpm for 13 minutes, adding zinc oxide, mica powder, talc powder, and titania while stirring, dispersing to a fineness of not more than 25 mu m for 30-45 minutes at 1300-1500 rpm.

   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple preparation of furniture varnish which has excellent wear resistance and is convenient to use.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: Heating and reacting (I) is carried out at 220 degrees C for 3 hours. Heating and reacting (II) is carried out at 280 degrees C for 1.5 hours. Final dispersion is preferably carried out at 1400 rpm for 40 minutes.

PN CN108913457-A
TI Coconut nectar sparkling wine prepared by e.g. mixing coconut nectar and add distilled water, adjusting sugar concentration using white sugar, adjusting pH using citric acid, sterilizing, and adding raw material liquid in fermenter.
AU WU Y
AE WU Y (WUYY-Individual)
GA 201898380V
AB    NOVELTY - Coconut nectar sparkling wine is claimed. The coconut nectar sparkling wine is prepared by (i) mixing coconut nectar and add distilled water in the ratio of 1:15-1:25, adjusting sugar concentration to 6-8 Baume with white sugar, adjusting pH to 4-5 using citric acid, and sterilizing raw material liquid, (ii) adding 70-80 vol.% raw material liquid in fermenter, adding 5-10 vol.% yeast culture solution, stirring, closing lid, carrying out anaerobic fermentation at 28 plus minus 2 degrees C for 15 days to obtain fermentation broth, and (iii) filtering fermentation broth, clarifying, adjusting sugar with white sugar to 5-6 Baume, adjusting pH to 3-5 using citric acid, adjusting alcohol content to 5-7 degrees , filling carbon dioxide and pasteurizing.
   USE - Used as coconut nectar sparkling wine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing coconut nectar sparkling wine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows flow diagram of the preparation of coconut nectar sparkling wine (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The preparation method specifically comprises (i) mixing coconut nectar and add distilled water in the ratio of 1:20, adjusting sugar concentration to 6 Baume with white sugar, adjusting pH to 4-5 using citric acid, heating through 70 degrees C tube heat exchanger for 20 minutes, filtering by 120 mesh sieve, and heating at 25 degrees C, (ii) adding 70 vol.% raw material liquid in fermenter, adding 10 vol.% yeast culture solution, stirring, closing lid, carrying out anaerobic fermentation at 26 degrees C, within 24 hours passing filtered purify oxygen, fermenting for 24 hours, raising temperature to 28 degrees C, performing anaerobic fermentation for 10-15 days, reducing visual bubbles and carbon dioxide, when sugar content is less than 0.5 Baume, then stopping fermentation to obtain fermentation liquid, and (iii) subjecting fermentation broth for coarse filtration, blending wine, adjusting sugar with white sugar to 5 Baume, adjusting pH to 4 using citric acid, adjusting alcohol content to 5 degrees , clarifying by secondary filtration through a diatomaceous earth filter, filling carbon dioxide at 1-2 Bar pressure and pasteurizing at 70-80 degrees C for 30-60 minutes.

PN CN108751143-A
TI Co-production of artificial stone panels involves adding unsaturated polyester resin, methyl ethyl ketone, cobalt naphthenate, modifier and antioxidant to aluminum hydroxide powder-calcium carbonate mixing and vacuum defoaming.
AU CHEN X
   XUE T
   ZHENG K
   LUO X
   QUAN B
   WANG L
   HUANG R
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 201888590E
AB    NOVELTY - Co-production of artificial stone panels involves water-milling clinker, dissolving, performing solid-liquid separation, carrying out flotation of obtained precipitate, separating sulfide, calcining separated sulfide, catalyzing flue gas generated by calcination by vanadium pentoxide, absorbing with concentrated sulfuric acid, obtaining sulfuric acid, adding carbon dioxide to liquid obtained by solid-liquid separation until the precipitate is no longer produced, drying the precipitate, obtaining aluminum hydroxide, pulverizing, obtaining aluminum hydroxide powder, mixing aluminum hydroxide powder with calcium carbonate, placing in fluidized bed, adding modifier by atomization, maintaining for 0.5-1 hour, obtaining mixture (I), removing the mixture, placing in reaction kettle, adding unsaturated polyester resin, methyl ethyl ketone, cobalt naphthenate, polysiloxane modifier, light stabilizer and antioxidant, uniformly stirring, vacuum defoaming, pouring into a mold and solidifying.
   USE - Co-production of artificial stone panels.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently and economically provides artificial stone panel without affecting the environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Clinker is obtained by mixing phosphogypsum, high-sulfur bauxite, additive and modifier, grinding, forming raw meal and subjecting to a kiln for baking.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The sulfur content in high sulfur bauxite is more than 0.7%. The additive is sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate or caustic soda. The modifier is anthracite, carbon or coal gangue. The kiln is an industrial rotary kiln, an industrial tunnel kiln or an industrial vertical kiln. Calcium carbonate is calcium carbonate having size of more than 400 mesh.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The light stabilizer is benzotriazole. The antioxidant is N,N-diphenyl-p-phenylenediamine or N-phenyl-N-cyclohexyl p-phenylenediamine. The artificial stone panel comprises 30-50 pts. wt. aluminum hydroxide powder, 30-60 pts. wt. calcium carbonate, 25-30 pts. wt. unsaturated polyester resin, 0.5-1 pt. wt. methyl ethyl ketone, 0.5-1 pt. wt. cobalt naphthenate, 0.2-0.5 pt. wt. polysiloxane modifier, 0.3-0.8 pt. wt. light stabilizer and 0.5-1 pt. wt. antioxidant.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The unsaturated polyester resin is dicyclopentadiene-type modified unsaturated polyester resin, dibromo neopentyl glycol unsaturated polyester resin or naphthalene dicarboxylic acid-type unsaturated polyester resin. The polysiloxane modifier is polydimethylsiloxane, polypropylmethylsiloxane, hydroxyalkyl polysiloxane or carboxypolysiloxane.

   INFINITED FIBER CO OY (INFI-Non-standard)

GA 201886287S
AB    NOVELTY - Process for refining textile materials                comprises subjecting the textile material to two or                more chemical treatment steps comprising alkaline                extraction, and/or enzymatic treatment steps.
   USE - The process is useful for refining textile                material that is used in the production of                cellulose carbamate, fiber spinning, replacing                viscose or as starting material for production of                viscose, N-methyl-morpholine-N-oxide, ionic liquids                or biocelso1 (all claimed). The process is useful                in the treatment of textile-based waste materials,                preferably cotton-based waste materials. The                textile material is used in different coating                applications, regenerated cellulosic films,                cellulosic sponges, foams, coagulated cellulosic                beads and particle coagulated cellulosic beads and                particles and for replacement of conventional                pre-treated cellulose. The textile material is                useful in recycling pre-consumer and post-consumer                textiles by enabling the regeneration of quality                cellulosic fibers from waste textiles.
   ADVANTAGE - The process utilizes textile waste materials                and is cost effective. The process utilizes                non-wood raw-material for producing cellulosic                fibers and results in a smaller carbon dioxide                footprint compared to conventional processes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Process: The chemical treatment                  step comprises treating the alkaline-treated                  material with an acid. The chemical treatment                  step is chosen from alkaline extraction at room                  temperature, hot alkaline extraction, acid                  treatment, ozone treatment, peroxide treatment                  and enzymatic treatment. The process comprises                  alkaline extraction, followed by an ozone                  treatment and/or a peroxide treatment. The                  textile material has solids content of greater                  than or equal to 40 wt.%, preferably 41-70 wt.%,                  before the treatment steps. The process comprises                  carrying out carbamation step on the chemically                  treated textile material, preferably with the                  material at a solids content of 70 wt.%.

TI Distillation apparatus for distillation of alcoholic beverage, edible oil and petrochemicals, has condenser unit that is connected to discharge side of pressure reduction pump for cooling vapor generated in reboiler unit.
AU NAKANO K
   SATO N
   YOSHINOYA T
   NAKAYAMA R
   LIU Y P
AE IHI CORP (ISHI-C)
GA 201885615C
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (100) has a reboiler unit (120) which supplies raw material liquid containing lower boiling component and high boiling point component having boiling point higher than lower boiling component to heat raw material liquid. The inlet side of pressure reduction pump (130) is connected to reboiler unit. A condenser unit (150) is connected to discharge side of pressure reduction pump for cooling vapor generated in reboiler unit. A recirculation pump (160) transduces condensate generated in condenser unit in reboiler unit. The inlet side is connected to condenser unit and discharge side is connected to reboiler unit.
   USE - Distillation apparatus for distillation of alcoholic beverage, edible oil and petrochemicals, removal of ammonia, collection of carbon dioxide and manufacture of pharmaceutical.
   ADVANTAGE - The modification of lower boiling component can be suppressed. The distillation efficiency (separation efficiency of lower boiling component and high boiling point component) of raw material liquid can be improved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of distillation apparatus.
   Distillation apparatus (100)
   Reboiler unit (120)
   Pressure reduction pump (130)
   Condenser unit (150)
   Recirculation pump (160)

The weight ratio of sugarcane extract and saccharide is 3:7-7:3. The carbonic acid powder dispersion solution contains 1-5 wt.% thickener, and 5-50 wt.% carbonic acid powder.
