TI Method for normalizing combustion in process of power production plant, involves implementing control function such that temperature and mass flow of combustor exhaust stream exiting combustor varies by no greater than specific value.
AU LU X
   FETVEDT J E
   MCGRODDY P M
   LV X
   XIJIA L
AE 8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
   8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
   8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
GA 201870893N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a variable fuel to a combustor (120), where the variable fuel includes a composition that varies during an operation of a power production process. The variable fuel in the combustor with an oxidant is combusted to provide a combustor exhaust stream. The combustor exhaust stream is passed through a turbine (135) to generate power. A control function is implemented such that temperature and mass flow of the combustor exhaust stream exiting the combustor varies by no greater than 10% as the composition of the variable fuel varies during the operation of the power production process, where the variable fuel is a syngas and a mixture of methane, carbon monoxide, and hydrogen, the oxidant is a mixture of oxygen and a diluent, and the diluent is carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for normalizing combustion in a process of a power production plant (claimed).

TI Preparing algae beverage based on marine microbial fermentation involves preparing algal enzyme by rehydrating fresh algae or dry algae, adding water, homogenizing in fermenting tank, sterilizing and adding Bacillus subtilis suspension.
AU YIN H
   DONG J
   YU J
   TIAN Y
   QIAN Z
   CHANG Z
   YAN P
   ZHANG Y
   CHEN H
   LIU X
AE TSINGTAO BREWERY CO LTD (TSIN-Non-standard)
GA 201868366F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing algae beverage based on marine microbial fermentation involves preparing algal enzyme by rehydrating fresh algae or dry algae after shearing, adding water, homogenizing in fermenting tank, sterilizing, adding sterilized fermentation matrix with access Bacillus subtilis suspension and plant Lactobacillus suspension to ferment at different temperatures, obtaining algae enzyme by solid-liquid separation of fermented seaweed enzyme, preparing hop tea by taking hop particle and boiled purified water in weight ratio of 1:100-150, boiling for 1 minute, stopping heating, cooling to room temperature, filtering to obtain flower tea wine, preparing pre-treating alga drink, taking 1.5-15 wt.% fermented algae, 1-10 wt.% flower tea wine, 2-5 wt.% syrup, 0.01-0.05 wt.% sour material and rest of water in mixing tank, stirring uniformly, obtaining pretreating algae by pre-treating alga drink, carbonating beverage with carbon dioxide obtain the desired algae beverage.
   USE - Method for preparing algae beverage based on marine microbial fermentation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to solve problem of seaweed fishy smell and prepared algae beverage has good drinking property and corresponding function of reserved algae.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Compositions: The red algae comprises algae and brown algae, where algae ferment is red seaweed enzyme and brown seaweed enzyme mixtures and weight fraction of red algae enzyme is 0.5-5 wt.% and of brown seaweed enzyme is 1-10 wt.%. The amount of added red algae enzyme is 0.5-2 wt.%, weight fraction of brown algae enzyme is 2-5 wt.%, and best addition of hop tea is 2-6 wt.%. The carbon dioxide content in beverage is 0.35-0.65 wt.%. Preferred Components: The syrup is high fructose syrup or glucose syrup. The acidic material is selected from citric acid, malic acid or lactic acid. The hop is incense and fragrant hop or hop selected from Qingdao heterophyllous, Tettanger, Cascade Saaz, Hallertauer or citral. The algae comprises one or more of agar, laver, Asparagus, carrageenan, Eucheuma and brown algae includes kelp, Pinnatifida or Sargassum fusiforme. Preferred Process: The preparation method of algae beverage is further characterized by adding sterilized fermentation substrate aseptically to prepared suspension of Bacillus subtilis by 1.5% inoculation amount, stirring, fermenting at 40 degrees C for 3 days, lowering temperature to 30 degrees C after stirring, adding prepared suspension of Lactobacillus plantarum aseptically to inoculated amount of 1%, stirring at 30 degrees C for 5 minutes, stopping stirring and allowing culture to stand for 2 days.

AE NANJING ENN ENVIROTECH CO LTD (XINA-C)
GA 2018491341
AB    NOVELTY - Recovery of rhodium from rhodium caprylate mother liquor comprises treating rhodium caprylate mother liquor with acetic acid to obtain a slurry; mixing the slurry, oxidant, and water to carry out supercritical oxidation treatment and gas-liquid separation to obtain a gas phase system and a non-gas phase system, and carrying out solid-liquid separation treatment to obtain a first solid phase; carrying out first acid treatment using nitric acid for the first solid phase to obtain a second solid phase, carrying out second acid treatment using nitric acid, carrying out solid-liquid separation to obtain a third solid phase, and mixing the second solid phase and the third solid phase to obtain rhodium crude product; and washing.

PT P
PN CN207116902-U
TI Laser pipe angle adjusting device, has main body axially formed with through-hole, and adjusting screw fixed in outer section, where adjusting screw supports pin and outer wall of main body is formed with annular groove.
AU WANG P
AE SHIJIAZHUANG LEISEN LASER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201823737Q
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model belongs to the technical field of carbon dioxide laser tube, the invention provides laser pipe angle adjusting device, comprising a cylindrical main body, the main body is provided with a through hole along the axial direction; the outer wall of the main body is divided into a front section and a rear section by the ring groove. the annular groove is circumferentially arranged with a plurality of screwing the adjusting screw adjusting hole angle of the front section and the rear section and the depth of the adjusting hole is less than or equal to the depth of the annular groove, the diameter of the adjusting hole is larger than the length of the annular groove; the adjusting hole comprises an inner section and outer section are set orderly from inside to outside, the inner section is provided with a pin, the section is set in the adjusting screw. The utility model solves the problem that the angle adjusting device is used for a long time easily damaged technical problem.
DC V08 (Lasers and Masers)
MC V08-A01C; V08-A01D; V08-A04B

PT P
PN CN207083621-U
TI Photovoltaic agricultural greenhouse, has photovoltaic assembly mounted with bracket, storage battery installed with shed bracket that is fixed with electric plate, and oxygen generator connected with carbon dioxide concentration detector.
AU LIU H
   LIU L
AE HENAN QIHUANGYUAN AGRIC DEV CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 201821814R
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a photovoltaic agricultural greenhouse, comprising a solar energy collecting plate, a bracket, an inner hose, greenhouse door, a connecting bracket and a storage battery; said solar energy collecting plate is installed above the photovoltaic component; and the photovoltaic component is installed on the telescopic frame, said inner tube is installed above the greenhouse bracket, the connecting bracket is embedded in the greenhouse bracket, the tent door and the tent bracket by hinge, said shed bracket set on the inner hose and the outer shed film; the photovoltaic component through transmission line connected with the storage battery, the storage battery is installed in the greenhouse bracket, a transverse bracket inside of the greenhouse bracket is provided with an electric plate; the inner wall of said connecting bracket is installed with oxygen-machine. The utility model is installed with a solar collecting plate and the photovoltaic module, the solar energy can be converted into electric energy stored for vegetable greenhouse with electricity, which not only saves power, but also uses the renewable energy.
DC P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)
MC P13-A01C; P13-E02; X16-B01

PT P
PN CN107759574-A; CN107759574-B
TI New 3-fluoro-1-tetrahydro-2H-pyran-2-yl-1H-pyrazole-5-carboxylic acid used for preparing 5-fluoro-1H-pyrazole compound.
AU FAN H
   GUAN Z
   XIA A
   LI H
   JIE Y
AE PHARMABLOCK SCI NANJING INC (PHAR-Non-standard)
GA 2018199784
AB    NOVELTY - 3-Fluoro-1-tetrahydro-2H-pyran-2-yl-1H-pyrazole-5-carboxylic acid (IV) is new.
   USE - The new compound (IV) is useful for preparing 5-fluoro-1H-pyrazole compound (I) (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The new compound (IV) can be prepared in a simple and rapid manner with high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - 3-Fluoro-1-tetrahydro-2H-pyran-2-yl-1H-pyrazole-5-carboxylic acid of formula (IV) is new. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of compound (IV), which involves (1) taking 5-fluoro-1-tetrahydro-2H-pyran-2-yl-1H-pyrazole of formula (II) as raw material, and then carrying out rearrangement reaction under the action of catalyst to obtain 3-fluoro-1-tetrahydro-2H-pyran-2-yl-1H-pyrazole of formula (III), and (2) carrying out carboxylation reaction on the compound (III) with carbon dioxide or dry ice under the action of strong base to obtain the product; and
   (2) a method for preparing a 5-fluoro-1H-pyrazole compound of formula (I), which involves carrying out esterification reaction on compound (IV) with aliphatic alcohol compound of formula: ROH (A) under the action of thionyl chloride to obtain the product.
   R=methyl or ethyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The step (1) is carried out at a temperature of 110-140 degrees C for 6-60 hours. The step (1) is carried out at a temperature of -78 degrees C to -30 degrees C. Preferred Components: The catalyst in step (I) is chosen from trifluoroacetic acid, p-toluenesulfonic acid monohydrate, trifluoromethanesulfonic acid, benzenesulfonic acid and/or methanesulfonic acid. The solvent used in step (I) is chosen from xylene, toluene and/or chlorobenzene. The strong base in step (2) is chosen from n-butyllithium, tert-butyllithium, lithium diisopropylamide, lithium bis(trimethylsilyl)amide, sodium bis(trimethylsilyl)amide, dichloro(2,2,6,6-tetramethylpiperidinato)magnesate(1-) lithium (1:1) and/or phenyllithium. The molar ratio of compound (III) and strong alkali is 1:1-1:3. The solvent used in step (2) is chosen from tetrahydrofuran, diethyl ether, 2-methyltetrahydrofuran and/or cyclopentyl methyl ether. The molar ratio of compound (IV) with thionyl chloride is 1:1.5-1:3.5.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E07-D08; E11-D02; E11-F02B; E11-F06; E11-H03; J04-E01; N05-E02; N07-D02B; N07-E05
IP C07D-231/16; C07D-405/04

   (4) The concentrated chili oil crude product are centrifuged at 4-25degrees Celsius and rotated with speed of 6000-8000 r/min for 10-15 minutes, and removed impurities and water to obtain concentrated chili oil. PN CN113016870-A
TI Preventing discoloration of potato after peeling involves pre-treating potato by placing potato in washing equipment, cleaning it with water to remove epidermal sludge, and transferring it to potato peeler for peeling by transfer equipment.
AU LIU Y
   LIU J
   LIU Z
   YANG C
   GUO L
   SUN J
AE JIANGSU KANGRUN AGRIC SCI & TECHNOLOGY (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202176955R
AB    NOVELTY - Preventing discoloration of potato after peeling involves pre-treating potato by placing potato in washing equipment, cleaning it with water to remove the epidermal sludge, and then transferring it to potato peeler for peeling through the transfer equipment. The internal temperature of potato peeler is set to 3 degrees C, and potato is peeled. The peeled potato is transferred to potato slicer for slicing again through the transfer device, where internal temperature of potato slicer is set to 3 degrees C, and then processed to obtain pre-processed potato slice. The potato slice is placed inside the pressurizing equipment, where air inside the pressurizing equipment is drawn out through suction equipment to make the inside of the pressurizing equipment in a vacuum state, and then oxygen supply equipment and carbon dioxide supply equipment are pressurized at the same time and transmits oxygen and carbon dioxide in a ratio of 1:4.
   USE - Method for preventing discoloration of potato after peeling.
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids potato enzyme and oxygen reaction, reduces the activity of the potato enzyme, prevents the potato chip discoloration, and effectively prolongs the storage period of the potato chips.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preventing discoloration of potato after peeling involves pre-treating potato by placing potato in washing equipment, cleaning it with water to remove the epidermal sludge, and then transferring it to potato peeler for peeling through the transfer equipment. The internal temperature of potato peeler is set to 3 degrees C, and potato is peeled. The peeled potato is transferred to potato slicer for slicing again through the transfer device, where internal temperature of potato slicer is set to 3 degrees C, and then processed to obtain pre-processed potato slice. The potato slice is placed inside the pressurizing equipment, where air inside the pressurizing equipment is drawn out through suction equipment to make the inside of the pressurizing equipment in a vacuum state, and then oxygen supply equipment and carbon dioxide supply equipment are pressurized at the same time and transmits oxygen and carbon dioxide in a ratio of 1:4, and when internal pressure of the pressurized equipment reaches 900MPa then pressure is maintained for 30 minutes. The potato slice is subjected to biological treatment by adding 85% water, 2% small molecule peptide, 5% honey, 2% lactic acid bacteria, 1% protease and 5% glucose oxidase to the mixing equipment, which is started for one operation to obtain mixed solution, where potato slice is added to mixing equipment, which is started for second operation so that potato slice and mixed solution are fully contacted. The potato slice is chemically treated by soaking it in preservation solution for 5 minutes at a temperature of 2 degrees C, potato slice is taken out, drained, cleaned and soaked in an edible carrageenan solution so that edible coating film is formed on outer surface of slice. The potato slice is stored and processed, and remaining potato slice is placed in a vacuum packaging machine for vacuum packaging. The potato slice is placed in composite bag, where vacuum machine extracts the gas in the composite bag to make inside of the composite bag in a vacuum state, and potato slice is stored in a low temperature environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The preservation solution comprises 0.04% of 15% natural vitamin E emulsion, 0.001% gallic acid, 0.01% L-ascorbic acid and 99.949% water. The preservation solution further comprises 0.03% kojic acid, 0.001% cysteine, 0.001% sulfur dioxide, 0.01% sulfite, and 99.958% water.

PN CN111660594-A
TI Manufacturing gas foamed double-color soles, by making polymer foam material into different color foamed particles, molding foamed particles in mold to form initial blank sole, introducing nitrogen and carbon dioxide to kettle, and foaming.
AU LAN S
AE LAN S (LANS-Individual)
GA 202092213S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for manufacturing gas foamed double-color soles, involves (a) making two different color foamed particles, and making the polymer foam material into two different color foamed particles, (b) molding a mold by feeding the foamed particles of one color between the upper mold and the middle mold, feeding the foamed particles of another color between the middle mold and the lower mold, forming the upper portion of the sole between the upper mold and the middle mold, forming the lower portion of the sole between the lower mold and the middle mold, then removing the middle mold, adhering the upper portion of the sole to the upper mold, and adhering the upper portion of the sole and the lower portion of the sole between the upper mold and the lower mold to form the initial blank sole, and (c) putting the initial blank sole into the reaction kettle, introducing nitrogen and carbon dioxide into the kettle, and releasing pressure to make the initial blank soles to expand and foam.
   USE - The method is used for manufacturing gas foamed double-color soles (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The upper portion of the sole and the lower portion of the sole in the mold are adhered into the initial blank embryo sole without worry about the separation of the upper and lower portions of the sole during later use. In the foaming process, nitrogen and carbon dioxide are introduced into the reaction kettle to help the initial blank soles foam without using foaming agents. The manufactured double-layer color sole is non-toxic and elastic, which meets the needs of manufacturers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for manufacturing gas foamed double-color soles, involves (a) making two different color foamed particles, and making the polymer foam material into two different color foamed particles, (b) molding a mold by the mold including an upper mold, a middle mold, and a lower mold, where the upper mold and the lower mold are combined with the middle mold, feeding the foamed particles of one color between the upper mold and the middle mold, feeding the foamed particles of another color between the middle mold and the lower mold, forming the upper portion of the sole between the upper mold and the middle mold, forming the lower portion of the sole between the lower mold and the middle mold, then removing the middle mold, adhering the upper portion of the sole to the upper mold, adhering the lower portion of the sole to the lower mold, closing the upper mold and the lower mold, closing the upper mold with the lower mold, and adhering the upper portion of the sole and the lower portion of the sole between the upper mold and the lower mold to form the initial blank sole, and (c) foaming by putting the initial blank sole into the reaction kettle, then introducing nitrogen and carbon dioxide into the kettle, keeping the initial embryo sole under pressure and heat for 1-2 hours in the kettle, and rapidly releasing pressure to make the initial blank soles to rapidly expand and foam to produce double-layer color soles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The step (a) includes: mixing 40-60 %mass ethylene-vinyl acetate (EVA), 10-30 %mass styrene-ethylene-butylene-styrene (SEBS), 10-30 %mass polyolefin elastomers (POE), 5-15 %mass thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU), and 0.1-1 %mass pigment A is mixed to obtain the first polymer foam material; entering the first polymer foam material into the granulator to make the first color foamed particles; preparing the second polymer foam by mixing 40-60 %mass EVA, 10-30 %mass SEBS, 10-30 %mass POE, 5-15 %mass TPU and 0.1-1 %mass pigment B; and entering the second polymer foam material into the granulator to make the second color foam particles, where the pigment A and the pigment B are different colors. Preferred Conditions: In step (c), the temperature in the reaction kettle is 100-200 degrees C and the pressure is 10-20 MPa. The temperature in the reaction kettle is 150 degrees C, with the pressure of 15 MPa, and the initial blank sole is kept under pressure and heat for 1.5 hours in the kettle. In step (b), when the upper mold and the middle mold are closed, the upper portion of the sole is formed at 180-200 degrees C, preferably 180 degrees C and a heating time of 40-60 seconds, preferably 40 seconds, when the lower mold and the middle mold are closed, the lower portion of the sole is formed at 180-200 degrees C and a heating time of 40-60 seconds, and when the upper mold and the lower mold are closed, the initial blank sole is formed at 180-200 degrees C, preferably 190 degrees C, and a heating time of 390-420 seconds, preferably 400 seconds. In step (b), when the lower mold and the middle mold are closed, the upper portion of the sole is formed at 180 degrees C and a heating time of 40 seconds.

TI Optimizing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of spice components in lychee peel by removing lychee pulp, washing peel, drying, crushing, sieving, and performing e.g. extraction pressure analysis and extraction temperature analysis.
AU YIN A
   ZHOU T
AE UNIV GUANGDONG PETROCHEMICAL TECHNOLOG (UNGP-C)
GA 202094317T
AB    NOVELTY - Optimizing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of spice components in lychee peel includes removing lychee pulp to obtain peel, washing, drying, crushing, and sieving; performing extraction pressure analysis, extraction temperature analysis, analytical temperature analysis and analytical pressure analysis; and performing response surface optimization analysis by selecting extraction pressure, extraction temperature and desorption temperature, analyzing top 3 factors in the degree of pressure contribution as the test factors, taking lychee peel essential oil extraction as evaluation index, and performing response surface analysis using DesignExpert software to obtain optimized extraction process parameters.
   USE - The method is for optimizing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of spice components in lychee peel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method obtains the best process for supercritical extraction of lychee peel essential oil at extraction pressure of 15 MPa, extraction temperature of 37 degrees C, analysis temperature of 35 degrees C, sesame oil extracted amount of 0.37 g and extraction rate of 0.185%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Optimizing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of spice components in lychee peel comprises:
   (A) removing lychee pulp to obtain peel, washing, drying, crushing to 0.3-0.6 mm powder, and sieving through 30-60 mesh sieve;
   (B) performing extraction pressure analysis by selecting 30-45 degrees C as extraction temperature and analysis temperature, selecting 4-8 MPa as analytical pressure, determining relationship between extraction amount of lychee peel essential oil and extraction pressure through pressure range of 10-20 MPa, and determining contribution of extraction pressure to extraction volume of lychee peel essential oil; performing extraction temperature analysis by selecting 30-45 degrees C as analytical temperature, selecting 10-20 MPa as extraction pressure and 4-8 MPa as analytical pressure, determining relationship between extraction amount of lychee peel essential oil and extraction pressure through pressure range of 10-20 MPa, determining relationship between extraction amount of lychee peel essential oil and extraction temperature at temperature range of 30-45 degrees C, and determining degree of contribution of extraction temperature to extraction of lychee peel essential oil; performing analytical temperature analysis by selecting 30-45 degrees C as extraction temperature, 10-20 MPa as extraction pressure and 4-8 MPa as analytical pressure, determining relationship between extraction amount of lychee peel essential oil and analysis temperature through temperature range of 30-45 degrees C, and determining degree of contribution of analytical temperature to extraction of lychee peel essential oil; and performing analytical pressure analysis by selecting 30-45 degrees C as extraction temperature and analysis temperature, selecting 10-20 MPas as extraction pressure, determining relationship between extraction amount of lychee peel essential oil and analytical pressure at pressure range of 4-8 MPa, and determining degree of contribution of analytical pressure to extraction of lychee peel essential oil; and
   (C) performing response surface optimization analysis by selecting extraction pressure, extraction temperature and desorption temperature, analyzing top 3 factors in the degree of pressure contribution as the test factors, taking lychee peel essential oil extraction as evaluation index, and performing response surface analysis using DesignExpert software to obtain optimized extraction process parameters.

TI Preparing a copper-based metal organic polyhedral composition material used as catalyst for catalyzing cycloaddition of propylene oxide in carbon dioxide, comprises e.g. dissolving sodium alginate in de-ionized water and stirring.
AU ZHANG Z
   QIAO X
   LV B
   TANG J
   ZHOU J
   CUI M
   CHEN X
   FEI Z
   LIU Q
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 201978583Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a copper-based metal organic polyhedral composition material comprises e.g. dissolving sodium alginate into de-ionized water, stirring until completely dissolving sodium alginate, where the aqueous solution of sodium alginate solution into divalent copper ion in sodium alginate solution, filtering and washing to obtain copper alginate hydrogel, placing the prepared copper-based alginate hydrogel in a silicate solution, filtering, washing, obtaining a copper-based alginate-silica hydrogel, and washing the prepared copper-based alginate-silica hydrogel for 3-5 times with methanol or N,N',-dimethylformamide, then adding to the ligand solution, where the ligand solution includes a ligand and a deprotonating solvent, and the ligand is isophthalic acid or 5-substituted isophthalic acid, the deprotonating solvent is 2,6-lutidine or pyridine, preferably, 2,6-lutidine, allowing to stand, filtering, washing, and drying.
   USE - The copper-based metal organic polyhedral composition material is used as a catalyst for catalyzing the cycloaddition of propylene oxide with carbon dioxide to form propylene carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition material: has dense network structure, and can inhibits the close packing of copper-based metal organic polyhedron and the shrinkage of alginic acid skeleton, has high catalytic activity for the reaction of propylene oxide with carbon dioxide cycloaddition to form propylene carbonate; and catalyst: is spherical, easy to separate and easy to recycle and reusable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a copper-based metal organic polyhedral composition material comprises (i) dissolving sodium alginate into de-ionized water, the mass ratio of sodium alginate and de-ionized water is 1:25-1:200 at 20-40 degrees C, stirring for 4-12 hours, until completely dissolving sodium alginate, where the aqueous solution of sodium alginate solution into divalent copper ion in sodium alginate solution with volume ratio of aqueous solution of bivalent copper ion is 1:1-1:5, the concentration of divalent copper ion in the bivalent copper ion solution is 0.05-0.5 mol/l at 20-40 degrees C, allowing to stand for 6-12 hours, filtering and washing to obtain copper alginate hydrogel; (ii) placing the copper-based alginate hydrogel prepared in the step (i) in a silicate solution, where the volume ratio of copper-based alginate hydrogel to the silicate solution is 1:1-1:5 and the concentration of silicate solution is 60-80 v/v%, and the reaction time is 6-24 hours, filtering, washing, obtaining a copper-based alginate-silica hydrogel; and (iii) washing the prepared copper-based alginate-silica hydrogel for 3-5 times with methanol or N,N',-dimethylformamide, then adding to the ligand solution, where the ligand solution includes a ligand and a deprotonating solvent, and the ligand is isophthalic acid or 5-substituted isophthalic acid, the deprotonating solvent is 2,6-lutidine or pyridine, preferably, 2,6-lutidine, allowing to stand for 6-12 hours, filtering, washing, and drying at 40-100 degrees C for 6-12 hours. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) copper-based metal organic polyhedral composition material, is produced by the production method; and
   (2) use of copper-based metal organic polyhedral composition material is used as catalyst for catalyzing the cycloaddition of propylene oxide with carbon dioxide to form propylene carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the mass ratio of sodium alginate and the de-ionized water is 1:50-1:100. In the step (i), the divalent copper ion is trihydrate copper nitrate or copper chloride, the divalent copper ion bivalent copper ion concentration in the aqueous solution is 0.1-0.3 mol/l, the volume ratio of aqueous solution of sodium alginate solution with divalent copper ion is 1:2-1:3, and the added amount of divalent copper ion in copper alginic acid hydrogel cross-linked is sodium alginate quality of 21 wt.%. In the step (ii), the copper-based alginate hydrogel with the silicate solution whose volume ratio is 1:2-1:3, the silicateis methyl orthosilicate, ethyl orthosilicate, propyl ester orthosilicate, tetrabutyl orthosilicate, preferably is ethyl orthosilicate, the solvent of silicate solution is methanol or ethanol, preferably ethanol, the silicate solution is 65-70 v/v%. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the reaction time of copper-based alginate hydrogel and silicate solution is 10-12 hours. In the step (iii), the ligand content in the ligand solution is: the mol ratio of bivalent copper ion and ligand in step (i) in the copper alginate hydrogel cross-linking is 1:1-1:2, preferably is 1:1.2-1:1.5 and the solvent dissolving ligand in ligand solution is methanol or N',N-dimethylformamide, the 5-substituted isophthalic acid is 5-hydroxyisophthalic acid, 5-aminoisophthalic acid, 5-sulfonic acid isophthalic acid monosodium salt, the deprotonating solvent is added in a molar ratio of deproton solvent to ligand of 1:2-1:3, preferably 1:2-1:2.5. In the step (iii), the drying temperature is 60-80 degrees C and the drying time is 8-10 hours. The molar ratio of propylene oxide to CO2 is 1:0.5-1:2, preferably 1:1-1:1.5; the copper-based metal organic polyhedral composition is used as a catalyst in an amount of 1-5 wt.% of the mass of propylene oxide, preferably 2-3 wt.%, and the use requires the addition of cocatalyst, which is tetrabutylammonium bromide in an amount of 5-10 wt.%, preferably 7-9 wt.% of the mass of propylene oxide, and a reaction pressure of 0.5-2 MPa, preferably the reaction temperature is 1-1.5 MPa, the reaction temperature is 60-120 degrees C, preferably 80-100 degrees C, and the reaction time is 4-10 hours, preferably 8-10 hours.

PN CN109497245-A
TI Preparation of passion fruit juice by pretreating            passion fruit, separating peel and meat, passivating            peel, subjecting to anti-browning treatment, adding            passion fruit meat juice mixture, soaking, heating,            draining, drying, and baking.
AU HAN H
AE FUJIAN BAIGUOLIANMENG FOOD TECHNOLOGY CO (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 201928801F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of passion fruit juice includes                selecting smooth, relatively heavy weight, round                and not rotten passion fruit, washing with water,                cutting, peeling, and shredding; adding peel to                boiling water to passivate and destroy the activity                of polyphenol oxidase, taking out, cooling in cold                water, and carbon dioxide drying; putting to                container, adding citric acid, calcium chloride,                sodium chloride, phytic acid andchitosan, adding                distilled water, mixing, and subjecting to                anti-oxidation, anti-browning and fresh-keeping                treatment; adding passion fruit meat to juicer,                extracting juice, adding to container, adding                sucrose, glucose, honey and trehalose, and heating                while stirring; adding pretreated peel, stirring,                sealing, soaking while stirring, water bath                heating, and draining; and putting flatly on a                plate, carbon dioxide drying, baking, heating,                quickly cooling, UV light sterilizing, and vacuum                packaging.
   USE - Method for preparation of passion fruit                juice.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can passivate and prevent browning                of juice, increases taste of passion fruit, and                uses citric acid to resist oxidation, phytic acid                to protect vitamin C and prevent nutrient loss,                calcium chloride can to react with pectic acid on                cell wall to form calcium pectate which increases                tissue hardness and reduce the degree of browning,                and chitosan for preservation. The juice is rich in                nutrients, and has increased nutritional value, and                no harmful substance added and no harmful                treatment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of passion fruit juice                comprises:
   (A) selecting smooth, relatively heavy weight,                round and not rotten passion fruit, washing with                water, cutting, peeling, and shredding;
   (B) adding peel to boiling water for 5-7                minutes to passivate and destroy the activity of                polyphenol oxidase, taking out, cooling in cold                water at 7-13 degrees C, and carbon dioxide drying                to 30-40 degrees C;
   (C) putting to container, adding 0.3-0.7 wt.%                citric acid, 0.5-0.9 wt.% calcium chloride, 1.2-1.6                wt.% sodium chloride, 0.3-0.5 wt.% phytic acid and                0.5-0.7 wt.% chitosan, adding distilled water,                mixing, and subjecting to anti-oxidation,                anti-browning and fresh-keeping treatment for 3-5                hours;
   (D) adding passion fruit meat to juicer,                extracting juice, adding to container, adding 15-19                wt.% sucrose, 3-7 wt.% glucose, 2-6 wt.% honey and                5-7 wt.% trehalose, and heating to 130-150 degrees                C while stirring for 26-34 minutes;
   (E) adding pretreated peel, stirring at speed                of 100-140 revolutions/minute for 5-9 minutes,                sealing, soaking for 10-14 hours while stirring                once every 2 hours, water bath heating to 70-80                degrees C for 3-5 minutes, and draining; and
   (F) putting flatly on a plate, carbon dioxide                drying at 40-50 degrees C for 10-16 minutes, baking                to 50-60 degrees C for 20-30 minutes, heating to                65-75 degrees C until water content is 16-20%,                quickly cooling to room temperature by blowing, UV                light sterilizing, and vacuum packaging at                0.05-0.09 MPa.

   USE - Gas generating device for determining sulfur dioxide oxidation rate (claimed).

carbon dioxide, nitrogen, oxygen and sulfur dioxide, entering the mixed gas the reactor through the gas mixer, heating up the resistance furnace, adjusting the temperature range through the temperature control system, maintaining the temperature of the constant temperature water bath device at 80 degrees C, attaching to the tube wall by centrifugal force in the serpentine condenser tube, passing other gas components directly through the chamber, describing condensation tube to realize the separation of sulfur trioxide, passing the mixed gas through the condensation tube, entering two impingement gas collectors containing 3% hydrogen peroxide solution, absorbing sulfur dioxide, oxidizing to sulfate to collect in the impact gas collector in the hydrogen peroxide solution, turning off the gas other than nitrogen, kepeing for 1 minute, passing mixed gas in the reactor completely through the condensation tube, removing the reactor and connecting of the condensation tube, washing condensing tube multiple of times using deionized water, collecting sulfuric acid condensed in the condensing tube, making up to volume, collecting impact gas collector, absorbing sulfuroxide gas hydrogen peroxide solution and constant volume, adding two parts collected solution containing sulfate ions to isopropanol, making into an 80% isopropanol solution, adding 2-4 drops hydrazine reagent as an indicator, using a calibrated bismuth perchlorate solution, forming the excess cerium ion a complex with the cerium reagent, changing the solution from bright yellow to pink, indicating the sulfate ion titration complete, reaching titration end point, calculating the content of sulfur dioxide and sulfur trioxide in the flue gas and calculating conversion rate of sulfur dioxide.

The adsorbent converts into calcium                  carbonate/calcium aluminate composite after                  adsorbing carbon dioxide, which can be used as                  building materials and cement additives to                  achieve permanent carbon fixation effect.

AU ZHAO K
AE ANHUI ZHISHENTANG PHARM CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2022A5638U
AB    NOVELTY - Producing Ganoderma lucidum spore oil extract                comprises grinding the Ganoderma lucidum spore                powder that meets the quality standard and broken                through the wall breaking machine, placing the                broken-wall Ganoderma lucidum spore powder with a                wall-breaking rate &#8805; 90% into the mixer,                and adding purified water according to the weight                ratio of spore powder to purified water of                1:(1.15-1.18), fully stirring the input materials                to obtain the mixture, moving mixture to a transfer                tank for storage, and placing the mixture into a                granulator for granulation, and the particle size                of the particles is 13-14 mesh to obtain Ganoderma                lucidum spore powder particles, dropping the                Ganoderma lucidum spore powder particles into a                dryer and drying, placing the dried spore powder                particles into a cooler for cooling treatment,                placing the qualified particles into the extraction                kettle, and simultaneously feeding the mixture of                supercritical carbon dioxide fluid and carrier                agent.
   USE - The method is useful for producing Ganoderma                lucidum spore oil extract for nourishing and                protecting liver.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is energy-saving and                environmentally-friendly, can quickly recycle the                unqualified product, re-granulating, ensures the                extracting effect, can quickly inject the mixed                solution of carbon dioxide fluid and carrying, has                extracting efficiency, improves the content of                ganoderma total triterpene in the spore oil,                improves the oil yield of the spore powder, low                cost and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing Ganoderma lucidum spore oil extract                comprises (i) grinding the Ganoderma lucidum spore                powder that meets the quality standard and broken                through the wall breaking machine, the broken wall                Ganoderma lucidum spore powder with a wall breaking                rate &#8805; 90%, (ii) placing the broken-wall                Ganoderma lucidum spore powder with a wall-breaking                rate &#8805; 90% into the mixer, and adding                purified water according to the weight ratio of the                spore powder to purified water of 1:(1.15-1.18),                fully stirring the input materials to obtain the                mixture, the stirring time is 12-15 minutes, (iii)                moving the mixture to a transfer tank for storage,                and placing mixture into a granulator for                granulation, and the particle size of the particles                is 13-14 mesh to obtain Ganoderma lucidum spore                powder particles, (iv) dropping the Ganoderma                lucidum spore powder particles into a dryer and                drying, and the drying temperature is controlled at                70-80&#176;C, and the drying time is 7-8 hours,                and in the baking process, overturning 3-4 times to                avoid the moisture of spore powder not being able                to evaporate, resulting in the high acid value of                the extracted spore oil, the moisture content of                dried spore powder particles is &#8804; 6%, (v)                placing the dried spore powder particles into a                cooler for cooling treatment, the temperature after                cooling is controlled at 40-45&#176;C, and the                cooling time is 1-1.5 hours, (vi) placing the                cooled spore powder particles into the screening                machine, and the particles after screening are                controlled to be divided into two categories, one                is qualified products, the particle size is 13-14                mesh, and the other is unqualified products, the                particle size is &#8804; 13 mesh, the                unqualified products for granulation, and carrying                out the qualified products, (vii) placing the                qualified particles into the extraction kettle, and                simultaneously feeding the mixture of supercritical                carbon dioxide fluid and carrier agent, adjusting                the extraction kettle pressure to 31-33 MPa, and                the extraction kettle temperature 41-46&#176;C,                extraction flow: 600-650 minutes, extraction time                80-100 minutes, extracting the broken-wall                Ganoderma lucidum spore powder particles in the                extraction kettle to obtain Ganoderma lucidum spore                oil, and (viii) placing the extracted Ganoderma                lucidum spore oil into the still, the ratio of                1:(10-11) by the weight ratio of Ganoderma lucidum                spore oil and purified water, the temperature of                purified water is 100&#176;C, and uniformly                stirring, and the residual air in the still is                extracted, and the pressure in the still is made                between 1-0.5 PMa, after still being heated to                110-120&#176;C, the distillation time is 40-50                minutes, and evaporating the purified water, and                collecting Ganoderma lucidum spore oil.

PN CN114480282-A
TI Establishing oxidative stress response model for            neurodegenerative diseases caused by pro-inflammatory            cytokines comprises taking rat suckling mice as            example, anesthetizing, decapitating, mincing, adding            medium, centrifuging cell suspension, and adding Griess            I and II reagents.
AU DONG B
   HU J
   LI Q
   ZHANG H
   SONG C
   GUO L
AE ANNING FIRST PEOPLES HOSPITAL (ANNI-Non-standard)
GA 202277115G
AB    NOVELTY - Establishing oxidative stress response model                for neurodegenerative diseases caused by class of                pro-inflammatory cytokines comprises (a) taking rat                suckling mice as example, anesthetizing,                decapitating, mincing, adding medium to terminate                digestion, gently pipetting, culturing in a                37degrees Celsius, separating the microglia by                biological shaker, collecting and centrifuging cell                suspension, discarding supernatant, resuspending in                cell culture medium, and counting cells, (b)                observing concentration-dependent relationship, and                observing relationship between oxidative stress                response or inflammatory response, (c) adding                Griess I and II reagents, mixing, using a                microplate reader to detect absorbance value and                calculating NO concentration value, (d) extracting                total protein of cells, preparing sodium dodecyl                sulfate-polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis gels,                performing electrophoresis, and (e) using GraphPad                Prism 8 software for statistical analysis, and                processing images by Image J software.
   USE - The method is useful for establishing                oxidative stress response model for                neurodegenerative diseases caused by                pro-inflammatory cytokines.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Establishing oxidative stress response model                for neurodegenerative diseases caused by class of                pro-inflammatory cytokines (a) taking rat suckling                mice as an example, taking 2-3 days old SD rats,                anesthetizing, decapitating, taking the cerebral                cortex, mincing, incubating with 0.25% trypsin for                25 minutes, adding an equal amount of medium to                terminate the digestion, gently pipetting to form a                cell suspension, filtering with 100 mum and 40 mum                cell sieves respectively, centrifuging, discarding                the supernatant, adding medium to suspend the cells                and mix, inoculating into a T75 culture flask,                culturing in a 37degrees Celsius, 5% carbon dioxide                incubator for 7-10 days, separating the microglia                by a biological shaker when the mixed glial cells                are confluent, collecting and centrifuging the cell                suspension, discarding the supernatant,                resuspending in cell culture medium, counting the                cells to obtain a cell suspension of 2.5x105                cells/ml, inoculating 0.5 ml/well into a 24-well                cell culture plate, placing in a 37degreesC, 5%                carbon dioxide incubator for 30 minutes, changing                to medium containing 5 ng/ml M-CSF, continue to                incubate for about 24 hours in a 37degreesC, 5%                carbon dioxide incubator to start the experiment;                (b) observing the concentration-dependent                relationship for 16 hours, where the IL-1beta group                used 0-20 ng/ml IL-1beta, and the TNF-alpha group                used 0-200 ng/ml TNF-alpha, the IL-1beta single                group used a concentration of 10 ng/ml, or the                TNFalpha single group used a concentration of 100                ng/ml, and observing the relationship between                oxidative stress response or inflammatory response                for 6-36 hours; (c) incubating, aspirating the cell                culture medium, centrifuging to take the                supernatant, placing 50 mul supernatant sample,                standard solution, and blank control into a 96-well                plate, adding 50 mul of Griess I and II reagents,                mixing uniformly, allowing to stand at room                temperature for 10 minutes, using a microplate                reader to detect the absorbance value at a                wavelength of 560 nm, and calculating the NO                concentration value with the sodium nitrite                standard curve; (d) carefully removing the cell                supernatant at the end of the experiment, using                RIPA lysis buffer to lyse the cells, extracting the                total protein of the cells, using the BCA kit to                detect the protein concentration, and determining                the loading amount, preparing 5% and 10% sodium                dodecyl sulfate-polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis                gels and performing electrophoresis, transferring                membrane, and blocking with 1% bovine serum albumin                for 1 hour, where the primary antibody is iNOS                antibody, diluting iNOS antibody at 1:2000,                diluting beta-actin antibody and beta-actin                antibody at 1:2500, and incubating overnight at                4degreesC, washing the membrane 3 times with                Tris-Buffered Saline and Tween 20 (Polysorbate-20)                (TBST) solution in the next day, incubating the                secondary antibody corresponding to the primary                antibody for 2 hours at room temperature, washing                the membrane for 3 times with TBST solution,                performing immobilon Western chemiluminescent HRP                substrate for visualization and obtaining results                with the Bio-Rad ChemiDoc MP imaging system; and                (e) using GraphPad Prism 8 software for statistical                analysis, and processing images by Image J                software, expressing all data as mean plus minus                standard error mean plus minus SEM, using                independent samples t test for pairwise comparison,                and using one-way analysis of variance for                comparison between groups, where P less than 0.05                indicates a significant difference asteriskP less                than 0.05, asteriskasteriskP less than 0.01,                asteriskasteriskasteriskP less than 0.001,                asteriskasteriskasteriskasteriskP less than                0.0001.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  medium consisted of alpha-minimum essential                  medium or Dulbecco's Modified Eagle Medium, 10%                  fetal bovine serum and 1% penicillin-streptomycin                  and the pro-inflammatory cytokines are                  pro-inflammatory cytokines interleukin 1 beta and                  tumour necrosis factor alpha.Preferred Method: The step (ii) comprises                  taking Newborn 2-3d-old SD rats, anesthetizing,                  decapitating, taking the cerebral cortex,                  mincing, and digesting with 0.25% trypsin for 25                  minutes, and adding an equal amount of medium                  into terminate the digestion, gently pipetting to                  form a cell suspension, filtering with 100mum and                  40mum cell sieves, centrifuging, discarding the                  supernatant, adding medium to suspend the cells                  and mix, inoculating into a T75 culture flask,                  culturing in a 37degrees Celsius, 5% carbon                  dioxide incubator for 7-10 days, separating the                  microglial cells by a biological shaker when the                  mixed glial cells are confluent, collecting the                  cell suspension, centrifuging, discarding the                  supernatant, and resuspending in cell culture                  medium, counting the cells, obtaining                  2.5x105cells/ml cell suspension, inoculating                  0.5ml/well to a 24-well cell culture plate, and                  allowing to stand for 30 minutes in a 37degrees                  Celsius, 5% carbon dioxide incubator, changing to                  the medium containing 5ng/ml M-CSF, and                  incubating for 24 hours in a 37degrees Celsius,                  5% carbon dioxide incubator to carry out the                  experiment. The step (iv) comprises diluting iNOS                  antibody at 1:2000, diluting beta-actin antibody                  at 1:2500, and washing membrane with TBST                  solution for 15 minute/time.

AB    NOVELTY - An environment management system (100) comprises an interior area (102) surrounded by multiple lateral support structures (104), each of multiple support structures has a first surface and a second surface, and a top and a bottom; an opening (108); an exterior ceiling (106); a ceiling plenum area (112); an interior having interior ceiling first surface and interior ceiling second surface; a first contaminant resistant material on interior ceiling first surface; a second contaminant resistant material on exterior ceiling within ceiling plenum area; a ceiling vent; an interior floor coupled to the bottom of the multiple support structures; a multiple exterior vents (110) coupled between the interior area and exterior area; UV light treatment system in the interior area; an environment treatment unit (130) including an ionic filtration element and particle filtration element; heating unit (132) in fluid communication with the environment treatment unit; and temperature sensor.
   USE - An environment management system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system monitors and controls the various air quality and environmental parameters in an interior area. Removing contaminants from the air, such as volatile organic compounds, or carbon monoxide, may lead to improved performance and prevent damage to the lungs during increased levels of exertion. Both the ionic filtration element and the particle filtration element can be removed from the environment treatment unit to facilitate maintenance, such as cleaning.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An environment management system (100) comprises an interior area (102) surrounded by multiple lateral support structures (104), each of the multiple support structures has a first surface and a second surface, and a top and a bottom; an opening (108) in at least one of multiple support structures that provides access from the interior area to an exterior area; an exterior ceiling (106); a ceiling plenum area (112); an interior ceiling coupled to the top of multiple support structures, the interior ceiling has an interior ceiling first surface and an interior ceiling second surface opposite the interior ceiling first surface, the ceiling plenum area is between the interior ceiling and exterior ceiling; a first contaminant resistant material is on the interior ceiling first surface; a second contaminant resistant material is on the exterior ceiling within the ceiling plenum area; a ceiling vent coupled between the ceiling plenum area and interior area; an interior floor coupled to the bottom of the multiple support structures; a multiple exterior vents (110) coupled between the interior area and exterior area; UV light treatment system in the interior area; an environment treatment unit in the interior area in fluid communication with the exterior area, the environment treatment unit (130) including an ionic filtration element and particle filtration element; heating unit (132) in fluid communication with the environment treatment unit; temperature sensor coupled to the environment treatment unit; an air flow sensor coupled to the environment treatment unit; a humidity sensor coupled to the environment treatment unit; a carbon dioxide sensor coupled to the environment treatment unit; a nitrogen dioxide sensor coupled to the environment treatment unit; an air pressure sensor coupled to the environment treatment unit; an ozone sensor coupled to the environment treatment unit; a carbon monoxide sensor coupled to the environment treatment unit; a particulate matter sensor coupled to the environment treatment unit; a total concentration of volatile organic compounds (TVOC) sensor coupled to the environment treatment unit; a processing unit coupled to the temperature sensor, air flow sensor, humidity sensor, carbon dioxide sensor, nitrogen dioxide sensor, air pressure sensor, ozone sensor, carbon monoxide sensor, particulate matter sensor, TVOC sensor, the environment treatment unit, UV light treatment system, and the heating unit, the processing unit being configured to control the environment treatment unit and the heating unit; a user interface coupled to the processing unit; where the processing unit controls the environment treatment unit and heating unit based on the readings provided by the temperature sensor, air flow sensor, humidity sensor, carbon dioxide sensor, nitrogen dioxide sensor, air pressure sensor, ozone sensor, carbon monoxide sensor, particulate matter sensor, TVOC sensor.

TI High-efficiency underground coal synthesis gas surface treatment process used for mining thin coal beds, involves introducing synthesis gas from the product well into a gravity cyclone separator to separate solid impurities, followed by decarburizing, dehydrating, pressurizing and dehydrogenating.
AU SHI S
   TONG S
   ZHENG Z
   WANG M
   YIN Y
   SUN J
   DING F
   GAO J
AE SINOPEC ZHONGYUAN PETROLEUM EXPLORATION (SNPC-C)
GA 202210520H
AB    NOVELTY - High-efficiency underground coal synthesis gas surface treatment process involves passing the syngas from the product well into the gravity cyclone separator to separate the solid impurities and coal tar in the syngas. Adopting methyl diethanolamine decarburization device to absorb CO2 (carbon dioxide) in the raw material gas, and the CO2 content is removed to below 50 parts per million to obtain decarburized gas. Methyl diethanolamine decarburization device in step 2 generates acid gas after absorbing CO2 in the feed gas, and adopts a CO2 advanced treatment device to remove and recover the sulfur in the acid gas. Pressurizing the decarburized gas obtained in the second step to 5.4 megapascal by a booster compressor. Dehydrating the pressurized gas by a two-tower isobaric molecular sieve dehydration process. When the dehydrated gas is cooled to -136 degreesC, it is led out of the main cold box and enters the dehydrogenation tower to separate the mixed gas containing hydrogen.
   USE - High-efficiency underground coal synthesis gas surface treatment process used for mining thin coal beds, deep coal beds, coal pressing under the third coal and coal beds with high sulfur, high ash and high gas.
   ADVANTAGE - High-efficiency underground coal synthesis gas surface treatment process has the advantages of low energy consumption, high utilization rate of the whole resource and remarkable economic benefit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High-efficiency underground coal synthesis gas surface treatment process involves passing the syngas from the product well into the gravity cyclone separator to separate the solid impurities and coal tar in the syngas, and then passing into the water washing tower to wash the coal tar and dusting by spraying water at the top of the tower to obtain the raw material gas, after washing with water, controlling the liquid phase temperature at the bottom of the tower to be 105 degreesC, the temperature of the feed gas at the top of the tower to be 45 degreesC, and the saturated water content of the feed gas to be reduced to 0.3%. Adopting methyl diethanolamine decarburization device to absorb CO2 (carbon dioxide) in the raw material gas, and the CO2 content is removed to below 50 parts per million to obtain decarburized gas. Methyl diethanolamine decarburization device in step 2 generates acid gas after absorbing CO2 in the feed gas, and adopts a CO2 advanced treatment device to remove and recover the sulfur in the acid gas, and liquefy the CO2 into liquid carbon dioxide. Pressurizing the decarburized gas obtained in the second step to 5.4 megapascal by a booster compressor to obtain a pressurized gas. Dehydrating the pressurized gas by a two-tower isobaric molecular sieve dehydration process. Passing pressurized gas into the top of the drying tower, and then flows out from the bottom of the drying tower after adsorption and dehydration through molecular sieves, and the water content in the pressurized gas is removed to less than 0.1 parts per million get dehydrated gas. The dehydrated gas enters the main cold box and flows downward, the refrigeration unit of the main cold box adopts a closed refrigeration cycle. When the dehydrated gas is cooled to -136 degreesC, it is led out of the main cold box and enters the dehydrogenation tower to separate the mixed gas containing hydrogen to obtain natural gas, the natural gas flows out from the bottom of the dehydrogenation tower and then enters the bottom of the main cold box to be cooled to -162 degreesC to obtain supercooled liquefied natural gas (LNG), which enters the storage tank after throttling and expansion. The mixed gas at the top of the dehydrogenation tower enters the cold box of the dehydrogenation tower and then passes into the reflux tank of the dehydrogenation tower. The bottom of the dehydrogenation tower reflux tank is connected to the dehydrogenation tower for reflux. The hydrogen component gas at the top of the reflux tank of the dehydrogenation tower enters the main cold box and heating to 20 degreesC.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a dedusting and degreasing flow chart of a high-efficiency underground coal synthetic gas. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Sewage cooler (AC5)
   Washing tower (T1)
   Gravity cyclone separator (V1)
   Three-phase separator (V6)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Methyl diethanolamine (MDEA) solution in the second step is composed of 31.48 wt.% MDEA, 59.86 wt.% water, and 8.66 wt.% piperazine. The refrigerant is a mixture consisting of 30% nitrogen, 24% methane, 12% propane, 19% ethylene, and 15% isopentane. Preferred Method: Sewage generated after the filtration of the washing tower is passed into the three-phase separator, and separating the sewage by the three-phase separator is cooled by the sewage cooler and then transporting back to the upper part of the washing tower by the pumping pump. The feed gas is passed into the bottom of the carbon dioxide (CO2) absorption tower, and the MDEA solution flows from the top of the tower to the bottom of the tower to absorb the feed gas. The MDEA solution after absorbing CO2 passes through the amine regeneration tower to absorb CO2 to regenerate MDEA. The amine regeneration tower in step 2 generates acid gas after regenerating MDEA, and the acid gas is passed into the CO2 advanced treatment device to collect liquid carbon dioxide after multi-stage pressurization, desulfurization, dehydration and mercury removal, and fractionation recovery in sequence. The acid gas is pressurized to 3.6 megapascal by multiple compressors, and then preheated and reacted with air heated at about 200 degreesC in the reactor to obtain reaction gas. The sulfur is separated by the sulfur separator and enters the sulfur forming and packaging equipment to obtain the sulfur product. The reaction gas is discharged from the top of the sulfur separator after passing through the sulfur agglomerator and the mist eliminator, and then passes into the molecular sieve drying tower and the dehydration tower in turn. Mercury, after dehydration and mercury removal, is passed into the demethanizer for fractionation. The overhead gas at the top of the demethanizer is condensed by a carbon dioxide cold box and then refluxed into the demethanizer. After reflux, it is reboiled at the bottom of the demethanizer. It is reboiled in the device, and then liquefied by the carbon dioxide cold box and then enters the product storage unit. The refrigeration unit of the main cold box adopts a closed refrigeration cycle, the refrigeration temperature range of the refrigeration unit is -149-177 degrees Celsius. The low-pressure refrigerant from the top of the main cold box, after being compressed by the first stage of the first refrigerant compressor, enters the interstage cooler of the refrigerant compressor, and the cooled refrigerant enters the interstage tank for gas-liquid separation to obtain the first stage. The first-stage gas-phase refrigerant and the first-stage liquid-phase refrigerant, the first-stage gas-phase refrigerant is pressurized to 3.63 megapascal (MPa) by the second refrigerant compressor, then cooled in the refrigerant condenser, and enters the refrigerant outlet separator for gas-liquid separation obtain the second-stage gas phase refrigerant and the second-stage liquid-phase refrigerant, respectively enter the main cold box, and mixing with the first-stage liquid-phase refrigerant in the second mixer after being mixed by the first mixer in the main cold box , and then passing into the first refrigerant compressor from the top of the main cold box to form a cycle.

PN CN112645364-A
TI Producing lithium carbonate by mixing spodumene and lithium polymer comprises mixing spodumene and lithium polymer, calcining, acid addition reaction, cooling, adding calcium carbonate slurry, filtering, removing impurities and causticizing.
AU HE K
   HE D
   WU Z
   WANG L
   XU X
   DAI D
   JIANG Y
   WANG X
AE GUANGXI TIANYUAN NEW ENERGY MATERIALS CO (FCET-C)
GA 2021435955
AB    NOVELTY - Producing lithium carbonate by mixing spodumene and lithium polymer comprises (i) mixing spodumene and lithium polymer in weight ratio of 1-3:1, calcining, cooling, fine grinding, acid addition reaction, and cooling the acid reaction product to less than or equal to 90 degrees C, adjusting into a slurry with water, where the solid content of the slurry is 10-70%; (ii) adding calcium carbonate slurry into the obtained slurry for stirring and leaching, where the temperature in the leaching tank is less than or equal to 60 degrees C and pH is 5.5-6, adding calcium oxide to adjust the pH to 8-9, and press-filtering by filter, purifying the filtered clear liquid with lithium hydroxide mother liquor or calcium oxide, adjusting the pH to 9-12, and removing impurities iron, manganese, aluminum and calcium in the filtered clear liquid; (iii) filtering the clear liquid in again to obtain a purified liquid and a purified filter residue, causticizing purified liquid with 50% alkaline solution or crude mother liquor.
   USE - The method is useful for producing lithium carbonate by mixing spodumene and lithium polymer.
   ADVANTAGE - The lithium carbonate has high grade and low impurity content, is mineral source with great extraction value, has great economic benefits and avoids the plight of insufficient spodumene resources.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing lithium carbonate by mixing spodumene and lithium polymer comprises (i) mixing spodumene and lithium polymer in weight ratio of 1-3:1, calcining, cooling, fine grinding, acid addition reaction, and cooling the acid reaction product to less than or equal to 90 degrees C, adjusting into a slurry with water, where the solid content of the slurry is 10-70%; (ii) adding calcium carbonate slurry into the obtained slurry for stirring and leaching, where the temperature in the leaching tank is less than or equal to 60 degrees C and pH is 5.5-6, adding calcium oxide to adjust the pH to 8-9, and press-filtering by filter, purifying the filtered clear liquid with lithium hydroxide mother liquor or calcium oxide, adjusting the pH to 9-12, and removing impurities iron, manganese, aluminum and calcium in the filtered clear liquid; (iii) filtering the clear liquid in again to obtain a purified liquid and a purified filter residue, causticizing purified liquid with 50% alkaline solution or crude mother liquor, where the pH of the causticized solution is 11-14, and the temperature is room temperature; (iv) filtering the solution after causticization to obtain causticizing liquid and causticizing filter residue, controlling the equivalent content of lithium hydroxide in the causticizing liquid within 30-75 g/l, processing the causticizing liquid through a precision filter and filtering to remove part of calcium ions; (v) sending filtered causticizing liquid to the freezing workshop and freezing at -5 to -20 degrees C tp separate sodium sulfate decahydrate and lithium hydroxide solution; (vi) purifying sodium sulfate decahydrate by evaporation and concentration, and taking crystal water out by heating to obtain anhydrous sodium sulfate, heating to 200-800 degrees C, filtering lithium hydroxide solution through a precision filter to remove part of the calcium ions, and evaporating and concentrating, crystallizing concentrated crude lithium hydroxide solution, centrifuging, and re-filtering by hot-melt and precision filter, and evaporating and concentrating by mechanical vapor recompression to obtain high-quality lithium hydroxide slurry; (vii) centrifugally separating and dissolving the refined lithium hydroxide slurry, and entering the carbonization kettle, and passing carbon dioxide gas with a pressure of 0.5 MPa for carbonization reaction, and keeping the gauge pressure in the carbonization kettle at 0.06-0.08MPa and temperature at 48-52 degrees C, after the reaction is completed, when the pressure is stable, stopping the introduction of carbon dioxide, and opening the vent valve of the carbonization kettle to vent the excess carbon dioxide to obtain the carbonization reaction liquid; (viii) centrifuging and separating carbonization reaction liquid to obtain solid lithium carbonate, and drying until the moisture content of the solid lithium carbonate is less than or equal to 5%; (ix) transporting centrifugal solid phase to the disc dryer for drying through a closed conveyor, pneumatically conveying dried materials to the lithium carbonate finished product cabin, and conveying materials from the disc dryer to the raw material flow dividing system, through the airflow crushing system, automatic packaging system, palletizing, and entering the finished product warehouse, where the controlled magnetic substance of the finished product not greater than 30 PPb.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), during calcination, the calcination temperature is 950-1200 degrees C, and the granularity of fine grinding is 200 meshes, when acid is added for reaction, 98% sulfuric acid is added with ratio of acid and material of 2-4:1, and after acid reaction, cooling to less than 60 degrees C by water cooling. In the step (ii), the filter cake filtered by the filter press is rinsed with tap water or process water, and the filter cake is purged with compressed air to make the moisture content of the filter cake to be less than or equal to 20%, and the rinsing water is returned for the slurry adjustment in step (i) and when purifying, if the lithium hydroxide mother liquor is added, the concentration of the lithium hydroxide mother liquor is 10-50%, and if calcium oxide is added, the calcium oxide is a powder with a mass fraction of more than 75 wt.% or a calcium oxide slurry with a solid content of 10-55%. In the step (iii), the purified filter residue is mixed with water to form a slurry with a solid content of 10-70%, and returned to step (i). In the step (iv), after adding water into the causticizing filter residue to adjust into a slurry with a solid content of 10-70%, return to the purification process of step (ii). In the step (viii), the centrifugal liquid obtained by centrifugal separation contains a small amount of lithium carbonate, and the centrifugal liquid is pumped back to the slurrying process of the lithium sulfate production section and the finished lithium sulfate solution is the lithium sulfate solution obtained by filtering out the filter residue in step (i) after slurry adjustment. In the step (ix), the moisture content of the material after drying is less than 0.2%. Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the solid content of the calcium carbonate slurry is 10-55%; and the calcium oxide added to adjust the pH value is a powder with a mass fraction of more than 75 wt.% or a slurry with a solid content of 10-55%. In the step (iii), the alkaline solution is a mixed solution of sodium hydroxide solution and/or lithium hydroxide solution.

PN CN111909424-A; CN111909424-B
TI Preparing modified nano-calcium carbonate for            preparing silicone rubber by mixing Chlorella protein            and deionized water, adding citric acid and protease,            heating, mixing calcium oxide, carbonizing, filtering            and adding modifier and stirring.
AU WEI M
AE GUANGXI XIAYANG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (GUAN-Non-standard)
   DUAN CHUNXU NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (DUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020B40635
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing modified nano-calcium                carbonate, involves (i) taking Chlorella protein                and deionized water, uniformly mixing, adding                citric acid to adjust pH to 5-6, adding protease,                and heating to obtain a mixed solution (A), (ii)                stirring and mixing high-purity calcium oxide and                deionized water to obtain a slurry (A), filtering                slurry (A), adding (13-15) times the mass of slurry                (A) with deionized water to obtain a slurry (B),                adding the mixed solution (A) obtained in step (i)                to the slurry (B), heating and stirring to obtain a                slurry (C), (iii) adding aqueous ammonia,                introducing carbon dioxide to carry out                carbonization reaction, filtering slurry to obtain                a precipitate, drying precipitate and crushing to                obtain nano-calcium carbonate powder, and (iv)                adding nano-calcium carbonate powder to a                high-speed mixer, stirring and heating, adding                modifier and continuously stirring.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing modified                nano-calcium carbonate for preparing silicone                rubber (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The calcium carbonate has excellent                dispersibility and compatibility with rubber sizing                materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing modified nano-calcium                carbonate for rubber, involves (i) taking Chlorella                protein and deionized water at a mass ratio of                1:(2-4), uniformly mixing, adding citric acid to                adjust the pH to 5-6, adding protease, and heating                at 50-60 degrees C under stirring conditions for                2-3 hours to obtain a mixed solution (A), where the                ratio of the added amount of the protease and the                mass of Chlorella protein is 1200-1500 U:1 g, (ii)                stirring and mixing high-purity calcium oxide and                deionized water at a mass ratio of 1:(3-5) to                obtain a slurry (A), filtering the slurry (A)                through a 100 mesh sieve, adding (13-15) times the                mass of slurry (A) to dilute the slurry (A) with                deionized water to obtain a slurry (B), adding the                mixed solution (A) obtained in step (i) to the                slurry (B), heating at 80-90 degrees C and stirring                at constant temperature for 1-2 hours to obtain a                slurry (C), where the ratio of the added amount of                the mixed solution (A) and the mass of slurry (A)                is (5-8):100, (iii) adding aqueous ammonia to the                slurry (C) obtained in step (ii) to adjust the pH                to 7-8, introducing carbon dioxide to carry out the                carbonization reaction, controlling the                carbonization temperature at 80-90 degrees C and                the flow rate of carbon dioxide gas at 100-150                l/hour, carbonizing for 1-2 hours, filtering the                slurry obtained after carbonization to obtain a                precipitate, drying the precipitate at 95-100                degrees C for 1-2 hours and crushing to obtain                nano-calcium carbonate powder, and (iv) adding the                nano-calcium carbonate powder obtained in step                (iii) to a high-speed mixer, stirring and heating                at a constant temperature of 100-110 degrees C,                adding modifier and continuously stirring for 1-2                hours to obtain the modified nano-calcium                carbonate, where the added amount of the modifier                is 1-3% of the mass of the nano-calcium carbonate,                and the modifier is obtained by mixing stearic                acid, ethanol and polyvinylpyrrolidone at a mass                ratio of 1:3:0.5. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included                for a method for preparing a silicone rubber, which                involves (a) taking 65-80 parts mass silicone                rubber base rubber, 5-10 parts mass methyl silicone                oil, 15-25 parts mass modified nano calcium                carbonate, 2-5 parts mass silane coupling agent,                3-8 parts mass nano-titanium dioxide and 1-2 parts                mass 1,2-dibromo-3-chloropropane (DCBP) vulcanizing                agent, and (b) mixing the vulcanized silicone                rubber on a two-roll rubber mixer for 5-10 minutes,                sequentially adding methyl silicone oil, modified                nano-calcium carbonate, silane coupling agent,                nano-titanium dioxide and DCBP vulcanizing agent,                continuously mixing for 40-50 minutes, rolling and                unloading, placing for 20-24 hours, performing                primary vulcanization on a 170-180 degrees C flat                vulcanizer for 5-10 minutes, performing compression                molding at 15 MPa, performing secondary                vulcanization in a high-temperature oven at 200-210                degrees C for 3-4 hours, cooling and demolding to                obtain the silicone rubber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves after                  cooling and demolding in step (ii), leaving still                  for 24-36 hours to obtain the silicone rubber.                  Preferred Conditions: In step (ii), the stirring                  is carried out at a speed of 300-400 rpm. In step                  (iv), the stirring is carried out at a speed of                  800-900 rpm. In step (b), the cooling rate is                  maintained at 12-15 degrees C/minute. Preferred                  Property: The purity of the high-purity calcium                  oxide is more than 99.5%.
