TI Shale reservoir rock core carbon dioxide            throughput experiment method, involves injecting carbon            dioxide into saturated oil rock core under confining            pressure to make carbon dioxide wrap around rock core            along outer wall of rock core.
AU ZHANG Y
   LUO Y
   LU H
   ZHANG H
   XU H
   YANG Z
   GUO H
   LI H
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 2022B4996T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves performing the simulated                swallowing in the simulated huff and puff process.                A carbon dioxide is injected into the saturated oil                core under the confining pressure, so that the                carbon dioxide is wrapped around the core along the                outer wall of the core and then enters the core.                The outlet of the core holder is closed or controls                the outlet pressure of the core holder to be higher                than the inlet pressure, injecting carbon dioxide                into the core holder at the inlet pressure under                the confining pressure holding until the inlet                pressure is increased to the set inlet                pressure.
   USE - Shale reservoir rock core carbon dioxide                throughput experiment method used in shale oil                exploration potential evaluation, enrichment rule                research and geologic characteristic                research.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can effectively avoid the                simulation in the process of removing and spitting                process is helpful for carbon dioxide throughput                mechanism research, providing support for shale oil                reservoir carbon dioxide development and can                effectively realize the huff-puff medium slowly                fully contact with the rock core, avoiding                generation of displacement in the experiment                process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a shale                reservoir rock core carbon dioxide spitting                experiment device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                the shale reservoir rock core carbon dioxide                throughput experiment method. (Drawing includes                non-English language text).
   S1Step for saturating a shale oil reservoir                core with oil
   S2Step for performing gas displacement on the                core
   S3Step for handling cores in a core                feeder
   S31Step for closing the outlet of the core                holder or controlling the pressure of the outlet of                the core holder to be higher than the pressure of                the inlet
   S32Step for keeping the outlet pressure lower                than the displacement pressure

PN CN113662154-A
TI Betel nut fiber expansion softening process based on liquid carbon dioxide phase change puffing technology comprises dipping betel nut seed in dipping pot with liquid carbon dioxide and filling gaseous carbon dioxide into steeping tank to raise pressure in tank.
AU FU X
   BIN H
AE HUNAN WUZICHUN FOOD CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2021E1110C
AB    NOVELTY - Betel nut fiber expansion softening process based on liquid carbon dioxide phase change puffing technology comprises (i) dipping the betel nut seed in the dipping pot with liquid carbon dioxide; (ii) filling gaseous carbon dioxide into the steeping tank to raise the pressure in the tank to 0.1-2.8 MPa; (iii) keeping the pressure stable under condition of dipping the areca seed for 10-90 minutes, recycling the liquid and gaseous carbon dioxide, reducing the pressure in the tank to 0.034-0.15 MPa, and solidifying the liquid carbon dioxide absorbed in the areca seed into dry ice; and (iv) contacting out heat exchange for betel nut seed containing dry ice and high temperature air flow in a short time, quickly vaporizing carbon dioxide in the areca seed, and expanding and softening the fiber structure of the betel nut with the sublimation of carbon dioxide as gas.
   USE - The method is useful for betel nut fiber expansion softening process based on liquid carbon dioxide phase change puffing technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, safe and convenient, and has no pollution, and high expansion rate and eliminates the seed soaking working procedure of the traditional betel nut processing process, improves the efficiency of fermentation production, and improves the problems caused by the traditional high-temperature and high-pressure processing process of the betel nut as damage of the structure and chemical composition of betel nut, the difficulty of fragrance retention of the product, short product tail, shortens the process time, and improves the product quality while the production cost is reduced.

PN US2020282412-A1
TI Apparatus for producing electrostatically charged and homogeneous carbon dioxide composite spray, has adjustable expansion tube assembly that is comprised of two capillaries which are adjustable within center through hole.
AU JACKSON D P
AE JACKSON D P (JACK-Individual)
GA 202088446R
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has nozzle electrodes that is comprised of an elongated structure with a nozzle tip with a center through hole. Three through ports form three landing guides for centering and positioning an adjustable expansion tube assembly. The adjustable expansion tube assembly comprises a first capillary within a second capillary. The first and the second capillaries are adjustable within the center through hole. The air-additive aerosol and the air-carbon dioxide aerosol combine away from the nozzles to form the electrostatically charged air-additive-carbon dioxide aerosol, which is projected at the substrate surface. The carbon dioxide particles and the additive interact to form the electrostatically charged and homogeneous carbon dioxide composite spray containing an additive mixture in the space between the nozzles and the substrate surface. The electrostatically charged and homogeneous carbon dioxide composite spray containing an additive is projected at the substrate surface.
   USE - Apparatus for producing electrostatically charged and homogeneous carbon dioxide composite spray.
   ADVANTAGE - The novel cluster spray arrangement with electrostatic field and velocity driven gradients for mixing additive and carbon dioxide particles, and induced airflow to assist composite spray propulsion and delivery enables the formation of virtually any variety of carbon dioxide composite fluid spray compositions. The injection pressure, propellant pressure and propellant temperature are decreased as needed to facilitate the formation and maintenance of a uniform pretreatment coating.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a nozzle electrode apparatus for producing electrostatic field.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a electrostatic field generating carbon dioxide composite spray nozzles, additive injector nozzle and axis-symmetric clustering arrangement of same to form a passively charging carbon dioxide composite spray apparatus.

   Capillary tube (116)
   Additive (120)
   Grounding wire (130)

   KWAK N S
   LEE I Y
   LEE D W
AE KOREA ELECTRIC POWER CORP (KEPC-C)
GA 2018138215
AB    NOVELTY - The device (100) has an absorption reactor (200) for absorbing carbon dioxide with an absorbent from exhaust gas. A regeneration reactor (300) is provided with the absorbent for absorbing the carbon dioxide from the absorption reactor. The regeneration reactor separates the absorbent and the carbon dioxide to recycle the absorbent. The absorption reactor includes a first rotating part (230) reacting absorbent to generate the exhaust gas by centrifugal force. The regeneration reactor comprises a second rotating part (330) to separate the centrifugal force by rotation of the absorbent reactor.
   USE - Device for processing carbon dioxide ejected from an engine of a large ship e.g. container ship and oil-tanker, to an atmosphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The device increases carbon dioxide separation efficiency by the absorption reactor and the regeneration reactor with the first rotating part and the second rotating part so as to maintain height of the regeneration reactor and the absorption reactor, so that removal ratio of the obtained carbon dioxide can be secured. The device reduces the height of the regeneration reactor and absorption reactor so as to require less space for a carbon dioxide collection hole, so that a spatial restriction problem and an installation fee problem can be resolved. The device utilizes thermal energy of the exhaust gas of high temperature ejected in an engine of a ship so as to avoid additional heating source for a salvage process, so that management cost for the carbon dioxide depositing process can be reduced.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a circuit diagram of a device for processing carbon dioxide ejected from an engine of a large ship to an atmosphere.

   Absorption reactor (200)
   Rotating parts (230, 330)

PT P
PN KR1955693-B1; WO2019156415-A1
TI Composite battery system comprises an aqueous secondary battery which is discharged by using carbon dioxide and hydrogen generated in discharge process, a reformer, a fuel cell, and a carbon dioxide supply unit.
AU KIM G T
   KIM C M
   KIM J W
   SHIN J Y
   KIM G
   KIM C
   KIM J
   SHIN J
AE UNIST ULSAN NAT SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (ULSA-C)
   UNIV SOOKMYUNG WOMENS IND ACAD COOP (UYSM-C)
GA 2019232896
AB    NOVELTY - Composite battery system comprises either: an aqueous secondary battery (100) in which carbon dioxide is used as raw material for discharging and hydrogen generated during discharging, a reformer for producing hydrogen-rich reformed gas from hydrogen-containing fuel and generating carbon dioxide as by-product, a fuel cell supplied with the reformed gas produced from reformer as fuel, and a carbon dioxide supply unit for supplying carbon dioxide generated in reformer to aqueous secondary battery, and supplying reforming gas produced from reformer with fuel; or an aqueous secondary battery which is discharged by using carbon dioxide as raw material and hydrogen generated in discharge process, a reformer for producing hydrogen-rich reformed gas from the hydrogen-containing fuel, a fuel cell supplied with reformed gas produced from reformer as fuel, and a hydrogen supply unit for supplying hydrogen generated in the water-based secondary battery to the fuel of the fuel cell.
   USE - Used as composite battery system.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite battery system generates hydrogen and sodium bicarbonate to generate electricity in the aqueous electrolyte by migrating sodium ions through the electrolyte at anode electrode and reacts with carbon dioxide, utilizes carbon dioxide which is greenhouse gas as fuel, and can produce sodium hydrogen carbonate while using carbon dioxide as fuel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Composite battery system comprises either: an aqueous secondary battery (100) which is discharged by using carbon dioxide as raw material and hydrogen generated in discharge process, a reformer for producing hydrogen-rich reformed gas from the hydrogen-containing fuel and generating carbon dioxide as a by-product, a fuel cell supplied with the reformed gas produced from the reformer as fuel, and a carbon dioxide supply unit for supplying carbon dioxide generated in the reformer to aqueous secondary battery, and supplying the reforming gas produced from the reformer with fuel; or an aqueous secondary battery in which carbon dioxide is used as a raw material for discharging and hydrogen is generated during discharging, a reformer for producing a hydrogen-rich reformed gas from the hydrogen-containing fuel, a fuel cell supplied with the reformed gas produced from the reformer as fuel, and a hydrogen supply unit for supplying hydrogen generated in the water-based secondary battery to the fuel of the fuel cell; or an aqueous secondary battery in which carbon dioxide is used as a raw material for discharging and hydrogen is generated during discharging, a reformer for producing hydrogen-rich reformed gas from the hydrogen-containing fuel and generating carbon dioxide as a by-product, a fuel cell supplied with the reformed gas produced from the reformer as fuel, a carbon dioxide supply unit for supplying carbon dioxide generated in the reformer to the water-based secondary battery, and a hydrogen supply unit for supplying hydrogen generated in the water-based secondary battery to the fuel of the fuel cell. The aqueous secondary battery comprises a cathode part (110) having an aqueous electrolyte contained in the first accommodation space and a cathode impregnated in the aqueous electrolyte; an anode part (120) containing an organic electrolyte contained in a second accommodation space, and an anode impregnated with the organic electrolyte and containing sodium metal; and a solid electrolyte arranged to selectively pass sodium ions between the cathode part and the anode part. In the composite battery system, a chemical reaction of carbon dioxide and water (CO2(g)+H2O(l) = HCO3-(aq) + H+(aq)) occurs in the cathode part by the carbon dioxide supplied from the outside during the discharge; an electrical reaction of hydrogen iron with elections (2H+(aq)+2e- = H2(g)) occurs at the cathode part during the discharge; and an electrical reaction of sodium (2Na(s) = 2Na+(aq)+2e-) occurs at the anode part during the discharge, where over all reaction at the time of discharge occurs is (2Na(s) + 2CO2(g) + 2H2O(l) = 2NaHCO3(aq) + H2(g)).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a discharge process of aqueous secondary battery.
   Aqueous secondary battery (100)
   Cathode part (110)
   Inlet (112)
   Outlet (113)
   Anode part (120)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRONICS - Preferred Components: In the composite battery system, electrolysis reaction of water (2H2O(l) = O2(g) + 4H+(aq) + 4e-) occurs at the cathode part during charging, chemical reaction of sodium (Na+(aq)+ e- = Na(s)) occurs at the anode part at the time of charging, and overall reaction at the time of charging takes place is (4Na+(aq) + 2H2O(l) = O2(g) + 4H+(aq)+4Na(s)) when the aqueous electrolyte is neutral electrolyte; reaction of (4OH-(aq) = O2(g) + 2H2O(l) + 4e-) occurs at the cathode part during the charging, reaction of (Na+(aq)+ e- = Na(s)) occurs at the anode part during the charging, and overall reaction upon charging occurs as (4Na+(aq) + 4OH-(aq) = O2(g) + 2H2O(l) + 4Na(s)) when the aqueous electrolyte is basic electrolyte; and reaction upon charging occurs as (4OH-(aq) = O2(g) + 2H2O(l) + 4e-) and (2Cl-(aq)= Cl2(g)+2e-) at the cathode part, reaction upon charging occurs as (Na+ (aq)+ e- = Na(s)) at the anode part, and overall reaction upon charging occurs as (4Na+(aq) + 4OH-(aq) = O2(g) + 2H2O(l) + 4Na(s)) and (2Na+(aq) + 2Cl-(aq) = Cl2(g) + 2Na(s)) when the aqueous electrolyte is seawater. The cathode includes an inlet (112) through which carbon dioxide is supplied to the first containing space and an outlet (113) through which hydrogen generated in a discharge process is discharged. The cathode is provided with a catalyst for selectively reducing the carbon dioxide during discharge, where at least one of platinum, carbon, and graphene is used as the catalyst. In the cathode, iridium, ruthenium oxide or perovskite oxide catalyst is used for an oxygen generating reaction (OER). The solid electrolyte is nasicon-based electrode material (Na3Zr2Si2PO12). The reformer is a methane-steam reformer that produces hydrogen (H2) by the reforming reaction of methane (CH4) and water vapor (H2O). The composite battery system further comprises a hydrogen supply unit for supplying hydrogen generated in the aqueous secondary battery to the fuel of the fuel cell.

PN WO2018018137-A1; CA3031837-A1; AU2017301227-A1; KR2019033600-A; IN201937003756-A; CN109790044-A; EP3490935-A1; BR112019001524-A2; US2019270647-A1; JP2019525887-W; EP3490935-A4; MX2019001129-A1; AU2017301227-B2; AU2022204541-A1; US11401168-B2; US2022332596-A1
TI Producing magnesia, comprises contacting carbon            dioxide-containing emissions with magnesium-containing            material, subjecting obtained magnesium carbonate to            calcination to give carbon dioxide by-product and            magnesia, and recycling.
AU MERCIER G
   PASQUIER L
   KEMACHE N
   CECCHI E
   BLAIS J
   PASQUIER L C
   BLAIS J F
AE INST NAT RECH SCI (NARE-Non-standard)
   INST NAT RECH SCI (NARE-Non-standard)
   INST NAT RECH SCI (NARE-Non-standard)
   NAT RECH SCI INST (NARE-Non-standard)
   INST NAT RECH SCI (NARE-Non-standard)
GA 201809059N
AB    NOVELTY - Producing magnesia, comprises either                contacting carbon dioxide-containing emissions or                carbon dioxide-containing gas with a                magnesium-containing material to produce magnesium                carbonate, subjecting the magnesium carbonate to                calcination to produce a carbon dioxide by-product                and magnesia, and recycling at least a portion of                the carbon dioxide by-product or as at least part                of the carbon dioxide-containing gas for contacting                the magnesium-containing material to produce the                magnesium carbonate.
   USE - The processes are useful for producing                magnesia.
   ADVANTAGE - The processes: utilize the carbon dioxide from                serpentinite, phyllosilicate, precipitated                magnesium carbonate derived from sequestration of                carbon dioxide emissions by a magnesium-containing                material or precipitated nesquehonite or carbon                dioxide emitted from industrial sites that can be                recycled in a cyclical process and not released                into the atmosphere, thus providing magnesia having                a low carbon footprint, and high-purity magnesia                product, hence eco-friendly and efficient; enable                increased economic viability by providing profit or                a cost of 0-10% of the value of the produced                energy; and provide valuable alkaline earth metal                carbonates from an alkaline earth metal containing                material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) producing magnesia, comprising contacting                a carbon dioxide-containing gas with a                magnesium-containing material in an aqueous slurry                (4), recovering precipitated magnesium carbonate                from the aqueous slurry, and subjecting the                precipitated magnesium carbonate to calcination to                produce a carbon dioxide by-product and                magnesia;
   (2) a magnesia production system, comprising                at least one carbonation unit (10) having a gas                inlet for receiving the carbon dioxide containing                gas (2), a slurry inlet for receiving an aqueous                slurry comprising an magnesium- containing                material, a carbonation vessel for the carbonation                of at least a portion of the magnesium of the                aqueous slurry with carbon dioxide to produce a                carbon dioxide depleted gas and a carbonate loaded                slurry comprising precipitable magnesium                carbonates, a slurry outlet for releasing the                carbonate loaded slurry comprising precipitable                magnesium carbonates, and a gas outlet for                releasing the carbon dioxide depleted gas, a                separation unit (20) comprising a slurry inlet for                receiving the carbonate loaded slurry comprising                precipitable magnesium carbonates from the slurry                outlet of the at least one carbonation unit, a                separation chamber for separation of the carbonate                loaded slurry comprising precipitable magnesium                carbonates into a solid phase and an aqueous phase                comprising the precipitable magnesium carbonates,                and a liquid outlet for releasing the aqueous                phase, and a precipitation unit (32) having a                slurry inlet for receiving the aqueous phase                comprising the precipitable magnesium carbonates                from the liquid outlet of the separation unit, a                precipitation chamber for precipitation of the                magnesium carbonates and formation of a                precipitation slurry, and a liquid outlet for                releasing the precipitation slurry, and a                calcination unit for receiving at least a portion                of the magnesium carbonates, producing a calcined                magnesia material and a carbon dioxide by-product                stream;
   (3) use of a phyllosilicate mining residue to                sequestrate carbon dioxide from industrial                emissions and produce magnesia;
   (4) use of precipitated magnesium carbonate                derived from sequestration of carbon dioxide                emissions by a magnesium-containing material for                production of magnesia via calcinations;
   (5) use of precipitated nesquehonite for                production of magnesia via calcinations;
   (6) producing magnesia, comprising supplying                magnesium carbonate consisting of precipitated                nesquehonite to a calcination unit and calcining                the precipitated nesquehonite to produce magnesia;                and
   (7) a magnesia product produced by calcination                of a precipitated magnesium carbonate derived from                contacting a magnesium-containing material in wet                or dry form with carbon dioxide from industrial                emissions in a carbonation unit to form a carbonate                loaded slurry, and precipitating the calcium or                magnesium carbonate from the carbonate loaded                slurry in a precipitation unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a process flow diagram of                wet carbonation.
   Carbon dioxide containing gas (2)
   Aqueous slurry (4)
   Carbonation unit (10)
   Separation unit (20)
   Precipitation unit (32)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step of contacting                  further comprises providing the                  magnesium-containing material in aqueous slurry                  and contacting the carbon dioxide-containing                  emissions and the carbon dioxide by-product with                  the aqueous slurry. The processes further                  comprises: in the contacting step, producing a                  carbonate loaded slurry comprising precipitable                  carbonates and free of precipitated alkaline                  earth metal carbonates, separating the carbonate                  loaded slurry into an aqueous phase comprising                  the precipitable carbonates and a solid phase,                  precipitating the magnesium carbonates from the                  aqueous phase, and separating the magnesium                  carbonates from the aqueous phase; subjecting the                  magnesium-containing material to heat activation                  pre-treatment prior to contacting with the carbon                  dioxide-containing emissions, where the heat                  activation pre-treatment is performed at a                  600-700 degrees C for 20-60 minutes; controlling                  the relative quantity of each carbon dioxide                  source stream in the carbon dioxide feed stream,                  where the controlling is performed according to                  pressure, temperature and/or composition of the                  carbon dioxide source streams; subjecting the                  magnesium-containing material to size reduction                  and removal of a magnetic fraction prior to                  carbonation; grinding a starting material to                  provide a particle size of 200-1000 mu to produce                  a sized material; removing the magnetic fraction                  from the sized material to produce a nonmagnetic                  fraction; and grinding the non-magnetic fraction                  to produce the magnesium-containing material                  having a particle size of at most 75 mu ;                  controlling at least one property of the carbon                  dioxide-containing gas contacted with the                  magnesium-containing material; at least one                  feature of any one of the magnesium carbonate                  production steps as above per se; and recovering                  a carbon dioxide-containing by-product stream                  from calcination; and subjecting the carbon                  dioxide-containing by-product to water removal to                  produce a treated carbon dioxide by-product                  stream prior to contacting the treated carbon                  dioxide by-product stream with the                  magnesium-containing material. The combustion gas                  is derived from a heat activation step of the                  magnesium-containing material prior to contacting                  with the carbon dioxide-containing emissions. The                  carbon dioxide derived from the heat activation                  pre-treatment is supplied to the contacting step.                  The magnesium-containing material is pre-treated                  to produce magnesium-containing particulate                  material prior to carbonation, and the                  magnesium-containing particulate material has a                  magnesium content of 1-35 wt.%, preferably 10-30                  wt.%. The contacting step comprises contacting                  the carbon dioxide with the magnesium-containing                  material in a dry form in at least one                  carbonation unit at a carbonation temperature of                  200-500 degrees C and a carbonation pressure of                  1-20 bars, for their carbonation to produce                  magnesium carbonates and a carbon dioxide                  depleted gas. The calcination is conducted in an                  indirect-heating calcination unit or                  direct-heating calcination unit. The step of                  controlling comprises regulating the relative                  quantities of the carbon dioxide-containing                  emissions and the carbon dioxide by-product are                  used to contact the magnesium-containing                  material. The step of controlling is performed to                  control gas pressure, gas temperature and/or                  carbon dioxide content of the carbon                  dioxide-containing gas contacted with the                  magnesium-containing material. The processes                  further comprise at least one feature of any one                  of the steps as above per se. The evaporite is                  pre-treated prior to carbonation. In the                  processes a carbon dioxide content of the portion                  of the carbon dioxide by-product is greater than                  a carbon dioxide content of the carbon                  dioxide-containing emissions, where the carbon                  dioxide content of the portion of the carbon                  dioxide byproduct is at least 10% greater than,                  preferably at least 50% greater than that of the                  carbon dioxide-containing emissions. The step of                  calcination is controlled by regulating flow                  rates of air and combustible and/or regulating                  oxygen content in the oxygen-containing gas, to                  obtain the carbon dioxide content of the carbon                  dioxide by-product that is greater than that of                  the carbon dioxide-containing emissions.                  Preferred Components: The carbon                  dioxide-containing emissions comprise combustion                  gas from the magnesia production facility, where                  combustion gas is derived from the calcination                  step. The carbon dioxide used to contact the                  magnesium-containing material is exclusively                  obtained from the magnesia production facility.                  The carbon dioxide-containing emissions comprise                  emissions from a separate emission source. The                  magnesium-containing material is contacted with a                  carbon dioxide feed stream that includes many                  carbon dioxide source streams. The carbon dioxide                  feed stream further comprises carbon dioxide                  derived from an additional magnesia production                  train, where the additional magnesia production                  train comprises a conventional production train.                  The magnesium-containing material comprises                  serpentine or is derived from serpentinite or its                  variants. The magnesium-containing material is                  derived from naturally occurring mineral                  materials. The magnesium-containing material is                  derived from at least one of basalt, peridotite,                  serpentinized peridotite, ophiolitic rock, mafic                  rock, ultramafic rocks, peridot, pyroxene,                  olivine, serpentine, magnesium oxide containing                  minerals and/or brucite. The magnesium-containing                  material is derived from industrial by-product                  material. The industrial by-product material                  comprises steelmaking slag and/or steelmaking                  worn magnesium oxide brick. The                  magnesium-containing material is derived from                  mining residue. The mining residue comprises                  phyllosilicate mining residue and/or chrysotile                  mining residue. The magnesium carbonate or the                  precipitated magnesium carbonate comprises                  nesquehonite. The magnesium-containing material                  comprises an evaporite. The evaporite comprises a                  magnesium chloride salt, a magnesium sulfide salt                  or a magnesium carbonate salt. The carbon dioxide                  content of the portion of the carbon dioxide                  by-product is 15-25 wt.% and the carbon dioxide                  content of the carbon dioxide- containing                  emissions is 10-20 wt.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The system further                  comprises: a carbon dioxide collection and supply                  system for collecting the carbon dioxide                  by-product stream as supplying the same as at                  least part of the carbon dioxide containing gas                  to the carbonation unit; and at least one                  additional feature of any one of the processes as                  above per se. The evaporite is derived from an                  evaporator unit.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN108938826-A
TI Extracting tea polyphenols by using resin            comprises e.g. drying and smashing the green tea            leaves, adding distilled water, fully homogenizing,            heating in a water bath, controlling the temperature of            the water paste slurry and adding ethanol.
AU WU S
AE GUANGXI CLOONEY TEA BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A0738D
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting tea polyphenols by using resin                comprises (i) drying and smashing the green tea                leaves into 50-100 meshes, adding distilled water,                fully homogenizing in a vacuum high shear                homogenizer to obtain water paste slurry, heating                in a water bath, controlling the temperature of the                water paste slurry at 53-72 degrees C, adding                ethanol into the water paste slurry, stirring at                speed of 300-400 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 15-20                minutes, and allowing to stand for 0.5-1 hours to                obtain slurry, (ii) ultrasonically treating the                slurry to obtain treated slurry, (iii) taking the                slurry treated in the step (ii), extracting by                supercritical carbon dioxide, adding sodium lauryl                sulfate, ethyl acetate and polysorbate into the                carbon dioxide fluid, controlling extraction                pressure to 26-30 MPa, extracting at 52-58 degrees                C, where the carbon dioxide flow rate is 20-25                l/hour, and the extracting for 1.5-3.5 hours to                obtain crude tea polyphenol, separating the crude                tea polyphenol from the slurry.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting tea                polyphenols by using resin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has low energy consumption; is                environmentally-friendly; and improves extraction                rate and purity of tea polyphenols.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting tea polyphenols by using resin                comprises (i) drying and smashing the green tea                leaves into 50-100 meshes, adding distilled water,                fully homogenizing in a vacuum high shear                homogenizer to obtain water paste slurry, heating                in a water bath, controlling the temperature of the                water paste slurry at 53-72 degrees C, adding                ethanol into the water paste slurry, stirring at                speed of 300-400 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 15-20                minutes, and allowing to stand for 0.5-1 hours to                obtain slurry, (ii) ultrasonically treating the                slurry to obtain treated slurry, (iii) taking the                slurry treated in the step (ii), extracting by                supercritical carbon dioxide, adding sodium lauryl                sulfate, ethyl acetate and polysorbate into the                carbon dioxide fluid, controlling extraction                pressure to 26-30 MPa, extracting at 52-58 degrees                C, where the carbon dioxide flow rate is 20-25                l/hour, and the extracting for 1.5-3.5 hours to                obtain crude tea polyphenol, separating the crude                tea polyphenol from the slurry, where the                separation pressure is 3-6 MPa, and separating at                40-50 degrees C, extracting the remaining slurry by                supercritical carbon dioxide, and adding acetone                into the carbon dioxide fluid, adding sodium lauryl                sulfate, controlling extraction pressure to 22-27                MPa, extracting at 50-56 degrees C, and where the                carbon dioxide flow rate is 20-25 l/h and                extracting for 1-2 hours to obtain crude tea                polyphenol b, separating the crude tea polyphenol                from the slurry, and where the separation pressure                is 3-6 MPa, and separating at 40-50 degrees C,                mixing the obtained tea polyphenol crude product                with tea polyphenol crude product b, adding                hydrochloric acid, adjusting the pH to 3-5.2,                centrifuging or filtering to obtain tea soup, (iv)                placing the tea soup on the LX-8 macroporous                adsorption resin column, where the diameter ratio                of LX-8 macroporous adsorption resin column is                1:5-15, where the sample loading is 37-56 times the                column volume, loading flow rate is 0.5-2 BV/h,                eluting the impurity with aqueous solution of                1.2-7.5 vol.%, where the amount is 20-26 times that                of the column volume, where the flow rate is 1-4                BV/h, eluting with 50-73 wt.% aqueous solution of                ethanol, where the amount is 12-18 times the column                volume and the flow rate is 1-4 BV/h, collecting                eluate with 56-73 vol.% in aqueous ethanol,                concentrating under reduced pressure, spray drying                or freeze drying to obtain tea polyphenol                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  ratio of the green tea leaves and the distilled                  water is 1:8-17. In the step (i), the                  concentration of the ethanol is 25-30%. The ratio                  of the amount of the green tea leaves and the                  ethanol is 1:0.4-0.6. In the step (iii), the                  total amount of sodium lauryl sulfate, ethyl                  acetate and polysorbate is 15-24 wt.% of the                  carbon dioxide fluid. The weight ratio of sodium                  lauryl sulfate, ethyl acetate and polysorbate is                  (1.2-2.5):(0.6-2):(1.5-3). The weight ratio of                  sodium lauryl sulfate, ethyl acetate and                  polysorbate is 1.8:2:2.6. The weight ratio of                  acetone and sodium lauryl sulfate is 1.6:0.8. In                  the step (iii), the concentration of the                  hydrochloric acid is 18-28%, and the amount is                  6-16 wt.% of the total amount of the crude tea                  polyphenol. Preferred Method: In the step (ii),                  where the frequency of the ultrasonic waves is                  25-30 KHz, preferably 30 kHz and processing time                  is 5-8 minutes, preferably 5 minutes.

PN CN112662194-A; CN112662194-B
TI High-performance composite material comprises            porous polymer matrix and fiber-reinforced phase;            porous polymer matrix is wrapped on surface of            fiber-reinforced phase to form high-performance            composite material.
AU ZHU W
   HU H
   LI D
AE UNIV BEIHANG (UNBA-C)
GA 202151697H
AB    NOVELTY - High-performance composite material comprises                porous polymer matrix and fiber-reinforced phase;                the porous polymer matrix is wrapped on the surface                of fiber-reinforced phase to form a                high-performance composite material.
   USE - Used as high-performance composite                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material: has lightweight.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the the high-performance composite                material comprising using manufacturing system, (I)                preparing porous polymer matrix by; (i) adding                polymer raw materials to the material distributing                hopper of the porous material preparation module,                heating and pressurizing, polymer melts and turns                into a polymer melt, pushing by the screw, passing                polymer melt into the screw cavity, during the                pushing process, injecting supercritical carbon                dioxide fluid by supercritical carbon dioxide                injection device; (ii) injecting high-pressure                cooling nitrogen through the high-pressure cooling                gas injection device to form a low-temperature and                low-pressure environment in the screw cavity,                providing temperature and pressure conditions for                the foaming of polymer melt containing                supercritical carbon dioxide, mixing supercritical                carbon dioxide with polymer melt in the screw                cavity; and (iii) fully mixing, reducing the                pressure of the expansion chamber, vacuum                extracting, separating out the carbon dioxide from                the polymer melt, forming pores inside polymer, but                layer of ''shell'' structure that does not match                the inside of the pore parameters will be formed on                the surface of the polymer; as the pressure and                temperature decrease, the number, size, and                distribution of pores in the polymer reach the                desired state; waiting for the porous polymer to                stabilize, transforming the porous polymer into tow                shape through the mold by pushing the screw, and                feeding into the composite material preparation                module by the wire feeder; (II) preparation of                composite material by; feeding fiber reinforcement                material into the mixing chamber through the fiber                spool, the porous polymer wraps the fiber tow under                the action of the second roller, completely                entering the fiber tow, combining porous polymer                and the fiber tow at the microscopic scale, and                forming the porous polymer matrix-fiber-reinforced                phase composite tow, feeding into the                multi-material printing module by the wire feeder;                and (III) multi-material printing; (i) heating and                melting the porous polymer matrix-fiber-reinforced                phase composite tow in layer-by-layer accumulation                forming device, extruding by the printing nozzle,                stacking and solidifying layer by layer according                to a predetermined track; placing laval nozzle on                the layer-to-layer bonding surface, and compressing                two layers of polymer with the first roller; and                (ii) after stacking layers, laval nozzle ejects                high-pressure supercritical carbon dioxide,                generating local high pressure environment at                bonding surface, the bonding material has residual                heat to form a local re-foaming external                environment; passing supercritical carbon dioxide                ejected from the laval nozzle into the porous                polymer bonding surface, removing the Laval nozzle,                rapidly reducing the pressure at the bonding                surface, separating out carbon dioxide, and                completing refoaming process at the bonding                surface, obtaining the final structure of the                composite material composed of porous polymer-fiber                reinforced material with continuous distribution of                pore parameters. Where manufacturing system                comprising porous material preparation module,                composite material preparation module, and                multi-material printing module, where, the porous                material preparation module includes material                distributing hopper, first temperature regulator,                supercritical carbon dioxide injection device,                high-pressure cooling gas injection equipment,                screw cavity, screw and mold are used to prepare                porous polymer as composite material matrix; the                composite material preparation module includes a                second temperature regulator, a mixing chamber, a                pressure regulator, a buffer zone and a fiber                spool, and used for micro-level fusion of porous                polymer matrix and fiber reinforcement phase to                form porous polymer composite tow; the                multi-material printing module includes a                layer-by-layer accumulation forming device and a                refoaming device, the refoaming device includes                first roller and a laval nozzle, used for                layer-by-layer accumulation to form the final                structure and local re-foaming of the porous                polymer bonding surface.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The porous polymer                  matrix is thermoplastic polymer. The size of the                  pores of the porous polymer matrix is in the                  micro-nano range, with average size of 10 nm to                  1000 mu m. The pores of the porous polymer matrix                  are continuously distributed in the                  high-performance composite material, and the pore                  parameters can be controlled. Preferred Method:                  In the step (I), when the high-pressure cooling                  nitrogen pressure is 0.5-5 MPa, the temperature                  of the screw cavity is 200-500 degrees C, and the                  pressure is 0.1-4 MPa, the porous polymer will                  produce ''eye''-shaped loose macropores; when the                  high-pressure cooling nitrogen pressure is 5-15                  MPa, the temperature of the expansion chamber is                  100-300 degrees C, and the pressure is 2-10 MPa,                  the porous polymer will produce ''round'' -                  shaped dense small holes.

TI Integrated ultra-high pressure hydrogen production system comprises reformer, three-phase heat exchange device, trap, water-cooled heat exchanger, carbon dioxide liquefaction device, and integrated device.
AU YUE X
   HAN D
   JIAO W
   LI J
   ZHAO J
   ZHOU S
AE ZHONGKE LIQUID SUNSHINE SUZHOU HYDROGEN (ZHON-Non-standard)
   CAS LIQUID SUNSHINE SUZHOU HYDROGEN TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (CASL-Non-standard)
GA 202018156L
AB    NOVELTY - Integrated ultra-high pressure hydrogen production system comprises reformer, three-phase heat exchange device, a trap, water-cooled heat exchanger, carbon dioxide liquefaction device, and integrated device for hydrogen separation and water gas reforming; the reformer is connected with a methanol water vapor inlet pipe and a mixed gas outlet pipe; the methanol water vapor inlet pipe is connected to a three-phase heat exchange device, and the reformer is adapted to make methanol water vapor into a mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide; the water gas reforming integrated device includes reaction chamber, and heating chamber is provided outside the reaction chamber, which is adapted to provide reaction temperature for the reaction chamber; a hydrogen separation chamber and a water gas reforming chamber are connected in the reaction chamber, the hydrogen separation chamber is located above the water gas reforming chamber.
   USE - Used as integrated ultra-high pressure hydrogen production system.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrogen production system: has hydrogen separation and water gas reforming properties; and can realize gas circulating and purifying, realize theoretical yield is 100%, and hydrogen yield is greater than or equal to 95%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Integrated ultra-high pressure hydrogen production system comprises reformer, three-phase heat exchange device, a trap, water-cooled heat exchanger, carbon dioxide liquefaction device, and integrated device for hydrogen separation and water gas reforming; the reformer is connected with a methanol water vapor inlet pipe and a mixed gas outlet pipe; the methanol water vapor inlet pipe is connected to a three-phase heat exchange device, and the reformer is adapted to make methanol water vapor into a mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide; the water gas reforming integrated device includes reaction chamber, and heating chamber is provided outside the reaction chamber, which is adapted to provide reaction temperature for the reaction chamber; a hydrogen separation chamber and a water gas reforming chamber are connected in the reaction chamber, the hydrogen separation chamber is located above the water gas reforming chamber, and the hydrogen separation chamber is in connected with the water gas reforming chamber; the hydrogen separation chamber is provided with a mixed gas inlet and a carbon dioxide mixed residual gas outlet, a hydrogen absorption tube is inserted into the hydrogen separation chamber, which is suitable for separating pure hydrogen, and the hydrogen absorption tube is connected to a pure hydrogen outlet tube; the water gas reforming cavity is provided with a catalyst filler, and the water gas reforming cavity is provided with a hydrogen mixed residual gas inlet; the mixed gas outlet pipe is connected to the mixed gas inlet, and the carbon dioxide mixed residual gas outlet is connected to the carbon dioxide mixed residual gas outlet, the carbon dioxide mixed residual gas outlet pipe is sequentially connected to a trap, a water-cooled heat exchanger, and a carbon dioxide liquefaction device, the carbon dioxide liquefaction device is connected to a liquid carbon dioxide outlet pipe and a hydrogen mixed residual gas outlet pipe, the hydrogen mixed residual gas outlet pipe is connected with the hydrogen mixed residual gas inlet; an air pump is provided on the hydrogen mixed residual gas outlet pipe to provide a hydrogen mixed residual gas delivery pressure; the methanol water vapor inlet pipe is connected to a liquid pump, and the pump pressure of the liquid pump is 40-100 Mpa, the water-cooled heat exchanger is connected to a water-cooled tower, and the operating temperature of the water-cooled heat exchanger is less than or equal to 30.8 degrees C, the pure hydrogen in the hydrogen outlet pipe is sent to hydrogen storage tank under the pump pressure of a liquid pump. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for ultrahigh-pressure hydrogen production comprising (1) liquid pump sending methanol water into methanol water vapor pipe, where the pump pressure is 40-100 Mpa, heating the methanol water to vaporize into methanol water vapor, and entering the reformer, reforming reaction of methanol steam in reformer generates hydrogen, sending the mixed gas of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, and then the mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide into the hydrogen separation chamber of the water gas reforming integrated device; where the gas phase composition of the mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide is 65-75% of hydrogen, 20-26% of carbon dioxide, and 0.3-3% of carbon monoxide; (2) where hydrogen absorption tube in the hydrogen separation chamber separates mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, collecting the separated pure hydrogen gas from the output of the hydrogen absorption pipe, sending pure hydrogen gas to the hydrogen storage tank under the pump pressure of the liquid pump; discharging the remaining carbon dioxide mixed waste gas from the hydrogen separation chamber, controlling the carbon dioxide mixed residual gas by a liquid pump, where water-cooled heat exchanger controls the temperature of the carbon dioxide mixed gas, sending carbon dioxide mixed residual gas to a carbon dioxide separation device for carbon dioxide liquefaction separation; where the gas phase components of the carbon dioxide mixed residual gas is 25-45% of hydrogen, 55-75% of carbon dioxide, 0-3% of water, and 0.3-3% of carbon monoxide; the pressure controlled by the liquid pump is 40-100 Mpa, and the temperature controlled by the water-cooled heat exchanger is less than or equal to 30.8 degrees C; (3) making carbon dioxide mixed residual gas into liquid carbon dioxide and hydrogen mixed residual gas in carbon dioxide separator, collecting liquid carbon dioxide, the components of the hydrogen mixed residual gas is 65-75% hydrogen, 20-26% carbon dioxide, and 3-9% carbon monoxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the integrated ultra-high pressure hydrogen production system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The methanol water is replaced by natural gas.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: The method further comprises (4) sending hydrogen mixed gas to the water gas reforming cavity of the water gas reforming integrated device, carrying water distribution to made into reformed mixed gas, water distribution is performed according to the carbon monoxide content, and water distribution ratio (carbon monoxide: water) is 1: 1-20; reforming the fed hydrogen mixed gas into a reformed mixed gas in a water gas reforming chamber, the gas phase composition of the reformed mixture is 62-77% hydrogen, 22-27% carbon dioxide, and 0.5-1.5% carbon monoxide; where the proportion of hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and carbon monoxide in the reformed mixed gas is corresponding to the proportion of hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and carbon monoxide in the mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and carbon monoxide; and (5) passing the reformed mixed gas into the hydrogen separation chamber, mixing with mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, subjecting the reformed mixed gas and the mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and carbon monoxide to hydrogen separation operation in the hydrogen separation chamber. In the method, cooling the output mixed gas of pure hydrogen and carbon dioxide by three-phase heat exchange device, vaporizing the methanol water into methanol water vapor through three-phase heat exchange device. Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide mixed residual gas outlet pipe and the pure hydrogen outlet pipe are connected to a three-phase heat exchange device. The hydrogen absorption tube is a niobium tube, the catalyst filler is a copper-based filler, and the operating temperature of the reaction chamber is 200-350 degrees C; or the catalyst filler is a zirconium-based filler, and the operating temperature of the reaction chamber is 350-550 degrees C. The hydrogen absorption tube is palladium film tube or palladium alloy film tube, the catalyst filler is zirconium-based filler, and the operating temperature of the reaction chamber is 250-550 degrees C. The pure hydrogen outlet pipe is connected to a hydrogen storage tank, and the pure hydrogen is sent into the hydrogen storage tank under the pump pressure of a liquid pump, and the hydrogen storage tank is connected to a hydrogenation machine.

PN IN202121004694-A
TI Making sparkling cold press fruit juice by sanitizing fruits, obtaining juice from sanitized fruits, pasteurization of cold press juice at high pressure, adding carbon dioxide in the juice in soda carbonation machine and transferring juice.
AU SAXENA V S
AE SAXENA V S (SAXE-Individual)
GA 202279309T
AB    NOVELTY - Producing sparkling cold press fruit juice comprises: sanitizing the fruits; obtaining juice from the sanitized fruits; pasteurization of cold press juice at high pressure; adding carbon dioxide in the juice in a soda carbonation machine; and transferring the sparkling juice in a packaging container and sealing it.
   USE - The method is useful for producing sparkling cold press fruit juice comprising coconut, apple, water melon and lime (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides juice that: shows significant improvements with digestive symptoms, swallowing ability, constipation and gall bladder emptying; keeps the blood slightly alkaline regardless of what person eat or drink; that it forces food to stay in stomach little longer, thus helping people feel fuller , making them eat less hence lose some weight; is carbonated drink helps in better water retention in body and also led to an increase in good high-density lipoprotein cholesterol, results good for heart health; and is derived from cold pressing tastes better than juice produced by a bladed-juicer, shows improved taste and retained over the shelf-life of the product and enhanced flavor will incentivize the consumption of healthier products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The fruits are sanitized by washing with sanitizing solutions containing chlorine, ozone, and hydrogen peroxide, bromine, acids or UV treatment. The pressure is 10-15 tons for obtaining cold press juice. The carbonation of juice is done in carbonator machine for 3-6 seconds. The high pressure pasteurization is done 300-600 MPa for 8-12 minutes. The carbon dioxide dissolved is 2-3 vol.% of pasteurized cold pressed juice. Preferred Components: The juice is cold pressed juice. The packaging container comprises polyethylene terephthalate bottle. The cold press juice is coconut, apple, water melon and lime.
   EXAMPLE - 10 kgs Fresh apples were taken washed with water and sanitized with chlorine water. The apples were then put in a cold press juicer and a pressure of 15 tons was applied in a cold press juicer to obtain cold press juice of apple. The cold press juice was then transferred in special containers for high pressure pasteurization. The pasteurization was carried out for 500 MPa was for 10 minutes then the pasteurized juice was transferred to sterilized bottles. The bottles were then transferred for carbonation, where carbon dioxide gas was introduced in the juice at high pressure to 2-3 vol.% the pasteurized cold pressed juice. The bottles were then hermitically sealed to obtain sparkling cold press fruit juice.

TI Generation of power involves heating circulation medium containing carbon dioxide, tertiary amine compound, piperazine and water, separating circulation medium into gas and liquid, and regenerating circulation medium by absorbing gas.
AU OGAWA T
   YAMAMOTO T
   MAWATARI T
AE TOSHIBA CORP (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
GA 202257888L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves heating a circulation medium containing carbon dioxide, a tertiary amine compound, a piperazine, and water. The separation of the circulation medium into a gas and a liquid by the heating is performed. A power generation turbine (5) is rotated with the separated gas. The circulation medium is preheated before heating by the separated liquid and the gas after being supplied to power generation with the liquid radiated by the preheating of the circulation medium are mixed to produce a mixture. The mixture is formed by mixing the above gas with the liquid produced by the above process. The mixture is cooled, and the circulation medium is regenerated by absorbing the gas supplied to the power generation by the pre-heating of the circulation medium to regenerate the circulation medium.
   USE - Method for generating electric power in electric power generation system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient and economical generation of low-temperature drivable power with high output.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an electric power generation system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the electric power generation system.
   Pressurizing pump (1)
   Heat exchanger (2)
   Heater (3)
   Gas-liquid separator (4)
   Power generation turbine (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The tertiary amine compound is triethanolamine and methyl diethanolamine. The circulating medium has triethanolamine concentration of 15-30 mass% and a piperazine concentration of 10-30 mass%. The circulating medium has a molar ratio (carbon dioxide/triethanolamine + piperazine) of carbon dioxide to triethanolamine and piperazine of 0.25 to 0.55.
DC X11 (Power Generation and High Power Machines)
MC X11-C; X11-U01
IP F01K-025/06; F01K-025/10

PN CN114106931-A
TI Extracting agarwood essential oil and extract in whitening and spot-removing composition, involves crushing agarwood extract with supercritical carbon dioxide at low temperature to obtain fraction, and filtering.
AU WEI S
   ZHU X
AE GUANGZHOU DONGZHIMENG COSMETICS CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022379705
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting agarwood essential oil and extract, involves crushing agarwood extract with supercritical carbon dioxide at low temperature to obtain fraction, and filtering to obtain agarwood crude oil. The C18 reverse phase column chromatography separation is performed on the obtained crude oil, where mobile phase comprises 0.1-1.5 wt.% acidic electrolyte aqueous solution and methanol, and then collected 5-95 wt.% above components respectively, followed by testing boiling point of each component, mixing and concentrating the components below 300degrees Celsius to obtain agarwood essential oil. The components are combined and concentrated above 300degrees Celsius to obtain agarwood extract.
   USE - Method for extracting agarwood essential oil and extract used in whitening and spot-removing composition (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The subsequent purification method adopts a reverse phase column chromatography, which is carried out at room temperature in the whole process, only a small amount of fraction is taken to measure the boiling point in a small test stage according to the retention time, so that the active substance does not deteriorate due to high temperature and complex chemical environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a whitening and spot-removing composition, which comprises agarwood essential oil, ascorbyl glucoside, 4-butyl resorcinol and potassium methoxysalicylate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Conditions: The temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide low-temperature extraction is 40-50degrees Celsius, the extraction time is 60-90 minutes, and the extraction pressure is 30-35 MPa. The crushing treatment is performed to crush agarwood into powder with a particle size of less than 1 mm. Preferred Components: The acidic electrolyte is formic acid, hydrochloric acid and trifluoroacetic acid. The acidic electrolyte is hydrochloric acid. The whitening spot-removing composition further comprises a humectant, a skin feeling regulator, a preservative, an emulsifier, and deionized water. The humectant comprises panthenol, 1, 3-butanediol, glycerol, butanediol, 1, 3-propanediol, betaine, dipropylene glycol, glyceryl polyether-26, erythritol, methyl propanediol, hydroxyethyl urea, sodium polyglutamate, panthenol, levan, trehalose poly, ethylene glycol, xylitol, sorbitol, pyrrolidone carboxylic acid, sodium lactate, urea, methyl glucose polyether, chitin derivatives, trehalose, tremella polysaccharide, sodium hyaluronate, panthenol, and ceramide. The skin feeling regulator comprises at least one of dextran, polymethyl methacrylate, polyethylene, ethylene/acrylic acid copolymer, silicone resin, composite material microspheres, silicon dioxide, oat flour, nylon-12, bioglycan and waterborne polyurethane. The preservative comprises chlorphenesin, methyl ester, phenoxyethanol, methylparaben, propylparaben, chlorphenesin, ethylhexyl glycerol, hexamidine dihydrochloride, benzyl alcohol, p-hydroxyacetophenone, sorbic acid/potassium sorbate, phenoxyethanol, parabens, polyols, chlorphenesin, glyceryl caprylate, glyceryl caprate, sodium diacetate, calcium propionate, sodium lactate, polylysine, diethylhexyl syringylidenemalonate, dehydroacetic acid and salts, salicylic acid/sodium salicylate, benzoic acid, iodopropynyl butylcarbamate, sorbitan caprylate and anisic acid. Preferred Compositions: The mass fraction of the acidic electrolyte is 0.3%. The agarwood essential oil is 0.5-1 wt.%, the ascorbyl glucoside is 0.5-1 wt.%, the 4-butyl resorcinol is 0.1-0.6 wt.%, and the potassium methoxysalicylate is 0.2-0.5 wt.%.

TI Reducing total scrap steel smelting cost of            Consteel electric furnace by using anthracite involves            reducing total scrap steel smelting cost of Consteel            electric furnace by using anthracite in MS1-MS2 stage,            and adding blocky anthracite.
AU TIAN P
   LI Y
   SONG B
   ZHAO W
   ZHANG R
   WANG J
   ZHU R
   SHI J
   DU H
AE SANXI JINCHENG ANTHRACITE MINING GROUP (SANX-Non-standard)
GA 202240653S
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing total scrap steel smelting cost of a                Consteel electric furnace by using anthracite                involves reducing the total scrap steel smelting                cost of the Consteel electric furnace by using                anthracite in the MS1-MS2 stage, adding blocky                anthracite, injecting oxygen by a side-blowing                oxygen lance and a furnace door carbon-oxygen                lance, and injecting argon (Ar) by a bottom-blowing                spray gun, in the MS3-MS4 stage, based on the                MS1-MS2 stage, the side-blown carbon gun blows                anthracite powder, side-blown oxygen gun blows                oxygen, bottom-blown spray gun blows Ar and carbon                dioxide (CO2) and the gasses are mixed, and MS5-MS6                stages, where the bottom blowing spray gun blows                carbon dioxide (CO2) on the basis of the MS3-MS4                stages.
   USE - Method for reducing total scrap steel smelting                cost of Consteel electric furnace by using                anthracite used in technical field of steel                metallurgy.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables adding different                granularities in different stages in the smelting                process of Consteel electric furnace, different                amounts of anthracite, combined oxygen supply                curve, realizing optimization of anthracite                utilization rate, low cost of anthratite, high heat                value, low ash and so on, improving the quality of                the molten steel, reducing the production                cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Conditions: The method                  particularly involves 300-450kg of anthracite                  blocks with the particle size of 10-15cm are                  added in MS1 stage, and the oxygen flow rate of                  the side-blown oxygen lance is 1500-3000Nm3. The                  oxygen flow of the oxygen lance of the furnace                  door is 3000-3500 Nm3. The flow rate of the                  furnace door carbon gun is 0-20kg/minute, and the                  flow rate of the bottom blowing Ar is                  50-70Nm3/hour. 300-450kg Smokeless coal blocks                  with the grain diameter of 3-5cm are added in the                  MS2 stage, and the oxygen flow rate of the                  side-blown oxygen lance is 2500Nm3. The oxygen                  flow of the oxygen lance of the furnace door is                  2500-33000 Nm3. The flow rate of the furnace door                  carbon gun is 0-30kg/minute, and flow rate of the                  bottom blowing Ar is 50-70Nm3/hour. The                  side-blown carbon gun is opened in MS3 stage,                  anthracite powder with the grain diameter less                  than 5mm is blown, flow rate is 0-90kg/minute,                  and the flow rate of the side-blown oxygen gun is                  2500-33000 Nm3. The oxygen flow of the furnace                  door oxygen lance is 3000 Nm3H, carbon gun                  blowing of furnace door. The flow rate of the                  anthracite powder is 60-90kg/minute, and the                  bottom blowing gas is CO2 and Ar at a bottom                  blowing flow rate of 120Nm3H, where CO2                  proportion is 50 vol.% or 75 vol.% and the                  proportion of Ar was 50 vol.% or 25 vol.%. The                  flow rate of the injected anthracite powder is                  0-20kg/minute, and the flow rate of the                  side-blown oxygen lance is 500-1000Nm3, blowing                  oxygen is stopped by the oxygen lance on the                  furnace door, where all bottom blowing gas is CO2                  flow rate of 120-160Nm3/hour, at the MS6 stage,                  blowing of the anthracite powder is stopped, and                  the oxygen flow of the side-blown oxygen lance is                  controlled to be 300 Nm3H, bottom blowing CO2,                  and flow rate of is 60-80 Nm3/hour. The Consteel                  electric furnace is used for smelting all scrap                  steel, no pig iron or molten iron is added, and                  the steel tapping amount of the electric furnace                  is 60 t, and in the tapping period, spray gun at                  the bottom of the furnace stops blowing CO2 to                  blowed and Ar with a blowing flow of 30-40                  Nm3/hour is started, and after tapping, during                  the blow-out period, the bottom lance stops                  blowing Ar and starts blowing O2 gas flow 30                  Nm3/hour.

PT P
PN RD692080-A
TI Method for performing amplification of carbon dioxide lasers in an extreme ultra-violet radiation source for lithography, involves supplying direct current power by generating highly turbulent flow within the discharge tube.
AU 
AE ANONYMOUS (ANON-C)
GA 2021E31200
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves supplying direct current power by generating highly turbulent flow within the discharge tube. The turbulent flow is accomplished by destroying all flow information contained in the upstream flow pattern. A pressure chamber is provided around the tube, and the gas is fed through inlet holes leading to an abrupt transition from radial to axial flow, which effectively removes any memory from the upstream flow pattern, and leads to a fully developed turbulent flow profile within a short distance of the inlet section. The gas molecules in the flow tube exhibit a flat, turbulent flow profile, with each molecule has a significant transverse component to its movement. The motion of the heavy ions is coupled to that of the gas molecules through collisions, and so exhibit a flat, turbulent profile.
   USE - Method for performing amplification of carbon dioxide lasers in an extreme ultra-violet radiation source for lithography.
   ADVANTAGE - Ensures that the intense mixing prevents the formation of streamers, as any developing streamer attempting to extend towards the cathode is diminished by the transverse motion. Thus a stable, laser-active glow discharge is provided at high specific power.
DC G07 (Photo-mechanical production of printing surfaces (G03F).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN KR2021094443-A; KR2372490-B1
TI Manufacturing microporous hollow furnace carbon black assembly useful in electrocatalyst carrier for fuel cell and for secondary batteries, comprises performing gas phase oxidation reaction of furnace carbon black using carbon dioxide.
AU NOHJAESEUNG
   LEE S M
   KANG D S
   LEE J H
   LEE S H
   KIM W
AE KUMOH NAT INST TECHNOLOGY IND ACAD COOP (KUMN-C)
   CARBOLAB CO LTD (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 2021898355
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing a microporous hollow furnace carbon black assembly comprises (i) preparing furnace carbon black, (ii) performing a gas phase oxidation reaction of the furnace carbon black using carbon dioxide, which is an oxidizing gas, and (iii) separating and obtaining a binder in which multiple hollow furnace carbon blacks are bonded from the gaseous oxidation reaction product, where each of multiple hollow furnace carbon blacks has a hollow structure and has multiple micro-sized pores.
   USE - The microporous hollow furnace carbon black assembly is useful in electrocatalyst carrier for fuel cell and carbon support for secondary batteries (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The microporous hollow furnace carbon black assembly: is suitable in the field of support, anode material and cathode material; and has excellent electrical properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for microporous hollow furnace carbon black assembly, comprising multiple microporous hollow carbon blacks combined with single or multiple integrated bodies, where the binder is integrated with multiplemicro-porous hollow furnace carbon blacks, where all or some of the combined microporous hollow furnace carbon blacks are connected to each other to form an internal tunnel in the hollow furnace carbon black assembly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The assembly includes at least one inner tunnel. The mesopore volume of the three-step complex satisfies the equation: B1 divide by A is equal to 3-8, where A is the mesopore volume of the furnace carbon black before performing the gas phase oxidation reaction, and B1 is the mesopore volume of the binder formed by performing the gas phase oxidation reaction. Preferred Method: The gas phase oxidation reaction is carried out in an oxidation chamber at 900-1300 degrees C for 80-150 minutes by supplying the oxidizing gas i.e. 99-99.999 vol.% carbon dioxide at a rate of 150-300 ml/minute.
   EXAMPLE - Furnace carbon black was prepared as raw material. The horizontal tubular furnace was preheated to 1000 degrees C. The raw materials were charged into a reaction tube of the oxidation chamber using a quartz boat, and then fixed at 1000 degrees C. The gas phase oxidation reaction was performed. 99.950 vol.% oxidizing gas containing carbon dioxide as a reactive substance was added at a flow rate of 150 ml/minute using a ball flow meter. Multiple micro-hollow furnace carbon blacks were combined from the vapor phase oxidation reaction product including the micro-hollow furnace carbon black and their binders to activate and separate an integrated binder to obtain the micro-porous hollow furnace carbon black assembly.

The conditions of the ultrasound are                  power 80-650 watts, preferably 100-500 watts. The                  ultrasonic time is 10-120 minutes, preferably                  30-60 minutes. The thickness                  distribution Delta d/dmax of the antimony                  material and/or bismuth nanosheet material is                  0.07-0.3, and the Delta d is calculated by the                  difference between dmax and dmin, where the dmax                  represents the thickness of the thickest antimony                  nanosheet material and/or bismuth nanosheet                  material in the antimony nanosheet material                  and/or bismuth nanosheet nanoplate material, and                  dmin represents the thickness of the thinnest                  antimony nanosheet material and/or bismuth                  nanosheet material in the antimony nanosheet                  material and/or bismuth nanosheet material. The                  thickness of the antimony nanosheet material                  and/or the bismuth nanosheet material is 0.5-1.5                  nm, preferably 0.79-1.2 nm.

TI Off-axis digital holographic diffraction            tomography system based on continuous terahertz wave            comprises carbon dioxide pump terahertz laser for            outputting continuous terahertz wave, and beam            splitting sheet divides terahertz wave.
AU ZHAO J
   WANG D
   JIN X
   WANG Y
   RONG L
AE UNIV BEIJING TECHNOLOGY (UYBT-C)
GA 2021513249
AB    NOVELTY - Off-axis digital holographic diffraction                tomography system based on continuous terahertz                wave comprises a carbon dioxide pump terahertz                laser for outputting continuous terahertz wave. A                first gold-plated off-axis parabolic mirror and a                second gold-plated off-axis parabolic mirror form a                beam expanding unit. A beam splitting sheet divides                the terahertz wave after beam expanding into                transmission illumination wave and reflection wave.                A measured sample is placed on an electric rotary                table. The reflected wave is reflected by a                gold-plated reflector as a reference light wave and                transmitted to a pyroelectric detector.
   USE - Used as off-axis digital holographic                diffraction tomography system based on continuous                terahertz wave.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: utilizes the angular spectrum                diffraction propagation formula and self-focusing                algorithm to complete the reconstruction of                off-axis digital hologram; gets the Rytov                approximate scattering field of the object; and                utilizes the filtering back propagation algorithm                to get the object function distribution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                off-axis digital holographic diffraction tomography                method based on continuous terahertz wave by using                system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a system optical path of                the off-axis digital holographic diffraction                tomography system based on continuous terahertz                wave.

AE NANTONG ALCHEMY BIOTECH DEV CO LTD (NANT-Non-standard)
GA 2021344924

   USE - The method is for deeply processing and purifying natural extract for manufacturing powder flavor for pickling vegetable (claimed).

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: The metal filler is stainless steel.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The pesticide residue is organic phosphorus and/or pyrethroid pesticide residue.
