PT P
PN US10953388-B1; WO2021134034-A1
TI Production of methane-rich gas for e.g. fuel, involves introducing heavy hydrocarbon stream e.g. heavy naphtha and/or kerosene and comprising no diesel to catalytic reactor comprising activated catalyst, and operating reactor adiabatically.
AU HARALE A
   KATIKANENI S P
   BAE J
   MA Y
   AL-NAIMI A
   KATIKANENI S
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
   KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 202129341J
AB    NOVELTY - Production of methane-rich gas from a heavy hydrocarbon stream involves introducing a heavy hydrocarbon stream such as heavy naphtha and/or kerosene and comprising no diesel to a catalytic reactor comprising an activated catalyst comprising 20 wt.% nickel, 70 wt.% cerium oxide component, and 10 wt.% gadolinium oxide component, applying the heavy hydrocarbon stream to the activated catalyst, operating the catalytic reactor at a temperature and pressure of 11-40 bar such that the catalytic reactor operates adiabatically, and producing methane-rich gas over the activated catalyst. The heavy naphtha comprises paraffin, 1-paraffin, aromatics, naphthenes, and olefins. The methane-rich gas comprises methane and at least one gas chosen from carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, and hydrogen.
   USE - Production of methane-rich gas e.g. methane, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, and/or hydrogen. Uses include but are not limited to hydrogen fuel for transportation vehicle, hydrogen feed stream for refinery application, glass factory, food industry, and metal industry, fuel cell for power generation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method maximizes conversion of heavy hydrocarbons to methane in the pre-forming stage which makes the combination of pre-forming stage and steam reforming more energy efficient.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The heavy hydrocarbon stream is heavy naphtha. The conversion rate of heavy hydrocarbons to methane-rich gas is more than 90%. The molar ratio of nitrate to glycine is 1:1.5. The activated catalyst alternately comprises 19.5 wt.% nickel, 0.5 wt.% ruthenium, 70 wt.% cerium oxide component, and 10 wt.% gadolinium oxide component.Preferred Process: Production of methane-rich gas further involves preparing the activated catalyst by adding a stoichiometric amount of cerium nitrate hexahydrate, gadolinium nitrate hexahydrate, and nickel nitrate hexahydrate to de-ionized water to create a dissolved solution, adding glycine to the dissolved solution to create a glycine-dissolved solution, heating the glycine-dissolved solution such that excess water is evaporated, initiating combustion, and producing a pre-catalyst powder, calcining the pre-catalyst powder in air at 800degrees Celsius for 4 hours, preferably by increasing the temperature to 800degrees Celsius over a period of 4 hours and maintaining the temperature at 800degrees Celsius for 4 hours to produce a calcined catalyst powder, and reducing the calcined catalyst powder in a reducing gas comprising 30 vol.% hydrogen and 70 vol.% nitrogen at 500degrees Celsius for 4 hours to produce the activated catalyst, shaping the activating catalyst into pellets, producing the methane-rich gas over the activated catalyst until coke formation on the activated catalyst forms a spent catalyst, and treating the spent catalyst with a regenerating gas comprising 30 vol.% hydrogen, 45 vol.% nitrogen, and 45 vol.% water at a regeneration temperature and atmospheric pressure to remove the coke formation on the catalyst, and treating the methane-rich gas in a reformer unit which is a conventional steam reformer and/or a membrane reformer to produce a hydrogen gas stream.Preferred Condition: The temperature in the catalytic reactor is 500-600degrees Celsius.

PN CN112458044-A
TI Performing cryopreservation and recovering human            placental mesenchymal stem cells comprises e.g. placing            cells and serum-free culture medium in 75T culture            flask, placing in carbon dioxide constant temperature            incubator for culturing.
AU ZOU Y
   WANG T
AE UNIV SICHUAN WEST CHINA HOSPITAL (USCU-C)
GA 202125214Y
AB    NOVELTY - Performing cryopreservation and recovering                human placental mesenchymal stem cells comprises                (i) placing serum-free culture medium in constant                temperature water bath at 37 degrees C for 15                minutes; (ii) placing freezing tube into the                constant temperature water bath, and taking out                before the ice crystals in the freezing tube are                completely melted; (iii) taking a centrifugal tube,                adding 4 ml serum-free culture medium, sucking the                cell suspension in the freezing tube into the                serum-free medium of the centrifuge tube, gently                beating and mixing, centrifuging the centrifugal                tube at 1000 revolutions/minute for 8 minutes, and                maintaining the temperature at 24 degrees C in the                centrifuging process; (iv) discarding the                supernatant in the centrifuge tube, placing cells                and 8 ml serum-free culture medium in 75T culture                flask for culturing, shaking back and forth to make                the cells uniformly dispersed; and (v) placing the                culture flask in 5% carbon dioxide constant                temperature incubator at 37 degrees C for                culturing.
   USE - The method is useful for performing                cryopreservation and recovering human placental                mesenchymal stem cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem of the existing                placental tissue recovery method with low                efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The methpd further                  comprises (vi) (via) aspirating the serum-free                  medium in the culture flask, and adding 2 ml of                  phosphate buffered saline into the culture flask                  for washing; (vib) sucking out the phosphate                  buffered saline in the culture flask, and adding                  7 ml serum-free culture medium into the culture                  flask; and (vic) placing the culture flask upside                  down under the microscope to observe the cell                  morphology at regular intervals, and completing                  the replacement of the serum-free medium after                  the cells have not changed color.

AU SON Y
   PENTHALA R
AE UNIV IND & ACADEMIC COOP IN CHUNGNAM NAT (UCHU-C)
GA 2020995556
AB    NOVELTY - Dyes (A) comprising anthraquinone compound (I) or (II), and aza-based compound (III), are new.
   USE - The dyes are useful for dyeing nylon fibers with supercritical fluid, preferably supercritical-based carbon dioxide (all claimed), and as hydrophobic disperse dye.
   ADVANTAGE - The dye is a blue-based hydrophobic disperse dye, has excellent solubility in supercritical fluid, and exhibits practically satisfactory color uniformity, can dye nylon fibers with supercritical fluid without a separate dispersant in quick manner, provides supercritical fluid dyed nylon fibers having excellent dyeing properties and fastness properties, omits wastewater treatment process and the drying process essential in the existing wet dyeing process, and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Dyes (A) comprising anthraquinone compound of formulae (I) or (II), and aza-based compound of formula (III), are new.
   R1 and R2 = 1-10C alkyl;
   L = 1-10C alkylene;
   R3 and R4 = H or 1-10C alkyl, where at least one of R3 and R4 is H;
   R5 = H or 1-10C alkyl; and
   R6 and R7 = 1-10C alkyl or hydroxyl-1-10C alkyl.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for dyeing nylon fibers with a supercritical fluid using dye comprising anthraquinone compounds (I) or (II) or aza-based compound (III).

PN CN111401613-A
TI Forecasting greenhouse crop yields involves obtaining information of multiple growth factors in greenhouse, such as temperature, light, air carbon dioxide content, soil state and disease status.
AU ZHANG L
   LIU L
AE HEILONGJIANG LIMIAO TECHNOLOGY DEV CO (HEIL-Non-standard)
GA 202067579V
AB    NOVELTY - Forecasting greenhouse crop yields involves obtaining the information of multiple growth factors in the greenhouse, such as temperature, light, air carbon dioxide content, soil state and disease status. The growth factor information is used to evaluate the growth of plants according to the growth temperature range of different crops, light intensity, water and fertilizer requirements, and common diseases that are easy to be infected as the criteria for determining the nutrients of the crops, and the time is integrated to obtain the daily net accumulation of nutrients for each crop. The planting density per unit soil area is obtained according to the daily net accumulation of nutrients for each crop according to the daily net accumulation of nutrients for each crop to obtain the net accumulated nutrients for the daily growth of the crops by recording the cumulative value of nutrients for 7 days, and inferred the yield forecast of each crop.
   USE - Method for forecasting greenhouse crop yields.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to forecast greenhouse crop yields which realizes the forecast of greenhouse crop yield by monitoring the conversion of nutrient components to agricultural products in a continuous week.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The light intensity, temperature and carbon dioxide in the air are automatically collected by the sensor at a fixed time and stored in the database. The fertilization and irrigation conditions are manually input every day to evaluate the soil status, and the daily pests and diseases are recorded manually to evaluate the health of the crops.

TI Solid cosmetic product i.e. solid shampoo, solid shower gel and/or solid shaving preparation, comprises carbon dioxide effervescent component comprising salt of carbonic acid and acidifying agent comprising citric acid and cream of tartar.
AU CONSTANTINE M
   CONSTANTINE M J
   AMBROSEN H E
   BIRD R J
   CAMPBELL D
   COMMISSO A
AE COSMETIC WARRIORS LTD (COSM-Non-standard)
GA 2019A8505N
AB    NOVELTY - Solid cosmetic product comprises a carbon dioxide effervescent component comprising a salt of carbonic acid and an acidifying agent comprising citric acid and cream of tartar, where the weight ratio of citric acid to cream of tartar is from about 4:1 to about 1:7; and water.
   USE - Solid cosmetic product i.e. solid shampoo, solid shower gel and/or solid shaving preparation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The solid cosmetic product gives an improved feel on the user s skin, inclusion of starch provides a creamy feel on the skin, softer on the skin when applied, highly moisturizing and cleansing effect, is suitable for washing both the skin and hair of the user.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a process for the production of a solid cosmetic product, which involves combining the salt of carbonic acid with the water to prepare a mixture, combining the mixture with citric acid and cream of tartar and solidifying the resultant composition; and
   (2) a cosmetic method, which involves contacting the solid cosmetic product with water to create a lather and contacting the skin or hair of a user with the lather.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solid cosmetic product further comprises starch, salt of alginic acid, calcium salt, component selected from a humectant, binder, filler, opacifier, essential oil, vitamin, perfume, fragrance, colouring, clay, vegetable butter, vegetable oil, fruit extract, herb extract, decorative item, and mixtures. The starch is selected from corn starch, rice starch, potato starch, tapioca starch, arrowroot, and mixtures, peferably, the starch is potato starch. The salt of alginic acid is selected from sodium alginate, potassium alginate, calcium alginate, and mixtures. The calcium salt is selected from the group consisting of calcium lactate, calcium chloride, calcium gluconate, calcium lactate gluconate, and mixtures. The solid cosmetic product comprises a carbon dioxide effervescent component comprising a salt of carbonic acid and an acidifying agent comprising citric acid and cream of tartar; a surfactant; a starch; and a salt of alginic acid. The salt of carbonic acid is selected from sodium bicarbonate, sodium carbonate, sodium sesquicarbonate and mixtures. The solid cosmetic product further comprises a surfactant selected from an anionic surfactant, a cationic surfactant, an amphoteric surfactant, a non-ionic surfactant and mixtures. The surfactant is selected from lauryl betaine, cocam idopropyl betaine, trimethyl glycine betaine, sodium cocoamphoacetate, disodium cocoamphodiacetate, sodium lauroamphoacetate, disodium lauroamphodiacetate, cocoamidopropyl hydroxysultaine, sodium lauryl sulfate, ammonium lauryl sulfate, sodium coco sulfate, sodium laureth sulfate, ammonium laureth sulfate, sodium myreth sulfate, sodium lauroyl sarcosinate, disodium laureth sulfosuccinate, sodium lauryl sulfoacetate, and mixtures. Preferred Conditions: The weight ratio of citric acid to cream of tartar is from about 1:1 to about 1:2. The weight ratio of the salt of alginic acid to the calcium salt is from about 5:1 to about 1:5. The solid cosmetic product is a solid shampoo, solid shower gel and/or solid shaving preparation. The process further involves combining the salt of carbonic acid and water with a surfactant and/or a starch, and combining the mixture with a salt of alginic acid and optionally a calcium salt. Preferred Compositions: Solid cosmetic product comprises 10-40 wt.% acidifying agent, 5-20 wt.% citric acid, 5-30 wt.% cream of tartar, 40-80 wt.% salt of carbonic acid, 0.1-5 wt.% water, 0.1-10 wt.% surfactant, 1-20 wt.% starch and 0.1-10 wt.% salt of alginic acid.

PT P
PN CN110607245-A; CN110607245-B
TI Capsule useful for relieving people's fatigue and increasing immunity comprises Ganoderma lucidum and Cordyceps militaris and is obtained by mixing materials, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, homogenizing and granulating.
AU ZHANG X
AE HANGZHOU SIYUTAI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 2020027878
AB    NOVELTY - Capsule comprises 50-60 pts. wt. Ganoderma lucidum and 40-50 pts. wt. Cordyceps militaris.
   USE - The capsule is useful for relieving people's fatigue and increasing immunity.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: controls the culturing of Ganoderma lucidum and Cordyceps militaris and the preparation of the capsule, promotes the production of active ingredients e.g. polysaccharides and adenosine, and prevents the destruction of active ingredients during the process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing capsule comprising (1) mixing the Ganoderma lucidum and Cordyceps militaris, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction at 15-30 MPa and 35-40 degrees C to obtain an ester-soluble extract and a filter residue, (2) mixing the filter residue with water, and stirring at 50-070 degrees C for 1-3 hours, where the mass ratio of the filter residue and water is 1: (8-15), filtering, filtrate, vacuum extracting and distilling at 60-80 degrees C to obtain a aqueous extract, which is mixed with an ester-soluble extract to obtain an extract, and (3) homogenizing the extract, granulating, and under vacuum condition to obtain granules, packing into capsules to obtain the capsules.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The Ganoderma lucidum is prepared by: (i) inoculating the Ganoderma lucidum into the seed culture medium, where the liquid content is 50-60 ml/100ml, culturing at 28-30 degrees C at a speed of 150-200 revolutions/minute for 6-7 days to obtain the Ganoderma lucidum seed liquid, (ii) inoculating the Ganoderma lucidum seed liquid to a Ganoderma lucidum fermentation medium, where the inoculation amount is 6-8%, the liquid filling amount is 300-350 ml/500 ml, culturing at 28-30 degrees C at a speed 200-250 revolutions/minute for 60-7 days to obtain the Ganoderma lucidum fermentation medium, and (iii) centrifuging and lyophilizing the Ganoderma lucidum fermentation medium to obtain the Ganoderma lucidum. The Cordyceps militaris is prepared by: (I) inoculating Cordyceps militaris into the seed culture medium, where the liquid content is 40-50 ml/100 ml, and culturing at 25-28 degrees C at a speed of 150-200 revolutions/minute for 3-4 days to obtain Cordyceps militaris seed liquid, (II) inoculating the Cordyceps militaris seed liquid into Cordyceps militaris fermentation medium, where the inoculation amount is 5-8%, and the liquid content is 1000-150 ml/250 ml, performing fermentation at 25-28 degrees C and 400-500 revolutions/minute for 5-6 days to obtain a Cordyceps militaris fermentation medium, and (III) centrifuging and lyophilizing the Cordyceps militaris fermentation medium to obtain the Cordyceps militaris. Preferred Composition: The preparation raw materials of Ganoderma lucidum fermentation medium comprises: 2-2.5 wt.% carbon source, 3-4 wt.% oats, 0.01-0.2 wt.% inorganic salt and water (balance). The preparation raw materials of the Cordyceps militaris fermentation medium includes 2-3 wt.% carbon source, 2-3 wt.% nitrogen source, 2-3 wt.% oatmeal, 0.1-00.3 wt.% inorganic salt, and water (balance). Preferred Components: The carbon source includes sucrose, glucose, lactose, maltose, mannitol, and/or glycerol. The nitrogen source includes peptone, yeast extract, beef extract, and/or yeast paste. The inorganic salt includes zinc, potassium, magnesium, and/or calcium salts. The capsules further comprises: auxiliary agent. The auxiliary agent accounts for 1-3 wt.% of the Ganoderma lucidum, and the auxiliary agent is calcium carbonate, cyclodextrin, magnesium sulfate, and/or microcrystalline cellulose.
   ACTIVITY - CNS-Gen; Muscular-Gen; Immunostimulant.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. Liquefied                  natural gas is sent to liquefied natural gas                  storage device and enters in the subsequent                  pressure swing adsorption extracting device for                  extracting hydrogen-rich gas. Preferred                  Properties: Purity of liquefied natural gas is                  greater than or equal to 99%.

PN FR3080545-A1; WO2019206686-A1; FR3080545-B1; CN112203746-A; EP3784366-A1; US2021094000-A1
TI Treatment of gaseous feedstock containing volatile compound involves contacting feedstock with monolith based on silica and titanium dioxide, and irradiating monolith with irradiation source to convert volatile compound into carbon dioxide.
AU BERNADET S
   FECANT A
   RAVAINE S
   BACKOV R V
   LE BECHEC M
   LACOMBE S
   BACKOV R
AE IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
   IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
   IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
GA 201990137Y
AB    NOVELTY - Treatment of gaseous feedstock comprising molecular oxygen and volatile organic compounds involves contacting (i) the gaseous feedstock comprising molecular oxygen and volatile organic compounds with a monolith comprising silica and titanium dioxide, and including type-I macropores having a volume of 0.1-3 ml/g, and a pore diameter of more than 50 nm to less than or equal to 1000 nm, and type-II macropores including a volume of 1-8 ml/g, and a pore diameter of more than 1 mu m to less than or equal to 10 mu m, and irradiating (ii) the monolith with irradiation source(s) producing at least one wavelength of less than 400 nm in order to convert the volatile organic compounds into carbon dioxide at a temperature of -30 degrees C to 200 degrees C, and at a pressure of 0.01-70 MPa.
   USE - Treatment of gaseous feedstock comprising molecular oxygen and volatile organic compounds.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient treatment of gaseous feedstock comprising molecular oxygen and volatile organic compounds using the monolith based on silica and titanium dioxide, having improved photocatalytic activity, stability and mineralization rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The monolith comprises 5-70 wt.% titanium dioxide. Preferred Properties: The monolith includes mesopores having a volume of 0.01-1 ml/g, preferably 0.05-0.5 ml/g, and a pore diameter of more than 2 nm to less than or equal to 50 nm, and macropores having a volume of less than 0.5 ml/g, and a pore diameter of more than 10 mu m. The monolith has a bulk density of 0.05-0.5 g/ml, and a specific surface area of 10-1000 m2/g, preferably 50-600 m2/g. Preferred Process: The monolith is prepared by mixing a solution comprising a surfactant with an acidic solution to obtain a solution (a), adding the solution (a) to a soluble silica precursor solution to obtain a solution (b), optionally adding the solution (b) to liquid organic compound(s) which is immiscible with the solution (b) to form an emulsion, maturing the emulsion in a wet state in order to obtain a gel, washing the gel with an organic solution, drying and calcining the gel to obtain a monolith based on silica, impregnating the monolith in a solution comprising soluble precursor of titanium dioxide, and optionally, drying the obtained product at a temperature of 5-120 degrees C, calcining in the presence of air at a temperature of 80-150 degrees C for 1-10 hours, calcining at a temperature of 150-250 degrees C for 1-10 hours, and calcining at a temperature of 300-950 degrees C for 0.5-24 hours to obtain a monolith based on silica and titanium dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The gaseous feedstock is diluted with a diluent fluid. The irradiation source is a source of artificial irradiation. The irradiation source produces at least one wavelength of 300-400 nm. The process (i) is performed in a fixed-bed reactor.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN110075888-A; CN110075888-B
TI Preparation of molybdenum carbide doped carbon catalyst for e.g. preparing hydrogenated molybdenum carbide doped carbon catalyst involves mixing glucose and urea, adding ammonium molybdate tetrahydrate, calcining, and passivating.
AU LIU B
   XIONG S
   LIAN Y
AE UNIV SOUTH CENT NATIONALITIES (USOC-C)
GA 201969289J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of molybdenum carbide doped carbon catalyst involves (1) mixing glucose and urea, placing at 175-200 degrees C, melting into paste, stirring until the solution is clear, adding ammonium molybdate tetrahydrate, stirring, (2) calcining at 200-300 degrees C for 8-15 hours, cooling to room temperature, grinding the product into a powder, placing in a tube furnace at 700-800 degrees C and baking in an argon atmosphere for 1-4 hours, and passivating after the temperature drops to room temperature. The mass ratio of the glucose, urea and ammonium molybdate tetrahydrate is 3-5:5-8:1-3.
   USE - Preparation of molybdenum carbide doped carbon catalyst for preparing hydrogenated molybdenum carbide doped carbon catalyst, and used for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methanol (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation process is simple, and the prepared catalyst has excellent stability and high activity under mild conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) hydrogenated molybdenum carbide doped carbon catalyst is prepared by reducing molybdenum carbide doped carbon catalyst at 450-550 degrees C under hydrogen atmosphere, and cooling; and
   (2) use of molybdenum carbide doped carbon catalyst for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The specific conditions of the calcination are heating in an argon atmosphere to 400-500 degrees C, preferably 400 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours, preferably 0.5 hour and raising the temperature to 700-800 degrees C, preferably 750 degrees C for 1-4 hours, preferably 2 hours and the heating rate is 5 degrees C/minute. The specific conditions for the passivation are introducing the mixture of oxygen and argon having a volume percentage of 1:99 , and the passivation time is 12 hours. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the glucose, urea and ammonium molybdate tetrahydrate is preferably 3:5:1.3.

PT P
PN CN109926096-A; CN109926096-B
TI Preparing porphyrin functionalized mesoporous silicon oxide material used in heterogeneous catalysis comprises adding e.g. mesoporous silica material and 5,10,15,20-tetra(4-pyridyl)porphyrin to solvent A, refluxing and washing with solvent.
AU YANG Q
   LI H
   JIANG J
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 2019591120
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing porphyrin functionalized mesoporous silicon oxide material comprises adding a mesoporous silica material, a halogen containing silane reagent and a metal complex of 5,10,15,20-tetra(4-pyridyl)porphyrin to the solvent A, refluxing at a temperature of 60-210 degrees C for 12-48 hours under the nitrogen atmosphere, cooling the reaction system to room temperature and filtering to obtain a solid product and (b) washing the solid product with a solvent, drying at 25-60 degrees C for 12-48 hours under vacuum.
   USE - The mesoporous silicon oxide material useful in heterogeneous catalysis including cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxide, alkyne hydration reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The mesoporous silicon oxide material reduces the cost and saves the reaction time by simultaneously using the grafting of the silicon oxide; when compared with the traditional two-step preparation process can load more of metal porphyrin; exhibits high activity and excellent cyclic stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of the porphyrin functionalized mesoporous silicon oxide material in heterogeneous catalytic reactions comprises cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide with epoxide and hydration reaction of alkyne.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The use method of porphyrin functionalized mesoporous silicon oxide material in the cycloaddition reaction of the carbon dioxide and the epoxide comprises adding an epoxy compound and the porphyrin functionalized mesoporous silicon oxide material to the autoclave, where filled with 0.5-3 MPa of carbon dioxide gas, heating at 40-150 degrees C for 1-36 hours,cooling and releasing the remaining carbon dioxide gas, filtering the remaining liquid to remove the metal porphyrin-functionalized mesoporous silica material to obtain a cyclic carbonate, where the mass ratio of the porphyrinfunctionalized mesoporous silica material to the epoxy compound is 1:2.5-300. The use method of porphyrin functionalized mesoporous silicon oxide material in the alkyne hydration comprises adding the porphyrin functionalized mesoporous silicon oxide material with an alkyne compound, an acid, water and solvent to the reaction flask, heating at 50-100 degrees C for 12-96 hours, cooling to room temperature, adding the solvent B to the remaining liquid, filtering to remove the porphyrin functionalized mesoporous silica material and evaporating the methanol to obtain a product ketone compound, where, per gram of metal porphyrin-functionalized mesoporous silica material, where 1 g of the porphyrin functionalized mesoporous silicon oxide material is mixed with 0.25-200 g alkyne, 0.05-50 mmol acid, 5-800 mmol water and 2.5-500 ml solvent B. Preferred Components: In the step (a) the metal complex of 0.01-1 mmol 5,10,15,20-tetra(4-pyridyl)porphyrin and 0.05-6 mmol silane is mixed per 1 g mesoporous silica material. The solvent A is toluene, tetrahydrofuran, N,N-dimethylformamide, N-methylpyrrolidone and/or dimethyl sulfoxide, where the concentration of the solution is 10-30 g/ml. The mesoporous silica material is SBA-15, SBA16, FDU-12, MCM-41 and/or silica aerogel. The halogen containing silane reagent is 3-bromopropyltrimethoxysilane, 3-iodopropyltrimethoxysilane, 3-chloropropyltrimethoxysilane, and/or 4-bromobutyltrimethoxysilane. The metal complex of 5,10,15,20-tetrakis(4-pyridyl)porphyrin is zinc 5,10,15,20-tetra(4-pyridyl)porphyrin, iron 5,10,15, 20-tetra(4-pyridyl)porphyrin, manganese 5,10,15,20-tetra(4-pyridyl)porphyrin and/or cobalt 5,10,15,20-tetra(4-pyridyl)porphyrin. The epoxy compound is ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, 1,2-butylene oxide, 1,2-epoxyhexane, epoxycyclohexane and/or styrene oxide. The alkyne compound is phenylacetylene, 4-methylphenylacetylene, 4-nitrophenylacetylene, 4-methoxyphenylacetylene and/or 4-bromophenylacetylene. The acid is concentrated sulfuric acid, concentrated hydrochloric acid, concentrated nitric acid, hydrobromic acid, p-toluenesulfonic acid, trifluoromethanesulfonic acid and/or sulfonated polystyrene divinylbenzene copolymer. The solvent B is methanol, ethanol, isopropanol and/or water.

GA 201954872G
AB    NOVELTY - The display has a mounting plate (1) connected with a limiting cylinder (11) and provided with an opening window (10). A display main body (2) is located on the limiting cylinder. TI Producing composition useful in active layer for            producing gas separation membrane, comprises reacting            cellulose-based compound with fluorine-substituted acid            to obtain first reactant, and dropping            fluorine-substituted acid anhydride.
AU LEE B
   BANG S
   SHIN C K
   LI B
   FANG S
   SHEN C
   LEE B S
AE LG CHEM LTD (GLDS-C)
   LG CHEM LTD (GLDS-C)
   LG CHEM LTD (GLDS-C)
   LG CHEM LTD (GLDS-C)
   LG CHEM LTD (GLDS-C)
GA 2019510836
AB    NOVELTY - Producing composition, comprises (a) reacting                cellulose-based compound with fluorine-substituted                acid to obtain a first reactant, and (b) dropping                fluorine-substituted acid anhydride into the first                reactant at temperature higher than room                temperature.
   USE - The composition is useful in active layer for                producing gas separation membrane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition has excellent carbon dioxide                permeability and carbon dioxide selectivity for                methane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   composition produced by the above                method;
   composition comprising cellulose-based                compound of formula (I);
   producing gas separation membrane, comprising                (i) preparing composition by the above method, (ii)                applying hydrophilic polymer solution on a porous                substrate to form a porous support (10), and (iii)                applying a composition on the porous support to                form an active layer (11); and
   gas separation membrane (100) comprising a                porous support, and an active layer made of                cellulose-based compound (I), provided on the                porous support.
   n=1-1000; and
   R1-R3=H or -(CO)CF , where at least one of                R1-R3 is -(CO)CF .
                                                                                                                     
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the gas separation membrane.
   10Porous support
   11Active layer
   100Gas separation membrane
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composition                  comprises 2.9-5 wt.% cellulose-based compound or                  (I). The composition further comprises 95-97.1                  wt.% second organic solvent. Preferred                  Components: The fluorine-substituted acid is                  alkanoic acid having 2-10C in which at least one                  fluorine atom is substituted. The                  fluorine-substituted acid anhydride is acid                  anhydride having 3-10C in which at least one                  fluorine atom is substituted. The                  fluorine-substituted acid is trifluoroacetic                  acid. The fluorine-substituted acid anhydride is                  trifluoroacetic anhydride. The temperature higher                  than the normal temperature is 30-80&#176; C.                  The second organic solvent is nitromethane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The porous substrate                  is a porous nonwoven fabric. The hydrophilic                  polymer includes polysulfone, polyethersulfone,                  polycarbonate, polyethylene oxide, polyimide,                  polyether imide, polyether ether ketone,                  polypropylene, polymethylpentene, polymethyl                  chloride and/or polyvinylidene fluoride. The                  porous support includes porous nonwoven fabric                  and hydrophilic polymer. The gas separation                  membrane has selectivity of carbon dioxide of                  5-40. Preferred Method: The method for applying                  the active layer-forming composition is a slot                  coating method.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Method for removing and capturing carbon dioxide            from flue gas, involves exposing fluid stream to            aqueous scrubbing solution in presence of packing            element including hydrophobic feature and hydrophilic            feature to increase gas-liquid mixing.
AU LIU K
   WIDGER L R
   FAN Z
   STOLAROFF J
AE UNIV KENTUCKY RES FOUND (KENT-C)
GA 201950779G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves exposing a fluid stream to                an aqueous scrubbing solution in presence of a                packing element (24) including a hydrophobic                feature and a hydrophilic feature to increase                gas-liquid mixing and a rate of acid gas                absorption. Counterflow between the fluid stream                and the aqueous scrubbing solution is established                within an absorber vessel (14). The acid gas                captured by the aqueous scrubbing solution is                recovered. The aqueous scrubbing solution is                regenerated. The packing element with a surface                including the hydrophobic feature and the                hydrophilic feature is provided.
   USE - Method for removing and capturing acid gas                i.e. carbon dioxide, from a fluid stream i.e. flue                gas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables providing turbulent force                from within a bulk solution for surface renewal                using inherent hydrophilicity of a solvent on a                patterned polarity packing surface to destabilize a                stable film and bring fresh amine from the bulk to                the surface.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an                apparatus for removing and capturing acid gas from                a fluid stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram                of a carbon capture system.
   LLongitudinal axis
   14Absorber vessel
   16Internal chamber
   24Packing element
   30Stripper

AU SUN S

   UNIV NORTH CHINA ELECTRIC POWER (UYHD-C)
   STATE GRID JIANGSU ELECTRIC POWER (SGCC-C)
   STATE GRID CORP CHINA (SGCC-C)
   STATE GRID JIANGSU ELECTRIC POWER CO LTD (SGCC-C)
GA 201925230Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining a data source from field monitoring data of gas intake and carbon emission parameter in period of time, where the carbon emission parameter comprises an outlet gas pressure, temperature, humidity, flow rate and carbon dioxide concentration. Abnormal data is removed and data unit is uniformed by preprocessing the obtained data source. An instantaneous discharging amount of carbon dioxide is calculated. An area difference analysis is performed on real-time monitoring data of an engine intake. A related coefficient obtained by an example analysis is compared with a correlation threshold value to verify rationality of monitoring data.
   USE - Area difference analysis based gas unit carbon emission data correlation detection method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables performing field on-line monitoring operation, finding anomaly monitoring data, selecting the length of correlation coefficient time section according to update frequency of field monitoring data so as to meet product difference analysis to the sample volume. The method enables performing on-line detection and abnormal data early warning, data validity judgment and parameter correlation analysis.

PN EP3424895-A1; WO2019020229-A1; WO2019020229-A9; EP3649098-A1
TI Producing fuel, comprises reacting carbon monoxide and hydrogen in presence of first catalyst to obtain first product mixture containing hydrocarbons and olefins, and reacting olefin with hydrogen and carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide.
AU LEITNER W
   HEUSER B
   PISCHINGER S
AE RHEINISCH WESTFAELISCHE TECH HOCHSCHULE (RWTH-C)
   RHEINISCH WESTFAELISCHE TECH HOCHSCHULE (RWTH-C)
GA 201912606F
AB    NOVELTY - Producing fuel, comprises (i) reacting carbon monoxide and hydrogen in the presence of a first catalyst to obtain a first product mixture containing hydrocarbons and greater than or equal to 10 to less than or equal to 45 wt.% olefins having 5-10C and (ii) reacting at least one olefin having 5-10C containing part of the first product mixture with hydrogen and carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide in the presence of a second catalyst which is different from the first catalyst to obtain alkanol having 6-11C containing fuel for internal combustion engines.
   USE - The fuel and substance mixture are useful for internal combustion engines (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces fuel for internal combustion engines with an improved emission characteristics.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) fuel for internal combustion engines, comprising greater than or equal to 50 to less than or equal to 90 wt.% hydrocarbons having greater than or equal to 6C and greater than or equal to 10 to less than or equal to 50 wt.% alkanols having 6-11C; and
   (2) use of substance mixture comprising the above mentioned components as a fuel for internal combustion engines.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first catalyst in the step (i) comprises iron and/or cobalt. The second catalyst in the step (ii) comprises a cobalt complex having 9-eicosyl-9-phosphabicyclo(4.2.1)nonane, 9-eicosyl-9-phosphabicyclo 3.3.1)nonane, 9-phenyl-9-phosphabicyclo(4.2.1)nonane or 9-phosphabicyclo(4.2.1)nonane as ligands.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The first product mixture obtained after the step (i) is separated into at least two fractions, and the fraction with the highest content of olefins having 5-10C is used as starting material in the step (ii). The fuel for internal combustion engines obtained after the step (ii) is separated into at least two fractions and the fraction with the highest content of alkanols having 6-11C is used as an enriched fuel for internal combustion engines. The first product mixture obtained after the step (i) is used into at least two fractions without prior separation. The fuel obtained after the step (ii) is mixed with a further hydrocarbon-containing component. The step (i) is carried out at greater than or equal to 275 to less than or equal to 400 degrees C, preferably greater than or equal to 150 to less than 275 degrees C. The step (ii) is carried out at greater than 100 to less than or equal to 250 degrees C, where the second catalyst contains a single catalyst, or the step (ii) is carried out greater than 50 to less than or equal to 150 degrees C, where the second catalyst contains more than one catalyst. Preferred Components: At least the carbon monoxide used in the step (i) is at least partially obtained from a dry reforming reaction, a steam reforming reaction, a reverse water gas shift reaction, a partial oxidation of hydrocarbons or a co-electrolysis of carbon dioxide and water. In the step (ii), at least a part of the hydrogen used is obtained from renewable sources.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN109161922-A; CN109161922-B
TI Carbon nanotube/nano tin oxide composite catalyst useful for electrochemical reduction of C1 fuel by carbon dioxide.
AU QIAO J
   ZHANG Q
   LIU J
   PENG L
   ZHOU Y
AE UNIV DONGHUA (UYDG-C)
   SHANGHAI JINYUAN SENIOR HIGH SCHOOL (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019064270
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon nanotube/nano tin oxide (SnOx) composite catalyst is claimed. The catalyst comprises carbon nanotubes as carrier and SnOx loaded nanosheets.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for electrochemical reduction of C1 fuel by carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has high catalytic activity and selectivity; inhibits the production of hydrogen by-products; exhibits long-term stability during electrolysis; and the method: has simple process, and suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon nanotube/nano tin oxide (SnOx) composite catalyst is claimed. The catalyst comprises carbon nanotubes as carrier and SnOx loaded nanosheets.
   x = 1, 2.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing catalyst comprising dissolving stannous chloride and sodium citrate in water, and stirring to obtain precursor solution, dispersing the carbon nanotubes in a solvent, and sonicating to obtain carbon nanotube solution, mixing above two solutions and sonicating, adding base, stirring, then carrying out hydrothermal reaction, cooling, centrifugally washing and drying to obtain final product;
   (2) gas diffusion electrode, where the gas diffusion electrode body is loaded with catalyst; and
   (3) preparing gas diffusion electrode comprising dissolving catalyst in a isopropanol solution, adding AS-4 alkaline anion binder solution and sonicating to obtain catalyst dispersion liquid, and applying the dispersion liquid onto the gas diffusion electrode body and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon nanotubes are functionalized carbon nanotubes; the SnOx nanosheets are nanosheets of a leaf-type structure. The functionalized carbon nanotubes are: carboxylated carbon nanotubes, aminated carbon nanotubes and/or hydroxylated carbon nanotubes. The mass ratio between stannous chloride and sodium citrate is 1:3-1:4; the mass ratio between stannous chloride and carbon nanotubes is 15:1-5:1; and the molar ratio between base and stannous chloride is 8:1-10:1. The carbon nanotubes have a diameter of 10-20 nm, the length is 20-30 mu m; the solvent is equal volume of mixed ethanol and deionized water; and the base is potassium hydroxide. Preferred Method: The hydrothermal reaction temperature is 100-120 degrees C and the time is 6-10 hours.

Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. PT P
PN CN109167076-A; CN109167076-B
TI Preparation of electrolyzed water catalytic membrane for preparing hydrogen for fuel cell, involves preparing mixed foamed powder using e.g. polystyrene plastic, expanding mixed powder, spraying on perfluorosulfonic acid film, and cooling.
AU ZHANG Y
AE ZHANG Y (ZHAN-Individual)
   HEBEI SHICHANG AUTO PARTS CO LTD (HEBE-Non-standard)
   JIUCHANG NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY ZHENJIANG (JIUC-Non-standard)
GA 201906303R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of electrolyzed water catalytic membrane involves grinding polystyrene plastic into a fine powder in a ball mill to obtain a polystyrene micropowder, uniformly mixing 44-60 pts. wt. polystyrene micropowder with 5-8 pts. wt. iron powder, 5-8 pts. wt. nickel powder and 30-40 pts. wt. molybdenum disulfide powder, mixing with supercritical carbon dioxide, added to a high-pressure reactor, micro-foaming the mixture under strong agitation for 2-4 hours, depressurizing to obtain a mixed foamed powder, adding the foamed powder to a mixed gas of carbon dioxide, nitrogen and ammonia in a closed vessel, expanding the mixed powder under the action of gas stream to obtain a fluffy powder, transferring to a spray gun, rapidly heating the surface of the perfluorosulfonic acid film by microwave, spraying the fluffy powder on the surface of the film together with the gas, and cooling to form a porous polystyrene-supported iron-nickel-doped molybdenum disulfide catalyst layer.
   USE - Preparation of electrolyzed water catalytic membrane (claimed) for preparing hydrogen for fuel cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical, and provides catalytic membrane with uniform catalyst dispersion, high catalytic efficiency and high hydrogen production rate than the existing method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mixed gas comprises 40-60 parts volume carbon dioxide, 20-30 parts volume nitrogen and 20-30 parts volume of ammonia. Preferred Properties: The perfluorosulfonic acid film has thickness of 0.3-0.8 mm. The catalyst layer has thickness of 0.1-0.2 mm.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. GA 2018A3983C
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a gas controller                communicatively connected to the gas supply. A                carbon dioxide gas emitter is communicatively                connected to the gas subcontroller and adapted to                being arranged near the growing plant, the gas                emitter is height adjustable as the plant grows.                The gas emitter remains in close proximity to the                plant.
   USE - System of promoting plant growth and                production in hoop house environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is able to maintain that close                proximity distribution by various portion including                height adjustment portion that permit the grower to                easily adjust gas application height throughout the                plant growth life cycle from seedlings or clones,                juveniles, to mature plants ready for harvest. The                sheets are typically stretched tight over the roof                hoop supports to aid in shedding the element, to                reduce noise from flapping in the wind, and to                improve structural integrity. The assembly permits                pivoting of the modules out of the way of the paths                between beds during maintenance and harvest.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                components of the system for promoting plant growth                and production.

PN CN108913113-A
TI Composite corrosion inhibitor for carbon dioxide well annulus for protective oil/gas gathering and transportation, comprises oil-soluble composite nonionic imidazoline corrosion inhibitor, fungicide, dispersed mutual solvent and base liquid.
AU WANG Y
   HAN Y
   LI Y
   WANG H
   SONG L
AE JILIN PETROLEUM GROUP XINGYE PETROCHEMIC (JILI-Non-standard)
GA 201898388F
AB    NOVELTY - A composite corrosion inhibitor comprises 3-10 wt.% corrosion inhibitor, 2-5 wt.% fungicide, 2-10 wt.% dispersed mutual solvent (A), 4-20 wt.% dispersed mutual solvent (B) and remainder of base liquid. The corrosion inhibitor is oil-soluble composite nonionic imidazoline corrosion inhibitor kd-02. The fungicide is double quaternary ammonium salt fungicide WS-320. The dispersed mutual solvent (A) is polyoxyethylene type nonionic surfactant alkylphenol ethoxylate. The dispersed mutual solvent (B) is polyol fatty acid ester or dehydrated sorbitan fatty acid ester. The base liquid is 7-grade white oil.
   USE - Composite corrosion inhibitor is used for carbon dioxide well annulus for protective oil/gas gathering and transportation.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite corrosion inhibitor has maximum temperature resistance of 160 degrees C or less, high corrosion rate and sterilization rate, and excellent stability and compatibility, and can be prepared by simple, safe and environmentally-friendly process using economical and easily available raw materials without generation of turbidity, precipitate and layering.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the composite corrosion inhibitor, which involves adding 7-grade white oil to reactor, adding remaining raw materials, and mixing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composite corrosion inhibitor preferably comprises 10 wt.% corrosion inhibitor, 2 wt.% fungicide, 2 wt.% dispersed mutual solvent (A), 4 wt.% dispersed mutual solvent (B) and remainder of base liquid.

PN CN108855155-A; CN108855155-B
TI Preparation of low-mercury catalyst, involves including carrier material selection, alkali soaking, activation, acidification, primary adsorption of mercury chloride, and immersion aid solution.
AU ZHANG L
AE ZHANG L (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 2018956920
AB    NOVELTY - A preparation of low-mercury catalyst involves including carrier material selection, alkali soaking, activation, acidification, primary adsorption of mercury chloride, immersion aid solution, and secondary adsorption of mercuric chloride.
   USE - Preparation of low-mercury catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The mercury content in the waste liquid is extremely low after testing. The mercury content can be directly discharged and does not pollute the environment. The recycling method is completely environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for recycle of low mercury catalyst, which involves weighing the low-mercury catalyst (1) into an extraction vessel, adding 15% aqueous hydrogen chloride solution, heating at 85 degrees C, and acidifying after stirring, extracting subcritical carbon dioxide (2) having volume ratio to acidified low mercury catalyst of 9:1, heating temperature of 50 degrees C, extraction pressure of 3.6 MPa, carbon dioxide flow rate of 24 kg/hour, extraction time of 4.5 hours, analytical pressure of 8 MPa, and analytical temperature of 23 degrees C to obtained activated carbon, extracting pyrrole (3) at a temperature of 32 degrees C, converting obtained mercury-containing filtrate into mercury oxide precipitate using sodium carbonate solution, and drying the mercury oxide precipitate to obtain constant weight.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The alkali immersion is carried out in alkali is 35% potassium hydroxide solution for 12 hours, and dried in a converter. The degree of vacuum is 0.08 MPa, and analyzing temperature of 125?OC. The mass ratio of carbonized material to the anhydrous potassium hydroxide is 4:5, and the analyzing temperature of 412 degrees C. The mercury chloride (1) comprises analyzing pressure of 0.6 MPa and the mass space velocity is 7 hour-1. The mercury chloride (2) comprises the pressure of 0.8 MPa, the temperature of 535 degrees C, and mass space velocity of 7.5 h-1. The immersion aid liquid includes microwave-ultrasonic treatment, microwave frequency of 330 MHz, an ultrasonic frequency of 130 kHz. The ratio of zinc chloride, barium chloride, tetraammine platinum chloride and potassium humate is 8:3:1:2. Preferred Conditions: The treatment time of mercury chloride is 10 minutes, and the analyzing temperature of the mercury chloride is 565 degrees C. Preferred Process: The step (1) involves preparing auxiliary liquid using hydrochloric acid and demineralized water to prepare a hydrochloric acid solution of pH 3, and adding 15 %mass synergistic accelerator. Preferred Properties: The activated carbon comprises specific surface area of 3012 m2/g, a specific gravity of 481 g/L, a ash content of 1.8%, a mechanical strength of 99.2%, a mesoporosity of 99.0%, and a hardness of 2H, after soaking in water for 15-25 days and drying.

AU KEMMOCHI C
   IRIYAMA K
AE ORGANO CORP (JAOR-C)
GA 2018249041
AB    NOVELTY - Deoxidization processing of to-be-processed water (BT) involves dissolving carbon dioxide gas in the to-be-processed water containing dissolved oxygen and a hardness component, obtaining carbonated water having a Langelier index of less than -1, and deoxidization-processing carbonated water by gas-liquid gas-exchange process using a gas-permeable film (2,4).
   USE - Deoxidization processing of to-be-processed water for manufacturing deoxidized carbonated water (claimed). Uses include but are not limited to refined sake, wine, shochu liquor, soft drink, and fruit juice drink.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient deoxidization processing of to-be-processed water with suppressed precipitation of carbonate in the liquid contact surface of gas-permeable film.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture of deoxidized carbonated water (AT); and
   (2) system (40) for deoxidization-processing to-be-processed water, which consists of a unit (X) for obtaining carbonated water and a unit (Y) for carrying out deoxidation treatment of carbonated water.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of deoxidization processing system.
   Gas-liquid gas-exchange unit (1)
   Gas-permeable films (2,4)
   System (40)
   Deoxidized carbonated water (AT)
   To-be-processed water (BT)
   Gaseous phase (p)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The unit (X) merges the inert gas containing carbon dioxide gas in a circulation pipe which circulates the to-be-processed liquid, and dissolves the carbon dioxide gas in the to-be-processed water. The unit (Y) introduces the carbonated water into a film surface (K) of gas-permeable film, and allows the carbonated water to contact the surface (K). The deoxidization processing system has a multistage unit configuration in which 2 or more gas-liquid gas-exchange units (1) are arranged in series.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The content of hardness component in the to-be-processed water is 10-2000 mg calcium carbonate/L. Preferred Process: Deoxidization processing of to-be-processed water further involves making an inert gas containing carbon dioxide gas into a gaseous phase (p), when preparing the carbonated water, and using nitrogen gas and/or carbon dioxide gas as a gaseous phase of gas-liquid gas-exchange in the deoxidation treatment of carbonated water, while air-evacuating the gaseous phase of gas-liquid gas-exchange.

PN CN110609062-A
TI Carbon dioxide tester for determining carbon dioxide content in foods such as beverages, in which titration measurement mode and conductivity measurement mode are used to determine carbon dioxide content in food.
AU LIU T
AE LIU T (LIUT-Individual)
GA 202000881U
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide tester has a micro-vacuum diaphragm pump (5), a sealed gas path, a sealed pressure-resistant silicon boron glass bottle (1), a conductivity measuring instrument, a shut-off valve (8), and a syringe (9). The micro-vacuum diaphragm pump is set to forms a gas circulation path by the use of a sealed design. The titration measurement mode and the conductivity measurement mode are used to determine the carbon dioxide content in food. The micro-vacuum diaphragm pump uses a chemically inert ethylene propylene diene monomer (EPDM) rubber diaphragm material, PTFE, polyethylene or polypropylene cavity and is driven by a brushless motor. The pipeline is a silicone pipe or a PTFE pipe. The sealed pressure-resistant silicon boron glass bottle is set to use 3.3 silicon boron glass with low expansion rate, high temperature resistance, high strength, high hardness, high light transmittance and high chemical stability. The volume is 100 mL and the polypropylene GL45 screw cap.
   USE - Carbon dioxide tester such as single-channel titration mode-carbon dioxide content for determining carbon dioxide content in foods such as beverages, beer, champagne and sparkling wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The overall device of the carbon dioxide tester is simple and small in size.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the single-channel conductivity mode-carbon dioxide content measuring device.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hydrogen involves using liquid oxide                and supercritical water by mixing coal, biomass                and/or rubbish with supercritical aqueous water to                generate hydrogen and carbon dioxide in the liquid                process, where the heat generated by the exothermic                reaction in the reaction process is absorbed by the                gasification reaction to prepare hydrogen.

   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare hydrogen with                new-type liquid oxide and supercritical water,                which has high reaction rate and high gasification                rate, and provides hydrogen with ultra-critical                water and oxide, and hydrogen peroxide                decomposition heat in the reaction process, and the                heat discharged by carbon and oxygen reaction. The                chemical energy of the coal (biomass, rubbish and                so on) is converted into hydrogen energy, realizing                the hydrogen production purpose.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a liquid oxide and supercritical water                hydrogen production device for realizing the method                for preparing hydrogen which comprises a fixed bed                reaction kettle the fixed bed reaction kettle                provided with coal and or biomass and or rubbish                liquid oxide, the reaction space of the                supercritical water gasification reaction, the                upper unit of the fixed bed is reaction kettle with                a feeding port, the lower unit is provided with a                slag discharging port, the lower unit of the fixed                bed reaction kettle provided with a supercritical                water inlet, the upper unit is provided with a                gasification gas outlet, the fixed bed is reaction                kettle with an inlet for passing liquid oxide into                the fixed bed reaction kettle, coal and/or biomass                and/or rubbish is added to the fixed bed reaction                kettle, after finishing feeding, stopping feeding                supercritical water, the oxide is liquidated into                the fixed bed reaction kettle, supercritical water                in the fixed bed reaction kettle coal and/or                biomass and/or rubbish liquid, the oxide reaction                to generate hydrogen and carbon dioxide mixed gas,                the generated hydrogen and carbon dioxide mixed gas                discharged from the gasification gas outlet of the                fixed bed reaction kettle;
   (2) a device for preparing hydrogen by using                the new liquid of oxide and supercritical water for                realizing the method for preparing hydrogen which                comprises an air floating bed reaction kettle, a                cyclone separator, a reflux bed reaction kettle,                the air floating bed reaction kettle provided with                supercritical water, coal and/or biomass rubbish or                the reaction space liquid the oxide reaction, the                mixture discharged by the reaction in the air                flotation bed reaction kettle entered into the                cyclone separator, the ash density is greater than                the density of the carbon the ash is discharged                through the cyclone separator, the carbon enters                into the reflux bed reaction kettle to the                gasification gas, and the reaction is continued in                the reflux bed reaction kettle recycling the                un-reacted in the air flotation, reaction kettle                and reciprocating reaction.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The liquid process is                  hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) beakers into water (H2O)                  and 0.5 oxygen gas (O2) in an exothermic                  reaction, carbon (C) is reacted with O2 to give                  carbon dioxide (CO2) in an exothermic reaction, C                  is reacted with supercritical 2H2O to give CO2                  and 2 hydrogen (2H2) in an endothermic reaction,                  The overall reaction is reacting H2O2 with 3.5C                  and 5H2O (supercritical) to give 3.5CO2 and                  6H2.

PT P
PN CN110972806-A
TI Culturing vermillion sulfur bacteria strains involves inoculating original species of vermillion sulfur bacteria in artificial cultivation medium for germination, where artificial cultivation medium includes oak wood shavings.
AU ZHANG Y
   WANG Y
   LI Y
   DING J
AE ANKANG AGRIC SCI RES INST (ANKA-Non-standard)
GA 2020314940
AB    NOVELTY - Culturing vermillion sulfur bacteria strains involves inoculating the original species of vermillion sulfur bacteria in the artificial cultivation medium for germination. The germination conditions include temperature of 15-18 degrees C, a relative humidity of 55-65%, a carbon dioxide concentration of 0.8-1.0%, and cultivation in the dark until the feed bag is covered with hyphae. The artificial cultivation medium is prepared by placing the artificial cultivation medium in a polyethylene bag for sterilization and standby. The formulation of the artificial cultivation medium includes 45 wt.% oak wood shavings, 40 wt.% Eucommia twig, 10 wt.% bran Skin, 2 wt.% sucrose, 1 wt.% gypsum, 1 wt.% humus, and 1 wt.% calcium carbonate. The low temperature induction is performed on the material bag overgrown with mycelium to generate primordia.
   USE - Method for culturing vermillion sulfur bacteria strains.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to culture vermillion sulfur bacteria strains, where primordium smoothly transitioned to reproductive growth, and the management of the bud differentiation stage is very critical, which is an important condition for obtaining high and stable production of sulfur bacteria.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Culturing vermillion sulfur bacteria strains involves inoculating the original species of vermillion sulfur bacteria in the artificial cultivation medium for germination. The germination conditions include temperature of 15-18 degrees C, a relative humidity of 55-65%, a carbon dioxide concentration of 0.8-1.0%, and cultivation in the dark until the feed bag is covered with hyphae. The artificial cultivation medium is prepared by placing the artificial cultivation medium in a polyethylene bag for sterilization and standby. The formulation of the artificial cultivation medium includes 45 wt.% oak wood shavings, 40 wt.% Eucommia twig, 10 wt.% bran Skin, 2 wt.% sucrose, 1 wt.% gypsum, 1 wt.% humus, and 1 wt.% calcium carbonate. The low temperature induction is performed on the material bag overgrown with mycelium to generate primordia. The primordium differentiates into vermillion sulfur bacterial fruit bodies under the conditions of a temperature of 14-18 degrees C, a relative air humidity of 80-90%, a carbon dioxide concentration of 1.5% -2.0%, and a light intensity of 200-500 lux. The growth of the vermillion sulfur bacterial fruiting bodies stops and harvested. The vermillion sulfur sulfuric acid bacterial strains are isolated from the harvested vermillion sulfur bacterial fruiting bodies and stored on an inclined plane. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for performing artificial cultivation of vermillion sulfur bacteria, which involves:
   (A) inoculating vermillion sulfur bacteria strain (Laetiporussulphureus) LS-AK405 (CGMCC No. 18155) in an artificial cultivation medium for development bacteria;
   (B) preparing artificial cultivation medium includes placing the artificial cultivation medium in a polyethylene bag for sterilization;
   (C) forming low temperature induction of primordia on the bag covered with mycelium;
   (D) differentiating primordium into vermillion sulfur bacterial fruit bodies under the conditions of a temperature of 14-18 degrees C, an air relative humidity of 80-90%, a carbon dioxide concentration of 1.5-2.0%, and a light intensity of 200-500 lux; and
   (E) harvesting to obtain vermillion sulfur bacteria when the fruit body of vermillion sulfur bacteria stops growing and the color changes from light yellow to orange.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The conditions for generating primordium at low temperature include relative air humidity 75-85%, carbon dioxide concentration 1.5-2.0%, light intensity 100-200lx, day temperature 18-20 degrees C, night temperature 8-12 degrees C. The vermillion sulfur bacteria is Laetiporus sulphureus LS-AK405 deposited at (CGMCC No: 18155). Preferred Process: The preparation method of the original species of vermillion sulfur bacteria includes inoculating the activated wild vermillion sulfur bacteria species on the vermillion sulfur bacteria liquid medium for cultivation to obtain the vermillion sulfur bacteria liquid liquid species. The liquid medium formulation includes 15g glucose, 15g sucrose, 5.0g peptone, 2g yeast extract, 0.5g potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 1.0g magnesium sulfate, 2mg vitamin B4, and 1000mL water. The liquid strain is inoculated into the original culture, 200-300 lux scattered light treatment is carried out to obtain the original species of sulfur bacteria of Vermilion red. Preferred Composition: The preservation medium recipe for oblique preservation includes 25g wheat, 20g bran, 80-150mL nutrient solution.

PN FR3074158-A1
TI Steam reforming natural gas using steam reforming reactor, carbon dioxide conversion reactor, called a water gas shift reactor, and hydrogen purification unit by pressure swing absorption, in order to produce a synthesis gas.
AU GUILLOU F
   SURLA K
   AMBROSINO J
   BERTHOLIN S
AE IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
GA 201946800F
AB    NOVELTY - Steam reforming natural gas using a steam reforming reactor (3000), a carbon dioxide conversion reactor (3001), called a water gas shift (WGS) reactor, and a hydrogen purification unit by pressure swing absorption (PSA) (4300), in order to produce a synthesis gas progressively enriched in hydrogen in the unit of WGS (3001) and in the purification unit (4300), in which process the calories necessary for the steam reforming reaction are brought to the level of the reactor, by combustion fumes (400) generated in a combustion chamber under pressure of 1.5-4 (preferably 2-3) bar, at a temperature level of 950 degrees C and 1300 degrees C, preferably 1000-1200 degrees C, combustion fumes which after giving a portion of their calories in the reactor to become fumes are fed into the exchanger (1007) at a temperature level temperature of 450-750 degrees C, so as to produce a superheated steam stream for covering the water vapor requirement of the process, is claimed.
   USE - The process is useful for steam reforming natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The process less expensive, and simplifies the exchangers placed on the flue gases at the outlet of the reactor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Steam reforming natural gas using a steam reforming reactor (3000), a carbon dioxide conversion reactor (3001), called a water gas shift (WGS) reactor, and a hydrogen purification unit by pressure swing absorption (PSA) (4300), in order to produce a synthesis gas progressively enriched in hydrogen in the unit of WGS (3001) and in the purification unit (4300), in which process the calories necessary for the steam reforming reaction are brought to the level of the reactor, by combustion fumes (400) generated in a combustion chamber under pressure of 1.5-4 (preferably 2-3) bar, at a temperature level of 950 degrees C and 1300 degrees C, preferably 1000-1200 degrees C, combustion fumes which after giving a portion of their calories in the reactor to become fumes are fed into the exchanger (1007) at a temperature level temperature of 450-750 degrees C, so as to produce a superheated steam stream for covering the water vapor requirement of the process by reaching a molar ratio (water/carbon) at the level of the steam reforming exchanger reactor of 2-3.5, preferably 2-3, the water required for the process from the flask in which the water is at its point of bubble, the generation of water vapor under pressure being at the level of the exchanger between the stream of raw synthesis gas directly from the steam reforming exchanger reactor and a flow of water of boiler at its bubble point from the steam generation flask and returning to the flask by the flow taken out of the exchanger, the synthesis gas being cooled at 5-50 degrees C above the boiling point of the boiler water, and the combustion chamber being fed by compressed air by means of the air compressor powered by electrical energy, either completely external to the process, or partly produced by the process itself, is claimed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of single combustion chamber steam reforming process.
   Combustion fumes (400)
   Exchanger (1007)
   Steam reforming reactor (3000)
   Carbon dioxide conversion reactor (3001)
   Hydrogen purification unit by PSA (4300)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The electrical energy required for the air compressor supplying the combustion chamber is: entirely of external origin to the process; partially supplied by the process by means of a Rankine cycle using as a hot source the steam water from the flask, and as a cold source of ambient air, and using an intermediate heat transfer fluid changing state between the hot source and the cold source, preferably pentane; partially supplied by the method by means of a Rankine cycle using as a hot source the gas of synthesis taken at the level of the flow, that is to say downstream of the exchangers, and as a cold source the ambient air, and using an intermediate heat transfer fluid changing state between the hot source and the cold source, preferably pentane; or partially supplied by the process by means of a Rankine cycle using the fumes as a hot source of combustion taken at the level of the exchanger, and as a cold source the ambient air, and using an intermediate heat transfer fluid changing state between the hot source and the cold source, preferably pentane. The fuel used in the combustion chamber is mainly constituted by the purge gas of the PSA purification unit of the synthesis gas from the steam reforming exchanger reactor. A backup fuel is used in the combustion chamber, which fuel is a light fuel gas available on site, i.e. fuel gas.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN109692149-A
TI Preparing halloysite-montmorillonite complex aerogel material comprises e.g. taking dispersion and lovastatin solution, dispersing, placing product in carbon dioxide supercritical high pressure extraction apparatus and supercritical drying.
AU LI H
   LI R
   LIU H
   YANG A
   LIAO X
AE UNIV TIANJIN CHENGJIAN (UTCJ-C)
GA 201941804G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing halloysite-montmorillonite complex aerogel material comprises e.g. (i) adding halloysite nanotube into a mixture of deionized water and ethanol, and ultrasonically dispersing to obtain halloysite dispersion, adding sodium styrene sulfonate, polydivinylsiloxane, initiator, dexamethasone into deionized water and ethanol, stirring, adding the solution into halloysite dispersion, ultrasonically dispersing, vacuuming, washing, dispersing water, heating and dispersing into water to obtain the dispersion; (ii) drying montmorillonite, dispersing in solvent, mechanically stirring montmorillonite dispersion, and ultrasonically treating to obtain a dispersion; and (iii) taking dispersion obtained in step (i), dispersion obtained in step (ii) and lovastatin solution, dispersing in a dispersion medium, adding initiator and stirring uniformly, heating, placing the product in carbon dioxide supercritical high pressure extraction apparatus, and supercritical drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing halloysite-montmorillonite complex aerogel material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increased soaking time, and achieves effect of taking the drug once and administering it multiple times.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing halloysite-montmorillonite complex aerogel material comprises (i) adding 0.5-15 pts. wt. halloysite nanotube into a mixture of 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol, and ultrasonically dispersing to obtain halloysite dispersion, adding 0.5-15 pts. wt. sodium styrene sulfonate, 0.05-2 pts. wt. polydivinylsiloxane, 0.01-0.5 pts. wt. initiator, 0.05-10 pts. wt. dexamethasone into a mixture of 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol, stirring uniformly, adding the solution into halloysite dispersion, ultrasonically dispersing, vacuuming, maintaining the vacuum, returning to normal pressure, repeating vacuuming for 3 times, washing the product, dispersing in 100 pts. wt. water, heating to 70-80 degrees C in a water bath to initiate polymerization for 50 hours, washing the dispersion and dispersing into 100 pts. wt. water to obtain the dispersion, where the average molecular weight of polydivinylsiloxane is 500-5000, preferably 1000-3000, the molecular vinyl content (i.e. the ratio of the number of moles of vinyl to the number of moles of monodimethyldivinylsiloxane throughout the amino group) is 0.1-5% of the amino-terminated polydimethyldivinylsiloxane alkyl or amino double-terminated polydimethyldivinylsiloxane; (ii) drying 0.05-15 pts. wt. montmorillonite, dispersing in 0.05-0.25 wt.% solvent, mechanically stirring montmorillonite dispersion, and ultrasonically treating to obtain a dispersion; and (iii) taking 0.5-15 pts. wt. dispersion obtained in step (i), 0.5-35 pts. wt. dispersion obtained in step (ii) and 0.1-20 pts. wt. lovastatin solution mixed in a mass fraction of 0.05-20 wt.%, dispersing in a dispersion medium, adding 0.01-2 pts. wt. initiator and stirring uniformly, heating to 70-90 degrees C in a water bath to initiate the polymerization reaction for 37 hours, placing the product in carbon dioxide supercritical high pressure extraction apparatus, and supercritical drying at 10-300 degrees C for 1 hour with a pressure of 1-20 MPa using carbon dioxide as a medium, where the average specific surface area of the material is 600-608 m2g-1, both nanoscale and microscale pores exist in the halloysite-montmorillonite complex aerogel material, the average nanoscale pore is 10-12 nm and the average micron-scale pore is 20-22 mu m.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises adding 1-10 pts. wt. halloysite nanotube into a mixture of deionized water and ethanol, and ultrasonically dispersing for 1 hour to obtain halloysite dispersion, adding 1-10 pts. wt. sodium styrene sulfonate, 0.1-1 pts. wt. polydivinylsiloxane, 0.01-0.1 pts. wt. initiator, 0.1-5 pts. wt. dexamethasone into a mixture of deionized water and ethanol, stirring uniformly for 10-60 minutes, adding the solution into halloysite dispersion, ultrasonically dispersing for 30 minutes, vacuuming, maintaining the vacuum for 1 hour, returning to normal pressure, repeating vacuuming for 3 times, washing the product, dispersing in water, polymerizing in a water bath at 70-80 degrees C for 30-60 minutes, maintaining in a constant temperature water bath at 40-60 degrees C for 12-24 hours after polymerization, and separately polymerizing in a constant temperature water bath at 80 degrees C, 90 degrees C, and 100 degrees C for 2-8 hours. The step (ii) comprises drying montmorillonite at 60-180 degrees C for 5-20 hours, taking 0.1-10 pts. wt. dried montmorillonite and dispersing in 0.1-20 wt.% solvent, mechanically stirring montmorillonite dispersion at 150-400 revolutions/minute for 5-35 degrees C at 4-10 hours, and ultrasonically treating at 50-300 W for 6-15 hours. The step (iii) comprises taking 1-10 pts. wt. dispersion obtained in step (i), 1-30 pts. wt. dispersion obtained in step (ii) and 0.5-15 pts. wt. lovastatin solution mixed in a mass fraction of 0.05-15 wt.%, dispersing in 10-10000 times the amount of dispersion medium, adding 0.01-1 pts. wt. initiator and stirring uniformly for 1 hour, polymerizing in a water bath at 70-90 degrees C for 30-60 minutes, maintaining in a constant temperature water bath at 30-50 degrees C for 12-24 hours after polymerization, and separately polymerizing in a constant temperature water bath at 80 degrees C, 90 degrees C, and 100 degrees C for 2-8 hours, where the freeze-drying is carried out at -5- 108 degrees C for 2-48 hours with humidity of 0-60 degrees C and vacuum degree of 10-5000 Pa, and supercritical drying for 2-4 hours, preferably 3 hours. Preferred Components: The initiator is dibenzoyl peroxide or azobisisobutyronitrile.
