PN CN109613136-A
TI Determining anaerobic digestion process using carbon stable isotope fractionation ratio by connecting hose to outlet of fermenter, collecting biogas with syringe, pumping to vacuum bottle, determining methane and carbon dioxide.
AU LV Z
   CHU Y
   YUAN B
   LIANG J
AE UNIV JIANGSU NORMAL (UJSN-C)
GA 2019356985
AB    NOVELTY - Method for determining an anaerobic digestion process using carbon stable isotope fractionation ratio, involves performing biogas collection by connecting the hose to the outlet of the fermenter, the hose has a gas stop valve, and the inner diameter of the hose and the 20 ml syringe opening are the same size, which helps to seal and collect gas, collecting 20 ml biogas with a 20 ml syringe and pumping into 20 ml vacuum bottle, determining delta 13/12 methane and delta 13/12 carbon dioxide by gas-filling 50 mu l above biogas was into the gas chromatograph, calculating delta 13C value (%) delta 13C-CH4 and delta 13C-CO2 values and calculating the ratio alpha of delta 13/12 methane and delta 13/12 carbondioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for determining an anaerobic digestion process using carbon stable isotope fractionation ratio.
   ADVANTAGE - The method breaks through the technical obstacles such as time-consuming, labour and cost, and application potential of traditional molecular biotechnology, achieves rapid reflection of methanogenic metabolic pathways during anaerobic digestion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for determining an anaerobic digestion process using carbon stable isotope fractionation ratio, involves (a) performing biogas collection by connecting the hose to the outlet of the fermenter, the hose has a gas stop valve, and the inner diameter of the hose and the 20 ml syringe opening are the same size, which helps to seal and collect gas, collecting 20 ml biogas with a 20 ml syringe and pumping into 20 ml vacuum bottle, (b) determining delta 13/12 methane and delta 13/12 carbon dioxide by gas-filling 50 mu l above biogas was into the gas chromatograph, the carrier gas is high purity helium is 99.999%, and column flow rate is 1.5 ml/minutes, the inlet temperature is 120 degrees C without split injection, the furnace temperature is 960 degrees C, the reduction furnace temperature is 640 degrees C, IRMS ionization energy is 80eV, emission current 1.5mA, acceleration voltage is 3kV, the degree of vacuum is 2x 10-8 kPa, (c) calculating delta 13C value (%) delta 13C-methane and delta 13C-carbondioxide values using the PDB international standard as the reference standard, and calculating value by using specific equation involving a predetermined parameters such as R(13C/12CVPDB), 13C/12C sample and 13C/12CVPDB, where R(13C/12CVPDB) is the carbon isotope abundance ratio of the international standard VPDB, 13C/12C sample represents the values of delta 13C-methane and delta 13C-carbondioxide measured in biogas, and 13C/12CVPDB represents the values of delta 13C-methane and delta 13C-carbondioxide under international standards, and the analytical precision is plus minus 0.2%, and (d) calculating the ratio alpha of delta 13/12 methane and delta 13/12 carbondioxide as alpha A, B is ( delta A+103)/( delta B+103), where delta A is delta 13/12 methane and delta B is delta 13/12 carbon dioxide, if alpha more than 1.065, the methanogenic process is a hydrophilic methanogenic metabolic pathway, if alpha less than 1.025, the methanogenic process is an acetic acid-type methanogenic metabolic pathway, if 1.025 not less than alpha not less than 1.065, and methane comes from the above two pathways.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: In the preparation method, the alpha 13/12 methane is the fraction of 13C and 12C in methane. The alpha 13/12 carbon dioxide is the 13C and 12C fractionation ratio in carbon dioxide.

PN CN109456801-A; CN109456801-B
TI Method for co-production of nano-silica composite material and hydrogen-rich high-energy gas from biomass involves pyrolyzing biomass, producing clean gas, adding hydrochloric acid, heating, oxidizing, activating and adding natural latex.
AU YANG X
   WANG X
   ZHU Y
   TIAN Y
   WANG Z
AE UNIV JILIN (UYJI-C)
GA 201926053N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for co-production of nano-silica composite material and hydrogen-rich high-energy gas from biomass involves adding biomass to a pyrolysis furnace, spraying the catalyst aqueous solution, dehydrating, pyrolyzing, producing high-energy gas, passing gas to a dilute solution of 2-5 wt.% calcium hydroxide, removing residual tar and dust to prepare clean gas, adjusting the total equivalent ratio of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide in the clean gas to 2-2.05, transferring the remaining solid to a reaction vessel, adding 3-5 wt.% hydrochloric acid solution, heating to 100-150 degrees C, cooling, filtering, washing the filter cake with water, drying, pulverizing, dispersing to prepare a silica/carbon powder material, oxidizing, adding activator, adding 1 wt.% ammonia solution, adding a natural latex solution having solid content of 15 wt.%, adding 1-2 wt.% formic acid, aging for 4-8 hours, washing with water 3-5 times, and drying.
   USE - Method for co-production of nano-silica composite material and hydrogen-rich high-energy gas for power generation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for co-production of nano-silica composite material and hydrogen-rich high-energy gas from biomass involves preparing a catalyst aqueous solution containing 5-20 wt.% catalyst consisting of ammonium chloride, potassium chloride, potassium carbonate, potassium hydroxide, calcium chloride, calcium hydroxide, sodium carbonate, sodium hydroxide and/or iron chloride, continuously adding biomass to a pyrolysis furnace, spraying the catalyst aqueous solution, dehydrating at 200-300 degrees C, pyrolyzing at 700-990 degrees C, reducing temperature to 350-400 degrees C, separating mixed gas and residual solid by a cyclone separator, removing the tar from the obtained mixed gas in electrostatic decoking tower, producing high-energy gas having total equivalent ratio of hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide of 1-2, passing resulting gas to a dilute solution of 2-5 wt.% calcium hydroxide, removing residual tar and dust to prepare clean gas, recycling the obtained wastewater for preparing aqueous solution of catalyst, adjusting the concentration of the calcium hydroxide solution and the reaction time for absorbing the excess carbon dioxide in the clean gas, adjusting the total equivalent ratio of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide in the clean gas to 2-2.05, transferring the remaining solid to a reaction vessel, adding 3-5 wt.% hydrochloric acid solution, heating to 100-150 degrees C, reacting for 20 minutes, cooling, filtering, returning the filtrate for preparing aqueous solution of the catalyst, washing the filter cake with water, drying, pulverizing, dispersing to prepare a silica/carbon powder material, treating the powder material in a rotary oxidation furnace at 30-50 degrees C, oxidizing for 30-90 minutes, adding the oxidized nano-silica/carbon powder to a high-speed stirrer, adding activator in mass ratio of (1-5):100, rotating at 300-700 rpm, activating for 13-60 minutes, adding activated substance to a reaction container, adding 1 wt.% ammonia solution, adding a natural latex solution having solid content of 15 wt.%, adding 1-2 wt.% formic acid to neutralize the precipitate, aging for 4-8 hours, washing with water 3-5 times, and drying at 100-120 degrees C for 8-12 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The pyrolysis furnace is a downdraft pyrolysis furnace consisting of a pyrolysis section, a tar conversion section, a water gas reaction section and a cooling section. The carbon content of the pyrolysis slag is controlled to 5-15 wt.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The addition amount of the catalyst is 5-20 wt.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The activator is a silane coupling agent, a titanate coupling agent and/or carboxylated polyethylene. The biomass is straw, rice husk, corn stover, corn cob or forestry waste.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. PN CN109182956-A
TI Surface pretreatment of car brake pad comprises heat-treating, cutting brake pad blank by press molding, subjecting to a sanding treatment, cleaning process, placing brake pad in an electric furnace and maintaining the pressure in furnace.
AU SUN Q
AE NINGGUO FEIYING AUTO PARTS CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2019092784
AB    NOVELTY - Surface pretreatment of car brake pad comprises (a) heat-treating and cutting the brake pad blank obtained by press molding to obtain a pre-treatment brake pad; (b) subjecting brake pad pretreated in step (a) to a sanding treatment and a cleaning process, placing the brake pad in an electric furnace; (c) placing brake pad processed in step (b) in a vacuum electric furnace, maintaining the pressure in furnace, introducing carbon dioxide and ammonia gas, controlling flow rate of carbon dioxide and ammonia gas for carbon and nitrogen infiltration and finally cooling to room temperature to obtain carbon and nitrogen infiltration treated brake pads;(d) sandblasting the brake sheet of carbon and nitrogen infiltration treatment, spraying quartz sand, corundum or iron sand on surface of the brake pad by using compressed air as a power to obtain sandblasted brake pad; and (e) subjecting brake pad after sandblasting process to secondary cleaning treatment, drying and applying the glue.
   USE - The method is useful for surface pretreatment of car brake pad.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improve the wear resistance, surface hardness, corrosion resistance, thermal fatigue resistance, anti-adhesion properties and service life and can reduce internal stress of the brake pad.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Surface pretreatment of car brake pad comprises (a) heat-treating and cutting the brake pad blank obtained by press molding to obtain a pre-treatment brake pad; (b) subjecting brake pad pretreated in step (a) to a sanding treatment and a cleaning process, placing the brake pad in an electric furnace, heating at 750-850 degrees C, allowing to stand for 65-70 minutes and heating again at 980-1010 degrees C for 41-45 minutes of heat preservation, cooling at room temperature of 40 degrees C/minute, heating up at 550-570 degrees C three times, warming for 20-23 minutes and cooling at room temperature with the furnace to obtain brake after treatment sheet; (c) placing the brake pad processed in step (b) in a vacuum electric furnace, maintaining the pressure in the furnace at 0 Pa, heating at 420-450 degrees C for 65-80 minutes, heating at 565-575 degrees C and then vacuuming, maintaining device pressure in the electric furnace at 500-550Pa, introducing carbon dioxide and ammonia gas, controlling flow rate of carbon dioxide at 1.6-1.7 m3/hour, and the flow rate of ammonia gas at 20-24 m3/hour for carbon and nitrogen infiltration for 3-4 hours and finally cooling to room temperature to obtain carbon and nitrogen infiltration treated brake pads;(d) sandblasting the brake sheet of the carbon and nitrogen infiltration treatment, spraying quartz sand, corundum or iron sand on the surface of the brake pad by using compressed air as a power, where the pressure of the compressed air is 0.9-1 MPa to obtain sandblasted brake pad; and (e) subjecting brake pad after the sandblasting process to secondary cleaning treatment, applying a layer of the treatment liquid, drying and applying the glue.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (e) the treatment liquid is a dilute solution prepared by mixing silane coupling agent and solvent with a silane coupling agent having concentration of 0.5-1%. In the step (a) heating the brake pad blank into an electric furnace at a heating temperature of 450-490 degrees C and a heating time of 9-10 hours. In the step (a) cooling the heat-treated brake pad blank, first drilling and chamfering it on the drill bed and then moving to the sander to remove the flash and burr. In the step (a) and (e) the cleaning process and the secondary cleaning process include surface degreasing and water washing and the surface degreasing is carried out by ultrasonic cleaning to remove the oil, wax and oxide layer on the surface of the brake pad having the ultrasonic time of 25-40 minutes. Preferred Components: The solvent of the dilute solution is water, methanol, and ethanol. The dilute solution contains silicone surfactant having a mass fraction of 0.1-0.2%.

PN CN108148787-A
TI Large scale culturing nostoc comprises e.g. artificially disinfecting and washing nostoc, grinding, centrifuging to obtain filamentous algae and algae section solution, detecting impurity-free algae to obtain purified algae species.
AU XIN M
   SHEN Y
   PANG Y
   SHI C
   GU R
   JIA X
AE QINGDAO HAORAN ECOLOGICAL MARINE FISHERY (QING-Non-standard)
GA 201847334V
AB    NOVELTY - Large scale culturing nostoc comprises e.g. (1) artificially disinfecting and washing the nostoc, grinding, centrifuging to obtain the filamentous algae and algae section solution; detecting the impurity-free algae to obtain purified algae species, inoculating the purified algae species in culture vessel, and culturing; (2) subjecting the algae species obtained in the step (1) to tertiary culture, inoculating the purified algae species into first-stage culture container, culturing to obtain first-stage seed, inoculating the first-stage seed in secondary culture container, culturing to obtain secondary seeds, inoculating the secondary seeds in three-stage culture container, and culturing to obtain three-stage seeds; (3) taking purified algae species as inoculum, placing in culture vessel at ratio of 3-5 g wet weight per liter of culture solution, introducing carbon dioxide by using air pump, where flow rate of carbon dioxide is to ensure entire reaction liquid is in moderate flow state.
   USE - The method is useful for large scale culturing nostoc.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, economical, and suitable for large-scale artificial culturing; uses simple equipment; has no season restriction; produces product having large size, rich microelement content, safe and non-toxic.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Large scale culturing nostoc comprises (1) artificially disinfecting and washing the nostoc, grinding, centrifuging to obtain the filamentous algae and algae section solution; detecting the impurity-free algae to obtain purified algae species, inoculating the purified algae species in culture vessel, and culturing; (2) subjecting the algae species obtained in the step (1) to tertiary culture, inoculating the purified algae species into first-stage culture container, culturing to obtain first-stage seed, inoculating the first-stage seed in secondary culture container, culturing to obtain secondary seeds, inoculating the secondary seeds in three-stage culture container, and culturing to obtain three-stage seeds; (3) taking purified algae species as inoculum, placing in culture vessel at ratio of 3-5 g wet weight per liter of culture solution, introducing carbon dioxide by using air pump, where flow rate of carbon dioxide is to ensure the entire reaction liquid is in moderate flow state; culturing in first-stage culture container for 25-35 days, growing the purified algae species into fine particles by virtue of filamentous growth, culture solution in the culture container turns black; two-stage culture vessel continuously culturing for 25-35 days, the first-stage seed grows into fine cylindrical shape, and the culture liquid turns black again; and culturing secondary seeds in the three-stage culture container until nostoc sphaeroides with the particle diameter of 1-5 cm; and (4) maintaining the culture temperature at 22-25 degrees C and the illumination at 80-100 mu Em-2.s-1, changing the culture medium for every 10 days, regularly checking whether the inflation is normal or not every day, to ensure the nostoc sphaeroides particles do not sink; and (5) sieving the three-stage cultured nostoc sphaeroides through 2 cm filter sieve, dehydrating, and naturally drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the method, placing the modified BG-11 liquid medium in the culture vessel in step (1), dissolving the whole solution in pure water during the preparation process, fixing volume, sterilizing the culture container by ultraviolet treatment for 12 hours, where the pH of culture solution is 7.8-8.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: In the method, the first-stage culture container is volumetric flask having a volume of 5 l; the two-stage culture vessel is 17 l pure water barrel; and the three-stage culture container is self-made transparent container having volume of more than 100 l.

PN CN107929785-A
TI Essential oil useful for removing peculiar smell, purifying air and refreshing brain, comprises mint essential oil, Artemisia leaves oil, borneol solution and medical alcohol composition.
AU JIANG Y
AE JIANG Y (JIAN-Individual)
GA 2018329154
AB    NOVELTY - Essential oil comprises 65-75% mint essential oil, 8-12% Artemisia leaves oil, 12-18% borneol solution and 4-6% of 10-20% medical alcohol composition.
   USE - The oil is useful for removing peculiar smell; purifying air; and refreshing brain.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple; and has high extraction efficiency and high purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the oil, comprising (i) uniformly stirring raw materials at a high speed, and precisely filtering, (ii) drying the mint till the water content is less than 1%, crushing the material to 0.1-0.5 mm using a mechanical crusher, pouring the powder into a sealed container, where the mass ratio of the power to carbon dioxide is 1:2-3, cooling to temperature at -50 to -40 degrees C under 6-7 atmospheric pressure, completely soaking the powder into the carbon dioxide liquid for 1-2 hours, suddenly releasing the pressure to normal pressure, collecting the powder, adding and mixing potassium phosphate solution, adjusting the pH to 4-5, adding cellulose, completely mixing and stirring, maintaining the temperature to 35-40 degrees C, reacting for 4-6 hours, adding n-pentane at 20-30 degrees C, mixing and stirring the pressure of organic phase under 2-3 atmospheric pressure for 15-20 minutes, allowing to stand and layering to obtain organic phase, distilling at 40-50 degrees C, simultaneously cooling the volatilized fraction at 20-25 degrees C and recycling, distilling the n-pentane to lower than 0.1%, adding tin catalyst, reacting at 35-40 degrees C for 2-3 hours under -0.08 to -0.09 Mpa, and distilling the fraction at 30-45 degrees C to obtain mint essential oil, (iii) drying Artemisia leaves until the water content is less than 1%, crushing the material to 0.1-0.2 mm using the mechanical crusher, pouring the powder into a sealed container, where the mass ratio of the power to carbon dioxide is 1:2-3, cooling to temperature at -50 to -40 degrees C under 6-7 atmospheric pressure, completely soaking the powder into the carbon dioxide liquid for 1-2 hours, suddenly releasing the pressure to normal pressure, collecting the powder, adding and mixing potassium phosphate solution, adjusting the pH to 4-5, adding cellulose, completely mixing and stirring, maintaining the temperature to 35-40 degrees C, reacting for 4-6 hours, adding n-pentane at 20-30 degrees C, mixing and stirring the pressure of organic phase under 2-3 atmospheric pressure for 15-20 minutes, allowing to stand and layering to obtain organic phase, distilling at 40-50 degrees C, simultaneously cooling the volatilized fraction at 20-25 degrees C and recycling, and distilling the n-pentane to lower than 0.1% to obtain Artemisia leaves oil, and (iv) adding borneol leaves to 2-3 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution, stirring at 30-40 degrees C and soaking, filtering, drying the filter residue until the water content is less than 1%, crushing to 0.1-0.2 mm, adding absolute ethyl alcohol at 90-100 degrees C, and reflowing and extracting under 1.5-2 atmospheric pressure for 30-60 minutes, and distilling out the absolute ethyl alcohol at 85-90 degrees C to obtain borneol solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The tin catalyst comprises 5-10% nano-tin dioxide, 0.5-1% tri-nano-iron tetroxide and 90-95% activated alumina. Preferred Method: The preparation of tin catalyst comprises mixing stannous chloride and ferrous chloride, adjusting the pH to 0.1-0.5, adding mixed solution of sodium metaaluminate and aqueous ammonia, adjusting the pH to 5.5-6.5 to obtain a mixture of stannous hydroxide, aluminum hydroxide and ferrous hydroxide, introducing air, heating at 90-95 degrees C, stirring and oxidizing stannous hydroxide to tin hydroxide, converting ferrous hydroxide to triiron tetroxide, filtering, and calcining at 250-300 degrees C. The specific surface area of the activated alumina is greater than 200 m2/g. The specific surface area of the tin catalyst is 25-40 m2/g. The particle size is 5-10 mu . The apparent density is 0.8-1.2 g/ml.

TI New low molecular weight carbon dioxide-cyclohexene oxide copolymer for epoxy resins.
AU WANG X
   LIU S

   WANG Z
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 2022111262

   USE - Low molecular weight carbon dioxide-cyclohexene oxide copolymer (I) for epoxy resins (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Low molecular weight carbon dioxide-cyclohexene oxide copolymer of formula (I), is new.    x=1-70;#y=1-40;#n=2-6; and#R1 is selected from group of formulae (II-III).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (claimed): Low molecular weight carbon dioxide-cyclohexene oxide copolymer of formula (I) is prepared by reacting cyclohexene oxide, catalyst, co-catalyst and initiator under carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain the low molecular weight carbon dioxide-oxidized cyclohexene copolymer of formula (I). Preferred Conditions: The number-average molecular weight of the low-molecular-weight carbon dioxide-cyclohexene oxide copolymer is 500-10,000. The carbon dioxide-cyclohexene oxide copolymer is polycyclohexene carbonate. The catalyzer comprises oligomeric porphyrin aluminum catalyst. The cocatalyst includes bis(triphenylphosphoranyl)ammonium chloride. The initiator includes one or more of carboxylic acid compounds, alcohol compounds and phenolic compounds. The molar ratio of the cyclohexene oxide to the initiator is (10-100): 1. The molar ratio of aluminum metal in the cyclohexene oxide and the oligomeric porphyrin aluminum catalyst is (5000-50000): 1. The molar ratio of the cyclohexene oxide to the cocatalyst is (5000-50000):2. The catalyzer is represented by formula (IV). The initiator comprises terephthalic acid and/or terephthalic alcohol. The temperature of the reaction is 70-150degrees Celsius. The time of the reaction is 4-24. The condition of the carbon dioxide atmosphere is that the pressure of carbon dioxide is 3-6MPa. After the reaction is completed, the steps of washing the product with methanol and heating and purifying under vacuum conditions are also included. The polycyclohexene carbonate includes the carbon dioxide-cyclohexene oxide copolymer. The epoxy-terminated polycyclohexene carbonate is a carbon dioxide-based epoxy resin. The preparation process of the epoxy-terminated polycyclohexene carbonate comprises mixing polycyclohexene carbonate, triethylamine and an organic solvent, then adding methacryloyl chloride to carry out the first reaction to obtain an olefin-terminated polycyclohexene carbonate. After the second reaction of the olefin-terminated polycyclohexene carbonate, a peroxide and an organic solvent, an epoxy-terminated polycyclohexene carbonate is obtained. The temperature of the mixing is -20-20degrees Celsius. The temperature of the first reaction is 25-40degrees Celsius. The time of the first reaction is 0.5-12h. The molar ratio of the polycyclohexene carbonate and triethylamine is 1:(2.8-4.5). The molar ratio of the polycyclohexene carbonate and methacryloyl chloride is 1:(2.4-3.0). The organic solvent includes one or more of dichloromethane, chloroform, ethyl acetate, tetrahydrofuran, dioxane and diethyl ether. The peroxide comprises one or more in m-chloroperoxybenzoic acid, peroxybenzoic acid, peracetic acid and trifluoroperoxyacetic acid. The molar ratio of the olefin-terminated polycyclohexene carbonate to the peroxide is 1: (2.4-4.0). The temperature of the second reaction is 40-50degrees Celsius. The time of the second reaction is 24-72h.x=1-30;#y=1-10; and#n=polymerization degree of oligoporphyrin aluminum, is 4-20.

PT P
PN US2022040626-A1; WO2022035453-A1
TI Cyclic pressure swing adsorption process for            purifying synthesis gas and natural gas to remove water            and carbon dioxide, involves contacting multicomponent            feed gas at elevated pressure with adsorbent bed to            obtain product gas.
AU LEITMAYR W
   KIM K
   HELD K
   SWINDLEHURST G R
   COLEMAN L J
AE LEITMAYR W (LEIT-Individual)
   KIM K (KIMK-Individual)
   HELD K (HELD-Individual)
   SWINDLEHURST G R (SWIN-Individual)
   COLEMAN L J (COLE-Individual)
   PRAXAIR TECHNOLOGY INC (PRAX-C)
GA 202221906D
AB    NOVELTY - Cyclic pressure swing adsorption (PSA) process                involves contacting a multi component feed gas at                elevated pressure with an adsorbent bed to obtain a                multi component product gas substantially free of                water and carbon dioxide (CO2) with high recovery                of the product gas components, where many adsorbent                beds in a PSA system are subjected to a series of                process steps including an adsorption step, three                or more pressure equalization steps at decreasing                pressure, a blow down step, a purge step in which                the purge gas comprises a portion of the product                gas or a gas originating externally from the                process that is substantially free of water and CO2                and comprises the major components of the product                gas, three or more pressure equalization steps at                increasing pressure, and a final re-pressurization                step.
   USE - Cyclic pressure swing adsorption (PSA) process                for purifying a multi component feed gas comprising                a synthesis gas having hydrogen, carbon monoxide,                methane, CO2, and water with high recovery of                hydrogen and carbon monoxide, or a natural gas                comprising methane, nitrogen, CO2, and water with a                high recover of methane and nitrogen, by removing                water and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides purification of synthesis                or natural gases to obtain multicomponent product                gas substantially free of water and carbon dioxide,                at high recovery rate and low capital cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cyclic pressure swing adsorption (PSA) process                involves contacting a multi component feed gas at                elevated pressure with an adsorbent bed to obtain a                multi component product gas substantially free of                water and carbon dioxide (CO2) with high recovery                of the product gas components, where many adsorbent                beds in a PSA system are subjected to a series of                process steps including an adsorption step, three                or more pressure equalization steps at decreasing                pressure, a blow down step, a purge step in which                the purge gas comprises a portion of the product                gas or a gas originating externally from the                process that is substantially free of water and CO2                and comprises the major components of the product                gas, three or more pressure equalization steps at                increasing pressure, and a final re-pressurization                step, the adsorbent beds contain an adsorbent layer                for the substantial removal of carbon dioxide                characterized by (a) carbon dioxide loading                capacity of greater than or equal to 0.1 mol CO2                per kg of adsorbent at CO2 partial pressure of 1000                Pa at 300 K, and (b) a differential capacity of the                product gas components of less than or equal to 0.1                mol per kg of adsorbent, and the differential                capacity is defined as the difference between the                loading capacity at partial pressures of 100,000 Pa                and 10,000 Pa at 300 K.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an alkali-promoted alumina                adsorbent bed to remove water and CO2 from the                multicomponent feed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The water                  concentration in the multicomponent product gas                  is less than or equal to 10 ppb, preferably less                  than or equal to 1 ppb, where CO2 is typically                  removed to below 100 ppb, preferably less than 25                  ppb. The purge gas is the tail gas from hydrogen                  PSA or a membrane unit, or flash gas or crude                  hydrogen gas from a cryogenic separation unit.                  Preferred Method: The synthesis gas is produced                  via steam methane reforming, autothermal                  reforming, or partial oxidation.

The number of adsorbent beds                  simultaneously processing feed gas is 2.

TI Manufacture of grout material used for e.g. civil            engineering work, involves mixing sodium silicate and            water to prepare low-concentration sodium silicate            solution, preparing low-concentration acidic material,            mixing cement with binder containing quicklime and            water, and mixing chemical solution(s).
AU KIM D
AE KIM D (KIMD-Individual)
GA 202229405E
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of grout material involves (a)                mixing sodium silicate and water to prepare a                low-concentration sodium silicate solution, (b)                preparing a low-concentration acidic material, (c)                impregnating the low-concentration acidic material                with carbon dioxide gas, (d) mixing the                low-concentration sodium silicate solution and the                low-concentration acidic material impregnated with                carbon dioxide gas to prepare a chemical solution                (A) in a low-concentration silica sol state, (e)                mixing cement with a binder and water to prepare a                sol-based gypsum-based chemical solution (B), and                (f) mixing the chemical solution (A) and the                chemical solution (B) to prepare a silicate in a                gel state. The step (b) is performed by using 2-8                vol.% low-concentration acidic material comprising                any one of sulfuric acid, hydrochloric acid, nitric                acid, and acetic acid.
   USE - Manufacture of grout material used for                construction of building and civil engineering                work.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical, enables manufacture                of grout material capable of preventing a decrease                in compressive strength and an increase in the                permeability coefficient by minimizing the leaching                phenomenon, and has improved safety and handling                easiness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of grout material involves (a)                mixing sodium silicate and water to prepare a                low-concentration sodium silicate solution, (b)                preparing a low-concentration acidic material, (c)                impregnating the low-concentration acidic material                with carbon dioxide gas, (d) mixing the                low-concentration sodium silicate solution and the                low-concentration acidic material impregnated with                carbon dioxide gas to prepare a chemical solution                (A) in a low-concentration silica sol state, (e)                mixing cement with a binder and water to prepare a                sol-based gypsum-based chemical solution (B), and                (f) mixing the chemical solution (A) and the                chemical solution (B) to prepare a silicate in a                gel state. In step (a), 150 L sodium silicate is                mixed with 350 L of water to prepare a                low-concentration sodium silicate solution, when                preparing 500 L chemical solution (A). The step (b)                is performed by using 2-8 vol.% low-concentration                acidic material comprising any one of sulfuric                acid, hydrochloric acid, nitric acid, and acetic                acid. The step (c) is performed by passing 0.5-10                mL/minute carbon dioxide gas through a gas                injection device for 1 L low-concentration acidic                material, and impregnating the carbon dioxide. The                weight ratio of the low concentration sodium                silicate solution with respect to the low                concentration acidic material impregnated with                carbon dioxide gas is 1:0.6-0.8. The binder is any                one of quicklime, slaked lime, calcium chloride,                calcium carbonate, and desulfurized gypsum which is                an industrial by-product. The weight ratio of                cement with respect to the binder is 1:0.2-0.6. The                weight ratio of the cement, the binder and water is                1:1.0-2.0.

The first and the second gas cooler is provided with a second cooling passage and a second heat exchange passage for cooling the second cooling passage.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide liquefaction device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide liquefaction device comprises a buffer tank, a multi-stage compressor, a gas-liquid separator and a liquid phase carbon dioxide storage device, where the air inlet of the buffer tank is connected to an upstream discharge port for discharging the preliminary purified carbon dioxide gas. The gas outlet of the buffer tank is in communication with the first-stage air inlet of the multi-stage compressor, and the gas-liquid separator is provided with a medium inlet, a top air outlet and a liquid outlet, and is characterized by a first gas cooler and a second gas cooler, the first gas cooler being provided with a first cooling passage and a first heat exchange passage for cooling the first cooling passage. The first and the second gas cooler is provided with a second cooling passage and a second heat exchange passage for cooling the second cooling passage, a final stage air outlet of the multi-stage compressor and the first cooling passage The inlet of the first cooling passage is in communication with the inlet of the second cooling passage. The outlet of the second cooling passage is in communication with the medium inlet of the gas-liquid separator, and the top outlet of the gas-liquid separator passes through the first throttle valve and the inlet of the first heat exchange passage Connected, the outlet of the first heat exchange channel is in communication with an external purge gas discharge port, and the liquid outlet of the gas-liquid separator is provided with a first discharge pipe and a second discharge pipe. The liquid outlet of the gas-liquid separator is connected to the inlet of the liquid phase carbon dioxide storage device through the first discharge line, and the liquid outlet of the gas-liquid separator passes through the second a discharge line is connected to the inlet of the second heat exchange passage, the second discharge line is provided with a second throttle valve, an outlet of the second heat exchange passage and the multi-stage compressor The interstage air inlets are connected. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for using a carbon dioxide liquefaction device, comprising (i) opening a buffer tank, a multi-stage compressor, a first throttle valve, a second throttle valve and a gas-liquid separator, (ii) introducing the initially purified carbon dioxide gas from the upstream discharge port into the buffer tank to obtain the buffered carbon dioxide gas, (iii) feeding the buffered carbon dioxide gas to the multi-stage compressor, and performing stepwise compression cooling treatment to obtain compressed and cooled carbon dioxide gas and (iv) sending the compressed and cooled carbon dioxide gas into the gas-liquid separator through the first cooling passage and the second cooling passage, performing the gas-liquid separation in the gas-liquid separator to obtain liquid carbon dioxide and purge gas, discharging the liquid phase carbon dioxide from the liquid outlet of the gas-liquid separator into the first discharge line and the second discharge line, where the first discharge line discharges the liquid carbon dioxide into the liquid carbon dioxide storage device for storage, discharging the liquid phase carbon dioxide into the second discharge line to obtain low temperature carbon dioxide, transporting the low temperature carbon dioxide to the second heat exchange channel to cool the second cooling passage, obtaining the carbon dioxide gas after heat exchange, which will be exchanged, sending the hot carbon dioxide gas into the interstage air inlet of the multistage compressor for compression, cooling, sending the exhaust gas into the first throttle valve through the top air outlet of the gas-liquid separator for throttling to obtain a low temperature purge gas, sending the low-temperature purge gas into the first heat exchange channel to cool the first cooling channel and obtain the heat-dissipated purge gas and discharging the heat-dissipated purge gas from the external purge gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the carbon dioxide liquefaction device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the gas pressure of the preliminary purified carbon dioxide gas is 0.01-0.03 MPaG and the gas concentration is 60-92%. In the step (ii), the gas concentration of the preliminary purified carbon dioxide gas is 60-85%. Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the pressure of the carbon dioxide gas after compression and cooling is 4-10 MPaG and the gas temperature is 35-45 degrees C. In the step (iv), the gas temperature of the carbon dioxide gas cooled by the compression-cooled carbon dioxide gas by the first gas cooler is 32-42 degrees C. In the step (iv), the temperature of the carbon dioxide after cooling the carbon dioxide gas by the second gas cooler is -30 to 20 degrees C. In the step (iv), the second throttle valve reduces the second discharge line to 0.3-2 MPaG and sends it to the second heat exchange passage. In the step (iv), the ratio of the liquid carbon dioxide discharged from the liquid outlet of the gas-liquid separator into the first discharge line and the liquid carbon dioxide discharged into the second discharge line is 0.2-2.

PN CN109030080-A
TI Sampling method of Mars drilling based on            supercritical carbon dioxide jet, involves recovering            cuttings samples for testing, and feeding test results            back to control center.
AU YANG X
   ZHOU X
   ZHU H
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 2018A4958B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves fixing drilling into a                trolley (2), installing a drill pipe and a drill                bit. The atmospheric carbon dioxide of the Martian                is extracted and is heated and pressurized to                convert the carbon dioxide gas into a supercritical                carbon dioxide jet. A portion of the carbon dioxide                is made into dry ice abrasive particles by a dry                ice grinder, and mixed with a supercritical carbon                dioxide jet. A supercritical carbon dioxide jet                mixed with dry ice abrasive particles is sprayed                from the top nozzle of the drill bit to hit the                rock surface to break the rock. When the jet hits                the rock, a drilling pump (10) is worked to drive                the three-wing drill bit forward. The cuttings                samples are recovered for testing, and the test                results are fed back to a control center (6).
   USE - Sampling method of Mars drilling based on                supercritical carbon dioxide jet.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has the advantages of high rock                breaking efficiency, low energy consumption, and                fast drilling speed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                sampling device of Mars drilling based on                supercritical carbon dioxide jet.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                overall structure of the sampling method of Mars                drilling.

   Control center (6)
   Drilling pump (10)

AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing water decomposition material                comprises (I) preparing a first mixture including a                solid raw material, (II) preparing a second mixture                including the first mixture, an anionic dispersant                and an organic binder, (III) drying and filtering                the second mixture to obtain a filtrate and (III)                calcining filtrate. The second mixture includes 100                pts. wt. solid raw material, 1-10 pts. wt. anionic                dispersant, and 1-10 pts. wt. organic binder, where                the organic binder includes glycol-based binder.                The solid raw material contains 50-90 wt.% iron                oxide-based active material and 10-50 wt.% support                having spinel structure. The support includes                magnesium aluminate, and the iron oxide-based                active material has an average size of &#8804;                17 &#956; m and needle-shaped ferric trioxide                hydrate having a specific surface area of 40-200                m2/g.

TF EXAMPLE - Preparation of the first mixture by solid raw                material comprising 70 wt.% ferric trioxide and 30                wt.% support having a spinel structure magnesium                aluminate as an iron oxide-based active material,                100 pts. wt. solid raw material and 100-150 pts.                wt. solvent (distilled water). Then 2 pts. wt.                dispersant (ammonium polycarboxylic acid salt) was                added to the first mixture based on 100 pts. wt.                solid raw material, and stirred at 400-650                revolutions per minute to prepare a first stirred                product. Then, 3 pts. wt. organic binder                (polyethylene glycol) was added to the first                stirred water, and stirred at 400-650 revolutions                per minute to prepareb a second mixture. Then the                second mixture was dried at 120-200&#176; C in                an air atmosphere reflux dryer for 24 hours,                filtered through a sieve, and a filtrate having a                size of 53-150 &#956; m was selected. The                filtrate was heated and maintained at a temperature                increase rate of 5&#176; o/minute step by step                at intervals of 100-200&#176; C, the                temperature was raised to 1000&#176; C and                maintained for 12 hours to calcinate the water                decomposition material prepared.

PN CN113788804-A
TI Extracting paclitaxel from branches of taxus media by supercritical carbon dioxide method comprises e.g. placing crushed taxus media in extracting tank, pumping carbon dioxide and cosolvent, mixing, extracting, collecting extract and concentrating and vacuum drying and detecting paclitaxel content.
AU WANG Q
AE WANG Q (WANG-Individual)
GA 2022001603
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting paclitaxel from branches of taxus media by supercritical CO2 method comprises (a) placing 6000 g crushed taxus media in an extracting tank of a supercritical device, (b) pumping CO2 and cosolvent into the cavity of each pump by the CO2 pump and cosolvent pump, mixing by a fluid mixer, flowing into a collector cavity in the extraction kettle, and extracting after reaching the extraction condition, (c) discharging the supercritical CO2 when extracting by separating kettle I and II, collecting the extract respectively from the separating kettle I and II, and (d) rotating and evaporating and concentrating the collected material, vacuum drying and detecting the paclitaxel content at 50degrees Celsius for 2 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting paclitaxel from branches of taxus media by supercritical CO2 method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method greatly improves the extraction efficiency, reduces the production cost of long-term operation, the active components of the extract are not easily destroyed, and the residual toxic and harmful pollutants are reduced to zero, which is more conducive to the protection of personnel and the environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The cosolvent is 95% ethanol solution and the dosage is 20%. The volume flow rate of the CO2 is 284 l/hour.Preferred Method: The extracting condition is as follows: the pressure reaches 30 MPa, the temperature is 45degrees Celsius, and the extracting time is 180 minutes. The temperature of the separating kettle I is 30degrees Celsius, the pressure is 6.5 MPa, the temperature of the separating kettle II is 30degrees Celsius, and the pressure is 5.5 Mpa. The detection method of the paclitaxel content adopts a high-performance liquid chromatograph to calculate the content of paclitaxel in the sample with the peak area by the external standard method, specifically precisely weighing a specific amount of paclitaxel, impurity I, and impurity II reference substance, adding acetonitrile to dissolve and dilute to make a solution containing about 0.5 mg paclitaxel and 2.5 mug impurity-I and impurity-II per 1 ml, as the system suitability test solution, where the impurity I is cephalomannine alcohol and the impurity II is 10-deacetylpaclitaxel, adding about 12 mg Taxol reference substance into a 100 ml measuring bottle, adding acetonitrile to dissolve and dilute to the scale, shaking up, as reference substance solution, taking the extracted substance, adding acetonitrile to dissolve and diluting to make a solution containing about 0.5 mg per 1 ml, as the test solution, taking 10 mul of the system suitability test solution and injecting into the liquid chromatograph, and the separation degree of paclitaxel peak, impurity I peak and impurity II peak should be more than 1, precisely measuring 10 mul test solution and inject it into a liquid chromatograph, recording the chromatogram, measuring the reference solution in the same way, and calculating by the peak area according to the external standard method, where the chromatographic condition of high performance liquid chromatograph is: chromatographic column: Agilent ZORBAX SBC18 (High efficiency liquid phase chromatogram) (150 mmx4.6 mm, 5 um), mobile phase: methanol: water: acetonitrile (23:41:36) flow rate 1 ml/minute, injection volume 10 microl, UV detection wavelength 227 nm.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of refined guanidine carbonate involves (i) adding guanidine hydrochloride to distilled water, stirring to form aqueous solution, adding sodium hydroxide solid at constant temperature and stirring to obtain mixed aqueous solution of guanidine group and sodium chloride, (ii) adding mixed aqueous solution to concentration kettle for evaporation treatment, naturally cooling and precipitating sodium chloride crystals to reach first concentrated solution, (iii) slowly introducing carbon dioxide to the first concentrated solution until pH is 9, and centrifuging to obtain a crude guanidine carbonate, (iv) adding guanidine carbonate crude product to distilled water to dissolve at constant temperature, and transferring to concentration kettle for concentration and filtration to obtain second concentrated solution, and (v) transferring second concentrated solution to crystallization kettle for cooling and crystallizing, and filtering to obtain high-purity product.
   USE - Preparation of refined guanidine carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The technology makes full use of the solubility of sodium chloride, guanidine hydrochloride and guanidine group in water in the reaction stage, reduces the content of sodium chloride in the concentration system, effectively reduces the content of sodium chloride in subsequent products, and improves the purity of guanidine carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), the guanidine hydrochloride is prepared by taking dicyandiamide and ammonium chloride as raw material to form pharmaceutical grade guanidine hydrochloride.Preferred Composition: In step (i), the concentration of the guanidine hydrochloride in distilled water is 200-400 g/l, and the amount of the sodium hydroxide added is 40-50 %mass of the quality of the guanidine hydrochloride. In step (iv), the mass ratio of the crude guanidine carbonate and distilled water is (1.1-1.2):1.Preferred Conditions: In step (i), the temperature of the constant temperature addition is 40-60degrees Celsius, and the stirring speed is 1000-2000 rpm. In step (i), the temperature of the evaporation treatment is 48-50degrees Celsius, the vacuum pressure is not greater than -0.085 MPa, and the evaporation time is 4 hours, where the water mass content in the solution after the evaporation treatment is not greater than 8%, and the temperature of the natural cooling is 30-35degrees Celsius. In step (iii), the introduction speed of carbon dioxide is 2-5 ml/minute. The mother liquor after centrifugation in the step (iii) is hydrochloricated and added to the distilled water in the step (i). In step (iv), the temperature of constant temperature dissolving is 65-68degrees Celsius. In step (iv), the temperature of the concentrated evaporation in the concentrated filtration is 60-65degrees Celsius, the vacuum pressure is not more than -0.085 MPa, the evaporation time is 5 hours, and the water content in the solution after the evaporation treatment is not greater than 10 %mass, where the insoluble matter is completely removed by filtration during concentration filtration to obtain a solution of guanidine carbonate. In step (iv), the temperature of the cooling crystallization is 30-35degrees Celsius, and the mother liquor after the crystallization is returned to the reaction kettle for reuse. The mother liquor is adjusted with hydrochloric acid and added to the solution of guanidine hydrochloride.

TI Composite membrane used for separating gas from            gas mixture, comprises selective layer comprising            polymeric matrix comprising amine polymer, graphene            oxide nanofiller, and mobile carrier chosen from ionic            liquid and amino acid salt, coated on support.
AU DENG L
   JANAKIRAM S
   ANSALONI L
AE UNIV NORWEGIAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYNO-Non-standard)
   UNIV NORWEGIAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYNO-Non-standard)
GA 2021C2280L
AB    NOVELTY - A composite membrane comprises a selective                layer comprising a polymeric matrix comprising an                amine polymer, a graphene oxide nanofiller, and a                mobile carrier chosen from ionic liquid and amino                acid salt, coated on a support.
   USE - Composite membrane is used for separating gas                from gas mixture e.g. separating carbon dioxide                from mixture containing carbon dioxide e.g. flue                gas, biogas (e.g. biogas upgrading), natural gas                (e.g. natural gas upgrading), or syngas, industrial                and domestic gas streams.
   ADVANTAGE - The economical composite membrane has high                modularity and easy scalability, high permeability                and selectivity, and excellent chemical property                and mechanical property, and significantly                increases efficiency of separation processes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) formation of the composite membrane, which                involves (i) forming an aqueous solution comprising                the polymeric matrix, graphene oxide nanofiller and                mobile carrier, and (ii) casting the aqueous                solution onto the support, such as hollow fiber                support; and
   (2) separation of gas from gas mixture, which                involves contacting the gas mixture with the                membrane.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining                alignment of graphene oxide-based fillers in                composite membranes fabricated using bar coating                technique.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The graphene oxide is                  physically or chemically modified graphene oxide.                  The graphene oxide is porous. The graphene oxide                  comprises a polymer grafted graphene oxide,                  preferably oxygen-containing polymer grafted                  graphene oxide. Preferred Properties: The                  graphene oxide nanofiller has an average lateral                  dimension of 1000 nm or less, preferably 400-800                  nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ionic liquid                  comprises a cation chosen from                  1-alkyl-3-methylimidazolium, 1-alkylpyridinium                  and N-methyl-N-alkylpyrrolidinium, and                  fluorosulfonyl-(trifluoromethanesulfonyl)imide,                  or comprises salt of a naturally occurring amino                  acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The polymeric matrix                  comprises a polymer comprising a tertiary                  amine-based repeating unit of formula (I), or a                  polyallylamine comprising a repeating unit of                  formula (II). The support is a flat sheet or in                  the form of hollow fibers, preferably hollow                  fibers. The selective layer comprises less than 5                  wt.%, preferably 0.1-0.3 wt.% graphene oxide                  nanofiller. The composite membrane comprises 1-40                  wt.%, preferably 5-25 wt.% mobile carrier. The                  support is made of polyethersulfone,                  polytetrafluoroethylene, polypropylene,                  sulfonated polysulfone, polyvinylidene fluoride,                  polyacrylonitrile and/or their related block                  copolymers, cellulose, such as cellulose acetate,                  polyimide, polyetherimide, aliphatic polyamide,                  polyether ether ketone, polyphenylene oxide                  and/or polysulfone. The support is porous. The                  support is flat sheet support, and the casting                  process uses a bar roller to apply the selective                  layer. The support is hollow fibers, and the                  casting process involves dip coating. The support                  is treated with a pore filling agent prior to                  casting the aqueous solution. Alternately, the                  composite membrane comprises selective layer                  comprising polymeric matrix comprising amine                  polymer, porous graphene oxide nanofiller or                  polyethylene glycol-modified graphene oxide                  nanofiller, and optionally mobile carrier coated                  on hollow fiber or flat sheet support, or the                  composite membrane comprises selective layer                  comprising the polymeric matrix comprising amine                  polymer and polyvinyl alcohol, porous graphene                  oxide nanofiller and optionally mobile carrier                  coated on hollow fiber support, or the composite                  membrane comprises selective layer comprising the                  polymeric matrix comprising amine polymer, porous                  graphene oxide nanofiller or polyethylene                  glycol-modified graphene oxide nanofiller and                  optionally mobile carrier coated on hollow fiber                  or flat sheet support.R1,R2 = H or 1-10C hydrocarbyl;m = 0-6;andR = 1-10C hydrocarbyl, preferably isopropyl                  or sec-butyl.Preferred Property: The selective layer has                  a thickness of 20 nm to 100 mu m, preferably                  100-500 nm.

PN WO2021194417-A1
TI Membrane useful in hydrogenation/dehydrogenation reaction or reforming reactions, comprises porous support layer having surfaces, palladium-based selective layer on first surface of support layer, and zeolite protective layer on surface of support layer.
AU KAWI S
   PATI S
   DEWANGAN N
   JANGAM A
   WANG Z
AE UNIV SINGAPORE NAT (UNUS-C)
GA 2021B2623V
AB    NOVELTY - Membrane comprises a porous support layer having a first surface and a second surface, a palladium-based selective layer on a first surface of the support layer, and a zeolite protective layer on a second surface of the support layer, where the support layer is between the palladium-based selective layer and the zeolite protective layer.
   USE - The membrane is useful in hydrogenation/dehydrogenation reaction or reforming reactions (claimed) including propane dehydrogenation, carbon dioxide hydrogenation, hydrocarbon reforming for hydrogen production and carbon dioxide reforming of methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The membrane effectively separates hydrogen from different feed stocks, has improved thermal and chemical stability, and improves poisoning of palladium-based membranes in the presence of hydrocarbons and other coke forming agents, particularly when used in hydrogen recovery applications.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of preparing the membrane involving (a) forming the palladium-based selective layer on the first surface of the porous support layer, and (b) forming the zeolite protective layer on the second surface of the porous support layer to obtain the product, where the porous support layer is between the palladium-based selective layer and the zeolite protective layer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The zeolite protective layer comprises an aluminosilicate zeolite, preferably linde type A (LTA) zeolite, H-sodalite (H-SOD) zeolite, titanium silicalite-1 zeolite, and/or Zeolite Socony Mobil-5 (RTM: Highly siliceous aluminosilicate zeolite). The support layer comprises a ceramic material comprising alumina (preferred), titania, zirconia, yttria, and/or ceria. The support layer has pores each having an average pore size of 50-200 nm. The support layer is a hollow fiber support layer, a tubular support layer, or a disc. The support layer is a hollow fiber support layer having an inner circumferential surface and an outer circumferential surface. The palladium-based selective layer is provided on the inner circumferential surface and the zeolite protective layer is provided on the outer circumferential surface. The palladium-based selective layer comprises palladium or a palladium alloy. The palladium alloy comprises palladium-silver alloy, palladium-silver-copper alloy, palladium-silver-cobalt alloy, palladium-copper alloy, palladium-nickel alloy, palladium-gold alloy, palladium-gold-cobalt alloy, palladium-gold-iridium alloy, palladium-gold-rhodium alloy, and/or palladium-ruthenium alloy. The membrane further comprises a catalyst layer on the zeolite protective layer. The catalyst layer comprises metal nanoparticles, preferably nickel-based nanoparticles. Preferred Method: The step of forming a palladium-based selective layer involves electroless plating the palladium-based selective layer on the first surface of the support layer. The step of forming a zeolite protective layer involves coating the zeolite protective layer on the second surface of the support layer by secondary growth hydrothermal method. The method further involves forming a support layer prior to the step of forming the palladium-based selective layer, and forming a catalyst layer on zeolite protective layer. The step of forming the support layer involves forming the support layer by phase inversion. Preferred Properties: The zeolite protective layer has a thickness of 0.5-3 mu m. The hollow fiber support layer has an outer diameter of 800-4000 mu m and an inner diameter of 500-2000 mu m. The palladium-based selective layer has a thickness of 0.5-5 mu m.
   EXAMPLE - Example 1 describes about preparation of membrane in detailed preparation of palladium-based selective layer formation, zeolite protective layer formation, characterization of membranes, use process of membranes, and comparative results. Example 2 describes about applicability of membrane in catalytic activity of propane dehydrogenation reaction. Example 3 describes about applicability of membrane in a membrane reactor for coupling propane dehydrogenation and carbon dioxide methanation reactions. Example 4 describes about applicability of membrane in a membrane reactor for methane decomposition for high pure hydrogen production, so do not support the claims.

TI Preparing cement base material useful in field of sewage treatment, comprises e.g. mixing complex microbial inoculum with porous carrier, mixing complex material with sand to obtain fine aggregate, mixing cement, fine aggregate and water.
AU ZHANG Y
   ZHANG X

   GUO S
   NI L

AE CHINA CONSTR IND & ENERGY ENG GROUP CO (CSCE-C)

GA 2021B3096J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cement base material comprises (a) taking purified sedimentary sludge, fly ash, ferroferric oxide, attapulgite, sodium bicarbonate, grinding, adding water to mix, granulating to obtain spherical particles, drying the spherical particles, and roasting to obtain porous carrier, (b) culturing Pseudomonas aeruginosa, Bacillus thuringiensis, Arthrobacter separately, adding high-efficiency transforming agent, and spray drying to obtain Pseudomonas aeruginosa, Bacillus thuringiensis, Arthrobacter spore powder, and (c) mixing Pseudomonas aeruginosa, Bacillus thuringiensis, Arthrobacter spore powder to obtain complex microbial inoculum, mixing the complex microbial inoculum with porous carrier to obtain complex material, mixing the complex material with sand to obtain fine aggregate, mixing cement, fine aggregate and water.
   USE - The cement-base material is useful in the field of sewage treatment (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method degrades organic matter and removes polymetallic ions through the cement-base material of the sewage treatment tank, decomposes organic matter into carbon dioxide and water, accelerates the hydration reaction of carbon dioxide to form mineral deposits with polymetallic ions to realize the simultaneous repair of multiple pollutants, and achieves better repair effect; and is convenient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for cement base material prepared by the above-mentioned method.

PT P
PN CN113275021-A; CN113275021-B
TI Preparing precious metal double-deposited quantum            dot photocatalyst used in photocatalytic reduction of            carbon dioxide, by stirring ammonium perrhenate and            thiourea, performing hydrothermal reaction, mixing            sodium thiosulfate pentahydrate and copper sulfate            pentahydrate, illuminating, and drying.
AU HUANG Q
   YANG J
   QI F
   ZHANG N
   LIU J
   LIANG Z
   TIAN C
AE UNIV CHONGQING POSTS & TELECOM (UCPT-C)
GA 202199512P
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing precious metal                double-deposited quantum dot photocatalyst,                involves (i) adding ammonium perrhenate and                thiourea to water at a mass ratio of                (500-550):(650-700), stirring to dissolve, and                performing hydrothermal reaction to obtain a                precipitate that is rhenium disulfide particles,                (ii) dripping sodium thiosulfate pentahydrate                solution to copper sulfate pentahydrate solution,                mixing, adding the rhenium disulfide particles                obtained in step (i), stirring for 10-15 minutes to                make the mixture uniform, continuously adding                sodium hydroxide solution, maintaining in a                constant temperature box for 30-60 minutes, drying,                attaching cuprous oxide quantum dots to the surface                of the rhenium disulfide particles to form rhenium                disulfide/cuprous oxide quantum dots, and (iii)                injecting platinum/gold solution of different                proportions to rhenium disulfide/cuprous oxide                quantum dots, uniformly stirring, illuminating for                30-40 minutes, washing, and drying.
   USE - The method is used for preparing precious                metal double-deposited quantum dot photocatalyst in                photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the photocatalytic                efficiency of the photocatalyst, and is simple and                easy to operate, low in equipment requirements, low                in cost and low in energy consumption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of                  sodium thiosulfate pentahydrate and copper                  sulfate pentahydrate in step (ii) is                  (80-100):(20-25). The mass ratio of cupric oxide                  quantum dots and rhenium disulfide particles                  produced by the reaction of sodium thiosulfate                  pentahydrate and copper sulfate pentahydrate in                  step (ii) is (5-30):(95-70). The concentration of                  the sodium hydroxide solution in step (ii) is 1-3                  mg/ml. The mass ratio of the rhenium disulfide                  particles and sodium hydroxide in step (ii) is                  (50-70):(2-5). The amount of rhenium                  disulfide/cuprous oxide quantum dots in the total                  mass of rhenium disulfide/cuprous oxide quantum                  dots and platinum/gold in step (iii) is 92 %mass.                  Preferred Method: The platinum/gold solution in                  step (iii) is prepared by (a) dissolving                  tetrachloroauric acid trihydrate and                  hexachloroplatinic acid in water respectively to                  form a tetrachloroauric acid trihydrate solution                  with a concentration of 0.01 mg/ mu l and a                  hexachloroplatinic acid solution with a                  concentration of 0.01 mg/ mu l, and (b) mixing                  the tetrachloroauric acid trihydrate solution and                  the hexachloroplatinic acid solution obtained in                  step (a) in different proportions and subjecting                  to light deposition to prepare a platinum/gold                  solution. Preferred Conditions: In step (i), the                  hydrothermal reaction is carried out at 220-240                  degrees C for 18-24 hours. In step (ii), the                  temperature in the constant temperature box is                  maintained at 60 degrees C. In step (iii), the                  light source used for light deposition is a xenon                  lamp, and stirring is performed while light                  deposition. The washing is carried out by                  performing suction filtration washing. The drying                  is carried out by drying at 50-70 degrees C to a                  constant weight.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

AB    NOVELTY - Quick preparation of 5-chloro-2-fluoro-4-(trifluoromethyl)benzoic acid (I) based on microchannel reaction technology involves pumping 2-chloro-5-fluorobenzotrifluoride solution (II) and 2,2,6,6-tetramethylpiperidinyl magnesium chloride lithium chloride reagent (III) into first section of microchannel reactor, carrying out Grignard exchange reaction, reacting to generate halogenated phenylmagnesium active intermediate, passing into second section of microchannel reactor, mixing with carbon dioxide gas, reacting to generate carboxylic acid magnesium salt, and acidifying.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Quick preparation of 5-chloro-2-fluoro-4-(trifluoromethyl)benzoic acid of formula (I) based on microchannel reaction technology involves pumping 2-chloro-5-fluorobenzotrifluoride solution of formula (II) and 2,2,6,6-tetramethylpiperidinyl magnesium chloride lithium chloride reagent of formula (III) into first section of microchannel reactor, carrying out Grignard exchange reaction, reacting to generate halogenated phenylmagnesium active intermediate, passing into second section of microchannel reactor, mixing with carbon dioxide gas, reacting to generate carboxylic acid magnesium salt, and acidifying..

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent in compound (II) is tetrahydrofuran, ether or n-hexane. The solvent in reagent is tetrahydrofuran, ether or n-hexane. Preferred Method: Grignard exchange reaction is done at 0-25 degrees C for 1-20 minutes. Reaction is done at 0-25 degrees C for 0.1-5 minutes.

   ADVANTAGE - The composition: is useful in air temperatures below 5degrees Celsius, below 0degrees Celsius and/or below -5degrees Celsius; allows smooth application onto wall surface; utilizes solvent based resins which provides improved adhesion, waterproofing and durability over conventional water based resins; utilizes cork and/or rubber particles which provides flexibility, spreadability amd/ or trovelability to the composition, hence composition is applied more easily; utilizes further solvent or thinning agent which reduces the viscosity of the composition; and provides a relatively flexible and crack resistant finish.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) construction panel or board including at least one layer of the composition, provided on an exterior surface of the construction panel or board; and

The method includes mixing colorants and/or further solvent or thinning agent in the composition. Preferred Components: The cork and/or rubber particles form a homogenous or substantially homogenous mixture with the carrier agent with the cork and/or rubber particles suspended in the carrier agent. The solvent based resin: includes styrene acrylic resin, styrene butadine rubber resin, acrylated rubber resin, a solvent based rubber resin and/or solvent based synthetic rubber resin; is Pliolite (Styrene acrylic resin); Sandtex Pliolite 365 (Styrene acrylic resin), Wethertex Pliolite (Styrene acrylic resin), Johnstones Pliolite (Styrene acrylic resin), Dulux Pliolite (Styrene acrylic resin) or Leyland Pliolite (Styrene acrylic resin). The rubber particles include recycled rubber. The cork and/or rubber particles have average diameter of 0.5-54 mm or less than or equal to5 fines, preferably less than or equal to 4 fines. The colorants include titanium dioxide. The further solvent or thinning agent includes white spirit, petroleum spirit, mineral spirit, mineral turpentine, and/or petroleum hydrocarbon.
   EXAMPLE - Typical composition comprised of: 60 wt.% Cork and/ or rubber granules; and 40 wt.% pre-pigmented Pliolite (Styrene acrylic resin) carrier agent.
