PN US2021172905-A1
TI Sensing device useful for monitoring battery pack,            and detecting analyte e.g. carbon dioxide, ethylene or            dimethyl carbonate, comprises several carbon-based            sensors including several three dimensional            graphene-based sensing materials.
AU GIBBS G C
   COOK D
   VANHEUSDEN K
   CHMIOLA J
   LIM S H
   LANNING B
   STOWELL M W
AE LYTEN INC (LYTE-Non-standard)
GA 2021630408
AB    NOVELTY - Sensing device for measuring battery comprises                (a) several carbon-based sensors enclosed within                the battery pack, where each carbon-based sensor                coupled between a corresponding pair of electrodes                and including several three dimensional (3D)                graphene-based sensing materials. The 3D                graphene-based sensing materials of a first                carbon-based sensor are functionalized with a first                material configured to detect a presence of each                analyte of a first group of analytes. The 3D                graphene-based sensing materials of a second                carbon-based sensor are functionalized with a                second material configured to detect a presence of                each analyte of a second group of analytes.
   USE - The sensing device is useful for measuring                battery pack, and detecting analyte. The analyte is                chosen from carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide,                nitrogen dioxide, one or more hydrocarbons                including methane, ethylene, ethane or propane, one                or more acids including hydrochloric acid or                hydrofluoric acid, one or more fluorinated                hydrocarbons including phosphorus oxyfluoride,                hydrogen cyanide, one or more aromatics including                benzene, toluene or ethanol, hydrogen, carbonate                based electrolytes including ethylene carbonate,                dimethyl carbonate, propylene carbonate, and one or                more reduced sulfur compounds (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The sensing device detects analyte with high                accuracy and sensitivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Apparatus: The carbon-based                  sensors are stacked on one another, or arranged                  next to one another. The carbon-based sensors                  comprise carbon-based inks printed on the                  substrate. The first carbon-based sensor                  comprises a first carbon-based ink, and the                  second carbon-based sensor comprises a second                  carbon-based ink different than the first                  carbon-based ink. The first carbon-based sensor                  is configured to generate a first output signal                  in response to detecting the presence of the one                  or more analytes of the first group of analytes,                  and the second carbon-based sensor is configured                  to generate a second output signal in response to                  confirming the presence of the one or more                  analytes detected by the first carbon-based                  sensor. The sensing device further comprises (i)                  an input terminal to receive an alternating                  current, and (ii) an antenna configured to                  receive an electromagnetic signal from an                  external device. The carbon-based sensor                  comprises several different graphene allotropes,                  where several different graphene allotropes of a                  respective carbon-based sensor include one or                  more microporous pathways or mesoporous                  pathways.Preferred Method: The first and second                  output signals comprise currents based at least                  in part on the alternating current, where (i) a                  first difference between the alternating current                  and the first output signal is indicative of the                  presence or absence of one or more analytes of                  the first group of analytes, and (ii) a second                  difference between the alternating current and                  the second output signal is indicative of the                  presence or absence of one or more analytes of                  the second group of analytes. The first output                  signal indicates a change in impedance of the                  first carbon-based sensor caused by exposure to                  one or more analytes of the first group of                  analytes, and the second output signal indicates                  a change in impedance of the second carbon-based                  sensor caused by exposure to one or more analytes                  of the second group of analytes, where a                  relatively small impedance change of a respective                  carbon-based sensor indicates an absence of a                  corresponding group of analytes, and a relatively                  large impedance change of the respective                  carbon-based sensor indicates a presence of the                  corresponding group of analytes. The first and                  second output signals comprise frequency                  responses of the 3D graphene-based sensing                  materials of the first and second carbon-based                  sensors, respectively to the electromagnetic                  signal. The frequency response of the 3D                  graphene-based sensing materials of the first                  carbon-based sensor is indicative of the presence                  or absence of one or more analytes of the first                  group of analytes, and the frequency response of                  the 3D graphene-based sensing materials of the                  second carbon-based sensor is indicative of the                  presence or absence of one or more analytes of                  the second group of analytes. The frequency                  responses are based on resonant impedance                  spectroscopy (RIS) sensing. The output signals                  indicate an operating mode of the battery pack.                  The output signal indicates a normal mode based                  on an absence of the analytes of the first group                  of analytes, a maintenance mode based on the                  presence of one or more analytes of the first                  group of analytes not exceeding a threshold                  level, and an emergency mode based on the                  presence of one or more analytes of the first                  group of analytes exceeding a threshold level.                  The first output signal is indicative of a                  concentration level of one or more analytes of                  the first group of analytes, and the second                  output signal is indicative of a concentration                  level of one or more analytes of the second group                  of analytes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The second group of                  analytes is a subset of the first group of                  analytes. The first group of analytes includes at                  least twice as many different analytes as the                  second group of analytes. The analytes of the                  first and second groups of analytes includes one                  or more volatile organic compounds (VOCs) chosen                  from carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, nitrogen                  dioxide, one or more hydrocarbons including                  methane, ethylene, ethane or propane, one or more                  acids including hydrochloric acid or hydrofluoric                  acid, one or more fluorinated hydrocarbons                  including phosphorus oxyfluoride, hydrogen                  cyanide, one or more aromatics including benzene,                  toluene or ethanol, hydrogen, carbonate based                  electrolytes including ethylene carbonate,                  dimethyl carbonate, propylene carbonate, or one                  or more reduced sulfur compounds including thiol                  having a form of R-SH. The 3D graphene-based                  sensing materials are configured to adsorb the                  VOCs.

   YANG X

GA 2020C64407
AB    NOVELTY - Pre-treating ultrasonic-thermal coupling scale inhibition before concentration of high calcium organic solution comprises subjecting the high-calcium organic solution to be concentrated to ultrasonic treatment, primary heating, secondary heating treatment in sequence, and discharging the carbon dioxide gas through the automatic exhaust device to complete the scale inhibition pre-treatment and conveying to the concentration process.

PT P
PN CN111821955-A
TI Composite material for carbon dioxide adsorption            and separation, prepared by e.g. preparing porous            polymer, soaking the porous polymer in organic amine            solution and then carrying out organic amine graft            modification.
AU YE J
   GUO S
   LIU H
   HUANG X
AE WUHAN HUITAN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 2020A8687T
AB    NOVELTY - Composite material is claimed. The composite                material is prepared by subjecting porous polymer                into organic amine graft modification and epoxy                modification, and then loading with metal-organic                frameworks to obtain metal-organic                frameworks/porous polymer composite material.
   USE - The composite material is useful for carbon                dioxide adsorption and separation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material: realizes the coupling                effect of macropores, mesopores and micropores                structurally; and optimizes the dynamic properties                of the material while maintaining high adsorption                performance and stable adsorption thermodynamic                performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing composite material comprising (i)                preparing porous polymer, (ii) soaking the porous                polymer prepared in step (i) in an organic amine                solution, and carrying out organic amine graft                modification for a certain period, (iii) immersing                the organic amine graft-modified porous polymer                prepared in step (ii) in an epoxy modifier,                reacting for a certain period of time, and                performing epoxy modification, and (iv) immersing                porous polymer prepared in step (iii) in a                precursor solution containing a metal source and an                organic ligand and then reacting for a certain                period of time to obtain metal-organic                frameworks/porous polymer composite material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The porous polymer is                  porous polyacrylate pellet. , The organic amine                  is tetraethylenepentamine, triethylenetetramine                  or diethylenetriamine and the epoxy modifier is                  propylene oxide or butylene oxide. The metal                  source is zinc acetate, zinc nitrate, copper                  fluorosilicate, zinc fluorosilicate, nickel                  fluorosilicate, copper nitrate, nickel nitrate,                  aluminum nitrate, and/or niobium pentoxide, and                  the organic ligand is 2-methylimidazole,                  tribenzoic acid, pyrazine and/or hydrofluoric                  acid. The monomers of the polyacrylate are                  glycidyl methacrylate and divinylbenzene, the                  emulsifier is Span 80 (RTM: Sorbitan monooleate),                  the porogen is toluene; the initiator A is                  benzoyl peroxide and the reducing agent A is                  N,N-dimethylaniline, the initiator B is ammonium                  persulfate, the reducing agent B is                  N,N,N',N'-tetramethylethylenediamine and the                  dispersant is polyvinyl alcohol. The raw material                  includes 50-55 wt.% acrylate monomer, 3-5 wt.%                  emulsifier, 40-45 wt.% toluene, 1-1.5 wt.%                  initiator A, 1-1.5 wt.% reducing agent A, and                  then adding 75-80 vol.% deionized water which                  accounts for the total volume of the                  above-mentioned components; and the aqueous                  system includes 98-98.5 wt.% deionized water,                  0.3-0.5 wt.% initiator B, 0.3-0.5 wt.% reducing                  agent B and 0.5-1 wt.% dispersant. Preferred                  Method: The organic amine graft modification                  includes immersing the porous polymer in an                  organic amine solution and reacting for a certain                  time, the epoxy modification includes immersing                  the grafted porous polymer in the epoxy modifier                  and reacting for a certain time, and the loading                  metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) includes                  immersing the modified porous polymer in a                  precursor solution containing a metal source and                  an organic ligand and reacting for certain period                  of time. The step (i) comprises firstly preparing                  the polyacrylate monomer, emulsifier, porogen,                  initiator A, reducing agent A, and water into an                  emulsion, and mixing initiator B, reducing agent                  B, dispersant and water to obtain aqueous system,                  then adding emulsion into the aqueous system to                  dispersing the emulsion into droplets to complete                  monomer copolymerization to obtain porous                  polyacrylate beads. In the step (ii): the                  reaction temperature is 90-110 degrees C; the                  reaction time is 12-24 hours, In the step (iii):                  the reaction temperature is room temperature, and                  the reaction time is 12-24 hours, and in the step                  (iv), when the porous polymer is soaked in the                  precursor solution, repeated pressure is used to                  make the precursor solution fully enter the pores                  of the porous polymer, the reaction temperature                  is 75-130 degrees C, and the reaction time is                  12-24 hours.

PN CN111822823-A; CN111822823-B
TI Processing narrow gap welding of ultra-thick plates comprises e.g. cutting thick plate, relatively welding two groove surfaces, using carbon dioxide gas shielded welding for surfacing welding, polishing welding meat, and assembling.
AU ZHU X
   ZENG R
   SUN Y
   YAN S
   WEI Z
   ZHAO C
AE DALIAN SHIPBUILDING IND CORP (CSHI-C)
GA 2020A7418J
AB    NOVELTY - Processing narrow gap welding of ultra-thick plates comprises (i) cutting 100-500 mm thick plate into 90 degrees straight section relative to welding end surface, relatively welding two groove surfaces of thick plate to form I-shaped groove, using carbon dioxide gas shielded welding for surfacing welding at center of both sides of groove surface in advance, welding meat with surfacing height of 4-8 mm, pre-heating thick plate at 150-250 degrees before surfacing, polishing welding meat, forming convex welding strip with cross section that is specifically triangular through centerline of this groove surface, and relatively welding two groove surfaces of thick plate to form double U-shaped groove, and (ii) relatively welding two groove surfaces of thick plate to form specific double U-shaped groove, assembling groove of thickness plate butt joint by tooling, ensuring that groove gap is between 10-18 mm, and controlling gap between bump surfacing within 0-4 mm.
   USE - The method is useful for processing narrow gap welding of ultra-thick plates.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is economical; and improves welding quality and production efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: The method further comprises (iii) using special welding gun for thick plate I-groove narrow gap welding when welding, forming welding gun middle connecting rod and gun body other than conductive connection into oblate or rectangular shape, protective sleeve shape is similar to oblate or rectangular shape of intermediate connecting rod, fixing and closing protective sleeve to middle connecting rod, connection position is above carbon dioxide air outlet of middle connecting rod, providing protective sleeve and conductive nozzle, carbon dioxide air outlet of middle connecting rod and lower part with ventilation passage or ventilation gap, and narrowest gap between conductive nozzle terminal is not more than 2 mm, and providing outside of protective sleeve with welding-resistant high-temperature insulating material layer or protective sleeve made of welding-resistant high-temperature insulating material, and (iv) preheating thick plate when welding, preheating adopting electric heating, flaming and other heating mode to heating at 150-250 degrees C, when welding, using two welding guns to symmetrically welding from both sides of specific double U-shaped groove center, carrying out first layer of primer welding and other layers of welding, immediately heating welding channel and both sides of welding channel, and keeping temperature at 200-300 degrees C for 2 hours.

PT P
PN CN111577225-A
TI Evaluation method for CO2 flooding in tight oil            reservoirs with different mineral composition cores,            involves detecting the mineral content in each rock            core, and thin tube experiment is performed to            determine minimum mixed-phase pressure.
AU HUANG X
   LI X
   REN Z
   QU L
AE UNIV XIAN SHIYOU (UXSY-C)
GA 202084718L
AB    NOVELTY - The evaluation method involves detecting the                mineral content in each rock core. The three rock                cores are screened with the highest content of                illite, montmorillonite, and quartz. The thin tube                experiment is performed to determine the minimum                mixed-phase pressure. The minimum mixed-phase                pressure is determined by performing saturated                simulation formation water to the three rock cores.                The dewatered and degassed crude oil are injected                into the rock core after the saturated simulated                formation water until the rock core does not                discharge water. The Carbon-dioxide (CO2) is                injected into the rock core under different                pressure. The residual oil distribution rule is                calculated in the rock core sample under the given                CO2 injection pressure according to the recovery                ratio of different stages.
   USE - Evaluation method for Carbon dioxide (CO2)                flooding in tight oil reservoirs with different                mineral composition cores.
   ADVANTAGE - The evaluation method reveals the potential                relationship between the typical mineral and the                distribution of the rest oil in the CO2 flooding                process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of the                evaluation method. (Drawing includes non-English                language text).
DC H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); Q49 (Mining (E21))
MC H01-B05B; H01-C07; H01-D06C; H01-D12; Q49-A; Q49-V35

The air supply device is provided with an air outlet that is located above the combustion chamber to form a high-temperature combustion net. The air supply device comprises air outlet assemblies, an air cylinder and an adjusting component. The air outlet assemblies are symmetrically located at two side walls of the air cylinder. The adjusting component drives the air outlet assemblies to move up and down. The air cylinder is uniformly provided with air outlet pipes. An end of the air cylinder is provided with an air inlet.
   USE - High temperature burning furnace.
   ADVANTAGE - The furnace can promote low-level combustion of carbon dioxide gas to produce high-temperature cracking reaction with free carbon in area under high temperature of the combustion chamber to produce carbon monoxide and high-temperature gas to re-intervene in the combustion, thus effectively improving heating efficiency of combustion process and device pyrolysis efficiency, reducing total emission of carbon dioxide and saving amount of fuel used.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a high temperature burning furnace. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
DC Q73 (Combustion equipment/processes (F23))

PN DE102019200238-A1; WO2020143970-A1
TI Electrolysis method for reducing carbon dioxide to            electrolysis product, involves setting electrolyte flow            through electrolytic cell by using pumps, and            maintaining electrolyte flow on cathode side during            regeneration period.
AU HARTMANN D
   KRAUSE R
AE SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
GA 202065299M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves performing a recurring                alternation between an electrolysis period with                applied electrolysis voltage and a regeneration                period with applied protective voltage (Up). An                electrolyte flow through an electrolytic cell is                set by using pumps, and is maintained on a cathode                side during the regeneration period. The protective                voltage is set, such that no electrical current                flow takes place in the electrolysis cell. A higher                electrolyte flow through the electrolysis cell is                set on the cathode side than during the                electrolysis during the regeneration period.
   USE - Electrolysis method for reducing carbon                dioxide to electrolysis product.
   ADVANTAGE - The effective regeneration of saline pores of                the gas diffusion electrode is carried out without                structural changes to the electrolyzer and without                additional equipment. The risk of irreversible                damage to the silver gas diffusion electrode is                reduced. The electrode-electrolyte system which                represents the cathode with the electrolyte present                in the electrolysis cell during operation is kept                in a thermodynamically stable state by the                protective voltage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawings show a schematic diagram                illustrating the application of the protection                potential, and a graphical representation                illustrating the application of the protection                potential in which the Faraday efficiency is                plotted over time.
   Anode (A)
   Cathode (K)
   Protective voltage (Up)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - The gas diffusion cathode comprises a metal,                  a metal compound or alloy, silver, gold, zinc,                  palladium and/or gallium, copper, copper compound                  or copper alloy.

PN TW202026125-A
TI Manufacture of lightweight composite shoe sole by            supercritical foaming, adding water dispersant and            thermoplastic polyurethane particles, introducing            carbon dioxide and nitrogen into autoclave, stirring,            heating, and mixing with polyurethane glue.
AU ZHIXONG G
AE GIA JIU ENTERPRISE MFG CORP (GIAJ-Non-standard)
GA 2021646677
AB    NOVELTY - A lightweight composite shoe sole                manufacturing process, including the following                steps: supercritical foaming step, water                dispersant, TPU particles are added to the                autoclave, carbon dioxide and nitrogen are also                introduced into the autoclave, the autoclave is                stirred and heated, and the particles The TPU                foamed particles with the required specific gravity                are obtained by saturation; in the mixing step, the                TPU foamed particles are mixed with the PU glue,                and the PU glue partially covers the TPU foamed                particles, and the TPU foamed particles do not                contact each other on their surfaces; the mold                clamping step , TPU film is laid on the mold cavity                of the shoe mold, filled with foamed shoe material                in the mold cavity and stacked on the TPU film, and                then the mold is closed; the oven baking step, the                shoe mold is placed in the oven in the film state                for baking; the finished product is in the oven The                shoe mold is taken out, and the mold is opened to                form a composite shoe sole with PU coating and TPU                foam particles combined with TPU film.

PN CN111349809-A; CN111349809-B
TI Preparing magnesium alloy additive manufacturing            wire material containing three-dimensional network            graphene comprises e.g. selecting magnesium alloy            system, heating and melting alloy at temperature higher            than melting point of alloy.
AU WANG X
   LI X
   XU H
   JIN Y
   LUAN P
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY (HAIT-C)
GA 202063014D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing magnesium alloy additive                manufacturing wire material containing                three-dimensional network graphene comprises (1)                selecting the magnesium alloy system, heating and                melting the alloy at a temperature higher than the                melting point of the alloy by 30-100 degrees C, (2)                adjusting temperature of magnesium melt to the                range of 640-700 degrees C, introducing carbon                dioxide CO2 gas with a flow rate of 0.1-5 l/min                into the magnesium alloy melt, keeping the                temperature stable during this process, converting                carbon dioxide to graphene, and ensuring that the                content of graphene in the magnesium alloy is 1-10                wt.% to obtain semi-solid composite melt, (3)                casting the compound melt with semi-solid                characteristics obtained by the reaction in step                (2), to obtain a three-dimensional graphene                composite ingot, (4) machining the                three-dimensionally connected composite ingot of                graphene obtained in step (3) and magnesium alloy                wire is obtained by direct extrusion or extrusion +                drawing deformation.
   USE - The three-dimensional network                graphene-containing magnesium alloy additive is                useful as a raw material for the deposition and                manufacturing of magnesium alloy electron beam fuse                (claimed).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alloy elements in                  the magnesium alloy system are aluminum, zinc,                  calcium, manganese, copper or zirconium. The                  alloy elements in the magnesium alloy system are                  aluminum, zinc, calcium, manganese, copper or                  zirconium. The pressure casting in step (3) is                  direct stirring casting or ultrasonic assisted                  stirring casting. The extrusion temperature in                  the four steps is 200-500 degrees C and the                  extrusion ratio is 70:1. In the step (4), the                  magnesium alloy wire material is obtained by                  extrusion and drawing deformation, when the ingot                  is extruded, the extrusion temperature is 400                  degrees C, the extrusion ratio is 10:1, and the                  extruded bar with phi 15 is obtained, secondly,                  the extruded bar is cold drawn 5 times to obtain                  the additive-made wire with diameter phi 2.

TI Preparing sodium magnesium phosphate photocatalytic material useful for reducing carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide under simulated sunlight irradiation, comprises e.g. placing sodium hypophosphite and nano magnesium oxide in mortar.
AU HAO Y
   KONG W
   QI X
   LI F
   MA Y
AE UNIV HEBEI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYKJ-C)
GA 202030358P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing sodium magnesium phosphate (Na3Mg3(PO4)3) photocatalytic material comprises placing sodium hypophosphite and nano magnesium oxide or magnesium hydroxide together in a mortar, grinding to 200-300 mesh powder size, transferring to the crucible, leaving a gap between the crucible cover and the crucible, and placing into the atmosphere to roast, dropping the temperature to room temperature, taking out and washing with distilled water 3-5 times, and drying to obtain an orange powder.
   USE - The material is useful for reducing carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide under simulated sunlight irradiation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material is safe, non-toxic and cheap, and environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the material, comprising (i) taking the phosphorus source and magnesium source in a mortar and grinding thoroughly until no obvious graininess exists, and (ii) placing the mixture milled into the crucible and placing in the muffle furnace, leaving a gap between the crucible and the crucible lid, and roasting at 480-520 degrees C in air atmosphere for 2-4 hours when the temperature of the muffle furnace drops to room temperature, and collecting samples.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The roasting atmosphere is air or vacuum atmosphere. The sodium hypophosphite is the phosphorus source, nano magnesium oxide or magnesium hydroxide is the magnesium source, and the molar ratio of the phosphorus source to the magnesium source is 1.0:1.0. The Na3Mg3(PO4)3/ magnesium oxide (MgO) photocatalytic material includes MgO and Na3Mg3(PO4)3, and the mass ratio of MgO and Na3Mg3 (PO4) 3 is: (88.4-97.2): (2.8-11.6). The mass fraction of MgO in the Na3Mg3 (PO4)3/MgO photocatalytic material is 93.1%, and the mass fraction of Na3Mg3 (PO4) 3 is 6.9%. In the step (i), the phosphorus source is sodium hypophosphite, the magnesium source is nano magnesium oxide and magnesium hydroxide, and the molar ratio of the phosphorus source to the magnesium source (1-10): 100. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the roasting temperature is 480-520 degrees C and the roasting time is 2-4 hours. In the step (i), sufficiently grinding to 200-300 mesh.

PN CN110693011-A
TI Heat processing of instant bird's nest comprises            soaking bird's nest, separating, manually picking,            adding auxiliary material, using carbon dioxide            generator, sterilizing, cooking, immersing in            pressure-transmitting medium and maintaining.
AU FANG M
   GAO Y
AE FANGJIA PUZI PUTIAN GREEN FOOD CO LTD (FANG-Non-standard)
GA 202008537G
AB    NOVELTY - Heat processing of instant bird's nest                comprises soaking the bird's nest in a vessel                filled with pure water for 2-8 hours, separating                the foamed bird's nest from the purified water,                manually picking the foamed bird's nest to obtain                pure bird's nest, taking the pure bird's nest and                fill it into an ethylene vinyl alcohol (EVOH)                bottle, adding an auxiliary material to the EVOH                bottle, using carbon dioxide generator to remove                the oxygen at the opening of the EVOH bottle and                quickly sealing to obtain a semi-finished bird's                nest, heating the sterilization pot to 70-80                degrees C, placing the semi-finished bird's nest in                the sterilization pot and cooking for 15-45                minutes, placing the cooked bird's nest                semi-finished product in a reaction kettle of a                high static pressure equipment, immersing in a                pressure-transmitting medium and quickly increasing                the pressure to 300-600 MP and maintaining for 3-30                minutes to obtain instant bird's nest.
   USE - The method is useful for geat processing of                instant bird's nest.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: helps for human body to absorb;                and has convenient processing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The foaming step comprises                  purifying the water below 10 degrees C and                  soaking for 5 hours. The sterilization step                  comprises heating the sterilizer to 75 degrees C                  for 30 minutes. The reaction step comprises                  increasing the pressure to 460 MP and the                  pressure holding time is 20 minutes The method                  further comprises rinsing the bird's nest once                  with active pure water and passing a soft brush,                  brushing the surface of the bird's nest and                  rinsing for 3-4 times. Preferred Components: The                  pressure transmitting medium in the reaction step                  is water. The auxiliary material includes rock                  sugar, stevioside, fruit juice powder, gelatin,                  medlar, ginger, xylitol and/or vitamin C. The raw                  material in the method comprises 1-13 pts. wt.                  rock candy, preferably 7 pts. wt., 1-5 pts. wt.                  stevioside, preferably 3.5 pts. wt., 1-15 pts.                  wt. juice powder, preferably 7 pts. wt., 1-4 pts.                  wt. gelatin, preferably 3.4 pts. wt., 1-4 pts.                  wt. wolfberry, preferably 3 pts. wt., 0.5-1.8                  pts. wt. ginger, preferably 1 pts. wt., 1-3.5                  pts. wt. xylitol, preferably 2.2 pts. wt., and                  0.05-0.18 pts. wt. vitamin C preferably 0.1 pts.                  wt.. The auxiliary material includes 3-10 pts.                  wt. honey.

AU XIE H
   GUO Y
   GUO G
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 201984120W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing lignosulfonate cellulose composite film involves mixing cellulose, an organic base, an organic solvent and an alcohol reagent, and reacting in an environment having carbon dioxide pressure of 0.1-10.0 mega pascal and a temperature of 30-100 degrees C for 0.1-12 hours to obtain a cellulose solution, uniformly coating the cellulose solution on the flat support layer, and then immersing it in the anti-solvent solution, the temperature of the anti-solvent solution is 10-60 degrees C, where soaking time is 15-24 hours. The film is removed from the support layer to obtain regenerated cellulose film. The regenerated cellulose membrane is soaked to a 1-20 wt.% of lignosulfonate. The solution temperature is 10-60 degrees C, and the soaking time is 1-24 hours, after taking out, washed with water and then dried at 25-100 degrees C for 3-96 hours to prepare a lignosulfonate cellulose composite film.
   USE - Method for preparing lignosulfonate cellulose composite film.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare lignosulfonate cellulose composite film that has simple preparation process, low preparation cost, process high efficiency and controllable product, functional and high-value of cellulose with important significance.

The saturated trihydric alcohol reagent is glycerol. The support layer is a glassplate, stainless steel plate or polyfluortetraethylene plate. PN CN110055409-A
TI Magnesium smelting useful for recycling waste gas residue, comprises mixing dolomite and magnesite with reducing agent aluminum powder, pressing, vacuuming, reacting solid reducing slag and recycling the sodium carbonate solution.
AU ZHANG C
   FU J
   CHU H
   GU M
AE UNIV ANHUI TECHNOLOGY (UYAT-C)
GA 2019669525
AB    NOVELTY - Magnesium smelting comprises (i) mixing the qualified mixed powder of 60-70 pts. wt. dolomite and 18-25 pts. wt. magnesite with 8-15 pts. wt. reducing agent aluminum powder, pressing the pellet, feeding into the reduction tank for calcinations and releasing the high-temperature carbon dioxide into the heat exchanger to preheat the combustion air of the reduction furnace to normal temperature; (ii) vacuuming reduction tank to an absolute pressure of less than 100 Pa, adjusting temperature of reduction furnace to reduce the magnesium production reaction, and generating high temperature magnesium vapor and solid reduction slag; (iii) reacting solid reducing slag in the step (iii) with the sodium carbonate solution to form sodium aluminate solution, adding normal temperature carbon dioxide obtained in the step (ii) through a sodium aluminate solution for carbon decomposition to obtain an aluminum hydroxide and a sodium carbonate solution, and recycling the sodium carbonate solution.
   USE - The magnesium smelting is useful for recycling waste gas residue (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the consumption of tons of magnesium and coal, reduces the emission of greenhouse gases and solid reducing slag, and improves economic benefits.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (iii), the aluminum hydroxide is calcined to obtain alumina, and the aluminum metal obtained by electrolyzing alumina is used to prepare the reducing agent aluminum powder in the step (i). The qualified mixed powder is that the mixed powder of dolomite and magnesite is ground and sieved in a sieve ball mill to obtain a material having a particle size of 100-200 mesh. The reducing agent aluminum powder has a particle size more than 150 mesh. In the step (iii), the sodium carbonate solution reacted with the solid reducing slag is supplemented by the outside. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the temperature of the reduction furnace is raised to 1100-1200 degrees C to carry out the reduction of magnesium production for 30-360 minutes. In the step (i), the pellet is calcined in a reduction tank at a temperature of 900-1000 degrees C, and the pressure is a negative pressure or a slight positive pressure. The dolomite, magnesite qualified mixed powder and the aluminum powder are uniformly mixed and then extruded into pellets by a ball press, and the equivalent diameter of the qualified pellets not greater than 50 mm. The concentration of the sodium carbonate solution which reacts with the solid reducing slag is 100-130 g/l, and the solution temperature is 80-95 degrees C. The temperature at which the aluminum hydroxide obtained in the step (iii) is calcined is 1000-1200 degrees C and the calcination time is 120-360 minutes.
DC M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)

PN CN109822702-A
TI High stability wood flame retardant, comprises calcium nitrate, urea, dispersing agent, foaming agent, water, first and second composite flame retardant components in specific mass parts.
AU WANG J
AE WANG J (WANG-Individual)
GA 201950092L
AB    NOVELTY - A high-stability wood flame retardant comprises 1-4 mass parts calcium nitrate, 4-8 mass parts urea, 3-7 mass parts dispersing agent, 2-5 mass parts foaming agent, 30-50 mass parts water, 25-45 mass parts composite flame retardant component A and 12-20 mass parts composite flame retardant component B.
   USE - High-stability wood flame retardant.
   ADVANTAGE - The high-stability wood flame retardant prevents uneven dispersion of the flame-retardant active ingredient by using inorganic processed material which combines with the hydroxyl group in the wood component. The flame retardant has reversible volume and modulus change under the action of carbon dioxide, and can combine with the hydroxyl group in the wood component to improve the mechanical properties of wood. The release of carbon dioxide makes the system to have shape memory effect and achieve better flame retardant and anti-stretching effect. The wood flame retardant increases the vesicle cavity of the system and improves the contact effect of each component, and has better stability under the action of the colloid-stable composite structure of the composite flame-retardant component A by using additive having amphiphilicity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The composite flame-retardant component A is prepared by mixing ferrous sulfate, sodium hypochlorite and water in a mass ratio of 7:1-3:25-35 and stirring at 30-40 degrees C to obtain a mixture, adding titanium sulfate to the mixture in mass ratio of 8-12:1:1, mixing sodium dihydrogen phosphate to the resulted mixture at 60-75 degrees C by heat preservation to obtain a substrate, adding the obtained substrate to the reagent A at a mass ratio of 15:1-4 to obtain an inorganic treatment material, mixing 2-vinyl-4,6-diamino-1,3,5-triazine, N,N-dimethylacrylamide, dimethyl sulfoxide and polyethylene glycol diacrylate in a mass ratio of 4:1-3:15-25:0.1, in a reaction kettle, adding polyethylene glycol diacrylate to 8-15 mass% titanium oxide and 3-6 mass% polyethylene glycol diacrylate to the additives, mixing polyethylene glycol diacrylate with 18-30 mass% 2-hydroxy-4-(2-hydroxyethoxy)-2-methylpropiophenone, transferring the resulted mixture to a transparent plastic mold by using UV crosslinking agent to initiate treatment with water immersion at room temperature, discharging the reaction materials, mixing the reaction materials, inorganic treatment materials and additives in a mass ratio of 6-10:3:1, shearing and dispersing the resulted mixture to obtain the composite flame-retardant component A. The reagent A is prepared by mixing and stirring cyanamide, boric acid and phosphoric acid solution in a mass ratio of 3:1:20-35 at 85-95 degrees C. The additive is prepared by mixing 60-80 pts. wt. (N-fluorenyl) dodecyl acrylamide, 30-50 pts. wt. polyethylene glycol phosphate, 4-8 pts. wt. para-dichlorobenzene and stirring at 60-45 degrees C. The composite flame retardant component B is prepared by mixing 13-27 mass parts sodium chloride, 1-4 mass parts hydroxyethyl cellulose, 0.03-0.06 mass parts methylene blue aqueous solution, 80-100 mass parts water and stirring 60-75 degrees C to obtain a mixed solution, mixing the obtained mixed solution with methyl acrylate, cross-linking agent, benzoyl peroxide and pore-forming agent in a mass ratio of 7-12:4:1:0.3:0.3 at 65-80 degrees C, insulating, stirring and heat treating to 85-95 degrees C, cooling, filtering, washing the filter residue, freeze-drying the washed residue at 110-125 degrees C, mixing the lyophilized material with polymethyl acrylate and reagent B in mass ratio of 5:1-3:12-20, heating to 130-145 degrees C, cooling, filtering, washing the filter cake with hydrochloric acid solution and drying to obtain a composite flame retardant component B. The crosslinking agent is prepared by mixing polyethylene glycol, with ethylamine and dichloromethane in a mass ratio of 1:3-5:12 at 2-5 degrees C, adding 6-10% acryloyl chloride to polyethylene glycol, stirring at room temperature, suction filtering, mixing the filtrate with ether in a mass ratio of 7-12:3, drying the filter residue to obtain a dry matter, dialyzing the dried product to obtain a dialyzate and freeze-drying the dialyzate to obtain the crosslinking agent. The pore-forming agent is prepared by mixing with 1,2-dichloroethane and n-heptane in a mass ratio of 4-8:3. The reagent B is prepared by mixing xylene and diethylenetriamine in a volume ratio of 3-7:2 to obtain the reagent B. The dispersing agent is prepared by mixing dodecyldimethyl betaine base and cetyltrimethylammonium bromide in a mass ratio of 5-8:2 to obtain a dispersing agent. The foaming agent is prepared by mixing 2-5:1 mass ratio petroleum ether and calcium hydrogen carbonate to obtain the foaming agent.

TI Method for purifying waste gas processing gas fertilizer, involves connecting industrial waste gas to air inlet of purification device, installing filter screen in air inlet pipe to filter out solid dust, followed by exposing gas.
AU YANG S
AE YANG S (YANG-Individual)
GA 2019400227
AB    NOVELTY - A waste gas processing gas fertilizer purifying method involves removing harmful gases such as sulfur dioxide and nitrogen oxide in the flue gas by the absorbing liquid, introducing the mixed gas of the obtained carbon dioxide and the harmless gas into the air chamber as the gas fertilizer, connecting the industrial waste gas to the air inlet of the purification device, installing a filter screen in the air inlet pipe to filter out the solid dust, arranging the circulating cold water pipe around the air inlet pipe, cooling the exhaust gas by the cold water, pumping the filtered exhaust gas into the purification device by using an air pump, and exposing the gas to the microbubble through the built-in aeration head to fully react with the absorption liquid, and exporting the prepared carbon dioxide gas fertilizer from the gas outlet through the gas outlet into the gas delivery system for transportation.
   USE - Method for purifying waste gas processing gas fertilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables purifying waste gas processing gas fertilizer with reduced emission of the industrial waste gas, in environment-friendly manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The absorption liquid is phosphate buffer solution containing 100 g/l urea, 25 g/l sodium sulfite and 14 g/l of calcium hydroxide solution. Preferred Components: The outlet conduit is provided with an outlet control valve.

PT P
PN CN108949683-A
TI Promoting proliferation of human bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells used for preventing osteoporosis by adding heparin sodium to bone marrow, centrifuging cells, washing cells, subculturing cells, and culturing cells in culture flask.
AU ZHAO L
   CHEN M
AE HUAIAN KANGYILAI STEM CELLS BIOTECHNOLOG (HUAI-Non-standard)
GA 2018A0487H
AB    NOVELTY - Method for promoting proliferation of human bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells involves collecting fresh bone marrow, adding heparin sodium as anticoagulant, adding same amount of normal saline, subjecting Percoll cell separation solution to density gradient centrifugation, washing the intermediate mononuclear cell layer with low-sugar Dulbecco's modified eagle medium (DMEM), inoculating into a Petri dish, adding whole medium, incubating in a 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 24 hours, changing the liquid, discarding unattached cells, changing cells for every 2-3 days, culturing cells to 90% confluence, trypsinizing the cells, subculturing cells, obtaining passage cells, digesting to prepare a cell suspension, inoculating into a culture flask, culturing in a whole medium, incubating at 37 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 24 hours, and replacing with a whole medium containing drug to continue culture, where drug-containing complete medium comprises alpha -cedrene or beta -cedrene.
   USE - The method is useful for promoting proliferation of human bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells (claimed), which is used for preventing osteoporosis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of alpha -cedrene or beta -cedrene for preparing a medium for promoting proliferation of human bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Component: The whole medium is DMEM/F12 medium containing 10% fetal bovine serum (FBS).
   ACTIVITY - Osteopathic.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Cell therapy.

PT P
PN US2018305209-A1; US10850981-B2
TI Producing a gas e.g. chlorine dioxide at a            variable rate, comprises dynamically mixing dry            particles comprising a precursor and dry particles            comprising a proton-generating species to produce a gas            i.e. chlorine dioxide.
AU ERNST W
   ISAAC T
AE ICA TRINOVA LLC (ICAT-Non-standard)
GA 2018832153
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a gas at a variable rate, comprises                dynamically mixing dry particles comprising a                precursor and dry particles comprising a                proton-generating species to produce a gas, where                the gas is produced at a rate that is varied by                varying the amount of time the dry particles                comprising the precursor and the dry particles                comprising the proton-generating species are                dynamically mixed and/or the rate at which the dry                particles comprising the precursor and the dry                particles comprising the proton-generating species                are dynamically mixed, the dry precursor particles                comprise a chlorine dioxide precursor and the gas                comprises chlorine dioxide, the dry precursor                particles comprise a carbon dioxide precursor                and/or the gas comprises carbon dioxide and when                the gas is chlorine dioxide 80% or more of the                precursor is converted to the gas.
   USE - The method is useful for producing a gas at a                variable rate, where the gas is chlorine dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes the milling, crushing                and/or abrading of the dry particles that scour the                surface of the dry particles or crush the dry                particles, thus exposing reactive surfaces and                allowing the conversion of the precursor to the gas                to continue.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The gas is carbon dioxide                  and 1-75% of the precursor is converted to the                  gas. The dry particles comprising the precursor                  and dry particles comprising the                  proton-generating species are dynamically mixed                  in a mixer comprising a tumbler, a vibratory                  mixer, a rotary mixer, a marinator mixer or a                  stirrer. Dynamically mixing the dry particles                  comprising the precursor and the dry particles                  comprising the proton-generating species                  comprises providing a first portion of the dry                  particles comprising the precursor and the dry                  particles comprising the proton-generating                  species, dynamically mixing the first portion of                  the dry particles comprising the precursor and                  the dry particles comprising the                  proton-generating species to form a mixture,                  providing a second portion of the dry particles                  comprising the precursor, and dynamically mixing                  the second portion of the dry particles                  comprising the precursor and the mixture.                  Dynamically mixing the dry particles comprising                  the precursor and the dry particles comprising                  the proton-generating species comprises providing                  the dry particles comprising the precursor and a                  first portion of the dry particles comprising the                  proton-generating species, dynamically mixing the                  dry particles comprising the precursor and the                  first portion of the dry particles comprising the                  proton-generating species to form a mixture,                  providing a second portion of the dry particles                  comprising the proton-generating species, and                  dynamically mixing the second portion of the dry                  particles comprising the proton generating                  species and the mixture. The dry particles                  comprising the precursor are provided                  continuously over the amount of time that the dry                  particles comprising the precursor and dry                  particles comprising the proton-generating                  species are dynamically mixed. The dry particles                  comprising the proton-generating species are                  provided continuously over the amount of time                  that the dry particles comprising the precursor                  and dry particles comprising the                  proton-generating species are dynamically mixed.                  The dry particles comprising the precursor and                  dry particles comprising the proton-generating                  species are dynamically mixed for a first amount                  of time, after which the dry particles comprising                  the precursor and dry particles comprising the                  proton-generating species are static for a second                  amount of time. After the second amount of time,                  the dry particles comprising the precursor and                  dry particles comprising the proton-generating                  species are dynamically mixed for a third amount                  of time. After the second amount of time a second                  amount of dry particles comprising the precursor                  are provided and the second amount of dry                  particles comprising the precursor are                  dynamically mixed with the first amount of the                  dry particles comprising the precursor and dry                  particles comprising the proton-generating                  species for a third amount of time. After the                  second amount of time a second amount of dry                  particles comprising the proton-generating                  species are provided and the second amount of dry                  particles comprising the proton-generating                  species are dynamically mixed with the first                  amount of the dry particles comprising the                  precursor and dry particles comprising the                  proton-generating species for a third amount of                  time. The method further comprises: milling,                  crushing and/or abrading, where the dry particles                  comprise the precursor and dry particles comprise                  the proton-generating species; and dynamically                  mixing an abrasive particle, a deliquescent                  and/or a desiccant, with the dry particles                  comprising the precursor and dry particles                  comprising the proton-generating species.                  Preferred Components: The chlorine dioxide                  precursor comprises a chlorine dioxide-producing                  compound comprising a metal chlorite, a metal                  chlorate, chloric acid and/or hypochlorous acid.                  The carbon dioxide precursor comprises a                  carbon-containing compound comprising carbonates,                  bicarbonates and/or sesquicarbonates. The dry                  particles comprising the precursor further                  comprise a porous carrier comprising zeolite                  crystals, silica, pumice, diatomaceous earth,                  bentonite or clay and the precursor is                  impregnated in the porous carrier, where the dry                  particles comprising the proton-generating                  species further comprise a porous carrier                  comprising zeolite crystals, silica, pumice,                  diatomaceous earth, bentonite or clay and/or the                  proton-generating species is impregnated in the                  porous carrier. The dry particles comprising the                  precursor comprise 1-50 wt.% of the precursor                  and/or the dry particles comprising the                  proton-generating species comprise 1-50 wt.% of                  the proton-generating species. The                  proton-generating species comprises an organic                  acid and/or an inorganic acid comprising acetic                  acid, citric acid, hydrochloric acid, phosphoric                  acid, propionic acid and/or sulfuric acid. The                  proton-generating species comprises a metal salt                  comprising ferric chloride, ferric sulfate,                  calcium chloride, zinc sulfate, zinc chloride,                  cobalt(II) sulfate, cobalt(II) chloride,                  manganese(II) sulfate, manganese(II) chloride,                  copper(II) sulfate, copper(II) chloride,                  magnesium sulfate, sodium acetate, sodium                  citrate, sodium sulfate, sodium bisulfate,                  hydrogen phosphate and/or disodium hydrogen                  phosphate.
   EXAMPLE - Initially charging and continuously mixing 40                lb of sodium chlorite impregnated on zeolite and 40                lb. of ferric chloride impregnated on zeolite                (13.6%, 14x 30 mesh) in a portable cement mixer.                Gaseous chlorine dioxide formed during the mixing                of the two solids, and passed out through the                opening in the mixer. At several time intervals,                samples of the mixed bed of solids were removed to                obtain carbon dioxide.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

   USE - Method for preparing environment-friendly easily degradable biological material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method is safe. The material is stable.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The stabilizer is diphenyl sulfone, silicone oil, paraffin and/or linoleic acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: Ultrasonic emulsifying is done at 55-70 kHz for 20-40 minutes. Extruding is done at 220-260 degrees C. UV irradiating is done for 30-60 minutes. PN CN108558351-A
TI Preparing low alkali planting concrete block using            artificial carbonized steel slag pellets as aggregate            comprises e.g. screening artificial carbonized steel            slag ball aggregate, and mixing steel slag micro-powder            and water.
AU CHANG J
   YU C
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 201877992W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing low alkali planting concrete block                using artificial carbonized steel slag pellets as                aggregate comprises (i) screening 70-80 wt.%                particle diameter is 9.5-19 mm of artificial                carbonized steel slag ball aggregate, mixing with                10-20 wt.% steel slag micro-powder, 0-2 wt.%                reinforcing agent, 6-8 wt.% water and stirring of                the slurry mixing and molding, (ii) first stage                comprises placing mold for 24 hours in indoor                ventilation environment to the carbide maintaining                optimal water content, placing in a carbonization                kettle, introducing carbon dioxide purity of 99.9%,                pressure of 0.1 MPa carbide, taking out 30 minutes,                second stage stripping into water curing for 1 day,                third stage comprises introducing 99.9% purity                carbon dioxide out block drying to the carbide and                maintaining optimal water content, and placing in                carbonization kettle, pressure is 0.3 MPa, and                taking out after 2 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing low alkali                planting concrete block using artificial carbonized                steel slag pellets as aggregate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: saves energy; is simple, requires                low equipment and economical; and has short                production cycle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: : The artificial                  carbonized steel slag pellet is prepared by the                  first stage comprises evenly mixing steel slag                  micropowder with a water-solid ratio of 13% and                  water and placing into a small dish and shaken                  into a ball, where a small ball with a particle                  size of 0.5-1 mm is formed, and the ball                  formation rate is 70%, raw cue ball, taking out                  for carbonization curing, where carbonization                  conditions comprises 99.9% carbon dioxide, 0.1                  MPa gas pressure conditions, carbonation curing                  for 1 hour, The second stage comprises pouring                  the carbon ball after the carbonization into                  vertical ball plate, starting the ball plate,                  spraying water to the surface of the mother ball                  to appear luster without combining, evenly ash to                  no ash, repeating the above operation, and cue                  ball grows up, third stage comprises continuing                  the above operation to grow the small ball to the                  maximum particle size to 25 mm, the fourth stage                  comprises taking out the small ball into the                  carbonization kettle and carrying out                  carbonization curing, where carbonization                  condition is 99.9% CO2, 0.3 MPa gas pressure                  condition carbonation curing for 1 hour.

PN CN108187614-A
TI Preparation of biomass porous carbon adsorbent used for removing flue gas mercury, by dissolving biomass in deionized water, fully stirring, hydrothermal reacting, heating, carbonizing, continuously, switching, activating and cooling.
AU LIU J
   CHEN F
   DONG Y
   WU D
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 201851189X
AB    NOVELTY - A biomass porous carbon adsorbent is prepared by dissolving biomass as carbon source in deionized water to obtain 10-30 wt.% biomass solution, fully stirring at 80-100 degrees C to obtain biomass gel, transferring into reaction kettle, hydrothermal reacting at 150-220 degrees C for 10-12 hours to obtain precursor, heating under inert gas atmosphere from room temperature to 600 degrees C at speed of 3-8 degrees C/minute, carbonizing at 600 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours to obtain carbonized precursor, continuously heating under inert gas atmosphere to 900 degrees C, switching inert gas to carbon dioxide, activating carbonized precursor at 900 degrees C for 2-4 hours, naturally cooling to room temperature, switching carbon dioxide to inert gas to obtain porous carbon product, grinding and crushing, using low-temperature plasma with operating voltage of 20-60 KV, modifying, and crushing and grinding under air atmosphere.
   USE - Method for preparing biomass porous carbon adsorbent used for removing flue gas mercury.
   ADVANTAGE - The product has high quality. The method is simple and reliable, has low cost, and does not use corrosive agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The biomass is lignin, starch and/or sucrose. Preferred Method: The adsorbent is prepared by dissolving biomass as carbon source in deionized water to obtain 15-25 wt.% biomass solution, fully stirring to obtain biomass gel, transferring into reaction kettle, hydrothermal reacting at 180-200 degrees C for 10-12 hours to obtain precursor, heating from room temperature to 600 degrees C at speed of 3-8 degrees C/minute and nitrogen gas flow rate of 50-200 mL/minute, carbonizing at 600 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours to obtain carbonized precursor, continuously heating under inert gas atmosphere to 900 degrees C, switching inert gas to carbon dioxide at flow rate of 50-200 mL/minute, activating carbonized precursor, naturally cooling to room temperature, switching carbon dioxide to inert gas to obtain porous carbon product, grinding and crushing, using low-temperature plasma at 3040 KV, modifying, and crushing and grinding under air atmosphere. The method further comprises screening to particle size of less than 150 mu m.

PN CN108174748-A; CN108174748-B
TI Accelerating color change during cultivation            period of mushroom sticks comprises e.g. providing air            conditioning temperature control and automatic            ventilation facilities, arranging white light LED and            irradiating daily.
AU ZHANG Z
   YAN B
   DONG H
AE SHANDONG QIHE BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201850228W
AB    NOVELTY - Accelerating color change during cultivation                period of mushroom sticks comprises (i) providing                air conditioning temperature control and automatic                ventilation facilities during growth of mushroom                when mycelium is full in whole bag, arranging white                light LED in the culture space so that the light                intensity of the culture space is 60-200 lux for 24                hours without interruption, adjusting ventilation                control environment and carbon dioxide                concentration at 2000-10000 ppm and setting ambient                temperature at 23-26 degrees C, and the humidity is                50-80%; and (ii) replacing white light LED light                source with the blue light LED light source after                the mushroom stick forms a nodule so that the                illumination light intensity of the culture space                is 40-200 lux, irradiating daily for more than 10                hours, adjusting carbon dioxide concentration in                ventilation environment to 2000-10000 ppm, setting                ambient temperature at 23-25 degrees C and humidity                at 50-80% until the color change of the stick and                completing culturing.
   USE - The method is useful for accelerating color                change during cultivation period of mushroom                sticks.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has a short color changing cycle                and good color conversion effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The white light LED                  light source is a white light LED light strip.                  The blue light LED light source is a blue light                  LED light strip. Preferred Method: The light                  intensity of the culture space in step (i) is 100                  lux. The ambient temperature in step (i) is set                  preferably at 23-24 degrees C at humidity 60-80%.                  The illumination space intensity of the culture                  space in step (ii) is 80 lux. The ambient                  temperature in step (ii) is set preferably at                  23-25 degrees C at humidity 50-70%. The method of                  irradiating daily in step (ii) preferably carried                  out for 16 hours. Preferred Composition: The                  concentration of carbon dioxide in step (ii) is                  preferably 2000-8000 ppm.

PT P
PN WO2018018571-A1; CN109476813-A; KR2019039135-A; IN201917006565-A; EP3491041-A1; US2019169393-A1; JP2019528191-W; EP3491041-A4; JP6906600-B2; US11104777-B2; CN109476813-B
TI Forming insulative apparatus used in e.g. freezer, involves connecting barrier material to mold, injecting polyurethane foam composition comprising polyol, isocyanate and supercritical carbon dioxide into mold, curing and applying vacuum.
AU CHAI N
   CHEN J
   YIN Y
   CHEN C
   PARENTI V
AE DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   CHAI N (CHAI-Individual)
   CHEN J (CHEN-Individual)
   YIN Y (YINY-Individual)
   CHEN C (CHEN-Individual)
   PARENTI V (PARE-Individual)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
GA 2018090488
AB    NOVELTY - Forming an insulative apparatus, comprises: connecting a barrier material to a mold; injecting a polyurethane foam composition comprising a polyol, an isocyanate and supercritical carbon dioxide into the mold; curing the polyurethane foam composition to form a polyurethane foam; and applying a vacuum to the mold to provide a pressure of 1-500 millibar.
   USE - The method is useful for forming an insulative apparatus (claimed), which is used in appliance insulating walls for refrigerators, freezers, hot water storage tanks and building applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides the insulative apparatus having a thermal conductivity of less than or equal to 16 (preferably 8-14) mW/mK at a pressure of less than or equal to 500 millibar, thus exhibiting improved thermal conductivity and/or improved manufacturability, as compared to other insulating panels, by utilizing the polyurethane foam and the pressure achieved by vacuum application that provide synergism in achieving the thermal conductivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for an insulative apparatus formed by the method, where the insulative apparatus comprises the mold, the barrier material connected to the mold, and the polyurethane foam inside the mold.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method further comprises combining the polyol, the isocyanate and the supercritical carbon dioxide in a pressure vessel, where the pressure vessel has a pressure of greater than 100 bar. Preferred Components: The polyurethane foam has: an average pore diameter of 2-100 mu m; an open cell percentage of greater than or equal to 95%; and a porosity of 80-98%. The polyol comprises a formulated polyol. The isocyanate comprises isocyanic acid polymethylenepolyphenylene ester. The barrier material has an oxygen transmission rate of 1e-20 m3 m/(m2 Pa day) to 1e-12 m3 m/(m2 Pa day).
   EXAMPLE - Polyol A (comprised of 47.55 g VORATEC SD301 (RTM: Low-viscosity glycerine-initiated polyether triol), 38 g VORANOL CP 260 (RTM: Polyether triol), 9.5 g Tercarol 5903 (RTM: Aromatic di-amine initiated polyether polyol)), catalyst (comprised of 0.57 g POLYCAT-41 (RTM: Dimethylaminopropyl-hexahydrotriazine,N,N',N'', 0.48 g POLYCAT-5 (RTM: N,N,N,N,N-Pentamethyldiethylenetriamine), 1.9 g Polycat-8 (RTM: N,N-Dimethylcyclohexylamine) and 2 g L 6164 (RTM: Silicone surfactant) were added to a pressure reactor that was maintained at 10 MPa and 40 degrees C. Supercritical carbon dioxide was injected into the pressure reactor to saturation of the contents of the pressure reactor. The temperature and the pressure inside the pressure reactor were maintained for 30 minutes to facilitate the carbon dioxide saturation while the contents of the pressure reactor were stirred. A high pressure homogenizer was used to form an emulsion with the contents of the pressure reactor. The emulsion had droplets with diameters of 5-300 nm. A polyurethane foam composition was formed when 101 g PAPI-135C (RTM: Isocyanic acid polymethylenepolyphenylene ester) was added to the pressure reactor and the contents of the pressure reactor were stirred for 1 minute and then the polyurethane foam composition was injected into a mold that was internally lined with SARANEX 23P (RTM: Barrier material). The polyurethane foam composition cured to form polyurethane foam completely filling the mold cavity, a vacuum was applied to achieve a mold cavity pressure of 10 millibar and the barrier material was sealed to obtain the insulative apparatus.
DC A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.); A84 (Household and office fittings - including carpets and carbon paper.); A93 (Roads, building, construction flooring.)
MC A08-B02; A11-B06C; A11-B12; A11-C02D; A12-R06; A12-S02C; A12-S02F
IP C08G-018/28; C08G-018/72; C08J-009/14; B29C-039/00; B29C-039/02; B29C-039/42; B29K-075/00; C08G-018/08; C08G-018/76; C08J-009/12; C08G-018/18; B29C-044/00; B29C-044/02; B29C-045/00; C08G-018/00; B65D-081/38; F16L-059/02; B29C-044/60; B29C-044/06; B29C-044/12; B29C-044/14; C08G-018/09; C08G-018/20; C08G-018/48

AU AIJUN S
   JIAQI H

GA 201810520A
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a device for solid                fermentation, the solid fermentation device is                totally enclosed type board room structure,                comprising a fermentation room, used for                controlling the body temperature of the                fermentation room, a temperature control system for                controlling the fermentation room body humidity of                the humidity control system; dissolved oxygen                control system for control of the fermentation room                carbon dioxide concentration and oxygen                concentration, control the temperature control                system, a humidity control system, a dissolved                oxygen control system of the general control box;                said temperature control system and a humidity                control system is an internal circulating pipeline                structure. the dissolved oxygen control system                exchange with the outside air, said main control                box respectively to realize independent control of                the temperature control system, a humidity control                system, a dissolved oxygen control system through                three sensors. the device has low cost, high cost                performance, and it can according to the specific                strain characteristics and independent of the                fermentation to the fermentation temperature,                humidity, carbon dioxide and oxygen concentration                for fast and precise process management, wide                application range and effectively improves the                fermenting quality.
