PT P
PN WO2018185459-A1; GB2564509-A; GB2564509-B; CA3056860-A1; IN201947043938-A; EP3606871-A1; US2020109108-A1; US10759744-B2; EP3606871-B1; RU2019135096-A; IN377652-B; RU2758773-C2; ES2882289-T3
TI Preparation of formaldehyde-stabilized urea used as e.g. fertilizer, involves subjecting synthetic gas to water-gas shift, recovering carbon dioxide, synthesizing methanol and then ammonia, reacting portion of ammonia, and stabilizing urea.
AU BARKER S
   DAVISON T
   PACH J D
   PACH Y D
AE JOHNSON MATTHEY PLC (JOHO-C)
   JOHNSON MATTHEY PLC (JOHO-C)
   JOHNSON MATTHEY PLC (JOHO-C)
   JOHNSON MATTHEY PLC (JOHO-C)
GA 201878121P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of formaldehyde-stabilized urea involves (a) generating a synthesis gas, (b) subjecting the synthesis gas to one or more stages of water-gas shift, (c) cooling the shifted gas, (d) recovering carbon dioxide, (e) synthesizing methanol to recover the methanol and a methanol synthesis off-gas, (f) subjecting portion(s) of the recovered methanol to oxidation, (g) subjecting the methanol synthesis off-gas to methanation in a methanation reactor containing a methanation catalyst to form an ammonia synthesis gas, (h) synthesizing ammonia, (i) reacting a portion of the ammonia, and (j) stabilizing urea. The process involves recovering a carbon dioxide-containing gas stream from the absorbent regeneration unit, compressing at least a portion of the recovered carbon dioxide-containing gas stream to form a compressed carbon dioxide-containing gas stream and passing the compressed carbon dioxide-containing gas stream to the methanol synthesis unit.
   USE - Preparation of formaldehyde-stabilized urea used as fertilizer and for manufacturing industrial chemicals.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient, simple, economical and environmentally-friendly preparation of formaldehyde-stabilized urea.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of formaldehyde-stabilized urea involves (a) generating a synthesis gas, (b) subjecting the synthesis gas to one or more stages of water-gas shift in one or more water-gas shift reactors to form a shifted gas, (c) cooling the shifted gas below the dew point and recovering condensate to form a dried shifted gas, (d) recovering carbon dioxide from the dried shifted gas in a carbon dioxide removal unit to form a carbon dioxide-depleted synthesis gas (32), (e) synthesizing methanol from the carbon dioxide-depleted synthesis gas in a methanol synthesis unit and recovering the methanol and a methanol synthesis off-gas, (f) subjecting at least a portion of the recovered methanol to oxidation with air to form formaldehyde in a stabilizer production unit, (g) subjecting the methanol synthesis off-gas to methanation in a methanation reactor containing a methanation catalyst to form an ammonia synthesis gas, (h) synthesizing ammonia from the ammonia synthesis gas in an ammonia production unit and recovering the ammonia, (i) reacting a portion of the ammonia and at least a portion of the recovered carbon dioxide stream in a urea production unit to form a urea stream and (j) stabilizing the urea by mixing the urea stream and a stabilizer prepared using the formaldehyde produced in the stabilizer production unit. The carbon dioxide removal unit operates by means of absorption using a liquid absorbent and comprises an absorbent regeneration unit (26). The process involves recovering a carbon dioxide-containing gas stream from the absorbent regeneration unit, compressing at least a portion of the recovered carbon dioxide-containing gas stream to form a compressed carbon dioxide-containing gas stream and passing the compressed carbon dioxide-containing gas stream to the methanol synthesis unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view-explaining preparation of formaldehyde-stabilized urea.
   Absorption unit (24)
   Regeneration unit (26)
   Carbon dioxide-depleted synthesis gas (32)
   Methanation unit (56)
   Stabilization unit (76)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The stages of water-gas shift comprise one or more stages of high temperature shift, low temperature shift, medium temperature shift, isothermal shift and sour shift, preferably a single stage of high temperature shift, a combination of high temperature shift and low temperature shift, a single stage of medium temperature shift, or a combination of medium temperature shift and low temperature shift. The carbon dioxide removal unit comprises an absorption unit (24) comprising one or more absorption vessels to which the dried shifted gas and an absorbent liquid are fed, and an absorbent regeneration unit comprising one or more absorbent regeneration vessels in which a carbon dioxide-laden absorbent liquid is regenerated by heating and/or reducing the pressure, to produce the carbon dioxide- containing gas stream. The carbon dioxide-containing gas stream comprises a vent gas stream containing carbon dioxide, hydrogen and other absorbed gases, and/or pure carbon dioxide stream, and further comprises steam and before compression, the carbon dioxide-containing gas stream is cooled to below the dew point to condense the steam as water which is recovered using a separator to produce a dry carbon dioxide-containing gas stream. The vent gas stream comprises 10-45 mol%, preferably 30-35 mol% hydrogen. The essentially pure carbon dioxide stream comprises less than 10 mol%, preferably less than 5 mol% hydrogen. The amount of vent gas passed to the methanol synthesis unit is 0.1-2.5 vol.% with respect to the dried shifted gas fed to the carbon dioxide removal unit. The amount of pure carbon dioxide passed to the methanol synthesis unit is 0.1-10 vol.% preferably 0.1-2.5 vol.% with respect to the total pure carbon dioxide separated in the carbon dioxide removal unit. The formaldehyde stabilizer production unit generates a stabilizer unit vent gas which is recycled to the process, directly or after one or more stages of vent gas treatment in a vent-gas treatment unit.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The synthesis gas generation stage involves steam reforming of a hydrocarbon such as natural gas, naphtha or a refinery off-gas, or the gasification of a carbonaceous feedstock, such as coal or biomass, preferably primary reforming in a fired or gas-heated steam reformer and secondary reforming in a secondary reformer with air or oxygen-enriched air. The methanol synthesis is operated in a single stage at an inlet temperature of 200-320 degrees C, preferably 200-270 degrees C to the catalyst. A crude methanol product recovered from the methanol synthesis stage is fed to the oxidation reactor.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); ; E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.); E35; E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PN WO2020210865-A1; AU2020259801-A1; KR2021154830-A; CN113825721-A; EP3956260-A1; US2022194787-A1; JP2022529349-W
TI Chemical looping process for production of            hydrogen and co-production of carbon dioxide involves            redox loop that comprises feeding solid oxygen carrier            in oxidized form or reduced form to reaction zone to            form combustion products.
AU LIM K
   HADLEY T D
   YAMAGUCHI D
   TANG L
   ORELLANA J
   LIM K S
   LEVERDION H
   YAMAGUCHI-KI
   TANGRYANGGWANG
   JOSE O
   LIN G
AE COMMONWEALTH SCI & IND RES ORG (CSIR-C)
   COMMONWEALTH SCI & IND RES ORG (CSIR-C)
   COMMONWEALTH SCI & IND RES ORG (CSIR-C)
   COMMONWEALTH SCI & IND RES ORG (CSIR-C)
GA 2020A4054U
AB    NOVELTY - A chemical looping process for production of                hydrogen and co-production of carbon dioxide                involves first redox loop that comprises feeding                1st solid oxygen carrier to a first reaction zone                (R1) in which first carbonaceous fuel is also fed,                which reacts with first solid oxygen carrier fed at                its maximum oxidizing state (fully-oxidized form),                leading to the formation of combustion products                carbon dioxide and water and solid oxygen carrier                at a lower oxidizing state (reduced form); and                feeding the first solid oxygen carrier in reduced                form to a second reaction zone (R2) into which air                is also fed, obtaining, from the oxidation of first                solid oxygen carrier, heat and solid oxygen carrier                in fully-oxidized form to be recycled to the first                reaction zone (R1); and second redox loop that                comprises feeding a second solid oxygen carrier to                a third reaction zone (R3), which reacts with                second solid oxygen carrier, leading to formation                of combustion products carbon dioxide and                water.
   USE - A chemical looping process for production of                hydrogen and co-production of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The process allows the continuous production                of separate streams of hydrogen gas (H2) and carbon                dioxide (CO2) with a high purity, at the same time,                with good thermal efficiency created by selective                exchange of heat through solid oxygen carrier                transfer from the two separate fuel reactor (FR)                reaction zones, and allows an adjustment of heat                required for driving the steam reactor (SR) loop by                regulating transfer of oxygen carrier. The process                produces hydrogen with less need for power                generation. The method and system have high                reactivity with carbonaceous fuel, such as                hydrocarbon fuels and favorable thermodynamics                regarding the fuel conversion to CO2; high redox                stability during redox cycles for favorable                economics; high resistance to agglomeration and                sintering; high reactivity with oxygen and steam;                high mechanical strength under fluidized                conditions; wide redox window; high mechanical                strength and resistance against attrition; high                oxygen storage capacity (or reversible oxygen) for                a larger H2 production; and high coking                resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A chemical looping process for production of                hydrogen and co-production of carbon dioxide                involves first redox loop that comprises feeding                1st solid oxygen carrier to a first reaction zone                (R1) in which first carbonaceous fuel is also fed,                which reacts with first solid oxygen carrier fed at                its maximum oxidizing state (fully-oxidized form),                leading to the formation of combustion products                carbon dioxide and water and solid oxygen carrier                at a lower oxidizing state (reduced form); and                feeding the first solid oxygen carrier in reduced                form to a second reaction zone (R2) into which air                is also fed, obtaining, from the oxidation of first                solid oxygen carrier, heat and solid oxygen carrier                in fully-oxidized form to be recycled to the first                reaction zone (R1); and second redox loop that                comprises feeding a second solid oxygen carrier to                a third reaction zone (R3) in which second                carbonaceous fuel is also fed, which reacts with                second solid oxygen carrier fed at its an                intermediate oxidizing state (oxidized form),                leading to formation of combustion products carbon                dioxide and water and solid oxygen carrier at a                lower oxidizing state (reduced form); and feeding                the second solid oxygen carrier in reduced form to                a fourth reaction zone (R4) into which steam is                also fed, which reacts with reduced form of solid                oxygen carrier, producing hydrogen and solid oxygen                carrier at an intermediate oxidizing state                (oxidized form) to be recycled to the third                reaction zone (R3) and/or first reaction zone (R1),                where the first reaction zone (R1) and third                reaction zone (R3) are interconnected allowing                transfer of greater than or equal to 1 portion of                first solid oxygen carrier from first reaction zone                (R1) to third reaction zone (R3). An INDEPENDENT                CLAIM is included for chemical looping system (300)                for production of hydrogen and co-production of                carbon dioxide comprising a first redox loop that                comprises a first fuel reactor into which is fed a                first solid oxygen carrier at its maximum oxidizing                state (fully-oxidized form) and first carbonaceous                fuel is a fed, which react to form combustion                products carbon dioxide and water and the solid                oxygen carrier at a lower oxidizing state (reduced                form); and an air reactor into which the first                solid oxygen carrier in reduced form and air is                fed, to obtain, from the oxidation of the first                solid oxygen carrier, heat and the first solid                oxygen carrier in fully-oxidized form to be                recycled to the first fuel reactor; and a second                redox loop that comprises a second fuel reactor                into which is fed a second solid oxygen carrier at                an intermediate oxidizing state (oxidized form) and                a second carbonaceous fuel, which react leading to                the formation of the combustion products carbon                dioxide and water and the second solid oxygen                carrier at a lower oxidizing state (reduced form);                and a steam reactor into which is fed the second                solid oxygen carrier in reduced form and steam,                which react to produce hydrogen and the second                solid oxygen carrier at an intermediate oxidizing                state (oxidized form) to be recycled to the second                fuel reactor and/or the first fuel reactor; where                the first fuel reactor and second fuel reactor are                interconnected to allow transfer of greater than or                equal to 1 portion of first solid oxygen carrier                from first fuel reactor to the second fuel                reactor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                system in parallel operation for small scale                application and moderate operating pressure.
   Chemical looping system (300)
   First reaction zone (R1)
   Second reaction zone (R2)
   Third reaction zone (R3)
   Fourth reaction zone (R4)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first solid oxygen                  carrier and second solid oxygen carrier contains                  greater than or equal to 1 element selected from                  elements which, in addition to metallic state,                  have greater than or equal to 3 different                  oxidation states and are capable of producing                  greater than or equal to 2 redox pairs in the                  order of the oxidation state. The first and                  second solid oxygen carriers comprise greater                  than or equal to 1 iron (Fe)-based, nickel                  (Ni)-based, tungsten (W)-based, copper                  (Cu)-based, cerium (Ce)-based, manganese                  (Mn)-based oxide. The first and second solid                  oxygen carriers comprise metal oxides selected                  from iron oxide (Fe2O3), tungsten oxide (WO3),                  tin oxide (SnO2), Ni-ferrites, zinc                  (Zn),(Mn)-ferrites, and Cu-ferrites. The element                  contained in the solid oxygen carrier is iron.                  Iron is present in the solid oxygen carrier in                  binary form FexOy and/or in ternary form FexZzOy,                  where x is greater than or equal to 1, y is                  greater than or equal to 0 and z is greater than                  or equal to 1 and Z is greater than or equal to 1                  element selected from Ni, titanium (Ti), Mn,                  aluminum (Al), chromium (Cr), gallium (Ga), Ce,                  zirconium (Zr), vanadium (V) and molybdenum                  (Mo).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first carbonaceous                  fuel comprises solid, liquid or gaseous                  carbonaceous fuel. The first carbonaceous fuel is                  selected from greater than or equal to 1 of                  coals, biomass, oils, or liquid hydrocarbons or                  gaseous hydrocarbons. The second carbonaceous                  fuel comprises a liquid or gaseous carbonaceous                  fuel. The second carbonaceous fuel comprises a                  liquid hydrocarbon or a gaseous                  hydrocarbon.

TI Device useful for preparing lycopene nano powder, comprises raw material dissolver and a crystallization separator, introduction tube is perpendicular to plane of top wall and the introduction tube is provided with a first valve.
AU HUI B
   FU J
   ZHANG Y
AE UNIV BEIJING UNION (UYBU-C)
GA 201931041L
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises raw material dissolver and a crystallization separator. The raw material dissolver has a cylindrical shape with an aspect ratio of 1:1-48:1 and introduction tube is disposed in the center of the top wall of the raw material dissolver for introducing carbon dioxide and raw materials. The introduction tube is perpendicular to a plane of the top wall and the introduction tube is provided with a first valve for controlling switching and flow rate of carbon dioxide and raw materials. The raw material dissolver is provided with a first temperature control unit capable of controlling temperature. The upper portion of the crystal separator has a cylindrical shape, and the lower portion has an inverted truncated cone shape and length to diameter ratio of the cylindrical portion is 1:2-1:4, where ratio of the height of the inverted truncated cone to the diameter of the upper base is 1:0.4-1:1 and diameter of the upper bottom surface to the lower bottom surface is 1:0.05-1:0.1.
   USE - The device is useful for preparing lycopene nano powder (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device: prepares lycopene with safe manner under environmentally-friendly condition; and is suitable for various food grade lycopene raw materials e.g. biological extracts and chemical synthetic products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Device comprises raw material dissolver and a crystallization separator. The raw material dissolver has a cylindrical shape with an aspect ratio of 1:1-48:1 and introduction tube is disposed in the center of the top wall of the raw material dissolver for introducing carbon dioxide and raw materials. The introduction tube is perpendicular to a plane of the top wall and the introduction tube is provided with a first valve for controlling switching and flow rate of carbon dioxide and raw materials. The raw material dissolver is provided with a first temperature control unit capable of controlling temperature. The upper portion of the crystal separator has a cylindrical shape, and the lower portion has an inverted truncated cone shape and length to diameter ratio of the cylindrical portion is 1:2-1:4, where ratio of the height of the inverted truncated cone to the diameter of the upper base is 1:0.4-1:1 and diameter of the upper bottom surface to the lower bottom surface is 1:0.05-1:0.1 and carbon dioxide discharge pipe is arranged in the center of the top wall of the crystallizer. The carbon dioxide discharge pipe is perpendicular to a plane of a top wall of the crystallizer and second valve is disposed on the carbon dioxide discharge pipe for controlling the switch and the flow rate. The crystal collection tube is arranged at the bottom of the crystallizer. The crystallization collecting tube is perpendicular to a bottom plane of the crystallization separator and third valve is disposed on the crystallization collecting pipe. The crystallization separator is provided with a second temperature control unit capable of controlling temperature and connecting pipe is arranged between the raw material dissolver and the crystallization separator. The two ends of the connecting pipe are respectively connected to the side wall of the raw material dissolver and the crystallization separator and perpendicular to the tangential direction of the sidewall of the raw material dissolver and the crystallizer. The connecting pipe is provided with a fourth valve for controlling the switch and the flow rate and volume ratio of the raw material dissolver and crystallizer is 1:3-1:20. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing lycopene nanopowder, comprising (i) opening loading first valve, the second valve, and the fourth valve in the apparatus for preparing lycopene nano powder, closing third valve, where carbon dioxide discharge pipe is connected to -0.06 MPa, applying negative pressure source lycopene raw material to be introduced from the introduction tube, where introduction amount of lycopene raw material is 1/4-1/8 volume of the raw material dissolver in the device for preparing the lycopene nano powder, and controlling temperature of the raw material in dissolver at 50-55 degrees C; (ii) emptying device to open the first valve, closing second valve, and the third valve, and the fourth valve and introducing carbon dioxide gas with a pressure of greater than 1 atmosphere from the introduction pipe to evacuate the air in the system; (iii) dissolving raw material, opening the first valve, closing the second valve, the third valve, and the fourth valve, introducing the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid from the introduction tube so that the pressure in the raw material dissolver reaches to 7.2-60 MPa and the temperature reaches to 32-65 degrees C, closing the first valve and allowing to stand for 30-300 minutes to saturate the solubility of lycopene in the lycopene raw material in the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid in the raw material dissolver; (iv) opening second valve by crystallization, opening fourth valve slowly, adjusting second valve and the fourth valve to maintain the pressure in the crystallization separator at 0.1-7.2 MPa at 4-32 degrees C, transferring into the crystallization separator after dissolving all the lycopene in the raw material dissolver, closing first valve and stopping input of the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid; and (v) controlling internal pressure drop of the device to 1 atmosphere and temperature in the crystallization separator at 70-90 degrees C and opening third valve to collect lycopene crystals.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic representation of device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method of preparing lycopene fermentation product extract comprises extracting a Blakeslea trispora fermentation product via an organic solvent or a supercritical carbon dioxide fluid. The method of preparing tomato fruit extract comprises extracting by an organic solvent extraction method or a supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction method.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lycopene raw material comprises lycopene chemical synthetic product with purity of greater than or equal to 96 wt.%, lycopene fermentation product extract with purity of 6-99 wt.% or tomato fruit extract with purity of 6-20 wt.%. The lycopene crystal has a crystal grain size ranging from 10 nm to 2 mu m.

PT P
PN CN109824059-A
TI Preparation of zeolite involves using colloidal silica and pseudoboehmite as colloidal matrix for hydrothermal synthesis process.
AU DU T
   WANG Y
   GONG H
   JIA H
   QIU Z
   ZHOU L
AE UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UYDB-C)
GA 201950061F
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of ZSM-5 zeolite involves using LudoxAS-40 (RTM: Colloidal silica) and pseudoboehmite as colloidal matrix for hydrothermal synthesis process.
   USE - Preparation of ZSM-5 zeolite.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of ZSM-5 zeolite with high quality and strong carbon dioxide adsorbing performance, which reduces amount of impurity crystals and increases volume of micropores, by economical process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The ZSM-5 zeolite is prepared by (S1) adding 0.5-0.6 part mass pseudoboehmite to container containing 9-11 parts mass deionized water at room temperature, stirring for 15-25 minutes, adding 0.1-0.3 part mass concentrated hydrated hydrochloric acid to obtain sol particles of pseudoboehmite, (S2) adding 58-59.2 parts mass tetrapropylammonium hydroxide templating agent and 1.1-1.3 parts mass ionic agent sodium chloride to sol particles of pseudoboehmite, stirring for 0.6-1.5 hours to obtain mixture of aluminum sources, (S3) simultaneously adding LudoxAS-40 (RTM: Colloidal silica) and rice husk ash silica powder to aluminum source mixture, stirring for 3-5 hours to obtain gum system mixture, (S4) placing obtained gum system mixture in a PTFE-lined high-pressure reaction kettle, heating at 150-185 degrees C for 3 days to obtain crystal sample, (S5) washing and drying the crystal sample, and calcining at 450-550 degrees C for 5-8 hours for removing tetrapropylammonium hydroxide templating agent.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass fraction of pseudoboehmite in step (S1) is 0.58. The mass fraction of deionized water is 10. The mass fraction of concentrated hydrochloric acid is 0.2. The mass fraction of ionic agent sodium chloride is 1.17. The mass percentage of LudoxAS-40 (RTM: Colloidal silica) and the mixed silicon source in the step (S3) is 25-75%. The mass ratio of aluminum source mixture to mixed silicon source is 0-1. The ratio of silicon, tetrapropylammonium hydroxide, aluminum, water, sodium and hydrochloric acid is 25:9:1:500:2.5:0.25.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass fraction of tetrapropylammonium hydroxide templating agent in step (S2) is 58.49. Preferred Conditions: The stirring time in step (I) is 20 minutes. The stirring time in step (II) is 1 hour. The heating temperature in step (S4) is 165 degrees C, and the heating duration is 4 days. The calcination temperature in step (S5) is 500 degrees C, and the calcination duration is 6 hours.

PN TW699232-B1; TW202110517-A
TI Preparation of patchouli extract by crushing patchouli raw material into patchouli powder and granulating to obtain patchouli granules, introducing liquid carbon dioxide to perform supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process on patchouli particles.
AU LIANG M
AE CROP PROTECTION SINGAPORE PTE LTD (CROP-Non-standard)
GA 2021682238
AB    NOVELTY - A method of preparing a patchouli extract is provided. The method includes the following steps. A patchouli material is grounded into patchouli powder and pelleted to obtain patchouli granules. Liquid carbon dioxide is introduced to perform a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process on the patchouli granules, wherein the extraction pressure is about 110-180 bar and the extraction temperature is about 40-60. During the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process, the extracted product obtained, based on the total weight of the introduced liquid carbon dioxide not exceeding 10 times the weight of the patchouli granules, is collected as the first patchouli extract. The first patchouli extract includes the first patchouli oil. During the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process, the extracted product obtained, based on the total weight of the introduced liquid carbon dioxide exceeding 10 times the weight of the patchouli granules but not exceeding 15 times the weight of the patchouli granules, is collected as the second patchouli extract. The second patchouli extract includes the second patchouli oil.

PN CN109679643-A; CN109679643-B; US2020224075-A1; US10934467-B2
TI Oxygen-reducing air/liquid carbon dioxide double            interfacial layer foam fracturing fluid useful for            producing shale gas comprises liquid carbon dioxide            phase, gas phase and nano-strengthening agent.
AU LV Q
   ZHOU T
   ZHENG R
   DONG C
   ZHENG H
   ZHANG J
   LIN M
   YANG Z
   WANG J
   DONG Z
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM BEIJING (UPEB-C)
GA 201941106L
AB    NOVELTY - Oxygen-reducing air/liquid carbon dioxide                double interfacial layer foam fracturing fluid                comprises liquid carbon dioxide phase, gas phase                and nano-strengthening agent, where the liquid                carbon dioxide phase is formed by dissolving a gas                soluble surfactant compound in liquid carbon                dioxide, the gas phase is a mixture of                oxygen-reduced air and saturated vapor of the                liquid carbon dioxide, and the nano-strengthening                agent is an aqueous solution of hydrophobic silicon                dioxide nanoparticles, particulate co-solvent, and                a water-soluble surfactant.
   USE - The foam fracturing fluid is useful for                producing shale gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The foam fracturing fluid has relatively high                viscosity and good temperature stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the foam fracturing fluid comprising (a)                preparing aqueous solution of the hydrophobic                silicon dioxide nanoparticles, particulate                co-solvent and water-soluble surfactant compound,                (b) injecting the nano-strengthening agent into a                high-pressure stirred tank, and then injecting the                oxygen-reduced air, and (c) injecting carbon                dioxide gas into the high-pressure stirred tank,                and then injecting gas-soluble surfactant                compound.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The                  nano-strengthening agent comprises 0.4-2 wt.%                  hydrophobic silicon dioxide nanoparticle, 0.1-0.5                  wt.% granular cosolvent, 0.1-0.6 wt.%                  water-soluble surfactant compound, and remaining                  amount of water. Preferred Components: The foam                  fracturing fluid has a foam quality of 40-90%.                  The gas soluble surfactant compound is                  2-trifluoromethane-3-decafluoropentane,                  3-ethoxytrifluorohexane, fluorobutane ethyl                  ether, fluorobutane methyl ether, and                  1,1,2,2-tetrafluoroethyl-2,2,3,3-tetrafluoropropyl                  ether. The initial volume content of the                  gas-soluble surfactant compound in the liquid                  carbon dioxide phase is 0.2-2%. The volumetric                  content of oxygen in the oxygen-reduced air is                  less than or equal to 5%. The gas partial                  pressure of the oxygen-reduced air is more than                  one time of the saturated vapor partial pressure                  of the liquid carbon dioxide. The hydrophobic                  silicon dioxide nanoparticles are silicon dioxide                  nanoparticles modified with silanol groups having                  a density of 0.3-0.5 /nm2. The wetting angle to                  the distilled water is 107-125 degrees , and the                  particle size is 10-40 nm. The particulate                  co-solvent is ethanol, ethylene glycol or                  isopropanol. The water-soluble surfactant                  compound is a compound of sodium                  bis(2-ethylhexyl) sulfonate succinate and sodium                  salt of alpha -sulfofatty acid methyl ester. The                  volume content of the nano-strengthening agent in                  the foam fracturing fluid is less than or equal                  to 9%. Preferred Method: The method comprises                  carrying out under the predetermined pressure of                  7-50 MPa, then stirring by using Waring Blender                  method at a rate of 3000-5000 revolutions/minute                  for 3-8 minutes.
DC H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).)

TI Absorbing and reusing carbon dioxide in compost by passing carbon dioxide gas generated by aerobic composting reactor into microalgae incubator, culturing microalgae, sedimenting, adding conditioning agent and returning to compost.
AU WEI Z
   ZHANG X

   WEI D
   LI Y
   LIU Z
   LUO S
   CAO J
AE UNIV NORTHEAST AGRIC (UNEA-C)
GA 2018682120
AB    NOVELTY - Method for absorbing and reusing carbon dioxide in compost using microalgae involves passing the carbon dioxide gas generated by the aerobic composting reactor through a pump into the microalgae incubator, anaerobially culturing the microalgae under illumintaion at 25 plus minus 3 degrees C and light intensity of 2500 plus minus 10 lx in the microalgae incubator, where the light-dark ratio is 18 hours:6 hours, adding the microalgae liquid and the microalgae residue into a sedimentation tank, then mixing with the conditioning agent and then returning to the compost in the aerobic composting reactor through the reflux pump until the mass ratio of carbon and nitrogen is 20-25:1 and moisture content is 55-65% and (b) repeating the step (a) to completely absorb carbon dioxide in the compost and recycling the carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for absorbing and reusing carbon dioxide in compost.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the release of greenhouse gas carbon dioxide without secondary pollution, saves manpower, material resources and energy and is environmentally-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (a), the compost in the aerobic composting reactor comprises kitchen waste, manure or straw compost, the conditioning agent comprises sawdust, bran or rice husk. In step (a), the moisture content of the compost is 60%. The step (a) involves anaerobically culturing the microalgae under illumintaion at 25 degrees C, 26 degrees C, 27 degrees C or 28 degrees C and light intensity of 2505 lx, 2510 lx, 2506 lx, 2507 lx or 2508 lx, where the light-dark ratio is 18 hours:6 hours. Preferred Medium: The microalgae culture medium in the microalgae incubator comprises 100 mg/l sodium nitrate, 75 mg/l magnesium sulfate heptahydrate, 10 mg/l dipotassium phosphate, 40 mg/l calcium chloride dihydrate, 20 mg/l sodium carbonate, 6 mg/l ferric citrate and 1 mg/l disodium EDTA dihydrate and has pH of 8.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fluoro(alkyl vinyl                  ether) unit is a perfluoro(propyl vinyl ether)                  unit.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The composition                  comprises 5.2- 7.2% mass hexafluoropropylene unit                  and 1.2-2.2% mass fluoro(alkyl vinyl ether) unit                  with respect to the total monomer units.                  Preferred Property: The copolymer has a melt flow                  rate at 372&#176; C of 0.7-8g/10                  minutes.
   EXAMPLE - Deionized water (40.25 kg) and 0.419 kg                methanol were placed into an autoclave with a                volume of 174 l and equipped with a stirrer, and                the inside of the autoclave was sufficiently                replaced with vacuum nitrogen. Then, the inside of                the autoclave was evacuated and 40.25 kg                hexafluoropropylene (HFP) and 0.82 kg                perfluoro(propyl vinyl ether) (PPVE) were charged                into the evacuated autoclave and the autoclave                heated to 25.5&#176; C. Subsequently,                tetrafluoroethylene (TFE) was added until the                internal pressure of the autoclave reached 0.970                MPa, and then 1.25 kg 8 %mass di(&#969;                -hydroperfluorohexanoyl)peroxide solution (DHP) was                added to the autoclave to initiate polymerization.                The internal pressure of the autoclave at the start                of polymerization was set to 0.970 MPa, and the set                pressure maintained continuously by adding TFE.                After 1.5 hours from the initiation of                polymerization, 0.419 kg of methanol was added.                After 2 hours and 4 hours from the start of                polymerization, 1.25 kg DHP was added and the                internal pressure was lowered by 0.002 MPa. After 6                hours, 0.96 kg added and the internal pressure                lowered by 0.002 MPa, 0.25 kg DHP added every 2                hours until the reaction was completed, and the                internal pressure was lowered by 0.002 MPa each                time. 0.13 kg PPVE added when the amount of TFE                continuously added reached 8.1 kg, 16.2 kg, and                24.3 kg. Further, when the amount of TFE added                reached 6 kg and 18.1 kg, 0.419 kg methanol was                added into the autoclave. The polymerization                terminated when the additional amount of TFE added                reached 40.25 kg. After the polymerization was                completed, unreacted TFE and HFP were released to                obtain wet powder. After the wet powder was washed                with pure water, it was dried at 150?0C for 10                hours to obtain 43.4 kg dry powder. The obtained                powder was melt-extruded at 370&#176; C with a                screw extruder to obtain copolymer pellets.

PN CN114848776-A
TI Processing plant refined and dried scallop            essential oil comprises e.g. mixing shaddock peel,            orange leaves, Ephedra, myrrh, Amomum fruit, Angelica            and turmeric with pure water, and mixing orange peel,            Chrysanthemum, Perilla and Ageratum to obtain mixture,            adding alcohol solution and olive oil.
AU JIANG R
   CHEN J
   LI M
AE GUIZHOU SAATCHITAIHO PHARM TECHNOLOGY CO (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2022A6093S
AB    NOVELTY - Processing plant refined and dried scallop                essential oil comprises (i) mixing shaddock peel,                orange leaves, Ephedra, myrrh, Amomumfruit,                Angelicaand turmeric with pure water, obtaining                mixture A, mixing orange peel, Chrysanthemum,                Perilla and Ageratum, removing impurities and                placing in a sealed container which is mixture B,                (ii) placing mixtures A and B, cooling, and                freezing, (iii) subjecting mixture A to water vapor                distillation for volatile oil extraction to obtain                extract A and residue B, (iv) thawing mixture B,                adding alcohol solution and olive oil, separating                to obtain extract and residue C, and combining                extract and extract A to obtain volatile oil, (v)                mixing residue B obtained in step (iii) and residue                C obtained in step (iv), preparing pulp, and adding                citric acid, (vi) extracting residue with alkali                liquid, and adding with calcium hydroxide, (vii)                concentrating and drying filtrate and (viii) mixing                volatile oil and dried product, and adding                lecithin, betaine and ethanol.
   USE - The method is useful for processing plant                refined and dried scallop essential oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: selects materials scientifically;                utilizes acid, alkali and alcohol for step-by-step                extraction in the extraction, which comprehensively                covers multiple free states of essential oils;                makes the prepared essential oils more fragrant,                and comprehensive nutrients; maintains the original                form of essential oils; adds minerals and                polysaccharides; and prepares stable solution state                with long lasting fragrance of essential oil and                better use experience.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing plant refined and dried scallop                essential oil comprises (i) preparing raw materials                comprising taking 20-30 pts. wt. shaddock peel,                13-19 pts. wt. orange leaves, 2-5 pts. wt. Ephedra,                7-15 pts. wt. myrrh, 4-9 pts. wt. Amomum fruit,                10-14 pts. wt. Angelica and 18-22 pts. wt.                turmeric, cleaning, mixing all raw materials with                pure water in a mass ratio of 1:0.3-0.5, sealing                and soaking for 20-30 minutes to obtain mixture A,                mixing 10-15 pts. wt. orange peel, 3-5 pts. wt.                Chrysanthemum, 20-25 pts. wt. Perilla, and 3-8 pts.                wt. Ageratum, removing impurities and placing in a                sealed container which is mixture B, (ii) placing                the mixtures A and B into freezer, cooling to -40                to 50&#176;C at a cooling rate of                5-8&#176;C/minute, freezing for 3-5 hours,                adjusting the temperature to -10 to 15&#176;C,                and freezing for 10-15 hours, (iii) water vapor                extracting by subjecting mixture A to water vapor                distillation for volatile oil extraction to obtain                extract A and residue B, (iv) supercritical                extracting by thawing mixture B, adding alcohol                solution and olive oil, ultrasonically processing                for 10-13 minutes, placing the mixture into                extracting kettle, filling the extraction kettle                with liquid carbon dioxide, adjusting the                temperature to increase the pressure, controlling                the temperature in extraction kettle to be                30-40&#176;C and pressure to 40-50 MPa,                extracting for 3-4 hours, introducing the solution                for dissolving extract into separator, separating                to obtain extract and residue C, combining the                extract and the extract A obtained in the step                (iii) to obtain volatile oil, (v) extracting                residue acid liquid by mixing the residue B                obtained in the step (iii) and residue C obtained                in the step (iv), adding 2-3 times the mass of pure                water to prepare pulp, stirring for 10-13 minutes,                adding citric acid, heating to 60-65&#176;C for                2-4 hours, boiling the solution, keeping the                temperature for 10-15 minutes, filtering while hot,                and collecting the filtrate and filter residue,                where the citric acid quality is 3-4% of solution                quality, (vi) extracting residue with alkali liquid                by mixing filter residue obtained in the step (v)                with water, placing in an ice-water bath, adding                with calcium hydroxide, stirring for 20-30 minutes,                heating to 40-50&#176;C, continuously                introducing carbon dioxide into solution for 10-15                minutes, continuously heating to boil, filtering                and titrating the filtrate to pH 5-5.5 with citric                acid, and mixing the obtained filtrate with the                filtrate obtained in the step (v), (vii)                concentrating and drying the filtrate obtained in                the step (vi) at 40-45&#176;C and 0.1-0.15                standard atmospheric pressure, and (viii) mixing                the volatile oil obtained in the step (iv) and the                dried product obtained in the step (vii), adding                ultrapure water, lecithin, betaine and ethanol, and                uniformly mixing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the                  specific operation of water vapor distillation                  method is: passing pure air into aqueous solution                  of mixture A for 10-20 minutes at                  70-80&#8451;, and extracting by using normal                  conventional steam distillation. In the step                  (iv), obtaining the alcohol solution by mixing                  ethanol and propanol according to a mass ratio of                  3:1-1.7.Preferred Components: The pure air is the                  air filtered by sterilized cotton yarn. The flow                  speed of the pure air is 2-4 m/second. In the                  step (iv), the power of the ultrasonic wave is                  800-900 W. In the step (iv), the dosage of the                  alcohol solution is 7-8 wt.% of the mixture. The                  dosage of the olive oil is 11-15 wt.% of the mass                  of mixture B. In the step (vi), the mass ratio of                  the filter residue and water is 1:15-18. The                  dosage of the calcium hydroxide is 10-15 wt.% of                  the solution. The flow rate of the carbon dioxide                  is 10-15 ml/minute. In the step (viii), the                  dosage of the ultrapure water is 1-1.3 times the                  mass of the volatile oil. The dosage of the                  lecithin is 8-13 wt.% of volatile oil. The dosage                  of betaine is 3-5 wt.% volatile oil. The dosage                  of the ethanol is 25-28 wt.% of volatile                  oil.

General compositions.); D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)

PN CN113637767-A
TI Use of mir-425-5p for regulating oocyte maturation, where marker molecule that regulates oocyte maturation is mir-425-5p.
AU TANG T
   YAO B
   KADILIYA J
AE UNIV NANJING MEDICAL (UYNM-C)
   PLA GEN HOSPITAL EASTERN THEATER COMMAND (PLAG-Non-standard)
GA 2021D7016H
AB    NOVELTY - Use of mir-425-5p is claimed for regulating oocyte maturation, where marker molecule that regulates oocyte maturation is mir-425-5p.
   USE - Mir-425-5p for regulating oocyte maturation (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a method for detecting expression level of mir-425-5p mimetic agent on oxidative stress-regulated oocyte maturation by collecting mature GV-stage oocytes to screen for oxidative stress condition, dividing selected GV-stage oocytes into 8 groups, giving it different concentrations of hydrogen peroxide and different culture durations for oxidative stress treatment, and screening it by observing PBE filter oxidative stress conditions; inducing oxidative stress by dividing selected GV stage naked eggs into two groups, transferring first group to milrinone + M2 droplets containing 25muM hydrogen peroxide and transferring second group to the control milrinone +M2 droplets, covering it with paraffin oil, placing it in an incubator with 5% carbon dioxide, temperature of 37degreesC and saturated humidity for 1.5 hours; extracting oocyte RNA by transferring GV-stage oocytes into a prepared EP tube containing 50muL of lysate, and storing it at -80degreesC, taking RNA and placing it in a 42degrees Celsius water bath for 10-30 minutes, taking 250muL of conditional buffer, adding it to purification column, leaving it at room temperature for 5 minutes, and centrifuging it at 16000g for 1 minute; taking 50muL of 70% ethanol and adding it to lysis solution, mixing it upside down, transferring it to treated purification column, centrifuging it at 100g for 2 minutes and at 16000g for 30 second, adding 100muL lotion 1 and centrifuging it at 8000g for 1 minute, adding 100muL lotion 2 and centrifuging it at 8000g for 1 minutes, adding 100muL lotion 2 and centrifuging it at 16000g for 2 minutes and then centrifuging it at 16000g for 1 minute; moving purification column into a new 0.5mL microcentrifuge tube, adding 11muL of NF water, standing it at room temperature for 2 minutes, centrifuging it at 1000g for 1 minute, centrifuging it at 16000g for 1 minute, and storing it at -80degreesC; detecting mir-425-5p content by using qRT-PCR technology and relative quantitative method, using U6 as internal reference gene to detect change in expression level of mir-425-5p in oocytes, where U6 has 22 nucleotide sequences ( SEQ ID NO: 3), which are caguacuuuuguguaguacaaa; and
   (2) a method for detecting expression level of mir-425-5p inhibitor on maturation of oocytes after oxidative stress by microinjecting mir-425-5p inhibitor by placing GV stage oocytes in the experimental group into droplets containing milrinone + M2 for microinjection; diluting Mir-425-5p inhibitor to a working concentration of 1muM, injecting 2.5pL of the mir-425-5p inhibitor working solution into each oocyte, injecting control group with inhibitor negative control having 22 nucleotide sequences (SEQ ID NO: 4), which are uuuuguacuacacaaaaguacug, transferring it to milrinone + M2 medium and culturing it for 20 hours to achieve complete inhibition; culturing oocyte by moving selected GV-stage naked eggs into milrinone+M2 droplets containing 25muM hydrogen peroxide, transferring control group into pure milrinone+M2 droplets, covering both experimental group and control group paraffin oil, incubating it for 1.5 hours in an incubator containing 5% carbon dioxide and saturated humidity at 37degreesC, taking and washing it with preheated M2 culture solution for 3 times, moving it into M2 droplets, and then putting it in the incubator; culturing oocytes for 3 hours to enter the GVBD phase, 7 hours to enter the MI phase, 9.5 hours to enter the AI-TI phase, 12-14 hours to exit the polar body, and entering the MII phase; taking out Petri dish after 3 hours of culture, placing it under an inverted microscope on a 37degrees Celsius temperature platform to observe and take pictures and counting GVBD rate, returning it to incubator to continue the culture, taking it out again after 13 hours, observing and taking pictures under an inverted microscope, and counting the PBE rate; observing S1-4 spindle morphology and chromosome arrangement by fixing oocytes of each stage in 4% PFA for 30 minutes, permeating 0.5% Triton X-100 for 20 minutes, washing it for 3 times with 1% BSA, closing it for 1 hour after 3-5 minutes each time, transferring it to 1:250 diluted alpha-tubulin and placing it at 4degreesC for more than 8 hours; washing it for 3 times with wash solution for 3-5 minutes each time, incubating secondary antibody at room temperature for 1 hour, washing it with wash solution for 3 times for 3-5 minutes each time, moving it to 1:500 diluted Hoechst solution to stain the nucleus for 10 minutes; making films and observing it with a laser confocal microscope to calculate ratio of abnormal spindles to chromosomes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The mir-425-5p as a marker molecule that promotes oocyte maturation is a miR-425-5p mimetic agent having 23 nucleotide sequences (SEQ ID NO: 1), which are aaugacacgaucacucccguuga. The mir-425-5p as a marker molecule that inhibits oocyte maturation is a miR-425-5p inhibitor having 23 nucleotide sequences (SEQ ID NO: 2), which are ucaacgggagugaucgucauu. The miR-425-5p mimetic agent prepares medicine for promoting oocyte maturation in an effective dosage of 100nMasterisk2.5pL. The miR-425-5p inhibitor prepares medicine for promoting oocyte maturation in an effective dosage of 1muMasterisk2.5pL.

PN CN112812156-A; CN112812156-B
TI Expression and purification of novel coronavirus COVID-19-S1 protein comprises e.g. selecting gene encoding coronavirus disease 2019-S1 protein, optimizing codon according to preference of 293F cells and measuring plasmid concentration.
AU YANG P
AE XIAMEN BAOTAI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XIAM-Non-standard)
   XIAMEN BIOTIME BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XIAM-Non-standard)
GA 2021586350
AB    NOVELTY - Expression and purification of the novel coronavirus COVID-19-S1 protein comprises e.g. (S1) selecting the gene encoding coronavirus disease 2019-S1 (COVID-19-S1) protein, optimizing the codon according to the preference of 293F cells, selecting the signal peptide, ligating the target gene with the selected signal peptide, introducing the Kozak region at the front end of the signal peptide, clone the optimized gene sequence into the pcDNA3.1-myc-HisA vector, constructing the expression plasmid, extracting the plasmid with a high-purity plasmid extraction kit, eluting with sterile water, and measuring the plasmid concentration with a spectrophotometer to be 1 mg/ml to obtain pcDNA3.1-myc-HisA-COVID-19-S1 The target DNA of the plasmid, (S2) taking 1 mu l target DNA of the pcDNA3.1-myc-HisA-COVID-19-S1 plasmid, transforming into Escherichia coli DH5 alpha competent strain, placing in ice bath for 30 minutes, performing heat shock treatment at 42 degrees C for 90 seconds.
   USE - The method is useful for expression and purification of the novel coronavirus COVID-19-S1 protein.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: recombines the signal peptide capable of highly secreting and expressing the protein in 293F cells with Kozak region and gene encoding human COVID19S1 protein; increases the expression and secretion of the target protein; solves the problems of low secretion and low purity of the novel coronavirus COVID19S1 protein, and provides a material basis for rapid immunological diagnosis, preparation of monoclonal antibodies, and research on analyzing protein structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Expression and purification of the novel coronavirus COVID-19-S1 protein comprises (S1) selecting the gene encoding COVID-19-S1 protein, optimizing the codon according to the preference of 293F cells, selecting the signal peptide, ligating the target gene with the selected signal peptide, introducing the Kozak region at the front end of the signal peptide, clone the optimized gene sequence into the pcDNA3.1 (RTM: Vector/plasmid map)-myc-HisA vector, constructing the expression plasmid, extracting the plasmid with a high-purity plasmid extraction kit, eluting with sterile water, and measuring the plasmid concentration with a spectrophotometer to be 1 mg/ml to obtain pcDNA3.1 (RTM: Vector/plasmid map)-myc-HisA-COVID-19-S1 The target DNA of the plasmid, (S2) taking 1 mu l target DNA of the pcDNA3.1 (RTM: Vector/plasmid map)-myc-HisA-COVID-19-S1 plasmid, transforming into Escherichia coli DH5 alpha competent strain, placing in ice bath for 30 minutes, performing heat shock treatment at 42 degrees C for 90 seconds, placing in ice bath for 5 minutes, adding 500 mu l Lysogeny broth culture medium, incubating for 1 hour on a shaker with a temperature of 37 degrees C and a rotating speed of 150 revolutions/minute, taking 30 mu l culture solution, spreading on the Lysogeny broth solid culture plate containing ampicillin antibiotics, incubating in an incubator at 37 degrees C for 16 hours, selecting single colony, adding to the Lysogeny broth liquid medium containing ampicillin, placed on a shaker with a temperature of 37 degrees C and a rotating speed of 220 revolutions/minute for 16 hours, taking 293F cells in good condition, placing in a culture flask, extracting with high purity plasmid small extract medium kit to obtain the target DNA of pcDNA3.1 (RTM: Vector/plasmid map)-myc-HisA-COVID-19-S1 plasmid, (S3) transfecting the target DNA of pcDNA3.1 (RTM: Vector/plasmid map)-myc-HisA-COVID-19-S1 plasmid into 293F cells by the poly ethyleneimine transfection method, placing in an incubator at 37 degrees C, 8% carbon dioxide and 120 revolutions/minute for 24 hours, supplementing with the same volume of OPM-293CD05 fresh medium as the culture medium, adding 1% by volume of OPM-293ProFeed feed medium every day when the cells grow to a density of 4x 106 cells/ml, adding back in the 37 degrees C, 8% carbon dioxide, 120 revolutions/minute incubator, continuously culturing for 96 hours to obtain 293F cell culture medium expressing COVID-19-S1 protein, (S4) centrifuging the 293F cell culture medium expressing COVID-19-S1 protein obtained in step S3 at 1500 revolutions/minute for 15 minutes, collecting the supernatant, filtering with a 0.45 mu m filter membrane, selecting a nickel column for protein purification, before using the nickel column, using 50 times the column volume containing 100 mM Tris pH 7.5 hydrochloric acid and 300 mM sodium chloride, equilibrating the column with 10 mM imidazole equilibration buffer, and then flowing the supernatant obtained by centrifugation through the column, eluting with 20 column volumes of eluate containing imidazole concentrations of 0 mM, 50 mM, 150 mM, and 300 mM, collecting eluates with different imidazole concentrations, where the 150mM imidazole eluate contains the target protein COVID-19-S1 protein, and dialyzing the purified COVID-19-S1 protein to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: In step (S1), the signal peptide is TPA signal peptide. The coding TPA signal peptide comprises fully defined sequence of 66 base pairs (SEQ ID NO: 1) as given in the specification. The Kozak region comprises a sequence of 5'-gccaccatgg-3' (SEQ ID NO: 2). The COVID-19-S1 protein encoded is the novel coronavirus spike protein S1 with a size of about 120kDa, and the gene sequence information encoding the novel coronavirus spike protein S1 comprises fully defined sequence of 2010 base pairs (SEQ ID NO: 1) as given in the specification. In the step (S4), the size of the dialysis bag is 10 KDa. The temperature is 4 degrees C. The dialysis time is 12 hours. The dialysis buffer is 20mM phosphate-buffer saline. The pH is 7.4. In the step (1), the protein sequence is the 16-685 amino acid sequence of the novel coronavirus spike protein S1. The signal peptide analysis method is a Western Blot detection method. Preferred Method: In the step (S3), The steps of using the the poly ethyleneimine transfection method to transfect the COVID-19-S1 target DNA includes dissolving DNA and the poly ethyleneimine respectively in opti- minimum essential medium (MEM), where the ratio of the volume of DNA or the poly ethyleneimine to medium is 1:20, mixing, slowly dropping the poly ethyleneimine (PEI) solution into the DNA solution, mixing well, allowing to stand at room temperature for 30 minutes, and then slowly dropping into the 293F cell solution. The process of selecting signal peptides includes (1) selecting novel coronavirus spike protein S1 as the protein sequence, and optimizing the gene sequence encoding the protein for the 293F cell host, where the optimized gene sequence is comprises fully defined sequence of 2088 base pairs (SEQ ID NO: 4) as given in the specification, then constructing the gene sequence into the pcDNA3.1 (RTM: Vector/plasmid map)-myc-HisA vector to obtain the target gene vector, (2) selecting the signal peptide that can effectively secrete and expressing the foreign target gene from the reported literature as the signal peptide to be selected, ligating the signal peptides to be selected with the target gene vector obtained in step 1), during ligating, before the signal peptide to be selected, two connection methods i.e. introducing Kozak region and not introducing Kozak region are selected respectively and obtaining recombinant protein, and (3) using a signal peptide analysis tool to analyze the amino acid sequence of the recombinant protein, selecting the signal peptide whose cleavage site analysis is accurate and falls before the protein sequence, which is a signal peptide suitable for the expression of the novel coronavirus spike protein.

PN CN111974397-A
TI Thermal-electric coupled bulk water oxidation catalyst with variable valence transition metal ions for energy systems of oxygen evolution such as electrolysis of water, is prepared by applying conductive substrate layer to prepare electrode.
AU YAN S
   LIU D
   YANG Y
   ZHU H
   QIN H
   ZHANG W
   ZOU Z
AE UNIV NANJING (UNAJ-C)
GA 2020B93830
AB    NOVELTY - Thermal-electric coupled bulk water oxidation catalyst with variable valence transition metal ions is prepared by applying a conductive substrate layer to prepare an electrode, where the conductive base layer is made of three-dimensional foam metal (nickel, copper, titanium, iron) and carbon paper, which provide a larger contact area between the catalyst and the electrolyte, where is thermal-electric coupling bulk water oxidation catalyst is a bulk catalytic material whose transition metal ions are used as catalytic active centers for catalytic reactions, catalytic material with high specific surface area, low crystallinity or flexible structure containing nickel (2+) ion, ferrous ion, cobalt (2+) ion and manganese (2+) ion variable valence transition metal ions prepared by electrodeposition or hydrothermal method, and bulk electrocatalyst is coupled to utilize power waste heat or industrial low-grade waste heat in electrocatalytic process to improve energy utilization.
   USE - The thermal-electric coupled bulk water oxidation catalyst is used for new energy systems of oxygen evolution reaction such as electrolysis of water, electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction, electrocatalytic nitrogen reduction and metal-air battery, preferably alkaline electrolyte (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The bulk electrocatalyst utilizes power waste heat or industrial low-grade waste heat in the electrocatalytic process to improve energy utilization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing transition metal hydroxide M(OH)2 catalyst, which involves (i) ultrasonically cleaning conductive substrate with acid, deionized water and ethanol as the working electrode to be deposited, (ii) taking metal nitrate and dissolving in deionized water to obtain a electroplating solution for electrodeposition, preparing the electroplating solution and placing in the electrolytic cell for electrodeposition, (iii) placing the working electrode to be deposited obtained in step (i) in the electroplating solution obtained in step (ii) under continuous magnetic stirring at a constant value of -1.4 to -1 V (relative to the reversible hydrogen electrode), and carrying out electrochemical reduction deposition treatment at electric potential, electrodeposition temperature of 20-50 degrees C and pH of 5-6.5, (iv) after deposition, washing all the electrodes with ethanol and water respectively, and drying to obtain the thermal-electric coupling transition metal hydroxide catalyst with low crystallization, where the acid used to treat the substrate in step (i) is nitric acid, sulfuric acid, hydrochloric acid and hydrofluoric acid, which are selected according to the characteristics of the substrate, the metal nitrate in step (ii) is nickel nitrate, cobalt nitrate, iron nitrate or manganese nitrate, the amount of metal nitrate solution is 0.5-6 mmol, the amount of deionized water is 50-300 ml, and in step (iii), the electrodeposition is carried out at electrodeposition potential of -1.4 to -1 V, electrodeposition charge of 0.5-5 C and treatment environment of dry environment at 50-80 degrees C for 1-5 hours;
   (2) a method for preparing an organic framework catalytic material containing transition metal hydroxide M(OH)2, which involves (i) ultrasonically cleaning the conductive substrate (foamed nickel, carbon paper) with acid, deionized water and ethanol, and placing in a PTFE reactor, (ii) taking transition metal nitrate and terephthalic acid and dissolving in deionized water and N,N-dimethylformamide respectively, (iii) pouring the transition metal nitrate solution and terephthalic acid solution in step (ii) to a reaction kettle, and reacting at 150-200 degrees C to obtain a metal-organic framework supported nickel foam or carbon paper electrode, washing with ionized water and ethanol and drying, and (iv) applying a voltage of 1.5-1.9 V to the electrode obtained in step (iii) and oxidizing for 5-10 hours, and electro-oxidizing to a stable current to obtain a transition metal hydroxide organic frame structure electrode, where the acid used for treating the substrate in step (i) is nitric acid, sulfuric acid, hydrochloric acid and hydrofluoric acid, which is selected according to the characteristics of the substrate, the amount of the metal nitrate solution in step (ii) is 0.5-6 mmol, the amount of deionized water is 50-300 ml, the amount of metal nitrate in step (ii) is 0.5-6 mmol, the amount of terephthalic acid is 2-10 mmol, the amount of deionized water is 20-60 ml, and the amount of N,N-dimethylformamide is 10-70 ml, and the reaction in step (iii) is carried out for 10-36 hours; and
   (3) the use of the thermal-electric coupling transition metal water oxidation catalyst for new energy systems of oxygen evolution reaction such as electrolysis of water, electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction, electrocatalytic nitrogen reduction and metal-air battery, preferably suitable for alkaline electrolyte, where the temperature of electrolyte is 20-95 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is a low-crystallized transition metal hydroxide ultra-thin nanosheet or transition metal hydroxide organic framework structure material with weak lattice atom binding force, and has a high specific surface area and porosity, preferably a variable valence transition metal hydroxide M(OH)2 and organic framework materials containing M(OH)2, where M is a single ion such as nickel (2+) ion, ferrous ion, cobalt (2+) ion, manganese (2+) ion or a mixture of multiple ions. The bulk catalyst is utilized as an electrocatalytic layer supported on any conductive substrate to prepare electrode devices, preferably foamed metals (nickel, copper, titanium, iron), carbon paper, carbon cloth and other materials with high specific surface area and porosity, where the thickness of the substrate is 1-2 mm, the porosity is 20-99%, the thickness of the carbon paper or carbon cloth is 0.1-0.3 mm, and the porosity is 20-99%. Preferred Conditions: The bulk electrocatalyst is coupled to utilize power waste heat or industrial low-grade waste heat in the electrocatalytic process, and the coupled thermal energy is configured to simultaneously promote the transition metal ion oxidation kinetics and oxygen evolution reaction kinetics.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following: (1) a method (M1) of preparing a potential natural bio-matrix from A.vera involves (a) taking a thick leaf of A.vera, (b) sterilizing the leaf in a solution having copper sulfate solution and 70% ethanol, (c) processing further leaf again under biosafety cabinet class 2, (d) taking gel from the leaf and processing in sterilized (autoclaved) mortar and pestle, and (e) suspending solution from gel in 50 ml falcon tubes and after filtering the gel through muslin cloth to obtain clearer solution; and (2) a method (M2) for culturing mammalian cells using A.vera involves (1) (a) culturing and sub-culturing secondary cell line, where HeLa cells are maintained in T-25 surfaces in appropriate complete media and cells were regularly observed for any contamination and media change was given at regular intervals and sub-culturing was done when the surface reached confluency (80%), (b) preparing bio matrix from A.vera, (c) coating 6-well plate with A.vera comprising 1-2 ml suspension of A.vera solution i.e. poured in respective wells to ensure that it spreads evenly over entire surface and there is no bubble formation and solution was taken out and discarded in waste beaker after 1-2 minutes, and coatings were dried for 2 hours in the biosafety cabinet class 2 and the cells were counted using modified Neubauer's chamber and a specific volume of cell suspension was pipetted onto the coated surface and cell culture was periodically observed, (d) using trypan blue exclusion for counting HeLa Cells, where the surface of cells was trypsinized and cell suspension was prepared in 50 ml falcon tube placed in 5% carbon dioxide incubator till further processing and a working solution of trypan blue in phosphate buffer saline (PBS) (0.4%) and cell suspension for counting in PBS and trypan blue were prepared and loaded the modified Neubauer's chamber with cell suspension made for cell counting, (e) transfering secondary cell line (HeLa) in the naturally made bio-matrix where cells transfer in bio-matrix was performed by cell suspension i.e. loaded on the coating of bio-matrix and it provides 2-dimensional growth, and (f) entrapping a specific volume of cell suspension in a certain volume of A.vera gel suspension, where the mixed suspension of cells with biomatrix was pipette into the culture plates and cell culture was periodically observed, or (2) (a) culturing and sub-culturing secondary cell line, where HeLa cells were maintained in T-25 surfaces in appropriate complete media and cells were regularly observed for any contamination under Olympus 1X73 inverted phase contrast fluorescence microscope and media change was given at regular intervals and sub-culturing was done, when the surface reached confluency (80%), (b) preparing bio matrix from A.vera, (c) coating 6-well plate with A.vera comprising 1-2 ml suspension of A.vera solution i.e. poured in respective wells to ensure that it spreads evenly over entire surface and there is no bubble formation and solution was taken out and discarded in waste beaker after 1-2 minutes, thus coatings were dried for 2 hours in the biosafety cabinet class 2 and the cells were counted using modified Neubauer's chamber and a specific volume of cell suspension was pipetted onto the coated surface and cell culture was periodically observed, (d) using trypan blue exclusion for counting HeLa cells, where surface of cells was trypsinized and cell suspension was prepared in 50 ml falcon tube placed in 5% carbon dioxide incubator till further processing and a working solution of trypan blue in PBS (0.4%) and cell suspension for counting in PBS and trypan blue were prepared and loaded the modified Neubauer's chamber with cell suspension made for cell counting, (e) transfering secondary cell line (HeLa) in the naturally made bio-matrix, where cells transfer in bio-matrix was done in which cells are entrapped within the bio-matrix of A.vera, and it initiates 3-dimensional growth in the form of spheroid formation and stacking, (f) entrapping specific volume of cell suspension in a certain volume of A.vera gel suspension, where the mixed suspension of cells with biomatrix was pipette into the culture plates and cell culture was periodically observed.

TI Carbon dioxide online monitoring device for industrial production waste gas used in industrial production, has infrared carbon dioxide sensor connected with computer through data line, and air guide shell provided with infrared carbon dioxide sensor below mesh plate.
AU ZHU X
AE ZHENJIANG DINGHONG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION TECHNOLOGY CO LT (ZHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2022148043
AB    NOVELTY - The device has an air inlet pipe (1) fixed on a top side of an air guide shell (2). A three-way valve (5) is fixed at a bottom side of the air guide shell. Three connecting holes of the three way valve are connected with the air guide shell through a connecting pipe (20) and adsorption main bodies (18). The air guide shell is provided with a mesh plate (3). The air guide shell is provided with a first infrared carbon dioxide sensor (4) below the mesh plate. The first infrared carbon dioxide sensor is connected with a computer (13) through a data line. A side wall of an absorbing main body (18) is provided with a sealing plate (15). The sealing plate is matched with a sealing groove.
   USE - Carbon dioxide online monitoring device for industrial production waste gas used in industrial production.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has rational structure, convenient combination and installation, convenient online real-time monitoring, convenient absorption, and high detection precision. The device detects carbon dioxide concentration uniformly dispersed in the carbon dioxide waste gas, so as to improve the detection precision of the device. The programmable logic controller makes the alarm work to alarm, so that the working staff of high concentration of carbon dioxide is checked, thus ensuring the quality of the industrial production product. The gas containing carbon dioxide enters the inside of one of the absorption bodies along the three-way valve and the first valve, and enters the inside of the air guide plate on the expansion tube along the air hole on the piston, and it is blown out from dispersing pipes and dispersed into the interior of Sodium hydroxide, which facilitates the absorption of carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for using a carbon dioxide online monitoring device for industrial production waste gas used in industrial production.

AU KIM Y P
AE ARI IND CO LTD (ARII-Non-standard)
GA 2019506106
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves recognizing the state of a solar module array (3) through the current sensor and the voltage sensor to determine whether the cause of the failure is to interfere with the power generation amount. A control unit is configured to generate an alarm signal and inform the manager of the generated alarm signal or perform automatic cleaning if the power generation amount is lowered so as to maximize power generation. The state that does not adversely affect the growth of crops is determined based on the amount and the light amount of carbon dioxide emitted from the crops and is stored in the database. The amount of carbon dioxide recognized by the control unit and the illuminance recognized through the internal illuminance sensor is determined based on the state in which photosynthesis of the crop is actively performed. The solar cell module is opened at an angle equal to an incident angle of light when the crop is grown.
   USE - Method for controlling solar sharing for solar cells.

The electricity is produced by the solar power generation at the same time as the harvest of the crops by making the photovoltaic power generation through the optical module array and the maximum effect is obtained without damaging the agricultural land.

PN US2018343901-A1; US10517320-B2
TI Producing concentrated volatiles from Cannabis involves providing source of water, and passing water through water treatment unit to form treated water, where water treatment unit cation, anion, membrane, filter, adsorbent and absorbent.
AU LEO D M
   FREITAS S R
AE INSECTERGY LLC (INSE-Non-standard)
   LEO D M (LEOD-Individual)
   FREITAS S R (FREI-Individual)
GA 2018967199
AB    NOVELTY - Producing concentrated volatiles from Cannabis involves providing a source of water, and passing the water through a water treatment unit to form treated water, where the water treatment unit cation, anion, a membrane, a filter, activated carbon, an adsorbent and an absorbent. The treated water is mixed with a macro-nutrient, a micro-nutrient, and a pH adjustment solution to form a liquid mixture. The pH of liquid mixture is 5.15-6.75. The liquid mixture is pressurized to form a pressurized liquid mixture. The pressurized liquid mixture is transferred to multiple growing assemblies. The multiple growing assemblies are illuminated with multiple lights. The Cannabis is grown in multiple growing assemblies, and then harvested and ground to obtain Cannabis powder. The volatiles are extracted from Cannabis powder with solvent. A portion of the volatiles is separated from the solvent and volatiles mixture.
   USE - Method for producing concentrated volatiles from Cannabis (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce concentrated volatiles from Cannabis in an easy and cost-effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The volatiles are mixed with another solvent to form another volatiles and solvent mixture. The latter volatiles and solvent mixture are cooled, filtered. The portion of the second solvent is evaporated from the latter volatiles and solvent mixture to create concentrated volatiles. The macro-nutrient is selected from nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, calcium, magnesium, sulfur, iron, manganese, boron, molybdenum, copper, zinc, sodium, chlorine, or silicon. The pH adjustment solution is nitric acid, phosphoric acid, potassium hydroxide, sulfuric acid, an organic acid, citric acid, or acetic acid. The former solvent selected from acetone, alcohol, butane, carbon dioxide, ethanol, gas, gaseous carbon dioxide, hexane, isobutane, isopropanol, liquid carbon dioxide, liquid, naphtha, pentane, propane, refrigerant gas, subcritical carbon dioxide, supercritical carbon dioxide, or vapor. The latter solvent is selected from liquid, acetone, alcohol, oil, or ethanol.
