TI Generation of producer gas from coal involves using heated blast in oxidation zone to heat coal, passing blast to reduction zone to produce carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and reacting heated coal with hydrogen and further with oxygen.
AU SAHU D
   SUBRAMANIAN M G
AE BHARAT HEAVY ELECTRICALS LTD (BHAR-C)
GA 201814683M
AB    NOVELTY - Generation of producer gas from coal involves preheating blast in an ash zone (1), introducing the blast to an oxidation zone (2), heating a layer of coal using the heated blast, introducing the blast into a reduction zone (3) to produce carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen, forming carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide gas in presence of coal, transferring excess carbon with air to form carbon monoxide, gasifying steam in blast to form hydrogen, treating coal with hydrogen to produce methane, decomposing steam into hydrogen and oxygen, and reacting hydrogen and oxygen with coal.
   USE - Generation of producer gas from coal.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently produces producer gas with higher calorific value, since hydrogen occurs in the form of free hydrogen and linked hydrogen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Generation of producer gas from coal involves injecting a blast into an ash zone for preheating, introducing the preheated blast to an oxidation zone, heating a layer of coal to 1000 degrees C by using the heat liberated from the blast, introducing the blast into a reduction zone in which carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen are produced, forming carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide gas inside a generator in the presence of excess coal at 1000 degrees C or more, transferring excess carbon with air at low temperature to form carbon monoxide at high temperature, gasifying steam in blast to form carbon monoxide and hydrogen gas, treating the heated coal with reactive hydrogen to produce methane, decomposing steam into hydrogen and oxygen at a higher temperature of 1000 degrees C, reacting hydrogen and oxygen with coal to form carbon monoxide and hydrogen, and distributing the granulated ash to make even distribution of air in the producer gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining the gasification process with improved parameters.
   Ash zone (1)
   Oxidation zone (2)
   Reduction zone (3)
   Drying zone (4)
   Producer gas outlet (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The blast used is a combination of air and steam mixture. Preferred Condition: The temperature of the oxidation zone is 1000-1200 degrees C. Preferred Process: The steam generates carbon monoxide and hydrogen by exothermic reaction.

TI Removing gas from circulatory system of subject involves delivering microbubbles containing first gas into existing gastrointestinal body cavity of subject, and introducing source of oxygen and/or scavenger or carbon dioxide into cavity.
AU SCRIBNER R M
AE RESPIROGEN INC (RESP-Non-standard)
GA 202221927A
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves delivering a set of micro bubbles containing a gas into an existing gastrointestinal body cavity of a subject (102), where the gas has a percentage by volume. A gas collecting device has a carbon dioxide collecting device that is inserted into the micro bubble located within the body cavity (104). The device has a gas-permeable outer layer and a material capable of absorbing the selected gas. The gas located within a circulatory system of the subject diffuses through a tissue wall of the gastrointestinal body through the micro bubbles. The layer is absorbed by the material.

It may help subjects survive avian flu, severe acute respiratory syndrome (SARS), and COVID. It reduces thrombogenic and immunogenic effects owing to lack of direct contact with blood, reduced risk of embolism by the OMBs or their lipid remnants because they are not in circulation, or prevention of blood acidosis due to excessive levels of carbon

AB    NOVELTY - Producing synthesis gas, comprises: (a)                reacting a hydrocarbon feed stream (4) together                with an oxidant gas stream, thus producing a first                synthesis gas stream in a reforming reactor                comprising a first catalyst (11); (b) providing a                heated carbon dioxide rich gas stream (2) to an                adiabatic post converter (20) comprising a second                catalyst active for catalyzing steam methane                reforming, methanation and reverse water gas shift                reactions; and (c) letting at least a part of the                first synthesis gas stream and the heated carbon                dioxide rich gas stream undergo steam methane                reforming, methanation and reverse water gas shift                reactions to thus provide a product gas stream in                the adiabatic post converter, where the product gas                stream is a synthesis gas stream.
   USE - The process is useful for producing synthesis                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The process provides reformed stream with                relatively low hydrogen molecule/carbon monoxide                ratio.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                system (100) for producing synthesis gas,                comprising reforming reactor comprising first                catalyst and is arranged to at least partially                react a hydrocarbon feed together with an oxidant                gas stream thus producing a first synthesis gas                stream, adiabatic post converter comprising second                catalyst active for catalyzing steam methane                reforming, methanation and reverse water gas shift                reactions, conduit for leading at least part of the                first synthesis gas stream into the adiabatic post                converter, means for adding a heated carbon dioxide                rich gas stream to the part of the first synthesis                gas stream upstream or in the adiabatic post                converter, and an outlet from the adiabatic post                converter for letting out a product gas stream,                where the product gas stream is a synthesis gas                stream.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The product gas stream is                  a synthesis gas stream with an hydrogen                  molecule/carbon monoxide ratio below 1.8. The                  part of the first synthesis gas stream and the                  heated carbon dioxide rich gas stream are                  combined to a combined gas stream upstream the                  adiabatic post converter. The heated carbon                  dioxide rich gas stream has a temperature of                  500-1100 degrees C prior to combination with the                  part of first synthesis gas stream and/or prior                  to being inlet into the adiabatic post converter.                  The process further comprises: the step of                  heating a carbon dioxide rich gas stream to form                  the heated carbon dioxide rich gas stream in a                  fired heater; the step of heating a carbon                  dioxide rich gas stream to form the heated carbon                  dioxide rich gas stream in an electrically heated                  heater; the step of heating a carbon dioxide rich                  gas stream to form the heated carbon dioxide rich                  gas stream by heat exchange with super heated                  steam; and the step of heating a carbon dioxide                  rich gas stream to the heated carbon dioxide rich                  gas stream by heat exchange with at least part of                  the product gas stream exiting the adiabatic post                  converter. The mole ratio between carbon dioxide                  in the heated carbon dioxide rich gas stream and                  hydrocarbons in the hydrocarbon feed stream is                  larger than 0.1. The hydrocarbon feed stream                  comprises steam and the sulfur/carbon ratio in                  the hydrocarbon feed stream is 0.2-2. The amount                  of steam, oxygen and carbon dioxide led into the                  reforming reactor and/or added to the part of the                  first synthesis gas stream upstream or in the                  adiabatic post converter is adjusted to provide a                  predetermined hydrogen molecule/carbon monoxide                  ratio of the product gas stream. The amount and                  temperature of the heated carbon dioxide rich gas                  stream are adjusted to ensure that the                  temperature of the product gas stream is at least                  800 degrees C. Preferred Components: The                  reforming reactor is an auto-thermal reforming                  reactor or a steam methane reforming reactor. The                  second catalyst is a steam reforming catalyst.                  The system further comprises: a fired heater,                  where the means for adding a heated carbon                  dioxide rich gas stream comprises means for                  heating a carbon dioxide rich gas stream by heat                  exchange within the fired heater; an electrically                  heated heater, where the means for adding a                  heated carbon dioxide rich gas stream comprises a                  means for heating a carbon dioxide rich gas                  stream by heat exchange within the electrically                  heated heater; a second heat exchange unit                  allowing heating a carbon dioxide rich gas stream                  by heat exchange with superheated steam; and a                  third heat exchange unit allowing heating a                  carbon dioxide rich gas stream to the heated                  carbon dioxide rich gas stream by heat exchange                  with at least part of the product gas stream                  exiting the adiabatic post converter.

TI Separation of hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide            from gaseous stream for e.g. on-shore application,            involves absorbing contaminants from gas stream into            solvent at different reaction times, and removing            contaminant-containing stream.
AU FREEMAN S A

GA 201821163Y
AB    NOVELTY - Separation of hydrogen sulfide and carbon                dioxide from gaseous stream, involves mixing                gaseous stream with a solvent which reacts with                contaminants (c1) and (c2) at reaction times (t1)                and (t2) respectively, in compact contacting unit                (308), allowing absorption of contaminants into                solvent for a residence time period, and removing                stream comprising the contaminant (c1) such the                concentration of the contaminant is reduced in                remaining stream. The reaction time (t1) is less                than the residence time period, and the reaction                time (t2) is greater than the residence time                period.
   USE - Separation of hydrogen sulfide and carbon                dioxide from gaseous stream. Uses include but are                not limited to onshore applications, remote onshore                applications, topsides facilities on offshore and                floating applications, and subsea processing                facilities.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently separates contaminants                from gaseous stream with high selectivity and                reduced hydrocarbon loss, and can be carried out in                small-sized equipment which requires lower capital                investment and operating expenses.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separation of hydrogen sulfide and carbon                dioxide from gaseous stream, involves passing a                gaseous stream to a compact contacting unit,                forming a mixed stream by mixing the gaseous stream                with a selective solvent which reacts with                contaminants (c1) and (c2) at reaction times (t1)                and (t2) respectively, allowing absorption of                contaminants into solvent for a residence time                period, and removing stream having a portion of the                contaminant (c1). The remaining mixed stream has a                lower concentration of the contaminant (c1) than                the initial mixed stream. The reaction time (t1) is                less than the residence time period, and the                reaction time (t2) is greater than the residence                time period. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                system (300) for separating hydrogen sulfide and                carbon dioxide from gaseous stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of                selective removal system.
   Selective removal system (300)
   Inlet separator (304)
   Compact contacting technology system as                compact contacting unit (308)
   Mixer (310)
   Storage unit (320)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The selective solvent                  is a tertiary amine which is                  methyldiethanolamine. The amine is formulated or                  sterically-hindered.

AB    NOVELTY - Device for breathing apparatus testing contains the respiration simulator 1, the carbon dioxide and nitrogen supply unit 2, the oxygen consumption simulating unit 3 and the control unit 4. The respiration simulator 1 contains the circulation contour, formed by the self-contained breathing apparatus (SCBA) 5, the flow stimulator (breathing simulator) 6, the GDM flow heater with the water evaporator (not shown) 7, the gas analyzer - the oxygen meter at inspiration 8, the flow meter 9 and the controlled valve 10, connected pneumatically in series with the input of the SCBA 5. The second half of the circulation contour is formed by the installed at the outlet SCBA 5, controlled by the valve 11, the gas analyzer - the oxygen meter at exhalation 12, the fridge 13 and check valve 14, connected to the flow stimulator (breathing simulator) 6. The carbon dioxide and nitrogen supply unit 2 comprises the control valve 15, the carbon dioxide flow meter 16, the check valve 17, the control valve 18 and the nitrogen flow meter 19. The carbon dioxide and nitrogen supply unit 2 through the check valve 17 is connected to the flow stimulator 6 through the check valve 17. The oxygen consumption simulation unit 3 includes the GDM discharge pump 20, the controlled valve 21, the controlled valve 22, the controlled valve 23, the GDM flow meter 24, and the controlled valve 25, connected to the atmosphere. The oxygen consumption simulating unit 3 is connected to the flow stimulator 6 input through the controlled valve 21.
   USE - Testing equipment.
   ADVANTAGE - Creation of the device, that provides the ability to simulate the device operation at various psycho-physiological person states by dropping the part of the breathing mixtures into the environment, reproduction of human respiratory dynamics and opportunities of infinitely variable depth of breathing during the operation, provision the ability to reproduce the various loads at tests performing.

TI Preparing magnetic controllable three-dimensional            porous material comprises e.g. preparing nickel            ion-iron(3+)-layered double hydroxide and            three-dimensional iron(III) oxide-cadmium oxide            heterostructure.
AU MA X
   DANG R
   ZHANG Y
   MA Y
   LI X
   YANG F
   LI H
AE UNIV YULIN (UNYL-C)
GA 201992764F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing magnetic controllable                three-dimensional porous material, comprises e.g.                (i) preparing nickel ion-iron(3+)-layered double                hydroxide, (ii) preparing three-dimensional                iron(III) oxide-cadmium oxide heterostructure,                (iii) removing cadmium carbonate heterophase in                iron(III) oxide-cadmium ion-nickel ion-carbon                dioxide-layered double hydroxide, (iv) regulation                of maghemite in iron(III) oxide-cadmium ion-nickel                ion-carbon dioxide-layered double hydroxide number                of isomers and size of the pores, (v) taking                organic dye (methyl orange) solution in three                quartz tubes, then adding nickel                ion-iron(3+)-layered double hydroxide and iron(III)                oxide-cadmium ion-nickel ion-carbon dioxide-layered                double hydroxide and porous iron(III) oxide-cadmium                ion-nickel ion-carbon dioxide-layered double                hydroxide as catalysts into quartz tube, placing                quartz tube under 300 W violet lamp, stirring,                degrading, filtering, then taking supernatant and                measuring absorbance on ultraviolet analyzer.
   USE - The magnetic controllable three-dimensional                porous material is useful as a magnetic                photocatalyst in the field of treating organic dye                wastewater (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product: has low forbidden band width,                excellent magnetic property, excellent adsorption                and photocatalytic activity; and is potential                recycling value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing magnetic controllable                three-dimensional porous material comprises (i)                taking 8.0220 g nickel(II) chloride hexahydrate,                3.0408 g ferric chloride hexahydrate and then                disposing into 500 ml mixed solution with total ion                concentration of 0.090 mol/l, measuring 100 ml                mixture in a high-pressure reaction vessel of                polytetrafluoroethylene, then adding 0.0283 g                sodium fluoride into polytetrafluoroethylene                autoclave for 5 minutes, then solution is uniform,                heat treating the reaction kettle in an environment                of 150&#176; C for 48 hours, washing the                resulting precipitate, filtering and drying at room                temperature to obtain nickel ion-iron(3+)-layered                double hydroxide (Ni2+-Fe3+-CO32-LDHs), where the                ratio between nickel and iron (n(Ni): n(Fe)) is                3:1, (ii) adding 5.1379 g cadmium dichloride                dihydrate (CdCl2.2/2H2O), 6.0815 g ferric chloride                hexahydrate (FeC13.6H20) into 500 ml mixed solution                with a total ion concentration of 0.090 mol/l,                measuring 100 ml mixture in a high-pressure                reaction vessel of polytetrafluoroethylene, then                adding 0.0567 g sodium fluoride into a                polytetrafluoroethylene autoclave for 5 minutes,                heat treating the reaction kettle in a                150&#176; C environment for 48 hours, washing                the resulting precipitate, filtering and drying at                room temperature to obtain three-dimensional                iron(III) oxide-cadmium oxide (&#945;                -Fe2O3-CdCO3) heterostructure, (iii) adding 0.2569                g CdCl2.2/2H2O, 1.3370 g nickel(II) chloride                hexahydrate, 0.6082 g iron(III) chloride                hexahydrate, and 5.9459 g urea into a mixed                solution having a total ion concentration of                0.090mol/l in 100ml polytetrafluoroethylene                autoclave, then adding 0.0378 g sodium fluoride in                the first group and 0.0057 g sodium fluoride in the                second group into the polytetrafluoroethylene                autoclave for 5 minutes, after the solution is                even, heat treating the reaction kettle in a                150&#176; C environment for 48 hours, washing                the resulting precipitate, filtering and drying at                room temperature to obtain final product, where the                molar ratio between cadmium, nickel and iron                (n(Cd):n(Ni):n(Fe)) in 0.3425 g CdCl2.2/2H2O,                1.0696 g nickel(II) chloride hexahydrate, 0.8109 g                iron (III) chloride hexahydrate and 5.9459 g urea                second group is 1:3:2, and the molar ratio between                cadmium, nickel and iron (n(Cd):n(Ni):n(Fe)) in                first group is 1:3:2, (iv) adding 1.1418 g                CdCl2.2/2H2O, 7.1307 g nickel(II) chloride                hexahydrate, and 2.7029 g ferric chloride                hexahydrate into a mixed solution with a total ion                concentration of 0.090 mol/l, adding 100 ml mixture                into a polytetrafluoroethylene autoclave, and then                carrying out ultrasonication for 5 minutes by                adding 0.0252 g sodium fluoride to a                polytetrafluoroethylene high pressure reactor,                after the solution is even, heat treating the                reaction kettle in a 150&#176; C environment                for 48 hour, washing the resulting precipitate,                filtering and drying at room temperature to obtain                final product, and preparing samples at                concentrations of 0.030 mol/l, 0.045 mol/l, 0.135                mol/l, 0.180 mol/l, and 0.225 mol/l, and (v) taking                10 ml 25 mg/l organic dye (methyl orange) solution                in three quartz tubes each time, then adding nickel                ion-iron(3+)-layered double hydroxide                (Ni2+-Fe3+-CO32-LDHs) and iron(III) oxide-cadmium                ion-nickel ion-carbon dioxide-layered double                hydroxide (&#945;                -Fe2O3-Cd2+-Ni2+-Fe3+-CO32-LDHs) and porous                iron(III) oxide-cadmium ion-nickel ion-carbon                dioxide-layered double hydroxide (&#945;                -Fe2O3-Cd2+-Ni2+-Fe3+-CO32-LDHs) as catalysts into                quartz tube, adding into the ultrasound system for                1-2 minutes to make the catalyst powder evenly                dispersed, placing quartz tube under a 300 W violet                lamp and continuously stirring for photocatalytic                degradation experiments, degrading for certain                period of time, filtering the solution through a                needle filter, then taking supernatant and                measuring absorbance on ultraviolet analyzer, where                the degradation rate of methylene blue was                calculated by absorbance: &#951; % is                (A0-At)/A0x 100%, where A0and Atare the absorbance                of the solution at the start and reaction time                t.

PT P
PN US2018354807-A1; WO2018227081-A1; US10913658-B2
TI Sequestration material removing carbon dioxide            from chemical process stream e.g. exhaust stream of            combustion system, comprises particles comprising            lithium borate, each particle has maximum            cross-sectional dimension and particle volume.
AU HARADA T
   HATTON T A
AE MASSACHUSETTS INST TECHNOLOGY (MASI-C)
   MASSACHUSETTS INST TECHNOLOGY (MASI-C)
   MASSACHUSETTS TECHNOLOGY INST (MASI-C)
GA 201898646W
AB    NOVELTY - Sequestration material (m1) comprises many                particles (100) comprising lithium borate, where                each particle of the particles has a maximum                cross-sectional dimension (102), each particle of                the particles has a particle volume, the particles                has a total particle volume defined by the total of                each of the individual particle volumes, and at                least 50% of the total particle volume is made up                of particles having maximum cross-sectional                dimensions of less than 100 mu .
   USE - The sequestration material is useful for:                removing carbon dioxide from a chemical process                stream (e.g. the exhaust stream of a combustion                system) and/or from an atmosphere containing carbon                dioxide (e.g. an atmosphere within a reactor or                other unit operation); capturing carbon dioxide;                and removing carbon dioxide from an exhaust stream                from a boiler (e.g. in a powered plant), from an                exhaust stream from an integrated gasification                combined cycle power plant, from an exhaust stream                from an internal combustion engine (e.g. from motor                vehicle), from an exhaust stream from a                pyro-processing furnace (e.g. as used in the cement                industry), and/or from a stream from a hydrogen                generation process (e.g. by sorption enhanced steam                reforming).
   ADVANTAGE - The sequestration material (m1): is capable of                interacting with carbon dioxide so that at least 1                mmole of carbon dioxide is sequestered per gram of                the sequestration material (claimed); captures a                relatively large amount of carbon dioxide per mass                of the sequestration material; is cycled repeatedly                while losing little if any ability to sequester                carbon dioxide; has high capacity; captures a                relatively large amount of carbon dioxide per mass                of the sequestration material; is highly                regenerable; and is cycled repeatedly while losing                little if any ability to sequester carbon dioxide,                thus the ability to repeatedly cycle the                sequestration material while maintaining the                ability of the sequestration material to sequester                carbon dioxide can drastically reduce the amount of                waste (e.g. poisoned or otherwise ineffective                sequestration material) produced by the                sequestration system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a sequestration material (m2) comprising                lithium borate and a salt comprising nitrate and/or                nitrite in combination with the lithium borate;                and
   (2) a sequestration material (m3) comprising                lithium borate, where the sequestration material is                capable of interacting with carbon dioxide so that                at least 1 mmole of carbon dioxide is sequestered                per gram of the sequestration material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of many                particles of sequestration material.
   Particles (100)
   Maximum cross-sectional dimension                (102)
   Boundary of particle (104)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sequestration                  material (m2) or (m3) is particulate, and                  comprises the particles comprising lithium                  borate. The sequestration material (m1) comprises                  lithium borate and a salt comprising nitrate                  and/or nitrite in combination with the lithium                  borate. The lithium borate comprises trilithium                  borate. The salt comprising the nitrate and/or                  the nitrite is a salt of an alkali metal and/or a                  salt of an alkaline earth metal. The salt                  comprising the nitrate and/or the nitrite is a                  nitrate and/or a nitrite of sodium, potassium,                  lithium, rubidium and/or cesium. The salt                  comprising the nitrate and/or the nitrite is a                  nitrate and/or a nitrite of sodium and/or                  potassium. In the sequestration material, at                  least 1 wt.% of the sequestration material is                  made up of the salt comprising the nitrate and/or                  the nitrite. In the sequestration material (m1),                  less than or equal to 30 wt.% of the                  sequestration material is made up of the salt                  comprising the nitrate and/or the nitrite. The                  salt comprising the nitrate and/or the nitrite                  comprises a nitrite. The salt comprising the                  nitrate and/or the nitrite comprises potassium                  nitrite and/or sodium nitrite.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN EP3818821-A1
TI Recycling carbon dioxide-containing gas stream, involves supplying carbon dioxide-containing gas stream originating from biological process to space in which vegetables, flowers, fruit, algae and/or plants are grown.
AU VERBEEK A J M
   VERBEEK L H M
AE BIOVERBEEK BV (BIOV-Non-standard)
GA 202150526K
AB    NOVELTY - Method for recycling carbon dioxide-containing gas stream involves supplying carbon dioxide-containing gas stream originating from a biological process to a space in which vegetables, flowers, fruit, algae and/or plants are grown.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling carbon dioxide-containing gas stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device, which comprises a unit for performing a biological process, where unit is provided for supplying air and unit for discharging a carbon dioxide-containing gas stream, where the unit for discharging a carbon dioxide-containing gas stream are connected with a unit for removing nitrogen constituents, the unit for removing nitrogen constituents is connected to a unit for removing smell constituents, and the unit for removing smell constituents is connected to a space for growing vegetables, flowers, fruit, algae and/or plants.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide-containing gas stream is supplied to a heat exchanger, in which the energy extracted from the carbon dioxide-containing gas stream is supplied to a space in which vegetables, flowers, fruit, algae and/or plants are grown. The carbon dioxide-containing gas stream is subjected to a step of removing nitrogen constituents before it is supplied to a space in which vegetables, flowers, fruit, algae and/or plants are grown, where the step of removing nitrogen constituents involves passing the carbon dioxide-containing gas stream through a scrubber, to which scrubber an aqueous acid stream is supplied. The carbon dioxide-containing gas stream is subjected to a step of removing smell constituents before it is supplied to a space in which vegetables, flowers, fruit, algae and/or plants are grown, where the step of removing smell constituents involves passing the aforementioned carbon dioxide-containing gas stream through an air-treatment unit, preferably a biological air-treatment unit, where the carbon dioxide-containing gas stream is passed through a separation unit, preferably a membrane unit, after the step of removing smell constituents, before it is supplied to a space in which vegetables, flowers, fruit, algae and/or plants are grown. The carbon dioxide-containing gas stream is first subjected to a step of removing nitrogen constituents, after the step of removing smell constituents is performed. The carbon dioxide-containing gas stream is supplied to a heat exchanger, in which the carbon dioxide-containing gas stream emerging from the heat exchanger is subjected to a step of removing nitrogen constituents, where the carbon dioxide-containing gas stream subjected to the step is subjected to a step of removing smell constituents, after which the resultant carbon dioxide-containing gas stream is supplied to a space in which vegetables, flowers, fruit, algae and/or plants are grown. The energy extracted from the carbon dioxide-containing gas stream in the heat exchanger is supplied to a space in which vegetables, flowers, fruit, algae and/or plants are grown. The nitrogen constituents obtained during the step of removing nitrogen constituents are supplied to the process from which the aforementioned carbon dioxide-containing gas stream originates. The biological process from which the carbon dioxide-containing gas stream originates is a composting process, where the composting process biomass and/or residual materials originating from growing mushrooms are composted, where an air stream is supplied to the biological process, where the carbon dioxide-containing gas stream is extracted from the biological process. The chemical composition of the carbon dioxide-containing gas stream originating from a biological process is measured before being supplied to a space in which vegetables, flowers, fruit, algae and/or plants are grown, where the chemical composition of one or more of gas streams, chosen from gas stream after the step of removing nitrogen constituents, gas stream after the step of removing smell constituents and gas stream from a separation unit, preferably a membrane unit, is measured. Preferred Device: The unit for performing a biological process is a composting unit. The unit for discharging a carbon dioxide-containing gas stream from the unit for performing a biological process are connected to a heat exchanger, where the carbon dioxide-containing gas stream is supplied to the unit for removing nitrogen constituents. The heat exchanger is connected to an object requiring heat, e.g. buildings, such as domestic dwellings, and/or to the space for growing vegetables, flowers, fruit, algae and/or plants. The unit for removing nitrogen constituents is provided with unit for supplying a wash liquid and unit for discharging nitrogen constituents containing effluent, where the unit for discharging nitrogen constituents containing effluent are connected to the unit for performing a biological process.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)

TI Improving oil recovery of leech layer, comprises            e.g. uniformly mixing a gas-soluble surfactant with            supercritical carbon dioxide and then injecting            supercritical carbon dioxide mixed fluid into the leech            layer.
AU LIU J
   LU P
   CHEN Q
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 201921254N
AB    NOVELTY - Improving oil recovery of leech layer,                comprises (i) uniformly mixing a gas-soluble                surfactant with supercritical carbon dioxide to                obtain supercritical carbon dioxide mixed fluid,                (ii) injecting supercritical carbon dioxide mixed                fluid into the leech layer, and (iii) shut the well                for soaking, and then open the well for                mining.
   USE - The method is useful for improving oil                recovery of leech layer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can realizes deep profile control;                and improves the oil recovery of the leech                layer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                device for improving the recovery of a leech layer,                comprising mixing device for mixing a gas-soluble                surfactant with supercritical carbon dioxide, an                injection device for injecting a supercritical CO2                mixed fluid, it is in communication with the mixing                device, and an injection well for introducing a                supercritical carbon dioxide mixed fluid into the                water layer, which is in communication with the                injection device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gas-soluble                  surfactant is sodium bis(2-ethylhexyl)                  sulfosuccinate salt, and/or polyoxyethylene                  polyoxypropylene lauryl ether. The mass ratio                  between gas-soluble surfactant and supercritical                  carbon dioxide is 0.2-3:100. The co-solvent is                  ethanol or pentanol. The addition amount of                  supercritical carbon dioxide mixed fluid is                  0.1-0.3 PV. Preferred Method: The method                  comprises adding cosolvent while mixing the                  aerosol-soluble surfactant with supercritical                  carbon dioxide, and the mass ratio between                  cosolvent and supercritical carbon dioxide is                  0.1-10:100. The mixing is carried out under the                  conditions of a temperature of greater than or                  equal to 32 degrees C and an absolute pressure of                  greater than or equal to 7.4 MPa. The temperature                  at the time of controlling the well is 50-150                  degrees C, and the absolute pressure is 20-120                  MPa.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The injection well                  includes a wellhead device and a wellbore in                  communication with the wellhead device.

AB    NOVELTY - Producing (P1) syngas and olefins comprises:                (a) feeding a catalytic partial oxidation (CPO)                reactant mixture to a CPO reactor, where the ore                CPO reactant mixture comprises oxygen, first                hydrocarbons, and optionally steam, at least a                portion of ore CPO reactant mixture reacts, via a                CPO reaction, in the CPO reactor to produce a CPO                reactor effluent, the CPO reactor comprises a CPO                catalyst, the CPO reactor effluent comprises                hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, water                and unreacted first hydrocarbons, and the CPO                reactor effluent is characterized by an M ratio of                the CPO reactor effluent, where the M ratio is a                molar ratio defined as given specification                equation; (b) feeding a cracking unit feed to a                cracking unit to produce a cracking unit product                stream, a hydrogen-rich stream, and a hydrocarbon                recovery stream; and (c) contacting at least a                portion of the CPO reactor effluent with at least a                portion of the hydrogen-rich stream to yield                hydrogen-enriched syngas.
   USE - The processes are useful for producing                syngas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing (P1) syngas and olefins comprises:                (a) feeding a catalytic partial oxidation (CPO)                reactant mixture to a CPO reactor, where the ore                CPO reactant mixture comprises oxygen, first                hydrocarbons, and optionally steam, at least a                portion of ore CPO reactant mixture reacts, via a                CPO reaction, in the CPO reactor to produce a CPO                reactor effluent, the CPO reactor comprises a CPO                catalyst, the CPO reactor effluent comprises                hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, water                and unreacted first hydrocarbons, and the CPO                reactor effluent is characterized by an M ratio of                the CPO reactor effluent, where the M ratio is a                molar ratio defined as given specification                equation; and (c)                contacting at least a portion of the CPO reactor                effluent with at least a portion of the                hydrogen-rich stream to yield hydrogen-enriched                syngas, where the hydrogen-enriched syngas has an M                ratio of the hydrogen-enriched syngas, and the M                ratio of the hydrogen-enriched syngas is greater                than the M ratio of the CPO reactor effluent.    (2) producing (P3) methanol and olefins;                and
   (3) producing (P4) syngas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The cracking unit                  comprises a cracking reactor and a separating                  unit, where the process further comprises cooling                  the CPO reactor, and the cooling the CPO reactor                  comprises heating the cracking reactor while                  cooling the CPO reactor by heat transfer between                  the CPO reactor and the cracking reactor. The CPO                  reactor has at least one CPO operational                  parameter comprising a CPO feed temperature of                  25-600 degrees C, a CPO effluent temperature of                  300-1600 degrees C, a CPO pressure of 1-90 barg,                  a CPO contact time of 0.001 milliseconds to 5                  seconds, a carbon to oxygen molar ratio in the                  CPO reactant mixture of 0.5:1-3:1, the carbon to                  oxygen molar ratio refers to the total moles of                  carbon of hydrocarbons in the reactant mixture                  divided by the total moles of oxygen in the                  reactant mixture, a steam to carbon molar ratio                  in the CPO reactant mixture of 0.01 :1-2.4:1,                  and/or the steam to carbon molar ratio refers to                  the total moles of water in the reactant mixture                  divided by the total moles of carbon of                  hydrocarbons in the reactant mixture. and a hydrocarbon recovery                  stream, where the cracking unit feed comprises                  second hydrocarbons, the cracking unit product                  stream comprises olefins,           stream and a hydrocarbon-enriched stream, where                  the hydrogen-enriched stream comprises at least a                  portion of the hydrogen of the hydrogen-rich                  stream and the hydrocarbon-enriched stream                  comprises at least a portion of the hydrocarbons                  of the hydrogen-rich stream, (c) feeding a                  catalytic has an M ratio of the                  hydrogen-enriched syngas, the M ratio of the                  hydrogen-enriched syngas is greater than the M                  ratio of the syngas, the hydrogen-enriched syngas                  has a hydrogen to carbon monoxide molar ratio of                  the hydrogen- enriched syngas, and the hydrogen                  to carbon monoxide molar ratio of the                  hydrogen-enriched syngas is greater than the                  hydrogen to carbon monoxide molar ratio of the                  syngas, (e2) introducing at least a portion of                  the hydrogen-enriched syngas to a methanol                  reactor to produce a methanol reactor effluent                  stream, where the methanol reactor effluent                  stream comprises methanol, water, hydrogen,                  carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, methane, first                  hydrocarbons, and optionally second hydrocarbons                  and/or C4+ hydrocarbons, (f2) separating at least                  a portion of the methanol reactor effluent stream                  into a crude methanol stream and a vapor stream,                  where the crude methanol stream comprises                  methanol and water and the vapor stream comprises                  hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide,                  methane, first hydrocarbons, and optionally                  second hydrocarbons and/or C4+ hydrocarbons, (g2)                  introducing at least a portion of the vapor                  stream to a first hydrogen recovery unit to                  produce a hydrogen stream and a residual gas                  stream, where the hydrogen stream comprises at                  least a portion of the hydrogen of the vapor                  stream, and the residual gas stream comprises                  carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, methane, first                  hydrocarbons, and optionally second hydrocarbons                  and/or C4+ hydrocarbons, (h2) recycling at least                  a portion of the hydrogen stream to the methanol                  reactor, (I2) optionally recycling at least a                  portion of the residual gas stream to the CPO                  reactor, (j2) separating at least a portion of                  the crude methanol stream into a methanol stream                  and a water stream and (k2) optionally cooling                  the CPO reactor, where the cooling the CPO                  reactor comprises heating the cracking unit while                  cooling the CPO reactor by heat transfer between                  the CPO reactor and the cracking unit. The                  process (P4) comprises: feeding CPO reactant                  mixture to a CPO reactor, where the CPO reactant                  mixture comprises oxygen, first hydrocarbons, at                  least a portion of a hydrogen-lean stream, and                  optionally steam, the hydrogen-lean stream                  comprises hydrogen, methane, and optionally 2-3C                  hydrocarbons, at least a portion of the                  hydrocarbons in the CPO reactant mixture react,                  via a CPO reaction, in the CPO reactor to produce                  a syngas, the hydrocarbons in the CPO reactant                  mixture comprise first hydrocarbons, methane, and                  optionally 2-3C hydrocarbons, the CPO reactor                  comprises a CPO catalyst, the syngas comprises                  hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, water                  and unreacted hydrocarbons and the CPO reactant                  mixture comprises hydrogen in an amount of less                  than about 15 mol.%. Preferred Components: The                  first hydrocarbons and/or the second hydrocarbons                  comprise methane, ethane, propane, butanes,                  naphtha, natural gas, natural gas liquids,                  associated gas, well head gas, enriched gas,                  paraffins, shale gas, shale liquids, fluid                  catalytic cracking (FCC) off gas, refinery                  process gases, stack gases and/or fuel gas from                  fuel gas header, where the olefins comprise                  ethylene. The hydrogen-enriched syngas comprises                  carbon dioxide in an amount of 0.25-5 mol.%. The                  M ratio of the hydrogen-enriched syngas is                  greater than or equal to 1.8. The                  hydrogen-enriched syngas has a hydrogen to carbon                  monoxide molar ratio of greater than 2, where the                  hydrogen to carbon monoxide molar ratio of the                  hydrogen-enriched syngas is greater than the                  hydrogen to carbon monoxide molar ratio of the                  CPO reactor effluent. The M ratio of the syngas                  is greater than or equal to 1.8 and/or the                  hydrogen to carbon monoxide molar ratio of the                  syngas is greater than 2. The CPO reactant                  mixture comprises hydrogen in an amount of less                  than 20 mol.%. The M ratio and/or the hydrogen to                  carbon monoxide molar ratio of the syngas is                  greater than the M ratio and/or the hydrogen to                  carbon monoxide molar ratio, respectively of a                  syngas produced by an otherwise similar process                  that feeds a CPO reactant mixture without the                  hydrogen-lean stream to a CPO reactor. The first                  hydrogen recovery unit and the second hydrogen                  recovery unit can independently comprise a                  pressure swing adsorption (PSA) unit, a membrane                  separation unit and/or a cryogenic separation                  unit. The second hydrocarbons comprise ethane,                  where the cracking unit comprises an ethane                  cracker, and the olefins comprise ethylene. The                  hydrogen-lean stream comprises a gas stream from                  a cracking unit, a gas stream from a refinery, a                  fuel gas stream and/or fuel gas from fuel gas                  header.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN111388504-A; CN111388504-B
TI Preparing dental epithelial cell exosome comprises e. g. taking engineered dental epithelial cell line (HERS-H1) preserved in liquid nitrogen, resuscitating with warm water, inoculating and adding epithelial cell medium.
AU QIAO J
   CHEN G
   ZHANG S
AE CHENGDU SHILIAN KANGJIAN BIOTECHNOLOGY (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2020824221
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing dental epithelial cell exosome comprises (i) taking engineered dental epithelial cell line (HERS-H1) preserved in liquid nitrogen, resuscitating with warm water at 40-50 degrees C, inoculating in a 10 cm Petri dish, adding epithelial cell medium containing 2% fetal bovine serum, storing in an incubator at 35-38 degrees C under 4-5% carbon oxide, changing the medium after 24-26 hours until the cells are fused to 80-95%, digesting with trypsin for passage, amplifying 105-108 cells, and extracting the engineered dental epithelial cell line extract; and (ii) transferring the engineered dental epithelial cell line into a T75 culture flask, adding epithelial cell medium containing 2% fetal bovine serum and storing in an incubator at 35-38 degrees C and 4-5% carbon dioxide, aspirating and discarding the medium when the cell density reaches 60-75%, washing the bottom of the culture flask with phosphate buffer saline 2-3 times, adding epithelial cell medium containing 2% exosomal-free serum.
   USE - The dental epithelial cell exosome and engineered dental epithelial cell exosome is useful for promoting in vivo periodontal and dental pulp regeneration (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The dental epithelial cell exosome does not contain major histocompatibility complexes, and avoids the immune rejection problem faced in stem cell-based regeneration, and is easy to store and transport.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing dental epithelial cell exosome comprises (i) taking engineered dental epithelial cell line (HERS-H1) preserved in liquid nitrogen, resuscitating with warm water at 40-50 degrees C, inoculating in a 10 cm Petri dish, adding epithelial cell medium containing 2% fetal bovine serum, storing in an incubator at 35-38 degrees C under 4-5% carbon oxide, changing the medium after 24-26 hours until the cells are fused to 80-95%, digesting with trypsin for passage, amplifying 105-108 cells, and extracting the engineered dental epithelial cell line extract; (ii) transferring the engineered dental epithelial cell line into a T75 culture flask, adding epithelial cell medium containing 2% fetal bovine serum and storing in an incubator at 35-38 degrees C and 4-5% carbon dioxide, aspirating and discarding the medium when the cell density reaches 60-75%, washing the bottom of the culture flask with phosphate buffer saline 2-3 times, adding epithelial cell medium containing 2% exosomal-free serum, storing in an incubator at 35-38 degrees C and 4-5% carbon dioxide, collecting the supernatant after two consecutive days of culture, centrifuging the collected supernatant at speed of 1900-2100 g for 28-32 minutes and remove dead cells and cell debris, and discarding the precipitate; (iii) taking the centrifuged supernatant, placing in a 100 KD membrane centrifugal filter, centrifuging for 28-32 minutes at 4500-5200 g at temperature of 3-5 degrees C, centrifuging twice, obtaining membrane supernatant is engineered dental epithelial cell extract, and storing the extract at -80 degrees C; and (iv) adding the exosome extraction reagent into the engineered dental epithelial cell line extract obtained in step (iii), where the dosage ratio of dental epithelial cell line extract and exosome extraction reagent is 1.8-2.2:1, storing at 3-5 degrees C for 12-18 hours, centrifuging for 50-70 minutes at temperature of 3-5 degrees C and rotation speed of 8000-12000 g, and taking the precipitate. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing a collagen gel engineered dental epithelial cell exosomal implant, comprising (a) adding the standard collagen gel on ice while pre-cooling the operating tools; (b) adding the collagen gel into pre-cooled glass centrifuge tube in a ratio of 8:1:1, mixing and placing 10/asterisk basal medium and buffer on ice; (c) re-suspending the engineered dental epithelial cell exosome in a mixed liquid solvent, where the final concentration of exosome is 1.5-2.5 mg/ml; and (d) transferring the liquid solvent mixed with engineered dental epithelial cell exosome into a pre-cooled glass tube, and storing on ice.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), taking the engineered dental epithelial cell line (HERS-H1) stored in liquid nitrogen, resuscitating with warm water at 45 degrees C, and inoculating in a 10 cm Petri dish, adding epithelial cell medium containing 2% fetal bovine serum, storing at 37 degrees C in a 5% carbon dioxide incubator, changing the medium after 24-36 hours, fusing the cells to 90%, and performing trypsin digestion for passage. In the step (ii), when the cell density reaches 60-75%, aspirating and discarding the medium, washing the bottom of the culture flask with phosphate buffer saline 2-3 times, adding epithelial cell medium containing 2% exosome-free serum, and storing in 37 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide incubator. In the step (iii), obtaining the extract of engineered dental epithelial cell line by taking the centrifuged supernatant, placing in a 100 KD membrane centrifugal filter, performing two centrifugation to initially screen the liquid containing factors with molecular weights close to exosome, concentrating at the same time, where the centrifugal conditions are temperature is 3-5 degrees C, the speed is 4500-5200 g, and the time is 28-32 minutes. The step (iv) obtains the engineered dental epithelial cell exosome by adding the exosome extraction reagent into the engineered dental epithelial cell line extract obtained in step (iii), where the dosage ratio of dental epithelial cell line extract and exosome extraction reagent is 1.8-2.2:1, storing for 12-18 hours at 3-5 degrees C, and centrifuging for 50-70 minutes at temperature of 3-5 degrees C and rotation speed of 8000-12000 g. Preferred Components: The exosome extraction reagent is Total exosome isolation-TM reagent (Life Technologies, USA), ExoQuick-TC (SBI) and/or exo-Easy Maxi Kit (qiagen).

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PN CN110742024-A
TI Breeding Drosophila melanogaster by breeding in glass tanks containing feed and apple, then breeding in cage having egg collection tray, then breeding in bottle until mature larvae crawls out of tray to wall, washing and sieving suspension.
AU LIU X
   AO G
   LIN J
   SHI S
   JI Q
AE UNIV FUJIAN AGRIC & FORESTRY (UYFN-C)
GA 202011798Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for breeding Drosophila melanogaster involves cleaning insect breeding glass tanks, layering artificial feed and apple pieces, sealing the tank, adding artificial feed into a petri dish, refrigerating the egg collection tray, anesthetizing initially emerged D.melanogaster with carbon dioxide, introducing into the tank, breeding, then introducing into D.melanogaster cage, placing the egg collection tray in the D.melanogaster cage, breeding, placing the egg collection tray in a cylindrical plastic bottle, cutting a square opening on bottle cap, sealing the opening with a gauze, breeding until the mature larvae of D.melanogaster crawls out of the egg collection tray to the wall of the plastic bottle and pupate on the wall of the bottle, removing the egg collection tray, rinsing the D.melanogaster pupae attached to the bottle wall with water and sieving the suspension to obtain a large number of D.melanogaster.
   USE - The method is useful for breeding D.melanogaster.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, suitable for mass breeding and cost-effective, saves time and labor, requires simple equipment and produces a large number of D.melanogaster in a short period of time, hence meets the experimental requirements for a large number of D.melanogaster.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for breeding Drosophila melanogaster involves (a) cleaning insect breeding glass tanks, naturally drying, wiping the tank using ethanol soaked cotton, layering two layers of absorbent paper towels at the bottom of the tank, layering A4 paper folded twice on the paper towel, adding 100 g artificial D.melanogaster feed on the A4 paper, cleaning and wiping an apple with ethanol, peeling and cutting the apple into pieces, evenly layering the apple pieces on artificial feed and sealing the tank with gauze and rubber bands, (b) adding artificial feed into a petri dish with a diameter of 9 cm, cooling the feed to room temperature to obtain a solid feed, inverting the petri dish upside down and refrigerating the egg collection tray, (c) anesthetizing initially emerged D.melanogaster with carbon dioxide, selecting and introducing 100 pairs of males and females into the tank, when their feeding and spawning development reaches 5-6 days, the offspring of D.melanogaster begin to pupate and when it develops to 9-10 days, the offspring will begin to emerge, (d) anesthetizing initially emerged D.melanogaster with carbon dioxide, selecting and introducing 600 pairs of males and females into D.melanogaster cage, placing the egg collection tray in the D.melanogaster cage, filling petri dish with 10% honey water soaked absorbent cotton to supplement nutrition, placing a fresh-keeping box containing sterile water in the insect cage to replenish moisture, drilling a 1 cm diameter hole in the lid of the fresh-keeping box, inserting the absorbent cotton sliver through the hole into the water and removing the egg collection tray in an insect cage after 24 hours, (e) placing the egg collection tray in a cylindrical plastic bottle, cutting a square opening with a length of 3 cm in the middle of the original bottle cap, sealing the opening with a gauze to ensure that fruit flies cannot fly out of the cylindrical plastic bottle and normal gas flow in the bottle, where first hatched larvae began to appear after 24 hours and the larvae began to pupate 3 days after feeding, the mature larvae of D.melanogaster crawls out of the egg collection tray to the wall of the plastic bottle and pupate on the wall of the bottle and (f) removing the egg collection tray from the plastic bottle using tweezers, rinsing the D.melanogaster pupae attached to the bottle wall with water, brushing the bottle using a brush to remove the unwashed the D.melanogaster and sieving the suspension containing the D.melanogaster in a plastic bottle through a 50-mesh sieve to obtain a large number of D.melanogaster.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The artificial feed is prepared by (i) adding 300 ml warm water to 50 g corn flour, 40 g sucrose and 20 g yeast powder, evenly stirring the mixture and adding 7 ml 95 volume/volume% ethanol and 3 ml acetic acid, (ii) adding 10 g agar to 500 ml water and heating to dissolve into an agar solution, (iii) adding the mixed solution obtained in step (i) to the agar solution obtained in step (ii), adding water to make up to 1000 ml, evenly stirring the mixture, heating to cook, adding 1 g potassium sorbate and evenly stirring the mixture and (iv) adding the feed into the feed tray, sealing with plastic wrap, cooling to room temperature to obtain solid feed and storing in a refrigerator. In step (d), the D.melanogaster insect cage has size of 30 cmx 30 cmx 30 cm. In step (e), the size of the cylindrical plastic bottle has length of 20 cm and diameter of 9 cm. The method involves breeding under indoor temperature 25 plus minus 1 degrees C, relative humidity of 70 plus minus 10% and photoperiod of 14 hours illumination:10 hours darkness in culture room.

MC A03-A00A; A03-A05; A12-W04; A12-W09; A12-W12B; P14-A01A; P14-A05; P14-E01F

PT P
PN CN110476797-A
TI Performing plant factory cultivation of redvein dock involves treating redvein dock seed, treating, germinating, dividing into root growth period and leaf growth period, supplementing nutrient solution, maintaining and harvesting product.
AU LI J
   LAI H
   YAN W
AE FUJIAN SINO-SCI BIOLOGY CO LTD (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 2019A0559L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for performing plant factory cultivation of redvein dock involves performing seed treatment of redvein dock seed, subjecting the exposed seeds to low-temperature treatment for 3 days, then sowing in a porous medium, tiling the porous medium in a root control device, placing in a seeding tray with water, after the perforated medium absorbs enough water, spraying water on the seed surface and covering with plastic wrap, germinating by placing the seeding tray in the germination box or germination chamber for germination at 15-20 degrees C under humidity of 30-100% until the seed turns to white, dividing the nursery period into root growth period and leaf growth period, supplementing nutrient solution, maintaining specific day and night temperature, humidity, temperature of the nutrient solution and carbon dioxide concentration, and harvesting by when the true leaves grow more than 4, and the length of the leaves is 2-15 cm.
   USE - The method is useful for performing plant factory cultivation of redvein dock.
   ADVANTAGE - The method breaks seasonal dormant cycle of redvein dock seeds through seed treatment, improves germination rate and germination potential, is beneficial to the annual industrial production, ensures quality and cleanliness of young leaves, saves cleaning costs, avoids damage to new shoots, reduces metabolic leaves and metabolic petioles, maintains plant clean, harvests 10-30 stubbles, and increases yield in a cost-effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for performing plant factory cultivation of redvein dock involves (a) performing seed treatment by immersing the redvein dock seed into a potassium permanganate solution with a concentration of 1-5% for 5-10 minutes, removing the floating seeds, separating full seeds that sink to the bottom, and spreading under light for 12-24 hours, (b) subjecting the exposed seeds to low-temperature treatment for 3 days, then sowing in a porous medium, tiling the porous medium in a root control device, placing in a seeding tray with water, after the perforated medium absorbs enough water, spraying water on the seed surface and covering with plastic wrap, (c) germinating by placing the seeding tray in the germination box or germination chamber for germination at 15-20 degrees C under humidity of 30-100% until the seed turns to white, (d) dividing the nursery period into root growth period and leaf growth period, during root growth period, after the seeds are exposed, removing the cling film, moving the porous medium with exposed seeds to the nursery bed for root growth, planting at density of 500-3000 plants/m2, cultivating with water, after the root system grows to 2-3 cm, reducing the water level, supplementing nutrient solution, after the first true leaf is unfolded, separating the plants from the porous medium in clear water, placing one separated plant in one hole in the solid medium for leaf growth, maintaining day and night temperature of 20-23 degrees C/18-20 degrees C and humidity of 30-90%, and maintaining the temperature of purified water and nutrient solution at 20-22 degrees C and carbon dioxide concentration at 400-800 ppm, and during leaf growth period, planting at density of 100-500 plants/m2, which is cultivated with nutrient solution to reduce the level of nutrient solution, maintaining liquid level not to touch the solid medium and keep the solid medium dry and increase the light intensity, maintaining during the leaf growing season the day and night temperature of 20-23 degrees C/18-20 degrees C, and maintaining humidity of 50-90%, temperature of the nutrient solution at 18-22 degrees C, humidity of 30-90% and carbon dioxide concentration at 400-1200 ppm, and (e) harvesting by when the true leaves grow more than 4, and the length of the leaves is 2-15 cm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The step (c) involves germinating for at least 24-140 hours. The step (b) involves performing low-temperature treatment at 4-8 degrees C. In step (d), the light intensity is 150-250 mu mol.m-2.s-1 and the light duration is 10-12 hours/day, the ratio of the optical quantum number of the wavelength 400-499 nm is 21-25%, the ratio of the optical quantum number of the wavelength 500-599 nm is 14-15%, the ratio of light quantum numbers with a wavelength of 600-699 nm is 46-50%, and the ratio of light quantum numbers with other wavelengths is 12-17%. In step (d), the light source conditions of the leaf growth stage is performed at light intensity of 200-350 mu mol.m-2.s-1 for 10-14 hours/day, where the ratio of light quantum light quanta with a wavelength of 380-399 nm is less than or equal to 0.1%, the ratio of the light quantum number of light quantum with a wavelength of 400-499 nm is 13-15%, the ratio of the light quantum number of the light quantum with a wavelength of 500-599 nm is 17-19%, the ratio of the light quantum number of the light quantum with a wavelength of 600-699 nm is 50-55% and the ratio of the light quantum number of the light quantum with a wavelength of 700-780 nm is 13-17%. When harvesting, the solid medium is replaced and the planting tank is cleaned after every 5-10 harvests. Preferred Components: The nutrient solution comprises 780 mg/l potassium nitrate, 150 mg/l ammonium dihydrogen phosphate, 330 mg/l potassium sulfate, 30 mg/l EDTA-sodium iron, 450 mg/l calcium nitrate tetrahydrate, 110 mg/l magnesium sulfate, 2 mg/l boric acid, 1.5 mg/l manganese sulfate monohydrate, 0.25 mg/l zinc sulfate heptahydrate, 0.13 mg/l anhydrous copper sulfate, 0.03 mg/l ammonium molybdate tetrahydrate, 0.1 ml/l, calcium-sugar alcohol liquid fertilizer, 0.6 ml/l magnesium-sugar alcohol liquid fertilizer and 0.3-0.8 ml/l purified potassium type clear liquid fertilizer.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.

PT P
PN CN110004007-A
TI Performing rice-flavor liquor production process            involves preparing saccharification equipment,            fermenter, rotary tank pump and three-tank combined            distillation apparatus, where saccharification device            comprises closed pot body.
AU ZHENG X
   DU S
   HU G
AE SHANGHAI JOY LIGHT IND MACHINERY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201963528E
AB    NOVELTY - Performing rice-flavor liquor production                process involves preparing a saccharification                equipment, a fermenter, a rotary tank pump and a                three-tank combined distillation apparatus. The                saccharification device comprises a closed pot                body, a heat exchange jacket, a saccharification                equipment stirring device and a sieve plate. The                top of the closed pot body is provided with a                personal hole door, a first cleaning device and a                first nozzle, and the bottom of the closed pot body                is provided with at least one first sewage outlet,                a first material outlet and a second nozzle. The                heat exchange jacket is disposed on an outer wall                of the middle portion and the bottom portion of the                closed pot body. The stirring paddle of the mashing                device stirring device is located in the closed pot                body and connected to a drive shaft. The sieve                plate is installed in the closed pot body and below                the stirring paddle.
   USE - Method for performing rice-flavor liquor                production process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform rice-flavor                liquor production process is realizes the fully                automatic control of the fermentation, greatly                reduces the work intensity and energy consumption,                and improves the accuracy of the technology and the                stability of the product quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing rice-flavor liquor production                process involves preparing a saccharification                equipment, a fermenter, a rotary tank pump and a                three-tank combined distillation apparatus. The                saccharification device comprises a closed pot                body, a heat exchange jacket, a saccharification                equipment stirring device and a sieve plate. The                top of the closed pot body is provided with a                personal hole door, a first cleaning device and a                first nozzle, and the bottom of the closed pot body                is provided with at least one first sewage outlet,                a first material outlet and a second nozzle. The                heat exchange jacket is disposed on an outer wall                of the middle portion and the bottom portion of the                closed pot body. The stirring paddle of the mashing                device stirring device is located in the closed pot                body and connected to a drive shaft. The sieve                plate is installed in the closed pot body and below                the stirring paddle. The closed pot body and the                pipeline are cleaned by the first cleaning device,                and the finished rice is put into the closed pot                body from the manhole door. The brewing water is                sprayed on the rice layer through the first nozzle,                and simultaneously opening the first stirring                device to stirred the rice layer, and the washed                rice water flows into the closed pot body through                the sieve plate. The bottom of the inner cavity is                discharged from the sewage outlet, and washed for                10-15 minutes. The first material outlet and the                first stirring device, and continuing to add                brewing water through the first nozzle, until the                water surface is 10-15 cm above the rice layer, and                then immersed for 8-12 hours. The first sewage                outlet is opened to drain the water after immersing                the rice, repeated the method in the to continue                cleaning for 5-10 minutes, opened the first sewage                outlet, filtered out excess water in the rice layer                through the sieve plate. The fermentation of the                crushed mash into the fermenter, the fermentation                time is 5-8 days, and the fermentation temperature                is controlled by the temperature control jacket to                28-32 degrees C throughout the fermentation                process, The pressure regulating device controls                the fermentation pressure, and the carbon dioxide                generated in the whole fermentation process is                concentrated and discharged by the carbon dioxide                discharge valve, and the second stirring device is                turned on to disperse the heat generated during the                whole fermentation process to ensure the                saccharification of the mash. The temperature is                uniform, and same time, the carbon dioxide in the                mashing mash in the whole fermentation process                overflows. The fermentation product is transferred                to a three-pot combined distillation apparatus for                distillation to obtain a raw wine. The obtained                original wine is stored or blended according to the                target alcohol content and stored.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The washing water is                  flowed into the bottom of the inner cavity of the                  closed pot body through the sieve plate, and                  re-injected into the closed pot body through the                  circulation of the first material through a                  circulation pump for circulation cleaning. The                  produced saccharification liquid by                  saccharification process flowed into the bottom                  of the inner cavity of the closed pot body                  through the sieve plate, and is re-injected into                  the mashed mash layer through the outer                  circulation pump through the first material                  outlet. The heat exchange jacket and/or the                  temperature control jacket is passed into an                  external coolant or hot water.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

PN CN109912302-A; CN109912302-B
TI High performance nickel-zinc ferrite material comprises e.g. iron(III) oxide, nickel(II) oxide, zinc oxide, copper(II) oxide, cobalt(III) oxide, manganese(II) carbonate, titanium dioxide, molybdenum trioxide and bismuth(III) oxide.
AU LIU Z
   YU H
   ZHONG X
   ZHANG H
   LONG K
AE SANQIAOHUI FOSHAN NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (SANQ-Non-standard)
GA 2019577292
AB    NOVELTY - High performance nickel-zinc ferrite material comprises 88-102 pts. wt. iron(III) oxide, 46-64 pts. wt. nickel(II) oxide, 25-45 pts. wt. zinc oxide, 3.4-7.6 pts. wt. copper(II) oxide, 2.2-4.6 pts. wt. cobalt(III) oxide, 1.5-3 pts. wt. manganese(II) carbonate, 1.1-1.9 pts. wt. titanium dioxide, 0.8-1.6 pts. wt. molybdenum trioxide, 0.7-1.3 pts. wt. bismuth(III) oxide, 1.3-2.1 pts. wt. calcium carbonate, 0.4-0.9 pts. wt. hafnium(IV) oxide, 0.3-0.6 pts. wt. selenium dioxide, 0.5-1 pts. wt. tantalum pentoxide and 0.2-0.4 pts. wt. samarium carbonate.
   USE - Used as high performance nickel-zinc ferrite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The nickel-zinc ferrite material: improves the magnetic properties of the material through reasonable raw material formula ratio and scientific production process; and has high magnetic permeability, high cutoff frequency, high saturation magnetic induction intensity, high Curie temperature, high electrical resistivity, high density, low loss, high stability and excellent comprehensive performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the high performance nickel-zinc ferrite material, comprising (i) taking the above mentioned raw materials, uniformly mixing the raw materials, using ball mill according to the ball, material and water in a mass ratio of 3-4:1:2-3, wet ball milling for 30-50 minutes, and performing dual-frequency ultrasonic alternating treatment for 20-40 minutes, (ii) placing the material obtained in the step (i) in 70% argon and 30% carbon dioxide atmosphere and the microwave power of 200-300 W and heating to 410-460 degrees C, maintaining for 1-2 hours, placing in 60% nitrogen, 20% carbon monoxide and 20% hydrogen atmosphere and microwave power of 150-250 W and heating to 860-920 degrees C, maintaining for 2-3 hours, air cooling to room temperature, placing in 80% argon and 20% carbon monoxide atmosphere and microwave power of 250-350 W and heating to 580-640 degrees C, maintaining for 1.5-2.5 hours, and air cooling to room temperature, (iii) ball milling the material obtained in the step (ii) by ball mill according to the ball, material and water in a mass ratio of 2-3:1:1-2 for 20-40 minutes by wet ball milling, and performing double-frequency ultrasonic treatment for 15-30 minutes, (iv) adding aqueous polyvinyl alcohol solution corresponding to the total mass of the material to the material obtained in the step (iii), stirring for 5-10 minutes, adding to the molding die, alternately treating with double frequency microwave for 25-40 minutes, and pressing forming, (v) heating the molded piece obtained in the step (iv) to 890-970 degrees C in a microwave oven under condition of 70% nitrogen, 20% argon and 10% hydrogen atmosphere and a microwave power of 300-400 W, maintaining for 2-3 hours, and cooling to room temperature, placing in 40% nitrogen, 30% argon and 30% carbon dioxide atmosphere and microwave power of 250-350W and heating to 550-600 degrees C, maintaining for 1-2 hours, placing in 50% argon, 40% carbon dioxide and 10% carbon monoxide atmosphere and microwave power of 200-300 W and heating to 960-1020 degrees C, maintaining the temperature for 2-3 hours, placing under the condition of 80% nitrogen, 10% carbon monoxide and 10% hydrogen atmosphere and microwave power of 100-200W and heating to 1220-1280 degrees C, maintaining for 3-4 hours, air cooling to room temperature, placing under condition of 50% argon and 50% nitrogen atmosphere and microwave power of 150-250 W and heating to 300-350 degrees C, maintaining for 0.5-1.5 hours, and air-cooling to room temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The high performance nickel-zinc ferrite material comprises 95 pts. wt. iron(III) oxide, 55 pts. wt. nickel(II) oxide, 35 pts. wt. zinc oxide, 5.5 pts. wt. copper(II) oxide, 3.4 pts. wt. cobalt(III) oxide, 2.2 pts. wt. manganese(II) carbonate, 1.25 pts. wt. titanium dioxide, 1.2 pts. wt. molybdenum trioxide, 1 pts. wt. bismuth(III) oxide, 1.7 pts. wt. calcium carbonate, 0.6 pts. wt. hafnium(IV) oxide, 0.4 pts. wt. selenium dioxide, 0.8 pts. wt. tantalum pentoxide and 0.3 pts. wt. samarium carbonate. Preferred Method: The rotational speed of the wet ball mill in the step (i) is 7000-9000 revolutions per minute (rpm). The condition of the dual-frequency ultrasonic alternating processing in the step (i) is: the dual-frequency ultrasonic alternating frequency is 30-40 KHz/70-80 KHz, the dual-frequency ultrasonic alternating working time is 1-3 seconds, and the ultrasonic power is 150-250 W. The rotational speed of the wet ball mill in the step (iii) is 8000-10000 rpm. The condition of the dual-frequency ultrasonic alternating processing in the step (iii) is: the dual-frequency ultrasonic alternating frequency is 45-55 KHz/90-100 KHz, the dual-frequency ultrasonic alternating working time is 2-4 seconds, and the ultrasonic power is 200-300 W. The stirring speed in the step (iv) is 200-400 rpm. The condition of the dual-frequency microwave alternating processing in the step (iv) is: the dual-frequency microwave alternating frequency is 900-1000MHz/2400-2500MHz, the dual-frequency microwave alternate working time is 3-5 seconds, and the microwave power is 200-300W. The conditions of the press forming in the step (iv) are: pressing pressure of 60-80 MPa, and dwell time of 10-20 minutes. Preferred Components: The mass fraction of the aqueous polyvinyl alcohol solution in the step (iv) is 10-15%.
