PT P
PN CN113351207-A
TI Preparing multi-wall catalyst useful for in            preparation of liquid fuel by carbon dioxide            hydrogenation regulation comprises e.g. dissolving            cobalt salt raw material and carbon template in aqueous            solution, and performing thermal synthesis.
AU LIU K
   LIU J
   MENG Y
   GUO L
   WEI Y
   XUE Z
   SUN S
AE LINHUAN COKING & CHEM CO LTD (HBMN-C)
   UNIV ANHUI (UANH-C)
GA 2021A95762
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing multi-wall catalyst comprises e.g.                dissolving cobalt salt raw material and carbon                template in a deionized aqueous solution, stirring                for 1-3 hours, controlling the mass ratio of the                cobalt salt raw material to the carbon template at                0.5-2.5, transferring to the hydrothermal synthesis                reactor under the conditions of 120-200 degrees C,                performing thermal synthesis for 5-15 hours,                filtering and washing with deionized water to                obtain the cobalt catalyst cobalt-carbon, moving                cobalt-carbon catalyst into a tube furnace with a                protective atmosphere, calcining at 500-900 degrees                C for 5-15 hours to obtain cobalt/carbon-hydrogen                catalyst, dissolving cobalt/carbon-hydrogen                catalyst, carbon template, iron salt raw material,                and auxiliary agent in a deionized aqueous                solution, stirring and mixing for 1-3 hours, and                moving into the hydrothermal synthesis reactor                under 120-200 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing multi-wall                catalyst, which is useful for in the preparation of                liquid fuel by carbon dioxide hydrogenation                regulation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: synthesizes high-efficiency                core-shell type catalyst with multi-wall structure;                provides a new idea for efficient conversion                process of carbon dioxide selective hydrogenation                to prepare high value-added chemical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing multi-wall catalyst comprises (a)                dissolving cobalt salt raw material and carbon                template in a deionized aqueous solution, stirring                for 1-3 hours, controlling the mass ratio of the                cobalt salt raw material to the carbon template at                0.5-2.5, transferring to the hydrothermal synthesis                reactor under the conditions of 120-200 degrees C,                performing thermal synthesis for 5-15 hours,                filtering and washing with deionized water to                obtain the cobalt catalyst cobalt-carbon, moving                cobalt-carbon catalyst into a tube furnace with a                protective atmosphere, calcining at 500-900 degrees                C for 5-15 hours to obtain cobalt/carbon-hydrogen                catalyst, (b) dissolving cobalt/carbon-hydrogen                catalyst, carbon template, iron salt raw material,                and auxiliary agent in a deionized aqueous                solution, stirring and mixing for 1-3 hours, where                the mass ratio of iron salt raw material to carbon                template is 0.5-2.5, the mass ratio of the amount                of cobalt/carbon-hydrogen catalyst added to the                iron salt raw material in the mixed solution is                0.005-0.05, and the mass ratio of the amount of                cobalt/carbon-hydrogen catalyst added to the                auxiliary agent in the mixed solution is within                0.1-10, moving into the hydrothermal synthesis                reactor under 120-200 degrees C, performing thermal                synthesis for 5-15 hours, filtering and washing                with deionized water to obtain the                iron-cobalt-carbon catalyst, introducing                iron-cobalt-carbon catalyst into a protective                atmosphere in the tube furnace, carrying out the                calcination process at 500-900 degrees C for 5-15                hours to obtain iron-cobalt-carbon catalyst with a                two-layer wall structure, (c) dissolving the                obtained iron-cobalt-carbon catalyst, carbon                template, iron salt raw material, and auxiliary                agent in a deionized aqueous solution, stirring and                mixing for 1-3 hours, where the mass ratio of iron                salt raw material to carbon template is 0.5- 2.5,                the mass ratio of iron-cobalt-carbon catalyst added                to the iron salt raw material in the mixed solution                is within 0.005-0.05, and the mass ratio of                iron-cobalt-carbon catalyst to the auxiliary agent                in the mixed solution is 0.1-10, transferring to                the hydrothermal synthesis reactor under the                conditions of 120-200 degrees C, performing thermal                synthesis for 5-15 hours, filtering washing with                deionized water to obtain the                iron-iron-cobalt-carbon catalyst, moving                iron-iron-cobalt-carbon catalyst into a tube                furnace with a protective atmosphere and calcining                at 500-900 degrees C for 5-15 hours to obtain                iron-iron-cobalt-carbon-hydrogen catalyst with a                three-layer wall structure. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                also included for multi-wall catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal cobalt raw                  material is cobalt nitrate, cobalt acetate,                  cobalt chloride, cobalt oxalate, and/or cobalt                  sulfate. The carbon template agent is glucose,                  sucrose, maltose, or lactose. The calcining                  protection atmosphere is nitrogen, argon gas, and                  hydrogen. The auxiliary agent is potassium                  nitrate, potassium carbonate, potassium oxalate,                  potassium chloride nitrate, sodium carbonate,                  and/or sodium chloride.

PT P
PN CN112859951-A; CN112859951-B
TI Dynamic control method of blowing carbon dioxide smelting stainless steel process comprises dividing oxidation period of smelting process into fast decarbonization period, temperature control period and deep decarburization period.
AU WEI G
   ZHOU Y
   ZHU R
   DONG K
   CHEN P
   ZHAO G
   REN X
   LI W
   WANG C
   CHEN Y
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 202162030B
AB    NOVELTY - The dynamic control method for blowing carbon dioxide (CO2) smelting stainless steel process involves dividing oxidation period of the smelting process according to the molten pool carbon content into fast decarburization period), temperature control period and deep decarburization period. The molten pool carbon content in the fast decarburization period is greater than 1.5 %. The molten pool carbon content in the temperature control period is greater than 0.5 to 1.5%. The molten pool carbon content in the deep decarburization stage is less than or equal to 0.5 %. CO2 blowing flow in the 3 stages is dynamically adjusted. The molten pool carbon-chromium reaction balance is controlled.
   USE - Dynamic control method for carbon dioxide blowing smelting stainless steel process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the high temperature melting loss of furnace lining refractory material, controls the whole oxidation of the molten pool, and reduces carbon monoxide partial pressure in the furnace by mixing blowing nitrogen/argon, and reduces the oxidation burning loss of chromium.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method involves dividing oxidation period of the smelting process according to the molten pool carbon content into fast decarburization period), temperature control period and deep decarburization period. The molten pool carbon content in the fast decarburization period is greater than 1.5 %. The molten pool carbon content in the temperature control period is greater than 0.5 to 1.5%. The molten pool carbon content in the deep decarburization stage is less than or equal to 0.5 %. CO2 blowing flow in the 3 stages is dynamically adjusted. The molten pool carbon-chromium reaction balance is controlled. The control method of CO2 blowing flow comprises, at the fast decarburizing period, calculating the alloy adding amount Wa (Wa=Wgx omega (Cr)g-Wox omega (Cr)o)/ 0.9 omega (Cr)a, 1), according to the furnace metal liquid component, the furnace metal liquid amount W0, and alloy component; then calculating stage 1 oxygen consumption phi 1-O2 ( phi 1-O2=(Wox ( omega (Si)o)/28+0.5 omega (Mn)0/55+2.5 omega (P)0/31)+Wax ( omega (Si)a/28+0.5 omega (Mn)0/55+2.5 omega (P)a/31)+0.5x (Wox omega (C)0+Wax omega (C)a-1.5%x (Wo+Wa))/12)x 22.4/0.95, 2), oxygen flow Q1-O2 (Q1-O2= phi 1-O2/t1, 3), CO2 consumption 1-CO2 ( phi 1-CO2=R1-CO2x phi 1-O2, 4), and CO2 flow Q1-CO2 (Q1-CO2= f1-CO2/t1, 5), where t1 is the rapid decarburizing period setting smelting time, and R1-CO2 is proportional coefficient. The furnace metal liquid component comprises molten iron carbon content omega (C) 0; silicon content omega (Si) 0, manganese content omega (Mn) 0, phosphorus content omega (P) 0 and chromium content omega (Cr) 0. The target component comprises carbon content omega (C)g in the molten steel, silicon content omega (Si)g, manganese content omega (Mn)g, phosphorus content omega (P)g and chromium content omega (Cr)g. The alloy component comprises carbon content omega (C)a, silicon content omega (Si)a, manganese content omega (Mn)a, phosphorus content omega (P)a and chromium content omega (Cr)a. In the temperature control period, according to the traditional technique of not blowing CO2, the temperature of the molten pool at this stage is T2-molten pool and after blowing CO2, the temperature T is controlled to control the temperature and the carbon content of the molten pool omega (C) 2-molten pool calculating CO2 consumption phi 2-CO2, CO2 flow Q2-CO2, oxygen consumption phi 2-O2, and oxygen flow Q2-O2. In the deep decarburizing period, according to the stage molten pool carbon content omega (C) 3-molten pool and target component, calculating the oxygen consumption phi 3-O2, oxygen flow Q3-O2 and CO2 consumption phi 3-CO2, CO2 flow Q3-CO2, N2/Ar consumption phi 3-N2/Ar, and N2/Ar flow Q3-N2/Ar.
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).); T06 (Process and Machine Control)
MC M24-C06; M24-D07; M24-E; T06-B13B; T06-D10

PT P
PN CN111826015-A
TI Powder coating is prepared by adding isophthalic acid, tris(hydroxymethyl)aminomethane, p-toluenesulfonic acid and xylene, introducing carbon dioxide gas, recovering solvent, adding ceramic composite nanoparticles and drying material.
AU WANG B
   SHI Z
   LI H
   JIN C
   HUI L
   WANG J
AE ANHUI XINDALU SPECIAL PAINT CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2020A75024
AB    NOVELTY - Powder coating is prepared by (i) placing silicone resin in a reaction flask, adding 1.4-1.8 %mass isophthalic acid, 2-2.2 %mass tris(hydroxymethyl)aminomethane, 0.5-0.6 %mass p-toluenesulfonic acid and 0.26-0.3 %mass xylene of the mass silicone resin to the reaction flask, heating at 90-95 degrees C, turning on the stirrer, introducing carbon dioxide gas, heat-preserving for 45-60 minutes, continuously heating at 140-145 degrees C, refluxing for 2-3 hours, continuously heating at 225-230 degrees C at a rate of 8-10 degrees C/hour, recovering the evaporated solvent until the pH value of the system reaches 8.6-8.9, and cooling to 132-135 degrees C, and (ii) adding 1.7-2.4 %mass benzophenone of the system mass, continuously stirring for 30-40 minutes, adding 0.074-0.078 %mass prepared ceramic composite nanoparticles of the system mass, rapidly stirring at 1200-1400 rpm for 18-25 minutes, cooling to 70-76 degrees C, filtering, and drying the material.
   USE - As powder coating.
   ADVANTAGE - The powder coating has strong electrical insulation, withstands the alternating hot and cold cycles while keeping the film from damaging, and is suitable for mass production, which greatly reduces the production cost of the enterprise.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Powder coating is prepared by (i) placing silicone resin in a reaction flask, adding 1.4-1.8 %mass isophthalic acid, 2-2.2 %mass tris(hydroxymethyl)aminomethane (tris), 0.5-0.6 %mass p-toluenesulfonic acid and 0.26-0.3 %mass xylene of the mass fraction of silicone resin to the reaction flask, heating at 90-95 degrees C, turning on the stirrer, introducing carbon dioxide gas, heat-preserving for 45-60 minutes, continuously heating at 140-145 degrees C, refluxing for 2-3 hours, continuously heating at 225-230 degrees C at a rate of 8-10 degrees C/hour, recovering the evaporated solvent until the pH value of the system reaches 8.6-8.9, and cooling to 132-135 degrees C, and (ii) adding 1.7-2.4 %mass benzophenone of the system mass, continuously stirring for 30-40 minutes, adding 0.074-0.078 %mass prepared ceramic composite nanoparticles of the system mass, rapidly stirring at 1200-1400 rpm for 18-25 minutes, cooling to 70-76 degrees C, filtering, and drying the material, where the ceramic composite nanoparticles are prepared by weighing 10.8-11.2 g zeolite powder, grinding through a 250-350 mesh sieve, drying in an oven at 70-80 degrees C for 2-4 hours, placing in a beaker, adding 80-85 ml phosphate buffer to the beaker under stirring at 350-400 rpm, placing on a magnetic stirrer, continuously stirring and dispersing for 15-25 minutes, leaving still and precipitating for 5-8 hours, performing suction filtration, washing with deionized water 4-6 times, placing the solid product in a vacuum drying oven at 80-90 degrees C for 10-12 hours, adding 43-45 ml silicon nitride dispersion to the dry materials, using an electric heating furnace to heat at 44-48 degrees C, continuously heating and stirring for 40-50 minutes, transferring to an autoclave, shaking and mixing, setting the reaction temperature at 220-240 degrees C, where the reaction pressure is 1.2-1.3 MPa, heat-preserving for 3-4 hours, after the reaction is over, naturally cooling with the furnace, filtering, washing with absolute ethanol 3-5 times, drying in an oven at 60-80 degrees C for 8-10 hours, placing the ground powder in a crucible, sending to a muffle furnace for calcination, and the heating rate is 3-3.2 degrees C/minute, heating at 630-660 degrees C, maintaining the temperature for 2-3 hours, and naturally cooling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - IMAGING AND COMMUNICATION - Preferred Method: The silicon nitride dispersion is prepared by weighing 2.2-2.6 g silicon nitride powder in 40-50 ml absolute ethanol, continuously stirring at 300-350 rpm for 30-40 minutes, adding 5-5.5 ml triethylamine, stirring for 20-30 minutes, and ultrasonically dispersing for 10-15 minutes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The silicone resin comprises 15-30 pts. wt. methyltrichlorosilane, 55-62 pts. wt. dimethyldichlorosilane, 35-48 pts. wt. diphenyldichlorosilane, 110-140 pts. wt. xylene, 10-20 pts. wt. ethanol, 18-22 pts. wt. n-butanol and 150-180 pts. wt. distilled water. Preferred Properties: In the preparation of the ceramic composite nanoparticles, the pH value of the phosphate buffer is 6.6-6.7. The particle size of the ceramic composite nanoparticles is 17-20 nm.

PN CN110151637-A
TI Preparing composite fruit-flavored hand cream comprises extracting tea saponin refining tea saponin, extracting passion fruit seed oil, mango nucleolar oil, oil phase and aqueous phase and aging.
AU TANG Y
   HE X
   SUN J
   LI C
   XIN M
   LI L
   ZHOU Z
   SHENG J
   ZHENG F
   YANG Y
   LI J
   LIU G
   LI Z
   LING D
AE GUANGXI ZHUANG NATIONALITY AUTONOMOUS RE (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201977363F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing composite fruit-flavored hand cream, (1) extracting tea saponin comprising removing tea seed mash, drying, pulverizing, collecting, extracting tea saponin by ultrasonic vibration, (2) concentrating crude tea saponin, purifying tea saponin by a macroporous resin, washing with water, subjecting eluate to rotary evaporation (3) crushing passion fruit seed by double roll crusher, passion fruit seed is extracted by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction technology, (4) crushing mango nucleolus (5) taking Span-60 (RTM: Sorbitan ester)passion fruit oil, mango kernel oil, coconut oil, lanolin, stearic acid, stearic acid glyceride, cetyl alcohol, vitamin E propylparaben and stirring a complete temperature, dispersing to obtain oil phase, (5) preparing oil phase, (5) preparing aqueous phase,(7) mixing oil phase and aqueous phase, quickly pouring aqueous phase into oil phase and stirred vigorously to obtain an emulsion, and slowly stirred and cooling and (8) statically aging.
   USE - The composite fruit-flavored hand cream is useful for reducing damage of UV to skin and preventing skin melanin precipitation.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite fruit-flavored hand cream is safe. The method is simple, environmentally friendly and conforms to the national ecological economic industrial policy, rapid absorption rate, no stimulation, moisturizing, healing, antibacterial ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing composite fruit-flavored hand cream, comprises (1) extracting tea saponin comprising removing tea seed mash and placing in hot air at 55-65 degrees C, drying in the box for 11-13 hours, pulverizing it into a powder in a pulverizer, collecting the 60-100 mesh tea seed meal powder, extracting tea saponin by ultrasonic vibration assisted water extraction and alcohol precipitation method to obtain crude tea saponin extract, (2) concentrating crude tea saponin, purifying tea saponin by a macroporous resin method, washing with water until colorless and 0.2% sodium hydroxide solution until color of the effluent becomes light, and washing with water until neutral, eluting with 60% ethanol, subjecting eluate to rotary evaporation to concentrate and drying to obtain a tea saponin extract having a purity greater than 95%, (3) crushing passion fruit seed by double roll crusher, removing miscellaneous fruit seeds and passion fruit seed is extracted by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction technology, extracting at 50-60 degrees C and extraction pressure of 30-40Mpa for 3-4 hours to obtain get passion fruit oil, (4) crushing mango nucleolus and passing through a 50-70 mesh sieve, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction technology for extraction, where extraction condition comprising extraction pressure of 25-35 MPa, extraction temperature of 40-50 degrees C , extraction time is 1.5-2.5 hours and flow rate of 28-30 l/ minutes and obtaining get mango nuclear oil, (5) taking 2-3 pts. wt. Span-60 (RTM: Sorbitan ester), 5-7 pts. wt. passion fruit oil, 2-4 pts. wt. mango kernel oil, 1-3 pts. wt. coconut oil, 1-3 pts. wt. lanolin, 1-3 pts. wt. stearic acid, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. stearic acid glyceride, 1-3 pts. wt. cetyl alcohol, 0.1-0.3 pts. wt. vitamin E, 0.04-0.06 pts. wt. propylparaben and placing in a beaker and stirring at 70-75 degrees C to a complete temperature, dispersing to obtain oil phase; (6) taking 1-3 pts. wt.Tween-60 (RTM: Polyethoxlated sorbitan ester), 4-6 pts. wt. glycerin, 0.1-0.3 pts. wt. tea saponin, 0.05-0.15 pts. wt. urine capsule, 0.05-0.15 pts. wt. methylparaben in another beaker, and adding 62-81 pts. wt. deionized water, stirring at 70-75 degrees C to complete dissolution to obtain aqueous phase, (7) mixing oil phase and aqueous phase, quickly pouring aqueous phase into oil phase and stirred vigorously to obtain an emulsion, and slowly stirred and cooling to 40-50 degrees C, adding 0.05-0.15 pts. wt. passion fruit flavor and 0.04-0.06 pts. wt. mango , stirring flavoring agent and cooling to room temperature to obtain mixed paste, (8) statically aging mixed paste at 20-30 degrees C for 2-4 days to stabilize paste.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: In the step (1) ultrasonic vibration assisted water extraction and alcohol precipitation method, comprises water extraction process condition is The liquid and solid ratio is 1:9-11, the extraction time is 55-65 minutes, the extraction temperature is 70-80 degrees C, the oscillation intensity is 220-230 revolutions/minute/min, the pH value is 7-9, the ultrasonic power is 90-110W, the ultrasonic frequency is 58-60 kHz. the liquid obtained by ultrasonic extraction and centrifugation is refined by alcohol precipitation method. The process conditions comprising ethanol concentration is more than 90%, solid-liquid ratio is 1:2-4, and alcohol precipitation time is 2.5-3.5 hours, the alcohol precipitation temperature is 70-80 degrees C. Preferred Components: The method comprises The addition amount of passion fruit flavor and mango flavor after oil phase and the aqueous phase are mixed is 0.1 pts. wt. and 0.05 pts. wt., respectively. In the step (5), the oil phase is comprises 2.5 pts. wt. Span-60 (RTM: Sorbitan ester) , 6 pts. wt. passion fruit oil, 3 pts. wt. mango nuclear oil, 2 pts. wt. coconut oil, 2 pts. wt. lanolin, 2 pts. wt. stearic acid, 1 pts. wt. monoglycerol stearate ester, 2 pts. wt. cetyl alcohol, 0.2 pts. wt. vitamin E, and 0.1 pts. wt. propyl paraben. In the step (6), the aqueous phase comprises 2 pts. wt. Tween-60 (RTM: Polyethoxlated sorbitan ester), 5 pts. wt. glycerin, 0.2 pts. wt. tea saponin, 0.1 pts. wt. allantoic sac, 0.1 pts. wt. methylparaben and 71.7 pts. wt. deionized water.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In the step (2), the macroporou resin is AB-8 macroporous resin.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological; Antibacterial.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)

PT P
PN CN110065960-A
TI Preparing nanometer spherical calcium carbonate powder involves mixing pretreated kaolin and sodium hydroxide solution, stirring the reaction , filtering, the filtrate is obtained, and obtaining the sodium aluminosilicate solution.
AU PAN H
AE PAN H (PANH-Individual)
GA 2019681348
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nanometer spherical calcium carbonate powder involves mixing pretreated kaolin and sodium hydroxide solution at a mass ratio of 1:2-1:3, stirring the reaction, filtering, the filtrate is obtained, obtaining the sodium aluminosilicate solution, mixing the sodium metasilicate alumina solution in absolute ethanol at a volume ratio of 10:1 to obtain a mixed solution, and mixing the mixed solution with polyethylene glycol at a mass ratio of 80:1-100:1, and stirring the reaction under a carbon dioxide atmosphere, aging, filtering, washing, and drying to obtain white carbon black. The white carbon black and the calcium chloride solution mixing at a mass ratio of 1:10-1:12, stirring the reaction, filtering, and drying to obtain a pretreated white carbon black, mixing gamma -methacryloxypropyltrimethoxysilane with water at a mass ratio of 1:20-1:30, adjusting the pH to 3.5-4.5, stirring and mixing, and obtaining a treatment liquid.
   USE - Method for preparing nanometer spherical calcium carbonate powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare nanometer spherical calcium carbonate powder has good appearance, and good easily-dispersible particle size uniformity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing nanometer spherical calcium carbonate powder involves mixing pretreated kaolin and sodium hydroxide solution at a mass ratio of 1:2-1:3, and stirring the reaction , filtering, the filtrate is obtained, and obtaining the sodium aluminosilicate solution, mixing the sodium metasilicate alumina solution in absolute ethanol at a volume ratio of 10:1 to obtain a mixed solution, and mixing the mixed solution with polyethylene glycol at a mass ratio of 80:1-100:1, and stirring the reaction under a carbon dioxide atmosphere, aging, filtering, washing, and drying to obtain white carbon black. The white carbon black and the calcium chloride solution mixing at a mass ratio of 1:10-1:12, stirring the reaction, filtering, and drying to obtain a pretreated white carbon black, Mixing gamma -methacryloxypropyltrimethoxysilane with water at a mass ratio of 1:20-1:30, adjusting the pH to 3.5-4.5, stirring and mixing, obtaining a treatment liquid, mixing the pretreated white carbon black with the treatment liquid at a mass ratio of 1:8-1:15, stirring the reaction, filtering, and drying to obtain a modified white carbon black. The modified white carbon black mixing with the surfactant at a mass ratio of 1:20-1:22, nd adding the modified white carbon black 0.2-0.3 times the initiator and the modified white carbon by 150-200 times the mass of water, after stirring and mixing, a modified white carbon black mixture is obtained. The modified white carbon black mixture and styrene are mixing at a mass ratio of 3:1-4:1, and stirring the reaction under a nitrogen atmosphere to obtain a pretreated polystyrene mixture, mixing the pretreated polystyrene mixture with methanol at a mass ratio of 2:1-4:1, filtering, and drying to obtain polystyrene microspheres. The polystyrene microspheres mixing with water at a mass ratio of 1:10-1:20, and ultrasonically dispersing to obtain a microsphere dispersion. The microsphere dispersion and the chitosan solution are mixed at a mass ratio of 1:1, centrifuged, and the supernatant liquid is removed to obtain a pretreatment microsphere dispersion. The pretreated microsphere dispersion mixing with water at a mass ratio of 1:1-1:2 and adding a sodium alginate solution having a mass of 1-2 times of the pretreatment microsphere dispersion, stirring and mixing, filtering, and drying to obtain pre-modified microspheres. The pre-modified microspheres mixing with the glutaraldehyde solution at a mass ratio of 1:5-1:6, and stirring the reaction, filtration, washing and drying to obtain a modified microsphere blank. The modified microsphere blank and tetrahydrofuran are mixed and soaked in a mass ratio of 1:8-1:10, filtered, washed and dried to obtain a modified microsphere. The modified microspheres mixing with sodium hydroxide solution at a mass ratio of 1:10-1:15, and introducing carbon dioxide, stirring the reaction, filtering, drying, and calcining to obtain nanometer spherical calcium carbonate powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The polyethylene glycol is a polyethylene glycol having a molecular weight of 2,000-2,500. The surfactant is any one of sodium dodecylbenzene sulfonate, sodium lauryl sulfate or a fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether. The initiator is any one of azobisisobutyro nitrile, potassium persulfate or sodium persulfate. The chitosan solution is prepared by mixing chitosan having a degree of deacetylation of 70-95% with an acetic acid solution having a mass fraction of 3-8% at a mass ratio of 1:8-1:10 solution. Preferred Condition: The calcination temperature is from 100-200 degrees centigrade.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC E31-U01; E34-D03A; A03-A; A04-C02B; A04-C02E; A05-H03A3; A08-M10; A08-R; A10-E09; A12-W14

PT P
PN CN109852134-A
TI Preparing aqueous metal ink comprises e.g. mixing homemade metal pigment with aqueous solvent, shearing and dispersing in a shear mixer, sealing the reaction kettle, introducing carbon dioxide gas into the reactor, heating and ventilating.
AU JIANG W
   HE J
   ZHANG S
AE JIANG W (JIAN-Individual)
GA 2019533239
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing aqueous metal ink comprises e.g. mixing 20-25 pts. wt. homemade metal pigment with 200-220 pts. wt. aqueous solvent in a stirred tank to obtain a mixed dispersion, subsequently adding 40-50 parts aqueous polyurethane having a molecular weight of 15000-20000 D, 8-12 pts. wt. antioxidant, 8-10 pts. wt. wetting and dispersing agent, and 10-15 pts. wt. surfactant, shearing and dispersing in a shear mixer at a rotation speed of 1500-2000 r/min to obtain an aqueous metal ink, where the homemade metal pigment is specifically prepared by: adding 200-250 ml cotton slurry black liquor into a reaction kettle with a gas valve, sealing the reaction kettle at an aeration rate of 40-50 ml/min, introducing carbon dioxide gas into the reactor through a gas valve, ventilating for 10-15 minutes, closing the vent valve, heating to 200-220 degrees C, and maintaining the reaction for 7-8 hours to obtain the reaction material, mixing the reaction material with quicklime, and heating to 80-90 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing aqueous metal ink.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves corrosion resistance and oxidation resistance of the copper plating layer, and has broad application prospects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing aqueous metal ink comprises mixing 20-25 pts. wt. homemade metal pigment with 200-220 pts. wt. aqueous solvent in a stirred tank to obtain a mixed dispersion, subsequently adding 40-50 parts aqueous polyurethane having a molecular weight of 15000-20000 D, 8-12 pts. wt. antioxidant, 8-10 pts. wt. wetting and dispersing agent, and 10-15 pts. wt. surfactant, shearing and dispersing in a shear mixer at a rotation speed of 1500-2000 r/min to obtain an aqueous metal ink, where the homemade metal pigment is specifically prepared by: (1) adding 200-250 ml cotton slurry black liquor into a reaction kettle with a gas valve, sealing the reaction kettle at an aeration rate of 40-50 ml/min, introducing carbon dioxide gas into the reactor through a gas valve, ventilating for 10-15 minutes, closing the vent valve, heating to 200-220 degrees C, and maintaining the reaction for 7-8 hours to obtain the reaction material, (2) mixing the reaction material with quicklime, heating to 80-90 degrees C, digesting for 4-5 minutes to obtain A caustic slurry, separating the caustic slurry by filtration, collecting the residue, placing the filter residue in an oven at a set temperature of 100-110 degrees C, and drying for 4-10 hours to obtain hydrothermal coke, (3) taking 100-120 g copper powder in a beaker, pouring 200-300 ml of sulfuric acid solution with a mass fraction of 35% along the beaker wall, heating, stirring until the copper powder is dissolved, then adding 25% concentrated aqueous ammonia to the beaker, initially precipitation occurs, and continuously adding ammonia until the precipitate is completely dissolved to obtain a copper ammonia solution, (4) dispersing copper ammonia solution, hydrothermal coke, glyoxylic acid, sodium tartrate, polymethyl acrylate and sodium carbonate in deionized water, mixing and stirring for 20-25 minutes to obtain an electroless copper plating solution, and (5) adding 400-500 ml electroless copper plating solution into a beaker, and heating up to 90-100 degrees C, placing 40-50 g reduced iron powder in the beaker, maintaining the reaction for 40-50 minutes to obtain a precipitate by filtration, placing the precipitate in an oven at a set temperature of 70-80 degrees C, drying for 2-3 hours, then placing in an ammonia decomposition hydrogen furnace, heating, continuously reducing and drying for 40-50 minutes, and blowing off the char ash to obtain a homemade metal pigment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The aqueous solvent comprises: ethylene glycol, propylene glycol or ethylene glycol butyl ether. The antioxidant comprises: mono-alkoxy pyrophosphate type titanate coupling agent, chelating titanate coupling agent, or monoalkoxy type titanate coupling agent. The wetting and dispersing agent is sodium polyacrylate or polyethylene glycol. The surfactant comprises: fatty acid polyoxyethylene ester, sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate, or sodium alkyl diphenyl ether disulfonate. In the step (1), the self-made metallic pigment, and the black liquor solid content of the cotton pulp is 25%. In the step (2), the mass ratio of the reaction material to the quicklime of the self-made metal pigment is 10:1. Preferred Method: In the step (3), in the specific preparation of the self-made metallic pigment: the temperature at which the sulfuric acid dissolves the copper powder is 60-65 degrees C. In the step (5), the reduction drying temperature in the ammonia decomposition hydrogen furnace is 150-200 degrees C. Preferred Composition: In the step (4), the raw materials of the components of the electroless copper plating solution comprises: 40-50 pts. wt. copper ammonia solution, 20-30 pts. wt. hydrothermal coke, 15-20 pts. wt. glyoxylic acid, 60-70 pts. wt. sodium tartrate, 20-25 pts. wt. polymethyl acrylate, 20-25 pts. wt. sodium carbonate, and 400-500 pts. wt. deionized water.

PN CN113563159-A; CN215403948-U
TI Production plant for preparing methanol by water gas, has water coal slurry gasification device, where water gas produced by gasification device is washed and entered into purification device to remove sulfide and portion of carbon dioxide.
AU WU L
   TAN H
   ZHANG L
   DUAN P
AE SHAANXI RUNZHONG CLEAN ENERGY CO LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 2021C6682L
AB    NOVELTY - A water gas production methanol plant has 1 water coal slurry gasification device. The water gas produced by the gasification device is washed and separated and entered into 2 purification device to remove sulfide and portion of carbon dioxide. The exhaust gas discharged from the purification device passes through 10 carbon dioxide separation device to separate carbon dioxide and enters 11 carbon dioxide gas pressurizing device to pressurize. The hydrogen sent from hydrogen supplement device enters 12 second synthesis tower to react to produce methanol, and the amount of hydrogen is adjusted through 15 hydrogen flow control valve which enters second synthesis tower.
   USE - Production plant used for preparing methanol (claimed) by water gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The production plant can improve hydrogen content of the synthesis device, and effectively utilize carbon element in water gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing methanol by water gas, which involves:
   a. washing and separating water gas produced by gasification device and entering into purification device to remove sulfide and portion of carbon dioxide;
   b. discharging exhaust gas from the purification device having large amount of carbon dioxide with volume content of 85% or more, removing nitrogen and other impurities in the exhaust gas by carbon dioxide separation device to purify carbon dioxide with content of 99.9% or more;
   c. pressurizing carbon dioxide gas pressurizing device to reaction pressure of synthesis tower of 8.8-9.2 MPa and sending hydrogen from hydrogen supplement device which enters into second synthesis tower;
   d. adjusting hydrogen flow rate by controlling opening of hydrogen flow control valve according to carbon dioxide gas flow rate;
   e. reacting under the action of catalyst under high temperature and pressure, cooling and generating crude methanol;
   f. using unreacted gas as circulating gas to enter synthesis device for reaction through cyclic compression, generating water at the same time during the reaction and sending crude methanol to methanol rectification device for purification to remove water and generated methanol products.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the production plant.
   Water coal slurry gasification device (1)
   Purification device (2)
   Carbon dioxide separation device (10)
   Carbon dioxide gas pressurizing device (11)
   Second synthesis tower (12)
   Hydrogen flow control valve (15)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The main components of the synthetic gas produced by purifying device are carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen.

PN CN113694688-A
TI Device for absorbing and capturing solid amine and desorbing carbon dioxide, comprises catcher for capturing carbon dioxide in flue gas, where catcher comprises processing chamber for adsorbing carbon dioxide in flue air.
AU LIU Y
   QIU F
AE BEIJING DERUNCHEN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTI (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2021E2593E
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises catcher for capturing carbon dioxide in flue gas, where catcher comprises a processing chamber for adsorbing carbon dioxide (CO2) in the flue air through a spherical solid amine adsorbent and a conveying pipe 26 connected with the processing chamber. The bottom end of the conveyor pipe is provided with a flue inlet 29, a first solid amine feeding port and an adsorption feeding port. The adsorbation discharge port of the processing room is connected to the adsorbant discharge port. A desorber is used for heating and desorbing the CO2 absorbed by the solid amine sorbent. The second solid amine feeding port of a cooler 15 is connected with a second solid amine discharge port, and third solid amine discharge ports of the cooler are connected to a third solid amine discharge port at the same time. The third solid amine discharge port is set at the cooler and is connected with the first solid amine feed port of the trap.
   USE - For absorbing and capturing solid amine and desorbing carbon dioxide used in atmospheric treatment and resource utilization in environmental protection, and for capturing large scale industrial flue gas carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The device ensures the discharge safety realizing continuous operation of capturing and desorbing CO2. It can be used for capturing large scale industrial flue gas CO2, and reduces the content of CO2 in the flue gases.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of absorbing and capturing solid amine and desorbing carbon dioxide using device, which involves:
   (a) Introducing flue gas into a conveying pipe by using a fan, fluidizing a spherical solid amine adsorbent in the conveying pipe, where temperature in the conveying pipe is 30-40degrees Celsius, pressure is 70kPa-120 kPa, solid-gas ratio is 2-30, gas flow rate is 2-20 m/second, and mixing contact time of the flue gas and the solid amine adsorbent is 3-20 seconds, so that the adsorption and the capture of CO are completed;
   (b) Conveying solid amine adsorbent to the top end of the conveying pipe, where gas-solid separation is completed in a flue gas cyclone separator in a settling chamber at the top end of the conveying pipe, gas is discharged through an exhaust port, solid amine adsorbent is settled to a collected dense phase section, and conveying a portion of the solid amine adsorbent to the conveying pipe to enter the next circulation;
   (c) Conveying the settled solid amine adsorbent to a desorber by gravity, and introducing CO2 desorbed last time into the desorber, then using heat exchange unit of the bubbling bed as a fluidizing agent for fluidizing the solid amine adsorbent to heat the solid amine adsorbent, when the solid amine adsorbent is heated to 80-110degrees Celsius and is desorbed for 3-10 minutes, then CO2 generated is desorbed from the spherical adsorbent;
   (d) Cooling the solid amine adsorbent, conveying the high-temperature solid amine adsorbent and subjecting to desorption from the desorber to the cooler by virtue of gravity, cooling the solid amine adsorbent by virtue of the air fluidization through the serpentine cooling pipe for 5-30 minutes to obtain solid amine adsorbent at the temperature of 40degrees Celsius, and conveying the solid amine adsorbent to the conveying pipe for next circulation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of a device for absorbing and capturing and desorbing CO2 of solid amine.
   Desorber (2)
   Cooler (15)
   Conveying pipe (26)
   Flue gas inlet (29)

PN CN111298611-A
TI System for removing sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide from flue gas of coal-fired boiler, consists of desulfurization system including desulfurization tower, decarbonization system, and desulfurization by-product treatment system.
AU BAI T
   JIN Z
AE UNIV SHANXI (UYSX-C)
GA 202058193D
AB    NOVELTY - A system consists of an interconnected desulfurization system, a decarbonization system and a desulfurization by-product treatment system. The desulfurization system includes a desulfurization tower, a pulp box and a limestone silo (S1). The decarburization system includes a decarburization tower, a calcination tower and a limestone silo (S2), which are sequentially connected. A gas outlet of the desulfurization tower is connected with a gas inlet of the decarburization tower. The liquid outlet of the desulfurization tower is connected with a liquid inlet of the desulfurization byproduct treatment system. The gas outlet of the decarburization tower is connected with a chimney, to discharge the flue gas after removing sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide. The desulfurization by-product treatment system includes an ammonium sulfate reactor, a sedimentation tank and a calcium carbonate centrifuge. The sedimentation tank is connected with an ammonium sulfate evaporator.
   USE - System is used for removing sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide from flue gas of coal-fired boiler (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide are economically removed from the flue gas of coal-fired boiler using the system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A system consists of an interconnected desulfurization system, a decarbonization system and a desulfurization by-product treatment system. The desulfurization system includes a desulfurization tower, a pulp box and a limestone silo (S1), which are sequentially connected. The decarburization system includes a decarburization tower, a calcination tower and a limestone silo (S2), which are sequentially connected. A gas outlet of the desulfurization tower is connected with a gas inlet of the decarburization tower. The liquid outlet of the desulfurization tower is connected with a liquid inlet of the desulfurization byproduct treatment system. The gas outlet of the decarburization tower is connected with a chimney, to discharge the flue gas after removing sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide. The desulfurization by-product treatment system includes an ammonium sulfate reactor, a sedimentation tank and a calcium carbonate centrifuge, which are sequentially connected. The sedimentation tank is connected with an ammonium sulfate evaporator. The ammonium sulfate evaporator and the ammonium sulfate centrifuge are connected in order to discharge the centrifuged ammonium sulfate solid. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for removing sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide in coal-fired boiler flue gas using the system, which involves introducing a flue gas containing sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide into the desulfurization system, reacting sulfur dioxide in the flue gas with limestone slurry in the desulfurization tower, generating gypsum slurry, introducing resultant flue gas into the decarburization tower, reacting with calcium oxide, generating calcium carbonate, returning calcium carbonate to the limestone silo (S2) in the decarbonization system, flowing the flue gas after removing sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide, into chimney, discharging, introducing gypsum slurry into an ammonium carbonate reactor in the desulfurization byproduct system, mixing with a ammonium carbonate solution, reacting, generating ammonium sulfate and calcium carbonate, introducing the reaction slurry in the ammonium carbonate reactor into the sedimentation tank for solid-liquid separation, introducing obtained ammonium sulfate solution into ammonium sulfate evaporator, and calcium carbonate solution into the calcium carbonate centrifuge, introducing separated solid calcium carbonate into the two limestone silos in the desulfurization system and the decarbonization system, returning the liquid to the ammonium sulfate reactor, introducing concentrated ammonium sulfate into ammonium sulfate centrifuge, obtaining ammonium sulfate solid, condensing steam generated by evaporation by a condenser, and returning the condensate to ammonium carbonate reactor. The ammonium carbonate solution is produced by urea and desalinated water.

PN CN114000172-A
TI Capturing and reducing carbon dioxide and            co-producing oxygen or chlorine, involves absorbing            carbon dioxide gas in flue gas using solution            containing hydroxide or/and carbonate to form            absorption solution, adding solution to electrolytic            cell device, and electrolyzing.
AU YU S
   WANG C
   SHI J
   FU D
AE UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UNEN-C)
GA 2022247623
AB    NOVELTY - The method for capturing and reducing carbon                dioxide (CO_2) and co-producing oxygen or chlorine                involves: absorbing CO_2 gas in a flue gas using a                solution containing hydroxide or/and carbonate to                form an absorption solution; setting up an                electrolytic cell device by two methods and adding                the solution using one of two methods, where the                method (a) comprises arranging a cation exchange                membrane in the middle of the device, and dividing                the electrolytic cell into two reaction chambers,                such as a cathode chamber and an anode chamber, and                the method (b) comprises providing the device with                two ion exchange membranes, and diving into three                reaction chambers; and switching on the power                supply of the device to carry out the electrolysis,                where the gas produced in the anode chambers and                the gas or/and liquid reduction products produced                in the cathode chambers are collected                respectively.
   USE - Method for capturing and reducing carbon                dioxide (CO_2) and co-producing oxygen or chlorine.                The method also prepares carbon monoxide, methane,                ethanol, formic acid and methanol directly from the                absorption solution that has absorbed CO_2. When                using the method (b), it can also generate pure                hydrogen, which can be used as clean energy
   ADVANTAGE - Using the electrolysis device, it is no longer                necessary to introduce pure CO_2 gas, and carbon                monoxide, methane, ethanol, formic acid and                methanol are prepared directly from the absorption                solution that has absorbed CO_2 in the flue gas as                a raw material. The method process waste gas with                any concentration of CO_2 and can also co-produce                oxygen or pure chlorine as a by-product, when using                the method (b), it can also generate pure hydrogen,                which can be used as clean energy. As the carbon                peak and carbon neutrality goals are approaching,                the technology can achieve CO_2 emission reduction,                and the by-produced hydrogen will also promote the                widespread use of hydrogen energy. A virtuous cycle                of promoting the transformation and upgrading of                the energy structure will be formed with carbon                emission reduction as the starting point.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method for capturing and reducing carbon                dioxide (CO_2) and co-producing oxygen or chlorine                involves: absorbing CO_2 gas in a flue gas using a                solution containing hydroxide or/and carbonate to                form an absorption solution; setting up an                electrolytic cell apparatus by two methods and                adding the solution was added using one of two                methods, where the method (a) comprises arranging a                cation exchange membrane in the middle of the                electrolytic cell device, and dividing the                electrolytic cell into two reaction chambers, such                as a cathode chamber and an anode chamber, where                the cathode chamber is provided with a cathode (1)                connected with a negative electrode of a power                supply, the anode chamber is provided with an anode                (2) connected with a positive electrode of the                power supply, the cathode chamber is filled with                the absorption solution, and the anode chamber is                filled with an acid solution, and the method (b)                comprises providing the electrolytic cell device                with two ion exchange membranes, diving the                electrolytic cell into three reaction chambers,                where an anode connected with the positive pole of                a power supply is arranged in a reaction chamber at                one end, and the reaction chamber is called an end                anode chamber (7), a cathode connected with a                negative electrode of a power supply is arranged in                the reaction chamber at the other end and the                middle reaction chamber, the two reaction chambers                are respectively called an end cathode chamber (9)                and a middle cathode chamber (8), the ion exchange                membrane between the end anode chamber and the                middle cathode chamber is a cation exchange                membrane, and the ion exchange membrane between the                end cathode chamber and the middle cathode chamber                is a cation exchange membrane (3) or an anion                exchange membrane, adding an alkali solution into                the end cathode chamber, adding an absorption                solution into the middle cathode chamber, and                adding an acid solution into the end anode chamber,                where the cathode in the both the methods is                copper, zinc, lead, silver, gold or platinum metal                electrodes or its alloys; and switching on the                power supply of the chambers and the gas                and/or liquid reduction products produced in the                intermediate cathode chambers are collected                respectively.

PN CN114214219-A
TI New genetically engineered strain capable of formate-assisted free fatty acid production by e.g. converting glucose into free fatty acid synthesis precursor substance acetyl coenzyme A, and synthesizing free fatty acid through fatty acid synthesis pathway, and adding formate during fermentation.
AU LIU Z
   WANG M
   CHEN B
   XIE R
   WANG S
   WANG K
   TAN T
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 202246313R
AB    NOVELTY - Genetically engineered strain capable of formate-assisted free fatty acid production, where formate-assisted free fatty acid production involves (a) converting glucose into free fatty acid synthesis precursor substance acetyl coenzyme A in microorganism, and synthesizing free fatty acid through fatty acid synthesis pathway of strain itself; (b) adding formate during fermentation, allowing transfer into microorganism, converting into carbon dioxide under the action of exogenously expressed formate dehydrogenase, producing reducing power required by free fatty acid production; (c) converting carbon dioxide obtained from formate and carbon dioxide produced by glucose conversion in cell into the precursor substance acetyl coenzyme A under the action of exogenously expressed 1,5-bisphosphate carboxylase/oxygenase and phosphoribulokinase; and (d) converting acetyl coenzyme A obtained from formate and glucose into free fatty acid via free fatty acid synthesis pathway, is new.
   USE - Used as a genetically engineered strain capable of formate-assisted free fatty acid production.
   ADVANTAGE - The genetically engineered strain is safe and free of toxicity, can use carbon dioxide and derivative formic acid to ferment and produce free fatty acids, and is environment-friendly, and ensures low production cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of the genetically engineered strain in producing free fatty acids.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The genetically engineered strain is recombinant Saccharomyces cerevisiae capable of heterologous expression of exogenous gene encoding formate dehydrogenase (FDH) and over-expressing the key gene in the Calvin cycle module. The exogenous gene FDH encoding formate dehydrogenase includes the gene FDH1 derived from Lactobacillus buchneri or codon-optimized gene FDH1 derived from Lactobacillus buchneri; gene FDH2 derived from Mycobacterium vaccae or codon-optimized gene derived from Mycobacterium vaccae, or gene FDH3 derived from Candida boidinii or codon-optimized gene FDH3 derived from Candida boidinii, gene FDH4 derived from Pseudomonas sp.strain 101 or codon-optimized gene FDH4 derived from Pseudomonas sp.strain 101, and the genes FDH1-4 respectively obtained after gene substitution, which does not change the formate dehydrogenase function. The key gene in the Calvin cycle module comprises RuBisCO gene encoding 1,5-bisphosphate carboxylase/oxygenase or codon-optimized gene RuBisCO encoding 1,5-bisphosphate carboxylase/oxygenase, and/or the gene PRK encoding phosphoribulokinase or the codon-optimized gene PRK encoding phosphoribulokinase. The genetically engineered strain is the recombinant Saccharomyces cerevisiae obtained by chassis microbial transformation, where the chassis microorganism transformation comprises knockout of the related gene of free fatty acid catabolism pathway. The related gene of the free fatty acid catabolism pathway comprises gene hfd1 encoding fatty aldehyde dehydrogenase, gene pox1 encoding fatty acyl coenzyme A oxidase, gene faa1 encoding long-chain fatty acyl-CoA synthetase and gene faa4 encoding the long-chain fatty acyl-CoA synthetase. Preferred Method: The use process comprises fermenting and culturing the genetically engineered strain, and producing free fatty acid. The use process comprises processing fermentation culture of the genetically engineered strain, and adding formate-containing compound in the fermentation stage, and producing free fatty acid; preferably the formate-containing compound is formate; the shake flask fermentation comprises adding 20 g/l glucose and adding 2-7.2g/L formate within 24 hours and producing free fatty acid; and the 5 l fermentation tank fermentation comprises continuously feeding glucose and adding 5 g/l formate every 12 hours, and producing free fatty acid.

PN CN113522293-A
TI Preparation of catalyst used for preparing hydrogen by dry reforming methane carbon dioxide, by weighing nickel(II) nitrate hexahydrate, copper (II) nitrate hexahydrate, magnesium nitrate hexahydrate and aluminum nitrate hexahydrate.
AU CHEN Y
   GU J
   WANG S
   LI D
   ZHANG J
   YUAN H
AE SOUTHERN MARINE SCI & ENG GUANGDONG LAB (SMAR-Non-standard)
   GUANGZHOU ENERGY INST CONVERSION CHINESE (CAEC-C)
GA 2021C2592D
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst is prepared by weighing nickel(II) nitrate hexahydrate, copper (II) nitrate hexahydrate, magnesium nitrate hexahydrate and aluminum nitrate hexahydrate to prepare metal salt solution with metal ion total concentration of 1 mol/L, using sodium hydroxide and sodium carbonate to prepare alkali solution, slowly dropping metal salt solution and alkali solution into ultra-pure water, controlling pH of solution to 8-10, uniformly stirring, crystallizing in oil bath at 65degrees Celsius for 12 hours, washing and drying, grinding, calcining and reducing .Calcining is done in air atmosphere at 300-600degrees Celsius for 3 hours. Reduction is reduction in mixed gas atmosphere of hydrogen and nitrogen at volume of 7: 34 at 400degrees Celsius for 4 hours at heating speed of 2degrees Celsius/minute. The catalyst is nickel-copper-magnesium-aluminum hydrotalcite layered catalyst and takes nickel and copper as active component.
   USE - The method is used for preparing catalyst for preparing hydrogen by dry reforming methane carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has layered structure, low device requirement, conversion rate of methane reaching to 89.4%, hydrogen yield reaching to 89.6%, good stability and anti-carbon ability and large surface area, pore volume and pore diameter, does not need to separate carbon dioxide in natural gas, greatly improves adsorption capacity of carbon dioxide to improve reforming reaction activity, and meets requirement of small methane hydrogen production reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The 0.1 wt.% tetrapropyl ammonium hydroxide surfactant is added to water before metal salt solution is mixed with alkali solution. The pH value of solution is 8.5-10. Drying is vacuum drying at 80degrees Celsius for 12 hours.

PN CN112588320-A
TI Hydrophobic catalyst useful for hydrogenating carbon dioxide to synthesize methanol comprises active component, cocatalyst and modified hydrophobic carrier, and cocatalyst comprising zinc oxide, aluminum oxide, and/or zirconium dioxide.
AU LIU H
   MU L
   NING W
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 202135954Y
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrophobic catalyst comprises an active component, a cocatalyst and a modified hydrophobic carrier. The active component comprises copper. The cocatalyst comprises zinc oxide, aluminum oxide, zirconium dioxide, gallium(III) trioxide, and/or indium oxide. The modified hydrophobic carrier comprises an oxide carrier modified with a modifier. The oxide carrier comprises silicon oxide, aluminum oxide, titanium oxide, zirconium oxide, and/or silicon aluminum oxide. The modifier comprises a silane coupling agent.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for hydrogenating carbon dioxide to synthesize methanol (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the hydrophobic catalyst, comprising (i) adding the oxide carrier to anhydrous ethanol, ultrasonically vibrating, magnetically stirring, uniformly dispersing the oxide carrier, adding the modifier, carrying out the water bath reaction at 55-65 degrees C for 5-7 hours, washing the reaction product with ethanol, and vacuumizing drying at 55-65 degrees C to obtain the modified hydrophobic carrier, and (ii) preparing the precursor salt solution of the cocatalyst, immersing the same volume in the modified hydrophobic carrier obtained in the step (i) for 11-13 hours, drying at 60-120 degrees C, and calcining at 340-360 degrees C for 5-7 hours; and
   (2) use of hydrophobic catalyst for hydrogenating carbon dioxide to synthesize methanol, comprising subjecting the catalyst to hydrogen reduction treatment before being used to catalyze the hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to synthesize methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalyst comprises 4-25 pts. wt. active component, 2-15 pts. wt. cocatalyst and 100 pts. wt. modified hydrophobic carrier. Preferred Components: The modifier comprises methyl triethoxy silane, dimethyl diethoxy silane, and/or trimethyl ethoxy silane. GA 202048921L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves operating a molten                carbonate fuel cell (120) comprising an anode (127)                and a cathode (129) at a transference of 0.95 or                less and an average current density of 60 mA/cm2 or                more, to generate an anode exhaust (125) comprising                hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, and a                cathode exhaust comprising 2.0 vol.% or less carbon                dioxide, 1.0 vol.% or more oxygen, and 1.0 vol.% or                more water.
   USE - Method of operating a molten carbonate fuel                cell with enhanced carbon dioxide                utilization.
   ADVANTAGE - Rather than attempting to transport the heat                generated by the exothermic fuel cell reaction(s)                away from the fuel cell, this excess heat can be                used as a heat source for reforming and/or another                endothermic reaction. This can result in more                efficient use of the heat energy and/or a reduced                need for additional external or internal heat                exchange. This efficient production and use of heat                energy can reduce system complexity and components                while maintaining advantageous operating                conditions. The cathode input stream can be cleaned                to reduce or minimize the content of                contaminants.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is a block diagram which shows a                configuration for a molten carbonate fuel cell and                associated reforming and separation stages.

TI Generating constant pressure inside an aerosol            package comprising the use of an adsorbent material and            an inert permanent gas used in home care, personal            care, medical, food and vehicle care applications.
AU COLLIAS D I
AE PROCTER & GAMBLE CO (PROC-C)
   PROCTER & GAMBLE CO (PROC-C)
GA 202039892L
AB    NOVELTY - Generating constant pressure inside an aerosol                package comprising the use of an adsorbent material                and an inert permanent gas, is claimed. The inert                permanent gas is adsorbed into the adsorbent                material to form an adsorbent matrix, where the                adsorbent matrix has an uptake constant K greater                than 0.015 g/g.psig at 100 psig, the adsorbent                matrix generates a pressure inside the aerosol                package and the pressure remains constant                throughout the useful life of the aerosol                package.
   USE - The method is useful for generating constant                pressure inside an aerosol package, which is useful                in applications comprising home care, personal                care, medical, food, and vehicle care applications                (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method adsorbs large quantities of inert                permanent gases and maintain the initial pressure                inside aerosol packages throughout the useful life                of the packages, thus acting as propellants.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an                aerosol package comprising a bag, a can, and a                valve, where the volume between the bag filled with                product and the can is a propellant volume, the                propellant volume comprises an MOF and carbon                dioxide, the carbon dioxide is partly adsorbed in                the MOF, forming the adsorption matrix with uptake                constant K, and the remainder of the carbon dioxide                is in equilibrium with the adsorbed carbon dioxide                at a pressure and the pressure remains constant                throughout the useful life of the aerosol                package.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The uptake constant K                  is greater than 0.02 g/g.psig at 100 psig,                  preferably greater than 0.08 g/g.psig at 100                  psig. The adsorbent material comprises metal                  organic framework (MOF) (preferred), covalent                  organic framework and/or zeolitic imidazolate                  framework. The adsorbent matrix generates a final                  pressure ratio pressure and the final pressure                  ratio is greater than 2, preferably greater than                  3. The MOF is chromium terephthalate MOF                  (MIL-101) or metal-organic framework-177. The                  inert permanent gas comprises nitrogen, carbon                  dioxide, helium and/or argon. The inert permanent                  gas comprises carbon dioxide. The adsorbent                  material comprises an MOF and the inert permanent                  gas comprises carbon dioxide. The pressure inside                  the aerosol package is 100 psig. The aerosol                  package comprises a bag-in-bottle package and a                  bag-on-valve package. The aerosol package is a                  bag-on-valve package, where the adsorbent                  material is MIL-101, the inert permanent gas is                  carbon dioxide and the pressure is 100 psig. The                  aerosol package is reused by refilling it with                  product after the end of its useful current                  life.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Nonaqueous electrolyte secondary battery for e.g.            mobile phone, comprises positive electrode comprising            active material comprising alkali metal element and            binder comprising cellulose and solvent in which carbon            dioxide gas is dissolved.
AU MUKAI T
   IKEUCHI Y
   SAKAMOTO T
   YAMASHITA N
   ISHIGURO R
   NAKAMURA S
AE ATTACCATO (ATTA-Non-standard)
   JAPAN STEEL WORKS LTD (NIKL-C)
   JAPAN STEEL WORKS LTD (NIKL-C)
   ATTACCATO (ATTA-Non-standard)
GA 2020373443
AB    NOVELTY - A nonaqueous electrolyte secondary battery                comprises a positive electrode, a negative                electrode, a separator which is arranged between                the positive electrode and the negative electrode,                and an electrolytic solution. The positive                electrode comprises a positive electrode active                material and a positive electrode binder. The                positive electrode active material comprises at                least an alkali metal element as a constituent                element. The positive electrode binder comprises a                cellulose and a solvent in which carbon dioxide gas                is dissolved. A portion or all of the surface of                the positive electrode active material is coated                with the cellulose. A portion or all of the surface                of the cellulose is coated with the carbonate                compound of the alkali metal element.
   USE - Nonaqueous electrolyte secondary battery e.g.                lithium-ion nonaqueous electrolyte secondary                battery. Uses include but are not limited to mobile                telephone, smartphones, tablet terminals, electric                vehicles, plug-in hybrid vehicles, and hybrid                vehicles.
   ADVANTAGE - The nonaqueous electrolyte secondary battery                effectively suppresses the decrease of carbon                dioxide concentration due to carbon dioxide                vaporization, deterioration of battery                characteristics, oxidative decomposition of                cellulose fiber, and swelling of the active                material layer, and actively decomposes the alkali                metal carbonate, and has excellent cycle                characteristics and output characteristics at high                temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                manufacture of non-aqueous electrolyte secondary                battery.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is                  N-methylpyrrolidone. The battery is manufactured                  by preparing mixed solvent comprising cellulose,                  fluid medium and N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The binder comprises                  5-80 %mass cellulose, and further comprises 20-95                  %mass thermoplastic resin having a polymer gel                  which is absorbed the electrolytic solution. The                  carbon dioxide gas is dissolved in a binder                  solvent at a concentration of 50-9000 mg/l. The                  cellulose has a fiber diameter of 0.002-1 mu m, a                  fiber length of 0.5-10 mu m and an aspect ratio                  which is ratio of fiber length and fiber diameter                  of 2-100000. The cellulose contains cellulose in                  which a hydrophilic group of cellulose is                  substituted with a hydrophobic group by a                  reaction between cellulose and an additive, and                  is hydrophobized by substituting a portion of                  hydroxyl groups with carboxyl groups. The                  cellulose is disentangled by disentangling                  process which is a chemical treatment or a                  physical treatment. The chemical treatment is                  performed by adding at least one reagents having                  a pH value of 0.1-13 and melting point of                  (-20)-(200) degrees C. The cellulose is subjected                  to ethylene oxide addition treatment or propylene                  oxide addition treatment.

PN US2019185392-A1; US10865163-B2
TI Functionalizing carbon-hydrogen bond comprises using carbon dioxide as directing group to convert carbon-hydrogen bond in substrate compound into e.g. carbon-carbon, carbon-boron, carbon-nitrogen, carbon-oxygen and carbon-chloride.
AU YOUNG M C
   KAPOOR M
AE UNIV TOLEDO (TOOD-C)
   UNIV TOLEDO (TOOD-C)
GA 201951682N
AB    NOVELTY - Functionalizing a carbon-hydrogen bond comprises using carbon dioxide as a directing group to convert a carbon-hydrogen bond in a substrate compound into a carbon-carbon, carbon-boron, carbon-nitrogen, carbon-oxygen, carbon-fluoride, carbon-chloride, carbon-bromine, carbon-iodine, carbon-phosphorus or carbon-sulfur bond, where the substrate compound comprises an amine, alcohol, thiol or phosphine functional group.
   USE - The method is useful: for functionalizing the carbon-hydrogen bond (claimed) which is useful in the pharmaceutical, agrochemical and other fine chemical industries; and other carbon(sp3)-hydrogen functionalization, as well as allylamine carbon(sp2)-hydrogen functionalization.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: enables a simple one-pot procedure where 1 degrees and 2 degrees aliphatic amines can be arylated selectively at their gamma -carbon-hydrogen position; and facilitates rapid access to new carbon-hydrogen functionalized carbon-carbon, carbon-boron, carbon-nitrogen, carbon-oxygen, carbon-fluoride, carbon-chloride, carbon-bromine, carbon-iodine, carbon-phosphorus or carbon-sulfur bonds on various aliphatic and aromatic structures bearing amine, alcohol, thiol or phosphine groups that can be used to bind carbon dioxide as a directing group.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for synthesizing a compound comprising reacting a substrate having an amine, alcohol, thiol or phosphine functional group with a reactant in the presence of carbon dioxide and a transition metal catalyst to produce a compound, where the substrate is functionalized at a gamma -carbon-hydrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon-hydrogen bond is in a gamma -position relative to the amine, alcohol, thiol or phosphine functional group. The substrate compound is reacted with a reactant in the presence of carbon dioxide and a transition metal catalyst. The transition metal catalyst comprises palladium. The amine is a primary amine. The primary amine is 1-ethylcyclopentan-1-amine, 1-ethylcyclohexan-1-amine, 1-ethylcycloheptan-1-amine, 4-(tert-butyl)-1-ethylcyclohexan-1-amine, 9-ethyl-9H-fluoren-9-amine, 3-methylheptan-3-amine, 3-methylnon-3-amine, 3-methylpentan-3-amine, 3-ethylpentan-3-amine or heptan-3-amine. The amine is a secondary amine which is N-(tert-pentyl)propan-1-amine, N-(tert-pentyl)butan-1-amine, N-(tert-pentyl)pentan-1-amine, 2-methyl-N-phenethylbutan-2-amine, N-benzyl-2-methylbutan-2-amine, N-(4-methoxybenzyl)-2-methylbutan-2-amine, N-(3-methoxybenzyl)-2-methylbutan-2-amine, N-(4-tolyl)-2-methylbutan-2-amine, N-(3-tolyl)-2-methylbutan-2-amine or N-propylpentan-2-amine. The amine comprises 6-ethyl-3,3,9,9-tetraisopropyl-2,10-dimethyl-4,8-dioxa-3,9-disilaundecan-6-amine, and a tertiary amine. The reactant comprises: an iodoaromatic compound which is phenyl iodide, 3-iodoisocoumarin, 5-iodo-1-tosyl-1H-indole, 1-ethoxy-4-iodobenzene or 1-benzyloxy-4-iodobenzene; and a heterocycle. The amine is a benzylamino or an allylamine. The compound comprises a gamma -arylated primary amine, and 2-methyl-4-phenylbutan-2-amine, 4-(3-fluorophenyl)-2-methylbutan-2-amine, 4-(4-fluorophenyl)-2-methylbutan-2-amine, 2-methyl-4-(3-(trifluoromethyl)phenyl)butan-2-amine, 2-methyl-4-(4-(trifluoromethyl)phenyl)butan-2-amine, ethyl 2-(3-amino-3-methylbutyl)benzoate, ethyl 3-(3-amino-3-methylbutyl)benzoate, ethyl 4-(3-amino-3-methylbutyl)benzoate, 2-methyl-4-(3-nitrophenyl)butan-2-amine, 2-methyl-4-(4-nitrophenyl)butan-2-amine, 4-(3,5-bis(trifluoromethyl)phenyl)-2-methylbutan-2-amine, dimethyl 5-(3-amino-3-methylbutyl)isophthalate, 4-(3-chloro-4-fluorophenyl)-2-methylbutan-2-amine, 4-(4-bromophenyl)-2-methylbutan-2-amine, 4-(4-iodophenyl)-2-methylbutan-2-amine, 7-(3-amino-3-methylbutyl)-3,4-diphenyl-1H-isochromen-1-one, 2-methyl-4-(N-tosyl-1H-indol-5-yl)butan-2-amine, 4-(3-methoxyphenyl)-2-methylbutan-2-amine, 4-(4-methoxyphenyl)-2-methylbutan-2-amine, 4-(4-ethoxyphenyl)-2-methylbutan-2-amine, 4-(4-benzyloxyphenyl)-2-methylbutan-2-amine, 2-methyl-4-(meta-tolyl)butan-2-amine, 2-methyl-4-(para-tolyl)butan-2-amine, 4-(1,1'-biphenyl-4-yl)-2-methylbutan-2-amine, 2-methyl-4-(naphthalen-1-yl)butan-2-amine, 4-(2-fluorophenyl)-2-methylbutan-2-amine, 2-(3-amino-3-methylbutyl)phenol, 4-(2-methoxyphenyl)-2-methylbutan-2-amine, 4,4'-(1,4-phenylene)bis(2-methylbutan-2-amine), 1-phenethylcyclopentanamine, 1-phenethylcyclohexanamine, 1-phenethylcycloheptanamine, 4-(tert-butyl)-1-phenethylcyclohexanamine, 9-phenethyl-9H-fluoren-9-amine, 3-methyl-1-phenylheptan-3-amine, 3-methyl-1-phenylnonan-3-amine, 2-amino-2-phenethylpropane-1,3-diol, 3-methyl-1-phenylpentan-3-amine, 3-methyl-1,5-diphenylpentan-3-amine, 3-ethyl-1-phenylpentan-3-amine, 3-ethyl-1,5-diphenylpentan-3-amine, 4-(1,1'-biphenyl-4-yl)butan-2-amine, 1-(1,1'-biphenyl-4-yl)pentan-3-amine, 1-(1,1'-biphenyl-4-yl)heptan-3-amine or 4-(1,1'-biphenyl-4-yl)-3,3-dimethylbutan-2-amine. The compound comprises a gamma -arylated secondary amine. The gamma -arylated secondary amine is 4-(1,1'-biphenyl-4-yl)-2-methyl-N-propylbutan-2-amine, N-(4-(1,1'-biphenyl-4-yl)-2-methylbutan-2-yl)butan-1-amine, N-(4-(1,1'-biphenyl-4-yl)-2-methylbutan-2-yl)pentan-1-amine, 2-methyl-N-phenethyl-4-phenylbutan-2-amine, N-benzyl-2-methyl-4-phenylbutan-2-amine, N-(4-methoxybenzyl)-2-methyl-4-phenylbutan-2-amine, N-(3-methoxybenzyl)-2-methyl-4-phenylbutan-2-amine, 2-methyl-N-(4-methylbenzyl)-4-phenylbutan-2-amine, 2-methyl-N-(3-methylbenzyl)-4-phenylbutan-2-amine, 2-methyl-N-(naphthalen-2-ylmethyl)-4-phenylbutan-2-amine, ethyl 4-(3-(benzylamino)-3-methylbutyl)benzoate or 1-(1,1'-biphenyl-4-yl)-N-propylpentan-3-amine.

AE SUZHOU OUFEI NANOMETER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2018647325

An output end of the gas mixing device is in communication with the cell culture bag, and an output end of the cell culture bag is provided with a one-way valve. The central controller dynamically adjusts opening degree of each proportional valve according to setting parameters of carbon dioxide, nitrogen, air and oxygen, so that the carbon dioxide cylinder, the nitrogen cylinder, the air cylinder and the oxygen cylinder are conveyed to gas mixing device according to a controlled flow rate. The mixed gas in the gas mixing device is continuously charged into cell culture bag in real time. A check valve discharges the mixed gas in the cell culture bag when the pressure of the mixed gas in the cell culture bag reaches a threshold.

The sum of the content (vol.%) of the carbon dioxide with respect to the total volume of the shield gas and content (vol.%) of the hydrogen with respect to the total volume of the shield gas is 1.30-4.40. The ratio of the content (vol.%) of the hydrogen with respect to the total volume of the shield gas, with respect to the sum of the content (vol.%) of the carbon dioxide with respect to the total volume of the shield gas and content (vol.%) of the hydrogen with respect to the total volume of the shield gas is 0.35-0.74.

TI METHOD FOR DETERMINING SPARKLING PROPERTIES OF CARBON DIOXIDE-SATURATED BEVERAGES.
AU MAKAROV A S
   LUTKOV I P
AE AS RUSSIA MAGARACH VITICULTURE & WINEMAK (ASMA-Non-standard)
GA 2021F0349L
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to the wine industry for the determination of sparkling properties in beverages saturated with CO2. A method for determining the sparkling properties of beverages saturated with CO2 includes pre-sampling; cleaning the bottle from labels; thermostat control for 1 hour at 20C; weighing a bottle with a sparkling drink, an aphrometer with a tap, a foam trap with a gas outlet tube, a gasket and a universal case; measuring the excess pressure of CO2 in the bottle; characterized in that after the excess CO2 pressure is reset to atmospheric pressure by gradually opening the aphrometer tap (preventing the release of foam), with the tap fully open under thermostatic conditions, the weight of the beverage bottle is determined on precision scales of high accuracy (class II) at regular intervals with an interval of 1 min for an hour and the construction of a CO2 desorption curve, where time in minutes is set along the abscissa axis, and the mass of carbon dioxide released (g) on the ordinate axis, makes it possible to cope with the above problem. During the measurements, the sparkling properties coefficient (K) is used as an indicator characterizing the sparkling properties of the drink, calculated by the formula: K = C:V, where C is the total carbon dioxide content in the sample of the drink (g), V is the rate of carbon dioxide desorption over the selected time interval (g/min).
   USE - Wine industry.
   ADVANTAGE - Invention ensures a more accurate measurement when using standard laboratory equipment.
