TI Preparation of 1.5 nm thickness bismuth nanowire            involves adding bismuth into glycol ether solution,            ultrasonically stirring, placing obtained solution into            oil bath, reacting, dispersing, heating, cooling,            filtering, washing and drying.
AU XU W
   YANG F
   RUAN M
   SONG P
   YANG J
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 2019A0504M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of 1.5 nm thickness bismuth                nanowire comprises adding bismuth into glycol ether                solution, ultrasonically stirring to obtain clear                solution, placing obtained solution into oil bath,                reacting under the protection of inert gas,                dispersing, heating at 60-80 degrees C, stirring                and reacting, adding sodium iodine solution,                heating at 140-160 degrees C, cooling at normal                temperature, placing into ultrasonic instrument,                ultrasonically stirring, quickly adding reducing                liquid sodium borohydride to form black gray                solution, filtering, washing and drying.
   USE - Method for preparing 1.5 nm thickness bismuth                nanowire.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can obtain product with carbon                dioxide electro-catalytic reduction, - 0.48 V to -                0.98 V spectral window and 85% Faraday efficiency                of generating formic acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                application of 1.5 nm bismuth nanowire in                electro-catalysis reduction of carbon dioxide                comprises dividing proton exchange film into                three-electrode electrolytic cell, mixing obtained                bismuth nanowire powder, ethanol and sodium                fluoride ion solution, ultrasonically dispersing,                uniformly coating into carbon paper as working                electrode, using platinum sheet as counter                electrode and saturated calomel electrode as                reference electrode, adding electrolyte solution                into cathode groove and anode groove, introducing                carbon dioxide, saturating, continuously                introducing carbon dioxide under constant potential                reduction, and setting electric potential control                range of constant potential reduction process into                - 0.18 V to - 1.18 V/RHE for 100 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: The method comprises                  reacting at 80 degrees C for 30-40 minutes.                  Preferred Components: The electrolyte solution is                  sodium bicarbonate, potassium carbonate or sodium                  sulfate solution.

PN DE102017203903-A1; WO2018162202-A1; AU2018232323-A1; CN110382741-A; EP3577255-A1; AU2018232323-B2; US2020032406-A1
TI Gas diffusion electrode useful in electrolysis of carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide, and in electrolytic cell of electrolysis system, comprises e.g. metal including silver, gold, copper and palladium and their mixtures and/or alloys.
AU KRAUSE R
   RELLER C
   SCHMID B
   SCHMID G
   KRAUS R
AE SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
GA 201872864B
AB    NOVELTY - Gas diffusion electrode comprises a metal M including silver, gold, copper, palladium and their mixtures and/or alloys, and a compound of the metal M having a solubility in water at 25 degrees C and normal pressure of less than 0.1 mol/l.
   USE - The gas diffusion electrode is useful in electrolysis of carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide, and in electrolytic cell of comprising electrolysis system (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The gas diffusion electrode increases carbon dioxide selectivity at high current densities.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) electrolysis of carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide using a gas diffusion electrode as cathode; and
   (2) preparing a gas diffusion electrode comprising mixing a mixture comprising a powder of the metal M and a powder of the compound of the metal M to obtain a gas diffusion electrode, electrochemically treating a gas diffusion electrode comprising the metal M with an agent to forma compound of the metal M having a solubility in water at 25 degrees C and normal pressure of less than 0.1 mol/l, or treating a gas diffusion electrode comprising the metal M with a gaseous agent to form a compound of the metal M having a solubility in water at 25 degrees C and normal pressure of less than 0.1 mol/l.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The compound of the metal M is inorganic. The metal M in the compound of the metal M has a valence of less than 2. The compound of metal M is of formulae (M1-xX) (I), (M2-yY1) (II), (M2-yY'w) (III) and (M3-zZ) (IV), preferably molybdates, tungstates, selenates, arsenates, vanadates, chromates, manganates, niobates of metal M and thio and/or seleno derivatives of molybdates, tungstates, selenates, arsenates, vanadates, chromates, manganates, niobates of metal M, and/or (MaXbY2cZd) (V). The compound of the metal M has a redox potential with respect to the standard hydrogen electrode at a pH of 7 at 25 degrees C and normal pressure which is below that of silver oxide. The gas diffusion electrode further comprises a polymer binder which is modified with silver ion bonding groups.x = 0-0.5;y = 0-1;z = 0-1.5;X = Cl, Br, Br3, I, I3, P3, As3, As5, As7, Sb3, Sb5 and/or Sb7;Y1 = S, S and/or Te;Y' = S, Se and/or Te;w = less than or equal to 2; andZ = P, As, Sb, Bi, P3, As3, As5, As7, Sb3, Sb5, and/or Sb7;a = greater than or equal to 2;b, d = 0-4; andc = 0-8.Provided at least two of b and c are not simultaneously 0. Preferred Method: The method comprises mixing a mixture comprising the powder of the metal M and the powder of the compound of the metal M to obtain a gas diffusion electrode, where the gas diffusion electrode is activated after the production. The activation is carried out by treatment with a reducing agent in a solvent, preferably at 20-200 degrees C, or the activation is carried out with a reducing gas or gas mixture; and adding at least one binder, preferably a polymer binder to the gas diffusion electrode, or mixing at least one binder, preferably a polymer binder with the mixture comprising the powder of the metal M and the powder of the compound of the metal M to obtain a gas diffusion electrode from the mixture.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AU YAN Z
AE SICHUAN HEXI NONGFU MINERAL WATER IND CO (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2018335828
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a mineral water sterilizing device, relating to drinking water disinfection equipment field, comprising a water inlet pipe, a gas-liquid mixing pump, a carbon dioxide gas, a water inlet tank, a water storage tank, a water inlet pipe connected with the gas-liquid mixing pump. carbon dioxide air bottle by the air inlet pipe is connected with gas-liquid mixing pump, a gas-liquid mixing pump is set on the mixing pump of the water inlet tank, a water tank is provided with an active carbon filtering layer, a sterilizing fibre layer, bottom of the water inlet box is provided with a first outlet, the first outlet is provided with a water outlet pipe; the inner cavity of the water tank by ultraviolet glass plate is divided into a sterilizing chamber of the ultraviolet emitting cavity of the upper part and the lower part, the top of the ultraviolet emitting cavity is installed with several ultraviolet lamp, in the sterilization chamber from the top down orderly set with photocatalyst sterilization net, an active carbon filter layer, the utility model can quickly reduce the calcium content in the mineral water, and it adopts ultraviolet sterilizing, fast and efficient and in the sterilizing process does not generate the derived harmful bacteria to cause secondary pollution.

PT P
PN IN394502-B
TI Preparing methanol from coal using external carbon            dioxide by employing gasifier in two different modes in            methanol plant by e.g. feeding coal and compressed air            to fluidized bed gasifier operating in airblown mode            and gasifying high ash.
AU KOTDAWALA R
   ITTAGUNTA N M
   CHINTALA T N
   DAGANI S M R
   CHELLAPAN T
AE BHARAT HEAVY ELECTRICALS LTD (BHAR-C)
GA 202255535Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing methanol from coal using any                external carbon dioxide by employing a gasifier in                two different modes in a methanol plant, comprises                e.g.: (i) feeding coal and compressed air to a                fluidized bed gasifier operating in airblown mode                at 950 degrees C and pressure in the range of 5-25                bars; (ii) gasifying high ash Indian coal with                25-40% ash content for generating syngas, the                syngas from gasifier system is fed to a hydrogen                sulfide removal unit where hydrogen sulfide is                removed using (methyl diethanolamine) based                absorption system; (iii) transferring syngas to                water gas shift reactor (WGSR) where the syngas is                passed for 6 hours to carry out reduction of WGSR                catalysts (based catalysts) at 350 degrees C along                with steam; and (iv) passing of conditioned syngas                into a carbon dioxide capture system after passing                through a cooler to reduce the temperature of the                syngas to 40 degrees C, for removal of carbon                dioxide into mono ethanolamine solution                (MEA).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing methanol                from coal using any external carbon dioxide by                employing a gasifier in two different modes in a                methanol plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is self-reliant and efficient                method of startup of coal to methanol plant;                utilize carbon dioxide free syngas available from                air blown mode gasification for the reduction of                catalyst for the methanol reactor; and provides                efficient method not only for starting of coal to                methanol plant to reduce startup time but also to                obliterate any requirement for using carbon dioxide                as an input, thus designing a cost-effective                solution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing methanol from coal using any                external carbon dioxide by employing a gasifier in                two different modes in a methanol plant, comprises:                (i) feeding coal and compressed air to a fluidized                bed gasifier operating in airblown mode at 950                degrees C and pressure in the range of 5-25 bars;                (ii) gasifying high ash Indian coal with 25-40% ash                content for generating syngas, the syngas from                gasifier system is fed to a hydrogen sulfide                removal unit where hydrogen sulfide is removed                using (methyl diethanolamine) based absorption                system; (iii) transferring syngas to water gas                shift reactor (WGSR) where the syngas is passed for                6 hours to carry out reduction of WGSR catalysts                (based catalysts) at 350 degrees C along with                steam; (iv) passing of conditioned syngas into a                carbon dioxide capture system after passing through                a cooler to reduce the temperature of the syngas to                40 degrees C, for removal of carbon dioxide into                mono ethanolamine solution (MEA), the conditioned                syngas from carbon dioxide absorber is mixed with                pure nitrogen in proportion of 1:10                (syngas:nitrogen), heated to 210 degrees C and into                the methanol reactor for reduction of methanol                catalyst; (v) reducing the methanol catalyst at 210                degrees C for 24 hours; (vi) heating the carbon                dioxide rich MEA solution from carbon dioxide                absorber 100 degrees C in cross heat exchanger and                transferring to carbon dioxide stripper where it is                heated using reboiler at 110 degrees C, where                stripped carbon dioxide gas from solution is                condensed, compressed and subsequently to carbon                dioxide tank for storage to be utilized in oxyblown                mode for methanol production; and (Vii) shifting                the operation of the fluidized bed gasifier to                oxyblown mode for methanol production after 24                hours, reduction process of methanol catalyst and                storing carbon dioxide in carbon dioxide tank in                the airblown mode, characterized in that compressed                air is used as gasifying agent in the airblown mode                and pure oxygen is used as a gasifying agent in the                oxyblown mode of the gasifier.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide                  obtained from the downstream process in the                  airblown mode of syngas generation is fed back to                  the gasifier to act as readily available                  fluidizing medium in the oxyblown mode along with                  steam. The syngas generated under airblown mode                  is used to reduce water gas shift reactor                  catalyst and methanol catalyst simultaneously.                  The methanol catalyst in the reduced form,                  obtained during airblown gasification is used to                  produce methanol directly by reducing startup                  time of the plant by more than 36 hours. The air                  is supplied from compressor of air separation                  unit (ASU) at 6 bar and 150 degrees C into                  gasifier to carry out airblown gasification. The                  steam is injected into the WGSR at 350 degrees C                  and in the ratio, steam to syngas 0.12. The                  method comprises 10-15% syngas from the carbon                  dioxide capture system is mixed with pure                  nitrogen in the ratio, 1:10 (syngas: nitrogen),                  heated to 210 degrees C and to the methanol                  reactor for catalyst reduction while the rest of                  the syngas is vented. Preferred Components: The                  methanol catalyst is a copper oxide or Zinc oxide                  based catalyst. The carbon dioxide capture system                  removes 95% of carbon dioxide from syngas.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); Q73 (Combustion equipment/processes (F23))

PN CN113830874-A
TI Adding carbonic acid by using carbonic acid solution dosing system e.g. maintaining certain temperature and pressure by carbon dioxide gas, installing water quality online detector at downstream of water flow in raw water, and processing signal received by signal receiver to control opening size.
AU LIN X
AE HAWEI SHANGHAI ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (HAWE-Non-standard)
GA 202208496X
AB    NOVELTY - Adding carbonic acid by using carbonic acid solution dosing system comprises (1) establishing kind of carbonic acid solution dosing system, (2) maintaining certain temperature and certain pressure by the carbon dioxide gas, and transporting to the mixer through the carbon dioxide gas pipeline, (3) entering the carbon dioxide gas with certain temperature and certain pressure to the mixer through the carbon dioxide gas filling port located in the middle throat part of the venturi tube, and (4) installing water quality online detector at the downstream of the water flow in the raw water or the water pipeline or at the water outlet of the pool, pond or sewage tank, connecting the signal receiver with the input end of the programmable logic controller (PLC) controller, and processing the signal received by the signal receiver by PLC to control the opening size of the carbon dioxide gas flow control valve to control the amount of carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - The method is useful for adding carbonic acid by using carbonic acid solution dosing system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can precisely adjust the water quality; and at the same time can greatly reduce the generation of air bubbles and greatly improve the utilization rate of carbon dioxide gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Adding carbonic acid by using carbonic acid solution dosing system comprises (1) establishing kind of carbonic acid solution dosing system, (2) maintaining certain temperature and certain pressure by the carbon dioxide gas, and transporting to the mixer through the carbon dioxide gas pipeline, (3) entering the carbon dioxide gas with certain temperature and certain pressure to the mixer through the carbon dioxide gas filling port located in the middle throat part of the venturi tube, at the same time, pressurizing the reaction water to certain pressure by the water pump and then enters the mixer, mixing carbon dioxide gas with pressurized reaction water to form gas-water mixture with a certain pressure in the mixer, forming the gas-water mixture with saturated carbonic acid solution through the annular coil or liquid storage tank between the mixer and the diffuser, sending the saturated carbonic acid solution to the diffuser to form supersaturated carbonic acid solution through long-distance pipeline, passing the supersaturated carbonic acid solution through the small holes and/or narrow gaps in the diffuser to form mixture of high-concentration carbonic acid solution and small amount of carbon dioxide microbubbles, which is sprayed into the water in reverse, meeting the carbonic acid solution to the alkaline substances in the water and begins acid-base neutralization reaction, and absorbing small amount of carbon dioxide microbubbles by the water flow in order to achieve the purpose of neutralizing acid and alkali, and (4) installing water quality online detector at the downstream of the water flow in the raw water or the water pipeline or at the water outlet of the pool, pond or sewage tank, connecting the water quality online detector with the signal receiver, connecting the signal receiver with the input end of the programmable logic controller (PLC) controller, connecting the output end of the PLC controller with the carbon dioxide gas flow control valve, transmitting the signal to the signal receiver in real time by the water quality online detector, processing the signal received by the signal receiver by PLC to control the opening size of the carbon dioxide gas flow control valve to control the amount of carbon dioxide gas.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); T01 (Digital Computers); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)

PN CN113772673-A; CN215924412-U
TI Process system for recovering and purifying carbon dioxide from lime kiln tail gas, has purification system that is connected with two-stage pressurized cooling system, and product gas storage tank connected with purification system.
AU MO P
   HOU R
   XU Z
   YUAN W
   LI H
AE CHENGDU HUAXI CHEM IND SCI & TECHNOLOGY (CHEN-Non-standard)
   CHENGDU YIZHI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2021E9594K
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a dust collector (1) for removing dust in exhaust gas, and a section of a pressurized cooling system connected with the dust collector. A gas-liquid separator (5) is connected to a first-stage pressurized cooling system. A first-stage purification and pressurization system is connected to the gas-liquid separator and used for further dehydration and purification. A pressure swing adsorption system is connected to the first-stage purification and pressurization system and used for carbon dioxide adsorption. An intermediate product gas buffer tank (12) is connected to the pressure swing adsorption system. A two-stage pressurized cooling system is connected to the intermediate product gas buffer tank. A purification system is connected with the two-stage pressurized cooling system. A product gas storage tank (19) is connected with the purification system.
   USE - Process system for recovering and purifying carbon dioxide from lime kiln tail gas in steel plant. Uses include but are not limited to carbon dioxide used as industrial raw material for food processing, fresh-keeping, beverages, fertilizers, fire prevention, oil development and other fields.
   ADVANTAGE - The system realizes the purification of carbon dioxide by different ways, which greatly improves the purity of the recovered carbon dioxide, and realizes the industrial recovery of the carbon dioxide gas in the tail gas of lime kiln.

PN CN113849762-A
TI Method for calculating air leakage in closed goaf, involves stopping liquid carbon dioxide injection when carbon dioxide concentration of first receiving point and second receiving point rises from beginning to steady fall, and record stabilization time.
AU WANG D
   SUN J
   LIU J
   HOU Y
   ZHANG L
   YU T
   ZHANG Y
   GAO L
   MA X
   BAO X
AE UNIV ANHUI JIANZHU (UYAH-C)
   YANZHOU COAL MINING CO LTD (YZCM-C)
GA 202208001L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves setting a release point on the air inlet side of the gob, and set two receiving points in the downstream direction of the wind flow in the gob, which provides the first receiving point and the second receiving point. The liquid carbon dioxide is continuously released at the release point on the air inlet side of the goaf at a stable rate, and continuously monitor at the first receiving point and the second receiving point. The liquid carbon dioxide injection is stopped when the carbon dioxide concentration of the first receiving point and the second receiving point rises from the beginning to the steady fall, and record the stabilization time. The samples were taken at 1-minute intervals from the two receiving points, and a handheld self-priming carbon dioxide detector was used for direct detection, and the detection time and carbon dioxide concentration value were recorded.
   USE - Method for calculating air leakage in closed goaf.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts liquid carbon dioxide to replace SF6, which is very economical, and the cost is greatly reduced.The method is released in a large area, and the test method is effective using liquid carbon dioxide to calculate the air leakage. The calculation result is more accurate than SF6 single measuring point.

AE CHINA NUCLEAR POWER DESIGN INST (CNNU-C)
GA 2020A1225W
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a cylinder cooling structure for cooling a cylinder block in vicinity of a dry gas seal in a turbine cylinder. The cooling structure configures a cooling branch section for carbon dioxide cooling gas, and configures a protective branch section for providing carbon dioxide protective gas to an area near a turbine bearing at same time. PN KR2020113123-A; KR2209865-B1
TI Chlorine and carbon dioxide emission kit for maintaining the freshness of food for a long time, includes composition from which chlorous acid source is released and the source from which carbon dioxide is released are independently sealed.
AU HEE L D
   CHUNG M Y
AE HEE L D (HEEL-Individual)
GA 2020978750
AB    NOVELTY - Chlorine and carbon dioxide emission kit includes composition from which the chlorous acid source is released and the source from which carbon dioxide is released are independently sealed. The composition is stored in a storage container without moisture, but the chlorite source composition contains 50-150 pts. wt. chlorite salts and organic acids based on 100 pts. wt. of ceramic powder. The carbon dioxide source composition is composed of the entire amount of ammonium carbonate alone, or a carbonate based on 100 pts. wt., organic acid is 50-150 wt.% by weight and hygroscopic material is 1.5-4.5 wt.%. The hydrogel polymer is applied to be stored in a mixture of 0.25-1.0 wt.%, and the amount of contact of the chlorous acid source composition and the carbon dioxide source composition with moisture in the air in the storage space is controlled to release chlorous acid gas.
   USE - Chlorine and carbon dioxide emission kit for maintaining the freshness of food for a long time.
   ADVANTAGE - The chlorine and carbon dioxide emission kit provides the effect of maintaining the freshness of food for a long period of time regardless of the type of food by a sustained release kit that discharges chlorous acid and carbon dioxide for maintaining food freshness controlled by a small amount.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The ceramic powder used in the chlorous acid source composition is elvan stone, ocher stone, olivine, kaolin, metakaolin, silica mineral, and diatomite having a size of 50 micrometer or less, wollastonite, pyrophyllite, dolomite, lithium minerals, magnesite, bauxite, bentonite, volcanic ash, pumice, borate, garnet, carbonate minerals, attapulgite, zeolite, perlite or sepiolite. The organic acids used in the chlorous acid source composition and the carbon dioxide source composition are succinic acid, tartaric acid, maleic acid, citric acid, and sulfomic acid, oxalic acid, fumaric acid, malic acid, tartaric acid, ascorbic acid, and sulfinic acid. The hygroscopic material used in the chlorous acid source composition and the carbon dioxide source composition is sodium chloride, calcium chloride or silica gel. The hydrogel polymer used in the chlorous acid source composition and the carbon dioxide source composition is agar, collagen peptide, hyaluronic acid, starch, hydrolyzed starch, cross-linked starch, dextrin, carrageenan, arabic gum or xanthan gum. The carbonate used in the carbon dioxide source composition is sodium bicarbonate, sodium carbonate, potassium bicarbonate, potassium carbonate or ammonium bicarbonate.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AB    NOVELTY - Composite catalyst comprises a combination of                a heterogeneous catalyst and a cyclic                anhydride.
   USE - The composite catalyst is useful for preparing                carbon dioxide-based copolymer in biodegradable                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite catalyst: can make the polymer                chain end of the growth is kept as carboxylic acid                metal end group without back bite; replace the                conventional catalyst catalytic epoxide and carbon                dioxide to form easy-back carboxy metal end groups;                improves the conversion of the epoxide; and reduces                the generation amount of the by-product in the                product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   a carbon dioxide-based copolymer, comprising                composite catalyst; and
   a preparing the carbon dioxide-based                copolymer, comprising carrying out polymerization                reaction on the composite catalyst, epoxide and                carbon dioxide in a solvent to obtain the final                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The heterogeneous                  catalyst comprises at least two of zinc                  dicarboxylate catalyst, a rare earth ternary                  catalyst and/or a bimetallic catalyst,                  preferably, the cyclic anhydride comprises maleic                  anhydride, succinic anhydride, glutaric                  anhydride, 3-ethyl-3-methylglutaric anhydride,                  cyclobutane-1, 2-dicarboxylic anhydride, itaconic                  anhydride, methyl succinic anhydride, allyl                  succinic anhydride, cyclopentane-1,                  2-dicarboxylic anhydride, 4-methyl-1,                  2-cyclohexanedicarboxylic anhydride, nadic                  anhydride, methyl-5-norbornene-2, 3-dicarboxylic                  anhydride, phthalic anhydride, 1,2,3,                  6-tetrahydrophthalic anhydride, 3,4,5,                  6-tetrahydrophthalic anhydride,                  methyltetrahydrophthalic anhydride, 1,                  2-cyclohexanedicarboxylic anhydride, dodecyl                  succinic anhydride or 2,2' -biphenyl dicarboxylic                  anhydride. preferably, the                  heterogeneous catalyst in the composite catalyst                  is a zinc dicarboxylate catalyst, and the initial                  concentration of the composite catalyst in the                  polymerization reaction is 2-80 &#181;g/ml,                  preferably 6-70 mg/ml and more preferably 10-60                  &#181;g/ml, preferably, the heterogeneous The carbon                  dioxide-based copolymer is specifically prepared                  by (i) mixing the composite catalyst, a part of                  solvent and a part of epoxide, and introducing                  carbon dioxide to perform prepolymerization                  reaction to obtain a prepolymer, and (ii) adding                  the residual solvent and residual epoxide into                  the resultant prepolymer and introducing carbon                  dioxide to carry out polymerization reaction to                  obtain the product, preferably, in the step (ii)                  the pressure of the system after the carbon                  dioxide is introduced is 4-10 &#181;Pa.

PT P
PN TW202002762-A; US2021100172-A1; TW714291-B1
TI ACTIVE INDOOR PLANTING DYNAMIC CARBON DIOXIDE CONCENTRATION ADJUSTMENT SYSTEM.
AU CHI C
AE ALLTON COMMUNICATION CO (ALLT-Non-standard)
   ALLTON COMMUNICATION CO (ALLT-Non-standard)
GA 2020C7138K
AB    NOVELTY - An active indoor planting dynamic carbon dioxide concentration adjustment system includes a planting assembly, multiple variable light intensity illuminating devices, a carbon dioxide sensor and a controller. The planting assembly is disposed in an indoor space, and the illuminating device is adapted to illuminate the planting assembly according to a control signal to cause photosynthesis of the planting assembly. The carbon dioxide sensor is adapted to detect a concentration of carbon dioxide in the indoor space and to emit a concentration signal. The controller is signally connected to the illuminating devices and the carbon dioxide sensor, and is adapted to receive the concentration signal and give the control signal. The controller dynamically adjusts the light intensity of the illuminating devices according to the concentration signal, thereby dynamically adjusting the efficiency of the photosynthesis of the planting assembly.

TI Production of monocyanamide by adding mother liquor to synthesis kettle, passing through carbon dioxide, controlling pressure, adding lime nitrogen, controlling temperature, continuously passing through carbon dioxide, and heat preserving.
AU ZHANG C
   LIANG W
   LIU G
   CUI W
   ZHANG N
   CAO B
AE SHANDONG EFIRM BIOCHEMISTRY & ENVIRONMEN (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202006792S
AB    NOVELTY - Production of monocyanamide comprises adding mother liquor to synthesis kettle, passing through carbon dioxide, controlling pressure at 0.2-1 MPa, adding lime nitrogen, controlling temperature at less than or equal to 30 degrees C and pH at less than or equal to 8.5, continuously passing through carbon dioxide, heat preserving, releasing pressure, unloading and filtering. The mother liquid is washing water or industrial water used after washing monocyanamide solution or monocyanamide residue.
   USE - The method is for production of monocyandiamide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces unit consumption of carbon dioxide by improving hydrogen cyanamide production process and absorbing carbon dioxide with positive pressure to produce hydrogen cyanamide, and has improved reaction efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lime nitrogen has effective nitrogen content of greater than or equal to 20%. The ratio of lime nitrogen feed rate and carbon dioxide flux is 0.5-3:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The monocyandiamide solution has monocyandiamide content of 5-9%. The ratio of mother liquor and lime nitrogen is 2-6:1. Preferred Method: The heat preserving is done for 0-45 minutes.

PN CN110152454-A
TI Ternary non-aqueous phase absorption system used for capturing carbon dioxide and absorbing pure carbon dioxide or mixed gas, comprises 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol, hydroxyethylethylenediamine and n-methylpyrrolidone.
AU LV B
   JING G
   ZHOU X
   ZHOU Z
AE UNIV HUAQIAO (UYHQ-C)
GA 201977329A
AB    NOVELTY - A ternary non-aqueous phase absorption system comprises 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol, hydroxyethylethylenediamine and n-methylpyrrolidone. The molar ratio of 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol and hydroxyethylethylenediamine is 4-6:1. The concentration of 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol is 1-3 mol/l.
   USE - Ternary non-aqueous phase absorption system is useful for capturing carbon dioxide and absorbing pure carbon dioxide or mixed gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The ternary non-aqueous phase absorption system has high carbon dioxide absorption rate, and can be prepared in energy-efficient manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of ternary non-aqueous phase absorption system for capturing carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol and hydroxyethylethylenediamine is 5:1. Preferred Method: The application method involves weighing 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol as main absorbent, hydroxyethylethylenediamine as carbon dioxide absorption enhancer and n-methylpyrrolidone as solvent, placing in 5-20 vol.% pure carbon dioxide or mixed gas, absorbing using 1.4-1.8 mol absorbent at 30-60 degrees C under viscosity of 1.2-3 mPa.second or 5-8 MPa.second and performing thermal desorption at 100-120 degrees C for 80-100 minutes.

PN WO2022204766-A1
TI Recovering lithium carbonate from solution            containing mixture of lithium sulfate and lithium            hydroxide, involves precipitating lithium carbonate            from solution containing mixture of lithium sulfate and            lithium hydroxide through addition of carbon            dioxide.
AU URBANI M D
   JOHNSON G D
   VINES N J
AE LI TECHNOLOGY PTY LTD (LITE-Non-standard)
GA 2022C7606N
AB    NOVELTY - Process for recovering lithium carbonate from                a solution containing a mixture of lithium sulfate                and lithium hydroxide, involves precipitating the                lithium carbonate from the solution containing a                mixture of lithium sulfate and lithium hydroxide                through the addition of carbon dioxide.
   USE - The process is used for recovering lithium                carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The process recovers lithium carbonate in cost                effective manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves                  passing a slurry resulting from the precipitation                  of lithium carbonate to solid liquid separation                  to remove entrained liquor from the lithium                  carbonate. The slurry resulting from the                  precipitation of lithium carbonate is passed to                  solid liquid separation, and subsequent washing,                  to remove entrained liquor from the lithium                  carbonate. The liquor from solid liquid                  separation and/or washing containing lithium                  sulfate and some minor impurities. The liquor,                  containing lithium sulfate, is directed to a                  process to convert lithium sulfate to lithium                  hydroxide and thus improving the recovery of                  lithium. The lithium carbonate is precipitated at                  atmospheric pressure. The lithium carbonate is                  precipitated at a temperature of ambient to                  100&#176; C. The lithium carbonate is                  precipitated at greater than 50&#176; C,                  preferably 50-100&#176; C. The lithium                  precipitation occurs over a residence time of 2                  hours. The lithium precipitation achieves a                  precipitation extent of 90% of the stoichiometric                  mass of lithium hydroxide contained in the                  solution containing both lithium sulfate and                  lithium hydroxide. The lithium carbonate is                  precipitated with the addition of in excess of                  stoichiometric carbon dioxide addition. The                  process involves (a) precipitation of lithium                  carbonate from a solution containing lithium                  sulfate and lithium hydroxide through the                  addition of carbon dioxide, (b) solid liquid                  separation and preferably washing of the                  precipitated lithium carbonate to recover a                  liquor containing lithium sulfate, (c) recycle                  the liquor to a subsequent step to convert                  lithium sulfate to lithium hydroxide, (d)                  purification of the lithium carbonate by pulping                  in water and addition of carbon dioxide to                  produce lithium bicarbonate solution, (e) solid                  liquid separation to remove insoluble impurities,                  (f) heating the lithium bicarbonate solution to                  strip carbon dioxide and re precipitate lithium                  carbonate, and (g) solid liquid separation,                  drying and packing of the purified lithium                  carbonate product. The lithium carbonate                  precipitation step (a) is operated at greater                  than 50&#176; C. The lithium carbonate                  precipitation step (i) involves the addition of                  in excess of stoichiometric carbon dioxide                  addition.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)

AU LI X
   LI J
AE ZHEJIANG JINRUN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
   ZHEJIANG JINRUN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 201994991W

   USE - The method is useful for removing base catalyst (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of effectively removing the base catalyst in the reaction system, without causing damage to the product, and provides zero emissions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Introduction amount of carbon dioxide is controlled by the pH value of the reaction solution, and stopping introducing carbon dioxide when the pH value of the reaction solution is 6.8-7.2. Carbon dioxide is introduced from the bottom of the reaction device and is stirred at the same time. The supernatant is removed after filtration or centrifugation. Fltration is a pressure filtration or suction filtration. Preferred Components: The base catalyst comprises one of sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, sodium methoxide and sodium ethoxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic reaction system is an ester exchange reaction system, an ester alcohol exchange reaction system or an alcohol polymerization reaction system. The ester exchange reaction system comprises an ester exchange reaction between oil and oil; where the ester-alcohol exchange reaction system comprises an ester-alcohol exchange reaction between oil and alcohol substances; and the alcohol-alcohol polymerization reaction system comprises a glycerol polymerization reaction. The organic reaction system is an organic reaction system after the reaction of raw materials; where the organic reaction system does not contain water. Preferred Condition: The temperature of the organic reaction system is less than or equal to 100 degrees C.

   ADVANTAGE - The method does not require solvent and external co-catalyst. The catalyst has good stability and can be recycled.

The method of preparing polyionic liquid catalyst specifically comprises dissolving amino ionic liquid 1-vinyl-3-ethylaminoimidazolium bromide and a quaternary ammonium ionic liquid vinylbenzyltriethylammonium chloride as an active component and polyethylene terephthalate as skeleton in dimethyl sulfoxide system, and adding an initiator azobisisobutyronitrile (AIBN) under the nitrogen atmosphere to carry out reflux reaction. The reflux reaction time is 12-48 hours. The reaction for catalytically converting carbon dioxide to prepare cyclic carbonate is carried out at 50-70 degrees C, under atmospheric pressure. In the method, the reaction time is 24-36 hours.

PN CN108497054-A
TI Controlling pests and diseases during storage of            ginger comprises fumigating prepared ginger with            Bacillus lentus 12a, or soaking in a fermentation            filtrate of Bacillus licheniformis 14 b.
AU DAN J
   LIU X
   WANG Q
   ZHANG X
   PENG Y
   LIU P
   MA Y
   YIN X
AE UNIV SHANDONG AGRIC (UYSD-C)
GA 201872526M
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling pests and diseases during storage                of ginger comprises fumigating prepared ginger with                Bacilluslentus 12a, or soaking in a fermentation                filtrate of Bacillus licheniformis14 b for 15                minutes.
   USE - The method is useful for controlling pests and                diseases during storage of ginger.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prevents high carbon dioxide                concentration from causing high carbon dioxide                damage to ginger.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises fermenting the ginger or Bacillus                  licheniformis14b fermentation filtrate with                  Bacilluslentus 12a of soaked ginger, naturally                  drying at room temperature and placing in a gas                  transfer box so that the gas ratio in the gas                  transfer box is O2:CO2= 9:1. The temperature in                  the atmosphere box is 12&#176; C. The                  preparation of the fermentation filtrate of                  Bacillus licheniformis14b comprises (a) taking                  preserved Bacillus licheniformis14b and streaking                  to NA medium: wort solid medium = 1:1 mixed                  medium, placing in an incubator at 32&#176; C                  constant temperature moisturizing culture for 24                  hours; (b) choosing a well-developed antagonist                  strain of Bacillus licheniformis14b single colony                  and inoculating into NB liquid medium: wort                  liquid medium = 1:1 mixed medium, incubating at                  32&#176; C of 60% medium for 48 hours, where                  the oscillation frequency is 130-180                  revolutions/minute (rpm); (c) inoculating the                  seed solution of the strain 14b prepared in the                  step (b) into the NB wort mixed liquid culture                  solution at a volume ratio of 5% by inoculum, and                  the loading amount is 50-100 ml/250 ml, where the                  initial pH of the medium is 7, fermenting at                  32&#176; C, where the oscillation frequency                  is 130-180 rpm, and fermenting for 48 hours,                  where the light intensity is 60% to prepare the                  Bacillus licheniformis14b fermentation broth; and                  (d) centrifuging the Bacillus licheniformis14b                  fermentation broth prepared in the step (c) at                  8000 rpm for 10-20 minutes, taking the                  supernatant and filtering through a microporous                  membrane filter of D=0.22 &#956; m, diluting                  the fermentation filtrate stock solution with the                  fermentation filtrate to 10-100 times with                  sterile water to prepare a fermentation filtrate                  of Bacillus licheniformis14b. The method for                  preparing the Bacilluslentus 12a comprises                  preserving Bacilluslentus 12a and inoculating                  into NB liquid medium: wort liquid medium = 1:1                  mixed medium and performing shaking culture for                  40-50 hurs, and then irradiating under UV light                  for 2 minutes. The method for fumigation of                  ginger by the Bacilluslentus 12a bacteris is:                  placing the Bacilluslentus 12a bacterial liquid                  on the lower layer of the separator of the sealed                  can, placing the upper layer of the sealed can                  with ginger, where can lid is sealed, and                  carrying out the fumigation for 20-30 minutes.                  The prepared ginger is washed by clean water, and                  absorbing the ginger by gauze surface excess                  water.

TI Forming solution of ammonium carbamate comprises            providing reactor comprising solution of ammonia,            feeding carbon dioxide through the solution of ammonia            to form mixture, and combining solution of sodium            hydroxide and the mixture.
AU COUTINHO C
   MITCHELL M
   VINCIGUERRA S
   KOTINO S
   VINCHIGUERRA S
AE SOLENIS TECHNOLOGIES LP (SOLS-C)
   SOLENIS TECHNOLOGIES LP (SOLS-C)
GA 201950598N
AB    NOVELTY - Forming a solution of ammonium carbamate                comprises: providing a reactor comprising a                solution of ammonia; feeding carbon dioxide through                the solution of ammonia to form a mixture; and                combining a solution of sodium hydroxide and the                mixture to form ammonium carbamate.
   USE - The method is useful for forming a solution of                ammonium carbamate (claimed).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The step of feeding                  carbon dioxide is further defined as feeding many                  bubbles comprising gaseous carbon dioxide through                  the solution of ammonia to form the mixture with                  the plurality of bubbles comprising at least 50                  vol.% carbon dioxide based on a total volume of                  the bubbles. The method further comprises: the                  step of forming the bubbles having a surface area                  to volume ratio of at least 8.100 m2/m3, where                  the bubbles comprise at least 95 vol.% carbon                  dioxide; the step of monitoring an amount of                  carbon dioxide fed through the solution of                  ammonia, where the step of monitoring the amount                  of carbon dioxide comprises at least one of the                  steps of determining pH of the mixture,                  determining conductivity of the mixture, and                  determining total heat generation within the                  mixture; and the step of combining water and the                  solution of ammonia prior to the step of feeding                  carbon dioxide. Preferred Components: The                  solution of ammonium carbamate comprises at least                  15 wt.% ammonium carbamate based on a total                  weight of the solution of ammonium carbamate. The                  solution of ammonia comprises at least 5 wt.%                  ammonia. The carbon dioxide is fed through the                  solution of ammonia to an ammonia/carbon dioxide                  molar ratio of 1.6-2.1. The solution of sodium                  hydroxide is combined with the mixture at a                  weight ratio of sodium hydroxide to carbon                  dioxide of 0.54-0.91. The solution of ammonium                  carbamate has a pH of at least 9.2, the solution                  of ammonium carbamate has a conductivity of at                  least 75 mS/cm and/or the solution of ammonium                  carbamate has a nitrogen content of at least                  47200 ppm.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN108676610-A
TI Preparing active substance involves subjecting salmon by-product to one or more subcritical carbon dioxide extractions to obtain cod liver oil, where product of cod liver oil is extracted by subcritical water extraction.
AU GUO J
   SONG W
   CAI L
AE ZHEJIANG HAIRUNDA TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 201884760R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing active substance involves subjecting salmon by-product to one or more subcritical carbon dioxide extractions to obtain cod liver oil.
   USE - Method for preparing active substance.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare active substance in simple manner which has high efficiency, and wide market and application prospect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The product of the cod liver oil is extarcted by subcritical water extraction to obtain a defatted squid protein. The product is obtained by extracting the defatted squid protein is subjected to salt leaching, ammonium sulfate fractionation precipitation and gel chromatography to obtain defatted squid glycoprotein. The portion of the impurities are removed from the salmon byproduct Rinse with deionized water, then remove the outer film and ground to obtain homogenate of the salmon by-product. The cod liver oil is obtained by subcriticalcarbon dioxide extraction of the salmon by-product, one-time or more subcritical carbon dioxide extraction is performed at 20-50 degrees C and 0.5-40 megaPascal for 2-4 hours, where flow rate of carbon dioxide is 5-70 Liters/h. The number of subcritical carbon dioxide extractions is two. The subcritical water extraction is performed to obtain the defatted squid protein, subcritical water extraction is performed at 110-170 degrees C, and 4-20 megaPascal for 0.5-2 hours, and where material to liquid ratio is 1 g:(60-100) mL. The subcritical water extraction to obtain the defatted salmon protein, the product after extracting the cod liver oil is subjected to subcritical water extraction. The product obtained after the subcritical water extraction is subjected to filtration treatment to obtain defatted squid protein aqueous solution and a squid degreased solid, and the defatted squid protein aqueous solution is dried and dehydrated to obtain a defatted squid protein. The dry dehydration treatment is a vacuum concentration and spray drying treatment of the defatted salmon protein aqueous solution to obtain defatted salmon protein, vacuum concentration spray drying treatment is performed at 82.7-90.6 kiloPascals, the defatted squid protein aqueous solution is polymerized into 10 to 200 mu m atomized particles, and is directly contacted with hot air for heat exchange, and dried for 10-15 minutes. The salt in the salt leaching is sodium chloride and/or potassium chloride, preferably sodium chloride. The salt leaching and ammonium sulfate fractionation and defatted squid glycoprotein is obtained by gel chromatography, where squid by-product obtained by the subcritical water extraction to obtain the defatted squid protein is added to the salt solution for leaching to obtain a crude defatted squid glycoprotein. The crude extract of the defatted squid glycoprotein is centrifuged, and the obtained supernatant is subjected to membrane concentration and desalting treatment to obtain a defatted squid glycoprotein concentrate. The defatted squid glycoprotein concentrate is subjected to ammonium sulfate fractionation to obtain defatted squid glycoprotein fractionated purified product. The defatted squid glycoprotein fractionated purified product is added to gel chromatography,to obtain eluate, and dried and dehydrated the eluate to obtain defatted squid glycoprotein. The salt leaching is performed at 65-80 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours, ratio of material to liquid is 1 g: (5-15) mL, where mass percentage concentration of the salt solution is 3-5%. The ammonium sulfate has a saturation of 10 to 40%, 40 to 70%, 70 to 90% in chromatography, the flow rate of the fractionated purified protein of the defatted salmon glycoprotein is controlled at 0.5-1 mL/s. The drying and dehydrating treatment is performed by vacuum concentration and spray drying treatment on the eluate to obtain defatted salmon protein. The vacuum concentration, spray drying treatment is performed at 82.7-90.6 kiloPascals, the defatted squid protein aqueous solution is polymerized into 10 to 200 mu m atomized particles, and is directly contacted with hot air for heat exchange, and dried for 10-15 minutes.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PN CN108499592-A
TI New catalyst for carbon dioxide cycloaddition reaction is prepared by adding carbamide and metalhalide to the mixed solution of ethanol and water, heating above system, stirring the mixture, and then placing mixture system.
AU LIU N
   XU T
   LI Q
   XUE B
   XU J
   LI Y
AE UNIV CHANGZHOU (UNCZ-C)
GA 201872470Y
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst (I), is new.
   USE - Catalyst for carbon dioxide cycloaddition reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has good catalytic performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Catalyst of formula (I), is new.
   n=Cu, Fe or Zn; and
   X=Cl, Br or I.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing catalyst of formula (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): Catalyst of formula (I) is prepared by adding carbamide and metalhalide to the mixed solution of ethanol and water, where mass ratio of carbamide to metal halide is 10: 1-10: 3. The mass ratio of dicyandiamide to ethanol and water mixture is 1:100-1:10. The mass ratio of ethanol to water is 5:1-1:5. The above system is heated to 40-80 degrees C under magnetic stirring for 2-4 hours. The above mixture system is placed in an aqueous system and evaporated to dryness, and then transferred to an oven and dried at 80 degrees C for 4 hours. The obtained solid is placed in a muffle furnace. The temperature is raised to 500 degrees C at a rate of 5-10 degrees C / min, and kept at 500 degrees C for 2 hours.

PN CN113860335-A
TI Producing sodium bicarbonate by metathesis used e.g. in food industry, comprises adding brine solution and ammonium bicarbonate into reaction tower, reacting in countercurrent with carbon dioxide gas, performing solid-liquid separation on sodium bicarbonate crystal slurry and passing carbon dioxide.
AU WANG Y
   LIAO Y
   TENG L
AE HENGYANG AIJIE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HENG-Non-standard)
GA 202214780V
AB    NOVELTY - Producing sodium bicarbonate by metathesis comprises adding brine solution and ammonium bicarbonate into the upper part of a reaction tower (1), mixing, stirring, and filtering to obtain filtrate, allowing the filtrate to fall down in the reaction tower, and reacting in countercurrent with the carbon dioxide gas passed into the lower part of the reaction tower, and generating sodium bicarbonate crystal slurry from the bottom of the reaction tower, performing solid-liquid separation on the sodium bicarbonate crystal slurry, mixing a part of carbon dioxide gas with ammonia and discharging from the top of the reaction tower, recycling to a washing tower (2) through the pipeline for cooling and deamination, passing the carbon dioxide treated by the washing tower into a carbon dioxide compressor (3), and returning after boosting.
   USE - The method is useful for producing sodium bicarbonate by metathesis, which is useful in food industry, as loosening agent or expansion agent, for producing various biscuits, bread and confectionery product, for generating agent of carbon dioxide in steam-water beverage, preserving agent of butter, for cleaning aircraft, circuit board and tooth surface, and for ore dressing, smelting, metal heat treatment, tanning, dye, fiber, rubber, foam plastic, metal sodium, detergent, personal daily care product, foam extinguishing agent and dry powder extinguishing agents.
   ADVANTAGE - The method recycles the carbon dioxide discharged from the reaction tower and removes ammonia, then using carbon dioxide compressor to boost to make it return to the lower part of the reaction to continue to react to realize the recycling of carbon dioxide, saving the resource to a large extent.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a flow structure illustrating the method for producing sodium bicarbonate by metathesis.
   Reaction tower (1)
   Washing tower (2)
   Carbon dioxide compressor (3)
   Washing liquid circulating pump (4)
   Knockout pump (5)

TI Intelligent flash point explosion-proof system has carbon dioxide fire extinguisher that is connected with liquid flow check valve, selection valve and self-locking pressure switch in sequence through fire extinguishing pipeline.
AU JIAO X

AB    NOVELTY - The system has an automatic fire extinguishing system that includes a temperature sensor (1), a controller (2), a nitrogen starting bottle (3), a carbon dioxide fire extinguisher (4) and a carbon dioxide nozzle (5). The temperature sensor is connected to the controller. The controller is connected to a carbon dioxide fire extinguishing pipeline (7) through a self-locking pressure switch (6). The controller is simultaneously connected with a solenoid valve (8) at the outlet of the nitrogen starting bottle. The nitrogen starting bottle is connected with a selection valve (9), an air flow check valve (10) and a bottle head valve of the carbon dioxide fire extinguisher through the pipeline. The carbon dioxide fire extinguisher is connected with a liquid flow check valve (12), the selection valve and the self-locking pressure switch in sequence through the carbon dioxide fire extinguishing pipeline, and finally connected with the carbon dioxide nozzle.
   USE - Intelligent flash point explosion-proof system.
   ADVANTAGE - The intelligent flash point explosion-proof system not only has the ability to deal with the fire of the instrument, but also can find out whether there is the leakage of combustible gas in the environment, can deal with the leakage of dangerous combustible gas in time, prevent the continuous diffusion of combustible gas, and reduce the damage degree of the explosion caused by combustible gas to the laboratory.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an intelligent flash point explosion-proof cabinet.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an intelligent flash point explosion-proof system.

PN US2019301679-A1
TI Compressor device for compressing dry ice flakes, has closable lid that is positioned to first closed position to vent gaseous carbon dioxide from cylinder and second closed position to leakably close cylinder to permit compression.
AU BURKOT S T G
   JOHANSON E
   KUIPER M K
   LIEBERMAN D H
   WAGNER L
AE TOKITAE LLC (TKTE-C)
   TOKITAE LLC (TKTE-C)
GA 2019835272
AB    NOVELTY - The device (10) comprises a hollow cylinder (12), a post equipped coaxially in the cylinder, and a piston that defines an opening and slidably equipped in the cylinder. The opening in the piston slidably receives the post. A biasing device (16) is operatively coupled to the piston. An inlet port (34) is connectable to a source of liquid carbon dioxide and configured to introduce liquid carbon dioxide into the cylinder. The inlet port is configured to expand liquid carbon dioxide to dry ice and to gaseous carbon dioxide. An openably closable lid (18) is positionable among a first closed position configured to vent gaseous carbon dioxide from the cylinder, a second closed position configured to leakably close the cylinder to permit carbon dioxide flakes to be compressed in the cylinder, and an open position.
   USE - Compressor device for compressing dry ice flakes.
   ADVANTAGE - The screen prevents the dry ice flakes from escaping the cylinder upon expansion of the liquid carbon dioxide through the opening of the inlet port, and the position of the screen and size of openings in the mesh are selected as desired for a particular application based upon factors, such as safety of a user and minimizing gas flow in direction of the user. The solid, liquid, and/or gaseous carbon dioxide can escape the cylinder in a void that may be formed between the cylinder and the lid while the dry ice flakes are compressed, thus helping to reduce and/or prevent an undesirable rise in pressure within the cylinder during compression of the dry ice flakes.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the perspective view of an apparatus for compressing dry ice in a configuration for filling with liquid carbon dioxide and venting gaseous carbon dioxide.
   Compressor device (10)
   Hollow cylinder (12)
   Biasing device (16)
   Openably closable lid (18)
   Inlet port (34)

PN JP2018122250-A; JP6918288-B2
TI Manufacture of water-repellent treated material e.g. textile material, involves mixing fluid of processing agent containing fluorine-based water repellent and solvent, and fluid of carbon dioxide, and injecting to solvent-permeable base.
AU FUJII T
   MURATA H
   NOMURA N
   KAWASAKI S
AE NAT INST ADVANCED IND SCI & TECHNOLOGY (NIIT-C)
   PEARL TONE KK (PEAR-Non-standard)
   NAT ADVANCED IND SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (NIIT-C)
GA 201862217E
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of water-repellent treated material (8) involves continuously supplying fluid of water-repellent processing agent containing a fluorine-based water repellent and an organic solvent, and fluid of carbon dioxide to a mixer (4), mixing, and injecting continuously the resultant mixed fluid to a solvent-permeable base material at 2.3 MPa or more.
   USE - Manufacture of water-repellent treated material e.g. textile material (claimed) such as silk fabric material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient and economical manufacture of water-repellent treated material having low environmental impact and excellent texture and with reduced usage amount of the hydrophobic organic solvent by adding high-pressure carbon dioxide as a diluent. The explosion and ignition risks of flammable solvents such as benzine are reduced by spraying the flammable solvent such as benzine in combination with high-pressure carbon dioxide, such that the method is carried out safely.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacturing apparatus of water-repellent treated material, which comprises a water-repellent processing agent pump (2) which can continuously supply the water-repellent processing agent to a high-pressure environment, a carbon-dioxide pump (3) which can continuously supply a carbon dioxide to high-pressure environment, a heating unit for heating water-repellent processing agent and carbon dioxide, a micro mixer for continuously mixing water-repellent processing agent and carbon dioxide, a measurement unit for measuring the temperature and pressure of the mixed fluid of the water repellent treating agent and carbon dioxide, and a high-pressure injection nozzle which can be stably injected from a high-pressure environment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the manufacturing apparatus of water-repellent treated material.
   Carbon-dioxide gas cylinder (1)
   Water-repellent processing agent pump (2)
   Carbon-dioxide pump (3)
   Mixer (4)
   Water-repellent treated material (8)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The base material is porous inorganic material or textile. The flow rate of fluid of carbon dioxide is 10-200 pts. wt. with respect to 100 pts. wt. fluid of carbon dioxide and fluid of water-repellent processing agent.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The organic solvent is a hydrophobic organic solvent, preferably petroleum-type hydrocarbon solvent containing benzene.

PT P
PN CN113620754-A
TI Preparing mineral organic fertilizer from insoluble potassium-rich rock, comprises e.g. mixing potassium hydroxide as activator and potassium-rich rock, mixing into paste, aging, cooling clinker, neutralizing with oxalic acid and spray drying into high potassium dry powder.
AU WEI X
   ZHANG W
   ZHANG Z
AE CHANGCHUN ZHENSHENG TESTING TECHNOLOGY (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021D70411
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing mineral organic fertilizer from insoluble potassium-rich rock, comprises mixing potassium hydroxide as an activator and potassium-rich rock over 200 mesh powder in a weight ratio of 1:0.4-0.7, adding little water to stir, mixing into a paste, aging for 4 Hours, drying, roasting at a temperature of 600-800degreesC for 40 minutes to 1.5 hours to prepare clinker, (ii) cooling the clinker obtained in step A slightly, adding 1-2 times the amount of water while it is hot, stirring, and extracting to obtain a slurry, (iii) blowing carbon dioxide to pH 10-10.5, continue to neutralizing to pH 6-7 with oxalic acid, and spray drying into high potassium dry powder, and (iv) mixing the high-potassium dry powder with nitrogen, phosphorus, and humic acid to prepare a nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium mineral-organic composite fertilizer.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing mineral organic fertilizer from insoluble potassium-rich rock.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for comprehensive utilization method of insoluble potassium-rich rock, comprising (i) mixing potassium hydroxide as an activator and potassium-rich rock over 200 mesh powder in a weight ratio of 1:0.4-0.7, adding little water to stir, mixing into a paste, and aging for 4 hours, drying, roasting at a temperature of 600-800degreesC for 40 minutes to 1.5 hours to prepare clinker, (ii) cooling the clinker slightly, adding 1-2 times the amount of water while it is hot, stirring and extracting to obtain a slurry, passing little carbon dioxide to make the pH 12, allowing to stand, filtering while hot to obtain the filtrate 1 and filtering cake 1, heating the filtrate 1, adding acid to adjust the pH less than or equal to 1, adding coagulant, stirring well while it is hot, and allowing to stand for 30 minutes, filtering 2 to obtain filter cake 2 and filtrate 2, after the filter cake 2 is fully washed, calcining to obtain silica, heating the filtrate 2 and neutralizing with potassium hydroxide to pH 5-6, allowing to stand for 30 minutes, and filter 3 while it is hot to obtain the filter cake 3, which is fully washed, and calcining to obtain aluminum oxide, the acidified with nitric acid made into potassium nitrate or molten salt, the one acidified with oxalic acid is made into potassium oxalate, using reverse flotation to remove sodium for acidification with hydrochloric acid, and using diaphragm electrolysis to obtain potassium hydroxide and hydrochloric acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the weight ratio of the potassium hydroxide to the potassium-rich rock is 1:0.5. In the step (iii), the chelating agent is an equivalent nitrilotriacetic acid (NTA) or ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid (EDTA) complexing agent with iron, manganese, calcium, magnesium, copper, and/or zinc ion. In the step (iv), the acid is hydrochloric acid, nitric acid or oxalic acid, the coagulant aid is 0.1% polyethylene oxide, and the added amount is feed liquid phi=5%.Preferred Method: In the step (i), the calcination temperature is 700degreesC, and the time is 1 hour. The step (iv) is specifically: adding urea and diammonium hydrogen phosphate according to the mass fraction of potassium oxide in the dry powder to prepare nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium mineral organic composite fertilizer. The specific steps are: directly adding humic acid to dilute, processing into potassium humate agricultural organic fertilizer. In the step (iii), the pH is adjusted to 6.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.

PT P
PN CN112979119-A; CN112979119-B; WO2022178960-A1
TI Processing high value urban wet waste comprises            mixing urban wet waste with water, extracting oil,            mixing with alkali, hydrolysis reaction, culturing            hydrolysis product and acetic acid-producing sludge,            solid-liquid separation and drying.
AU CHEN Y
   ZHENG X
   ZHANG X
   CHEN C
   WANG Q
   GAO M
AE UNIV TONGJI (UYTJ-C)
   UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
   UNIV TONGJI (UYTJ-C)
   UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 2021720092
AB    NOVELTY - Processing high value urban wet waste                comprises (a) mixing urban wet waste with water,                and extracting oil to obtain oil extraction                mixture, (b) mixing oil extraction mixture with                alkali for hydrolysis reaction to obtain hydrolysis                product, (c) anaerobically culturing hydrolysis                product and first acetic acid-producing sludge,                collecting generated gas, and performing                solid-liquid separation to obtain first liquid and                first solid, (d) passing the gas into mixture of                urban waste and second acetic acid-producing                sludge, anaerobically culturing, and performing                solid-liquid separation to obtain second liquid and                second solid, and (e) mixing first liquid and                second liquid, then adding magnesium salt,                stirring, performing solid-liquid separation to                obtain precipitate, i.e. fertilizer containing                nitrogen and phosphorus, and upper liquid, i.e. a                liquid containing acetic acid, then mixing first                solid, second solid and humic acid, and drying to                obtain third solid.
   USE - The method is useful for processing high value                urban wet waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has high-efficiency hydrolysis,                high-value biological conversion, simultaneous                recovery of released nitrogen and phosphorus, and                deep utilization of residues; can realize the                high-value treatment of urban wet waste into acetic                acid; can biologically convert the by-products of                carbon dioxide and hydrogen into acetic acid; can                recycle released nitrogen and phosphorus into                slow-release fertilizers; can realize the                high-value treatment of urban wet waste; and can                recycle generated waste gas and waste                residue.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing high value urban wet waste                comprises using urban wet waste as raw material,                including (a) mixing urban wet waste with water,                and extracting oil to obtain oil extraction                mixture, (b) mixing oil extraction mixture with                alkali for performing hydrolysis reaction to obtain                hydrolysis product, (c) anaerobically culturing the                hydrolysis product and first acetic acid-producing                sludge, then collecting the generated gas, and                performing solid-liquid separation after culturing                to obtain first liquid and first solid, (d) passing                the gas into the mixture of urban waste and second                acetic acid-producing sludge, anaerobically                culturing, and then performing solid and liquid                separation to obtain second liquid and second                solid, and (e) mixing first liquid and second                liquid, then adding magnesium salt, adjusting the                pH value, stirring, performing solid-liquid                separation to obtain precipitate, i.e. fertilizer                containing nitrogen and phosphorus, and upper                liquid, i.e. a liquid containing acetic acid, then                mixing the first solid, second solid and humic                acid, and drying to obtain third solid, where the                first acetic acid-producing sludge is domesticated                sludge, which can convert glucose into acetic acid,                and the second acetic acid producing sludge is                domesticated sludge, which can convert carbon                dioxide and hydrogen into acetic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The particle size of                  urban wet waste in the step (a) is 0.1-1 mm. The                  solid content in the mixture formed by mixing                  urban wet waste and water in the step (a) is                  20-180 g/l. The oil content in the mixture after                  oil extraction in the step (a) is less than 3%.                  The alkali is sodium hydroxide. The volume ratio                  of first acetic acid-producing sludge to urban                  wet waste is 6-10:100. The concentration of                  second acetic acid-producing sludge based on the                  total volume of mixture is 500-7000 mg/l. The                  condition of the anaerobic culture is that the pH                  value is 6-8. The magnesium salt is magnesium                  chloride. The added amount of humic acid is                  10-100% the total dry weight of first solid and                  second solid. Preferred Method: The domestication                  process for preparing first acetic acid-producing                  sludge comprises adding glucose to the mixture of                  sludge and urban waste, and anaerobically                  culturing under a pH of 6-11 at 20-80 degrees C                  to obtain first acetic acid-producing sludge. The                  domestication process for preparing second acetic                  acid-producing sludge comprises introducing                  hydrogen and carbon dioxide into a mixture of                  sludge and urban waste, and anaerobically                  culturing under a pH of 5-9 at 20-50 degrees C to                  obtain second acetic acid-producing sludge. The                  domestication process of first acetic                  acid-producing sludge comprises three periods,                  where the first period has solid content in the                  mixture of 3800-4500 mg/l, the concentration of                  glucose based on the total volume of sludge,                  urban waste and glucose is 600 mg chemical oxygen                  demand(COD)/l to 1000 mg/l, and the first period                  is carried out for 3-7 days, the second period                  has the glucose concentration of 1000-1400 mg                  chemical oxygen demand(COD)/l per day, and the                  second period is carried out for 8-12 days,                  and/or the third period has the glucose                  concentration, which increases daily, and the                  daily increase amount is 80-100 mg chemical                  oxygen demand(COD)/l, and the third period is                  further carried out by adding 30-70 mg chemical                  oxygen demand(COD)/l per day acetic acid for                  30-35 days. The hydrolysis reaction is carried                  out under the pH of 8-12 at 5-80 degrees C. The                  anaerobic culturing in the step (c) is carried                  out under the pH of 6-12 at 20-80 degrees C. The                  step (c) comprises adding third solid to the                  hydrolysis product and first acetic                  acid-producing sludge, where the added amount of                  third solid is not greater than 70% the dry                  weight of first acetic acid-producing sludge. The                  step (e) comprises adjusting the pH value to                  8-10, stirring for 5-50 minutes, and adding                  ammonia nitrogen salt and/or phosphate after                  adding magnesium salt. The drying is carried out                  at 20-120 degrees C.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)
MC C04-A09J; C04-D02; C05-A01B; C05-B02A4; C05-C01; C05-C02; C05-C07; C10-C04E6; C11-A; C12-M10A; C14-Y; D04-A01J; D04-B10A; D05-A04A; D05-G
