PT P
PN CN112798732-A
TI Detection method of calcium carbonate content in polyolefin material, involves determining accurate content of calcium carbonate by acid-base titration, thermal gravimetric analysis and inductive coupling plasma emission spectrum detection.
AU KUANG L
   SUN A
   WU B
   XIA J
   HUANG H
AE GUOGAOCAI POLYMER MATERIAL IND INNOVATIO (GUOG-Non-standard)
   SHANGHAI KINGFA SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (KGFA-C)
GA 202156657B
AB    NOVELTY - Detection method of calcium carbonate content in polyolefin material, involves determining accurate content of calcium carbonate by acid-base titration, thermal gravimetric analysis and inductive coupling plasma emission spectrum detection method. The acid-base titration detection involves calcining sample, cooling, dripping diluted acid, performing neutralization reaction by adding diluted acid and diluted alkali solution with stirring, recording volume of diluted alkali solution added, calculating based on consumption of diluted alkali and diluted acid solution. The thermal gravimetric analysis detection involves weighing polyolefin sample, putting in sample aluminum oxide crucible, obtaining weight loss spectrum and detecting emission spectrum. The inductance coupling involves weighing polyolefin sample in microwave digestion tube, performing digestion procedure, filtering, moistening, washing, transferring to volumetric flask, setting calcium element analysis line and testing.
   USE - Detection method of calcium carbonate content in polyolefin material e.g. polyethylene, polypropylene, acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene copolymer, polyamide, polycarbonate, polystyrene, PVC and polyethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem of low accuracy obtained by using single method results and improves accuracy of test result.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Detection method of calcium carbonate content in polyolefin material, involves determining accurate content of calcium carbonate by acid-base titration, thermal weightlessness analysis and inductive coupling plasma emission spectrum detection method. The acid-base titration detection involves calcining sample, cooling sample ash to room temperature, dripping diluted acid solution in sample ash to react, continuously stirring and monitoring pH value to ensure complete reaction of calcium carbonate, and maintaining amount of diluted acid in excess until system is acidic, recording volume of diluted acid solution, dripping diluted alkali solution with stirring, performing excessive acid neutralization reaction, dripping and monitoring pH value of solution, until pH of system is neutral, recording volume of diluted alkali solution added and calculating content of calcium carbonate based on consumption of diluted alkali and diluted acid solutions. The thermal gravimetric analysis detection involves weighing polyolefin sample, putting in thermal gravimetric analyzer sample aluminum oxide crucible, setting thermal weight loss analyzer at 30 degrees C initial temperature at rate of 20 degrees C/minute, raising temperature to 750 degrees C, obtaining carbon dioxide weight loss based on weight loss step of spectrum, converting content of calcium carbonate by carbon dioxide content and detecting emission spectrum. The inductance coupling involves weighing polyolefin sample in microwave digestion tube, adding mixed acid digestion solution in digestion tube, setting digestion procedure by raising temperature to 400 W, keeping for 5 minutes, heating to 700 W for 10 minutes, keeping for 30min, digesting, filtering digestion solution, moistening, washing, transferring to volumetric flask, testing with inductance coupling plasma emission spectrometer and setting calcium element analysis line.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The diluted acid solution is diluted hydrochloric acid solution comprising 0.02 mol/L hydrochloric acid solution, where initial concentration of concentrated hydrochloric acid is 12.27 mol/L, using pipette gun and removing 163 L concentrated hydrochloric acid to 100mL volumetric flask, adding pure water to constant volume to scale and shaking uniformly. The dilute alkali solution is sodium hydroxide solution comprising 0.02 mol/L sodium hydroxide solution, accurately weighing 0.0839g sodium hydroxide (content 96 %) solid, transferring to 100 mL volumetric flask, adding pure water to constant volume to scale and shaking uniformly. The mixed acid digestion solution comprises 7.5 mL concentrated nitric acid and 0.5 mL hydrofluoric acid. Preferred Process: The detection method for content of calcium carbonate less than 4 %, uses inductively coupled plasma emission spectroscopy, content in range of 4-6.5 %, uses average value of acid-base titration and inductively coupled plasma emission spectrometry for detection, content of calcium carbonate in range of 6.5-11 %, uses average value of acid-base titration, thermal weight loss analysis and inductively coupled plasma emission spectroscopy for detection and content of calcium carbonate greater than 11 %, uses average value of acid-base titration and thermal weight loss analysis for detection.

TI Preparing Eucommia seed oil capsule used for e.g. improving vision, by preheating Eucommia seed oil and Acer truncatum seed oil, adding peppermint essential oil, mixing, adding fruit and vegetable powder and folic acid, and canning capsules.
AU WANG J
AE WANG J (WANG-Individual)
GA 2021244258
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing Eucommia seed oil capsule involves (i) using a carbon dioxide supercritical method to extract Eucommia seed oil and A.truncatum seed oil at low temperature for maximum retention of alpha -linolenic acid in Eucommia seed oil and nervonic acid and vitamin E in A.truncatum seed oil, (ii) preheating Eucommia seed oil and A.truncatum seed oil at 40 degrees C for 50 minutes, (iii) adding peppermint essential oil to the preheated Eucommia seed oil and A.truncatum seed oil, allowing to stand for 20 minutes, start mixing, and after the mixture is evenly mixed, allowing to stand at room temperature for 30 minutes, and (iv) adding fruit and vegetable powder and folic acid to the evenly mixed Eucommia seed oil, A.truncatum seed oil and peppermint essential oil, placing in a sterile clean room at 1:1 atmospheric pressure for 24 hours, and canning capsules.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing Eucommia seed oil capsule for improving intelligence and vision, preventing Alzheimer's disease, reducing incidence of cardiovascular and cerebrovascular accidents, and preventing and treating hypertension, hyperglycemia, and hyperlipidemia.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared Eucommia seed oil capsule is easy to use and seamless capsule so that the capsule is not easy to oxidize, and has good taste, extended shelf life, and sweet taste as it contains peppermint essential oil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing Eucommia seed oil capsule comprising 60-70 wt.% Eucommia seed oil, 20-30 wt.% Acer truncatum seed oil, 1-10 wt.% peppermint essential oil, 0.2-1 wt.% folic acid, 1-8 wt.% fruit and vegetable powder, and 10-25 wt.% gelatin, involves (i) using a carbon dioxide supercritical method to extract Eucommia seed oil and A.truncatum seed oil at low temperature for maximum retention of alpha -linolenic acid in Eucommia seed oil and nervonic acid and vitamin E in A.truncatum seed oil, (ii) preheating Eucommia seed oil and A.truncatum seed oil at 40 degrees C for 50 minutes, (iii) adding peppermint essential oil to the preheated Eucommia seed oil and A.truncatum seed oil, allowing to stand for 20 minutes, start mixing, and after the mixture is evenly mixed, allowing to stand at room temperature for 30 minutes, and (iv) adding fruit and vegetable powder and folic acid to the evenly mixed Eucommia seed oil, A.truncatum seed oil and peppermint essential oil, using a stirring device to mix fully, placing in a sterile clean room at 1:1 atmospheric pressure for 24 hours, and after 24 hours, starting canning capsules.

PN KR2222628-B1
TI Composition for impact-absorbing pad for artificial turf comprises polymer mixture containing e.g., biomass plastic, powdered diatomaceous earth, calcium carbonate, iron oxide, foaming agent, crosslinking agent, and release agent.
AU SIN S D
AE JMCGR CO LTD (JMCG-Non-standard)
GA 202123550W
AB    NOVELTY - An impact-absorbing pad composition for artificial turf comprises 100 pts. wt. polymer mixture comprising 5-30 wt.% biomass plastic, 30-50 wt.% low-density polyethylene (LDPE), and 10-30 wt.% ethylene vinyl acetate (EVA); 0.5-10 pts. wt. 150-mesh size powdered diatomaceous earth; 1-20 pts. wt. calcium carbonate; 0.1-5 pts. wt. iron oxide; 2-5 pts. wt. foaming agent; 0.05-2 pts. wt. crosslinking agent; and 0.05-2 pts. wt. release agent. Biomass polyethylene contains 20-25 wt.% biomass complex, and biomass polyethylene comprises linear LDPE (LLDPE) and medium density polyethylene (MDPE) in a weight ratio of 4:1. Diatomaceous earth is freshwater diatomaceous earth containing 93 wt.% silica (SiO2), 2.7-4.1 wt.% alumina (Al2O3), 0.5-1.5 wt.% iron(III) oxide (Fe2O3), 0.1-0.3 wt.% magnesium oxide (MgO), 0.05-0.6 wt.% calcium oxide (CaO), 0.01-0.08 wt.% potassium oxide (K2O), 1.5-3.5 wt.% sodium oxide (Na2O), and 0.05-0.17 wt.% (TiO2).
   USE - A composition for impact-absorbing pad for artificial turf of a stadium (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The biomass plastic contained in the impact-absorbing pad is a plant resource, microbial metabolite, and seaweed in which carbon in the atmosphere is fixed by photosynthesis, and has the effect of contributing to the prevention of global warming by not increasing the total amount of carbon dioxide during chemical decomposition. The ultra-porous particles of diatomaceous earth contained in the shock-absorbing pad absorb formaldehyde and volatile organic compounds (VOC), which are harmful substances in the air, to decompose themselves, and by washing with rain water, it has the effect of reducing fine dust. The ultra-porous material using diatomaceous earth can reduce temperature of a stadium by increasing the surface moisture content of the impact-absorbing pad, and the hydrated alkaline moisture can sterilize mold and various bacteria and inhibit the habitat as an antioxidant action. In addition, the impact-absorbing pad uses diatomaceous earth, which has low thermal conductivity and thermal expansion coefficient, high melting point, maintains the uprightness of the pile layer under high temperature in summer, and prevents stiffness and breakage at low temperature in winter, having good adaptability to temperature change. By manufacturing an artificial turf mat with the impact-absorbing pad for artificial turf containing biomass and diatomaceous earth in the composition, an eco-friendly turf field having a complex function of reducing carbon dioxide and fine dust can be created, allowing construction of a stadium that provides a pleasant environment to users and contributes to the prevention of global warming.
TF EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

   USE - The method is useful for preparing hydrogen by                reforming reaction of methanol water and                purifying.

           exchanger, (ii) continuously passing methanol water                passing through the heat exchanger into the                vaporizer for heating and vaporizing the methanol                water, and the vaporized methanol is called                vaporized methanol, (iii) passing vaporized                methanol and water vapor into the superheater,                heating the temperature of methanol to the                temperature required for reforming reaction of                methanol, (iv) passing the vaporized methanol                heated by the superheater into the cracking reactor                for reforming reaction, and generating                high-temperature mixed gas containing at least                hydrogen and carbon dioxide, (v) passing the           exchanger, preheating the raw methanol water and                reducing the temperature of the mixed gas, which is                called the first cooling post mixed gas, (vi) the cooler, condensing vaporized methanol and                purifying the hydrogen, which is called the mixed                gas after the second cooling, (vii) passing second                cooled mixed gas into the gas-liquid separator,                passing the water vapor in the mixed gas after the                second cooling through the gas-liquid separator,                liquefying vaporized methanol for further purifying                hydrogen, which is called the first purified mixed                gas, and(viii) passing first purified mixed gas                into the adsorption tower mechanism for further                purifying hydrogen, which is called the second                purified mixed gas.

PT P
PN CN112062750-A; CN112062750-B
TI Preparing baloxavir intermediate, by dissolving 3,4-difluorobromobenzene, adding lithium diisopropylamide, performing methylation, post-treatment, reacting with Grignard reagent, performing acidification, brominating and reducing.
AU LI W
   ZHANG W
AE HEDING NANJING MEDICAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HEDI-Non-standard)
GA 2020C64340
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing baloxavir intermediate 2,3-difluoro-5,11-dihydro-10-thia-dibenzo(a,d)cyclohepten-5-ol compound (VI), involves (i) dissolving 3,4-difluorobromobenzene, adding lithium diisopropylamide, performing methylation, and performing post-treatment to obtain 1-bromo-3,4-difluoro-2-methyl-benzene compound (I), (ii) reacting compound (I) with Grignard reagent, introducing carbon dioxide gas, and adding acid for acidification to obtain 3,4-difluoro-benzoic acid compound (II), (iii) brominating compound (II) to obtain 2-bromomethyl-3,4-difluoro-benzoic acid compound (III), (iv) adding diphenyl disulfide and first reducing agent to ether solvent, dripping methanol, and dripping solution of compound (III) to obtain 3,4-difluoro-2-phenylsulfanylmethyl-benzoic acid compound (IV), (v) subjecting compound (IV) to ring-closure reaction to obtain 2,3-difluoro-11H-10-thia-dibenzo(a,d)cyclohepten-5-one compound (V), and (vi) reducing compound (V) into alcohol (VI).
   USE - The method for preparing baloxavir intermediate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids the use of highly toxic and malodorous thiophenol and the use of expensive reagents, and reduces the process cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing baloxavir intermediate 2,3-difluoro-5,11-dihydro-10-thia-dibenzo(a,d)cyclohepten-5-ol compound of formula (VI), involves (i) dissolving 3,4-difluorobromobenzene in first organic solvent, adding lithium diisopropylamide, adding a methylating reagent for methylation, and performing post-treatment after the reaction to obtain a 1-bromo-3,4-difluoro-2-methyl-benzene compound of formula (I), (ii) reacting the compound (I) with a Grignard reagent, introducing carbon dioxide gas for reaction, and adding acid for acidification to obtain a 3,4-difluoro-benzoic acid compound of formula (II), (iii) brominating the compound (II) with a brominating agent in second organic solvent to obtain a 2-bromomethyl-3,4-difluoro-benzoic acid compound of formula (III), (iv) adding diphenyl disulfide and first reducing agent to the ether solvent, controlling the temperature at 30-50 degrees C, dripping methanol, controlling the temperature at below 20 degrees C during the process of adding methanol, and dripping the solution of compound (III) in ether solvent to obtain a 3,4-difluoro-2-phenylsulfanylmethyl-benzoic acid compound of formula (IV), (v) subjecting the compound (IV) to a ring-closure reaction under catalysis of polyphosphoric acid to obtain a 2,3-difluoro-11H-10-thia-dibenzo(a,d)cyclohepten-5-one compound of formula (V), and (vi) using a second reducing agent to reduce the compound (V) into alcohol to obtain the compound (VI). An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a 2-bromomethyl-3,4-difluoro-benzoic acid compound (III) obtained by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (iii) involves dissolving the compound (II) in second organic solvent, adding N-bromosuccinimide and azobisisobutyronitrile, and reacting at 70-100 degrees C to obtain the compound (III). Preferred Conditions: In step (v), the ring-closure reaction is carried out at 90-100 degrees C. Preferred Components: The methylating agent used in step (i) is chosen from dimethyl sulfate and methyl iodide, preferably dimethyl sulfate. The first organic solvent used in step (i) is tetrahydrofuran. The Grignard reagent used in step (ii) is chosen from isopropyl magnesium chloride, isopropyl magnesium bromide, methyl magnesium chloride, methyl magnesium bromide, phenyl magnesium chloride and phenyl magnesium bromide. The acid used in step (ii) comprises hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, phosphoric acid and/or nitric acid. The second organic solvent used in step (iii) comprises dichloroethane, ethyl acetate and/or methylhydrofuran. The brominating agent used in step (iii) is N-bromosuccinimide or bromine. The ether solvent used in step (iv) comprises tetrahydrofuran, methyl tert-butyl ether and/or 2-methyltetrahydrofuran. The first reducing agent used in step (iv) comprises sodium borohydride and/or potassium borohydride. The second reducing agent used in step (vi) comprises sodium borohydride and/or potassium borohydride. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of 3,4-difluorobromobenzene, lithium diisopropylamide and dimethyl sulfate in step (i) is 1:(1.05-1.2):(1.05-1.2). The molar ratio of compound (I) and the Grignard reagent is 1:(2-2.5). The molar ratio of compound (II), N-bromosuccinimide and azobisisobutyronitrile in step (iii) is 1:(1.2-1.5):0.15. The molar ratio of compound (IV), diphenyl disulfide and the first reducing agent in step (iv) is 1:(0.5-0.6):(2-2.5). The molar ratio of compound (V) and the second reducing agent in step (vi) is 1:(0.5-0.6).

PT P
PN CN111944741-A
TI Performing suspension culture and domestication of            Madin-Darby canine kidney (MDCK) cell line useful for            obtaining cell for producing human influenza vaccine,            by culturing MDCK adherent cells in serum-containing            medium.
AU ZHU S
   ZHANG C
AE SHANGHAI RONGSHENG BIOTECH CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020B7301F
AB    NOVELTY - Method for performing suspension culture and                domestication of Madin-Darby canine kidney (MDCK)                cell line involves (a) culturing MDCK adherent                cells cultured in a serum-containing medium,                discarding serum-containing medium, rinsing, and                adding trypsin solution, (b) flapping bottom of the                container, and adding serum-containing medium, (c)                transferring MDCK cell suspension to shake flask,                adding serum-containing medium, adjusting cell                density, and setting shaker speed, temperature and                carbon dioxide (CO2) concentration, passaging MDCK                cells with serum-containing medium, and maintaining                cell density, (d) centrifuging culture medium                containing MDCK cells, discarding supernatant,                resuspending cell cluster in a chemically defined                medium, performing suspension culture, and                passaging the MDCK cells to complete single                suspension growth, and (e) obtaining MDCK cell line                suspension cultured in a chemically defined medium,                and setting rotation speed, temperature and CO2                concentration.
   USE - The method is useful for performing suspension                culture and domestication of MDCK cell line, which                is useful for obtaining cell for producing human                influenza vaccine (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The domestication method is performed in a                rapid manner, only needs 10 generations which is                much higher than the gradual serum reduction                method, has universal application value, and its                domestication time is shorter which is equivalent                to the one-step domestication method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for performing suspension culture and                domestication of Madin-Darby canine kidney (MDCK)                cell line involves (a) culturing MDCK adherent                cells cultured in a serum-containing medium until                cell confluency is 80-90%, discarding the                serum-containing medium, rinsing, and adding                trypsin solution for 5-10 minutes, (b) flapping the                bottom of the container gently to dissociate the                MDCK cells after the cells become round, and adding                serum-containing medium to terminate the digestion,                (c) transferring collected MDCK cell suspension to                a shake flask, adding serum-containing medium,                adjusting cell density to 0.8-1.5x 106 cells/ml,                and setting the shaker speed to 100 revolutions per                minute (rpm), temperature to 37 degrees C, and                carbon dioxide (CO2) concentration to 5% for                suspension culture, passaging MDCK cells 3-4 times                with serum-containing medium, and maintaining cell                density after each passage at 0.8-1.5x 106                cells/ml, (d) centrifuging culture medium                containing MDCK cells, discarding supernatant,                resuspending cell cluster in a chemically defined                medium, performing suspension culture, and                passaging the MDCK cells to complete single                suspension growth, and (e) obtaining MDCK cell line                suspension cultured in a chemically defined medium,                and setting rotation speed of the shaker to 130                rpm, the temperature to 37 degrees C and CO2                concentration to 5%. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for a cell for human influenza vaccine                production, which is obtained by the suspension                culture acclimation method of the MDCK cell                line.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Medium: The serum-containing                  medium uses Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium                  (DMEM) as a basic medium, and 10% fetal bovine                  serum. The digestion-terminated serum-containing                  medium uses DMEM as a basic medium, and is                  supplemented with 10% fetal bovine serum, and the                  added amount is 1-2 ml.    ACTIVITY - Virucide.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Vaccine.

PN CN111802440-A
TI Fresh-keeping storage of freeze-dried sea fish food by soaking selected sea fish food material in wrapping film solution, freezing, cutting, freeze-drying using freeze-dried protective agent, packaging, filling with nitrogen and sealing.
AU LIAO W
AE QINGDAO GUISHI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 2020A8663X
AB    NOVELTY - Method for fresh-keeping storage of freeze-dried sea fish food involves (1) selecting salmon fish, cod fish or tuna fish, removing fish skin and bone, selecting sea fish food raw materials with moist flesh color and complete shape, (2) dissolving high-concentration dextran in purified water and preparing into 2% wrapping film solution, (3) soaking the selected sea fish meat in the wrapping film solution, stirring, soaking and infiltrating the wrapping film liquid, (4) performing quick-freezing, (5) cutting the frozen sea fish meat through a crawler-type automatic cutting machine into required size particles in a low-temperature workshop, (6) putting the cut meat particles into a freeze-drying bin, applying a freeze-dried protective agent and performing freeze-drying processing, (7) putting the freeze-dried sea fish food into a packaging bag, filling the bag with carbon dioxide or 95% nitrogen and sealing and (8) placing the obtained packaged sea fish food in a cold storage.
   USE - The method is useful for fresh-keeping storage of freeze-dried sea fish food.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts food-grade wrapping film solution for performing fresh-keeping on the sea fish raw materials and simple and practical overall storage method and provides excellent fresh-keeping effect, long effective storage period and less nutrient loss.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for fresh-keeping storage of freeze-dried sea fish food involves (1) selecting salmon fish, cod fish or tuna fish, removing fish skin and bone, selecting sea fish food raw materials with moist flesh color and complete shape, (2) dissolving high-concentration dextran in purified water and preparing into 2% wrapping film solution with a viscosity of 20-50 cp, (3) soaking the selected sea fish meat in the wrapping film solution, gently stirring, soaking for 1 minute and uniformly infiltrating the wrapping film liquid into the fish meat, (4) performing quick-freezing by putting the fish meat on a crawler-type instant freezer and freezing for half an hour to -36 degrees C, (5) cutting the frozen sea fish meat through a crawler-type automatic cutting machine into required size particles in a low-temperature workshop, (6) putting the cut meat particles into a freeze-drying bin, applying a freeze-dried protective agent and performing freeze-drying processing, so that the moisture content of the dried meat particles is 2-6%, (7) putting the freeze-dried sea fish food into a packaging bag, filling the bag with carbon dioxide or 95% nitrogen with volume of 0.5-0.8 times based on the volume of the packaging bag and sealing and (8) placing the obtained packaged sea fish food in a cold storage at 10-15 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The wrapping film solution comprises dextran and purified water in a ratio of 98:2. The wrapping film solution can be composed of trehalose and purified water in the ratio of 98:2. The wrapping film solution comprises mixture and purified water in the ratio of 98:2, where the mixture comprises trehalose and dextran in the ratio of 6:4. The freeze-dried protective agent is dextran, where the dextran and the fish meat are in ratio of 99.5:0.5.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)
MC D02-A03A; D03-H02A
IP A23B-004/037; A23B-004/06; A23B-004/10; A23B-004/16; A23B-004/20

PN CN111671826-A
TI Preparing agar wood perfume oil mixture comprises e.g. taking agar wood shell material and mixing with water, heating to boiling, collecting condensed water to prepare agarwood distilled water, taking agar wood board stock, and washing.
AU SUN Z
AE ZHONGXIANG ALOESWOOD IND DEV HAINAN FREE TRADE ZONE CO LTD (ZHON-Non-standard)
GA 202095768G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing agar wood perfume oil mixture comprises e.g. taking agar wood shell material and mixing with water, heating to boiling, and collecting condensed water to prepare agarwood distilled water, taking agar wood board stock and agarwood peel oil, washing, drying, grinding into powder to prepare agarwood powder, adding the boiled agarwood shell material to water, mixing according to the material-to-liquid ratio of g/ml 1:0.8-1.2, adding 1-3 wt.% Lactobacillus powder and fermenting for 20-28 hours to prepare agarwood fermented product, carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction on the fermented agarwood, firstly extracting 0.8-1.2 hours under the conditions of 40-60 degrees C, 0.4-0.6 MPa to obtain agarwood oil A, extracting at 20-30 degrees C, 4-6MPa for 0.4-0.6 hours to obtain agarwood oil B, extracting at 15-25 degrees C, 14-16 MPa for 0.2-0.5 hours to obtain agarwood oil C, combining agarwood oil A and agarwood oil B, agarwood oil C.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing agar wood perfume oil mixture.
   ADVANTAGE - The agar wood perfume oil mixture: has a remarkable effect on wound healing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing agar wood perfume oil mixture comprises (1) taking agar wood shell material and mixing with water, heating to boiling, and collecting condensed water to prepare agarwood distilled water, (2) taking agar wood board stock and agarwood peel oil, washing, drying, grinding into powder to prepare agarwood powder, adding the boiled agarwood shell material to water, mixing according to the material-to-liquid ratio of g/ml 1:0.8-1.2, adding 1-3 wt.% Lactobacillus powder and fermenting for 20-28 hours to prepare agarwood fermented product, (3) carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction on the fermented agarwood, firstly extracting 0.8-1.2 hours under the conditions of 40-60 degrees C, 0.4-0.6 MPa to obtain agarwood oil A, extracting at 20-30 degrees C, 4-6 MPa for 0.4-0.6 hours to obtain agarwood oil B, extracting at 15-25 degrees C, 14-16 MPa for 0.2-0.5 hours to obtain agar wood oil C, combining agarwood oil A and agarwood oil B, agarwood oil C, mixing at a mass ratio of 1:2-3:0.5-0.8 to obtain a third-grade agarwood essential oil, and (4) adding third-grade agarwood essential oil and agarwood distilled water into a high-pressure vessel and mixing in a mass ratio of 1:5-10 to prepare agarwood oil mixture. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for agar wood perfume oil mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (4), the high-pressure container is an autoclave or a high-pressure homogenizer. In the step (1), the material-to-liquid ratio g/ml of agarwood shell material and water is 1:8-12. In the step (2), the mass ratio of the agarwood board stock and agarwood peel oil is 1:3-5. In the step (2), the number of meshes ground into powder is 150-200 mesh. Preferred Method: In the step (1), the boiling time is 10-20 minutes.

PN CN111575428-A
TI Gas-solid reduction shaft furnace has cooling gas            inlets that are connected through pipeline, cooling gas            valve group is arranged in middle, and inlet of cooling            gas valve group is connected to gas pressurizing device            through pipeline.
AU WANG P
   ZHOU Q
   LI S
   LI J
   TANG E
   FU B
   CHEN Q
AE WUHAN KESIRUIDI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 202084758T
AB    NOVELTY - The furnace has first shaft furnace (10) that                is provided with a first feed port, a first top gas                overflow port, a first high-temperature reducing                gas loop and a first high-temperature reducing gas                nozzle. The second shaft furnace (20) is provided                with a second feed inlet, a second furnace top gas                overflow outlet, and a second high-temperature                reducing gas nozzle. The intermediate gas passage                (30) is in communication with the first and the                second gas annular passages. The first cooling gas                inlet and the second cooling gas inlet are                connected through a pipeline, a cooling gas valve                group (45) is arranged in the middle, and the inlet                of the cooling gas valve group is connected to the                gas pressurizing device through the pipeline in                turn. The coal gas purification system has the                function of removing carbon dioxide and hydrogen                sulfide. The external reducing agent is of biomass                charcoal, crushed coke, semi-coke, lignite,                bituminous coal, and anthracite.
   USE - Gas-solid reduction shaft furnace.
   ADVANTAGE - The furnace improves the reduction efficiency,                shortens the reduction time, increases the output                of the shaft furnace, and greatly simplifies the                gas production and reforming process of the                gas-based direct reduction process. he selection of                raw materials has a wide range and strong                adaptability. The combination of external gas and                external reducing agent is flexible. The addition                of external reducing agent provides a medium for                secondary gas generation, loosens the charge,                reduces the adhesion of iron-containing pellets or                lumps into the furnace, and increases the                temperature of the high-temperature reducing gas                into the furnace. The utilization rate of the                effective components of the coal gas is high, the                heat recovery of the high-temperature reducing gas                and the cooling gas is sufficient, and the                temperature of the furnace top gas is lower. The                furnace ensures the synchronization and unity of                the temperature fields of the two shaft furnaces,                which is conducive to the synchronous and efficient                operation of the double shaft furnaces, and the                output and reduction quality are fully guaranteed.                The process flow is simple and the investment                intensity is low. The gas resources are fully                utilized and the system carbon dioxide emissions                are reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for producing sponge iron using a gas-solid                reduction shaft furnace.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                flowchart illustrating a gas-solid reduction shaft                furnace. (Drawing includes non-English language                text)
   First shaft furnace (10)
   Second shaft furnace (20)
   Intermediate gas passage (30)
   Cooling gas valve group (45)
   High temperature reducing gas reversing valve                group (50)

PT P
PN CN110987557-A; CN110987557-B
TI Producing rock-like body stretching crack sample based on polyhydroxyalkanoate comprises making die according to rock-like crack sample and using polyhydroxyalkanoate thread to suspend and fix crack prefabricated sheets.
AU DAI F
   FENG P
   LIU Y
   DU H
   YAN Z
   LI B
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 202031306M
AB    NOVELTY - Producing rock-like body stretching crack sample based on polyhydroxyalkanoate comprises making die according to the size requirements of the rock-like crack samples to, using polyhydroxyalkanoate to make the crack prefabricated sheets according to the size requirements of the cracks to be made, using the polyhydroxyalkanoate thread to suspend and fix the crack prefabricated sheets in the internal space of the die, adding alkaline rock-like slurry to the die, allowing the rock-like slurry covering the cracked prefabricated sheet, vibrating, smoothing, avoiding bubbles in the die, allowing to stand until the rock-like slurry solidifies and forms, placing it under standard curing conditions for curing, during the curing process, degrading the crack prefabricated sheet and polyhydroxyalkanoate thread into carbon dioxide and water, escaping from the solidified sample to form a built-in open crack and polishing the surface of the sample to obtain an rock-like body stretching crack sample.
   USE - The method is useful for producing rock-like body stretching crack sample based on polyhydroxyalkanoate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures that the trace emissions of degradation product gas will not damage the cracks and rock-like samples; not only simplifies the production process, but also avoids the damage of the prefabricated cracks by removing the prefabricated pieces and simulates the built-in open cracks in real engineering rock mass without affecting the mechanical properties of rock-like mass and reduces the test error.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing rock-like body stretching crack sample based on polyhydroxyalkanoate comprises (a) making die according to the size requirements of the rock-like crack samples to, using polyhydroxyalkanoate to make the crack prefabricated sheets according to the size requirements of the cracks to be made, (b) using the polyhydroxyalkanoate thread to suspend and fix the crack prefabricated sheets in the internal space of the die according to the position of the required cracks in the rock-like crack samples, (c) adding alkaline rock-like slurry to the die, allowing the rock-like slurry covering the cracked prefabricated sheet, vibrating, smoothing, avoiding bubbles in the die, allowing to stand until the rock-like slurry solidifies and forms, (d) removing the formed sample from the die, placing it under standard curing conditions for curing,during the curing process, degrading the crack prefabricated sheet and polyhydroxyalkanoate thread into carbon dioxide and water, escaping from the solidified sample to form a built-in open crack and (e) polishing the surface of the sample obtained in step (d) to obtain an rock-like body stretching crack sample.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of producing rock-like body stretching crack sample based on polyhydroxyalkanoate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The rock-like slurry is prepared from cement and water. The basicity of rock-like slurry is provided by cement. The diameter of the polyhydroxyalkanoate does not exceed 500 mu m. The size of the fixing holes on the cracked preform and the fixing holes on the side plate of the die match the diameter of the polyhydroxyalkanoate. The position, size and quantity of the cracks to be made in the crack-like samples of rock-like masses, multiple prefabricated cracks are produced, which are respectively suspended and fixed in the internal space of the die by polyhydroxyalkanoate. Preferred Method: The method comprises providing the fixing holes at both ends of the crack prefabricated sheet, providing on the side plate of the die, passing the polyhydroxyalkanoate thread through the fixing hole on the crack perform, suspending the fixing hole on the side plate of the die and fixing the crack preform in the internal space of the die.

PN CN110863142-A
TI Preparing grate involves transporting quartz sand and water glass to raw material room for heavy mixing, placing into the sand mixer to be mixed into water glass sand according to the proportion, placing existing model in the sand box.
AU LU J
AE NANTONG TENGFEI METAL CASTING CO LTD (NANT-Non-standard)
GA 202020901E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing grate involves transporting quartz sand and water glass to the raw material room for heavy mixing, placing into the sand mixer to be mixed into water glass sand according to the proportion. The existing model is placed in the sand box and tamped with water glass sand. The carbon dioxide is placed into the drying furnace to solidify, taken out the model to form a cavity, and then buckle the shape and wait for casting. The stainless steel scraps, non-ferrous metals and other raw materials ara placed into an intermediate frequency furnace to melt it into molten iron, and transferred to a ladle to maintained until the desired casting temperature is reached, and then drag the ladle to the prepared sand mold cast from above, the molten iron is poured into the model until it solidifies. The casted casting from the sand box is removed after a holding time of 1-3 hours.
   USE - Method for preparing grate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare grate improves the efficiency of the production, enhances service life and oxidation resistance, in cost-effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing grate involves transporting quartz sand and water glass to the raw material room for heavy mixing, placing into the sand mixer to be mixed into water glass sand according to the proportion. The existing model is placed in the sand box and tamped with water glass sand. The carbon dioxide is placed into the drying furnace to solidify, taken out the model to form a cavity, and then buckle the shape and wait for casting. The stainless steel scraps, non-ferrous metals and other raw materials ara placed into an intermediate frequency furnace to melt it into molten iron, and transferred to a ladle to maintained until the desired casting temperature is reached, and then drag the ladle to the prepared sand mold cast from above, the molten iron is poured into the model until it solidifies. The casted casting from the sand box is removed after a holding time of 1-3 hours. The sand on the castings out of the box is polished and cleaned by a shot blasting machine and a sander, the sand mold is removed, and the sand mold is finally separated from the casting. The separated waste sand is conveyed to the sand mill and sifter by a belt, the large metal blocks are removed by screening, and the bonded sand blocks are separated and reused by crushing, placed the casting into a nitriding furnace, passed in nitrogen, and heated at about 850-950 degrees C to cool with the furnace, processed into a grate by the lathe processing process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The grate comprises 0.3-0.5 wt.% carbon, 1.0-2.5 wt.% silicon, 0.3-1.5 wt.% manganese, 0.01-0.04 wt.% sulfur, 0.005-0.03 wt.% phosphorus, 25-28 wt.% chromium, 3.0-6.0 wt.% nickel, 0.1-05 wt.% molybdenum, and rets is iron and other unavoidable impurities. Preferred Conditions: The intermediate frequency furnace uses a microcomputerized thyristor intermediate frequency electric furnace, and the cooling water in the intermediate frequency electric furnace is used after cooling.

PN CN109603751-A
TI Preparation of biochar-supported zero-valent iron            composite material used for adsorbing and degrading            chlorinated organic component in soil or water, by            preprocessing rice straw, adding zero-valent iron and            then leaving and filtering.
AU WAN J
   ZHANG S
   LONG T
   ZHOU Y
   LI Q
   FAN T
   WU Y
   YANG L
   HE Y
   WANG L
   CHEN Q
   ZHAO Y
   ZHANG L
   DING W
AE NANJING INST ENVIRONMENTAL SCI NAT BUREA (NAJI-C)
GA 201935929U
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of biochar-supported                zero-valent iron composite material involves (1)                preprocessing rice straw, washing, removing                impurities, drying at 60 degrees C, crushing using                crusher to obtain straw powder, mixing the straw                powder with hydrochloric acid solution, drying at                110 degrees C to obtain modified straw powder,                adding ammonium bicarbonate, mixing with deionized                water, (2) placing in muffle furnace, introducing                nitrogen, thermally decomposing ammonium                bicarbonate into carbon dioxide and ammonia,                pickling and ashing with mixed solution of                hydrochloric acid and nitric acid, drying to obtain                porous biochar spheres and (3) adding zero-valent                iron, dissolving ferrous sulfate in aqueous                solution of ethanol to obtain ferrous sulfate                heptahydrate solution, mixing porous biochar                spheres with ferrous sulfate heptahydrate solution,                adding sodium perborate solution using peristaltic                pump, introducing nitrogen, leaving for 1 hour                under nitrogen atmosphere and then filtering and                drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparation of                biochar-supported zero-valent iron composite                material, which is useful for adsorbing and                degrading chlorinated organic component in soil or                water (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables easy and economical                preparation of biochar-supported zero-valent iron                composite material with high yield and reduction                activity and excellent adsorption effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation method of biochar-supported                zero-valent iron composite material involves (1)                preprocessing rice straw, washing, removing                impurities, placing in clean room for 12 hours,                placing in drying box, drying at 60 degrees C,                crushing using crusher to obtain straw powder,                mixing the straw powder with 2 mol/l hydrochloric                acid solution in a solid-liquid ratio of 1:5,                stirring using magnetic stirrer for 2 hours,                washing, drying at 110 degrees C to obtain modified                straw powder, adding ammonium bicarbonate, mixing                with deionized water, granulating using granulator                to obtain pellet, (2) placing in muffle furnace,                introducing nitrogen, preheating at 150 degrees C                for 1 hour, heating at 350 degrees C, carbonizing                for 3-5 hours, thermally decomposing ammonium                bicarbonate into carbon dioxide and ammonia,                heating at 700 degrees C, cooling to room                temperature, pickling and ashing with mixed                solution of hydrochloric acid and nitric acid in a                molar ratio of 1:2, adjusting pH value, drying to                obtain porous biochar spheres and (3) adding                zero-valent iron, dissolving ferrous sulfate in                aqueous solution of ethanol to obtain ferrous                sulfate heptahydrate solution, mixing porous                biochar spheres with ferrous sulfate heptahydrate                solution under nitrogen atmosphere, stirring using                magnetic stirrer at 45 degrees C for 1 hour, adding                1 mol/l sodium perborate solution using peristaltic                pump, introducing nitrogen, leaving for 1 hour                under nitrogen atmosphere and then filtering and                drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The heating step is                  carried out at 700 degrees C for 2 hours under                  heating rate of 5-10 degrees C/minute. Preferred                  Components: The mass ratio of the ammonium                  bicarbonate to the modified straw powder is 4:1.                  The mass ratio of the biochar spheres to the                  zero-valent iron is 1:1-3:1. Preferred Property:                  The pellet has particle diameter of 0.5-1                  cm.

PT P
PN CN109455713-A
TI Preparing industrial high-efficiency deaminated activated carbon comprises impregnating activated carbon in phosphoric acid solution, absorbing, calcining, impregnating in carbonate solution, sealing in plastic bag and calcining.
AU WANG H
   YANG J
AE JIANGSU PURESTAR ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTIO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201925391Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing industrial high-efficiency deaminated activated carbon comprises selecting activated carbon as a base charcoal to obtain a first mixed liquid, mixing pure water with phosphoric acid, heating while stirring, finally heating, impregnating the activated carbon in the first mixed liquid, rapidly stirring, absorbing the activated carbon with the first mixed liquid, transferring into a plastic bag, sealing, calcining the once-impregnated activated carbon to obtain a semi-finished product, preparing a second mixed liquid by dissolving activated carbon having carbon dioxide in pure water, placing carbonate in pure water to obtain a second mixed liquid, mixing the first mixed liquid and the second mixed liquid to obtain a third mixed liquid, impregnating the semi-finished product in the third mixed liquid for secondary immersion, and placing the activated carbon absorbed with the third mixed liquid in a plastic bag and sealing and secondarily calcining.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing industrial high-efficiency deaminated activated carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The deaminated activated carbon has no pungent odor, has high strength, strong adsorption capacity, large adsorption capacity, less ash, fast adsorption rate, long protection time, good protection effect and long life protection effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing industrial high-efficiency deaminated activated carbon comprises (a) selecting activated carbon as a base charcoal, where the activated carbon has a water capacity of greater than or equal to 75%, moisture content of less than or equal to 5%, a strength of greater than or equal to 85%, benzene vapor protection time of greater than or equal to 50 minute and preparing into a first mixed liquid, (b) mixing pure water with phosphoric acid in a mass ratio of 1:1.5, heating to 30-60 degrees C while stirring for 10-15 minutes, and finally heating to 60 degrees C, (c) impregnating the activated carbon in the first mixed liquid, rapidly stirring, absorbing the activated carbon with the first mixed liquid, transferring into a plastic bag and sealing, (d) calcining the once-impregnated activated carbon to obtain a semi-finished product, (e) preparing a second mixed liquid by dissolving activated carbon having 2.5% carbon dioxide in pure water, and placing 20 g carbonate in 500 ml pure water to obtain a second mixed liquid, (f) mixing the first mixed liquid and the second mixed liquid in a mass ratio of 2:1 to obtain a third mixed liquid and maintaining the temperature at 40-55 degrees C, (g) impregnating the semi-finished product in the third mixed liquid for secondary immersion, and placing the activated carbon absorbed with the third mixed liquid in a plastic bag and sealing for more than 15 hours and (h) secondarily calcining.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (d) comprises adding once impregnated activated carbon when the primary calcination when the temperature in the furnace reaches 140-170 degrees C, where the temperature of the air outlet is 140 plus minus 5 degrees C and continuously reacting for 20-50 minutes at 140 plus minus 5 degrees C. The step (h) comprises adding activated carbon after secondary impregnation when the secondary calcination is performed when the temperature in the furnace reaches 60 degrees C, after the activated carbon temperature reaches more than 80 degrees C, continuously reacting for 35-50 minutes, controlling the temperature of the activated carbon to be 90 plus minus 5 degrees C. Preferred Components: In the step (c): the mass of the first mixed liquid accounts for a percentage of the mass of the activated carbon is equal to the water capacity of the activated carbon - the moisture content of the activated carbon. In the step (g): the third mixed liquid used for the second impregnation is used in a distance of not greater than 20 hours.

   WANHUA CHEM NINGBO CO LTD (WHCH-C)
GA 201900876P
AB    NOVELTY - A down-bed-fluidized bed tandem gasification method involves connecting down bed gasifier in series with fluidized bed gasifier, such that inner diameter of chamber of the down bed gasifier gradually decreases from top to bottom, providing pyrolysis material in middle of chamber of the down bed gasifier, dividing interior of the chamber into upper region and lower region, spraying pulverized coal, oxygen and water vapor into the bed of the down bed gasifier from the top, such that gasification reaction occurs in the upper region, generating hydrogen-rich gas and ash, passing a mixture of coal and/or solid waste and carbon dioxide, from inlet of the pyrolysis material, entering lining of the chamber downwards 5-30 degrees into cavity of the down bed gasifier, such that generating heat and performing pyrolysis in the lower region followed by producing syngas, discharging the syngas from upper discharge port of the fluidized bed gasifier and discharging ash from outlet of the gasifier.
   USE - Down-bed-fluidized bed tandem gasification method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing gasification by upgrading solid coal with low metamorphism degree and avoiding the need of special requirements for thermal stability of raw materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A down-bed-fluidized bed tandem gasification method involves connecting down bed gasifier in series with fluidized bed gasifier, such that inner diameter of chamber of the down bed gasifier gradually decreases from top to bottom, providing pyrolysis material in middle of chamber of the down bed gasifier, dividing interior of the chamber into upper region and lower region, spraying pulverized coal, oxygen and water vapor into the bed of the down bed gasifier from the top, such that gasification reaction occurs in the upper region, generating hydrogen-rich gas and ash, passing a mixture of coal and/or solid waste and carbon dioxide, from inlet of the pyrolysis material, entering lining of the chamber downwards 5-30 degrees into cavity of the down bed gasifier, such that generating heat and performing pyrolysis in the lower region, generating crude gas, tar and semi-coke, mixing crude gas, tar and semi-coke with hydrogen-rich gas and ash, obtaining semi-coke that is not fully vaporized and stream of ash and tar, passing obtained product and gasification agent from lower portion of fluidized bed gasifier, such that semi-coke and ash contained in gas stream are vaporized with the gasifying agent to form syngas and ash and tar and crude gas in the gas stream undergo cracking reforming reaction, producing syngas, discharging the syngas from upper discharge port of the fluidized bed gasifier and discharging ash from outlet of the fluidized bed gasifier. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a down bed gasifier-fluidized bed gasifier device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrogen-rich gas comprises carbon monoxide, hydrogen, methane and water vapor. The solid waste is biomass or composition close to coal or charred biomass, preferably petroleum coke, activated carbon or resin.

PN CN108659060-A
TI Preparing stachyose comprises cutting underground stem, leaching in water, dehydrating, drying, granulating, adding lime milk, precipitating, filtering, ion exchanging, decolorizing, refining, concentrating and fluidized bed drying.
AU ZHANG H
   SHEN L
   TAO H
   WEN J
AE DALIAN DONGLIN FOOD CO LTD (DALI-Non-standard)
GA 201883630Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing stachyose comprises pre-processing fresh ground ring underground stem, removing soil and stones, passing the cleaned underground stem through an iron remover, cutting, transferring into a leaching device, adding water, transferring the leachate discharged from the device into a leaching liquid tank, taking out the soaked shredded stem from the top of the leaching device, dehydrating, drying and granulating as a feed, adding lime milk to the leachate, adjusting the pH, introducing carbon dioxide into the leachate, precipitating the lime milk in the leachate along with impurities, filtering to obtain a filtrate, taking into an ion exchanger to remove anions and cations from the filtrate to reduce salt, decolorizing, refining in a mixed resin column, concentrating, fluidized bed drying to obtain white stachyose powder.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing stachyose.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and has high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing stachyose comprises (a) weeding, removing soil and stones from the fresh ground ring underground stem and cleaning, (b) passing the cleaned underground stem through an iron remover, and then cutting into a diameter of 0.3-0.4 cm in a cutting machine, (c) transferring into leaching device, and adding water, (d) transferring the leachate discharged from the device into a leaching liquid tank, taking out the soaked shredded stem from the top of the leaching device, dehydrating, drying and granulating as a feed, (e) adding lime milk to the leachate until a Baume degree of 16-18 Be degrees , adjusting the pH to 10.3-10.8, where the impurities in the leachate gradually aggregate at this time, introducing carbon dioxide into the leachate, adjusting the pH to be 8.5-9, and precipitating the lime milk in the leachate along with impurities, (f) filtering the liquid to obtain a filtrate, transferring the filtrate into a supernatant tank to control the concentration of the dry matter in the filtrate to be 12-14%, (g) feeding the filtrate into an ion exchanger to remove anions and cations from the filtrate to reduce salt, (g) decolorizing the ion-exchanged liquid in an adsorption resin column to adsorb soluble proteins and pigments, (h) refining the decolorized liquid in a mixed resin column to remove salt and odor, (i) concentrating the purified liquid in a vacuum at 60-70 degrees C and controlling the concentration of discharge based on dry basis to be 40-45%, and (j) drying in a concentrated liquid dryer at a negative pressure in the dryer of 100-200 Pa, fluidized bed drying, and controlling the moisture to be below 5% to obtain white stachyose powder having a particle size of 90-110 mesh.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (c): the temperature is 40-50 degrees C for 2-3 hours. The temperature of the dryer is 170-180 degrees C.

PN CN108485054-A
TI Ethylene-vinyl acetate masterbatch comprises preset amount of ethylene-vinyl acetate carrier, inorganic pigment, fiber, dispersing agent, magnesium hydroxide flame retardant and filler.
AU WANG L
   LIU T
AE HEFEI COLOUR PLASTIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HEFE-Non-standard)
GA 201871291N
AB    NOVELTY - An ethylene-vinyl acetate masterbatch comprises 60-80 pts. wt. ethylene-vinyl acetate carrier, 30-40 pts. wt. inorganic pigment, 20-30 pts. wt. fiber, 3-5 pts. wt. dispersing agent, 6-10 pts. wt. magnesium hydroxide flame retardant and 1-2 pts. wt. filler.
   USE - Ethylene-vinyl acetate masterbatch.
   ADVANTAGE - The environmentally-friendly flame retardant ethylene-vinyl acetate masterbatch has low processing cost, high antibacterial rate, small color difference, uniform color, high color stability, and effectively protects people personal health and property safety.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of ethylene-vinyl acetate masterbatch, which involves soaking inorganic pigment in of deionized water for 2-3 times for 1-2 days, filtering, washing the obtained filter residue with distilled water for 3-5 times to remove water-soluble impurities, drying in the microwave at a power of 400-500 W for 2-3 minutes, feeding the obtained pigment and aluminate coupling agent, triethylenediamine and polybutadiene epoxy resin in mass ratio of 20-25:4:1:2 into ultrafine pulverizer, mixing for 1-1.5 hours to obtain modified pigment having particle diameter of 0.5-10 mu m, mixing ethylene-vinyl acetate carrier, fiber, dispersant, magnesium hydroxide flame retardant and filler, stirring at speed of 1000-1200 rpm for 30-50 minutes, cooling, sending to a pulverizer for pulverization to obtain mixture having a particle size of 100-300 mu m, feeding the above modified pigment into an autoclave, adding carbon dioxide, adjusting the pressure in the high pressure reactor to 60-70 MPa, and the temperature to 100-130 degrees C, so that carbon dioxide turns to supercritical fluid, stirring with a magnetic stirrer, completely dissolving modified pigment into the supercritical fluid after 3-5 hours, and adding above mixture, continuously stirring for 20-30 hours, keeping temperature constant to reduce the pressure to 1-5 MPa, continuously stirring for 10-15 hours, carrying out deflation through the vent valve, cooling and taking out the crude color masterbatch, melt-extruding crude color master batch at speed of 200-300 rpm by twin-screw extruder to obtain extruded material, placing above extruded material in running water at temperature of 30-40 degrees C for cooling, drying microwave at power of 400-500 W for 3-5 minutes, granulating pellet by pelletizer to obtain ethylene-vinyl acetate masterbatch with a particle size of 1-10 mm. The screw temperature in zone (I) is 110-120 degrees C, screw temperature in zone (II) is 115-125 degrees C, screw temperature in zone (III) is 120-130 degrees C, screw temperature in zone (IV) is 130-140 degrees C, screw temperature in zone (V) is 125-135 degrees C. The water flow rate is 20-30 l/minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic pigment is titanium white powder, carbon black, yellow cadmium, yellow lead chrome, red cadmium, red iron oxide, yellow iron oxide, iron blue, cobalt blue, brown iron oxide, green chromium oxide or green cobalt. The filler is composed of calcium carbonate, calcium stearate, and barium sulfate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The dispersing agent is dispersing agent YY-5023, maleic anhydride grafted polyethylene wax, and silane coupling agent with mass ratio of 1:1:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Components: The fiber is composed of bamboo charcoal fiber and coconut fiber with mass ratio of (3-4):1.

TI Conductive adhesive tape doped with ultra-thin polypyrrole comprises conductive gel emulsion, polycarbonate diol, isophorone diisocyanate, diethylene glycol, phosphorus trichloride, pentaerythritol, dioctyl sebacate, and benzotriazole.
AU ZHANG Y
   BAO H
   MA H
   LIU Y
   YU S
   CHEN K
   YUAN F
   ZHOU J
AE JIANGSU YALONG NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201860224G
AB    NOVELTY - Conductive adhesive tape doped with ultra-thin polypyrrole comprises 13-20 pts. wt. conductive gel emulsion, 106-110 pts. wt. polycarbonate diol, 46-50 pts. wt. isophorone diisocyanate, 7-9 pts. wt. diethylene glycol, 3-4 pts. wt. phosphorus trichloride, 4-6 pts. wt. pentaerythritol, 2-3 pts. wt. dioctyl sebacate, and 0.8-1 pts. wt. benzotriazole.
   USE - Used as conductive adhesive tape doped with ultra-thin polypyrrole.
   ADVANTAGE - The conductive adhesive tape doped with ultra-thin polypyrrole: has improved dispersion compatibility of the gel in the polyester, uniform stability, and high elongation at break.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - AN INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing conductive adhesive tape doped with ultra-thin polypyrrole, comprising (i) taking benzotriazole, adding 3-4 times of dimethylformamide, and stirring uniformly to obtain amide solution, (ii) taking polycarbonate diol and isophorone diisocyanate into a reaction kettle, inletting nitrogen, adjusting the temperature of the reaction kettle to 75-80 degrees C, maintaining temperature for 2-3 hours, adding diglycol, reacting at 55-60 degrees C for 4-5 hours, discharging, mixing with the solution, and stirring to room temperature to obtain polyurethane solution, (iii) taking phosphorus trichloride, adding into polyurethane solution, ultrasonically processing for 2-3 minutes, adding pentaerythritol, rising temperature to 85-90 degrees C, maintaining temperature for 1-2 hours, adding dioctyl sebacate, and stirring to room temperature to obtain the esterified polyurethane solution, (iv) taking esterification polyurethane solution, mixing with conductive gel emulsion, feeding to a reaction kettle, inletting nitrogen, adjusting the temperature of the reaction kettle to 60-65 degrees C, preserving heat for 2-3 hours, discharging, filtering and washing the precipitate, and drying at room temperature to obtain conductive doped polyurethane, (v) adding conductive doped polyurethane into a screw extruder, extruding, stretching, and shaping to obtain conductive doped polyurethane film, (vi) placing the conductive doped polyurethane film into a high-pressure reaction kettle, using supercritical carbon dioxide fluid purge system, purging air, heating the system to 100-105 degrees C, sealing reaction kettle, injecting carbon dioxide fluid saturation, controlling pressure at 14-14.5 MPa for 6-7 hours, fastly vacuum extracting, placing in ice water bath, and cooling and forming to obtain polyurethane foam substrate, (vii) coating the adhesive on the polyurethane foam substrate, coating the polyurethane foam substrate with adhesive disposed in the pressure vessel, filling nitrogen pressure to 3-4 MPa for 2-5 minutes, and forming adhesive layer on the polyurethane foam substrate to obtain the adhesive tape main body, and (viii) applying a release film to the outside of the adhesive coating on the tape body to obtain ultra-thin polypyrrole doped conductive tape.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The conductive adhesive tape comprises 30-40 pts. wt. pyrrole, 0.7-1 pts. wt. initiator, 20-25 pts. wt. nano silica aerogel, 0.6-1 pts. wt. lauryl dimethyl amine oxide, 4-6 pts. wt. rosin alcohol, and 0.5-1 pts. wt. 4, 4'-diphenyl methane diisocyanate. The conductive gel emulsion is prepared by (i) adding 20-30 times of deionized water to initiator, and stirring uniformly, (ii) taking pyrrole and lauryl dimethyl amine oxide, mixing, adding 17-20 times of deionized water in the mixture, stirring uniformly, adding rosin alcohol, and stirring at 60-65 degrees C for 10-15 minutes to obtain pyrrole dispersion, (iii) taking nano silica aerogel, adding to the resultant dispersion, stirring uniformly, feeding into the reaction kettle, inletting nitrogen, adjusting the temperature of the reaction kettle to 70-75 degrees C, heat preserving for 3-4 hours, discharging and cooling, stirring, and reacting for 5-6 hours to obtain conductive gel, and (iv) taking 4,4'-diphenylmethane diisocyanate, adding to the conductive gel, and ultrasonically treating for 5-10 minutes to obtain the conductive gel emulsion. The initiator is ammonium persulfate, sodium persulfate or potassium persulfate.

PN US2018066191-A1
TI Making processed biogas i.e. gaseous fuel composition comprises e.g. inputting renewable unprocessed organic-carbon-containing feedstock into a system comprising first and second subsystem and passing the feedstock through first subsystem.
AU SCALZO P J
   TAIT C D
   VAN THORRE D M
   CATTO M L
AE BIOMASS ENERGY ENHANCEMENTS LLC (BIOM-Non-standard)
   WOLF S F (WOLF-Individual)
GA 201818869J
AB    NOVELTY - Making a processed biogas that is a gaseous fuel composition, comprises e.g.: inputting an unprocessed organic-carbon-containing feedstock (414) that is renewable and includes free water, intercellular water, intracellular water, intracellular water-soluble salts, and at least some plant cells comprising cell walls that include lignin, hemicellulose and microfibrils within fibrils, into a system comprising a first and a second subsystem; and passing the unprocessed organic-carbon-containing feedstock through the first subsystem, preferably a beneficiation subsystem process.
   USE - The process is useful for making a processed biogas that is a gaseous fuel composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: provides the processed biogas which has an energy density of at least 700 BTU/ft3 (26 MJ/m3), a carbon monoxide concentration of less than 20 vol.%, and a carbon dioxide concentration of less than 15 vol.%; provides the processed organic-carbon-containing feedstock that has a water content of less than 20 wt.%, a water-soluble salt content that is decreased by at least 60 wt.% on a dry basis over that of unprocessed organic-carbon-containing feedstock, and a cost per weight of removing the water and water-soluble salt that is reduced to less than 60% of the cost per weight of similar water removal from known mechanical, known physiochemical, or known thermal processes ; and utilizes renewable unprocessed organic-carbon-containing feedstock (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Making a processed biogas that is a gaseous fuel composition, comprises: inputting an unprocessed organic-carbon-containing feedstock (414) that is renewable and includes free water, intercellular water, intracellular water, intracellular water-soluble salts, and at least some plant cells comprising cell walls that include lignin, hemicellulose and microfibrils within fibrils, into a system comprising a first and a second subsystem; passing the unprocessed organic-carbon-containing feedstock through the first subsystem, preferably a beneficiation subsystem process, to result in processed organic-carbon-containing feedstock having a water content of less than 20 wt.% and a salt content that is reduced by at least 60 wt.% on a dry basis from that of the unprocessed organic-carbon-containing feedstock; and passing the processed organic-carbon-containing feedstock through the second subsystem, preferably an oxygen-deprived thermal subsystem process, to result in the processed biogas having an energy density of at least 700 BTU/ft3 (26 MJ/m3), a carbon monoxide concentration of less than 20 vol.%, and a carbon dioxide concentration of less than 15 vol.%.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic side view of a reaction chamber in a beneficiation subsystem.
   Reaction chamber (400)
   Solvent (412)
   Unprocessed organic-carbon-containing feedstock (414)
   Vapor explosion section (420)
   Compaction section (430)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The beneficiation subsystem process comprises: inputting the unprocessed organic-carbon-containing feedstock into a beneficiation subsystem reaction chamber (400); exposing the feedstock to hot solvent (412) under pressure for a time at conditions specific to the feedstock to make some regions of the cell walls comprising crystallized cellulosic fibrils, lignin, and hemicellulose more able to be penetratable by water-soluble salts without dissolving more than 25% of the lignin and hemicellulose; removing the pressure so as to penetrate the more penetratable regions to create porous feedstock with open pores in the plant cell walls; and pressing the porous feedstock with conditions that include an adjustable compaction pressure versus time profile and compaction time duration, and between pressure plates configured to prevent felt from forming and blocking escape from the reaction chamber of intracellular and intercellular water, and intracellular water-soluble salts, and to create processed organic-carbon-containing feedstock that has a water content of less than 20 wt.% and a water-soluble salt content that is decreased by at least 60 wt.% on a dry basis from that of unprocessed organic-carbon-containing feedstock. The oxygen-deprived thermal subsystem process further comprises inputting the processed organic-carbon-containing feedstock into an oxygen-deprived reaction chamber configured to heat the processed organic-carbon-containing feedstock in an atmosphere that contains less than 5% oxygen to a temperature sufficient to convert at least some processed organic-carbon-containing feedstock into processed biogas and processed biochar. The oxygen-deprived thermal subsystem process further comprises: inputting the processed organic-carbon-containing feedstock into a horizontal sublimating reaction chamber largely contained within a hot box and configured to be able to heat from an ambient temperature to an operating sublimation temperature, operate at a sublimation temperature, and cool from a operating sublimation temperature to an ambient temperature without leaking any hot product gas fuel from the reaction chamber into the hot box or atmosphere, or leaking any oxygen from outside the hot box into the hot box; heating the processed organic-carbon-containing feedstock to a sublimating temperature before it is able to form a liquid phase; maintaining the temperature at a sublimation temperature for a residence time that is as long a time as needed to convert the processed organic-carbon-containing feedstock to processed biogas and processed biochar; and separating the processed biogas from the processed biochar. The oxygen-deprived thermal subsystem process further comprises: inputting the processed organic-carbon-containing feedstock into a vertical sublimating reaction chamber; heating processed organic-carbon-containing feedstock to a sublimating temperature before it is able to form a liquid phase; maintaining the temperature at a sublimation temperature for a residence time that is as long a time as needed to convert the processed organic-carbon-containing feedstock to processed biogas and processed biochar; and separating the processed biogas from the processed biochar. The beneficiation subsystem process and the oxygen-deficient thermal subsystem process, further comprise: inputting the unprocessed organic-carbon-containing feedstock into a reaction chamber; exposing the feedstock to hot solvent under pressure for a time at conditions specific to the feedstock to make some regions of the cell walls comprising crystallized cellulosic fibrils, lignin, and hemicellulose more able to be penetratable by water-soluble salts without dissolving more than 25% of the lignin and hemicellulose; removing the pressure so as to penetrate the more penetratable regions to create porous feedstock with open pores in the plant cell walls; and pressing the porous feedstock with conditions that include an adjustable compaction pressure versus time profile and compaction time duration, and between pressure plates configured to prevent felt from forming and blocking escape from the reaction chamber of intracellular and intercellular water, and intracellular water-soluble salts and to create processed organic-carbon-containing feedstock, and the oxygen-deprived thermal subsystem process, further comprises inputting the processed organic-carbon-containing feedstock into an oxygen-deprived reaction chamber configured to heat the processed organic-carbon-containing feedstock in an atmosphere that contains less than 5% oxygen to a temperature sufficient to convert at least some processed organic-carbon-containing feedstock into processed biogas and processed biochar.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
