PT P
PN AT521022-A4; AT521022-B1
TI Generating electrical energy and heat comprises utilizing e.g. residual gases biogenic gases from methane, and hydrocarbons, compressing residual gases are compressed and performing oxidation.
AU GRUBER-SCHMIDT J
AE GS GRUBER-SCHMIDT GMBH (GSGR-Non-standard)
GA 2019875760
AB    NOVELTY - Generating electrical energy and heat, comprising a container with a compressor, a control valve, a combustion chamber, a pressure swing adsorption with a control valve, with heat exchanger, with a condenser for water vapor and a water tank, a condenser for carbon dioxide with a tank, a compressor, a thermionic generator, a magneto-hydrodynamic generator, a heat exchanger , a vapor metal condenser having an associated tank, a pump having a liquid metal control armature, a noble gas tank, a compressor with associated control armature, comprises utilizing residual gases biogenic gases from methane, and hydrocarbons, landfill gas from methane and carbon dioxide, process gases from carbon monoxide and hydrogen, flare gases from methane and hydrogen in gaseous state, which have a calorific value of 1.5-10 kWh/m3, preferably 5 kWh/m3. The residual gases are compressed by a hydraulic piston compressor to a pressure of 30-100 bar, preferably 50 bar.
   USE - The method is useful for generating electrical energy and heat.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can be carried out with a high energy density, allows conversion of the energy-rich heat directly into electrical energy, utilizes the energy-rich waste heat as process heat, does not produce exhaust gas emissions or highly radiating residues, thus it has zero emission and s environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Generating electrical energy and heat, comprising a container with a compressor, a control valve, a combustion chamber, a pressure swing adsorption with a control valve, with heat exchanger, with a condenser for water vapor and a water tank, a condenser for carbon dioxide with a tank, a compressor, a thermionic generator, a magneto-hydrodynamic generator, a heat exchanger , a vapor metal condenser having an associated tank, a pump having a liquid metal control armature, a noble gas tank, a compressor with associated control armature, comprises utilizing residual gases biogenic gases from methane, and hydrocarbons, landfill gas from methane and carbon dioxide, process gases from carbon monoxide and hydrogen, flare gases from methane and hydrogen in gaseous state, which have a calorific value of 1.5-10 kWh/m3, preferably 5 kWh/m3. The residual gases are compressed by a hydraulic piston compressor to a pressure of 30-100 bar, preferably 50 bar. The flow rate of 100-5000 m3/h, preferably 2000 m3/ h is regulated by a hydraulically driven control valve. The residual gases in a combustion chamber are oxidized together with oxygen from the air. The oxidation takes place in a combustion chamber (9) on the surface of balls. The balls in a combustion chamber are made of ceramic material aluminum nitride, to withstand a temperature of 2200 degrees C. The balls have a diameter of 15-50 mm, preferably 30 mm, to allow a turbulent flow of the residual gases, oxygen and exhaust gas. The pressure loss in a combustion chamber is 0.1-0.5 bar, preferably, 0.25 bar. The exhaust gas from a combustion chamber comprises carbon dioxide and water vapor, and has 5-50%, preferably 15% carbon dioxide. The exhaust gas from a combustion chamber has a minimum temperature of 1000-2000 degrees C, preferably 1800 degrees C. The exhaust gas via heat exchangers is cooled to a temperature of 300 degrees C. The water vapor in the exhaust gas in the condenser is condensed and cooled to a temperature of 50 degrees C. The recovered water is stored as condensate in a tank. The exhaust gas from carbon dioxide and inert gases in the condenser is cooled to a temperature of 10 degrees C. The liquid carbon dioxide from the exhaust gas in a tank is stored. The inert gases from the exhaust gas are discharged to the environment via the control valve. The proportion of inert gases from the exhaust gas are present in a proportion of 0.1-5%, preferably 2%. The waste heat from the exhaust gas is used in the form of a closed circuit The fluid has 20% helium, 40% potassium mercury, 40% sodium mercury, and a hydraulically driven reciprocating compressor supplies the helium to a pressure of a 1-5, preferably 3 bar. A pump compresses the liquid potassium mercury and sodium mercury to pressure of 1-5 bar, preferably 3 bar compressed. The vaporous sodium mercury and potassium mercury are condensed in a condenser at a temperature of 150 degrees C. The liquid metal is stored in a tank which is stored in a tank. That helium is stored in a tank, which is fed to the circuit via a hydraulic piston compressor with the associated control valve. The mixture of helium, sodium mercury and potassium mercury is used as fluid in the circuit and a temperature of 1200-2000 degrees C, preferably 1700 degrees C. The mixture of helium and sodium mercury and potassium mercury is compressed to pressure of 1-5 bar, preferably 3 bar. The mixture of helium and sodium mercury and potassium mercury in a thermionic generator is cooled to 800-1200 degrees C, preferably 1000 degrees C. The thermionic generator uses the heat from the gas and steam mixture to heat a cathode and thus enables the emission of electrons and a temperature of 1200-1700 degrees C, preferably 1600 degrees C. The electrons of the cathode are captured by a cooled anode, the temperature of the anode is 200-500 degrees C, preferably 250 degrees C. An ultra-high vacuum with a minimum pressure of 10-12, preferably 10-7 mbar is provided between the cathode and anode. A gate in the form of a charged grid electrode is between the cathode and the anode to suck the electron cloud in front of the cathode and to accelerate the electrons. The gas and vapor mixture after the thermionic generator is fed to a magneto-hydrodynamic generator. The gas and vapor mixture is inductively converted into an excited plasma state via magnetic coils. The plasma state of the gas and vapor mixture has a high conductivity due to the high density of sodium ions and potassium ions. The plasma in the magneto-hydrodynamic generator is accelerated to supersonic speed via a Laval nozzle. The acceleration of the plasma is so great that the compression impingement takes place in the Lavall nozzle at the nozzle end, thus allowing a maximum electrical yield. The pressure ratio is supercritical in front of the Lavall nozzle entrance and at the Lavall nozzle exit so that the mass flow through the nozzle and thus the magneto-hydrodynamic generator is constant. The magnetic coils in the magneto-dynamic generator have a magnetic inductance of 0.5-10 Tesla, preferably 7 Tesla. The electrodes in the magneto-dynamic generator offset by 90 degrees to the magnetic coils are arranged to allow the maximum electrical yield according to the law of induction. The temperature of the gas and vapor mixture at the outlet of the magneto-hydrodynamic generator is 300-600 degrees C, preferably 400 degrees C. The gas and vapor mixture in the heat exchanger is cooled to a temperature of 300-400 degrees C, preferably 350 degrees C. The heat obtained with the heat exchanger is used as process heat, which has a flow temperature of 95 degrees C and a return temperature of 60 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide from the tank is fed to the heat exchanger via the pump and the associated control valve. The carbon dioxide is compressed via the pump to a pressure of 30-70 bar, preferably 50 bar. The carbon dioxide is heated with the heat exchanger to a temperature of 96-250 degrees C, preferably 150 degrees C. The heated carbon dioxide after the heat exchanger has a vapor state. The heated carbon dioxide of the combustion chamber is supplied. The heat exchanger used as a recuperator. The heat from the exhaust gas after the magneto-hydrodynamic generator is used to preheat the gas and vapor mixture to a temperature of 250-400 degrees C, preferably 350 degrees C. The gamma radiation that results from the fusion of electrons and positrons has a low energy of 0.1-0.6 Mev, preferably 0.51 MeV. The gamma radiation in the plasma reactor is converted to thermal neutron radiation using beryllium oxide as an isotope and the neutrons have a kinetic energy of 0.1-0.6 MeV, preferably 0.5 MeV. The plasma in the plasma generator is heated to a minimum temperature of 800-2000 degrees C, preferably 1700 degrees C. The electrons from a hot cathode are used as the source for the positrons, which are fired at a target of tungsten with energy of 10 MeV. The isotope emitters e.g. sodium22, cobalt60 or cadmium133 can be used as the source for the positrons required, which have a particle density of 1015- 1020, preferably 1016 N/sec.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN WO2021220455-A1; JP2020550883-X
TI Vaporization utilization hydrocarbon manufacturing system has heat transfer device for vaporization which supplies first and second vaporization amount to first and second vaporizers using heat medium respectively.
AU SHINZO I
AE EUREKA ENG INC (EURE-Non-standard)
GA 2021C23283
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1a) has a reaction tube (41) that generates and sends out reaction gas containing hydrocarbon by hydrogenating hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide gas under a hydrogenation reaction catalyst filled inside. A hydrogenation reactor (40) is provided with a cooling unit (42) that transfers reaction heat generated in the hydrogenation reaction from reaction gas to heat medium to maintain the inside of reaction tube. A heat transfer device (50) for vaporization supplies the first vaporization amount required to vaporize liquefied hydrogen at the first flow rate into the hydrogen gas at the first pressure and first temperature at the first generated volume per unit time to a first vaporizer (30) using the heat medium and the second vaporization amount required to vaporize liquefied carbon dioxide at the second flow rate into carbon dioxide gas at second pressure and second temperature at the second generated volume per unit time to a second vaporizer (35) using heat medium.
   USE - Vaporization utilization hydrocarbon manufacturing system.
   ADVANTAGE - The pressurizing energy of the raw material gas is significantly reduced. Hydrocarbons including methane are produced inexpensively and with low energy if carbon dioxide emitted from the combustion of fossil fuels is recovered and reacted with carbon dioxide-free hydrogen, which contributes to the prevention of global warming. The pressurizing energy of hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide is significantly reduced, and hydrocarbons such as methane are produced inexpensively and with low energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A liquefied hydrogen supply device (10) supplies liquefied hydrogen at the first flow rate. A liquefied carbon dioxide supply device (20) supplies liquefied carbon dioxide at the second flow rate of a predetermined ratio with respect to the first flow rate. The first vaporizer vaporizes the liquefied hydrogen supplied from the liquefied hydrogen supply device into hydrogen gas. The second vaporizer vaporizes the liquefied carbon dioxide supplied from the liquefied carbon dioxide supply device into carbon dioxide gas. The hydrogen gas and the carbondioxide gas are supplied as a mixed gas by connecting with the first vaporizer and the second vaporizer. A liquefied raw material supply device supplies one of the hydrogen and carbon dioxide as a liquefied raw material in a liquid state at a third flow rate. The other raw material gas supply device supplies the other of the hydrogen and the carbon dioxide as the other raw material gas in a gaseous state at the fourth flow rate, the fourth pressure, and the fourth temperature. The third vaporizer vaporizes the liquefied raw material supplied from the liquefied raw material supply device into a raw material gas. The one raw material gas and the other raw material gas are supplied as a mixed gas containing hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide gas as components by being communicated with the third vaporizer and the other raw material gas supply device. The reaction tube generates and sends out a reaction gas containing the hydrocarbon by hydrogenating the hydrogen gas and the carbon dioxide gas under the hydrogenation catalyst filled inside. The third vaporizer uses the heat medium to vaporize the liquefied raw material at the third flow rate into the one raw material gas at the third pressure and the third temperature at the third generated volume per unit time. The second vaporizer uses the heat medium to vaporize the liquefied carbon dioxide of the second flow rate into the carbon dioxide gas at the second pressure and the second temperature at the second generated volume per unit time. A start-up heat supply device supplies the amount of heat required for starting the vaporization-utilized hydrocarbon production system to the vaporization heat transfer device. The liquefied carbon dioxide supply device supplies the liquefied carbon dioxide supplied from the carbon dioxide gas liquefier at the second flow rate of the predetermined ratio with respect to the first flow rate. A heat utilization unit in which the heat medium is circulated and the reaction heat is used is provided.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram illustrating an overall structure of the vaporization utilization hydrocarbon manufacturing system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Vaporization utilization hydrocarbon manufacturing system (1a)

   First vaporizer (30)
   Second vaporizer (35)
   Hydrogenation reactor (40)
   Reaction tube (41)
   Cooling unit (42)
